Finally, a proof-of-concept pentahedral device is experimentally characterized, demonstrating diverse functionalities including the generation of highly directive beams, tri-beam radiation, dual-vortex beams, and dual-beam in orthogonal linearly polarized (LP) and decoupled co-/cross-circularly polarized (CP) channels. Measured and simulated results coincide well and illustrate the effectiveness of the space-polarization multiplexing concept in versatile wavefront control over a pentahedral platform.

title: From Time-Frequency to Angular Domain: A Transformative Paradigm for Massive MIMO Systems
authors: Felipe Vico, Helen Urgelles, Jose F. Monserrat, Yiqun Ge
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Wireless systems have traditionally organized transmission, estimation, and feedback on a time–frequency grid. While effective at moderate array sizes, this paradigm becomes difficult to scale to ultra-large MIMO: the amount of channel state information (CSI) grows rapidly with antenna count, and feedback and processing overhead can become impractical in wideband operation. This paper argues that much of this burden can be reduced by working in a representation that matches propagation structure. By mapping channel information to the angular domain, the channel can be described by a small number of dominant propagation paths, which often remain limited even as arrays grow. Building on this idea, we compare conventional FFT-based angular projection with two adaptive low-rank alternatives based on partial singular value decomposition (SVD): an iterative Krylov-subspace method and a randomized SVD scheme. Unlike fixed-grid FFT compression, the SVD-based approaches estimate the dominant subspaces directly from channel observations, mitigating basis mismatch in near-field or irregular scattering conditions. We analyze complexity and protocol implications, and evaluate performance using ray-traced simulations at cmWave and mmWave frequencies. In the considered scenarios, partial SVD methods approach full-CSI capacity while substantially reducing feedback overhead, with the randomized variant offering the most favorable accuracy–latency trade-off.

title: Building Knowledge Automata with Neural Networks and Long Short-Term Memory
authors: Shree Smeka J, V Sheeja Kumari, M. Vijayaraj, S. P. Santhoshkumar, T. Nalini, Sunil Kumar Sahu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The creation of Knowledge Automata intelligent systems that can learn, reason, and change over time is one of the main challenges in the rapidly developing field of artificial intelligence (AI). In order to create such automata, this work focuses on integrating Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) architectures into artificial neural networks. The shortcomings of traditional recurrent neural networks (RNNs) are successfully addressed by LSTM networks, a specialized type of RNN that excels at simulating temporal patterns and long-range dependencies in sequential data. Context-aware decision-making and memory retention are made possible by the suggested method, which makes use of LSTMs for inference and pattern recognition in sequential inputs. In order to improve flexibility, learning effectiveness, and natural language processing capabilities, we create a hybrid architecture that combines LSTM with additional neural networks. On tasks involving sequential and temporal data, experimental evaluations show that the LSTM-based knowledge automata perform better than classical models. The findings show increased precision, adaptability, and durability, which makes them ideal for uses like automated decision support systems and intelligent personal assistants. This work highlights the potential of LSTM-enhanced architectures in the future of AI and advances intelligent systems by offering a strong framework for building dynamic, adaptive knowledge automata.

title: Comparative Deep Learning and Explainable AI Approaches for Automated Detection of Arsenic Skin Lesions
authors: Md. Siam Uddin Molla Antor, Nitun Kumar Podder, Nakib Aman, Md Raihanul Haque, Shahriar Siddique Arjon, Tamanna Yasmin
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Arsenic contamination continues to be a significant public health threat in some parts of South Asia, leading to severe skin lesions, as well as an increased likelihood of skin cancer, from long-term exposure. This study presents a deep learning framework for the automated detection of arsenic skin lesions with the use of a modified VGG16 architecture, including Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) for explainability. The proposed framework was developed and evaluated on an open-access dataset of arsenic-induced skin lesions, achieving validation accuracy of 99.50%, which is better accuracy compared to lightweight baseline (i.e., MobileNetV2, SqueezeNet) models. To bolster interpretability and build a sense of clinical trust, additional visualization techniques, including LIME and t-SNE, were deployed within this study to confirm that the model was detecting relevant areas in the lesions. The experimental results presented in this study demonstrate excellent classification performance that was achieved with little overfitting, highlighting the framework as promising for successful real-world screening and potential for early intervention in austere clinical settings. In the end, this work establishes the foundation for strong and explainable AI solutions to assist in the early diagnosis of environmental dermatosis.

title: Comparative Analysis Of SMS Spam Classification
authors: Abubakarsiddik Desai, Bhaskar Kamble, Mayuri Hegade, Rakshita Mathapati, Gujanatti Rudrappa
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The number of mobile phone users is growing steadily each day. Short Message Service (SMS) is a text messaging application accessible on smartphones and entry-level mobile devices. Consequently, the usage of SMS has increased in recent years. Spam messages have increased in tandem with this trend. Spammers send spam messages to achieve financial or business advantages, such as promoting market growth, spreading details of lottery tickets. Thus, spam classification has gained particular attention in recent times. In the presented work, Machine Learning (ML) methods viz., Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Naive Bayes (NB) are are applied for SMS spam classification. The comparative study showed that and accuracy of 94% and 88.8% was achieved for SVM and NB respectively. The SVM outperformed NB in terms accuracy, precision, recall and F1 score with values of 0.94, 0.95, 0.93 and 0.94 respectively.

title: Enhancing the Accuracy of Colorectal Tumor Classification through Preprocessing Strategies
authors: A. Santhoshi, A. Muthukumaravel
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Colorectal cancer is a significant health concern, demanding accurate and timely tumor classification for optimal patient care. The proposed paper explores the novel application of advanced preprocessing techniques to improve the accuracy of colorectal tumor classification. The study employed TCGA dataset split into training, validation, and test sets, totaling 1000 samples and 20 features per sample. Preprocessing strategies included noise reduction, contrast enhancement, adaptive filtering, and feature selection based on deep feature extraction using a pre-trained Convolutional Neural Network, Principal Component Analysis for dimensionality reduction, z-score normalization, and adaptive data normalization. The results showcased the transformative potential of preprocessing: noise reduction yielded an accuracy of 92.3%, deep feature extraction attained 93.7% accuracy, and adaptive data normalization resulted in 94.6% accuracy. Moreover, when a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier was employed, the overall classification accuracy reached 97.8% with preprocessing, compared to 89.2% without. These findings highlight the pivotal role of preprocessing in achieving precise and efficient colorectal tumor classification, with potential implications for early detection and personalized treatment.

title: Effective Classification of Paddy Leaf Diseases: An Ensemble Technique with Transfer Learning Approach to Expand Agricultural Productivity
authors: Md. Shahid Uz Zaman, Mohiuddin Ahmed, Rizwana Mostafa
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: As a prominent rice-producing and consuming nation, Bangladesh plays a vital role in the global rice industry, essential for its economic stability and food security. Timely detection and classification of diseases in rice plants are imperative for ensuring healthy growth and effective treatment application. However, the current manual approach to disease detection is labor-intensive and relies heavily on farmer expertise. Therefore, there is a pressing need for automated systems. Leveraging Machine Learning concepts offers a promising solution, enhancing efficiency compared to visual observation methods. In this study, we propose an Ensemble Pre-trained Net (EPN) Model, which integrates two carefully crafted customized pre-trained models ( Model-1 and Model-2) to classify nine distinct types of paddy diseases and normal paddy leaves. Both of these customized models combine traditional Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures with transfer learning techniques, utilizing Densenet201 and Densenet169 as foundational models. The fusion of Model-1 and Model-2 in the EPN framework demonstrates a notable accuracy of 96.50% on the test dataset. The utilization of meticulously engineered pre-trained models within our EPN model showcases enhanced efficiency in classifying paddy leaf diseases compared to other prior automated systems.

title: Edge Enabled Privacy Preserving Multi Violation Detection and Behavioral Risk Scoring for Intelligent Transport Systems in India
authors: Prabhu D, Haneesha Basam, Sasi Kumar Reddy Siddala, Vinod Kumar Nevuluri
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper presents a novel, low cost, edge deployed AI system for real time detection of critical two wheeler traffic violation including helmet non usage, triple riding, mobile phone use while riding, wrong side driving, and red light jumping beyond urban intersections on Indian highways and rural roads. We integrate a privacy preserving pipeline that hashes personally identifiable data to protect user privacy, and propose a behavioral risk scoring model that aggregates detected violations over time to help prioritize enforcement efforts. Our prototype, built using lightweight deep learning models on affordable edge devices, demonstrates promising results with an mAP of 91.2% and real time inference at 18 FPS, while significantly reducing bandwidth and storage requirements compared to centralized systems. This framework aims to extend Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS) into non urban contexts, enhancing road safety through proactive and privacy conscious AI enforcement.

title: Automated Stroke Detection from CT Images Using CNN-GA-LSTM with Grad-CAM Visualization
authors: Leela V, Vijaylakshmi S, Miruthula D, Santhosh Karthick M, Sibi P
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Stroke is one of leading causes of death and disability worldwide, necessitating early and accurate diagnosis. This paper presents a deep learning-based stroke identification system using CT brain images, incorporating Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for automatic feature extraction, Genetic Algorithm (GA) for feature selection, and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) for classification. Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) is integrated to provide the visual interpretability by highlighting stroke-affected regions in the brain scans. The model was trained and evaluated on labeled dataset, with CNN only achieving an accuracy of 89.2%. When combined with LSTM, accuracy increased to93.4%, and the inclusion of GA further improved accuracy to 96.5%. Grad-CAM did not influence performance metrics but offered critical visual explanation to enhance clinical trust. The proposed model demonstrates high accuracy and interpretability, making it a reliable tool for automated stroke diagnosis in the medical applications.

title: Performance Evaluation of Levenberg–Marquardt Backpropagation Based ANN MPPT for Photovoltaic Systems Under Dynamic Load and Environmental Variations
authors: Mahabaleshwara Bhat P, Subramanya Bhat
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This work presents a maximum power point tracking (MPPT) technique for photovoltaic (PV) systems that employs an artificial neural network (ANN), with emphasis on its effectiveness under changing load and environmental conditions. The voltage at the maximum power point is estimated using an ANN model trained with the Levenberg–Marquardt (LM) backpropagation algorithm, based on input parameters such as temperature and solar irradiance. A Proportional–Integral–Derivative (PID) controller, tuned through a Genetic Algorithm (GA), is employed to handle the error between the actual PV output voltage and the voltage predicted by the ANN. A PWM signal generated from the output of the GA tuned PID controller is used to control the boost converter's switching, thereby adjusting the output voltage according to the ANN's predicted value. The system is developed and simulated in MATLAB/Simulink, and its performance is analyzed across different load scenarios to assess tracking accuracy and stability. The proposed method maintains a high tracking efficiency of up to 99.32%, with power output closely matching the PV input throughout the simulation. It effectively adapts to dynamic conditions, achieving faster convergence, improved power extraction, and a rapid settling time of about 12 ms with minimal overshoot of 11.7%.

title: SecureGrid: A Robust Voting Ensemble Model for Non-Technical Loss (NTL) Detection in Smart Meter Data
authors: Md. Sabbir Hossain Molla, Md. Tanjil Islam Aronno, Md Tanvir Rahman Sahed, Md Nasif Mahmud Zishan, A K M Nazrul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Power theft is a serious concern for electricity providers all over the world. It makes the power supply less reliable, raises the load consumption, and costs more money. As the global energy sector moves towards using smart grid (SG) technologies, the use of advanced data analysis tools has created new ways to deal with these problems, but electricity theft detection (ETD) is still a major issue. Traditional methods often do not work well because of issues like imbalanced data distributions, overfitting, high-dimensional data, and too much dependency on individual detection techniques. This study’s goal is to address these problems by developing a more effective and robust ETD model using modern prediction algorithms. This study addresses one of the voting ensemble models that employs some of the best-known machine learning boosting classifiers, like extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost), light gradient boosting (LightGBM), and categorical boosting (CatBoost) to enhance the prediction accuracy. We use a gated recurrent unit (GRU) to extract features, which makes it easier to work with time series data resulting in the improvement of model performance. There are three major tasks performed: synthetic minority oversampling technique with tomek link (SMOTETomek) was used for data pre-processing, a recurrent neural network (RNN) was used for the temporal feature extraction, and SecureGrid was proposed as a new voting-based ensemble model. The proposed model shows that it can achieve a test accuracy of 93%, an F1 score of 78%, and 97% ROC-AUC in the data set of the State Grid Corporation of China (SGCC). The proposed model outperforms traditional boosting models that have been used on similar datasets. We found that our proposed SecureGrid model has the ability to significantly enhance ETD in SGs. It offers a robust and near to accurate solution which is much better than current approaches.

title: Real-Time Narrative Generation: A Framework for API-Driven Story Summarization Using Pre-Trained Language Models
authors: Sakhawat Hossen, Md. Abdullah Sumon, M Waqif Ul Huq, Md. Arafath Yeasin Nihan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: In today’s fast-paced digital ecosystem, real-time access to accurate and contextually relevant narratives has become increasingly vital across domains like journalism, education, and digital storytelling. Traditional narrative generation systems—built on static, pre-compiled datasets—fail to adapt to emergent topics and rapid information updates. This research proposes a dynamic, API-driven narrative generation framework that integrates real-time content retrieval with cutting-edge pretrained language models, including T5, Pegasus, and GPT-4. The framework utilizes the Wikipedia REST API as a live knowledge source, incorporating lightweight preprocessing, prompt engineering, and inference strategies to generate semantically rich, fluent summaries. Our system demonstrates significant gains in ROUGE and BLEU metrics across multiple models and includes a detailed human evaluation study involving 15 participants across diverse academic backgrounds. In contrast to prior work, our approach enables on-the-fly summarization without domain-specific training or manual curation. The codebase and data pipeline have been released publicly to ensure full reproducibility. We further include an ablation study to quantify the effects of individual pipeline components such as preprocessing and prompt formatting. Future directions include multilingual expansion using mT5, integration with web news and social media APIs (e.g., RSS feeds, Twitter), and domain-specific adaptation using parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods. This work offers a scalable, transparent, and adaptable foundation for the next generation of narrative AI systems.

title: Strengthening SSH Security with Multi-Factor Authentication and eBPF Monitoring
authors: Asish Gummidi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: SSH access remains a high-risk entry point for cyberattacks, often exploited through credential theft, user misconfigurations, and social engineering. While security measures like credential rotation and certificate-based authentication help, they do not fully prevent unauthorized access. Traditional Linux security mechanisms, such as Mandatory Access Controls (MAC), lack the flexibility to enforce real-time, fine-grained SSH session protection. This paper explores the integration of Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) to add an extra layer of identity verification, reducing the risk of compromised credentials. Additionally, it examines the use of eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) to enhance SSH security by enabling real-time session monitoring, anomaly detection, and adaptive access control policies. By combining MFA with eBPF-driven security enforcement, this approach strengthens SSH defenses against evolving threats while maintaining system performance and scalability.

title: Intelligent Currency Recognition and Fake Note Detection Using Deep Features and UV Image Analysis
authors: Maheswari S, M. Amutha, Bharathi Ramesh Kumar, Boovitha D, T. Chandrasekar, G. Vijayakumar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Forgery of money has remained a big threat to economies, particularly those whose countries operate on cash. Conventional color and edge-based traditional methods are not very effective in the detection of good-quality fake notes and lack scalability. It presents a smart dual-spectrum framework of currency authentication that combines DNN with UV image analysis. A two-branch ResNet converts simultaneously both the RGB and UV input streams, and a statistical UV activation mapping method improves counterfeit discrimination. A personalized dataset of rare and counterfeit blue chips of five denominations was employed during the evaluation of the system. The model presented had an accuracy of 98.2% precision of 97.9%, a recall of 98.4 % and an AUC of 0.991. TF versions compiled with TensorRT into the GPU platform had an average inference time of 22 ms. the system is faster, more accurate, and more capable of pattern analysis on UV patterns compared to existing methods, and therefore it is more than adequate to be used in a banking and retail implementation of currency validation.

title: Quantum-Safe Cryptography for Future-Proof Network Security
authors: Guntaka Prabhakara Reddy, A. Prashanthi, V.S.N. Murthy, P S S N Mowlika, D Venkatesh, Mrutyunjaya S Yalawar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Quantum computing brings a severe threat to classical cryptographic systems which is why the need to construct quantum-safe cryptographic algorithms. This paper discusses QS cryptographic approach as the cornerstone of the secure network of tomorrow that can mitigate future risks in existing systems. We consider lattice, hash, and code-based quantum resistance algorithms and cryptographic techniques. Thus, by evaluating quantum technologies regarding their capabilities to counter quantum threats, the purpose of this study is to reveal effective heuristics to achieve secure communication in the post-quainter world. Other factors that have been considered include the issue of how to implement such ideas, how best to measure organizational performance and how the above ideas can be integrated into the current organizational infrastructure. The findings point towards the need to make a shift towards quantum-safe security in order to facilitate sustainable security within the network.

title: Optimized Hybrid CNN-Random Forest Model for Thermal Imaging-Based Water Stress Detection in Large-Scale Agriculture
authors: Mynavathi R, Rajendran P, Karisni V, Kiran M, Baranidharan S, Kavisri G
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This study formulates and tests a thermal imaging-based method for real time crop water stress detection and classification with high-resolution thermal sensors and machine learning. Conventional irrigation practices are based on visual observation or fixed timing, which results in inefficient water use, delayed detection of stress, and lower crop yields. Current detection methods are imprecise and inflexible, and hence making real-time corrections is challenging. The solution proposed employs deep learning in the form of the ResNet-50 CNN algorithm for feature extraction and a random forest classifier for increased accuracy at 97.4% for stress classification. Measuring canopy temperature, thermal indexes, and gradients in real time provides an effective way to make irrigation decisions that reduce waste and increase production. The data approach enhances irrigation, reduces resource loss, provides nutrients optimally, and improves resilience to climate change. This study improves sustainable agriculture by integrating Artificial Intelligence with thermal imaging in a low cost and scalable solution that increase productivity and long-term food security.

title: Adaptive AI Algorithms for Cyber Defense: Predictive Behavioral Analysis to Mitigate Zero-Day Attacks
authors: Murali Ponaganti, Ola Khresat, T. Vijetha, Sathiya Priya S, Ragini Y P, Hemalatha G
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The paradigm of cyber threats— with the increasing sophistication and abundance of zero-day threats—presents a critical problem to the rule-based or signature-based systems of traditional network security. These threats work on up-to-now unknown vulnerabilities and therefore they can easily pass out detection mechanism. In reply, this study brings a general overview of adaptive AI algorithms for predictive behavioral analysis intended for zero-day attacks' early detection and counteraction in the changing and diverse world of digital settings. We propose a solution that leverages unsupervised anomaly detection, reinforcement learning and context-aware neural models to continuously adapt in real-time to new system behaviors while preemptively catching malicious activities before damage occurs. At the heart of the framework there resides a self-learning behavioral profiling engine, that learns the normal behavior of users, processes and network communications in a time windows manner. Anomalies with respect to these trained behavioral baselines are detected through hybrid detection methods that combine statistical outlier analysis with online neural decoding. Furthermore, reinforcement learning-based agents are developed for modeling the attacker/defender interplay and embedding desired patterns of defense into the mechanisms of defense by reactive actors that change their behaviors based on feedback from the system, environmental signals, and the like. This two-tiered model improves detection accuracy and considerate action mechanisms help to decrease false positives and to reduce overall service disruption. The effectiveness of the framework is demonstrated via its deployment on simulated testbeds with different network topologies and real-world cybersecurity datasets such as corporate network logs and malware repositories. The results show a remarkable enhancement in early recognition of threats, especially zero days, over the conventional IDS. The adaptive AI models are repeatedly proven to generalize well; they can effectively uncover new attack patterns with little supervision without being explicitly trained on the specter of previously unseen tactics. This work presents a new, scalable AI-empowered defense cyber architecture that closes the loop between proactive threat detection and automatic response. Thru its ability to predict behavior, as opposed to just simply matching signatures, the proposed system provides a resilient and adaptable solution to cyber defense that has the potential to change the game in how organizations protect themselves from advanced, complex cyber threats that are constantly evolving."

title: Hybrid Statistical and Deep Feature-Based Detection of Steganography in DCT and LSB Domains
authors: D. Navya, Swati V, Penumarthi Hima Varshini, Neetu Srivastava
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Steganography facilitates secret communication by hiding information in digital media, and this presents great challenges to digital forensics and information security. This paper introduces a single steganalysis framework that can identify steganographic content hidden in spatial (Least Significant Bit, LSB) and frequency (Discrete Cosine Transform, DCT) domains. We derive discriminative features through statistical means and use dimensionality reduction algorithms like PCA, SVD, and Deep Matrix Factorization to improve representation. These features are then classified through both traditional and deep learning-based models. For further detection accuracy improvement, ensemble learning techniques including a strong stacking classifier are used. Statistical hypothesis testing is used to confirm cover and stego feature separability. Experimental findings show that though individual classifiers result in moderate performance, ensemble-based methods greatly enhance performance, especially for DCT-based steganography. Further, a pipeline for real-time inference is incorporated for deployment in practice. The contribution of the work is in showcasing the value of integrating machine learning with statistical domain expertise towards high-accuracy and interpretable steganalysis.

title: ACoD: Detecting Abusive Comments from Transliterated Bengali Text Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Mehedi Hasan, Shawly Ahsan, Mohammed Moshiul Hoque
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Abusive comment detection refers to the process of identifying and flagging comments or messages in online communication platforms that contain abusive, offensive, or harmful language. Due to inadequate resources and related language processing tools, detecting abusive comments is challenging in low-resource languages like Bengali. The prevalence of transliterated Bengali comments on social media adds to the challenge, as monolingual techniques are unable to capture them. Regrettably, the absence of a publicly accessible, transliterated Bengali corpus for investigating abusive comments highlights a significant gap in available resources. Therefore, this work introduces a new BACoD dataset comprising 6162 transliterated Bengali texts labeled into two categories: abusive and non-abusive. This work explores several machine learning (LR, RF, SVM, MNB) and deep learning (CNN, LSTM, BiLSTM) models for the downstream task and evaluates the developed dataset. Experimental analysis revealed that the CNN+BiLSTM with Keras embedding attained the best performance (W F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> score of 0.92).

title: ViT-CBAM: An attention-based deep learning approach for Thoracic disease classification
authors: Sauda Adiv Hanum, Israt Binteh Habib, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Thoracic diseases such as pneumonia, fibrosis, and cardiomegaly present significant diagnostic challenges, particularly in resource-constrained settings. We have proposed a framework that combines the global feature extraction capabilities of Vision Transformers (ViT) with the Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) to improve the refinement of local feature. The model is evaluated on a multi-label chest X-ray dataset comprising 15 classes, including one normal and 14 pathological conditions. Our experimental results performs superior performance compared to other existing methods by achieving a macro-averaged AUC of 0.8692, a Hamming loss of 0.0777, and a classification accuracy of 92.23%. The hybrid ViT-CBAM architecture shows marked improvement in detecting challenging disease categories, highlighting its potential for reliable and interpretable thoracic disease diagnosis in clinical practice.

title: Intrusion Detection in Smart Grids Using Deep Belief Network and K-Means Clustering Model for Electric Vehicle Charging Systems
authors: P. Chandra Sekhar, Murali Karri, S. Sree Vidhya, Anil Lokesh Gadi, Sree Lakshmi Lingineni, Anvesh Perada
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The safety and reliability of today's electricity grids depend on intrusion detection in smart grids. Intelligent detection systems that can accurately identify possible risks are urgently needed due to the increasing volume and complexity of smart grid data. To guarantee high-quality data input for analysis, this study offers a new method that starts with data pretreatment, which includes cleaning and encoding. The Binary Cuckoo Search algorithm is used for feature selection in order to identify the most relevant attributes. As a follow-up, it provides an MDPCA-and DBN based fuzzy aggregation approach. To reduce dataset size and fix class imbalance, the MDPCA method divides the training dataset into subsets with comparable attribute distributions. To achieve more precise training, a distinct sub-DBN classifier is trained for each subset. Outperforming multiple other models examined in this research, the proposed model known as Modified Density PCA-DBN achieves an outstanding accuracy of 95.84%. The efficacy of integrating MDPCA and DBN inside a fuzzy aggregation architecture for strong intrusion detection is proven by these outcomes. By facilitating more precise and extensible threat detection procedures, this paradigm provides a viable approach to improving cybersecurity in smart grid systems.

title: Explanations of Data Systems Concepts in CS Education: An Updated View on Notional Machines
authors: Daphne Miedema, Martin Goodfellow, Chandrika Satyavolu, Georgiana Haldeman, Leonard Busuttil, Laura Farinetti, Giovanna Guerrini, Yuhan Pan, Sujeeth Goud Ramagoni, Raja Sooriamurthi, Xiaoying Tu
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 Working Group Reports on Innovation and Technology in Computer Science Education
abstract: None

title: Kinematic Coordination and Muscle Synergy Patterns of Grasping during Lower-limb Locomotion_supp3-3663964.mp4
authors: Yan Huang

title: Types I & II Partition-based Fuzzy Classifiers for Enhanced Interpretability, Smaller Output Variance and Denoising Capability
authors: Maosen Long, Fu-Lai Chung, Shitong Wang
abstract: While the existing type I or II first-order Takagi-Sugeno-Kang (TSK) fuzzy classifiers generally take an additive type I or II first-order rule space followed by a non-additive defuzzification without or with type-reduction, this study enriches their family by opening a reverse thinking about a non-additive type I or II first-order rule space, followed by a simple additive defuzzification with the avoidance of explicit type-reduction calculation. Towards this end, the novel Types I & II Partition-based first-order TSK Fuzzy Classifiers (TIP-FC and TIIP-FC) and their training methods are developed for first-order linearity of consequent of each rule on all the fuzzy partitions themselves or their simple variants rather than each original feature, thereby having their distinct values in the sense of enhanced interpretability, smaller output variance, and/or denoising capability. Extensive experimental results on the adopted benchmark datasets indicate that both TIP-FC and TIIP-FC keep at least comparable to and even superior over the comparative methods in the sense of interpretability, classification performance, and/or denoising capability.

title: Cross-Modal Attention-Modulated Feature Enhancement Network for Visible-Infrared Ship Detection
authors: Yaxin Lei, Wuxia Zhang, Xiaochen Niu, Hailong Ning, Xiaoqiang Lu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Ship detection is crucial for tasks such as harbor dynamic surveillance and maritime traffic management, aiming to identify and locate ships. Multimodal object detection based on visible-infrared data is well suited for ship detection because it enables all-weather object detection. However, it lacks paired visible-infrared ship datasets, and there still exist problems such as insufficient consistency and complementarity of cross-modal semantic information, as well as a large amount of redundant information when fusing different modalities. To address these problems, two pseudo-infrared datasets are established, and a Cross-modal Attention-modulated Feature Enhancement Network (CAMFEN) approach for ship detection is proposed. CAMFEN mainly utilizes the Cross-modal Collaborative-Differential Enhancement Feature Fusion module (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$ \rm{C}^{2}\rm{DEFF}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) to effectively fuse the information of different modalities, which consists of Collaborative Attention Modulation Block (CAMB), Differential Attention Modulation Block (DAMB), and Global Feature Guided Fusion Block (GFGFB). The CAMB achieves cross-modal semantic alignment through channel attention modulation, eliminates geometric offset with spatial adaptive calibration, strengthens common semantics representation from the perspective of collaborative integrity, and equalizes modal contribution. The DAMB selects discriminant difference features through channel attention screening, uses spatial attention to focus on target regions, and mines complementary or contradictory information from the perspective of difference specificity, enhancing modal specificity and suppressing redundant noise. Finally, the enhanced single modality features obtained by CAMB and DAMB are fed into GFGFB, which guides the optimized fusion of enhanced single modality features from a global perspective. The proposed method has been validated on HRSC2016 and DOTAv1.0 datasets, and the experimental results show that CAMFEN outperforms existing ship detection methods.

title: Real-time fire detection from videosurveillance cameras: where are we now?
authors: Vincenzo Carletti, Antonio Greco, Alessia Saggese, Bruno Vento
abstract: Automatic fire detection in video surveillance is a critical capability for safeguarding industrial sites, public infrastructures, and natural environments. Although recent advances in deep learning have substantially improved early fire detection, the performance of existing methods remains highly dependent on scene conditions such as viewing range, background activity, and the presence of fire-like distractors. This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of real-time fire detection techniques on the ONFIRE and FIRE-TASTIC benchmarks, currently the most extensive datasets available for video-based fire monitoring. Through a systematic comparison of methods that differ in detector quality, temporal modeling, and semantic reasoning capabilities, we highlight the strengths and limitations of current design paradigms and analyze their behaviour across scenarios that differ in terms of range and background activity. Our results reveal clear performance trends, emphasizing the crucial role of careful detector training, scenario-aware adaptation, temporal analysis, and lightweight vision–language integration. Building on these insights, we outline promising research directions, including the development of more representative datasets, improved semantic confirmation modules, and robust continual learning strategies capable of maintaining reliability in dynamic real-world deployments.

title: Journal of Lightwave Technology Information for Authors
authors: N/A

title: Towards Validating an Artificial Intelligence Concept Inventory for Non-Experts (AICI-NE): Common Misconceptions and Item Development
authors: Linda Mannila, Julie Henry, Tobias Bahr, Christos Chytas, Harold Connamacher, Barbara C. N. Müller, Simone Opel, Andreas Scholl
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 Working Group Reports on Innovation and Technology in Computer Science Education
abstract: None

title: Computational Investigation of Abstraction in Claude Monet's Water Lilies through Brushstroke Analysis
authors: Jia Li, Chaewan Chun, Kathryn Brown, James Z. Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Claude Monet's late paintings of Water Lilies exhibit stylistic transformations that are often characterized by art historians as increasingly abstract and gesturally expressive. However, it remains challenging to define and systematically identify this stylistic shift. Here, we introduce a machine learning framework for analyzing Monet's evolving brushwork using streamline curves: computational representations that capture the dynamic movement patterns inherent in brushstrokes. From 554 image patches sampled from 47 paintings spanning early (pre-1913) and later (post-1913) periods of Monet's output, we extract streamlines and compute geometric features for each, including smoothness of curvature and directional variability. Each image is represented as a set of streamline feature vectors, a data type referred to as distributional. A new deep neural network architecture named Composition to Attribute (C2A) is designed for classifying distributional data. We hypothesize that Monet's so-called 'abstract' style does not uniformly characterize all late period Water Lilies, and that non-abstract flowers, regardless of period, share similar brushwork qualities. Under these assumptions, building on C2A, weproposeanovel learning paradigm namedDiscover Embedded Group with Asymmetry (DEGA) which enforces a shared distribution of DNN-extracted features for non-abstract flower patches across both pe riods while distinguishing the abstract ones. DEGA reveals a meaningful two-dimensional feature space, where one dimension differentiates abstract from mimetic Water Lilies, while the other separates abstract f lowers from close-up flowers of the early period. Our findings suggest that the so-called 'abstract' qualities of Monet's late style retain certain visual affinities with his earlier approach to depicting close-up floral motifs. When this brushwork is used in more expansive scenes, the depiction of flowers shifts away from realistic renderings of individual petals toward a looser, more allusive expression, conveying a sense of floral presence rather than botanical detail. This study highlights the value of computational analysis for a more accurate understanding of an artist's stylistic development.

title: Engineering Sensor Spoofing Protection into the Android Operating System
authors: Roy Hershkovitz, Yossi Oren
abstract: Sensor spoofing attacks are a serious threat to mobile phones, as they can manipulate sensor readings to subvert the behavior of applications that rely on these readings. Previous work has shown how machine learning defenses provide effective protection against sensor spoofing attacks without hardware modification. Unfortunately, these defenses require changes to the applications themselves. In this paper, we present SDIOS (Sensor Defense in the Operating System), an approach that engineers sensor spoofing protection into the operating system level, without requiring any modifications to the applications. At its core, SDIOS incorporates an autoencoder based on a Gramian Angular Field (GAF) representation of the sensor readings. We describe the design and implementation of SDIOS, and evaluate its performance and compatibility on a variety of devices. Our results show that SDIOS is able to detect and prevent sensor spoofing attacks in real time, while retaining compatibility with existing applications, but that its performance impact is significant, especially on resource-constrained devices where the machine learning pipeline is run on the central processing unit (CPU).

title: Reconfigurable Topological Power Router Based on Interferometric Edge States
authors: Xinyu Zhang, Sijie Li, Changyuan Yu, Wenlong Gao, Menglin L. N. Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Topological photonic crystals, particularly valley photonic crystals, offer a robust platform for controlling light propagation. However, most existing topological devices possess fixed transmission characteristics and lack real-time reconfigurability. Here, we propose a reconfigurable topological power router that overcomes this limitation. The device is a four-port structure implemented in a substrate-integrated platform, where two input ports excite topological edge states. These states interfere within a compact region, enabling dynamic control over the power distribution between the two output ports. The power splitting ratio is continuously tunable, determined solely by the relative phase difference between the input signals or the operating frequency. We present both theoretical analysis and experimental results that validate this interferometric control mechanism. This work bridges the robustness of topological transport with the flexibility of interferometry, establishing a new pathway for dynamic power control in topological circuits.

title: A Novel CNN-BiLSTM Model with MultiHead Attention for Efficient Intrusion Detection
authors: Omar Hasnain Mahmud, Abdullah Al Noman, Mohammad Nadib Hasan, Md Jiabul Hoque, Abdullah Al Afiq
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This study explores enhancing Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) through Deep Learning (DL) techniques, particularly CNN-BiLSTM architectures. Traditional IDS techniques struggle to detect evolving threats due to the rapid increase in cyber threats, including advanced attacks targeting network infrastructures. This work examines hybrid models, specifically Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for feature extraction and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks for analyzing temporal relationships in network traffic. Two benchmark datasets, NSL-KDD and UNSW-NB15, train the models, achieving impressive accuracy rates of over 98.96% and 99.99% in binary classification tasks. The paper addresses high false alerts, anomaly detection, and the model's adaptability to various network environments. The results show that the proposed DL-based IDS models provide a solid foundation for accurate, scalable, and reliable intrusion detection, effectively identifying both normal and malicious traffic.

title: Advancements in AIML and IoT Technologies for Real-Time Heart Rate Monitoring and Patient Care
authors: Sandeep Kumar Saini, Rippandeep Kaur, Saweta Verma, Priya Sharma, Jyoti Sharma, Ranjeeta Saini
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing healthcare by improving disease diagnosis, treatment strategies, and patient management through advanced technologies like machine learning, natural language processing, and computer vision. AI-powered tools enhance diagnostic precision, forecast patient outcomes, and enable personalized treatment plans, easing the workload on medical professionals and optimizing hospital resources just like digital microscope, detection using Machine learning, disease detection using deep learning etc.However, AI adoption in medicine poses challenges, including ethical concerns, data privacy risks, and regulatory issues. Addressing biases in AI algorithms and ensuring equitable healthcare access are critical considerations.IOT Technologies play very important role in Patient Monitoring. In proposed work Heart rate is monitor and with the help of IOT technologies heart rate is observed on cloud and automatic heart rate is stored and graphical representation are generated. ESP32 with ECG AD8232 sensor and ubidotes. proposed work is used to take the real time ECG monitoring system and store the data on cloud.IOT base monitoring devices are very important in pandemic like covid 19. Due to that doctors can easily monitor and provide proper diagnose to patient with there own safety. Key applications such as AI-assisted medical imaging, robotic surgery, virtual health assistants, and predictive analytics are enhancing healthcare efficiency while reducing errors. Proposed work explores AI’s transformation role in healthcare, analyzing its applications, benefits, and challenges with proposed ECG AD8232 sensor based hart rate monitoring system using IoT. while discussing emerging trends and future innovations that could further enhance AIdriven medical solutions.

title: A Novel Genetic Algorithm and TransSVM-Based Approach for Stress Detection in Social Network Messages
authors: M. Rani, P. Mano Paul, S. S. Bhaviya, P. Muthu Pandian, Sridhar D, Priyadharshini T
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Stress is a major problem in modern life because it can lead to major health problems if not addressed early on. Analysing these signs necessitates efficient computing methods, as social media posts frequently contain subtle indications of mental distress, even when stress is not directly stated. The branch of machine learning known as NLP is useful for identifying instances of stress in communications shared on social networks. This research used LDA to extract features from social media message text after preprocessing for data purification. A new hybrid categorisation model was created by combining a SVM with a GSA. This method optimizes feature subset selection and SVM parameters simultaneously for binary classification tasks by using a discrete GSA and a continuous-valued GSA, respectively. The experimental results reveal that the suggested GSA-SVM system was able to recognize stress-related cues with an accuracy of 99.22%. Thanks to this model's promising accuracy, stress can be detected in social networks at an early stage, allowing for more timely treatments and a possible reduction in stress-related health problems.

title: Deep Learning-Based Classification for Border Defence Mechanism
authors: Subbulakshmi A, Sandhiya M, Silambarasi Palanivel, Denceli D, Shivani B S, Chezhiyan M
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Ensuring border security and maintaining national security need the creation of strong defence systems. Conventional border defence tactics frequently depend on manual surveillance and human intervention, which can be resource-intensive and prone to human mistake. Using deep learning techniques, this research proposes a novel approach to classify border defence measures. The primary objective of this project is to develop and implement an intelligent system that can automatically identify and recognize various border defence measures, such as walls, fences, trenches, and sensor-based systems, using photos and sensor data. Two different kinds of deep learning algorithms—convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and recurrent neural networks (RNNs)—will be employed to achieve this. The suggested procedure will involve multiple steps. Initially, a large dataset including of images and sensor data from several border defense systems would be collected and organized. The deep learning models will be trained on this dataset. While RNNs will be used to extract temporal patterns from sensor data, CNNs will be used to extract spatial characteristics from images. In order to achieve optimal performance, the model parameters will be fine-tuned during the training process using optimization approaches.

title: AI-Powered Medical Imaging: Innovations and Challenges
authors: Sonali V. Patil, Soumyakant Dash, Komuravelly Sudheer Kumar, Babitha Lincy R, Kiran Kumar Reddy Penubaka, Thella Preethi Priyanka
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This research seeks to improve medical imaging diagnostic accuracy by taking advantage of state-of-the-art AI methods, including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers (ViTs), coupled with explainable AI (XAI) and federated learning for the sake of preserving privacy. The study is centered on the shortfalls of conventional diagnostics, where they are at a loss with vast and intricate data. The approach utilizes deep learning-based models to identify tumors, find lung nodules, and demarcate lesions with an emphasis on interpretability and privacy in data. Highlighted findings identify that ViTs perform better for challenging tasks like tumor classification (94.1% accuracy), whereas CNNs are superior when the task is simple, for example, the detection of nodules. The study shows that AI models can become powerful decision aids that could help clinicians gain higher accuracy as well as more confidence in making diagnoses. These findings point to the promise of AI in revolutionizing clinical practice through the improvement of diagnostic workflows. Further studies should investigate wider dataset integration, model scalability, and resolving ethical issues in the use of AI in healthcare.

title: Health Monitoring of Distribution Transformer Using DGA and Furan Analysis: A Diagnostic Approach for Predicting Insulation Aging
authors: Abdur Razzak Khan, Vinukumar Luckose, Noor Farhana Halil
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Analysis of furan compounds and dissolved gases in transformer oil provides critical insight into the condition and aging of cellulose insulation in distribution transformers. Regular monitoring of these parameters is a key component of a comprehensive transformer maintenance program. This research identifies the high concentration of dissolved gases and furan compounds of oil with the reported values indicating minimal degradation and fault. The total furan content in oil was recorded at 0.04 mg/L, with 2-furfural being the dominant compound at 0.01 mg/L, 2-furfuryl alcohol 0.01 mg/L and 5-methyl-2-furfural 0.02 mg/L. Other furans, including 2-acetylfuran and 5-hydroxymethyl-2-furfural were either undetectable or present in negligible amounts. The water content was 6, dielectric strength and acid number were 75 and 0.02 mgKOH/g. Dissolved gas concentration were monitored under IEEE standard ranges. This finding suggests that the transformer insulation remain in good condition with no significant thermal stress or accelerating aging observed. However, in dissolved gas analysis the CO and CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentration was high (862 ppm and 6486 ppm) and this elevated levels suggests potential overheating within the transformer. Consequently, this research recommends continuous monitoring to ensure long-term reliability and to detect early signs of deterioration, thereby reducing distribution transformer failures.

title: NeuroExplain: An Explainable Deep Learning Framework for Multiclass Emotion Recognition from Gameplay EEG
authors: Diptadip Maiti, Madhuchhanda Basak, Sudipta Hazra
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper presents a novel framework for emotion detection from electroencephalogram (EEG) signals, integrating both deep learning and traditional machine learning approaches to enhance classification accuracy and model interpretability. The proposed pipeline includes preprocessing, feature extraction, class balancing using SMOTE, and the evaluation of multiple classifiers, including XGBoost, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and a deep Artificial Neural Network (ANN). Among these, XGBoost achieved the highest accuracy of 94.98%, followed by Random Forest (93.49%) and ANN (93.00%). The performance of these models was evaluated using metrics such as accuracy, F1-score, and confusion matrix. Additionally, the interpretability of the top-performing models was further enhanced using explainable AI techniques, such as SHAP and Integrated Gradients (IG), which provided valuable insights into the key EEG features contributing to emotion classification. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of ensemble-based methods and deep learning for EEG-based emotion detection, while highlighting the importance of model transparency in real-world applications. The framework holds significant potential for improving emotion detection systems and offers avenues for future research, including the exploration of advanced feature extraction methods, real-time classification, and further optimization of explainability techniques.

title: Real-Time Brain Tumor Localization and Classification Using YOLOv11 and MONAI MedNeXt
authors: Fazlay Rabby, Md. Hridoy Hossain, Md. Rifat Aknda, Bahadur Zamn Shezan, Sanjida Salwa Mou
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Early and precise detection of brain tumors remains a major challenge in clinical radiology, particularly in real time applications. This study introduces a novel hybrid deep learning framework that combines YOLOv11 for rapid tumor localization with MONAI’s MedNeXt for advanced 3D classification and grading. Leveraging a clinically annotated, locally sourced dataset of 3,500 MRI scans along with two benchmark datasets, the system addresses key challenges of accuracy, interpretability, and deployment readiness. YOLOv11 integrates a hybrid CSP-Darknet–Swin Transformer backbone with anchor-free detection and Soft-NMS refinement for precise localization across diverse tumor morphologies. MedNeXt, enhanced with SimCLR-based contrastive pretraining, attention mechanisms, and multi-head classification, accurately distinguishes tumor type and severity. The pipeline incorporates Grad-CAM++ for explainability and Monte Carlo Dropout for uncertainty estimation, ensuring clinical transparency and decision support. Optimized using ONNX and TensorRT, the system supports PACS/DICOM and EMR integration for real-time inference in hospital environments. The model outperforms advanced baselines including InceptionNetv4 and EfficientNetB0 with results of 99.60% accuracy, 99.76% precision, 99.52% recall, and 99.64% F1-score. Designed with a focus on deployment in resource-constrained settings, this framework advances AI-assisted radiology by offering a robust, interpretable, and scalable solution for brain tumor diagnostics.

title: Enhanced Java Plum Leaf Disease Classification Using ShuffleNetV2 and GSA-Tuned AdaBoost
authors: Md. Fahim Kabir Chowdhury, Md. Sayem Kabir, Sadman Samir Rafith, Kazi Tanvir, Farzana Nazera, Dipta Gomes
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Java plum is an economically and nutritionally significant fruit crop, but its cultivation is severely hampered by various leaf diseases that lead to substantial yield reductions. Traditional manual inspection for disease detection is labor-intensive, time-consuming, and often unreliable for early diagnosis. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel hybrid deep learning framework, SFN-ADB-GSA, for the automated and accurate classification of Java plum leaf diseases. The proposed model integrates a lightweight ShuffleNetV2 (SFN) backbone for efficient feature extraction, an AdaBoost (ADB) classifier for robust decision-making, and the Gravitational Search Algorithm (GSA) for systematic hyperparameter optimization of the classifier. Evaluated on a balanced, six-class dataset of 6,000 augmented leaf images, the SFN-ADB-GSA model demonstrates state-of-the-art performance, achieving a test accuracy of 97.89%, a Matthews Correlation Coefficient (MCC) of 97.5%, and an F1-score of 97.9%. Comprehensive comparative analysis and ablation studies suggest that our hybrid approach performs favorably compared to other deep learning backbones, traditional classifiers, and alternative optimization methods. The results establish a new performance benchmark for this task, offering a highly accurate, efficient, and scalable solution to aid farmers in timely disease management, thereby enhancing agricultural productivity and sustainability.

title: Zero-Knowledge AI: Privacy-First ML Inference in Distributed Ecosystems
authors: Mahendran Chinnaiah, Abhaar Gupta, Saurabh Srivastava, Ashok Ghimire
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: At a time when data privacy laws and cyber-attacks are on the rise, Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) hold the potential of a transformational paradigm of safe (privacy-preserving) machine learning (ML) inferences. In this paper, we present a new architecture that facilitates Zero-Knowledge AI, in which sensitive data inputs and internal model parameters remain unknown during the model inference procedure across distributed ecosystems. The proposed framework can help preserve privacy standards like GDPR and HIPAA, inference accuracies, and scalability of these inferences by utilising mechanisms to observe cryptographic zero-knowledge protocols, as well as federated learning protocols. We describe the construction of ZK-friendly models to apply to neural inference pipelines, efficient zk-SNARK-based model validation, decentralized trusting schemes, and privacy-respecting model auditing. Testing over a variety of healthcare and financial datasets indicates that our Zero-Knowledge AI solution results in high privacy guarantees with limited throughput losses. The work provides a strong basis on how to implement trusted and privacy-first AI systems in the real life and distributed operating environment.

title: Guest Editorial: Special Issue on Recent Advances in Integrated Sensing and Communications—Part I
authors: Fan Liu, Christos Masouros, Jie Xu, Tony Xiao Han, Sang G. Kim, Yonina C. Eldar, Stefano Buzzi, Anna Guerra, Shinya Sugiura, Sundeep Prabhakar Chepuri, Xianghao Yu
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications

title: Optimized Deep Learning Architecture for Maize Disease Detection Using Efficient Channel Attention and Transfer Learning
authors: Lucy Dash, Suprava Ranjan Laha, Binod Kumar Pattanayak, Debasish Swapnesh Kumar Nayak, Pranashi Chakraborty, Salankara Sarkar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Maize is a major staple food crop worldwide. However, its yield is threatened by foliar diseases, such as northern corn leaf blight, common rust, and gray leaf spot, due to which timely disease detection is essential to ensure food safety and minimize pesticide usage. We propose a hybrid deep-learning model by combining a CNN trunk (7 × 7 convolution, batch normalization, ReLU) with EfficientNet inverted-bottleneck blocks, along with Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) to improve cross-channel feature calibration in a lightweight model with a competitive approach for 4.5M parameters. PlantVillage maize recall images were resized to 224×224 (Maize-leaf-PV) and augmentation (rotations, flips, zooms, brightness, and channel shifts) to mimic field diversity. The model was trained using the Adam optimizer with a cosine-annealing schedule for 350 epochs on TPU pods, batch size 64, dropout rate 0.5, early stopping, and model checkpointing to avoid overfitting. The model achieved an accuracy of 98.85 with F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>-scores of 93.37 (Blight), 99.45 (Common Rust), 89.57 (Gray Leaf Spot), and 99.90 (Healthy). These are significantly better than state-of-the-art baselines, including ResNet-50 (95.58%), Inception-v3 (95.99%), and EfficientNetB2 (94.00%), while decreasing the computational cost and model memory footprint. The lightweight structure is conducive to real-time application in a precision-agriculture system and allows targeted response to reduce crop yield losses and environmental harm. Towards implementation: robustness validation under diverse field conditions with UAV and smartphone imaging, transfer learning for additional crops, ensemble and severity-estimation modules, optimization for mobile-device implementation to democratize access to automated disease monitoring, collaborative farmer feedback loops, and cloud-based deployment pipelines for continuous model improvements.

title: CDAF: A Contrastive Distribution Alignment Framework for Generative Augmentation in Imbalanced IIoT Intrusion Detection
authors: Guangzhao Chai, Youxi Wu, Jing Liu
abstract: In industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) intrusion detection, severe class imbalance and inter-class feature overlap often degrade the performance of traditional classifiers in recognizing minority classes. This paper proposes a Contrastive Distribution Alignment Framework (CDAF), which aims to achieve high-quality generative augmentation through feature-space alignment constraints and a curriculum-based training strategy. CDAF learns a structured latent space conditioned on the target class, enabling the generator to produce more representative and class-consistent synthetic samples. The framework further constructs “generated–target” and “generated–non-target” sample pairings, introducing a contrastive feature alignment regularization term that minimizes the distributional distance between generated samples and target-class features, while simultaneously enlarging their margin from non-target classes. Furthermore, we design a stage-wise weight transfer mechanism termed ECLG (Enhanced-Contrast Late-Generative), which emphasizes inter-class discrimination during the early training phase and focuses on generation quality in the later phase, thereby enabling dynamic optimization of the generative distribution. Empirically, we show that CDAF’s contrastive enhancement loss effectively reduces feature overlap under inter-class distance constraints, and the overall framework significantly improves minority-class detection on three benchmark IIoT datasets (X-IIoTID, WUSTL-IIoT-2021, and ToN IoT).

title: PES-UNet: A Physics-Inspired Enhanced Hybrid Network for Cloud Classification from FY-4A Satellite Data
authors: Bo Wang, Huitang Li, Wei Cheng, Qinghong Sheng, Yang Du, Jun Li, Xiao Ling
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Cloud types are generated by various atmospheric thermodynamic and dynamic processes, reflecting the current atmospheric state and predicting future weather changes. Ac curate cloud classification is crucial for climate prediction. With the increasing demand for accurate cloud classification in remote sensing, CNNs have emerged in recent years as an essential computational tool. However, cloud shapes can change dramatically with environmental conditions, making it challenging to capture and recognize complex cloud boundaries. Additionally, CNNs often overlook the contextual features of clouds during their evolution, which results in the omission of fine-scale structures across various cloud types and compromises classification accuracy. To overcome these limitations, we introduce a physically constrained, interactive cloud classification framework—PES-UNet—that per forms pixel-wise semantic segmentation on satellite cloud imagery while simultaneously refining relevant physical parameters. First, the UNet network is employed to estimate complex atmospheric transmittance images by leveraging dark channel prior, which facilitates the preliminary delineation of cloudy versus cloud free regions. Subsequently, we design a feature fusion module that enables bidirectional interaction between CNN and Swin Transformer representations, ensuring the integration of detailed local features with comprehensive global semantics, facilitating fine-grained cloud type classification based on cloud region judgment. To evaluate the proposed architecture, we carry out cloud classification experiments using preprocessed FY4A L1 data covering most of China. The results demonstrate that, compared to other CNN, Transformer, or hybrid networks, PES UNet significantly enhances the accuracy of cloud classification in FY4A remote sensing imagery, achieving the highest match with Himawari-8 products. The network's ability to classify each pixel with semantic precision highlights its strength in considering both the fine-grained structure and the broader context of clouds during their evolution.

title: LLMs as Assistants in Software Architecture Design
authors: Humberto Cervantes, Yuanfang Cai, Rick Kazman
abstract: To achieve desired software quality attributes, architects must evaluate and choose from a myriad of design concepts, patterns, and technologies. This paper explores how Large Language Models (LLMs) can assist in this decision-making process. We compare design decisions made by LLMs and human architects across several design problems. Our results show that while LLMs suggest relevant options, human architects often disagree with or distrust those suggestions because the models frequently lack context and are inconsistent. Humans still need to make the final decisions in design, but LLMs are valuable as assistants: for generating design options, serving as a sounding board, acting as a creative catalyst and an education aid. We conclude with a discussion of the implications for architectural practice, education, and future research directions.

title: Maximizing Revenue for Reliability-Aware Edge Application Deployment
authors: Lu Zhao, Bo Li, Jian Zhou, Cheng Chen, Fu Xiao, Yun Yang
abstract: Multiaccess edge computing (MEC) enables low-latency service delivery by deploying application instances on edge servers. However, edge servers are prone to failures, making it challenging to meet diverse user reliability requirements. A common approach is to deploy redundant instances across multiple edge servers, which improves reliability but increases costs and limits the number of users that can be served within budget. Therefore, efficient deployment strategies are needed to balance cost-effectiveness and reliability guarantees, thereby maximizing the app vendor’s revenue. In this article, we investigate the problem of <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">R</u>evenue maximization for <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">R</u>eliability-aware <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">E</u>dge <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>pplication <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</u>eployment (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{R}^{2}\text{EAD}$</tex-math></inline-formula>). Our objective is to maximize the app vendor’s revenue by deploying its applications on heterogeneous edge servers, subject to budget and resource constraints and users’ diverse reliability requirements. We prove that the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{R}^{2}\text{EAD}$</tex-math></inline-formula> problem is <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {\text{NP}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-hard and propose an efficient approximation algorithm named <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{R}^{2}\text{EAD}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-A. By reducing the problem to a nonmonotone submodular maximization problem with curvature <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha$</tex-math></inline-formula> under multiple knapsack constraints, we prove that <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{R}^{2}\text{EAD}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-A achieves a constant approximation ratio of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\frac{1}{\alpha }(1 - e^{-\alpha })$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{R}^{2}\text{EAD}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-A outperforms the representative approaches across all tested cases.

title: Fast Calculation of Far-Field Radiation Pattern for Large-Scale Conformal Array Based on Active Element Pattern Reconstruction
authors: Youji Cong, Rui Wang, Junxiao Wang, Wenjun Chen, Teng Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a fast computation method for the far-field radiation pattern of large-scale conformal arrays based on active element pattern (AEP) reconstruction. The inherent inconsistency of individual AEPs in conformal arrays prevents direct application of the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT), resulting in time-consuming computations. To accelerate the process, Zernike polynomials are used to reconstruct each AEP into a set of basis radiation patterns with corresponding coefficients. Thus, the conformal array pattern can be expressed as a sum of several basis pattern sets multiplied by the array factor. Additionally, the three-dimensional nonuniform fast Fourier transform is employed to efficiently compute the array factor. Validation is performed on a large-scale cylindrical array and an irregularly curved conformal array, with results compared against those from direct calculation. Computational results demonstrate the advantages and effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: SCMamba: A Spatial–Channel Multi-scale State Space Network for High Resolution Image Scene Classification
authors: Xing Qing, Meng Zhang, Xiangyuan Duan
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: High-resolution remote sensing images play a crucial role in urban planning and environmental monitoring; however, their multi-scale and diverse backgrounds pose significant challenges for classification tasks. To address this issue, we propose an enhanced framework that integrates multi-scale features and spatial–channel attention on top of a state space model (SSM) backbone. Specifically, we draw upon the concept of a bidirectional feature pyramid (BiFPN) for multi-scale feature fusion, incorporating skip connections and residual improvements to strengthen information flow across different feature levels. Meanwhile, leveraging large-kernel convolutions and channel attention, we design a spatial–channel fusion module tailored to the demands of remote sensing images, thereby enabling deeply separable reweighting for irregular ground objects and multi-channel spectral responses. Experimental results show that on the GID dataset, our method achieves a Precision of 94.61%, surpassing CNN-based networks (VGG16, ResNet-18), Transformer-based architectures (ViT, Swin-T), and other Mamba-based models (Vim-tiny, VMamba-tiny, Samba) by approximately 13%, 6%, and 8%, respectively. Meanwhile, on the EuroSat and AID datasets, our method achieves Precision values of 98.19% and 94.65%, respectively, thereby providing a feasible solution for the efficient intelligent analysis of urban remote sensing imagery. The code will be available at https://github.com/qxin-g/SCMamba.

title: Small-Signal Modeling and Analysis of a Grid-Forming PEM Hydrogen Electrolyzer
authors: Basil Hamad, Yasser Abdel-Rady I. Mohamed, Ahmed Al-Durra, Ehab El-Saadany
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: Green hydrogen production using proton exchange membrane electrolyzers (PEMELs) is increasingly integrated with renewable energy resources to enhance sustainability, stor-age, and grid resilience. Analyzing the stability and dynamics of such systems is complex and requires comprehensive modeling. This paper develops a detailed multiphysics small-signal model of a grid-forming PEMEL employing a unidirectional dc/dc buck converter and a bidirectional dc/ac inverter for grid integration. The model captures electrical, electrochemical, thermal, and fluid dynamics, and is validated against a nonlinear time-domain multiphysics model. Key factors affecting eigenvalues and mode shifts include stack temperature, current consumption, gas pressures, short circuit ratio (SCR), PI controller gains, and converter LC sizing. It has been demonstrated that low SCR operation can hinder system stability due to the inherently slow dynamics of PEMELs, leading to intermittent hydrogen produc-tion. Furthermore, temperature variations are shown to influence system stability conditions. The proposed model not only supports PEMEL integration in grid dynamic studies, but provides insights toward temperature and mass-flow management, highlighting the importance of detailed modeling.

title: MCD-YOLO: An Improved YOLOv11 Framework for Manhole Cover Detection in UAV Imagery
authors: Fengchang Li, Shaomei Li, Qing Xu, Zhenyan Yu, Ning You, Liunan Ren
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) remote sensing provides an effective solution for monitoring urban infrastructure. Accurate detection of manhole covers in high-resolution UAV imagery is essential for the safe operation and maintenance of underground utility networks. However, detecting manhole covers in complex urban environments remains challenging due to their small size, visual similarity to surrounding structures, and frequent occlusion. To address these challenges, we propose a novel detection model termed manhole cover detection YOLO (MCD-YOLO). First, to exploit the regular geometric structure of manhole covers, we design an EdgeExtract module to enhance the C3k2 block in the backbone network. This module fuses image gradient information and high-frequency features to strengthen the geometric edge representation of manhole covers, thereby improving their discriminability against complex backgrounds. Second, we propose an oriented context interaction (OCI) module that employs multi-orientation depthwise separable convolutions to capture both local features and global contextual dependencies, effectively suppressing interference from structurally similar background elements. Finally, we design a distribution-guided localization (DGL) module that dynamically calibrates classification confidence based on the statistical distribution of bounding box regression offsets, significantly reducing high-confidence false positives caused by localization errors under occlusion. Extensive experiments on our self-constructed manhole cover (MHC) dataset and the public VisDrone2019 dataset demonstrate the superior performance of MCD-YOLO.

title: Modeling Pedestrian Priority at Unsignalized Crossings Using Machine Learning: Toward Data-Driven Transport Policy Solutions
authors: Noura Hamdan, Tibor Sipos, Nora Krizsik
abstract: Pedestrian safety at unsignalized crossings remains a pressing urban mobility challenge, where priority negotiation between pedestrians and vehicles directly influences crash risk and traffic efficiency. This study presents a data-driven framework employing machine learning to predict pedestrian priority, integrating behavioral, temporal, vehicular, and contextual features while accounting for spatial heterogeneity via Location ID and addressing class imbalance in each location using SMOTENC oversampling. Three models, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Support Vector Machines, were trained and tested on behavioral, temporal, and contextual features. The XGBoost model achieved the highest predictive performance with a test accuracy of 0.78 (95% CI: 0.73–0.82).
