Feature importance analysis identified pedestrian behavior, waiting position, free time, and vehicle standing time as dominant predictors, with Location ID contributing meaningfully to capture spatial variation. In contrast, demographic factors such as age and sex exhibited negligible predictive value. These results underscore that temporal-behavioral dynamics and situational factors primarily govern priority negotiation, underscoring the importance of behaviorally responsive safety measures. The results provide direct implications for urban transport policy, including adaptive signage, crossing design modifications, and targeted interventions to encourage safe pedestrian behavior. Overall, the study demonstrates the dual methodological and practical contributions of machine learning in transport safety: enhancing predictive modeling of pedestrian–vehicle interactions and translating empirical evidence into actionable strategies for safer, more equitable urban crossings.

title: Efficient Feature Aggregation and Scale-Aware Regression for Monocular 3-D Object Detection
authors: Yifan Wang, Xiaochen Yang, Fanqi Pu, Qingmin Liao, Wenming Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Monocular 3D object detection has received considerable attention for its simplicity and low cost. Existing methods typically follow conventional 2D detection paradigms, first locating object centers and then predicting 3D attributes via neighboring features. However, these approaches mainly focus on local information, which may limit the model’s global context awareness and result in missed detections, as the global context provides semantic and spatial dependencies essential for detecting small objects in cluttered or occluded environments. In addition, due to large variation in object scales across different scenes and depths, inaccurate receptive fields often lead to background noise and degraded feature representation. To address these issues, we introduce MonoASRH, a novel monocular 3D detection framework composed of Efficient Hybrid Feature Aggregation Module (EH-FAM) and Adaptive Scale-Aware 3D Regression Head (ASRH). Specifically, EH-FAM employs multi-head attention with a global receptive field to extract semantic features and leverages lightweight convolutional modules to efficiently aggregate visual features across different scales, enhancing small-scale object detection. The ASRH encodes 2D bounding box dimensions and then fuses scale features with the semantic features aggregated by EH-FAM through a scale-semantic feature fusion module. The scale-semantic feature fusion module guides ASRH in learning dynamic receptive field offsets, incorporating scale information into 3D position prediction for better scale-awareness. Extensive experiments on the KITTI and Waymo datasets demonstrate that MonoASRH achieves state-of-the-art performance. The code and model are released at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/WYFDUT/MonoASRH</uri>

title: Attention-Based Neural-Augmented Kalman Filter for Legged Robot State Estimation
authors: Seokju Lee, Kyung-Soo Kim
abstract: In this letter, we propose an Attention-Based Neural-Augmented Kalman Filter (AttenNKF) for state estimation in legged robots. Foot slip is a major source of estimation error: when slip occurs, kinematic measurements violate the no-slip assumption and inject bias during the update step. Our objective is to estimate this slip-induced error and compensate for it. To this end, we augment an Invariant Extended Kalman Filter (InEKF) with a neural compensator that uses an attention mechanism to infer error conditioned on foot-slip severity and then applies this estimate as a post-update compensation to the InEKF state (i.e., after the filter update). The compensator is trained in a latent space, which aims to reduce sensitivity to raw input scales and encourages structured slip-conditioned compensations, while preserving the InEKF recursion. Experiments demonstrate improved performance compared to existing legged-robot state estimators, particularly under slip-prone conditions.

title: Kinematic Coordination and Muscle Synergy Patterns of Grasping during Lower-limb Locomotion_supp2-3663964.pdf
authors: Yan Huang

title: Self-Supervised Contrastive Learning for Histopathological Image Classification
authors: Sadia Tahsin, Tanjim Mahmud, Mohammad Shahadat Hossain, Abubokor Hanip
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Breast cancer remains one of the leading causes of cancer-related deaths in women worldwide. Histopathological image analysis is crucial for accurate diagnosis, yet the lack of annotated data and high inter-observer variability pose significant challenges. In this work, we explore the SimCLR framework for self-supervised contrastive learning on the BreaKHis 400X histopathology dataset. We pretrain a ResNet18 encoder using unlabeled images and evaluate the learned features on a set of downstream classification tasks. The results demonstrate that SimCLR-based features, when combined with simple classifiers such as logistic regression and MLPs, achieve competitive performance compared to fully supervised models. We also present a supervised ResNet18 model as a baseline. Our experiments highlight the potential of self-supervised learning in medical imaging, particularly for resource-limited scenarios where labeled data is scarce.

title: Early Detection of Hepatitis C Using a Hybrid Predictive Model Integrating SVM, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting
authors: Darshan M R, Devdarshan J, G Akshay, Prema N S
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Hepatitis C virus (HCV) is a serious public health global concern that needs an immediate and precise diagnosis to improve treatment results and reduce additional propagation. Traditional machine learning (ML) algorithms have been used to detect HCV, they are prone, face problems of data imbalance, overfitting, and inappropriateness in hyperparameter tuning. Here we propose a Hybrid Predictive Model (HPM) that contains the combination of Support Vector machine (SVM), Gradient Boosting (GB), and Random Forest (RF) for overcome these shortcomings and enhance diagnosis accuracy. The HPM employs RF in feature selection, and SVM as the baseline classification algorithm, and GB for adaptive hyperparameter tuning.The model is evaluated with a publicly available hepatitis dataset from UCI Machine Learning Repository, contains 615 patient records. The experimental results show that HPM gives a accuracy of 99. 81%, which is higher than the baseline models: SVM (97. 3%), RF (99. 2%) and GB (98. 12%). The results indicate the performance of ensemble learning methods and the use of Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) in handling class imbalance, thereby allowing more early and accurate HCV detection.

title: Trajectory Optimization in UAV-based Non-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Kalpana Naidu, Mahathi Sarvepalli, Prathesh Srinivas, Vishnu Murali Krishnan, Kota Nageswara Rao
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Non-terrestrial networks (NTNs) are becoming a critical component of modern wireless communication, particularly in the advancement of 6G networks. Unlike traditional ground-based base stations, NTNs utilize high-altitude platforms and satellites to extend reliable connectivity to remote, rural, and disaster-affected regions where conventional infrastructure is limited or unavailable. These non-terrestrial base stations play a vital role in bridging connectivity gaps and ensuring seamless communication in challenging environments. This paper presents a novel approach to optimizing the trajectory of an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) serving as an NTN platform. By leveraging reinforcement learning, the proposed method adjusts the UAV’s flight path to maximize network coverage and data rates while minimizing the operational resources required. This optimization enhances the adaptability and efficiency of NTN-based communications, contributing to the development of more resilient, scalable, and high-performance wireless networks capable of supporting diverse applications.

title: Performance Assessment of 1×4 Linear Leaf Shaped Versus Square Shaped Patch Antenna Arrays
authors: P.Jothilakshmi, R. Mohanasundaram
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: A comparison between a traditional square-shaped antenna array and a 1x4 linear leaf-shaped antenna array intended for satellite communication applications is presented in this paper. With CST Microwave Studio, both arrays are designed and simulated at the 7.5 GHz X-band frequency. Important performance parameters are assessed and contrasted, such as gain, radiation pattern, side lobe levels, bandwidth and return loss. Because of its tapered geometry, which also helps to lessen mutual coupling between elements the leaf-shaped array exhibits better impedance matching and a wider bandwidth. In addition, when compared to the square-shaped array the leaf-shaped design achieves better radiation efficiency and higher peak gain. Based on radiation pattern analysis the leaf-shaped arrays narrower beamwidth and suppressed side lobes make it appropriate for targeted satellite communication links. The study demonstrates how the leaf-shaped antenna array improves overall antenna performance and offers guidance for designing small effective antenna arrays for satellite systems.

title: The Efficient Parameter Extraction of Lossy Interconnect Lines by Accurate Characterization of Skin Effect in FEM
authors: Xue-Feng Cheng, Yi Ren, Zhuo-Ran Lu, Hao-Bo Yuan, Qiang Ren, Tian-Peng Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: An exponential basis function (EBF) method is proposed for efficient and accurate electromagnetic modeling of lossy interconnect lines using the finite element method (FEM). In conventional FEM, dense volumetric meshes are required to resolve the skin effect in lossy conductors, which leads to high computational cost and limited accuracy. To address this challenge, the proposed method introduces EBFs featuring an attenuation factor aligned with the conductor thickness, thereby capturing the skin effect-induced current decay with fewer basis functions. Moreover, the volume current distribution is normalized to the surface current, which enables the conductor to be modeled solely with surface meshes and eliminates volumetric meshing inside conductors. In addition, the corresponding FEM matrix formulation is derived for face-mesh elements, thus integrating the method seamlessly into standard FEM workflows. Numerical results show that the proposed method achieves accuracy comparable to full 3D-FEM with coarser meshes, while outperforming impedance boundary methods in both accuracy and numerical stability.

title: Dual Frequency Branch Framework with Reconstructed Sliding Windows Attention for AI-Generated Image Detection
authors: Jiazhen Yan, Ziqiang Li, Fan Wang, Ziwen He, Zhangjie Fu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: The rapid advancement of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and diffusion models has enabled the creation of highly realistic synthetic images, presenting significant societal risks, such as misinformation and deception. As a result, detecting AI-generated images has emerged as a critical challenge. Existing research emphasizes extracting fine-grained features to enhance detector generalization, yet they often lack consideration for the importance and interdependencies of internal elements within local regions and are limited to a single frequency domain, hindering the capture of general forgery traces. To overcome the aforementioned limitations, we first utilize a sliding window to restrict the attention mechanism to a local window, and reconstruct the features within the window to model the relationships between neighboring internal elements within the local region. Then, we design a dual frequency domain branch framework consisting of four frequency domain subbands of DWT and the phase part of FFT to enrich the extraction of local forgery features from different perspectives. Through feature enrichment of dual frequency domain branches and fine-grained feature extraction of reconstruction sliding window attention, our method achieves superior generalization detection capabilities on both GAN and diffusion model-based generative images. Evaluated on diverse datasets comprising images from 65 distinct generative models, our approach achieves a 2.13% improvement in detection accuracy over state-of-the-art methods.

title: Enhancing Underwater Images via Adaptive Semantic-aware Codebook Learning
authors: Bosen Lin, Feng Gao, Yanwei Yu, Junyu Dong, Qian Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Underwater Image Enhancement (UIE) is an ill-posed problem where natural clean references are not available, and the degradation levels vary significantly across semantic regions. Existing UIE methods treat images with a single global model and ignore the inconsistent degradation of different scene components. This oversight leads to significant color distortions and loss of fine details in heterogeneous underwater scenes, especially where degradation varies significantly across different image regions. Therefore, we propose SUCode (Semantic-aware Underwater Codebook Network), which achieves adaptive UIE from semantic-aware discrete codebook representation. Compared with one-shot codebook-based methods, SUCode exploits semantic-aware, pixel-level codebook representation tailored to heterogeneous underwater degradation. A three-stage training paradigm is employed to represent raw underwater image features to avoid pseudo ground-truth contamination. Gated Channel Attention Module (GCAM) and Frequency-Aware Feature Fusion (FAFF) jointly integrate channel and frequency cues for faithful color restoration and texture recovery. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that SUCode achieves state-of-the-art performance, outperforming recent UIE methods on both reference and no-reference metrics. The code will be made public available at https://github.com/oucailab/ SUCode.

title: Causality-aware Safety Testing for Autonomous Driving Systems
authors: Wenbing Tang, Mingfei Cheng, Renzhi Wang, Yuan Zhou, Chengwei Liu, Yang Liu, Zuohua Ding
abstract: Simulation-based testing is essential for evaluating the safety of Autonomous Driving Systems (ADSs). Comprehensive evaluation requires testing across diverse scenarios that can trigger various types of violations under different conditions. While existing methods typically focus on individual diversity metrics, such as input scenarios, ADS-generated motion commands, and system violations, they often fail to capture the complex interrelationships among these elements. For instance, identical motion commands can produce different collision risks in varying scenes, and the same collision may result from different commands under different scenarios. This oversight leads to gaps in testing coverage, potentially missing critical issues in the ADS under evaluation. In this paper, we propose <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Causal-Fuzzer</monospace>, the first causality-aware fuzzing technique that enables efficient and comprehensive testing of ADSs by constructing causal graphs to model the interrelationships among scenarios, actions, and violations. Unlike existing methods that treat diversity metrics independently, we recognize these elements are causally interconnected and use their relationships to identify more diverse violations triggered by fundamentally different causal mechanisms. Specifically, <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Causal-Fuzzer</monospace> proposes (1) a causality-based feedback mechanism that quantifies the combined diversity of test scenarios by assessing whether they activate new causal relationships, and (2) a causality-driven mutation strategy that prioritizes mutations on input scenario elements with higher causal impact on ego action changes and violation occurrence to enable interpretable and efficient test generation. We evaluated <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Causal-Fuzzer</monospace> on an industry-grade ADS Apollo, with a high-fidelity simulator LGSVL. Our empirical results demonstrate that <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Causal-Fuzzer</monospace> significantly outperforms existing methods in (1) identifying a greater diversity of violations (96.5 violations on average, compared to 66.9 for the best baseline method), (2) providing enhanced testing sufficiency with improved coverage of causal relationships (13.6 unique sceneaction- violation patterns on average, compared to 8.6 for the best baseline method), and (3) achieving greater efficiency in detecting critical scenarios, strong robustness under noise conditions, and good generalizability across varying scenario complexities and violation types. Our source code and experimental results are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://sites.google.com/view/causal-fuzzer</uri>.

title: Federated Learning for Early Heart Disease Prediction using Multilayer Perceptron Models
authors: Aniketh Gandhari, Topalle Siddha Sankalp, Vivin Chandrra Paasam, Y. Vijayalata
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Heart disease continues to be a leading global health concern, and early detection is critical to improving patient outcomes. While machine learning models have shown promise in predicting cardiovascular risk, training them on sensitive medical data raises privacy issues. This paper explores the use of Federated Learning (FL) to build heart disease prediction models without centralizing patient data. We simulate a federated setup using five clients, each training a local Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) model on partitioned data, with updates aggregated on a central server via the Federated Averaging algorithm. The dataset is preprocessed using feature scaling, one-hot encoding, and class balancing through SMOTE before distribution. Our experiments show that the federated MLP performs competitively with a centralized baseline, achieving an AUPRC of 0.9424 and strong recall, while ensuring that raw patient data never leaves local storage. These results highlight the potential of FL in privacy-sensitive domains like healthcare, where data access and compliance constraints often limit centralized approaches.

title: Brain Tumor Detection and Classification Using Deep Learning framework with XAI
authors: MD Basith, Pappula Praveen, Pundru Chandra Shaker Reddy
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Brain tumors (BTs) are serious and often fatal. BT diagnosis is essential for timely intervention, better treatment results, and better patient subsistence and quality-of-life. Automation is essential correct brain tumor diagnostics in comparison to manual analysis. A pre-trained MobileNetV2 is used in the CerebralNet architecture for BT detection and classification in this work. To imitate real-world imaging artifacts, a novel probabilistic image augmentation process uses different strategies. This method with the expanded Brain MRI Dataset greatly improves model resilience. On the imbalanced original (BM) and augmented (ABM) datasets, rigorous evaluation shows remarkable performance, exceeding 97% accuracy on the BM dataset and 98.5% on the ABM dataset. This work uses LIME for model interpretability, revealing the model's decision-making process. These results show that the novel augmentation technique and Atrous MobileNetV2 architecture can improve BT detection and classification, improving therapeutic outcomes.

title: Anomaly Detection in IoT Networks Using Deep Learning and Federated Analytics
authors: Sridhar Sriharsha Rachakonda, Ramesh Lakshmikanth, Ketan Gupta, Vivek Kumar. M
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The proliferation of IoT devices has introduced significant security challenges, particularly in detecting anomalies that may indicate cyber threats. Traditional centralized anomaly detection methods face scalability, privacy, and real-time processing limitations. This paper proposes a novel approach combining deep learning (DL) with federated analytics (FA) to enhance anomaly detection in IoT networks while preserving data privacy. A deep autoencoder model is employed to learn normal network behavior and identify deviations, while FL enables collaborative model training across distributed IoT devices without raw data exchange. This ensures privacy compliance and reduces communication overhead. Additionally, edge computing integration allows real-time detection by processing data locally. Experiments on real-world IoT datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high detection accuracy while minimizing false positives. The federated approach enhances robustness against adversarial attacks and adapts to dynamic network conditions. Comparative analysis shows superior performance over centralized and standalone DL models. A privacy-preserving DL framework for IoT anomaly detection, Federated optimization to improve model generalizability, and reduced latency through edge-assisted inference. This work provides a scalable and secure solution for modern IoT ecosystems, balancing detection efficacy with computational efficiency.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Approach for Predicting Doctor Profession Likelihood Using Astrological Indicators
authors: Bandi Meghana, Aggi Harshitha, Bandameedi Harini, M.A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper aims to develop a model that estimates a person’s inclination to pursue a medical career with the aid of Vedic astrology using machine learning techniques. The model applies Vedic astrological principles with birth chart analysis by evaluating the zodiac sign, house of the ruling planet, conjunctions, and the moon’s phase. A supervised learning framework is applied with a dataset containing birth records of 10,000 individuals labeled as doctors or non-doctors based on their profession. The model’s primary attributes include the person’s sun sign, moon sign, ascendant, and Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Saturn, Rahu, and Ketu. The model benefits from including additional features such as conjunctions of the moon with other celestial bodies and its strength and other relevant parameters. For this classification scope, we opted for XGBoost as the model because of its efficiency on structured data, while overshadowed data classes were handled using the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) to combat data imbalance. The system has been developed into a web application called AstroMed, where users input their astrological information and receive feedback concerning potential alignment with the medical profession. The model achieved an accuracy of 76.57%, demonstrating its ability to capture significant correlations between astrological indicators and consistency in pursuing a medical career.

title: AI-Driven Biotelemetry: Enhancing IoT-Based Human Area Networking with RedTacton for Smart Healthcare Using Nordic nRF7002 (paired with nRF5340)
authors: Partho Kumer Nonda, Md. Arifuzzaman, Md. Shadman Rafid Khan, Al-Ispahani Arif Jahan Jiko, Md. Rokonuzzaman Mim, Rakibul Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: AI and IoT-based biotelemetry are revolutionizing predictive health monitoring by enabling real-time physiological data gathering, analysis, and anomaly diagnosis. This study presents an AI-powered predictive health monitoring system that uses RedTacton technology for secure data sharing and NORDIC NRF7002 (PAIRED WITH NRF5340) for efficient processing. The system uses biological sensors to continuously measure blood glucose, heart rate, SpO2, ECG, temperature, and stress levels. RedTacton-based human body communication securely sends sensor data to an IoT cloud for remote analysis and visualization. RedTacton outperforms Bluetooth, NFC, and RF-based networks in terms of reliability, security, and power efficiency, according to a comparison study. Compared to competitors like the Raspberry Pi Pico W and STM32F4, the NORDIC NRF7002 (PAIRED WITH NRF5340) is a superior choice because to its low cost and built-in wireless capabilities. Machine learning models, including SVM for stress analysis, CNN for ECG classification, Random Forest for glucose prediction, and LSTM for anomaly detection, are used in the artificial intelligence framework. A Python-based system guarantees real-time anomaly identification and triggers alarms for timely response. Experimental validation, which compares this study with that of ESP8266, confirms the usefulness of NORDIC NRF7002 (PAIRED WITH NRF5340) in biotelemetry, as this study had lower Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and Root Mean Square Error (RMSE). To combat cyber threats, security improvements include tamper-proof enclosures, multi-factor authentication, and AES-256 encryption.

title: Early Detection of Knee Osteoporosis Using Fine-Tuned VGG19 Model on X-ray Images
authors: Raghavendra Sridhar, Jatin Sharma, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Deepak Banerjee, Gaurav Sharma
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Usually resulting in major mobility problems and pain, knee osteoporosis (KO) is a common disorder compromising bone strength and raising the risk of fractures. Effective therapy depends on early diagnosis; but conventional techniques include clinical examination and bone mineral density tests can ignore early-stage bone abnormalities. This work uses a customized VGG19 deep learning model to predict knee osteoporosis from X-ray pictures. Leveraging transfer learning, the VGG19 model known for its great performance in image classification was modified for binary classification (healthy against osteoporotic). With both Healthy and Osteoporosis categories, the model was trained and validated on a dataset of knee X-rays including 1,258 training images, 315 validation images, and 472 testing images. With great accuracy of 97.5%, high precision, recall, and F1-scores of 0.97 for both classes, the model performed really remarkably. With just 12 false negatives and low misclassification no false positives the confusion matrix exposed Although sporadic variations in validation loss indicated minor overfitting, the general training and validation accuracy curves showed consistent learning and good generalization. This work validates the ability of deep learning models especially VGG19 in automating knee osteoporosis detection and thereby enhancing diagnosis accuracy. This strategy can greatly improve patient outcomes by giving doctors a strong instrument for early diagnosis. Future research will concentrate on lowering overfitting and enhancing model stability maybe by means of further hyperparameter optimization and regularizing approaches.

title: Dual Band Quad-port D-shaped Slot MIMO Antenna Array for K and Ka Bands Application
authors: Showrajit Saha, Md. Farhad Hossain, Debprosad Das, Mohammad Habibur Rahman Razib, Md. Azad Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This paper presents a dual band quad-port Dshaped slot MIMO antenna array optimized for K and Ka-bands applications. The antenna is comprised of a microstrip-fed patch containing a D-shaped slot, designed on a 48×48 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> rogers rt 5880 substrate (ε<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf> = 2.20). The proposed design employs a T shaped feed network to feed a two-element array, and eight identical patches are further arranged in a 2×4 configuration, forming a four-element MIMO array. The antenna operates efficiently within the frequency ranges of 21.48–22.08 GHz and 30.99–32.28 GHz, achieving peak gains of 10.36 dB at 21.76 GHz and 9 dB at 31.50 GHz across the respective bands. The strategic placement of the ports and optimized element spacing contribute to low mutual coupling, resulting in an isolation of −31.80 dB and an ECC of less than 0.00019 across the dual-band range. The diversity performance metrics are also favorable, the array maintains a diversity gain (DG) of 10 dB , a channel capacity loss (CCL) below 0.001 bits/s/Hz, a mean effective gain (MEG) ranging from −3.0154 dB to −4 dB, and a total active reflection coefficient (TARC) remaining below −32.30 dB throughout the operational bands. The proposed design is particularly suited for advanced millimeter wave applications, including high-speed radar, satellite communication, satellite internet, high-resolution radar imaging and next-generation millimeter-wave wireless systems.

title: Enhancing Urban Mobility with V2X-Driven Smart Traffic Lights for Emergency and Public Traffic
authors: Anjali Madhale, Rahul Kudachi, Rahul Basapur, Ranjita Nayanegali, Vinayak Dalavi
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Communication between vehicles and various entities, known as Vehicle-to-everything (V2X), has emerged as a key element in the advancement of intelligent transportation systems (ITS), enhancing both traffic safety and flow. In response to this, we introduce a V2X-enabled Smart Traffic Light System, called Trafficx, which is implemented using Raspberry Pi and ESP32 modules. This system allows for real-time coordination between the emergency vehicle infrastructure and ordinary civilian vehicles, helping to manage proactive traffic. Using edge computing in conjunction with wireless communication, the system offers a decentralized but efficient approach to traffic management, presenting an economical and scalable solution to the current demands of urban mobility. To evaluate the system, a realistic traffic simulation was conducted using SUMO(Simulation of Urban MObility), a renowned open-source, microscopic, and highly portable traffic simulation tool. The simulation results demonstrate a marked improvement in the response times of emergency vehicles with the proposed system. In addition to simulation, a hardware prototype was developed to identify emergency vehicles such as ambulances, fire trucks, and police cars with a custom object detection model running on the ESP32-CAM. Upon detection, the system automatically turned all other traffic signals red, granting priority to the emergency lane. The experimental results indicated a reduction in emergency vehicle wait times of approximately 60%, which ensured faster intersection clearance and improved response efficiency. The study also highlights the scalability of system and its potential integration with expansive smart city traffic management networks, especially during critical emergency scenarios.

title: Towards Developing an AI-Enhanced Blockchain-Based Real Estate Management System
authors: Jannatul Ferdousi, Israt Jahan Oishi, Solaiman Islam Naiem, Shadman Azad Rivan, Zohirul Islam, Muhammad Nazrul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This article proposes a platform with Artificial Intelligence (AI) for explainable house price prediction and blockchain-enabled smart contracts for secure and transparent property transactions. The three contributions of this work are: (i) a system architecture that combines explainable AI models with decentralized ownership transfer, (ii) cross-comparison of machine learning and deep learning baselines including XGBoost, LSTM, and Transformer-based models, and (iii) a case study on the apartment data of Dhaka city to demonstrating the viability of the platform. Although the Ethereum’s smart contracts ensure automated and verifiable transfers; the article continues to examine scalability problems (e.g., gas cost) and law enforceability. The test outcomes reveal improved prediction accuracy, improved user trust through interpretability, and improved transaction workflows, making the system a compelling foundation for real estate practice modernization.

title: Cerebral Palsy Speech Disorder Detection Using CNN, Whisper and T5
authors: Ameen Muzawar, Pratik Desai, Sahil Sawant, Tejas Bandodkar, K.M Chaman Kumar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: CP patients face considerable speech disability, such as Dysarthria and Apraxia. Current speech therapy and voice recognition systems generally cannot handle the different speech patterns of CP patients. This study presents an AI-driven Speech Correction System aimed at providing a communication enhancement approach along with diagnostics. Utilizing sophisticated machine learning, the system adapts to individual speech variations and offers immediate corrections. A mixture of supervised and unsupervised learning frameworks was implemented to let the system work for each patient. Evaluation outcomes indicated enhancements in accuracy, response time, and overall effectiveness relative to current alternatives. The system enhances communication, integrates with assistive technologies, and significantly improves life quality for CP patients, highlighting AI's role in speech accessibility.

title: Seamless Integration of the PV System with the Three-Phase Dual Inverter for Open-End Winding IM Drive
authors: Balakrishna Kothapalli, G T Sundar Rajan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper presents a seamless integration of a photovoltaic (PV) system with a three-phase dual inverter configuration to drive an open-end winding induction motor (OEWIM), offering enhanced fault tolerance and efficient energy conversion. To ensure maximum power extraction under dynamic environmental conditions, a Beluga Whale Optimization (BWO) algorithm is employed for Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT), providing fast convergence and high tracking accuracy. The dual inverter topology improves fault resilience, reduces harmonic distortion, and enables flexible voltage control, making it ideal for variable speed drive applications. A Model Predictive Control combined with Space Vector Modulation (MPC–SVM) scheme governs the motor drive, ensuring precise speed regulation, reduced torque ripple, and reliable performance during sudden load and irradiance changes. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed architecture in maintaining stable operation, smooth power exchange, and optimal energy utilization, making it well-suited for renewable-powered industrial and transportation systems facing unpredictable load profiles.

title: CNN Based ThreadGuard-XGBoost: An Inception-Boosted Ensemble Framework for Intellectual Property Protection in Silk Designs
authors: Sruthy Murugan Usha, Marimuthu U., B.Lavaraju, Lordson Samuel M, J.Shrijaa, Sreekrishaanth Yadav
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The ThreadGuard-XGBoost framework is an advanced model designed to protect the intellectual property (IP) of Kanchipuram silk designs from counterfeiting by utilizing a hybrid ensemble learning approach. This model integrates the powerful Inception Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for feature extraction with the efficiency of XGBoost for classification tasks. The dataset consists of high-resolution silk images annotated with detailed metadata, including weaving techniques and color patterns. The model was evaluated based on several performance metrics, achieving an overall accuracy of 97.65% in classifying authentic and counterfeit designs. Additionally, the framework demonstrated high precision (97.7%), recall (97.85%), and an AUC of 0.987, highlighting its robust capacity to detect counterfeits while maintaining a low false positive rate. The model also excelled in forgery detection, with a True Positive Rate (TPR) of 97.96% for authentic designs and 96.00% for counterfeit ones. In real-world testing, the framework proved to be scalable, maintaining a fast processing time of 0.42 seconds per image when tested on a large dataset of up to 5000 images. The model provides a reliable solution for protecting the IP of Kanchipuram silk designs and has potential applications in other textile industries.

title: Exploring Fault Tolerant Hierarchical IoT Architecture with Cost-Aware Routing for Resilient Data Aggregation
authors: Md. Sazed Ul Karim, Marzia Akhter Sonia, Sahidur Rahman, Maksuda Mostofa Munmun, S M Saiful Islam, Sayma Alam Suha
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The expansion of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has necessitated the development of resilient and scalable data processing architectures capable of handling latency, bandwidth, and fault tolerance challenges in distributed environments. This paper presents a hierarchical fault-tolerant IoT–Fog–Cloud architecture that ensures reliable data aggregation through dynamic, cost-aware routing mechanisms. The proposed framework employs redundant communication pathways and integrates Dijkstra and Bellman-Ford algorithms for route selection, each augmented with a multi-metric cost function that jointly optimizes latency, energy consumption, and fog node processing capacity. A hardware-based experimental testbed comprising ten edge nodes, three fog nodes, and one cloud node, was constructed to evaluate system performance under both nominal and fault-induced conditions. Results demonstrate the architecture’s robustness in maintaining continuous data transmission during fog node failures, with minimal performance degradation. Comparative analysis reveals that Dijkstra’s algorithm offers superior performance in stable networks, while Bellman-Ford provides adaptability in dynamic, failure-prone environments. The study further investigates the sensitivity of routing efficiency to tunable cost parameters and highlights the system’s capability to balance throughput and resilience in constrained IoT deployments. These findings advance the state of fault-tolerant network design by validating a practical, cost-optimized solution for scalable and resilient IoT infrastructures.

title: Transforming RAN into a Service: The Confluence of Cloud-Native and AI Technologies
authors: S. Saravanan, T. Thanigasalam, V Ganesan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The transformation of Radio Access Networks (RAN) from rigid, hardware-centric deployments into flexible, service-oriented infrastructures marks an important shift in the evolution of wireless communication. This paper introduces the method of RAN-as-a-Service (RANaaS), an emerging architectural paradigm enabled by the integration of cloud-native technologies and artificial intelligence (AI). By decoupling traditional RAN functions and exposing them as dynamically orchestrated microservices, RANaaS allows for elastic, SLA-driven provisioning of radio resources. Simultaneously, AI augments the network with predictive analytics and real-time adaptation capabilities, enabling mutual awareness between RAN and application services. Such mutual service awareness empowers applications to influence network behavior and vice versa, optimizing end-to-end performance, particularly in latency-sensitive and bandwidth-intensive use cases. This work explores the enablers, architectural principles, and operational implications of RANaaS, while identifying key challenges such as orchestration complexity, multi-vendor interoperability, and inference latency. The convergence of cloud-native design and AI in the RAN context not only enhances resource agility and service quality but also lays the foundation for intent-based, autonomous 6G networks.

title: Engineering AI-Driven Predictive Maintenance Systems for Medical Equipment
authors: Mario Antony, Monali Gulhane, Nisha Wankhade, Nitin Rakesh, Kalpana Malpe, Pawan Kumar Verma
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The standard of patient care has gotten a lot better as medical technology has grown so quickly. But the dependability and life of medical tools are very important for keeping these progresses going. Unplanned downtime because of broken equipment can make it harder to care for patients and cost more to maintain. Scheduled checks and fixes done after the fact are common ways of maintaining things the old way, which might not catch problems before they break. This paper shows a new way to use AI-driven systems for predicted repair on hospital tools. Using machine learning algorithms and IoT (Internet of Things) devices, the suggested system checks on the health of equipment all the time, looks at data in real time, and guesses when problems might happen before they do. The AI model is taught by looking at old data from medical devices to find trends of wear and failure. This lets repair plans be made ahead of time. This forecast method increases the usage of equipment, lowers the cost of upkeep, and makes sure that medical gadgets are working at their best, which makes healthcare centres more efficient overall. In addition, the method makes medical operations safer by lowering the chance that tools will break down at crucial times. Case studies from healthcare settings show how well the system works in different kinds of medical technology, like surgery tools, testing machines, and life-support systems. The paper talks about the problems with execution, like combining data and making sure the model is correct, and gives ways to get around these problems. In the end, the suggested AI-driven predictive maintenance system would completely change how medical equipment is handled, leading to better care for patients, lower costs, and flawless operations.

title: Implementation of a ROS 2 Framework for Mobile Robot Localization and Navigation Using AMCL and Nav2
authors: Adnan Sabith Taha, Meraj Hossain Jibon, Md Shamim Hossen, Tarek Ahamed, Nabid Nur Hasan Sabby, Md Anisur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Autonomous vehicle development faces persistent challenges in integrating localization, path planning, and environment interaction, especially in early-stage testing where real-world trials are costly and constrained. This study presents a simulation-based autonomous navigation system implemented in the Gazebo environment using the ROS 2 framework. A differential-drive vehicle was modeled using URDF, equipped with simulated sensors including LiDAR and odometry. Localization was performed using Adaptive Monte Carlo Localization (AMCL), and navigation was achieved through the ROS 2 Navigation Stack (Nav2), supporting global path planning, obstacle avoidance, and dynamic goal execution. The system was validated entirely in simulation, providing a low-cost, modular foundation for autonomous vehicle research and prototyping.

title: An Intelligent Decision Support System for Human Resource Planning Using Fuzzy Logic and AHP Techniques
authors: Kabirdoss Devi, V. Ramu, K. Sampath, S. Venkatesan, Murugan Kandaswamy
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Human Resource Planning (HRP) is an important part of running a business that makes sure there are enough people with the relevant abilities available when required. Because people are subjective and it's hard to judge qualitative elements, traditional HRP approaches typically don't make decisions that are very accurate. This study suggests a new and unified technique that combines Fuzzy Logic with the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) to create an intelligent Decision Support System (DSS) that makes human resource planning more reliable and accurate. The method employs fuzzy sets to show how unclear and vague human judgment may be, and it also ranks numerous HR factors, including as skill levels, performance, experience, and organizational needs, in order of priority. The system helps you be ready for recruiting, assigning staff, and succession in a manner that is orderly, scalable, and adaptable. This used a real-world HR dataset to evaluate the created DSS, and it showed huge improvements in how effectively it planned and how consistently it made judgments. The proposed model is useful for HR in making better judgments in circumstances that are hard to understand and convoluted.

title: Advanced Power Management Strategies for Hybrid Electric Vehicles
authors: Sundeep Siddula, Dongari Archana, Nunavath Mukesh, Reddaboina Aravind Kumar, T Pavan Teja
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The shift toward sustainable transportation solutions has driven the advancement of hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs) that incorporate innovative energy management systems. This abstract explores the impact of integrating fuel cells, batteries, and ultracapacitors within these systems, highlighting their combined potential to enhance vehicle performance and environmental benefits. Fuel cells offer high energy efficiency and extended range by converting hydrogen into electricity, while batteries provide substantial energy storage and power for continuous driving and regenerative braking. Ultracapacitors, with their rapid charge and discharge capabilities, handle peak power demands and boost vehicle responsiveness. The synergy of these technologies within a unified energy management system optimizes power distribution, extends vehicle range, reduces emissions, this strategy not only enhances fuel efficiency but also helps cut down on greenhouse gas emissions and reduces reliance on fossil fuels. but also fosters the development of more adaptable and resilient transportation systems. The study underscores the comprehensive advantages of such hybrid configurations, including enhanced performance, greater energy efficiency, and increased operational flexibility, marking a significant step in the evolution of eco-friendly automotive technologies.

title: Attention-Driven Deep Learning Models for Predicting Fetal Development Stages
authors: H S Vijaya Kumar, Tejashree D
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Prompt and reliable interpretation of fetal ultrasound images is crucial for efficient prenatal care. Manual detection of common scan planes like abdominal circumference (AC), biparietal diameter (BPD), and femur length (FL) is susceptible to inconsistency and adds to the workload of clinicians. To overcome this, we examine the capability of several deep convolutional network architectures for the automatic labelling of fetal ultrasound planes. Models such as Simple CNN, CNN with Batch Normalization, Deeper CNN, CNN with Dropout, and Wider CNN were trained and tested on a labelled dataset of four classes: AC_PLANE, BPD_PLANE, FL_PLANE, and NO_PLANE. Among them, the CNN with Batch Normalization yielded the maximum accuracy of 98%, with an F1-score of 0.98 on all classes. This automated scan plane classification serves as a crucial preprocessing step for downstream biometric estimation and segmentation tasks, ultimately supporting obstetricians in improving diagnostic efficiency and fetal health monitoring.

title: Advanced Traffic Optimization and Automated Toll Collection System using Automated Number Plate Recognition and Modified YOLO Algorithm
authors: Md. Rashedul Alam, Nilay Biswas, Md. Zulfiker Mahmud
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Innovative solutions in transportation management, have been made possible by the quick development of computer vision technologies. This project presents the Automated Toll Booth System (ATBS) through the integration of YOLOv8 (an object detection algorithm) and the Own build ATBS API for accurate number plate recognition to streamline toll collection procedures. In order for the suggested system to function, YOLOv8 must be deployed in order to identify incoming vehicles and extract the zones of interest that include number plates. The ATBS API is then used to process these areas, extracting alphanumeric letters from the number plates through the use of optical character recognition (OCR) technologies. To ensure smooth toll collection, the retrieved plate numbers are then compared to a database of registered cars. The ATBS’s high precision number plate recognition, flexibility to handle different traffic volumes, and real-time processing capabilities are some of its key advantages. Additionally, the system is made to require as little manual intervention as possible, which lowers operating costs and increases efficiency. The efficacy of the suggested ATBS is confirmed by extensive testing and performance analysis, indicating its potential to transform toll collection systems by providing a dependable, automated solution. This study adds to the continuing endeavors to improve traffic management systems and transportation infrastructure through the application of computer vision technologies.

title: Emotion Detection in Speech Using Lightweight and Transformer-Based Models: A Comparative and Ablation Study
authors: Lucky Onyekwelu-Udoka, Md Shafiqul Islam, Md Shahedul Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Emotion recognition from speech plays a vital role in the development of empathetic human-computer interaction systems. This paper presents a comparative analysis of lightweight transformer-based models, DistilHuBERT and PaSST, by classifying six core emotions from the CREMA-D dataset. We benchmark their performance against a traditional CNN-LSTM baseline model using MFCC features. DistilHuBERT demonstrates superior accuracy (70.64%) and F1 score (70.36%) while maintaining an exceptionally small model size (0.02 MB), outperforming both PaSST and the baseline. Furthermore, we conducted an ablation study on three variants of the PaSST, Linear, MLP, and Attentive Pooling heads, to understand the effect of classification head architecture on model performance. Our results indicate that PaSST with an MLP head yields the best performance among its variants but still falls short of DistilHuBERT. Among the emotion classes, angry is consistently the most accurately detected, while disgust remains the most challenging. These findings suggest that lightweight transformers like DistilHuBERT offer a compelling solution for real-time speech emotion recognition on edge devices. The code is available at: https://github.com/luckymaduabuchi/Emotion-detection-.

title: Multilingual Digitization of Ancient Manuscripts: OCR-Based Preservation and Translation
authors: Madhavabotla Lakshmi Shrividya, Omsai Tejaswini Sureddi, N Neelima, Manish Rajan Walvekar, Vivek Venugopal
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Digitization of sanskrit manuscripts is essential in order to preserve knowledge and to make it more accessible to researchers and scholars. This paper presents a solution using SanskritDoc AI, an advanced Optical Character Recognition (OCR) based pre-processing and enhancement platform tailored for sanskrit manuscript digitization and it is also designed with accuracy to enhance the extraction of multilingual translation and transliteration of manuscript text. This system uses advanced image pre-processing methods such as Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE), Gaussian blur to filter noise, and morphological operations to enhance the quality of degraded scripts. The extracted text is then translated by Google Translate in order to render contextual sense and word-by-word transliteration by Indic Transliteration Library. The significant contribution of this work is multilingual support and editable Word documention. Experimental trials show OCR accuracy of up to 95%, reflecting the effectiveness, scalability, and potential of this system in preserving culture.

title: Optimizing Strategies in the Iterated Prisoner’s Dilemma Using Genetic Improvement Algorithms
authors: Siddhant Poojary
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The Iterated Prisoner’s Dilemma (IPD) offers a rich framework for understanding strategic interactions in various domains, from economics to politics. This paper explores the use of genetic improvement algorithms to generate optimal solution sequences for competing strategies within the IPD. By leveraging evolutionary methods, the study seeks to find the best response sequences that maximize scores against well-established strategies from the Axelrod library. Our methodology emphasizes the iterative refinement of strategies through genetic algorithms, focusing on mutation rates, crossover, and population size. The results reveal distinct patterns in strategy optimization, offering insights into both deterministic and stochastic opponent behaviors. These findings have potential applications in enhancing decision-making processes in competitive environments.

title: Revolutionizing Dermatology with AI: Skin Type Classification Using ResNet50
authors: Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Vishnu Kant, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Raghavendra Sridhar, Lekha Rani Bhambhu, Deepak Banerjee
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Skin type classification is essential in dermatology, skincare product development, and AI-driven cosmetic applications. Often lacking accuracy, conventional approaches include visual examination, self-reported questionnaires, and sebum testing techniques that cause misclassification and poor skincare advice. Leveraging deep learning and image processing for accurate categorisation into oily, dry, and normal skin types, this work presents an automated skin type classification model utilising a fine-tuned ResNet50 architecture. Model training and assessment were conducted using a 3,153 high-resolution picture collection drawn from the open-source Kaggle platform. Images were preprocessed using data augmentation, normalising, and scaling (224×224 pixels) to improve generalisation. Although the training was optimized using the Adam optimiser with categorical cross-entropy loss, the pretrained ResNet50 model was fine-tuned by changing its fully connected layers. Guiding model evaluation was accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. With few misclassifications—especially between oily and dry skin—the highly tuned ResNet50 model found skin types by obtaining an 84% accuracy. Confusion matrix study confirmed the model's reliability even in cases when sometimes-overlapping skin features led to errors. Suggested overfitting by training and validation accuracy curves points to the requirement of further regularising methods. This work investigates artificial intelligence prospects in dermatology using an automated, scalable, and reasonably priced substitute for current skin assessment techniques. Expanding dataset variety, improving model generalisation, and including explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) approaches for better openness and pragmatic relevance will be the main topics of future studies.

title: Exploring the Potential of Reinforcement Learning in Banking Processes
authors: Waheeduddin Khadri Syed, Prashamsh Takkalapally, Janamolla Kavitha Reddy, Rakesh Ramakrishnan, Karim Hudani, T.Kiruthiga
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: In recent years, reinforcement learning (RL) has received attention in artificial intelligence because of its capacity to analyze complicated selection-making procedures in dynamic environments. RL is a machine learning technology that learns from interactions with the environment, using rewards or offense to move towards a desired outcome manually. It has brought its utility to diverse industries, including finance and banking. Reinforcement learning in banking operations can enhance performance, minimize errors, and develop client satisfaction. One key area where RL may be applied is fraud detection and prevention. By constantly studying past experiences, RL algorithms can adapt and detect new fraudulent techniques and make more accurate choices in detecting suspicious transactions. RL can also be utilized in personalization and banking support systems. By mastering client interactions and behavior, RL can generate personalized products and services, leading to customer satisfaction and retention.

title: Cybersecurity and Privacy Threats in Social-Media: Extracting and Classifying Leaked Personal Information Using Machine Learning
authors: Krity Dhar, Dhiman Sarma
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The chance of inadvertently disclosing personal information has significantly grown due to the rapid expansion of social networking sites, posing serious privacy and security issues. Sensitive data such as phone numbers, email addresses, bank account details, and personal identifiers are frequently exposed through public posts and comments. Users often embed such information into passwords for memorability, thereby further amplifying their vulnerability to cyberattacks. Once harvested, this data can be exploited to infer passwords or facilitate automated bot-based intrusions. While conventional methods, including regular expressions (regex) and Named Entity Recognition (NER), have been used to extract sensitive data, they rarely include risk-level categorization processes and frequently have low accuracy. This study presents a customized Personal Information Extraction (PIE) model that integrates regex-based patterns, SpaCy’s NER, and BERT-based NER with a suite of machine learning classifiers to accurately extract personal data and classify content as either "High Risk" or "Low Risk." The proposed approach leverages a labeled corpus, the PIE dataset, comprising 3,468 social media posts and comments. Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency(TF-IDF) is utilized for extracting important features from input text, and various classifiers—including K-Nearest Neighbors(KNN), Naive Bayes(NB), Logistic Regression(LR), Decision Tree(DT), Random Forest(RF), AdaBoost, XGBoost, Extra Trees, Gradient Boosting, Support Vector Machine(SVM), and Ridge Classifier—are evaluated. Experimental results demonstrate that the Extra Trees classifier achieves the best performance, attaining 86.17% accuracy, outperforming the baseline models in both extraction and classification tasks. The proposed method offers an effective, scalable, and automated framework to strengthen digital privacy and risk management on social platforms.

title: Unsupervised Anomaly Detection for Smart IoT Devices: Performance and Resource Comparison
authors: Md. Sad Abdullah Sami, Mushfiquzzaman Abid
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The rapid expansion of Internet of Things (IoT) deployments across diverse sectors has significantly enhanced operational efficiency, yet concurrently elevated cybersecurity vulnerabilities due to increased exposure to cyber threats. Given the limitations of traditional signature-based Anomaly Detection Systems (ADS) in identifying emerging and zero day threats, this study investigates the effectiveness of two unsupervised anomaly detection techniques, Isolation Forest (IF) and OneClass Support Vector Machine (OC-SVM), using the TON_IoT thermostat dataset. A comprehensive evaluation was performed based on standard metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score) alongside critical resource utilization metrics such as inference time, model size, and peak RAM usage. Experimental results revealed that IF consistently outperformed OC-SVM, achieving higher detection accuracy, superior precision, and recall, along with a significantly better F1-score. Furthermore, Isolation Forest demonstrated a markedly superior computational footprint, making it notably more suitable for deployment on resource-constrained IoT edge devices. These findings underscore Isolation Forest’s robustness in high-dimensional, imbalanced IoT environments and highlight its practical viability for real-time anomaly detection.

title: A Dual Attention Mechanism-Based Enhanced Residual Network for Skin Cancer Detection
authors: Sajib Tripura, Adil Hussain, Dhonita Tripura, Mostak Ahamed, Ayesha Aslam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Medical imaging of skin diseases has been used to detect and classify them employing deep learning-based algorithms.But models using deep learning will not be effective in detecting and classifying rare skin diseases. This is because rare skin diseases possess a minimal number of data samples. Therefore, the dataset will be incredibly biased, and since there is a bias in learning, the majority of the models perform better. Deep learning models cannot identify infected small segments of skin diseases within the entire picture area. This paper presents a dual attention mechanism-enhanced residual Network (DAM-ResNet). The research adopts the HAM10000 database, which has seven skin lesion classes. The article is based on the technique of mixed resampling to tackle the imbalanced data. The proposed model is trained and validated utilizing the training and validation datasets. The performance evaluation is conducted with the test set. The proposed method shows outstanding performance on the HAM10000 dataset, for multiclass classification, achieving over 99% accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 scores for all classes, including skin lesions. The comparison with the literature study demonstrates that the proposed model obtains higher accuracy. This outstanding performance underscores the model's capability to precisely distinguish among various types of skin cancer with minimum errors.

title: Efficient VLSI Implementation of 256 bit AES-Advanced Encryption Standard
authors: Tharun Tejas G, B N Shobha, Naveen K B
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper presents the design, simulation, and FPGA synthesis of a 256-bit Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) encryption core, implemented in Verilog HDL. The design was verified using Intel’s ModelSim simulator and synthesized using Intel Quartus Prime targeting the Cyclone V FPGA. AES-256, known for its high security, is realized in a hardware-efficient, resource-conscious manner for embedded cryptographic applications.The proposed architecture integrates all standard AES transformations—SubBytes, ShiftRows, MixColumns, and AddRound-Key—along with a dynamic 256-bit key expansion mechanism. The design employs a Finite State Machine (FSM)-controlled, single-round iterative approach, reusing one AES round module across all 14 encryption rounds. This reduces hardware overhead, routing complexity, and power consumption, while retaining functional correctness and scalability.Although inherently iterative, the architecture adopts pipeline-aware modularization principles through structured FSM timing and round-stage encapsulation. This hybrid strategy enables efficient resource usage and maintains design clarity, drawing from pipelining methodology without implementing full parallel stages.To ensure flexibility and deployment suitability, the AES core is mapped onto Cyclone V FPGA, demonstrating favorable resource utilization, timing closure, and operational latency, validating its use in real-time, low-power cryptographic systems.

title: Drone-Assisted Precision Agriculture with Hybrid Machine Learning Models for Sustainable Farming
authors: Davinder Paul Singh, P. Chandra Prakash Reddy, G. Devayani, S. Poongothai, G. Suganthi, G. Charles Babu
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Precision agriculture experiences difficulties in effective crop monitoring and resource management due to restricted real-time data analysis and the generalised use of inputs. This study presents a drone-assisted, hybrid machine learning framework that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) for sustainable agriculture. Aerial footage of high resolution obtained from drones is analysed for feature extraction and classification to evaluate crop health and suggest specific treatments. The suggested method significantly boosts accuracy and operating efficiency relative to baseline models such as SVM, KNN, and Decision Trees. The hybrid CNN+XGBoost model attained an accuracy of 96.4%, precision of 95.1%, recall of 94.7%, and an AUC of 0.982 through attentive model training and validation utilising datasets from Kaggle. The results confirm the system's capacity to accurately identify stressed vegetation. The suggested framework, in contrast to standard models, integrates environmental and economic characteristics, facilitating sustainable input utilisation. This research enhances machine learning applications in agriculture while providing a scalable and resource-efficient solution. The integration of drone technology with advanced analytics facilitates the emergence of next-generation smart farming systems. Future enhancements encompass real-time analytics, multi-spectral sensor integration, and adaptive learning to generalise across different agro-climatic zones.

title: A Hybrid Attention-Based ST-CNN and Penman-Monteith Framework for High-Resolution Soil Moisture Recharge Prediction Using Satellite Driven Data
authors: Tonmoy Paul, Pritam Sarkar
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: In monsoon-affected tropical deltas such as Bangladesh, where precipitation, evapotranspiration, and anthropogenic use of water combine complexly, accurately predicting soil moisture recharge (SMR) is essential for efficient agricultural water management. Most of the time, conventional procedure-based or machine learning approaches fail to do an adequate job of illustrating how SMR changes over time and space at high resolution. To deal with this problem, we present an innovative hybrid approach that brings together the Penman-Monteith evapotranspiration model with a spatiotemporal convolutional neural network that is based on attention (ST-CNN-Attention). Bangladesh’s all 64 districts have been included in the model, which utilizes the complete NASA POWER dataset encompassing the years 2004–2024. It includes 22 daily climatic and geographic parameters. To maintain the reliability of the hydrological system, it employs CNN-based pattern recognition from satellite data, LSTM networks with attention mechanisms that focus on vital monsoon dynamics, and physically controlled evapotranspiration. This is the very first high-resolution SMR forecast system built specifically for Bangladesh’s predominant polder hydrology. It is more accurate yet simpler to utilize. This structure is versatile so it can be utilized to arrange proper irrigation, alert of droughts ahead of time, and take ways to deal with climate change. Also, it could be beneficial in other delta regions that have been affected by rainfall and have water-management problems that are equivalent to those in the above locations.

title: Metaheuristic Neural Network Predictions of Flow Coefficient of Three Way Lever Operated Ball Valve for Hydraulic Working Fluid
authors: Gourav Vivek Kulkarni, Mahesh M Sattigeri, Shreeram Pai, Siddharth Ningannavar, Ganesh Pattanashetti, Naveen Kurabet
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This study presents a novel approach for predicting flow coefficients (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">V</inf>) in ball valves by integrating metaheuristic optimization algorithms. Artificial Bee Colony (ABC), Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), and Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA) with multilayer perceptron neural networks were considered accordingly. Accurate flow coefficient estimation is necessary for optimizing valve design and ensuring efficient fluid control in mechanical systems. The results, ranging valve sizes of 1.5" to 3" and neural network iterations from 100 to 2000, showed that WOA consistently outperformed ABC and ACO, achieving the lowest mean absolute errors as low as 0.09 within training datasets and 0.13 beyond training datasets, thereby showing up to 40% improvement over other algorithms. ABC and ACO showed higher errors and greater errors, reaching up to 0.41 in some cases. These findings demonstrate WOA’s superior convergence speed and prediction stability. By demonstrating metaheuristic neural network applications for flow coefficient prediction, this study highlights the scope of such hybrid models to solve nonlinear fluid dynamics challenges effectively. The performance of WOA-optimized neural networks encourages their adoption in advanced mechanical engineering tasks, including valve performance analysis and control, and can also be considered for predictive modeling techniques.

title: Enhanced Edge Detection for Noisy Images Using Sobel and Canny on FPGA with Median Filtering Optimization
authors: Poorvika T P, Manojkumar S B, Srividya C N, M B Anandaraju, Naveen K B, B N Shobha
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Edge detection is a critical operation in image processing, supporting applications such as autonomous vehicles, surveillance systems, and object recognition. However, conventional methods like Sobel and Canny struggle under high-noise conditions such as salt-and-pepper noise.This paper proposes noise-resilient edge detection architecture implemented on an FPGA using the Xilinx Vivado development suite. The design integrates a median filtering stage prior to applying Sobel and Canny edge detectors, significantly improving edge clarity while maintaining computational efficiency. Grayscale conversion and 3×3 median filtering are implemented using pipelined shift registers to enhance throughput. The system is deployed on a Xilinx xc7a35tcsg324 FPGA and demonstrates a low hardware footprint—utilizing only 4.35% of LUTs and 20% of BRAM. Comparative experiments validate that the proposed median filtering stage effectively suppresses salt-and-pepper noise while preserving critical edge structures, outperforming mean filtering in both visual quality and system accuracy. Although the current implementation lacks adaptive thresholding, it provides a scalable framework for future integration with AI-based filtering techniques and high-resolution image processing.

title: Graph-Augmented YOLO: A Hybrid Pipeline for Robust Brain Tumor Localization and Classification in MRI Scans
authors: Lamisa Mashiat, Shefayatuj Johara Chowdhury
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Early and precise identification of brain tumors in MRI serves an essential part in patient survival and in designing effective treatment strategies. Although convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and object detection approaches like YOLO demonstrate strong performance in tumor localization, they generally analyze each detection in isolation, neglecting spatial and contextual relationships that could enhance classification accuracy. We propose a hybrid framework that integrates YOLOv8 for tumor localization with graph neural networks (GNNs) for relational reasoning. Detected regions are represented as graph nodes, with edges encoding spatial proximity, allowing classification to incorporate inter-lesion context. A curated dataset containing 5,249 annotated MRI scans was used to assess two architectures of graph neural network— Graph Attention Networks and Graph Convolutional Networks—across four tumor classes: Glioma, Meningioma, Pituitary, and the absence of tumor. The YOLO– GCN pipeline achieved the best performance with 92% accuracy, surpassing YOLO-only (88%) and CNN baselines. GAT also improved results, reaching 91% accuracy. Confusion matrix and embedding analyses confirmed reduced misclassifications between visually similar tumor types, particularly Meningioma and Pituitary. Coupling YOLO-based detection with graph-based reasoning enhances robustness, interpretability, and diagnostic reliability, providing a promising framework for computer-aided neuro-oncology.

title: Rainfall Prediction Using Exploratory Data Analysis and Machine Learning
authors: MD. Tasin Tazwar, Nippon Datta, Payel Sen, Muhammad Mujibur Rahman Shourav, Umida Khudaybergenova, Hayitov Abdulla Nurmatovich
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Forecasting rainfall is essential for managing water resources, agriculture, and preventing disasters. In this study, we suggest a machine learning-based rainfall prediction system that improves forecast accuracy by addressing a number of real-world data issues. In order to identify and handle missing values via mode imputation, data pretreatment techniques were first used. Using statistical methods (mean ± 3*standard deviation), outliers were found and dealt with properly to enhance model performance. Using the SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique) framework, the dataset’s class imbalance was addressed. To facilitate model selection and comprehend inter-feature interactions, a correlation matrix was created. We have evaluated three different machine learning algorithms, which are Random Forest, Logistic Regression, and Decision Tree. The highest accuracy, 93%, has been achieved by Random forest, whereas the accuracies of LR and DT are 87% and 85%, respectively.

title: Decentralized Partial Model Personalization with Guaranteed Nonconvex Convergence_supp1-3656454.pdf
authors: Xueqian Wang
abstract: Compared with general Federated Learning (FL), Decentralized FL (DFL) has diminished central communication burdens and lower risks of disruption. To be compatible with real-world scenarios, some existing works produce several local personalized models rather than a universal model for all edge devices. However, they still suffer from inferior performance due to the full model aggregation in heterogeneous datasets. Therefore, we propose a DFL framework DFedMDC through partial model personalization, which can adapt to resource-heterogeneous environments (e.g., the Internet of Things (IoT)). Specifically, it personalizes the “right” components of local models and trains the shared model ui and personal model vi alternately in each client i. To further accelerate the convergence process, we propose DFedSMDC with properly directed noise perturbation into the gradient update. Theoretically, we provide convergence analysis of both algorithms in the general non-convex setting. It can shed light on how vital factors affect the convergence rate, such as these alternate updates of partial models ui, vi, data heterogeneity δ2 as well as various communication topologies (characterized by the spectral gap 1 − λ). Empirically, we confirm the state-of-the-art (SOTA) superiority of the proposed methods on several real-world datasets with various data distributions, relative to both SOTA personalized FL (PFL) and DFL baselines.
