title: RIS-Aided LEO Satellite Communications With Two Generalized Spatial Modulation Designs
authors: Chaorong Zhang, Benjamin K. Ng, Chan-Tong Lam
abstract: This paper presents innovative approaches to enhance LEO satellite communications by integrating reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) with generalized spatial modulation (GSM). We propose two RIS-aided LEO satellite GSM schemes (RIS-LEO-GSM and RIS-LEO-IGSM) that exploit RIS phase modulation to improve transmission efficiency with low power consumption. Both schemes significantly enhance spectral efficiency (SE) and provide flexible antenna configurations. The RIS-LEO-IGSM scheme, in particular, demonstrates superior performance by transmitting multiple constellation symbols per time slot, capitalizing on multi-antenna activation. Analytical performance of SE and average BER is also provided in this paper with some interesting insights. Simulation results confirm the BER and SE improvements of the proposed schemes, showing their potential for 6G satellite systems demanding high through put and reliability.

title: Adaptive Flight Planning for Neural 3D Reconstruction With Low-Altitude UAVs
authors: Daniel Johannes Bugelnig, Filip Lemic, Álmos Veres-Vitályos, Genís Castillo Gómez-Raya, Xavier Costa-Pérez, Bernhard Rinner
abstract: Contemporary advances in the Neural 3 Dimensional (3D) Reconstruction (N3DR) have enabled new possibilities for detailed scene understanding and immersive environment modeling. However, the quality of reconstruction remains heavily sensitive to viewpoint selection and flight trajectory, particularly in low-altitude Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) applications, where energy, time, and occlusions pose significant challenges. This article presents an intelligent and adaptive path planning framework that dynamically optimizes UAVs’ viewpoints to maximize neural reconstruction quality while respecting real-world constraints. Unlike static or heuristic approaches, our system integrates online feedback from the reconstruction engine to guide UAVs toward underexplored or uncertain regions in real time. We review key principles of view planning, identify limitations of current strategies, and introduce a modular framework capable of fusing scene priors, uncertainty maps, and flight constraints into trajectory optimization. Finally, we describe an experimental evaluation methodology that leverages a real-world testbed deployment and synthetic benchmarks to validate the effectiveness of dynamic planning on both reconstruction accuracy and operational efficiency. This work aims to bridge aerial robotics, Internet of Things (IoT)-enabled vision systems, and Artificial Intelligence (AI)-driven planning for robust 3 Dimensional (3D) mapping in complex environments.

title: The Two-Dimensional Inverse Scattering of Second-Order Nonlinear Media Based on Contrast Source Inversion Method_supp1-3661746.pdf
authors: Bo O. Zhu
abstract: Optical nonlinear media are the building blocks of nonlinear optics, which are commonly characterized by nonlinear polarizability. The inversion of polarizability for nonlinear media is an important problem in nonlinear optics. However, the maturity and accuracy of nonlinear media inversion methods lag behind those of ordinary linear media inversion methods because analytically and rigorously solving the Maxwell equations with nonlinear media is quite complicated. In order to derive inversion formulas, traditional nonlinear media inversion methods have to make various physical or mathematical approximations to the Maxwell equations. Essentially, the inversion of polarizability in nonlinear media belongs to the inverse scattering problem. Inspired by the advantages of numerical algorithms for solving the inverse scattering problem in linear media, we generalize the contrast source inversion method, which is one of the most popular methods for linear media, to second-order nonlinear media. The two-dimensional media distribution and undepleted-pump approximation are considered. Numerical tests conducted on either transverse magnetic or transverse electric data or joint data show that the generalized method can successfully reconstruct the nonlinear media distributions, and the joint inversion gives the best reconstruction quality in all test cases.

title: Evaluation of s-EMG Sensor Locations for Upper-limb Compensatory Movement Detection
authors: Mahshad Berjis, Marie-Eve LeBel, Daniel J. Lizotte, Ana Luisa Trejos
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Musculoskeletal rehabilitation is crucial for restoring upper limb function after elbow trauma or stroke. In unsupervised rehabilitation, patients may develop compensatory movements that hinder recovery. While wearable devices for home rehabilitation are a promising supplement to clinical therapy, they may overlook movement quality. Detecting compensatory motions in wearable systems is challenging, as placing sensors on all involved muscles reduces wearability, increases computational and power demands, and complicates sensor management. The objective of this study was to identify optimal locations for surface electromyography sensors for detecting compensatory movements. Data were collected from 40 healthy individuals performing various uniplanar and multiplanar tasks under conditions simulating both healthy and impaired movements. Sensor combinations that showed significant differences between healthy and compensatory patterns were identified through statistical analysis and feature importance techniques. The classification performance of these sensor combinations was then evaluated. Results indicate that 11 sensors placed on the upper trapezius, deltoids, biceps, triceps, latissimus dorsi, erector spinae, rectus abdominis, and external oblique muscles were key for accurate detection (accuracy = 81.43%, F1 score = 0.8549). Additionally, the number of sensors can be reduced to seven without compromising accuracy and F1 score, though performance for some tasks may drop. These findings can improve the design of wearable devices to detect and reduce compensatory movements in patients recovering from upper limb injuries.

title: Synchronous integrated detection system for animal gait and cortical functional network connectivity
authors: Shaopo Wan, Qianqian Wang, Wei Han, Dongyang Huang, Jiaqing Yan, Hui Ji, Yanpeng Ning, Yi Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Deciphering the spatiotemporal patterns of gait behavior and synchronized cortical functional networks is highly significant in neuroscience research. However, current small-animal gait analyzers cannot synchronize gait and cortical functional network connectivity (CFNC), thus limiting our understanding of CFNC dynamics under different gaits. To address this issue, we developed a small-animal gait analyzer that can synchronize gait with whole-brain local field potentials in real time. Using this device, we precisely analyzed the real-time dynamic changes in the CFNC during fine gait movements and mapped the dynamic remodeling of the CFNC during the gait cycle in real time in mouse models of neuropathic pain and ischemic stroke, directly correlating phase-specific network reorganization with motor deficits. Our results demonstrate that the device can monitor the CFNC in real time across different gaits and reveal the specificity of cortical functional networks underlying some motor dysfunctions. This device has the potential to be used to dissect neural representations of motor control and assess treatment efficacy.

title: Design and Simulation of an HDR CMOS Image Sensor Employing Programmable Linear-Logarithmic Counter with Non-Linear Response Pixel
authors: Mirella M. de O. Carneiro, Fernanda D. V. R. Oliveira, Victor R. R. de Oliveira, José Gabriel R. C. Gomes, Milena F. Pinto
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Industrial Electronics Society
abstract: This article presents a high dynamic range (HDR) CMOS image sensor, which is designed using a 0.18 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m technology, resulting from integrating two previously studied HDR sensor architectures. In this image sensor, we use the readout circuit from a previous design about an HDR CMOS image sensor employing programmable linear-logarithmic counter for low-light imaging applications, combined with the pixel, which is slightly modified, from a previous work about a pixel for asynchronous HDR acquisition through adaptive tone mapping. The pixel resembles the design of a 3T pixel, but includes two more transistors (five in total) in order to allow for the use of the average photocurrent information from the entire pixel array. It is thus possible to obtain an image sensor with programmable sensitivity and HDR. The proposed pixel holds (considering a 10 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m ×10 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula> m photodiode) a fill factor of 65%, a pixel pitch of 16.93 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m and a pixel area of 15.12 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m ×10.16 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula> m. By configuring sensor parameters, it is possible to perform tone mapping and to control the image brightness, contrast, detail levels and the number of bits used in the image. As a consequence, the resulting image may include either more or less detail, as defined by application-specific (or user-selected) parameters. The simulated dynamic range of the proposed image sensor is equal to 95.72 dB.

title: Performance Analysis of AI Driven Secure LEACH Protocol for Energy Efficient IoT Network
authors: Anagha K S, Arpita Vinay Patil, Shahil Kumar, Prathiksha Y P, Siddiq Iqbal
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are the communication backbone of contemporary Internet of Things (IoT) applications but are energy inefficient and prone to security attacks. This paper suggests a novel reinforced Secure LEACH protocol that combines Artificial Intelligence (AI), deep learning, and permissioned blockchain to resolve these issues. Intelligent cluster head (CH) selection based on energy and distance is performed using K-Means clustering, whereas Isolation Forest and deep learning models identify malicious node behavior in real time. Tamper-proof data storage and secure communication between authenticated nodes are done through a blockchain ledger. MATLAB simulation outcomes illustrate 30–35% improvement in energy efficiency, enhanced node lifetime, and more than 97% anomaly detection accuracy. The suggested protocol performs better than conventional LEACH regarding security, energy efficiency, and network stability. This makes it extremely appropriate for secure and scalable deployment in safety-critical IoT applications such as smart cities, healthcare systems, and industrial monitoring.

title: Bibliometric Analysis of Machine Learning Knowledge Dynamics From 2012 to 2022
authors: Maarten Voorneveld
publish: 2024 IEEE International Conference on Engineering, Technology, and Innovation (ICE/ITMC)
abstract: This research explores the dynamics of Machine Learning (ML, as a scientific discipline. Using the 'search regime' approach, to trace intellectual structures of ML research, with the primary research question is to uncover the characteristics of the Machine Learning search regime from 2012 to 2022. Web of Science is employed to gather publication data, and numerical analyses are conducted at both micro and macro levels. The study observes a substantial increase in knowledge production since 2012, prompting a detailed investigation of Machine Learning knowledge production from 2018 to 2022. Departments in the field have become more diverse, and knowledge production involves stable international cooperation. The actors in the field, predominantly universities, exhibit stability. Geographically, the field has seen significant development, with concentration in the USA and East Asia, differentiation in disciplines, and a stable fraction of topics. University input dominates the actor landscape in the Machine Learning field.

title: The Impact of Data Harmonization and Boruta Feature Selection on the Generalizability of Autism Screening
authors: Pratik Pandit, Surekha Dholay
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Enabling timely intervention for individuals with Autism Spectrum Disorder is contingent upon accurate and early screening, a task that continues to present significant hurdles. To address this, we developed a robust, generalized machine learning model for ASD screening applicable across diverse age groups. Our method involves harmonizing four public datasets spanning toddlers, children, adolescents, and adults into a single, cohesive dataset. A data pipeline, featuring Winsorization for outlier management was employed. The Boruta algorithm for feature selection was also employed to distill the initial feature set down to the 13 most impactful predictors. We then trained and evaluated a diverse set of eight machine learning classifiers, employing a stratified 10-fold cross-validation protocol for robust assessment. Our evaluation revealed strong performance across the models. Notably, the Gradient Boosting classifier achieved a 98% recall score, indicating its strong ability to correctly identify nearly all actual autism cases, which is crucial in minimizing missed diagnoses. These findings suggest that the proposed methodology, particularly the use of ensemble models in harmonized data, holds significant promise for the development of a highly reliable and universally applicable computational aid for the Autism detection.

title: Quantitative Evaluation of Cultural Heritage Development in Ancient Towns from the Perspective of Tourist Experience
authors: Lei Yu, Yuxiang Ma, Guoqiang Chen

title: Enhancing Hyper-Connectivity in High-Density LoS Channels for Massive MIMO IoT Systems_supp1-3663562.pdf
authors: Byung Moo Lee
abstract: In this paper, we focus on enhancing hyper-connectivity in high-density line-of-sight (LoS) channels for Massive MIMO IoT systems, particularly in scenarios involving unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) or related consumer IoT devices. In LoS environments, the need for complex channel estimation is significantly reduced, as LoS channels are rather deterministic with zero variance. However, supporting massive consumer IoT deployments over LoS channels presents a challenge due to decreased channel resolvability, making outage probability a critical factor. We investigate the connectivity of massive consumer IoT devices in LoS channels and derive the bounds for spectral efficiency (SE) and energy efficiency (EE) under a specified outage probability. Recognizing the widespread use of multicarrier signals, we apply a clipping peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) reduction scheme to further improve EE. Based on these derived metrics, we propose a simple yet effective dropping scheme to increase per-user SE and EE, while reducing overall power consumption. Simulation results validate our theoretical analysis and demonstrate the benefits of the proposed approach.

title: Zero-Shot Fault Diagnosis in Manufacturing Processes via Attribute Co-Occurrence Relationships
authors: Wei Dai, Boyang Zhao, Yun Lin, Qinglin Zheng, Yazhou Li
abstract: In the transition from pilot to mass production, certain fault classes lack available samples, creating a zero-shot condition that challenges the training of fault diagnosis models. Zero-shot learning (ZSL) maps features to shared attribute vectors formed by engineering knowledge information, offering a potential solution. In manufacturing processes, disturbances propagate across operations, causing multiple attributes to co-occur. However, the internal co-occurrence relationships among attributes are often overlooked, which may lead to discrepancies in attribute prediction. To address this issue, we propose a zero shot fault diagnosis method for manufacturing processes that leverages attribute co-occurrence relationships to identify unseen faults. To capture this relationship, mutual information is first employed to quantify attribute co-occurrence and construct an adjacency matrix of attribute relations. In parallel, distributional features are extracted using multi-delay ordinal patterns, and a distance-based loss function is designed to align these features with the attributes. The adjacency matrix and distributional features are then input into a graph convolutional network, with attribute relationships embedded into the distributional features to maintain consistency. Finally, distributional features support zero-shot fault diagnosis via mapping and similarity measures. Experimental validation on the Tennessee Eastman Process (TEP) and fuel rods manufacturing process demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Unsupervised Disentanglement of Brain Heterogeneity for Identifying Subtypes of Alzheimer's Disease_supp1-3663181.pdf
authors: Ran Song
abstract: Neuroanatomical heterogeneity in Alzheimer's disease (AD) hinders precision diagnosis and treatment, as distinct brain phenotypes may correspond to different disease subtypes. However, MRI-based subtype classifications are often confounded by co-occurring pathologies and non-AD factors, such as genetic predisposition and environmental influences, limiting their clinical interpretability. We propose 3D-DisAD, an unsupervised deep learning framework that disentangles AD-specific neuroanatomical variations from unrelated influences and clusters patients into subtypes with homogeneous brain phenotypes. The framework comprises two synergistic networks: (1) Contrastive Disentanglement Network, which separates AD-specific variations from those shared by AD patients and healthy controls; and (2) Transformation Generation Network, which refines these disease-specific variations by transforming healthy brain representations into realistic, pathology-consistent anatomies via diffusion-based generative modeling. Evaluated on four public datasets, 3D-DisAD reveals strong correlations between the disentangled AD-specific variations and diverse clinical and biological profiles, validating their relevance. Using these variations, we identify four AD subtypes with significant differences in biomarkers, cognitive trajectories, and genetic signatures, and uncover distinct longitudinal progression patterns that suggest potential windows for early intervention. By disentangling AD-specific variations, our method enables more precise patient stratification and personalized treatments, particularly in the early stage of AD. Code is available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/cnuzh/3D-DisAD</uri>.

title: Integrating YOLOv10 and Dual-Head Inference for Automated Diabetic Retinopathy Diagnosis
authors: Syed Md. Azizul Alam, Abdullah Al Noman, Abdullah Al Afiq, Hasan Jaki, Md. Humayun Kabir, Md Jiabul Hoque
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) remains a leading cause of vision impairment globally, necessitating early and accurate detection for effective intervention. This study presents an advanced automated DR diagnosis framework by integrating YOLOv10, a state-of-the-art object detection model, with a dual-head inference architecture. The proposed system utilizes a custom-annotated retinal fundus image dataset, prepared and augmented using Roboflow, to enhance lesion localization and classification performance. The dual-head design enables simultaneous detection of DR-related lesions and grading of disease severity, supporting both binary and multiclass classification tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that the YOLOv10-based model achieves outstanding accuracy, with a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 0.984 across all classes, and maintains high precision and recall for both DR and No-DR categories. These findings highlight the robustness, reliability, and clinical applicability of the proposed approach, offering a scalable solution for real-time, automated diabetic retinopathy screening and diagnosis.

title: Modified C-ED sensing under Maximum Select Logic with improved detection accuracy using Whale Optimization Algorithm in CR-WSN
authors: G D Vignesh, G Saravanan, A M Balamurugan, T Sudharsan
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Cognitive Radio–Wireless Sensor Networks (CR-WSNs) seeks to address the spectrum scarcity problems of traditional Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) by opportunistically utilizing under-utilized licensed frequency bands. By identifying and exploiting these spectrum holes in an effective manner, CR-WSNs enhance overall network performance under dynamic channel conditions. Nonetheless, achieving energy efficiency continues to remain a critical challenge due to the constrained battery resources of sensor nodes. This research work proposes a collaborative sensing approach based on Energy Detection (ED), employing Max Selection logic enhanced with nature inspired meta heuristic Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA). The integration of WOA into the Max Selection scheme significantly improves detection probability compared to simple collaborative ED without WOA, while effectively minimizing the probability of false alarms by dynamically adjusting the threshold under intense channel variations. However, this enhancement of detection accuracy is achieved at the cost of increased average energy consumption due to computational overhead at the SU-CH nodes when compared to the simple collaborative ED approach without WOA.

title: AI-Driven Classification of Tsunami-Generating Earthquakes: Harnessing Random Forest, SVM, and Logistic Regression for Early Detection
authors: Imen Ziadi, Nejla Essaddi, Mongi Besbes
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Predicting whether an earthquake will generate a tsunami is critical for early warning systems and disaster mitigation. In this study, we present an AI-driven approach to classify earthquakes as tsunami-generating or non-tsunami events. We utilize three machine learning models-Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Logistic Regression (LR)-trained on USGS earthquake data from 2015 to 2025, considering features such as magnitude, depth, latitude, and longitude. Our exploratory data analysis highlights key correlations and feature distributions, while model evaluation demonstrates high classification performance, with Random Forest achieving up to 91% accuracy. We further investigate feature importance and provide ROC curves and confusion matrices for comparative analysis. The results show that AI-driven classification can effectively support early warning systems, offering a scalable and data-informed tool for seismic hazard assessment.

title: A Generic and Robust Agent to Accomplish Quantum Control Tasks
authors: Shihui Zhang, Zibo Miao, Qixiang Zhang, Xin Yi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: In view of inevitable uncertainties or disturbances in quantum systems, efficient and systematic numerical methods for robust control design are of significant importance. Although reinforcement learning has recently been applied in the field of quantum control, completing quantum control tasks in practice is always challenging due to the lack of data from real-world environments. In this paper, a generic and robust agent can be constructed catering to system imperfections by applying a curriculum meta-reinforcement learning quantum control (CMQC) approach. The proposed approach incorporates an objective module that can break down a complex quantum control objective into multiple sub-objectives using an exponential decay strategy, enabling the agent to gradually learn to solve the final goal through experience transfer and knowledge consolidation. Furthermore, a meta module is employed to achieve adaptation to environmental imperfections. This allows the agent to adjust its strategy in real time, obtaining strong robustness even in the presence of uncertainties. The superiority of CMQC is demonstrated in robust control problems regarding state transfer in a two-level quantum system and realization of the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R_{x}(\pi)$</tex-math></inline-formula> gate, with reduced computational cost and improved accuracy achieved. Our results pave the way towards more reliable and secure quantum information processing with uncertainties involved.

title: Scalable Cooperative Maneuvering Using Conflict Analysis: Merging in Mixed Traffic
authors: Hao M. Wang, Sergei S. Avedisov, Onur Altintas, Gábor Orosz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: This paper discusses scalable cooperative maneuvering using conflict analysis, where conflicts are managed between multiple vehicles in mixed-autonomy environments. Two different classes of cooperation, enabled by vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication, are considered–status sharing and intent sharing. In status sharing, connected vehicles share their current states, such as position and velocity, while in intent sharing the information about vehicles’ future trajectories is exchanged. The scalability of conflict analysis is studied through traffic scenarios where an ego vehicle interacts with multiple remote vehicles in a safety- and time-critical manner. We show that pairwise conflict analysis, which decomposes a large-scale conflict management problem to multiple sequentially solvable smaller-scale problems, is a key component of scalability. Conflict analysis, while being scaled up to more complex traffic scenarios, preserves the efficient consideration of different types of V2X information, time delay effects, and flexible control design. These results are demonstrated using simulations with real traffic data.

title: An Improved Lightweight YOLO Model for Real-Time Blueberry Growth Stage Detection to Facilitate Smart Irrigation
authors: Yi Luo, Junxi Bian, Yun Yu
abstract: Intelligent irrigation, guided by real-time crop water status, is paramount for sustainable, high-yield agriculture. For high-value crops like blueberries, accurately detecting phenological growth stages is a key prerequisite for precise water management. However, vision-based monitoring in field environments is challenging due to dynamic lighting, severe occlusion from foliage, and subtle inter-stage variations. To address these issues, this paper proposes a lightweight and efficient object detection model, YOLO-Berry, for real-time blueberry growth stage identification. We first established a comprehensive field-image dataset of blueberries, encompassing multiple growth stages under diverse environmental conditions. The proposed model enhances the YOLO11n baseline by incorporating a Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network (BiFPN) for superior multi-scale feature fusion, a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) to focus on critical regions, and lightweight Ghost and C3Ghost structures to minimize computational costs. Experimental results show that YOLO-Berry achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 96.7%, a 6.5 percentage point increase over the baseline. Moreover, it demonstrates a 2.7% gain in Precision, a 5.2% rise in Recall, and a 17.9% reduction in parameters, while maintaining a rapid inference speed of 15 ms per frame. The detection outputs are used to generate growth stage statistics, which inform an automated irrigation decision-making system and facilitate early yield prediction. This work provides a robust technological foundation for vision-guided precision irrigation in modern smart agriculture.

title: A Model-Based Systems Engineering Framework for Technology Roadmaps (MBSE-TRM): Application to Electronic Warfare Systems_supp1-3663309.docx
authors: Reeshen Reddy

title: ParallelGSE: Efficient and Secure Shortest Path Search on Encrypted Graphs
authors: Qing Fan, Weixiao Wang, Chuan Zhang, Zhitao Guan, Yong Xie, Ming Lu, Liehuang Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Graph searchable encryption (GSE) for shortest path queries allows users to discover the closest connection between two individuals in encrypted social network graphs, while safeguarding both social data security and the privacy of user queries. The static GSE is commonly favored for its higher efficiency, while it faces significant challenges in resisting query recovery attacks. In contrast, the dynamic GSE is better suited for changing real world, but enhancing its search efficiency remains a formidable challenge. In this paper, we propose the ParallelGSE, a flexible graph partitioning and parallel search framework that supports both dynamic and static encrypted social graphs, striking a balance between query privacy and practical implementation. ParallelGSE partitions the original graph into multiple subgraphs, distributes them across several semi-honest servers, and performs searches in parallel without relying on a trusted server. Formal security reduction demonstrates ParallelGSE is resistant to isomorphic query recovery attacks. Simulated experiments on seven real-world graph datasets validate rationality of graph partition and improvements of storage, computation and communication costs. Especially, compared to the state-of-the-art (SOTA) static PathGES (ACM CCS 2024) and dynamic GraphShield (IEEE TKDE 2022), the search efficiency of static ParallelGSE can be 3 orders of magnitude faster than that of PathGSE, while dynamic ParallelGSE achieves over <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$20\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> greater search efficiency than GraphShield.

title: Tightly-Secure Simulation-Sound Quasi-Adaptive NIZK Arguments
authors: Mojtaba Khalili
abstract: Quasi-adaptive non-interactive zero-knowledge (QA-NIZK) arguments are fundamental cryptographic primitives widely used in privacy-preserving technologies such as anonymous credentials, group signatures, e-cash, and blockchain-based applications. We present the first tightly secure unbounded simulation sound quasi-adaptive non-interactive zero-knowledge argument system from simple assumptions. The construction has a security loss of O(1), a compact common reference string, constant size proofs, and its security relies on the hardness of the well-known SXDH assumption. Our result improves state-of-the-art (Couteau and Hartmann, CRYPTO 2020) in terms of the proof size (about three times), a lower security loss, and also with respect to the underlying hardness assumptions. The tight security reduction enables shorter key-length recommendations, leading to improved concrete efficiency. Our main technical contribution is a novel proof technique inspired by the randomization technique of the Naor-Yung double-encryption paradigm and the adaptive partitioning due to Hofheinz (EUROCRYPT 2017).

title: Fast Convergent Inertial Proximal Gradient Algorithms with Dry Friction for Nonconvex Optimization
authors: Zhiran Ma, Jiahong Guo
abstract: This paper investigates fast first-order methods for nonconvex optimization, inspired by a second-order dynamical system with vanishing damping and strongly convex dry friction. By discretizing this system, we propose a strongly convex dry-friction inertial proximal gradient algorithm, and establish its convergence and geometric convergence rate, under mild conditions. Moreover, an accelerated variant, which combines Nesterov’s accelerated gradient method with the strongly convex dry-friction inertial proximal gradient method, is introduced. Numerical experiments are reported to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithms.

title: Surrogate-assisted Level Set Topology Optimization for Pixelated Antenna Design
authors: Haohua Chen, Kai Fu, Xiwen Cai, Lei Yue, Liang Gao
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a novel antenna topology optimization framework consisting of two components. First, a Kriging-based level set method (KLSM) is employed to formulate the antenna optimization problem. The KLSM defines level set values at a small number of knot points and uses Kriging interpolation to generate the geometric representation of the antenna topology. The KLSM reduces the design space dimensionality and transforms the complex discrete pixelated antenna optimization problem into a low-dimensional continuous optimization problem via variable substitution. Second, a dual surrogate-assisted genetic algorithm (DSGA) is proposed to solve the KLSM-based antenna optimization problem. The DSGA employs two surrogate-assisted strategies to improve the traditional genetic algorithm. A local search is performed around the current best individual by using the radial basis function (RBF). A global search is performed by using expected improvement of high-dimensional Kriging to prescreen potential candidate individuals. The proposed surrogate-assisted topology optimization method is validated through the design of a pixelated monopole antenna. Results show that the proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in both convergence speed and optimization accuracy.

title: Alzheimer’s Disease Classification Using Pre-Training and Optimization Techniques
authors: Subra Harshita Nistala, Sofia Francis
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a neurodegenerative disease characterized by progressive memory loss and cognitive symptoms. This paper offers a hybrid classification model based on deep learning and optimization algorithm to improve the AD diagnosis of MRI images. Features are extracted from pre-trained CNN, ResNet18 and VGG19, then shaped by the metaheuristic algorithms such as WOA, Improved WOA, PSO, ACO, and IMPA. The selected features are then classified using state of art variants of KNN, i.e. Fuzzy KNN (FKNN), Weighted KNN (WKNN) and Dual Weighted KNN (DWKNN). The proposed method outperformed existing models in multi-class Alzheimer’s classification, achieving 92.27% accuracy.. The effectiveness of our approach is demonstrated by evaluation metrics including accuracy, F1-score, precision, recall and ROC-AUC-score. The study results indicate that the developed method is applicable for use in low-resource and clinical settings.

title: Designing Abstractive Summarization Systems based on Transformer for Urdu
authors: Masum Ahmed Mozumder, Tanvinur Rahman Siam, Md. Robiul Islam Rabby, Chowdhury Mohammad Mehedi, Fariha Tasnim, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Abstractive summarization of low-resource languages such as Urdu remains underdeveloped despite the large number of speakers worldwide. The existing methods are largely extractive based, which can lead to less fluent and coherent summary. We trained and evaluated Transformer-based abstractive summarization models for Urdu by fine-tuning mT5-small and mBART-50 on a curated dataset of 15,000 article-summary pairs in this work. Our experiments demonstrate that fine-tuning substantially enhances the summarization performance measured by ROUGE. mBART in particular reported that 55.35% ROUGE-1 and 51.44% ROUGE-LSum, which outperformed mT5. These findings show the benefits of multilingual pretrained models for LRLs, when suitably adopted. This contribution benefits NLP inclusion and provide real-world utility in terms of education, journalism, governance in Urdu-speaking areas.

title: SeANet: Sentiment Analysis in Social Media Comments on Bengali Mega Project using Transformer Learning
authors: Fahim Shakil Tamim, Jumael Ahmed Khan, Farshid Hossain Siyam, Md. Samiul Alom, Mohammed Moshiul Hoque
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Understanding public sentiment toward mega infrastructure projects is essential for fostering informed decision-making and meaningful community engagement, particularly in linguistically underrepresented regions. This paper introduces SeANet, a transformer-based approach to classify sentiment from Bengali text data related to public comments on megaprojects. This study also presents a benchmark dataset (SeAMeD) for downstream tasks, comprising 5,724 Bengali social media comments, annotated into three categories: positive, negative, and neutral. Extensive experiments were conducted employing machine learning models (LR, SVM, RF, and MNB), deep learning models (LSTM+RNN, LSTM+BiLSTM), and fine-tuned transformer models (B-BERT, dBERT, mBERT, and XLM-R). The experimental outcomes demonstrate that XLM-R surpassed the exploited models, attaining the highest F1-score of 0,68.

title: Performance Analysis of FSO Links with Log- Normal Pointing Errors for 5G/6G Backhaul Applications
authors: Paramjeet Singh, Dushyant Singh Chauhan
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Free Space Optical (FSO) communication offers high data rates and secure wireless transmission, making it a viable solution for next-generation communication systems. However, its performance is highly sensitive to atmospheric turbulence and misalignment between transmitter and receiver, commonly referred to as pointing errors. This study investigates the influence of log-normal distributed turbulence and Gaussian-distributed pointing jitter on the outage probability and bit error rate (BER) of FSO systems. Simulation results reveal that both factors significantly degrade system performance, with turbulence affecting reliability more consistently, and pointing errors becoming more critical under weak turbulence. A 3D surface analysis further highlights the combined impact of these impairments. Additionally, the study compares the performance of BPSK, 16-QAM, and 64-QAM modulation schemes under varying alignment conditions. The results demonstrate that while higher-order modulations perform better under ideal conditions, they are more susceptible to pointing errors. These findings are valuable for designing robust FSO links in practical scenarios, including potential applications in 5G and 6G communication networks.

title: Multimodal Machine Learning Models for Intelligent Interpretation of Text, Image and Audio Inputs
authors: R.Swathiramya, V.V.Karthikeyan, P.Sumathi, Sruthy K V, Afreen Hussain, R.Senthilkumar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Multimodal learning architectures have emerged as a critical paradigm in artificial intelligence (AI), enabling systems to process and integrate heterogeneous data modalities such as text, vision, audio, and sensor inputs for comprehensive understanding and decision-making. Traditional unimodal models often fail to capture the rich, complementary information available across different modalities, leading to suboptimal performance in complex real-world tasks. This paper explores the design and development of multimodal learning architectures that effectively fuse features from diverse modalities through early, late, and hybrid fusion strategies, along with attention-based mechanisms and crossmodal transformers. Recent advancements, including contrastive learning and large-scale pretraining on multimodal datasets, have significantly improved the performance of applications such as visual question answering, speech recognition, sentiment analysis, and autonomous systems. The proposed framework emphasizes modality alignment, feature representation learning, and robust fusion techniques to address challenges such as data heterogeneity, modality imbalance, and missing modalities. Moreover, the role of selfsupervised learning and generative models, including multimodal large language models (MLLMs), is discussed in enhancing cross-modal understanding and zero-shot generalization. We highlight benchmark datasets, evaluation metrics, and case studies that demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed approaches. Finally, open challenges such as interpretability, computational efficiency, and ethical considerations are outlined, with future research directions aimed at building scalable, generalizable, and human-centric multimodal systems.

title: A Robust Intrusion Detection System Using Enhanced Ensemble Learning Techniques
authors: Sreeramula Nikhitha, Thangella Sarayu, M. Gundu Pranathi, D Sandhya Rani, M. A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: These days, it is necessary to make computer networks secure from attackers and intruders. Traditional intrusion detection systems depend on signature-based detection which is unable to identify new abnormalities or threats. This paper proposes an intrusion detection system using a combination of Hidden Naive Bayes(HNB), Averaged One-Dependence Estimator(AODE), and Bayesian Network (BN)models. To enhance detection accuracy, we combine the models using majority voting.. We trained and tested these models on the CIC-IDS 2018 dataset and used label encoding for processing text data.The results show that Hidden Naive Bayes (HNB) achieved an accuracy of 92.5%, while both AODE and Bayesian Networks (BN) reached 95%. The final ensemble model also achieved 95% accuracy, highlighting the system’s effectiveness, precision, and ability to detect threats with fewer false alarms.

title: Octa-Band Microstrip Antenna Covering L to Ku Bands for Next-Generation Wireless, Satellite, and Radar Applications
authors: Abdul Jabber Antor
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Microstrip patch antennas have gathered significant attention due to their low profile, lightweight structure, ease of fabrication, and planar configuration, making them highly suitable for integration into modern wireless devices. However, achieving multi-band characteristics within a single antenna structure presents challenges in terms of impedance matching, radiation efficiency, and design complexity. In this work, we propose a novel octa-band microstrip copper patch antenna designed using CST Studio Suite, which is configured on a 1.5mm thick FR-4 substrate and occupies a compact area of 50×70 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The antenna operates efficiently at eight resonant frequencies: 1.95 GHz, 2.78 GHz, 3.78 GHz, 6.17 GHz, 8.03 GHz, 10.79 GHz, 13.21 GHz, and 14.45 GHz. These frequencies cover a broad spectrum from the L-band to the Ku-band, enabling the antenna to support diverse applications such as GSM communication (1.8–2.0 GHz), WiMAX and S-band systems (2.5–3.7 GHz), C-band radar and satellite links (4–8 GHz), X-band surveillance and tracking systems (8–12 GHz), and Ku-band satellite communication (12–18 GHz). The simulated S-parameter (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>) results demonstrate strong impedance matching across all bands, with reflection coefficients far below –10 dB at each resonant frequency. The proposed antenna offers a promising solution for compact, wide-coverage wireless systems requiring stable performance over multiple frequency bands.

title: Advanced Predictive Maintenance for Autonomous Offshore Wind Farms Using Random Forest Algorithms
authors: Dr.V.Sujatha, Dr.S.Vimaladevi, V. R. Niveditha, Chandrakala D, R. Selvamanikandan, S Murugan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Offshore wind farms are essential for sustainable energy production but have maintenance difficulties owing to severe environmental conditions and restricted accessibility. This paper presents an innovative predictive maintenance framework using Random Forest algorithms to improve the operational dependability of autonomous offshore wind turbines. IoT-enabled sensors constantly gather data on essential factors like vibration, temperature, rotor speed, and acoustic signals from vital turbine components. The dataset, consisting of more than 1.2 million sensor readings from 150 turbines during a 12-month period, was used to train and verify the model. The Random Forest method achieved a fault classification accuracy of 96.3%, with a precision of 94.8% and a recall of 95.5%. The proposed solution decreased unscheduled downtime by 41% and prolonged component lifespan by 22% compared to conventional scheduled maintenance. Moreover, operating expenses decreased by 18%, mostly due to early fault identification and focused treatments. The findings illustrate the effectiveness of machine learning-based maintenance in offshore settings. The system optimizes decision-making, facilitates autonomous maintenance operations, and boosts energy production and asset durability in renewable energy systems.

title: Satellite-Based Air Quality Assessment over HSIA, Dhaka:Using Statistical and Deep Learning Models
authors: Anup Kumar Modak, S.M. Sadakatul Bari, Muhammed Kamrul Islam, Md Sakir Hossain, Md Afzal Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This study evaluates the air quality at Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport (HSIA), Dhaka, from 2019 to 2024 using Sentinel-5P satellite data and advanced forecasting models. Concentrations of NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, SO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, CO, and O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> were analyzed for spatiotemporal patterns using Google Earth Engine (GEE). Results show that NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and SO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> levels peak during winter, with significant reductions observed during the 2020 COVID-19 lockdown and rebounds in subsequent years. Time series decomposition reveals strong seasonal cycles for all pollutants. Forecasting was performed using Seasonal ARIMA and Stacked LSTM models for 2025–2029. SARIMA yielded better performance for NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and CO due to their linear-seasonal nature, while LSTM outperformed for O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>, capturing nonlinear patterns. While NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and SO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> are projected to decline post-2024, CO and O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> are expected to remain stable with minor fluctuations. These findings highlight HSIA as a persistent pollution hotspot and support the use of satellite-based remote sensing and deep learning approaches for scalable, cost-effective air quality monitoring and management in urban airports and densely populated urban areas.

title: Automated High Fidelity Urban Zone Classification using Text Data Clustering
authors: Akhil Vaidya, Omkar Surve, Darsh Tulsiyan, Rupali Sawant
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This study presents an automated framework for generating high-fidelity urban zone classifications through text-based clustering of OpenStreetMap data, addressing the scalability and consistency challenges of traditional manual mapping approaches. Our methodology leverages Natural Language Processing techniques to classify urban land use from textual descriptions of geographic features. The framework systematically segments target regions into 1km<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> spatial tiles, extracts comprehensive text data including building names and points of interest, applies advanced text embedding techniques, and employs K-means clustering to identify distinct functional zones. We evaluated three embedding approaches: TF-IDF, Sentence Transformers, and Universal Sentence Encoder (USE), with clustering optimization determined through Elbow and Silhouette methods. Implementation on a 2,500 km<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> region in Mumbai’s Metropolitan area demonstrates the framework’s effectiveness, with USE-based embeddings achieving exceptional performance metrics of 97.056% accuracy and 97.049% F1-score when validated against manually labeled ground truth data. The automated classification successfully distinguishes between residential, commercial, industrial, and natural zones while significantly reducing manual effort and maintaining temporal consistency. This framework provides a scalable, cost-effective solution for large-scale urban planning applications, offering reliable zone classifications that can adapt to rapidly evolving urban landscapes and support data-driven urban development strategies.

title: Enhanced Digital Payment Security Using Predictive Analytics and AI-Powered Anomaly Detection Techniques
authors: Keerthna G, C Jayamala, Ashwini Jotiram Patil, P. Sundharesalingam, Priyanga S, Mohit Tiwari
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The digital payment system in India keeps expanding rapidly because of developments made in the Unified Payments Interface (UPI) and mobile wallets together with AI-based fraud detection technology. Distributed adoption remains limited primarily due to existing security hazards, transaction breakdowns, and rising cyber threats, particularly throughout rural locations. The proposed framework builds a digital payment framework with security enhancement and better fraud recognition capabilities so speed of transactions can be accelerated using machine learning technology. However, the model combines three elements of anomaly detection with predictive analytics and deep learning that reduce fraudulent transactions and build improved transaction success rates. Test results show that the created fraud detection method reaches 98.2% accuracy which decreases false alarms by 28%. The transaction optimization model shortens processing time from 320 ms down to 180 ms which leads to significant network congestion cuts. The evaluation with rival systems indicates that adoption has risen by 17 %. AI payment solutions demonstrate their ability to create a protected digital payment system that will be available to all.

title: Edge AI-Driven Battery Management for Electric Vehicles: Models, Hardware, and Future Directions
authors: Sanjana Jayavant Zangaruche, Rutuja Deepak Pakhare, Saptami Madan Gadekar, Sakshi Dinesh Sangelia, Shrusti Basavaraj Allagi, Prakash K Sonwalkar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The integration of Edge Artificial Intelligence (Edge AI) into electric vehicles (EVs) represents a significant advancement in intelligent battery management systems (BMS). Traditional BMS approaches, while foundational, are increasingly inadequate in addressing the demands of real-time processing, complex degradation patterns, and the evolving requirements of modern EV architectures. This paper explores how Edge AI overcomes these limitations through low-latency data processing, high-accuracy predictions enabled by advanced machine learning models, and improved scalability and security. We examine the performance of models such as Support Vector Machines (SVM), Long Short-Term Memory networks (LSTM), and hybrid architectures in estimating key battery parameters like State of Charge (SOC) and State of Health (SOH). Further, we assess the role of emerging edge hardware technologies, including low-power AI chipsets and real-time operating systems, in enabling efficient deployment of intelligent BMS. Techniques such as sensor fusion and Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communication are also analyzed for their potential to enhance EV connectivity and responsiveness. The paper concludes by outlining critical challenges and future directions in developing lightweight AI models, secure data protocols, and sustainable battery lifecycle solutions, which are essential for the widespread adoption of safe, efficient, and intelligent electric vehicles.

title: AI-Based Wearable Systems for Detecting Cardiovascular Anomalies
authors: Suhas R. Mule, Monali Gulhane, Diksha Gabhane, Nitin Rakesh, Rahul M., Pawan Kumar Verma
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Cardiovascular illnesses (CVDs) are still the top cause of death in the world. This highlights the importance of identifying cardiovascular health problems early and monitoring them closely. Traditional ways of finding heart problems often use fixed and invasive methods, which might not be possible for regular checks, especially for people with a high risk. This study suggests an intelligent AI system that can find problems with the heart in real time, offering a noninvasive and effective way to start early evaluation and treatment. The system uses high-tech devices to gather bodily data like blood pressure, heart rate, and an EKG (ECG). It then uses machine learning techniques to look at this data for signs of possible problems, such as seizures, ischemia, and other issues. It uses both controlled and unsupervised learning methods to make sure that the AI model correctly sorts and predicts circulatory events. The portable system constantly checks the wearer's cardiovascular health and sends real-time alerts so that medical help can be given right away if needed. This proactive method not only improves patient health outcomes but also personalizes healthcare by providing information on each person's health trends based on data. The system can work continuously and quietly, making it suitable for both professional and everyday use. It gives patients and healthcare workers an easy-to-use tool for controlling cardiovascular risk. With its unique approach to early diagnosis and avoidance of cardiovascular diseases, the suggested AI-based innovative system is a big step towards better cardiovascular health management.

title: Molecular Docking and Dynamics of Neem (Azadirachta indica) Phytochemicals for Binding Affinity and Stability Evaluation
authors: Siam Ahmed
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Azadirachta indica (Neem) is a well-established medicinal plant in traditional and Ayurvedic systems, recognized for its broad-spectrum pharmacological activities, including antibacterial, antiparasitic, and anticancer effects. These therapeutic properties are primarily attributed to its structurally diverse phytochemicals, notably terpenoids, flavonoids, and limonoids. In this study, ten bioactive compounds Nimbiol, 6-Deacetylnimbin, Kulinone, Azadirachtin, Nimbin, Salannin, Gedunin, Quercetin, Margolonone, and Azadiradione were selected from the IMPPAT database for in silico evaluation as potential ligands in drug discovery. The selection criteria included reported bioactivity, structural diversity, and the availability of three-dimensional (3D) conformers for molecular modeling. Compounds such as Azadirachtin, Gedunin, and Nimbin have been previously documented for their antiparasitic and anticancer properties, while Quercetin is well known for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity. The inclusion of complex limonoids and tetranortriterpenoids in the ligand set enhances chemical diversity and increases the potential for selective and high affinity binding to disease-associated protein targets.This study uses docking molecules methods to look at how well Neem derived phytochemicals may bind to and interact with important protein targets. The results give us more information about how drug-like and useful these natural compounds are for treating diseases, and also show how useful they are in computer-aided drug development processes that look for drugs that can work on multiple diseases.

title: Clinically Interpretable Bone Fracture Detection Using EfficientNet-B0 and XAI: A Deep Learning Approach for Radiographic Image Analysis
authors: Md. Abu Johab, Al Arian Ahmad, MD.Tahidul Islam, Md. Roton Ahmed, Nakib Aman
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The severity and complexity of bone fracture should be diagnosed quickly and correctly to facilitate the process of making clinical decisions and treating a patient in a timely manner. This is essential to be diagnosed early and treated early. This possibility has emerged due to advances that have been made in the field of deep learning in the recent past which hint at the possibility of automating this detection process. This study introduces a DL model which relies on EfficientNet-B0 and entirely incorporated with Explainable AI (XAI) methods, on bone X-ray images as an example of fracture identification. The model that was developed reported a high level of accuracy of 99.93% which outstripped numerous currently developed models. The results were compared with standard metrics and Grad-CAM visualization of the results indicates important regions of bones that determine the model predictions. It is a sound and simple model that is able to encourage usage in clinical settings and result in the accurate diagnosis and further informed treatment decisions due to the shortened delays. Future work will focus on validating across multiple datasets, extending to multiclass classification including fracture type and location, and involving expert validation of explainability results.

title: PSO-Enhanced Wildfire Risk Forecasting with Decentralized Multi-Robot Navigation via Voronol Path Planning
authors: Naren N S, Shweta Loonkar, Karishma Desai
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Natural disasters are rising in intensity and frequency, ultimately overwhelming traditional response systems. An emerging solution in swarm intelligence – inspired by nature – is gaining traction and is showing promise in solving disaster management problems efficiently. This paper integrates Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) tuned XGBoost for predicting fire risk zones based on environmental attributes, along with Voronoi-based path planning for dynamic wildfire navigation and obstacle/fire-hazard avoidance. A* vs Voronoi based path-planning methods are compared based on path planning and step count. The model - which is trained on an agglomerated dataset – is evaluated with log-loss, F1 score and feature importance. Ultimately, a proof of concept adaptable to real-world wildfire scenarios is presented, with edge deployment as a major future scope.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Hybrid Deep Learning Architectures for Detecting Schizophrenia from EEG Signals
authors: Abu Omayed, MD. Asif Iqbal, Muhammad Alamgir Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Schizophrenia (SZ) is a mental health condition for which objective, EEG-based detection could greatly aid early diagnosis and treatment. This study proposes a novel triple-hybrid deep learning model that combines a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) into a unified architecture for automated schizophrenia detection from electroencephalogram (EEG) signals. The CNN-LSTM-GRU hybrid model is designed to utilize complementary features of CNN (spatial feature extraction) and LSTM/GRU (temporal pattern learning), representing a technical advancement in EEG-based schizophrenia classification. We evaluate the proposed model on EEG data from 81 subjects (49 patients, 32 controls) and conduct a comparative analysis against multiple architectures, including a standalone CNN, a standalone GRU, dual-hybrid combinations (CNN-GRU and CNN-LSTM), and a CNN with a Support Vector Machine classifier (CNN-SVM). The results demonstrate that our triple-hybrid model achieves the highest accuracy of 97.04%, outperforming the CNN-GRU hybrid (96.26% accuracy), the GRU-based model (96.62%), and all other comparative models. This superior performance highlights the novelty and effectiveness of the CNN-LSTM-GRU approach in capturing complex EEG biomarkers of schizophrenia. These findings spotlight the potential of such hybrid deep neural network architectures to improve state-of-the-art schizophrenia detection and pave the way for more automated, reliable mental health diagnostics.

title: Understanding User Adoption of Cybersecurity Practices through Awareness and Perception for Enhancing Network Security in Bangladesh
authors: Md Mehedi Hasan, Kh. Mustafizur Rahman, Avishek Nath, Md. Nafis Fuad, S M Inthakab Kabir, Afsana Ferdous Emee
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This study was conducted to explore the factors that influence the adoption of cybersecurity practices among internet users in Bangladesh. It focused on important variables such as PT, SE, SI, PEOU, PU, ATSP, and CSA. Using the AIDUA framework and analyzing survey data collected from 194 respondents through PLS-SEM, both direct and indirect effects of these factors on users’ security behavior were examined. The findings indicated that CSA plays a central mediating role, especially by strengthening the influence of PU and attitude towards security practices on adoption. Although PT and SE showed significant direct impacts, their mediation through awareness was found to be limited, highlighting the complex nature of adoption processes. Practically, the study points to the importance of focused awareness campaigns, training sessions, and improvements in usability to better encourage cybersecurity adoption. From a social perspective, fostering a culture of security awareness may help in creating safer online environments and reducing cyber risks, particularly in developing countries like Bangladesh. The originality of this research lies in its comprehensive approach, integrating psychological, social, and technological factors within the AIDUA framework, applied specifically in a developing country context where localized studies have been lacking. Some limitations include the use of convenience sampling and a relatively small sample concentrated in urban areas, which might affect how generalizable the results are. Future research could benefit from longitudinal studies, including rural populations, and exploring additional cultural or organizational factors to provide deeper insights.

title: SVM-Based Predictive Model for Managing Coastal Aquifer Salinity Intrusion Risks and Forecasting
authors: A. Latha, R. Kamaraj, Potturi Sandhya, Babu Vinothkumar, T. R. GaneshBabu, S Murugan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Salinity intrusion in coastal aquifers significantly endanger freshwater supplies, especially in low-lying regions susceptible to increasing sea levels and heightened groundwater exploitation. This work introduces a prediction model using Support Vector Machine (SVM) technology to evaluate and mitigate salt intrusion hazards in coastal aquifers. Using hydrological data, historical salinity measurements, and environmental factors, the SVM model efficiently elucidates the intricate interactions affecting salinity dynamics. The model was trained and evaluated using datasets from diverse coastal locations, exhibiting good accuracy in forecasting salt levels across varied situations. The results demonstrate that the SVM model is an essential instrument for decision-makers in sustainable water resource management, allowing proactive methods to alleviate the effects of salt intrusion. Moreover, the model provides insights into appropriate groundwater extraction methods and identifies regions susceptible to salinization. This methodology highlights the significance of advanced machine learning methods in environmental management and provides a foundation for future study in coastal aquifer investigations.

title: Revolutionizing Health Monitoring: Machine Learning for Risk Factor Analysis
authors: Palaparthi Meghana, Y. Sangeetha
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Cardiac Arrest persevere towards persist the common health issue worldwide, exacerbated by the absence of real-time monitoring and diagnostic delays. Traditional medical practices tend to rely on sporadic check-ups and a narrow set of clinical parameters, which may hamper early identification of prospective risks. This paper describes a machine learning support gain for proactive CVD risk estimation, incorporating demographic information, clinical measures, lifestyle parameters, and real-time health data. The dataset of 334 patient records from live databases and Apollo Hospital’s Electronic Health Records (EHRs) contains 18 attributes like BMI, blood pressure, cholesterol, ECG findings, and glucose. Preprocessing included outlier elimination, feature scaling, and class balancing utilizing the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE). Two prediction models Decision Tree (DT) and Support Vector Machine (SVM) were created also proved based on accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC. The DT model produced 95% efficiency with balanced precision-recall measurements and high interpretability through feature importance analysis, whereas the SVM produced 91% accuracy, showing robust performance. which can analyze patient risk levels and give individualized recommendations, facilitating timely intervention and facilitating personalized care. The method integrates interpretable machine learning with real-time data to enhance decision-making in a timely manner in cardiovascular treatment.

title: Predictive Analytics using Geolocation in Logistics: A Review
authors: Sumit Sen, Lokendra Chauhan
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Geolocation-based predictive analytics is revolutionizing logistics by merging real-time spatial intelligence with advanced forecasting models to enable smarter, faster, and more adaptive decision-making. This review synthesizes key technologies—such as GPS, RFID, UAVs, and IoT—that underpin this transformation, and explores how they integrate with machine learning, edge computing, blockchain, and big data platforms. We examine predictive modeling techniques applied to demand forecasting, dynamic routing, fleet maintenance, and anomaly detection. Sector-specific applications in commercial freight, cold chain logistics, and military supply chains are highlighted to demonstrate practical value. Emerging paradigms, including digital twins, federated learning, and explainable AI, are discussed as critical enablers for secure, scalable, and privacy-aware logistics infrastructures. The paper concludes by identifying persistent challenges such as data quality, system interoperability, and model interpretability—offering a roadmap for future research and implementation. This comprehensive review underscores the need for integrated, resilient, and data-driven logistics systems capable of operating at scale in dynamic environments.

title: Self-Supervised Learning for Tomato Leaf Disease Classification: A Comparative Study of SimCLR, MoCo, and BYOL
authors: Md. Atik Ishrak, Tanjim Mahmud, Abubokor Hanip, Mohammad Shahadat Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Tomato leaf diseases pose a significant threat to global agriculture by reducing crop yield and quality, thereby endangering food security and economic stability. Timely and accurate disease detection is essential for effective crop management and mitigation. Traditional supervised learning approaches require large, annotated datasets, which are often costly and labor-intensive to obtain in agricultural settings. Self-supervised learning (SSL) has emerged as a promising alternative, enabling models to learn robust feature representations from unlabeled data through proxy tasks. According to our results on the PlantVillage dataset, SimCLR combined with a ResNet-18 backbone and Logistic Regression classifier achieved the highest classification accuracy of 92%, outperforming MoCo and BYOL variants. We provide an in-depth analysis of the strengths and limitations of each SSL framework, discuss practical considerations for their deployment in agricultural image analysis, and outline future research directions to further advance SSL applications in plant pathology.

title: Finalyze: A RAG-Based Framework for Intelligent Financial Document Analysis
authors: Shlok Mehta, Tisha Negandhi, Sunil Ghane
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Financial documents are packed with valuable insights but are often long, complex, and hard to interpret—especially under rapidly changing market conditions. This paper introduces Finalyze, an intelligent assistant built using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and large language models (LLMs) to automate financial document analysis. Finalyze supports multi-format inputs (PDF, CSV, XLSX), semantically segments content, and enables users to ask natural language questions or obtain contextual summaries. It also integrates real-time stock market sentiment using live news feeds. Compared to existing tools like Bloomberg and AlphaSense, Finalyze offers broader document support, interactive query handling, and real-time analysis—while remaining accessible to individuals and small teams. Experimentally, Finalyze achieves a context-recall of 78.26%, a context relevance of 86.99%, and an interface response time of 1.8-2.3 seconds—demonstrating high accuracy and responsiveness. These results highlight the system's ability to bridge static document data with dynamic financial context, making it a practical tool for analysts, auditors, and researchers.

title: IoMT-Based AI-Driven Respiratory Risk Prediction App with Firebase Integration for Low-Resource Settings
authors: Asma Sadia Khan, Arifa Tanzim, Tanjim Mahmud, Abdurahim Boltayev, Abubokor Hanip, Mohammad Shahadat Hossain
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This paper presents a practical, low-cost Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) system for real-time respiratory health monitoring. This framework integrates an ESP32 microcontroller with MAX30102 and LM35 sensors, which capture blood oxygen saturation (SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>), heart rate (BPM), and body temperature. A Random Forest model was trained on a publicly available COVID-19 dataset and converted to TensorFlow Lite for on-device risk classification. The model achieved 97% accuracy, 100% precision, and an AUC-ROC of 0.996 in offline testing. Real-time data is transmitted via Bluetooth to an Android application for live display. Firebase integration enables remote data storage and automated email alerts for abnormal readings. In testing across 20 individuals, the system reached an average real-time accuracy of 93.3%, including 100% accuracy for stable (non-risk) conditions. Built with affordable, open-source components, this system offers a reliable and scalable solution for monitoring respiratory conditions in home-based or resource-constrained environments.

title: FD-hPINNs-Based Inverse Design Method for Metallic Metastructure
authors: Junjie Shao, Yin-Qing Pan, Jinsong Fan, Hongyuan Chang, Jin-Pin Liu, Ren Wang, Bing-Zhong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) have attracted widespread attention for the integration of physical constraints and data-driven learning. However, mainstream PINNs frameworks rely on automatic differentiation (AD) to compute partial derivatives, leading to unstable convergence, rapidly increasing computational cost with network scale, difficulties in balancing loss terms, and limited structural expressiveness, constraining applicability in complex electromagnetic (EM) design tasks. A finite-difference hard-constraint physics-informed neural network (FD-hPINNs) is proposed for the inverse design of EM devices. The approach replaces AD with finite-difference (FD) for derivative computation, enhancing the physical correlation among spatial sampling points and effectively reducing the high computational cost that grows with network scale, while maintaining compatibility with various network architectures. In addition, boundary conditions and structure parameters are directly embedded into the partial differential equations (PDEs) computation as hard constraints, simplifying the loss-function composition and improving the adaptability to complex structures. The proposed method is validated with a metallic metastructure in 2-D free space, and the results are compared with those obtained from AD-PINNs, demonstrating the enhanced capability of the FD-hPINNs in complex EM inverse design problems. Furthermore, the design is reimplemented and experimentally verified on a dielectric substrate, where the measurements match well with the simulations, confirming the effectiveness of the FD-hPINNs in practical EM device inverse design.

title: COMPAS 2025 Breaker Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)

title: A Bioinspired Gear-Rolling Knee Exoskeleton for Enhanced Human-Exoskeleton Kinematic Compatibility
authors: Hui Li, Ruilin Liu, Jiahao Du, Shengli Luo, Duojin Wang, Hongliu Yu
abstract: Objective: This paper aims to reduce human–exoskeleton knee joint misalignment arising from the nonuniform and subject-specific motion of the tibiofemoral joint, thereby improving kinematic compatibility between the user and the exoskeleton. Methods: A bioinspired gear-based knee exoskeleton is proposed, featuring a planetary gear mechanism that approximates the physiological rolling behavior of the human knee and a three-stage gear compensatory transmission that accommodates residual sliding-induced mismatch. A virtual human–exoskeleton interaction model based on sliding misalignment metric was developed to quantify kinematic misalignment and guide gear-parameter optimization. The proposed design was evaluated through numerical simulations, prototype implementation, human-subject experiments, and bench tests to characterize kinematic consistency and drive performance. Results: Relative to a single-axis knee joint, the proposed mechanism reduced knee joint misalignment by approximately 70%. Torque transmission tests conducted on a mechanically decoupled platform showed close agreement between commanded and measured output torques, with peak deviations of approximately 8%–15% during extension and 5%–13% during flexion. Back-drivability characterization further indicated low passive resistance, with back-driving torque accounting for less than 5% of the rated assistive torque. Conclusion: The proposed gear-based knee joint enables anatomical motion approximation and internal misalignment accommodation while maintaining reliable torque transmission within a deterministic kinematic structure. Significance: This work provides a generalizable mechanical and modeling framework for addressing knee joint misalignment in wearable exoskeletons and supports the development of systems with improved human–robot kinematic compatibility.

authors: N/A
