title: Energy Management and Wake-up for IoT Networks Powered by Energy Harvesting
authors: David E. Ruíz-Guirola, Samuel Montejo-Sánchez, Israel Leyva-Mayorga, Zhu Han, Petar Popovski, Onel L. A. López
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Things (IoT) presents sustainability challenges, including increased maintenance requirements and overall higher energy consumption. This motivates self-sustainable IoT ecosystems based on Energy Harvesting (EH). This paper treats IoT deployments in which IoT devices (IoTDs) rely solely on EH to sense and transmit information about events/alarms to a base station (BS). The objective is to effectively manage the duty cycling of the IoTDs to prolong battery life and maximize the relevant data delivered to the BS. The BS can also selectively wake up specific IoTDs to gather extra information following initial detection. We propose a K-nearest neighbors (KNN)-based duty cycling management to optimize energy efficiency and detection accuracy by considering spatial correlations among IoTDs’ activity and their EH process. We evaluate machine learning approaches, including reinforcement learning (RL) and decision transformers (DT), to maximize information captured from events while managing energy consumption. All three approaches (KNN, RL, and DT) achieve significant energy savings over state-of-the-art methods. Moreover, the RL-based solution approaches the performance of a genie-aided benchmark as the number of IoTDs increases.

title: Continuum Modeling of High-Field Transport in Semiconductors
authors: M.G. Ancona, C.R. DeVore, S.J. Cooke
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: Continuum approaches are renowned in mathematical physics for their parsimony, robustness, and numerical efficiency, and for these reasons are especially valuable for physics-based modeling. For this paper the relevant field is semiconductor device engineering where the original continuum description of diffusion-drift theory remains in wide use. However, this theory is known to be inadequate for describing high-field transport, and efforts to improve on it, while having a long history, are generally regarded as phenomenological and useful only when buttressed by extensive experimental characterization. With this as motivation, we develop a new physics-based approach that we call drag history theory using classical field theoretic methods rather than via the traditional route based on the Boltzmann equation. Critical to our theory are various material response functions that we characterize specifically for silicon using Monte Carlo simulations of a high-voltage diode. Most important is the response function for the drag force felt by the electron gas as it flows through the lattice wherein one needs to properly account for its non-instantaneous nature. By weighing the contributions of mechanical and thermal inertia and thermal diffusion, we also discuss the conditions under which the full description can be simplified with significant computational benefits.

title: Personalized Federated Learning for Egocentric Video Gaze Estimation with Comprehensive Parameter Freezing
authors: Yuhu Feng, Keisuke Maeda, Takahiro Ogawa, Miki Haseyama
abstract: Egocentric video gaze estimation ipredicts where an individual looks in first-person videos providing an essential understanding of user attention. However, it must accommodate the variability in individuals’ gaze patterns and support learning in scenarios in which data are distributed across multiple devices. To overcome these challenges, we introduce a Personalized Federated Learning (PFL) framework with Comprehensive Parameters Freezing (FedCPF) that improves gaze estimation accuracy and personalization in distributed settings. Our approach employs a transformer-based backbone to capture temporal dependencies and spatial correlations in egocentric video frames.Within a PFL setting, only those parameters that exhibit the most significantly updated parameters during local training are selected and subsequently frozen, developing tailored client models that preserve high-impact features. By freezing these key parameters, FedCPF reduces the communication overhead and mitigates issues from heterogeneous data distributions while allowing clients to fine-tune models for their unique gaze behaviors. We validate FedCPF through extensive experiments on the EGTEA Gaze+ and Ego4D datasets. The experimental results demonstrate that our comprehensive parameter freezing strategy effectively balances global collaboration and local adaptation, making FedCPF a promising solution for accurate, adaptive gaze estimation in federated learning.

title: Occlusion SAR Target Recognition Based on Learnable Fractional Gabor Transform and Local Scattering Extraction Network
authors: Chang Liu, Lingyu Wang, Xin Lin, Penghui Huang, Xiang-Gen Xia, Qing Ling, Lang Xia, Xiangcheng Wan
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Aiming at the problem of significant performance degradation of synthetic aperture radar (SAR) image recognition network under target occlusion conditions, this letter proposes a learnable fractional Gabor transform and local scattering extraction network (FGT-LSENet) for occluded SAR target recognition. In the proposed method, we firstly design the learnable Gabor transform module to guide the network to perform global information extraction of features with multi-level fusion. Subsequently, the local scattering module is utilized to extract the strong scattering features that occlude the target key structure. Finally, the convolutional, global, and local scattering information are fused and the network is optimized using triplet loss function and central loss function. Experiments with different degrees of occlusion on the moving and stationary target acquisition recognition (MSTAR) dataset show that the proposed method achieves higher recognition rates with different degrees of occlusion compared to existing network models.

title: Area- and Utilization-Efficient LLM Accelerator With Fused Speculative Decoding for Edge-Side Inference
authors: Kaiqi Chen, Zikang Zhou, Yaqi Chen, Jun Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: The inference of large language models (LLMs) on edge devices has always been a challenge, the autoregressive inference and enormous number of parameters result in long inference latency. Although speculative decoding is proposed for inference acceleration, it poses challenges for hardware accelerator design, as the differing computational characteristics of the drafting and verification stages make it difficult to optimize both chip area and hardware utilization. This brief introduces fused speculative decoding (FSD) to optimize LLM inference on edge devices by unifying all the operations to general matrix multiplications during inference. The proposed FSD-Infer algorithm fuses the drafting and verification phases of conventional speculative decoding and enables weight sharing, reducing off-chip memory access, and inference latency without requiring retraining or fine-tuning. For hardware, we introduce FSD-Acc, an area- and utilization-efficient hardware accelerator that efficiently executes the fused operations enabled by FSD-Infer. Experimental results show that compared with autoregressive inference, FSD-Infer reduces EMA by up to 26.11%, enhances arithmetic intensity by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10.26\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula>, and speeds up GPU inference by up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.38\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula>. When deployed on Xilinx ZCU102 FPGA board at 200 MHz working frequency, FSD-Acc achieves the best area efficiency and energy efficiency, outperforming the state-of-the-art LLM accelerator for edge inference by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.53\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula>(TPS/kDSP), <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5.90\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula>(TPS/kLUT) and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.02\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula>, respectively.

title: Improved Data Augmentation and Cross-Modal Feature Fusion for Sonar Target Classification Under Reverberation Background
authors: Yuqi Zheng, Lerong Hong, Boyu Zhao, Hongzhuang Cao
abstract: Effective sonar target classification in reverberant underwater environments remains a significant challenge due to limited sample data and complex noise interference. This article presents an innovative approach that combines enhanced Wasserstein generative adversarial network with gradient penalty (WGAN-GP) with frequency-domain constraints and cross-modal feature fusion to address these challenges. The WGAN-GP model, enhanced with spectrum-aware loss, generates high-fidelity reverberation signals. This method, coupled with a bright spot model for target echo simulation, creates a robust dataset that alleviates the issue of small sample sizes, which is critical in sonar systems. Furthermore, a multimodal convolutional network leveraging Gramian angular field (GAF) and Markov transition field (MTF) representations is introduced for effective feature extraction and classification. The proposed system achieves superior classification accuracy, averaging 94.8% at 10 dB signal-to-reverberation ratio (SRR), outperforming traditional models. Experimental validation in diverse marine environments, including the South China Sea, demonstrates the framework’s high generalization ability and strong performance under varying SRR conditions. These results establish the proposed method as a powerful tool for robust, real-time sonar target classification in challenging environments.

title: Review for "Descriptor: Synthetic Person Information Dataset for Entity Resolution (SPIDER)"
authors: Daniel Joseph Ringis

title: Design and Implementation of an Anthropomorphic Robotic Hand with Key Kinematic Properties of Human Hand_supp3-3663977.mp4
authors: Caihua Xiong
abstract: Designing an anthropomorphic robotic hand with few actuators while replicating the dexterous motion capabilities of the human hand remains a challenging problem. In this work, a kinematic synergy analysis method is proposed to identify joints with high motion independence and to quantify the synergistic motion characteristics of joints with strong motion dependence. Based on these findings, design principles for intra finger and inter-finger coupling–compliance mechanisms are developed, enabling the mechanical embodiment of joint kinematic synergies within and between fingers. Furthermore, a design principle for anthropomorphic robotic hands that reduces actuator count while retaining diverse motion functions is formulated. This principle is embodied in a 12-actuator, 21-joint prototype named SX-Hand, featuring a flexible thumb and an articulated palm. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that SX Hand achieves the maximum score in the Kapandji test, performs all 33 grasp types defined in the comprehensive GRASP taxonomy, and executes dexterous in-hand manipulations. The proposed approach provides an effective conceptual and technical route for reproducing rich motion functions with reduced actuation, offering valuable guidance for the development of efficient anthropomorphic robotic hands and other bionic systems.

title: Dual Adaptive Disentangled Representation Learning with Multimodal Data for Disease Diagnosis
authors: Xiumei Chen, Wenliang Pan, Tao Wang, Xinyue Zhang, Wei Xiong, Ting Tian, Qianjin Feng, Meiyan Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: The use of imaging and genetic data for biomarker detection and disease diagnosis can deepen the understanding of disease pathogenesis and assist in clinical diagnosis. However, current methods face two major challenges: 1) the significant heterogeneity between multimodal data hampers modality fusion. 2) Effectively exploring consistency and variability information from similar diseases for enhancing model performance is difficult. In this paper, we propose a novel unified frame work, termed dual adaptive disentangled representation learning (DADRL), to simultaneously achieve disease-shared and disease specific biomarker detection as well as disease diagnosis. Our DADRL comprises three components: 1) a biology information constraints-based modality fusion strategy is applied to adaptively explore inter- and intra-modal correlations, thereby effectively fusing multimodal data. 2) A unified framework that integrates modality fusion and disease diagnosis is proposed to mine disease-related information for simultaneously accomplishing disease-related biomarker detection and disease diagnosis. 3) Disentangled representation learning and several adaptive metric constraints are incorporated into the unified framework to adaptively separate disease-specific information from disease shared feature representations for effectively identifying disease shared and disease-specific biomarkers, thereby deepening the understanding of disease pathogenesis. Extensive experiments on multiple real datasets and simulated data demonstrate that our method significantly improves performance of biomarker detection and disease diagnosis.

title: Investigation of AC Loss in an impregnated 3-Parallel Strands HTS Coil for a Superconducting Armature of Electrical Machine for Aeronautics
authors: Arthur Jamois-Le Gouguec, Bruno Douine, Paul Gning, Jean L´evˆeque, Christophe Viguier
abstract: The design of a superconducting armature is currently one of the major challenges for the development of a cryogenic turboelectric chain for aeronautics. To limit the voltage rise in aircraft, HTS coils are designed with parallel strands and must withstand both thermal constraints related to the presence of AC losses and mechanical constraints during operation. In this study, the AC losses of an impregnated 3-parallel strands racetrack double pancake HTS coil were investigated for currents and frequencies ranging from 30 to 150 A and from 40 to 440 Hz, respectively. Experimental results show that despite an overall hysteresis loss behavior, mechanical losses of vibrational origin seem to be part of the loss balance, particularly at low current and high frequency where they appear to be preponderant.

title: Transition-edge sensor characteristics in a modified bias circuit for multiplexed readout
authors: Stephen J. Smith, Paul van der Hulst, Nicholas A. Wakeham, Edoardo Cucchetti, W. Bertrand Doriese, Malcolm Durkin, Christian Kirsch, Joel N. Ullom, Joseph S. Adams, Simon R. Bandler, James A. Chervenak, Tannaz Farrahi, Fred M. Finkbeiner, Joshua D. Fuhrman, Samuel V. Hull, Richard L. Kelley, Caroline A. Kilbourne, Haruka Muramatsu, Frederick S. Porter, Kazuhiro Sakai, Ambarish C. Venkatasubraman, Edward J. Wassell, Michael C. Witthoeft, Sang H. Yoon
abstract: The X-ray Integral Field Unit (X-IFU) on the NewAthena X-ray telescope will use a 1,504-pixel array of transition-edge sensor (TES) microcalorimeters, read out via time division multiplexing (TDM). To allow testing of multiplexer chips before TES integration, each unit cell includes a “TES-bypass” resistor that substitutes for a TES during characterization. After integration, this resistor remains as a high-resistance shunt: minimal for DC operation but can influence the frequency response due to its placement with the Nyquist inductor. We modeled the modified bias circuit, derived the small-signal response, and evaluated noise contributions, including Johnson noise from the bypass resistor. We analyzed the effects on pulse height, slew-rate, and energy resolution. Simulations were compared with measurements from prototype X-IFU microcalorimeters. Our results indicate that as long as the resistor value exceeds the TES dynamic resistance, performance is largely unaffected.

title: Ineffective Calibration Testing of Residential Branch Circuit Breakers
authors: Jesse Aronstein
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Industry Applications
abstract: Previous test results demonstrated that some presently-marketed residential branch-circuit breaker brands have a double-digit defect rate. The defective breakers do not trip as required at 135% of rated current. The unique test results reported in this present paper clearly show that these calibration defects cannot be reliably detected by testing at 200% or 300% of rated current. One hundred defective breakers of three brands from the previous tests are retested. They all fail to trip at 135% of rated current, confirming that they are defective. The defective breakers then all pass the UL489 calibration test at 200% of rated current (must trip within 120 seconds), and they all also pass the NEMA AB4 calibration test at 300% of rated current (must trip within 50 seconds). These higher-current tests have a 100% error rate for detection of these improperly calibrated breakers. This explains how defective breakers come to be shipped from some factories. For some residential branch circuit breaker brands, ineffective testing - relying only on high-current calibration testing - has allowed defective breakers into the supply chain for many decades, increasing the risk of fire and injury for occupants of the buildings in which they are installed.

title: Software Updates for Large-Scale Autonomous Vehicles Deployment: Challenges and Opportunities
authors: Arpan Bhattacharjee, Weisong Shi
abstract: As self-driving vehicles transition from experimental prototypes to commercial fleets, they rely on increasingly complex software that must evolve regularly to stay safe and competitive. This article examines the primary challenges of software over-the-air (OTA) updates for large-scale autonomous vehicle (AV) deployments and outlines the technical and operational frameworks for managing these challenges. We argue that OTA capability is not optional but essential for fleet safety and competitiveness. Nevertheless, the complexity of AV code bases, the fragility of tightly coupled systems, the logistics of phased rollouts, network limitations, and cybersecurity threats all complicate updates. By posing key questions that engineers and operators must address—and by grounding the discussion in recent incidents involving Waymo and Zoox—we demonstrate how careful architecture, simulation-driven validation, robust security, and scalable infrastructure can transform OTA into a reliable pillar of AV operations. We conclude with research directions for the next decade of OTA development.

title: Marathi Text Summarization Using Multilingual BERT and TextRank
authors: Pushkar Joglekar, Akshay Shingote
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Text summarization has therefore become a necessity in grappling with the speedy flow of information in the many languages. This work proposes automatic text summarization for Marathi language text using a modified mBERT model with TextRank algorithm. While summarizing Marathi texts, the deep learning of mBERT is used to draw out semantic features of substrings, and TextRank is applied to rank and select the appropriate sentences and generate accurate summaries. This helped us to use mBERT in better understanding the syntax and semantic of Marathi while fine-tuning this model on 80K Marathi news articles curated from the news websites. The results obtained highlight a significant enhancement in the quality of the summaries generated, compared to baseline models in terms of precision, recall and F1 score. The work presented here also contributes towards enhanced understanding of NL processing in Indian languages and provides a solution to the problem of content summarization in low resource languages. The study thus recommends possible improvements in the methods of providing information and helped in gaining better understanding of the information for the Marathi speaking people, thereby helping in making effective decisions.

title: Cloud-Based Injury Forecasting in Contact Sports with ARIMA Time Series Model
authors: N. Saranya Devi, R. Meenakshi, A Neelima, B. Parvathi, Monisha R, Sarveswari K
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Injuries in contact sports pose considerable issues, impacting individuals' health, team performance, and overall career length. This research presents a cloud-based injury forecasting system using the Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) time series model to anticipate injury incidents in contact sports. The ARIMA model systematically analyses historical injury data from several sports disciplines to identify and capture underlying patterns and trends, enabling precise estimates of future injury risks. Cloud-based technology improves accessibility and functionality, enabling coaches, medical personnel, and athletes to analyze real-time data and get prompt information on injury risks. It demonstrates that the ARIMA model is proficient in forecasting injury incidents, facilitating a proactive strategy for injury management in athletic environments. Cloud technology provides scalability and promotes collaboration across sports organizations, improving data exchange and communication. This novel method allows the creation of personalized injury prevention measures, hence enhancing athlete safety and performance. Future studies will aim to improve the forecasting model by integrating advanced machine learning (ML) methodologies and augmenting the dataset to improve predictive accuracy and dependability.

title: A Dual-Mode LLM Framework for Medical and General Language Translation for Breaking Barriers in Healthcare Communication
authors: Intishar Rahman Anindo, Md. Ashiq Ul Islam Sajid, Mohammad Sakib Mahmood, Nayeyan Prottush, Md Kishor Morol
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Effective communication between healthcare providers and patients is frequently hindered by the complexity of medical language, contributing to misinterpretations and reduced health literacy. This study presents a robust, dual-mode translation framework powered by large language models (LLMs), combining neural architectures with rule-based safety layers to enable bidirectional translation between medical and general language. In addition to simplifying clinical language for patients, our system accurately reconstructs layperson descriptions into medically precise terms—empowering providers with clearer symptom narratives. We significantly enhance prior work by introducing domain-specific evaluation metrics (e.g., drug name preservation, dosage accuracy, ambiguity detection), conducting human-in-the-loop clinical validation, and benchmarking against specialized medical models such as MedPaLM, BioBERT, ClinicalBERT, and GPT-4 with medical prompting. Our curated dataset exceeds 60,000 annotated pairs across 15 specialties, ensuring generalizability across healthcare contexts. Clinical trials in outpatient settings demonstrate a 35% improvement in patient comprehension and high physician satisfaction (8.7/10), with zero safety incidents recorded. The system architecture supports real-world deployment via FHIR-compatible APIs and complies with regulatory frameworks such as HIPAA and FDA 510(k). These results indicate the model’s potential to meaningfully bridge the communication gap in healthcare while setting a new standard for safe, transparent, and clinically grounded AI translation tools.

title: Text and Audio-based Cyberbullying Detection on Social Media Platforms
authors: Suganya D, Srija G K K, Sowmiya T, Nisha Thirumurugan, Vidhya K
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Cyberbullying has become a serious issue in online platforms, impacting social well-being and mental health. The emergence of social media platforms has issue, making it complex to regulate abusive content. The traditional rule-based and keyword-based moderation methods frequently fail to capture the multimodal and contextual nature of cyberbullying. The previous research has explored deep learning and machine learning techniques in Natural Language Processing for text-based detection, while audio-based methods use signal processing and neural networks to distinguish abusive speech. Furthermore, multimodal techniques integrating text, speech, and visuals have been developed to improve detection accuracy. A new approach proposes a hybrid model that integrates the CNN (Convolutional Neural Network) model for audio analysis and the BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformer) model for text analysis to improve cyberbullying detection. CNN extracts audio features like pitch, tone and sentiment to identify online aggressive speech, while BERT uses deep contextual understanding to detect text-based bullying. By integrating these approaches, the model provides an efficient and comprehensive method to detect online harassment. Furthermore, an alert message system to inform users when potentially harmful content is identified, encourage polite and responsible communication.

title: Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Models for Forecasting NIFTY50 and NIFTY500 Indices using Technical Indicators
authors: Praveen M Naik, Ashit C, Ashray K, Adithya Maradithaya, Ankith Kumar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The stock market forecasting is a challenging issue because of volatility, nonlinear, and dynamic nature. This paper explores the predictive capabilities of three deep learning algorithms: LSTM, GRU, and CNN-LSTM in predicting the weekly closing prices of the primary Indian stock indices such as NIFTY50 and NIFTY500. We developed a custom Weekly dataset having historical features such as Open, High, Low, Close and Volume (OHLCV) data and technical indicators. Technical indicators are derived using OHLCV data like Relative Strength Index (RSI), point difference, percentage difference are used. Evaluation of the models is performed on the basis of such metrics as the Mean Squared Error (MSE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score. Findings indicate a superior performance of CNN-LSTM in predicting NIFTY50 and LSTM in predicting NIFTY500, which proves the usefulness of hybrid deep learning models in time series forecasting in the financial sector.

title: Early Diabetes Detection with Hybrid ML Models
authors: Apurva Apurva, D.Vetrithangam D. Vetrithangam
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Prediction of diabetes early is important in minimising the health complications associated with the complications in the long term. This study proposes such a machine learning based framework that uses a PIMA Indian Diabetes dataset that can be used to accurately predict diabetes. It will contrast the operation of a single Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) classifier and a combined one with LightGBM+K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN) ensemble. Compound preprocessing procedures can be employed to enhance models stability and performance rigorous preprocessing operations, such as normalization and feature selection. The LightGBM model showed a significant accuracy of 94.3 % and an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.975, indicating that it has been able to differentiate diabetes and non diabetic cases competently. Moreover, due to the combination of KNN as a post-processing layer on predictions made by LightGBM, the performance improved, achieving the accuracy of 95.4% and the AUC of 0.983. These findings have revealed that the hybrid model is more accurate than the standalone classifier as high-speed gradient boosting of LightGBM is combined with locality-based refinement by KNN. The suggested framework presents an efficient and extensible framework to early detection of diabetes and it can be used in clinical decision support systems.

title: BreastHistoNet: A Lightweight Multi-Scale CNN for Real-Time Multi-Class Breast Cancer Histopathology Classification
authors: Vinayagam P, Kanagamalliga S, Kurugonda Mrudhul, Modepalli Harish
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Breast cancer is one of the major causes of death in women, and histopathological analysis is the gold standard test to diagnose it. The manual analysis of tissue slides is rather slow and has the possibility of being affected by variance. A lightweight deep learning model, BreastHistoNet, which is efficient and capable of multi-class classification of breast histopathological images. The model combines multi-scale convolutional blocks to learn a wide range of morphologic patterns, and channel recalibration blocks to highlight attention on diagnostically informative features. Having been trained on the BreakHis dataset, BreastHistoNet has an accuracy of 96.2%, F1-score of 95.8%, precision of 95.7% and AUC of 0.984, which is better than previous approaches. It is optimized in TensorRT to perform real-time inference, with an Inference time of 8.2 ms per image in a small size of 8.6 MB. These findings prove the scalability of BreastHistoNet as a deployable, interpretable, and reliable approach in helping pathologists to diagnose breast cancer as accurately as possible.

title: A Deep Learning Approach for Alzheimer's Disease Classification from Brain MRI
authors: Sallauddin Mohmmad, Eshwar Nomula, Gulab Singh Chauhan, Ravi Kumar Rachavaram, Billakanti Srinivasa Rao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Alzheimer's Disease (AD) is a degenerative neurological disease that impacted cognition and memory and hence early and correct diagnosis was critical. The focus of this research was on classifying brain MRI images to find the severity of Alzheimer. A publicly sourced MRI dataset obtained from Kaggle that comprised augmented as well as original images used in this research. Three Neural Networks architectures were evaluated in this research that are VGG19, ResNet50, and a custom CNN. VGG19 and ResNet50 with transfer learning were used with pre-trained ImageNet weights. On the unseen original test set, the VGG19 model obtained the best classification accuracy of 98.95%, and the custom CNN obtained 97.70%, and ResNet50 95.98%. The results illustrated the efficiency of transfer learning and the excellent performance of custom architectures in medical image classification. The research also revealed the significance of applying augmented data in training and validation to enhance model generalization and robustness.

title: Cybersecurity Threats Prevention through PUF Based Dynamic Logic Morphing on IoT Edge Devices
authors: Tonmoy Kumar Dey, Fakir Sharif Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: As the proliferation of IoT edge devices accelerates across critical infrastructures, ensuring their cybersecurity becomes increasingly imperative. These devices are particularly vulnerable to hardware-based attacks, including the insertion of hardware Trojans during untrusted manufacturing and deployment stages. To address this challenge, this paper presents a lightweight and effective hardware level security framework that utilizes a Physical Unclonable Function (PUF) for dynamic logic obfuscation on IoT edge devices. Unlike traditional static obfuscation approaches, the proposed method integrates PUF responses to dynamically configure and morph functional logic paths within the hardware. This coupling ensures that the circuit remains unintelligible and non-functional without the correct PUF challenge response pair, thus resilience to reverse engineering, side-channel analysis and hardware Trojan insertion. The proposed method significantly strengthens device level protection with a hardness index of 25% more than without morphing and without incurring substantial overhead. Experimental results on standard ISCAS’89 benchmark circuits demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed approach in enhancing security while maintaining resource efficiency, delivering it highly suitable for deployment in resource constrained IoT edge environments.

title: Early Detection of Chicken Diseases Using EfficientNetB3: A Deep Learning Approach for Poultry Health Monitoring
authors: Raghavendra Sridhar, Jatin Sharma, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Deepak Banerjee, Gaurav Sharma
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Although the poultry sector is a major source of animal protein, illnesses in hens may cause major food security issues and financial losses. Reducing death rates, improving output efficiency, and stopping the spread of illnesses all depend on early disease identification. Manual observation and laboratory tests are two classic approaches of disease identification that take time and call for knowledgeable staff. This paper suggests to automatically detect four major chicken diseases: Coccidiosis, Healthy, New Castle Disease, and Salmonella using EfficientNetB3, a convolutional neural network (CNN). Comprising 8,067 photos of chickens split into training, validation, and testing sets, the dataset comes from the Kaggle platform. Especially for Coccidiosis and Salmonella, the model attained an overall accuracy of 98% with great precision, recall, and F1-scores. Performance measures and the confusion matrix revealed that the model can minimally misclassify chicken diseases and effectively identify them. Moreover, the training and validation loss and accuracy curves showed that the model efficiently reduced overfitting, thereby attaining ideal performance following seven epochs. These findings imply that EfficientNetB3 offers a strong tool for early illness diagnosis and could be a scalable, effective solution for chicken health monitoring, therefore enhancing management techniques in poultry farming. Aiming to lower financial losses and enhance animal welfare in the poultry sector, this work provides the basis for including advanced deep learning models into practical chicken health systems.

title: Quantum Computing for Medical Imaging: Detecting Malaria from Red Blood Cells
authors: Thilakavathy. P, Ananya. S, Abina. R
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: One of the most deadly infectious diseases in the world, malaria claims thousands of lives annually, particularly in youngsters. Conventional diagnostic methods including antigen testing, PCR, and microscopic blood smear analysis are either expensive, require specialised labour, or have poor accuracy. We suggest a quantum-inspired method for automated red blood cell (RBC) image-based malaria diagnosis in order to overcome these difficulties. To detect malaria-infected cells, our approach uses a Variational Quantum Classifier (VQC) that combines quantum feature mapping and classical preprocessing. In order to minimise dimensionality while maintaining important information, features are retrieved using contourlet transforms and contour area detection, and then optimised with minimum redundancy maximum relevance (mRMR). When compared to traditional approaches, VQC achieves greater accuracy in classifying the optimised features. Once the infection has been established, a basic rule-based expert system is also added to identify the type of malaria parasite. In addition to demonstrating the effectiveness of VQC for biomedical applications, this work lays the groundwork for the use of quantum models in actual illness detection.

title: A Next-Generation Medical Consultation System Using Augmented Reality (AR) Based On 3D X-Ray Visualization And AI-Driven Diagnostic Analysis
authors: Tamilarasan M, Angelin Nivya G, Harshini M, Kumaresh C
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The usage of artificial intelligence (AI) techniques particularly deep learning algorithms, has recently increased dramatically and the healthcare sector is only one of many industries affected. Using deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) enhanced with augmented reality visualization inputs we present a novel approach to medical image analysis in this paper. The proposed system aims to provide an effective economical and non-invasive method of remote disease detection we employ a deep convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture that has been optimized for X-ray image processing. Furthermore we use data augmentation methodologies to strengthen the model's capacity to generalize and handle varied patient groups and medical conditions more effectively. Our approach incorporates denoising techniques using advanced image preprocessing, segmentation via enhanced feature extraction methods, classification using Deep Convolutional Neural Networks (DCNN) integrated with Augmented Reality (AR) visualization capabilities, and real-time communication protocols which improves the accuracy and dependability of the system in identifying medical anomalies based on radiological image signatures. The telemedicine platform achieves strong performance together with scalability through its React.js frontend design and Node.js backend with Express.js and MongoDB database system. Medical images are processed through TensorFlow framework and their safe storage occurs within Firebase Storage. The system integrates AR.js for web-based applications which allows immersive 3D visualization of medical scans. The system enables real-time communication between doctors and patients.

title: A Deformable Weighted Network with Enhanced Imaging for Efficient CT Lung Infection Segmentation
authors: Vijayalakshmi D, T Sandhya Kumari, Jamana Pujitha, Bojanki Madhuri, Vineetha Rani J S, Nammi Gayathri Bhavani
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Lung infection continues to be a predominant source of cancer-related mortality, and precise segmentation of CT scans is essential for early diagnosis and formulation of treatment strategies. Lung infection segmentation presents a significant challenges due to uneven morphology, class imbalance, and intricate overlapping features on CT images, including lung tissue, bone, and arteries. Conventional segmentation techniques, such as U- Net, frequently encounter challenges, causing either over or under-segmentation in infected regions. To overcome the limitations mentioned above, a deep learning framework called WDU-Net (Weighted Deformable U-Net) is proposed, which can enhance the efficient segmentation of lung CT images. CT scan images are enhanced and given to the WDU-Net, which uses Deformable Convolutions (DC) to effectively handle irregular shapes of infected region, and combine a Weight Generation (WG) module to focus on important details while mitigating the influence of noise. The effectiveness of the proposed algorithm is evaluated on publicly available CT scan images and performance metrics like Dice coefficient and Intersection of Union (IoU) have been computed along with the existing methodologies. Experimental result shows the proposed methodology outperforms in terms of high Dice coefficient and Intersection of Union (IoU)compared to the state of art approaches.

title: Integrating Selective Kernel & Central Difference Convolutions for Melanoma Detection
authors: Ujjwal Dhruv, Ankit Yadav, Priyanka Meel, Dinesh Kumar Vishwakarma
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Melanoma is one of the most common types of cancer worldwide, it is the deadliest due to its rapid body-wide spread. Spread through the lymphatic system and bloodstream, melanoma can quickly metastasize to vital organs, making treatment crucial and early detection for convalescent survival. Awareness of risk factors, such as UV exposure and genetic predispositions, is essential in combating this aggressive form of cancer. Improving patient outcomes requires early detection, but in many regions of the world, access to professional dermatological evaluation is still restricted. In this work, we suggest a deep learning model based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for abruptly classifying skin lesions into malignant and benign sets hang on dermoscopic pictures. This model aims to enhance diagnostic fineness and provide a viable solution for areas lacking sufficient dermatological resources. By leveraging huge datasets of annotated images, the CNN can learn to identify subtle features that differentiate between malignant and benign beatings, ultimately aiding in early detection and improving survival rates for patients. Ten thousand photos make up the dataset, and in order to improve generalization and lessen overfitting, a great deal of preparation was done, including data augmentation and standardization. The model was trained for 50 epochs with the Adam optimizer. Which has a precision of 93.6%, AUC of 97.2, MCC of 82.7, validation accuracy of 91.3% and recall of 88.6%. The model proved particularly accurate in differentiating between benign and malignant tumors. This capability is critical for early detection and treatment of skin cancer, which can greatly improve patient outcomes. The results show that the model could be used in real practice to help dermatologists make more accurate diagnoses.

title: SmartParkX: A Real-Time IoT-Based Smart Parking System with RFID Authentication, Infrared Slot Monitoring, and Web-Controlled Gate Automation
authors: Koushik Biswas Arko, Md. Jobarul Islam, Md. Mainul Islam, Abida Afrin, Sadia Afrin, Fahmida Sultana Oishi, A.K.M Tamim Rahman, Bishwajit Banik Pathik, Md. Mortuza Ahmmed
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This paper describes SmartParkX, an IoT-based smart parking system that incorporates RFID access control, infrared slot detection, and a web-based interface for real-time monitoring and gate automation. SmartParkX utilizes an Arduino Mega to handle the sensor and RFID components and an ESP8266 to provide Wi-Fi access and a hosted dynamic dashboard. An RFID tag on an entrance gate will allow authorized vehicles to enter the parking facility, and the infrared sensors will sense the slot availability in that parking space. A secure administrator panel facilitates manual control of the gate, access to the logs, and simulates a QR-based payment method. Through real-time communication, users exchange data to track car history, calculate cost, and slot state. SmartParkX provides a low-cost solution that is scalable for modern urban parking challenges and changing needs in a parking industry by providing a fully integrated ecosystem of automation, visibility and build user digital interaction.

title: Multi-Agent AI Systems for Decentralized Decision-Making
authors: Manisha Bhende, Shripad Joshi, C. Gouri Sainath, Surendarkumar S, Moghal Yaseen Pasha, Gayatri Parasa
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The growing complexity of decision-making in dynamic, decentralized settings requires more efficient, scalable multi-agent systems. Traditional methods of a centralized decision-making approach have proven to be rather slow to adapt in real-time and to scale effectively toward highly performing adversarial environments. This research aims to develop a decentralized multi-agent system that provides an efficient solution to decision-making by combining policy learning through deep reinforcement learning to allow agents to make optimized decisions and market-based task allocation mechanisms. To realize this, a multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) framework is set up where each agent independently learns the optimal policy based on local observations. The proposed methods allowed for significant improvements in efficiency in all aspects of the system, including up to 33.7% improvement in task completion time and 35.5% reduction in decision-making latency over baseline systems. These results demonstrate that the proposed system is robust and scalable to a variety of cooperative and competitive settings. This research provides useful information for developing more efficient, decentralized systems and techniques for real-world tasks such as autonomous systems, robotics, and distributed networks. Future research should explore further scalability and integration of ethical and regulatory considerations for real-world applications.

title: Multiclass Skin Lesion Classification Using EfficientNetB4 and a Custom Feed-Forward Neural Network
authors: Mst. Israt Jahan Eva, Md. Sabbir Ahammed, Most. Fahmida Rahman, Jumana
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Skin cancer has become increasingly common across the globe, and timely and reliable detection is essential to improve patient outcomes. However, clinical diagnosis is often challenging because many types of skin lesions appear visually similar, frequently resulting in diagnostic uncertainty. This article introduces a deep learning framework that compares seven CNN feature extractors in combination with different classification strategies. Training and evaluation of the proposed framework on the HAM10000 dataset involved label smoothing, cosine learning rate scheduling, test-time augmentation (TTA), and selective fine-tuning. Among all models evaluated, EfficientNetB4 combined with a custom classifier marked the best performance level, with a validation accuracy of 91. 02%, Macro precision of 87. 88%, Macro recall of 88. 46%, Macro F1 score of 87. 96%, ROC-AUC of 98. 64%, and TTA accuracy of 92. 30%, while maintaining a low inference time and moderate model size. These results establish EfficientNetB4 as a highly effective backbone for accurate, efficient, and deployable skin lesion classification, especially in mobile and teledermatology settings.

title: Deep Sequential Models for Stock Market Prediction: LSTM Neural Networks in Action
authors: Vijay A Sangolgi, Mithun B Patil, Onkar Jamma, Sohan Katageri
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This research explores stock price movement prediction using Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural networks, a variant of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) adept at handling sequential data. The goal is to build a data-driven model to classify future stock trends based on historical performance. Twenty years of historical stock data were collected from Yahoo Finance, focusing on the "Adjusted Close" prices to ensure consistency through corporate actions like dividends and stock splits. The data was normalized using MinMax scaling and organized using a sliding window of 100 data points to capture temporal dependencies. The LSTM architecture includes two stacked LSTM layers with dropout to mitigate overfitting, followed by dense layers for binary classification (e.g., price up or down). The model was trained over 10 epochs using the Adam optimizer . Evaluation was conducted using a confusion matrix, accuracy, and loss metrics, providing insight into the model’s ability to correctly classify future price movement. Results indicate that LSTM networks can learn from past market behavior and make meaningful short-term directional forecasts. Future work may incorporate more classes or technical indicators to improve prediction quality.

title: Parkinson’s Disease Tremor Detection using Support Vector Machine and Accelerometer Data
authors: Sandeep C S, Martin Margala
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Parkinson’s Disease (PD) is a neurodegenerative condition that advances over time, and overwhelming symptoms like tremors, rigidity, and bradykinesia identify it. Recognizing the tremors earlier and in the right way is of utmost importance so that the correct intervention can be planned and the patient will feel better. The current study presents a system that uses supervised learning to identify tremors from Parkinson's disease by applying Support Vector Machine (SVM) classification to data from accelerometer sensors. The newly developed system monitors hand tremors using a combination of wrist-attached wearable devices and tri-axial accelerometer technology. Noise removal and signal pattern normalization are the primary data preprocessing techniques that are implemented to wipe out any inconsistencies and localization issues in the signals. As a matter of fact, with the feature extraction process, which includes statistical and time-domain analysis, we can take out all the new features, which should represent the signal data more effectively. The SVM classifier is then used to make predictions based on the extracted features, and this step is done after a grid search and cross-validation that help in the tuning process. The method hence proposed is excellent at specificity and precision in the detection of Parkinson's tremors versus normal types of motion. As a result, wearable sensors and heavy machinery have created a noninvasive way to detect the disease early and keep track of the patient's condition in everyday situations.

title: Deep Neural Network-Based Approach for the Simultaneous Localization of Multi-Line Short-Circuit Faults in Power Networks
authors: Mbakob Ndagoua Polster Kader, Fouad Slaoui Hasnaoui
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper proposes a deep neural network-based approach for the simultaneous localization of short-circuit faults on multiple lines in meshed power networks. The IEEE 9-bus system was used as the study case, simulating various scenarios of single-line-to-ground, double-line-to-ground, and three-phase faults with variations in fault resistance and distance. Preprocessed three-phase voltage and current data were employed to train a feedforward neural network model designed for regression. Three architectures were evaluated and compared, with the optimal model achieving a mean squared error (MSE) of 0.0036, ensuring accurate and robust estimation of fault distances. The results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method for real-time multi-line localization, even under complex configurations.

title: Adaptive Anomaly Detection for Robust Network Security
authors: Chetana Bamandi, Fahad Alam, Girish Chandra, Harsh Gaurav, T M Kiran Kumar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of digital technologies, ensuring robust network security has become more crucial than ever. The growing number of interconnected devices has increased the chances of cyber threats, making it essential to have strong anomaly detection system measures in place. This undertaking introduces a predictive approach to detecting and preventing intrusions by applying methods from deep learning (DL) and machine learning (ML). Our method focuses on analyzing real-time data to spot odd trends and possible threats, helping improve detection accuracy and response times. We have developed a hybrid model that brings together supervised and unsupervised learning techniques to effectively recognize and counter different types of cyber threats. The system is designed to handle large amounts of data efficiently, covering processes like data preprocessing, feature selection, and model training to enhance security outcomes. This research highlights the significance of incorporating predictive analytics into cybersecurity systems to tackle the ever-evolving cyber threat landscape. Our results illustrate how the proposed combination of techniques works better than conventional methods by providing greater recognition precision, better scalability, and fewer false alarms. The system continuously learns from data in real time, which makes it more adaptable to changing network conditions along with emerging threats. In order to continue to boost information security, upcoming advances are expected to incorporate characteristics like transparent artificial intelligence, federated learning, and real-time data integration strategies.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Predicting Trends and Personalizing User Experience in E-Commerce
authors: Ganesh Kanna Jegannathan, Pallavi Singh, Dushyant Singh, Rohit Abhimalla, Sonal Saxena, Rohit Bansal
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: E-commerce platforms have an important problem of precisely forecasting consumer trends and customising user experiences to enhance engagement and sales. Current methodologies frequently fail to accurately represent complicated user behaviours and multi-faceted data. This study introduces an innovative hybrid deep learning framework that integrates convolutional neural networks (CNN), recurrent neural networks (RNN), and attention processes to overcome these constraints. This integrated approach enables the framework to outperform baseline models, including LSTM, CNN-only, and collaborative filtering techniques, in trend prediction and personalised recommendations. Experimental results indicate a Mean Squared Error (MSE) of 0.012, a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.025, and a ܴ<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value of 0.95, with precision, recall, and F1-score metrics higher than 0.92. These improvements result in increased user engagement, greater advertising effectiveness, and streamlined inventory management for e-commerce enterprises. The suggested approach provides a scalable and effective mechanism for enhancing personalised e-commerce services, surpassing conventional methods and facilitating further research in real-time and context-aware personalisation.

title: Neuro-Causal Multimodal Transformer for Interpretable Arrhythmia Subtyping in Congenital and Pediatric ECG with Intracardiac Fusion
authors: Al Mamun, Nayun Mia, Trishna Podder, Isha Das, Jarin Subah
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Automated arrhythmia subtyping in pediatric and congenital heart disease (CHD) patients is challenging. We propose a Neuro-Causal Multimodal Transformer (NCMT) that, for the first time, fuses 12-lead surface ECG with 7-lead intracardiac electrograms (IEGM) for high-fidelity classification. Using a causal attention mechanism, our model ensures predictions are based only on past and present data. Evaluated on the Leipzig Heart Center dataset of 39 pediatric/CHD patients, our model achieved 93.4% accuracy and an F1-score of 0.912 in subtyping challenging arrhythmias like AVNRT, AVRT, and AFL, outperforming existing baselines. Integrated interpretability via SHAP and attention maps provides transparent, clinically relevant insights into the model’s decisions, establishing a framework for trustworthy AI in pediatric electrophysiology.

title: Emotional Analysis of a Subject using the GSR and ECG by Applied Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Akshaya D. Shetty, Usha Desai, Akash Saxena
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: In the analysis of the mental health disorder, the emotional responses play a very important role. The emotional signals directly reflect the Autonomic Nervous System. Here in this study two bio-signals such as Electrocardiogram (ECG) and Galvanic Skin Response (GSR) is used to extract the Four Emotions- Happy, Anger, Fear and Sad. The dataset consists of the raw physiological signals – ECG and GSR. Signal Quality is strengthened using the Band pass Butterworth filter of frequency from 0.5 Hz to 40 Hz. Machine Learning models such as Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Support Vector Machine, and Logistic Regression is applied. The ensemble learning using these models is done, trained and tested. Comparative analysis between the ensemble and deep learning is also carried out. From an ensemble model accuracy obtained is 92.81%. This model helps to do the effective multi class emotional analysis which is very helpful for the analysis of mental states of an individual.

title: Intelligent Crop Surveillence: A Machine Learning-Based Approach for Precision Agriculture
authors: Puneeth Thotad, Shanta S. Kallur, Amir Hamza, Medha Kudari, Anupama S Nandeppanavar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Agriculture is the backbone of India, sustaining millions of lives and playing a vital role in the nation's economy. One of the pressing challenges farmers faces is detecting crop diseases at an early stage before they spread and damage yields. To address this, we developed an AI-powered Agriculture Monitoring System that helps identify diseases in major crops like corn, wheat, sugarcane, rice, and potatoes. The system processes clear, high-resolution leaf images by enhancing features such as discoloration, texture changes, and unusual patterns to accurately detect symptoms of disease. We curated a rich dataset consisting of 19 categories, covering both healthy and diseased crop conditions, and trained the system using five different deep learning models—VGG16, EfficientNet-B3, MobileNet-V2, EfficientNet-B0, and ResNet-50—applying a multi-head approach where separate models were trained for each crop and then integrated into a unified solution. Unlike traditional platforms, our system runs on a user-friendly web interface developed with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript on the frontend and Django (Python) for the backend. Additionally, it features an intelligent chatbot built using Hugging Face and Groq, offering real-time assistance and tailored recommendations. Farmers can upload images and receive instant results along with treatment suggestions like the best pesticides to use, preferred soil types, and preventive care. Performance evaluation through accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score showed reliable results, making this system a practical and accessible tool that empowers farmers with timely insights and supports smarter, more sustainable farming practices. We achieved the highest accuracy with VGG-16 techniques, about 94% in crop disease detection.

title: Simulation of E91 Quantum Key Distribution Protocol Using Qiskit
authors: Naheen Mohd. Kadir, Md. Ali Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: We use the Qiskit quantum computing framework to simulate the E91 Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) protocol. By measuring entangled photon pairs, the E91 protocol uses quantum entanglement to safely transfer cryptographic keys between Alice and Bob. In order to investigate the key generation rates, Bell’s inequality violation, and the protocol’s resilience to eavesdropping, we simulate the protocol in this work using IBM’s quantum simulator. The findings demonstrate that the E91 protocol may effectively create a secure key by taking advantage of quantum entanglement and identifying any eavesdropping attempts by breaking Bell’s inequality, with a calculated Bell’s parameter of S = 2.47, exceeding the classical limit which is 2. Additionally, key extraction is shown to be possible by the analysis of measurement results, where the key creation rate improves with the number of entangled particles, achieving a key generation rate of 1.59 bps for 25 singlet states, 5.37 bps for 100 singlet states, 10.52 bps for 250 singlet states, and 12.24 bps for 500 singlet states, with corresponding key lengths of 7, 25, 57, and 106 bits, respectively.

title: Hybrid Ensemble Learning for Liver Disease Classification Using CatBoost and Random Forest Models
authors: Dr.Sesham Anand, M Sathi Ekambareesh, V T Ram Pavan Kumar M, Dr.K Rajeshwar Rao, Dr.Shaik Janbhasha, Siva Kumar Pathuri
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Liver disease is a significant global health problem and early identification is the key feature in treatment of such diseases with better outcomes. The proposed study aims at developing a well-stacked ensemble learning technique to predict liver diseases with the structured medical data containing features relating to clinical and biochemical sets. The model also combines two very strong classifiers Random Forest (RF) and CatBoost with each other, which are used to resolve the complex interaction between features and enhance the stability of prediction. To increase the generalizability, overcome overfitting, and ease the understanding of the proposed model, the k-fold cross-validation was used on a benchmark liver disorders dataset to train and test the model. The experimental outcomes reveal that the RF-CatBoost ensemble recorded a classification accuracy of 96.8%, which set it above the traditional models including Decision Tree and Support Vector Machine (SVM) achieved 91% and 88% respectively. Performance was also corroborated through the precision, recall, F1-score and area under the receiver operating characteristic by showing that the ensemble performed better than its counterparts. The findings show that the model has the capability to handle imbalance and intricate feature associations hence is an effective tool of diagnosing early liver diseases as well as clinical decision support.

title: A Hybrid BERT-T5 Model for Improved Disease Classification and Personalized Treatment Recommendations in Healthcare
authors: Lakshmanan M, Rakshita R, Ronak Das, Sanjay Kumaran, Vyom Soni
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The rapid advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) have significantly impacted healthcare, particularly in the areas of disease prediction and personalized treatment recommendations. Transformer-based models like BERT and T5 have shown promising results in medical applications, with BERT excelling in disease classification due to its bidirectional attention mechanism, which allows it to understand complex medical texts and extract relevant information. However, BERT's inability to generate natural language responses limits its use in treatment recommendation systems. On the other hand, T5 excels in generating human-like, coherent text, making it suitable for personalized treatment planning. Despite this, T5 is prone to hallucinations, occasionally generating irrelevant or inaccurate recommendations. This paper proposes a hybrid BERT-T5 model that combines the strengths of both architectures to overcome the limitations of individual models. The hybrid model uses BERT for precise symptom classification and T5 for generating personalized treatment plans. The experimental results show that the hybrid model outperforms the standalone BERT and T5 models in terms of accuracy, coherence, and consistency, achieving 91% accuracy in symptom classification and 87% accuracy in treatment recommendation generation. Additionally, the hybrid model reduces hallucinations to 5%, significantly enhancing the reliability of the generated outputs. These findings highlight the potential of hybrid transformer models for improving clinical decision support, offering a robust solution for personalized healthcare applications. The proposed model promises to enhance disease classification and treatment recommendation systems, contributing to better clinical decision-making and patient care.

title: Harnessing Deep Learning for Smart Diagnosis of Strawberry Leaf Scorch
authors: Sheikh Afaan Farooq, Ajatray Swagat Bhuyan, Richa Sharma, Parminder Singh
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Strawberry leaf scorch, largely stemming from the Diplocarpon earliana fungus, impacts strawberry plant foliage, potentially diminishing overall plant vigor and fruit production. The ailment usually manifests as small, reddish or purplish blemishes on the upper side of leaves, expanding and coalescing into scorched, desiccated zones. Affected leaves may exhibit curling, become fragile, and eventually decay, lessening photosynthesis and inducing premature leaf shedding. Scorch commonly flourishes in warm, moist environments, especially during extended durations of leaf dampness, for disease control, growers generally use cultivars bred for resistance, ensure suitable plant spacing with sufficient airflow by pruning, and, in most cases, utilize fungicides during stages vulnerable to infection. This investigation details a deep learning-based classifier crafted to differentiate between robust strawberry leaves and those affected by leaf scorch. Image data undergoes an initial denoising phase to boost visual clarity and maintain key visual details. These refined images then inform the training of a ResNet-50 architecture, which effectively discerns disease markers such as lesion structures and plant tissue compromise. The final classifier in this investigation exhibits good efficiency in identifying strawberry leaf scorch obtaining an accuracy of 94.41%. This study emphasizes the prospects of using deep learning technologies for assisting early disease recognition and improving crop health surveillance systems.

title: A Hybrid Feature Selection Based Heart Disease Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Shivam Chaurasiya, Rohit Bajaj
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Heart disease or cardiovascular disease (CVD) is a term used to refer to various diseases that involve the heart and blood vessels such as coronary artery disease, arrhythmias, and heart failure. It is a major health problem in the world, contributing a high percentage of the premature mortalities and disabilities across the world. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), poor diet, physical inactivity, smoking, and alcoholism have been noted as some of the lifestyle changes that have led to the rising prevalence of CVD. Moreover, comorbidities such as diabetes, hypertension and obesity also increase the risk. Heart disease is essential to detect early since it will allow the implementation of efficient intervention and management, which in turn can help to avoid death and lead a better life. Nonetheless, the conventional diagnostic procedures are typically costly, invasive, or resource-erudite, and therefore, they may post-pone or hinder the diagnosis, especially in the under-resourced clinical facilities. This paper will introduce a Machine Learning (ML)-powered heart disease prediction model focused on its performance and ease. One hybrid feature selection strategy is employed to identify the most useful clinical parameter. The different ML models are then trained on complete and reduced feature sets in order to determine their predictive accuracy. This comparison framework is aimed at finding an ideal trade-off between diagnostic accuracy and computational efficiency.

title: Autonomous Pole Climbing Robot for Real-Time Power Line Inspection and Fault Detection
authors: Indra Jit Barua, Muzahidul Islam Salman, Somen Dutta, Nazia Sultana Plabon
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Manual inspection of power lines remains hazardous, time consuming, and prone to human error, especially in high voltage environments. To address this, we designed and implemented an autonomous pole climbing robot equipped with a ZMPT101B voltage sensor and IoT enabled real time monitoring. The robot’s novel gripper mechanism ensured stable vertical mobility, while its sensor array achieved <5% error in voltage measurements across 10 trials. Remote operation via ESP8266 allowed seamless data transmission to a handheld controller. This system not only reduces risks for utility workers but also enhances grid reliability by enabling faster fault detection demonstrating a scalable, cost-effective alternative to traditional inspection methods.

title: ClimaCastNet: A Multimodal Forecasting Framework Leveraging Atmospheric Dehazing and Deep Ensembles for Predicting Heatwave and Cold Wave Events in India
authors: R.Prasanthi Kumari, Mahaveerakannan R, Cuddapah Anitha, Xma R Pote
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: ClimaCastNet is a novel multimodal forecasting framework designed to enhance the prediction of extreme temperature events such as heatwaves and cold waves in India. The framework integrates atmospheric dehazing techniques with deep ensemble learning to improve forecast accuracy under diverse climatic conditions. Existing forecasting models often struggle with degraded performance due to atmospheric noise, such as haze, dust, or fog, and typically lack robust spatiotemporal context modeling. These limitations hinder the ability to generate reliable early warnings, particularly in data-sparse or noisy environments. To overcome these issues, ClimaCastNet employs Spatiotemporal Attention-Based Graph Neural Networks (ST-GNNs) that capture both the spatial dependencies across different geographical locations and the temporal dynamics of climate signals. Atmospheric dehazing modules preprocess satellite imagery and environmental sensor data to reduce noise and enhance input quality. Deep ensembles further increase the robustness and uncertainty quantification of predictions. The proposed framework is used to forecast extreme temperature events across various Indian regions by fusing meteorological variables, remote sensing data, and historical climate patterns. Experimental results demonstrate that ClimaCastNet significantly outperforms existing baselines in accuracy and lead time, enabling more effective risk management and disaster preparedness. The model demonstrates particular strength in forecasting extreme events under low-visibility and noisy data conditions.

title: Predicting Agricultural Waste Biogas Potential in Anaerobic Digestion Using XGBoost Algorithm
authors: D. Muthukumaran, Sujitha R, M. Muthulekshmi, Ganne Naga Sujini, T.M. Nithya, Vasantharaj S
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The rising need for renewable energy has stimulated interest in agricultural waste as a medium for biogas generation via anaerobic digestion. This research uses the XGBoost algorithm to forecast biogas potential from agricultural waste. Data on essential factors like waste composition, moisture content, and digestate properties was gathered. The XGBoost model was trained on this dataset to identify predictive correlations and evaluate biogas output accurately. The findings demonstrate that the model attained a superior accuracy in predicting biogas potential, surpassing traditional techniques. The research illustrates the viability of using advanced machine learning methods to improve biogas output predictions. These insights may aid farmers and energy producers in enhancing waste management methods, promoting sustainable energy solutions, and reducing environmental effects. The results highlight the significance of incorporating machine learning into agricultural waste management for efficient biogas use.

title: Integrating Generative AI into Digital Transformation: A Unified Pilot-to-Production Framework for Key Economic Sectors
authors: Madan Mohan Ganapam, G Venkata Anjani Kumar, Moulika Kasa
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Generative AI (GenAI) is set to become the next big investment area for enterprises globally, but in most cases, when the proof of concept is promising, organizations are finding it difficult to get the proof of concept into production and into a system that delivers actual business value. In this paper presents a unified framework for scaling GenAI across healthcare, finance, retail, and manufacturing and validate the framework by exploring nontrivial scaling applications with industry clients. Eight 12 month longitudinal case studies plus a survey of 212 executives are employed for triangulation via technical telemetry, organizational-readiness metrics, and 10 year cost-benefit models. Our results show that (i) data platform maturity is the strongest accelerator of time until production (β=0.42,p<0.001), (ii) the adoption of ISO 42001's governance reduces 100 years ¼ severity incident risk and preserves as much as nine percent of net present value, and (iii) internal rate of return of median deployment is 41%, and payback takes a year or less for all deployments. There is a five level maturity ladder that ranges from ideation sandboxes to autonomous GenAI agents that links incremental capability building to gating KPIs and risk controls. Drawing from dynamic-capabilities theory, this study extends the theory by providing a specification of how they must co-evolve in order to convert transient pilots into durable advantage in the form of LLMOps, trust layers and "fusion teams." Guided by evidence based playbook, executives are equipped with concrete steps to make strategy, data fabric, culture and governance work hand in hand to take pilots out of the lab to production and get the cross-industry potential of GenAI unlocked.

title: Smart Resource Management in Cloud Infrastructure: A Survey on Orchestration, Scheduling, and Energy Optimization
authors: Sai Raghavendra Varanasi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The cloud computing infrastructures have grown into complicated and dynamical platforms that require excellent management, energy efficiency, and flexibility to dynamic conditions. This paper gives a detailed study of autonomic systems, cloud service models (SaaS, PaaS, IaaS), and core technologies (such as virtualization and containerization) on which cloud systems are built. It also investigates how cloud deployment models (public, private, hybrid, and community clouds) were classified, and how orchestration and task scheduling are important concepts in optimization of cloud resource usage. The orchestration technology such as Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC), Docker Swarm, and Kubernetes is used to automate the incorporation and organization of cloud services. Scheduling methods are subdivided into static scheduling, dynamic scheduling, heuristic scheduling, and real-time scheduling to increase the use and responsiveness of resources. Further, the paper explores energy optimization methods in data centers, in which time-varying resource allocation, real-time thermal management, and energy-based routing procedures have been discussed. Other approaches that are considered to mitigate power consumption are software-oriented like the virtualization and multicore architectures. The above-proposed study can be perceptively informative regarding how to obtain scalability, sustainability, and energy efficiency in contemporary cloud ecosystems through the autonomic management and intelligent orchestration strategies.

title: Predictive Analytics for Sepsis Progression Using ICU Patient Data and Machine Learning Models
authors: R. Shanthi, Meenakshi Sharma, Prerana Nilesh Khairnar, R. Banupriya, Rajalakshmi S, B. Jegajothi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: SEPSIS remains a critical healthcare challenge with high morbidity and mortality rates, necessitating early detection and intervention to improve patient outcomes. Sepsis remains a critical challenge in Intensive Care Units (ICUs), requiring timely prediction to improve patient outcomes. This study employs Gradient Boosting Machines (GBM) combined with SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) for predicting sepsis progression while ensuring interpretability. GBM, a robust ensemble learning method, is used for its superior predictive accuracy on structured clinical data. However, traditional ML models often function as "black boxes," limiting their practical applicability in clinical decision-making. To address this, SHAP is integrated to provide feature importance analysis, enhancing model transparency and aiding clinicians in understanding key predictors of sepsis. The research follows a structured methodology: (1) Handling missing data using imputation techniques such as mean/median imputation, forward/backward fill, and k-nearest neighbors (KNN) imputation; (2) Applying SHAP-based feature selection to identify the most relevant physiological and clinical indicators for early sepsis detection; (3) Addressing class imbalance using the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE); and (4) Evaluating the model's performance. Experimental results, conducted on the MIMIC-III dataset, demonstrate that SHAP- based feature selection enhances model interpretability while maintaining high predictive accuracy. The GBM model trained on selected features outperforms the full-featured model, achieving an AUC-ROC of 0.94 and an F1-score of 0.88. This study underscores the effectiveness of GBM-SHAP integration in clinical predictive modeling, offering a transparent, high-performance approach for sepsis progression prediction, ultimately aiding in early intervention and improved patient management in ICUs.

title: COMPAS 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
