title: DNGaussian++: Improving Sparse-View Gaussian Radiance Fields with Depth Normalization
authors: Jiahe Li, Jiawei Zhang, Xiaohan Yu, Xiao Bai, Jin Zheng, Xin Ning, Lin Gu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Synthesizing novel views from sparse views has achieved impressive advances with radiance fields, yet prevailing methods suffer from high consumption or insufficient refinement capability. This paper introduces DNGaussian, a depth-regularized framework based on 3D Gaussian Splatting, offering real-time and high-quality few-shot novel view synthesis at low costs. Our motivation stems from the remarkable advancement of recent 3D Gaussian Splatting, despite it will encounter a geometry degradation when input views decrease. In the Gaussian radiance fields, we find this degradation in scene geometry primarily lined to the positioning of Gaussian primitives and can be mitigated by depth constraint. Consequently, we propose a Hard and Soft Depth Regularization to restore accurate scene geometry under coarse monocular depth supervision while maintaining a fine-grained color appearance. To further refine detailed geometry, we introduce Global-Local Depth Normalization, enhancing the focus on small local depth changes. Although DNGaussian shows impressive performance, its patch-wise regularization obscures the inconsistency in cross-patch errors. Additionally, primitives can still be irreversibly trapped in local minima under sparse views, even if depth regularization is applied. In this paper, we propose an extended version, DNGaussian++. First, a Geometry Instance Regularizer is developed to enable depth regularization for continuous consistency by exploiting reliable instance-level depth cues. Leveraging the depth gradient guidance, we then propose a Depth-Guided Geometry Reorganization to address the aforementioned local minima problem with high representation efficiency. Extensive experiments show that DNGaussian++ exhibits state-of-the-art performance in multiple datasets and scenarios with high efficiency, and the broad applicability and effectiveness are verified on various backbones and tasks.

title: Multi-Expert Learning Framework with the State Space Model for Optical and SAR Image Registration
authors: Wei Wang, Dou Quan, Ning Huyan, Chonghua Lv, Shuang Wang, Yunan Li, Licheng Jiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) image registration is crucial for multi-modal image fusion and applications. However, several challenges limit the performance of existing deep learning-based methods in cross-modal image registration: (i) significant nonlinear radiometric variations between optical and SAR images affect the shared feature learning and matching; (ii) limited textures in images hinder discriminative feature extraction; (iii) the local receptive field of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) restricts the learning of contextual information, while the Transformer can capture long-range global features but with high computational complexity. To address these issues, this paper proposes a multi-expert learning framework with the State Space Model (ME-SSM) for optical and SAR image registration. Firstly, to improve the registration performance with limited textures, ME-SSM constructs a multi-expert learning framework to capture shared features from multi-modal images. Specifically, it extracts features from various transformations of the input image and employs a learnable soft router to dynamically fuse these features, thereby enriching feature representations and improving registration performance. Secondly, ME-SSM introduces a state space model, Mamba, for feature extraction, which employs a multi-directional cross-scanning strategy to efficiently capture global contextual relationships with linear complexity. ME-SSM can expand the receptive field, enhance image registration accuracy, and avoid incurring high computational costs. Additionally, ME-SSM uses a multi-level feature aggregation (MFA) module to enhance the multi-scale feature fusion and interaction. Extensive experiments have demonstrated the effectiveness and advantages of our proposed ME-SSM on optical and SAR image registration. Specifically, ME-SSM improves the correct matching rate (CMR) by 7.14% and 1.95% based on thresholds 1 and 3, respectively, on the SEN1-2 dataset, and increases the CMR by 2.12% based on threshold 3 on the OS dataset. The code is available at https://github.com/Miraitowa515/ME-SSM.

title: A new method for extracting typical diseases of highway asphalt pavement in high-resolution images based on YOLO network
authors: Xinxiu Zhang, Qin Zhu, Zhihua Zhang, Shuwen Yang, Hui Xu, Fukui Zhang, Li Hou, Jinhao Cai, Yiqian Ma, Lujia Zhao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The exponential expansion of global highway infrastructure has intensified challenges in road inspection and maintenance operations, creating substantial demand for advanced automated interpretation systems. Current methodologies for detecting typical asphalt pavement distresses in high-resolution imagery demonstrate critical limitations in processing efficiency and detection accuracy, failing to meet contemporary requirements for highway maintenance. This study presents an innovative YOLO-based framework for automated detection of asphalt pavement pathologies in high-resolution images. Utilizing high-resolution pavement distress imagery acquired through specialized linear array camera systems as primary data sources, we implemented precise annotation of pavement deterioration patterns using LabelMe image annotation software. This process generated comprehensive data labels detailing spatial distributions and morphological characteristics of asphalt pavement pathologies. Through strategic application of image segmentation enhancement algorithms and standardized graphic segmentation techniques, we developed two specialized datasets: a Multi-Scale Multi-Disease (MSMD) dataset and a Single-Disease Semantic Segmentation (SDSS) dataset. The experimental design incorporated a Stratified-Shuffle-Splitting methodology to ensure non-repetitive, stratified random partitioning of datasets. This approach maintains feature distribution consistency between training and testing subsets while guaranteeing complete data segregation. Meanwhile, inter-rater reliability for the sample set was examined using the kappa coefficient. We conducted comparative analysis of five successive YOLO iterations (v8-v12), implementing progressive multi-scale incremental training protocols. Empirical results demonstrate that our incremental learning strategy enables continuous model performance enhancement, with YOLOv11 emerging as the optimal architecture for automated pavement distress detection. The YOLOv11 implementation achieved state-of-the-art performance metrics: an F1-score of 0.86 at confidence threshold 0.497, coupled with precision-recall metrics reaching 0.874 mAP@0.5. These results satisfy practical engineering requirements for automated pavement assessment, showing advantages in computational efficiency and detection accuracy. The proposed framework demonstrates significant potential for revolutionizing current pavement maintenance practices through AI-powered automated interpretation systems.

title: Effect of DBD Plasma Deposition of Fluorine Elements on the AFEP Insulation Performance
authors: Zhang Zhaohua, Xia Guowei, Gao Leixin, Qiao Chunying, He Ziming, Luo Liheng, Xie Jun, Xie Qing
abstract: Gas-insulated switchgear (GIS) is a key piece of equipment in ultrahigh-voltage (UHV) substations. As its core component, the performance of the insulation rod directly determines the operational reliability of the equipment. Aramid fiber-reinforced epoxy resin composite (AFEP) material, due to its excellent insulation and mechanical properties, has become an ideal material for insulation rods. However, aramid fibers have low surface activity and poor interfacial bonding with epoxy resin, which can easily induce partial discharges. Therefore, this article proposes a dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) plasma element deposition modification scheme. Using CF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> as a precursor, aramid fibers were modified through the physical etching and chemical grafting effects of plasma. The influence patterns and regulation mechanisms of plasma fluorine element deposition on the interfacial bonding characteristics and insulation properties of AFEP were studied. The results show that plasma can graft fluorine-containing groups onto the surface of aramid fibers, forming a fluorinated layer. This enhances the interfacial bonding strength through chemical bonding and physical anchoring effects, with the interlaminar shear strength (ILSS) increasing by up to 45.19%. Meanwhile, fluorination is beneficial for increasing trap energy levels and significantly improving the breakdown field strength, with a maximum increase of 71.1%. Combined with molecular dynamics simulations, the mechanism by which fluorine element deposition affects insulation properties was revealed.

title: Energy-Distribution-Based Parameter Screening of Corona Discharge Reactors: Achieving Efficient Trimethylamine Removal With Minimal Ozone per Removal Unit_supp1-3657532.docx
authors: Jishen Zhang
abstract: Volatile organic compounds (VOCs) tend to accumulate in the confined, humid environment of household refrigerators, posing health risks. Trimethylamine (TMA), an odorant generated from decaying meat, is designated as the indicator gas for deodorization performance by the Chinese national standard GB 21551.4-2010. Nonthermal corona discharge is effective in decomposing TMA but inevitably produces ozone, a byproduct whose emission varies with reactor configuration and operating conditions. To address this, a physics-informed, reduced-order descriptor based on the mean electron energy distribution in the discharge region is proposed to guide reactor design. A 2-D plasma calculation is used to obtain the mean electron energy distribution. Two energy thresholds are introduced to define an effective energy region <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$S_{1}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> (where the mean electron energy exceeds the threshold relevant to TMA bond scission) and an excess energy region <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$S_{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> (where the mean electron energy exceeds the threshold associated with ozone propensity). Their area ratio <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\eta = S_{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$S_{1}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> is introduced as an indicator for ozone propensity. Numerical calculations are conducted for 120 configurations in wire-plate and needle-ring reactors to obtain <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\eta $</tex-math> </inline-formula> values. The selected configurations with stable corona discharge were tested for TMA degradation rates (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V_{1}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) and ozone generation rates (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V_{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), and a performance factor <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha = V_{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V_{1}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> is defined. A strong correlation (adjusted <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2} =0.9682$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) between <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\eta $</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha $</tex-math> </inline-formula> supports the descriptor’s validity. Considering both calculation and experimental results, the wire-plate reactor outperforms the needle-ring reactor. The wire-plate reactor with 0.1-mm wire diameter, 10-mm gap, and 9-kV voltage achieves the lowest <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha $</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\eta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>, identifying it as the optimal configuration. The proposed framework enables rapid, low-cost optimization of corona-discharge deodorization systems for confined spaces, such as household refrigerators.

title: SynLiTS: Phase Prompting-Driven Diffusion Synthesis and Context-Aware Fusion for Unaligned Liver Tumor Segmentation
authors: Shiyun Chen, Li Lin, Zhicheng Jin, Pujin Cheng, Jianjian Chen, Haidong Zhu, Kenneth K. Y. Wong, Xiaoying Tang
abstract: Given the rich and complementary information contained in multi-phase CT images (CTs), they play an indispensable role in liver cancer diagnosis and prognosis, wherein an important prerequisite is liver tumor segmentation. However, spatial misalignments across phases and the limited availability of high-quality multi-phase CT datasets significantly hinder the performance of liver tumor segmentation. To tackle these challenges, we here propose SynLiTS, a novel multi-phase liver tumor segmentation framework. Its core idea is to synthesize multi-phase CTs with strictly-aligned liver tumors based on pseudo-normal multi-phase CTs. Specifically, an FFC-based Inpainter is first designed to generate pseudo-normal CTs by reconstructing dilated liver tumors. The pseudo-normal CTs and randomly generated tumor masks are then combined via a phase prompting-driven diffusion model to synthesize multi-phase liver tumor CTs with diverse tumor characteristics. In this way,multi-phase CTs with perfectly-aligned liver tumor labels are obtained. We also construct a real multi-phase liver tumor dataset, named MPLiTS. Finally, the synthesized and real multi-phase CTs are used to train a liver tumor segmentation model, which incorporates a context-aware fusion module to effectively learn and integrate multi-phase information. SynLiTS is evaluated on both internal and external datasets, and the results show that it outperforms state-of-the-art methods by large margins. Code will be released at https://github.com/Chyiun/SynLiTS.

title: Resource-Efficient and Layer Interdependence-Aware CNN Pruning Leveraging Filter Replacement_supp1-3658318.pdf
authors: Sadegh Tofigh
abstract: Convolutional neural network (CNN) pruning has traditionally relied on heuristically designed importance criteria, often leading to limited generalizability and inconsistent performance. In this article, we propose a novel framework centered around filter replacement (FR), introducing pruning as a process of replacing selected filters with zero filters. Through a rigorous analysis, we derive an upper bound on the absolute error in the output of the subsequent layer and use this bound to define an efficient importance function. This importance function exhibits $\gamma $ -weakly submodular properties, enabling the development of a simple, low-complexity, and data-free oblivious algorithm for selecting filters to prune. In addition, we extend the FR framework to include nonzero filter alternatives, leveraging a best-approximation technique to construct optimal replacements for the pruned filters. Extensive experiments on benchmark networks and datasets validate the effectiveness of our method. The proposed approach achieves state-of-the-art results, with a complexity comparable to basic techniques such as $l_{2}$ -norm pruning. Notably, our pruning method achieves 76.52% accuracy (ACC) in ResNet-50 on the ImageNet dataset, surpassing the baseline of 75.15%, while reducing network parameters by 25.5%. Our proposed resource efficiency (RE) metric assesses that the layer interdependence-aware pruning (LIAP) method is up to $10^{11}$ times more efficient than existing techniques, setting a new standard for resource-aware CNN pruning.

title: A Multiobjective Evolutionary Algorithm Based on Bipopulation With Uniform Sampling for Neural Architecture Search_supp1-3659508.pdf
authors: Yu Xue
abstract: Neural architecture search (NAS) automates neural network design, improving efficiency over manual approaches. However, efficiently discovering high-performance neural network architectures that simultaneously optimize multiple objectives remains a significant challenge in NAS. Existing methods often suffer from limited population diversity and inadequate exploration of the search space, particularly in regions with extreme complexity values. To address these challenges, we propose a multiobjective evolutionary algorithm (MOEA)-BUS, an innovative MOEA based on bipopulation with uniform sampling for NAS, aimed at simultaneously optimizing both accuracy and network complexity. In MOEA-BUS, a novel uniform sampling method is proposed to initialize the population, ensuring that architectures are distributed uniformly across the objective space. Furthermore, to enhance exploration, we deploy a bipopulation framework where two populations evolve synergistically, facilitating comprehensive search space coverage. Experiments on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet demonstrate MOEA-BUS’s superiority, achieving top-one accuracies of 98.39% on CIFAR-10, and 80.03% on ImageNet. Notably, it achieves 78.28% accuracy on ImageNet with only 446 M MAdds. Ablation studies confirm that both uniform sampling and bipopulation mechanisms enhance population diversity and performance. In addition, in terms of Kendall’s tau coefficient, the SVM achieves an improvement of at least 0.035 compared to the other three commonly used machine learning models, and uniform sampling provided an enhancement of approximately 0.07.

title: A View-Oriented Skeleton Generation Method for Improving Multi-Table Text-to-SQL Translation Accuracy
authors: Hongyong Zhou, Tian Tian, Zihe Duan, Zesan Liu, Dadi Wang, Xiaowu Zhang
abstract: Recently, research on natural language to structured query (Text-to-SQL) has advanced rapidly, aiming to enable models to automatically translate natural language queries into executable SQL statements. When handling certain categories of multi-table queries, specifically those that can be abstracted as single‑table or natural‑join views, models must not only generate SQL syntax but also correctly infer join relationships. To substantially reduce generation difficulty and improve translation accuracy for these query types, this paper proposes a view‑level semantic abstraction‑based SQL generation method (View‑SQL). It first generates an SQL skeleton on a pre-built view, after which a rule-based module completes the table names and the FROM clause to produce the final executable SQL statement. To achieve this, View-SQL introduces two collaborative processing paths. The first is the View Path (Text2SQLSkeleton), where a View Classifier determines which type of view the natural language query corresponds to, and a T5-based decoder generates the SQL skeleton for that view. The second is the Non-View Path (Text2SQL), which is invoked when a query cannot be mapped to any existing view. In this path, the model adopts BERT and multi-head attention mechanisms to select relevant tables and columns before generating the SQL statement. Through this hierarchical framework, View-SQL effectively reduces the complexity of multi-table inference. Experimental results demonstrate that on our validation split, since the Spider test set is not publicly available, View-SQL achieves accuracy improvements of 1.55% and 2.13% on the Spider-syn and Spider EM datasets, respectively.

title: Resonant Beam Multi-Target DOA Estimation
authors: Yixuan Guo, Qingwei Jiang, Mingliang Xiong, Wen Fang, Mingqing Liu, Qingqing Zhang, Shuaifan Xia, Qingwen Liu, Gang Yan
abstract: With the increasing demand for internet of things (IoT) applications, especially for location-based services, how to locate passive mobile targets (MTs) with minimal beam adjustment has become a challenge. Resonant beam systems are considered promising IoT technologies with advantages such as beam self-alignment and energy concentration. However, resonant systems are difficult to apply to multi-user scenarios due to co-frequency interference. To establish a resonant system for multi-target localization, this paper designs an innovative resonant system architecture based on frequency division multiple access (FDMA), which enables a base station (BS) to establish connections with multiple mobile targets (MTs) with different carriers, and establishes a multi-channel cyclic model through a retro-directive array (RDA) to achieve one-to-many electromagnetic wave propagation and MTs direction of arrival (DOA) estimation through echo signals. Simulation results show that the proposed system supports resonant establishment between the BS and multiple MTs. This helps the BS maintain high DOA estimation accuracy when faced with multiple passive MTs, ensuring that the DOA error is less than 1° within a range of 5 m and 50° field of view, and the accuracy is higher than that of active beamforming localization systems under the same conditions.

title: Towards Self-Optimizing 6G Networks Through Network Digital Twin Intelligence: A Real Network Traffic Evaluation
authors: John Sengendo, Fabrizio Granelli
abstract: Driven by advancing digital ecosystems, the next-generation network vision promises unprecedented levels of intelligence, adaptability, and automation. Key 6G properties such as native artificial intelligence integration, a highly cognitive architecture, ultra-low latency, high-dense connectivity, and self-optimization require automated algorithms and intelligent enabler technologies. Network Digital Twins (NDTs) are a key enabler for mobile networks, due to their capability in replicating physical network operations. In this letter, we discuss how NDTs enable AI-native and self-optimizing networks. Leveraging real network traffic data, our framework achieved up to 98.3% voice/SMS traffic prediction accuracy, self-optimized load reduction, improved energy efficiency, and anomaly detection.

title: Frequency-Enhanced Dual-Stream Parallel Network for Multienergy Load Forecasting
authors: Ziyi Wang, Siyi Zhou, Xingyu Liang, Min Xia, Jun Liu
abstract: Accurate and efficient multienergy load forecasting is fundamental for the secure and economic operation of integrated energy systems (IESs). However, current methods face several critical challenges as follows. 1) Insufficient perception of low-energy transient features leads to significant deviations in marginal scenario predictions. 2) Existing architectures struggle to simultaneously capture long-term trends and short-term dynamics efficiently. 3) Many high-accuracy models suffer from high computational complexity, limiting their practical deployment. To overcome these limitations, we propose the frequency-enhanced seasonal-trend recurrent neural network (FESTRNN), which incorporates two newly designed modules: the energy-enhanced spectral module to amplify critical frequency signals, and the dual-stream collaborative temporal forecasting module to efficiently model multiscale temporal dependencies. Experimental results on the ASU dataset demonstrate that FESTRNN achieves state-of-the-art accuracy across various forecasting horizons with significantly lower computational costs. This study provides a reliable basis for real-time scheduling in IES, offering strong practical value.

title: Decomposition of SF6-free Insulation Gases Due to Partial Discharges
authors: Ueli Straumann, Lise Donzel, Martin Seeger, Saskia Buffoni
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Suitable SF₆-free insulation gases, such as mixtures with C4-fluoronitrile, should not only perform well regarding insulation and arc quenching, but also fulfill other requirements, such as stability against decomposition. One source for decomposing gases comes from partial discharge activity, which may be present in technical applications due to defects, such as free conductive particles. In this paper, an experiment series is presented, which subjects insulation gases to corona discharges. Different gas mixtures at different pressures were exposed to high voltage DC corona discharges for several weeks to determine the charge-based C₄F₇N decomposition by-product formation rates. The experiment is first assessed theoretically regarding expected decomposition rates and ion currents. Then measurement results are presented, covering decomposition and decomposition by-product formation rates. The decomposition rate in (mol/C) can be used to estimate the impact of partial discharge activity on the C₄F₇N concentration for real high voltage equipment contaminated with particles. Even for pessimistic scenarios, the C₄F₇N depletion over a 40-years’ time horizon is marginal and does not affect the performance of the switchgear.

authors: Daniel Joseph Ringis

title: Design of Mental Health Prediction and Analysis System using Machine Learning and IOT
authors: Pankaj H. Chandankhede, Kanchan S. Vaidya, Ankush D. Kadu, Sanjay S. Khonde, Vipin Kamble, Sagar Bahad
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The "Mental Health Using EEG" system is an innovative approach designed to assess an individual’s mental well-being by integrating sophisticated sensor technology with AI-based analysis. This system employs an EEG sensor to monitor brain wave activity, coupled with SPO2 and heartbeat sensors to measure blood oxygen levels and heart rate, creating a holistic profile of physiological indicators. Data is processed through an ESP32 microcontroller and transmitted to cloud storage (Firestore) for AI-powered analysis, aimed at detecting potential mental health conditions such as stress, anxiety, or depression. Real-time updates are displayed on an LCD, supporting continuous monitoring and early identification of mental health concerns. Cloud integration provides scalable data storage and access, while AI analysis offers actionable insights for proactive mental health management, paving the way for personalized, preventative care.

title: A Deep Learning Based Framework for Health Condition Detection in Budgerigars Using CNN
authors: Md Minhaz Uddin, Md. Shihab Uddin Chy., Muhammad Mubinur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Budgerigars are one of the most common pet birds around the world. It is a small and attractive bird, often kept for companionship. There is a global trend of keeping this bird as a pet. These birds require a lot of care to avoid becoming sick. Unfortunately, budgerigars can fall sick in many ways and, in some cases, may even die. In this study, we have focused on detecting the health condition of budgerigars. We have used several pre-trained neural network (CNN) models to classify bird health status into normal and abnormal categories. Among the classification models, EfficientNet-B0 has performed the best, achieving an accuracy of 92.71%. Finally, we have developed a simple web application to classify the bird’s health condition based on user-uploaded images.

title: AI-Driven Product Ranking: A DistilBERT-Based Solution for Contextual E-Commerce Queries
authors: Thippaluri Yahid Basha, Vissakodeti Lakshmi Lokeswar, Yelisetti Sandeep
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Efficient product ranking is an essential component of improving the search experience on e-commerce websites. This research presents a relevance-based ranking system, which utilizes a fine-tuned DistilBERT model, especially optimized for contextual query-product matching. The model, trained on the Amazon KDD dataset, which consists of more than 571,223 training samples and 79,337 test samples, shows its capability to evaluate relevance between user queries and product descriptions effectively. To adapt the pre-trained DistilBERT model for multi-class classification tasks, a systematic fine-tuning methodology was employed. Prior to model training, comprehensive preprocessing was undertaken, with a primary focus on concatenating product attributes to uphold and enhance data quality. Experimental results reflect 76.05% accuracy and 0.7515 as a weighted F1-score that marks the performance of the model regarding finding and ranking contextually relevant products. This strategy, by providing a scalable, customized e-commerce experience, highlights the potential transformer-based models can bring to the redesign of the product search and recommendation system. To address cross-domain adaptation and additional optimization, limitations and future work are mentioned.

title: Neural-Inspired AI Systems for Predictive Neuroscience
authors: Selvapandian, Sandeep Kumar, P. A. Deshkar, Dipesh, Indrajitkumar Parmar, Ankur Garg
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The use of neural-inspired AI systems enables the identification of complex brain activity patterns using neural networks. Neurology and AI are working to anticipate mental health, neurological illnesses, and cognitive decline. This AI models could revolutionize our understanding of the brain by replicating its complicated neuronal processes. This model enables early diagnosis in neuroscience, personalized therapy, and predictive modeling. Recent advances in deep learning, notably brain-based neural networks, have advanced predictive neuroscience. These systems can uncover hidden patterns in massive amounts of brain data, making it easier to predict illness progression, brain aging, and the efficacy of therapy. Neural-inspired AI systems, which utilize RNNs, CNNs, and reinforcement learning, can more accurately forecast neurological disorders, including Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, and schizophrenia. AI and neuroscience could enhance brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) by simplifying neural data processing. Patients with neurological diseases would benefit from more flexible communication. It isn't easy to create AI models that explain things and connect what computers guess to what living creatures understand. This article discusses neuron-based AI systems, predictive neuroscience, and brain health checks.

title: Performance Analysis on Diabetic Retinopathy Detection Using Machine Learning
authors: Husna Tabassum, Najmusher H, Manjushree K, Seema Shivapur, B. Mary Nathisiya, Swarnamba S
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy is a high-risk diabetes disease that affects the retina. The condition is brought on by the blood vessels in the soft tissues at the rear of the eye becoming damaged. The early stages of diabetic retinopathy may be symptom-free or only cause modest visual problems. However, it may lead to blindness, which can be identified with the aid of machine learning (ML), a subset of artificial intelligence (AI) that includes deep learning (DL) as its subset, which is the capacity of a machine to act and solve problems in a manner similar to that of human intelligence. Medical professionals may use machine learning to identify, diagnose, focus on key information, and act to address a difficult scenario with an accurate conclusion in a short amount of time for retinal illnesses. In this work, a machine learning method that makes use of deep learning characteristics to identify diabetic retinopathy is examined (DR). High variability in fundus makes it difficult for classification, mainly in diabetic retinopathy, which involves retinal development of newly formed blood vessels and loss of vision. Hence, accurate retinal vascular analysis, which may be accomplished by retinal segmentation, is necessary to provide a precise result. The technique of automatically identifying blood vessel borders is called retinal segmentation. With an accuracy of up to 93.33%, the image classifier processes the region-merged features that are lost during segmentation.

title: A Hybrid Cryptographic Framework for Secure File Distribution with Multi-Factor Authentication and Comprehensive Audit Logging.
authors: Sankara Mahalingam M, N. Suresh Kumar, Pinnamaneni Praneeth, Gullapudi Lalith Kumar, Konda Sai Kiran, Kancheti Chaitanya
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The following paper details a filesharing web application executed in Flask. It has the primary aim of ensuring that sensitive data remain confidential, intact and can only be accessed by authorized individuals. This system provides AES like Fernet encryption to secure files uploaded on the system and only authorized users can decrypt in a secure fashion. Flask-Login authenticates the users with password hashing. Role-based access control limits file operations on the basis of user roles. The data and file metadata about the users is secure to keep them using SQLite and SQL Alchemy ORM. The application has a clean user interface designed using Bootstrap that user can easily interact with. A lightweight, modular solution addressing the various essential cybersecurity requirements of a small organization, an academic institution or a secure collaborative team environment. The practical implementation of the fundamental concepts of security can be noted in the project and can be extended to sensitive audit logging or hybrid encryption in future research.

title: Influence of Look-Back Window Size on Gated Recurrent Neural Networks for Global Forex Prediction
authors: Rakesh H.M, Harsha Ramireddy, Bhagyalakshmi K, Sudindra V R, Gurulingu P, Mohan Kumar Gajendran
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Selecting the input look-back window is a critical yet often under-examined design choice in short-horizon foreign exchange forecasting. This paper quantifies how look-back length affects one-day-ahead performance of gated recurrent networks across eight widely traded USD currency pairs from April 2017 to March 2025. Using only OHLC prices and log returns, we run a controlled grid of 160 experiments that crosses ten window lengths (1–252 trading days) with two architectures (LSTM and GRU) under a uniform rolling evaluation protocol, holding all other hyperparameters, scaling, and optimization settings fixed. Accuracy is assessed with mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) and R-squared. Three findings emerge. First, short windows dominate: windows of 1, 3, or 7 days yield the lowest MAPE in most configurations, with the 1-day window alone best in the majority. In terms of R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, short windows also generally yield the highest values, indicating that they explain the largest proportion of variance in next-day returns, with LSTM achieving top R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> on five pairs and GRU on three. Second, the optimal window is pair-specific: moderately longer context can help for smoother or more policy-managed pairs (for example, USD/CNY), while freely floating pairs (for example, USD/JPY) favor the shortest context. Third, longer windows (90–252 days) rarely help and often degrade accuracy. At their respective tuned windows, LSTM achieves the lowest MAPE on five of eight pairs, while GRU leads on three. Both metrics jointly point to the same broad conclusion that shorter look-backs are more effective. The results recommend treating look-back length as a first-class, per-instrument hyperparameter and caution against assuming that more historical context improves next-day forecasts.

title: Cyberbullying Detection for Textual, Image and Visual using Hybridization of BERT, ResNet-50 and C3C
authors: Suganya D, Srija G K K, Sowmiya T, Nisha Thirumurugan, Vidhya K
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Cyberbullying has become a significant issue on social media with the exploding visuals and videos. Many established detection systems based in text are capable of detecting abusive behavior, but they often lack the capacity to detect abusive behavior present in images, memes, or videos. It proposes a multimodal detection system using deep learning for cyberbullying detection. The system takes advantage of text, visual, and video-based features for cyberbullying detection, from an explicitly curated existing dataset of labeled abusive and non-abusive posts. Text was analyzed by Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT), images were analyzed by Residual Network with 50 Layers (ResNet-50), and videos by Convolutional Three-Dimensional (C3D). These features were extracted, aligned, normalized and fused as one representation to classify as abusive or non-abusive. The proposed fusion model was compared against unimodal baselines and resulted in accuracy 92.32%. The results were promising and one can conclude that visual-textual fusion models can improve detection of more subtle and implicit types of cyberbullying. If pairing with the right imaging model, the model could provide a feasible option for real-time applications, which illustrates the concept’s potential for use in automated moderation systems.

title: A Machine Learning Framework for Fault Detection in IoT Enabled Smart Sensor Networks
authors: Omprakash Gurrapu, Jami Venkata Suman
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The growth of Internet-of-Things (IoT) devices and smart sensors has transformed the data collection and automated decision-making capabilities in various fields, from industrial automation to environmental monitoring. However, with this rapid growth comes considerable risk due to vulnerabilities where spoofed sensors can place false data into mission-critical systems. Most traditional fault detection techniques rely on rule based thresholds or human-defined heuristic rules that cannot adapt to changing attack vectors and complex, high-dimensional datasets in the same way that a human reviews a practical environment. The contribution of this work is a unified machine learning framework for real-time fault detection in IoT-enabled smart sensor networks, using ensemble and deep-learning models constructed for deployment in cloud environments. The framework will include feature extraction, model training, and real-time inference and automated response modules that will allow for fast isolation and mitigation of anomalies. Evaluation of the approach on a simulated smart factory dataset shows that this approach is able to detect both abrupt and gradual sensor faults with an F1-score of 0.94, which is 12% improvement over baseline methods. Finally, the integration with cloud orchestration tools, allows for a scalable solution for the deployment of multiple model instances along with dynamic resource management for the purpose of ensuring low-latency responses at varying loads.

title: Design and Development of High Power Microwave Conical Corrugated Horn Antenna
authors: Mrunal A Marihal, Sourav Biswas, Shesh Kumar, P. Raja Ramana Rao, S. K. Datta
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: A conical corrugated horn antenna is the most efficient among the horn antenna family for producing an optimized radiation pattern. In this work, we design and simulate a conical corrugated horn antenna over X-band in CST software. The simulation finds a gain of 21.2dBi with 1.18 be a voltage standing wave ratio (VSWR). Following the analysis, the conical corrugated horn antenna is realized based on Aluminum. In the measurement, the antenna exhibits a gain of 20.25dBi and a VSWR of 1.08, i.e., nearly similar to the values obtained in the simulation. Our work suggests that such an antenna will be useful for high-power microwave applications.

title: Reconfigurable Dual-Band Multi-Mode Power Amplifier for 5G and Beyond Applications
authors: Ganesh Racha, K Lal Kishore
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Real-time communications require precise portable User Equipment (UE) to support text, voice, image, and video processing applications. The advancement in communications use different frequency bands and different communication modes with 1G/2G/3G/4G/5G and beyond generations to support multiple applications. The design of UE for supporting 5G and beyond requires the support of backward compatibility using 5G New Radio (NR) frequency bands. This UE device needs a design of a Power Amplifier (PA) with a better performance metrics. The Sub-6 GHz spectrum PA with dual band of 900-1800 MHz and 2.4-5 GHz is designed to support GSM, LTE, LTE Wi-Fi, Wi-Fi modes using 5G NR spectrum. This paper describes the design of a Dual-Band Multi-Mode PA with a reconfigurable structure of loading and tuning circuitry. With the aid of reconfigurability, the area of the design is reduced by using controlled logic. The proposed PA is designed for 5 dBm input power, a matching impedance load of 50 Ω by using sinusoidal input, Cadence EDA tools, and 45 nm CMOS Technology.

title: Development of Robust AI-Driven Framework for the Detection and Prevention of Loan Fraud in Banking Sector
authors: Pratik Badri, Kapil Kumar Goyal, Mohit Menghnani, Sumeet Mathur
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The growing sophistication of loan fraud in the financial sector calls for smart and data-reliant methods to catch and block such activity. Loan scams are becoming more common and endanger the stability and revenues of banks worldwide. The goal of this project is to improve financial fraud detection and prevention by using cutting-edge machine learning methods. After a number of models were put into practice and compared, the suggested Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) showed the best capacity to detect fraudulent transactions, with the greatest accuracy of 97.56%, as well as noteworthy precision and recall scores. These results demonstrate the model's impressive capacity to differentiate between valid and fraudulent transactions, while simultaneously decreasing the occurrence of false positives and increasing the detection rates. Findings also stress the significance of dealing with unbalanced datasets and picking appropriate characteristics to boost model efficacy. The work adds to their understanding of the benefits and drawbacks of various ML methods for detecting financial fraud by comparing older and more contemporary techniques. The findings can assist financial institutions and regulatory bodies in implementing more effective fraud prevention systems, thereby protecting assets and maintaining customer trust.

title: IoT- Based Smart 3-Phase Cable Fault Detection System with Distance Locator
authors: Rajesh Babu Damala, Vasupalli Manoj, Pavan Uttaravalli, Narayana Rao Balaga, Karthik Tamalapakula, Sai Geethika Yenuguthala
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: In modern power transmission systems, underground cables are increasingly preferred over overhead lines due to their enhanced safety, reliability, and resistance to environmental disturbances. However, locating faults within these cables remains a significant challenge, as traditional methods involve manual testing, physical inspection, and complex equipment, leading to increased downtime and maintenance costs. This project introduces an IoT-based smart fault detection system designed specifically for three-phase underground transmission lines. The system employs an Arduino microcontroller integrated with voltage sensors and an ESP8266 Wi-Fi module to continuously monitor voltage levels across all three phases. Upon detecting a voltage anomaly, the system accurately identifies the type of fault— such as line-to-ground (LG), line-to-line (LL), or three-phase faults—and calculates the distance of the fault from the power source using Ohm’s Law and the known resistance of the cable. The detected information is displayed locally on an LCD screen and simultaneously uploaded to an IoT platform like ThingSpeak or Blynk for real-time remote monitoring. This approach ensures rapid fault diagnosis, minimizes system disruption, and eliminates the need for manual fault tracing. The proposed solution significantly improves the efficiency and intelligence of power transmission networks, aligning with the vision of future-ready smart grid infrastructure suitable for both urban and remote areas.

title: Innovation Incentives under Technology Blockade: Strategic Game Insights from the Semiconductor Industry_supp1-3663310.pdf
authors: Mengya Li
abstract: Semiconductor industry has become the core domain of technological competition among countries nowadays. Suppliers with high-end chip technology, a key generic technology in the semiconductor industry, can significantly disrupt downstream product manufacturers and reshape the competitive dynamics by imposing a technology blockade. This paper focuses on high-end chip market, and builds a game model within a vertical supply chain structure to analyze how technology blockade affects the technological catch-up incentives for local chip suppliers. Our analysis reveals that before technology blockade, the monetary externality directs initial benefits to downstream manufacturers, diminishing high-end chip suppliers' rent extraction capacity. Local chip suppliers ' technology advancement reduces high-end chip suppliers' rent extraction capacity. This incentivizes high-end chip suppliers to talent foreclosure, and raises the cost of government support. The low-to-mid-end market plays an important role in accumulating innovation resource. After technology blockade, the chip supply chain is bifurcated into a high-end and a local mid-to-low-end chip supply chain. Government attention allocation intensity negatively moderates innovation policies intensity and, together with the low-to-mid-end market, alleviates the policies pressure from multiple dimensions. When the roles of high-end chip talents exceed a certain threshold, even if innovation policies are withdrawn, local chip suppliers can also be self-sufficient. As local chip supplier's technology catch-up, a new game situation gradually emerges in high-end market, characterized by the coexistence of the local and the existing high-end chip supply chain. The core findings are robust to the relaxed key assumptions, which substantiates the research rigour of this paper.

title: PCRNet: Progressive Correlation Refinement for Few-Shot Hyperspectral Image Classification_supp1-3663241.pdf
authors: Bin Fan
abstract: Few-shot hyperspectral image classification (FHSIC) is crucial for precise land cover identification when labeled samples are scarce, mitigating the prohibitive annotation costs associated with hyperspectral image (HSI) data. However, existing FHSIC methods primarily follow a feature refinement paradigm, which struggles to effectively model the holistic relational structure among samples. Meanwhile, the commonly used attention mechanism tends to focus solely on sample-specific local information, limiting its ability to capture global category knowledge and promote cross-domain generalization. To address these challenges, we propose an efficient Progressive Correlation Refinement Network (PCRNet). Our work pioneers a correlation refinement paradigm, shifting the objective from enhancing feature representations to explicitly reasoning upon the structured query-class relationships. Specifically, PCRNet first constructs a learnable generalized correlation matrix that encodes the inter-sample relationships. Then, we present a simple yet effective memory-guided global attention mechanism to facilitate the injection of category commonality priors into the relational structure from a holistic perspective. This is further combined with query-level adaptive modulation and symmetrical gated integration modules to achieve progressive refinement of the generalized correlation matrix. Finally, the refined correlation is projected as the classification logit, enabling accurate identification of land covers. Experiments on four benchmark HSI datasets demonstrate the state-of-the-art performance of our approach. We believe this new paradigm opens a promising direction for future FHSIC model design. Code is available at https://github.com/guoying918/PCRNet.

title: Improving ocean color atmospheric correction near clouds with neural network_supp1-3660269.docx
authors: Jilin Men
abstract: Atmospheric correction is a prerequisite for deriving accurate ocean color products from satellite observations. However, current atmospheric correction algorithms directly mask clouds and around area to avoid the influence of cloud adjacency effects (CAEs), which degrade the quality of satellite observations, limiting the spatial-temporal continuity of water quality monitoring. In this study, we introduce the CAEs-effected Atmospheric correction for Polar-orbit satellites (CAP) algorithm, demonstrated using MODerate resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer-Aqua (MODISA) as an example. CAP is a physically constrained data-driven approach designed to enhance atmospheric correction performance in the presence of CAEs. The algorithm integrates the physical foundation of the standard atmospheric correction scheme implemented in SeaDAS with the cloud-adjacency correction information extracted from GOCI data. The GOCI-based CAE features are transferred to MODISA spectral bands and jointly used for model training. Validation using independent testing datasets shows that CAP achieves band-averaged correlation coefficients (R) of 0.97 with minimal bias and significantly reduces relative percentage differences near cloud edges. CAP improves signal-to-noise ratios by up to 53.7%, particularly in the red bands, and increases the number of valid observations by ~40%. Global-scale composites confirm that CAP can generate Rrs fields consistent with the results of the standard atmospheric correction in SeaDAS without applying straylight masks, thereby recovering valuable observations previously discarded. By mitigating CAE-induced biases while maintaining accuracy in cloud-free conditions, CAP enhances the reliability of bio-optical retrievals and expands observational coverage for ocean color applications. The methodology of integrating geostationary and polar-orbit sensor data provides a transferable framework for future satellite missions and can be extended to other challenging atmospheric correction scenarios.

title: IEEE CS Career Center
authors: N/A

title: Fixing Drone Auth: A Practical and Secure, Blockchain-Enabled UAV Authentication Scheme_supp1-3663713.pdf
authors: Chang Xu
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly deployed in safety-critical and privacy-sensitive applications, including infrastructure inspection, precision agriculture, emergency response, and on-demand connectivity. However, UAVs' mobility and intermittent connectivity expose them to diverse security threats, making robust yet efficient authentication and key agreement (AKA) essential. We present PSB-UAKA, a <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">P</u>ractical and <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</u>ecure <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</u>lockchain-enabled <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">U</u>AV <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>uthentication and <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">K</u>ey <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>greement scheme. PSB-UAKA combines physical unclonable functions (PUFs) with fuzzy extractors to avoid maintaining a challenge–response pair (CRP) database while improving resilience to device-capture attacks. It further leverages Merkle-tree proofs for lightweight authorization verification and adopts session-bound pseudonym rotation to support unlinkability and tolerate desynchronization. We formalize a UAV-specific threat model and prove session authenticity and key indistinguishability in the Real-or-Random (RoR) framework. Our comparative analysis shows that PSB-UAKA provides roughly 25% broader security coverage than representative baselines, with notable gains in anonymity and desynchronization resilience. Experimental results indicate low computational overhead (below 0.066 ms on user devices and 1.436 ms on UAVs) and up to 54% reduction in UAV-side storage. Finally, extensive NS-3 simulations under realistic mobility and wireless conditions demonstrate lower authentication latency and stable throughput relative to state-of-the-art schemes, suggesting that PSB-UAKA is practical for real-world UAV deployments.

title: Reflected Group Delay of a Capacitively Coupled Resonator for Characterization of Dielectric Constant and Loss Tangent of Microstrip Substrates
authors: Gaurav Walia, Paul D. Laforge
abstract: This paper presents a novel method to determine dielectric constant and loss tangent of substrates used in microstrip circuits. The method is based on the reflected group delay of a microstrip resonator coupled to the input by means of an interdigital capacitor. The proposed method is first validated through simulations carried out using Advanced Design System by Keysight Technologies. The results obtained from simulations established that the proposed method can characterize the microstrip substrates having dielectric constant in the range of 1.8 to 7.2 at a loss tangent of 4×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-4</sup> with an error of less than 5% and 80% respectively. Final validation of the method is performed by the testing of Teflon and some standard boards from Rogers Corp. The error in the extracted values of dielectric constant and loss tangent of the boards used in testing are also found in agreement to the suggested values during simulation. Dielectric characterization of Acrylonitrile Butadiene Styrene (ABS) and Polylactic Acid (PLA) are also done to present a potential application of the proposed method in characterizing 3-D printing materials for microstrip applications.

title: CDFIT: A Transformer Using Cross-Modal Dual-Stream Feature Interaction for Multispectral Pedestrian Detection
authors: Zihao Huang, Wenshi Li, Yuzhen Zhang, Jiaren Guo, Jianyin Zheng, Guang Ji, Yanyun Tao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Modality imbalance is a significant challenge for multi-modal interaction at various depths in multispectral pedestrian detection under varying illumination environments. To overcome the limitations of current cross attention in addressing the modality imbalance, we propose the Cross-Modal Dual-Stream Feature Interaction Transformer (CDFIT). CDFIT capitalizes on the Transformer’s ability to learn long-range dependencies, extracting global intra-modal and inter-modal correlations during the feature interaction phase. Crucially, in order to effectively eliminate the interference of the self-attention within one modality to the alternative one, we propose horizontal and vertical correlation decoupling modes to divide and reassemble the attention maps in CDFIT. This facilitates more purified inter-modal attention while preserving relevant intra-modal self-attention, reducing the information interference. Meanwhile, in CDFIT, we expand Transformer into dual-stream pathways to align and assemble the information from RGB and thermal modalities across depths separately, thereby greatly enhancing the performance of multispectral object detection. Comprehensive experiments and ablation studies on benchmark datasets demonstrate that CDFIT achieves superior performance compared with state-of-the-art methods.

title: Zeroth-Order Federated Fine-Tuning for Large AI Models in Resource-Constrained Wireless Networks
authors: Tianle Wang, Yong Zhou, Yuanming Shi, Nan Cheng, Haibo Zhou, Hangguan Shan
abstract: Large artificial intelligence (AI) models have demonstrated impressive performance in a wide range of fields. Despite their versatility, adapting large AI models to specific downstream applications often requires fine-tuning on decentralized and privacy-sensitive data, posing significant challenges in resource-constrained wireless networks. In this paper, we propose a novel zeroth-order federated fine-tuning framework for efficient fine-tuning large AI models to alleviate the computation, communication, and memory bottleneck issues. Specifically, to address the computation limitation on edge devices, we adopt the split learning architecture, hosting the most computation-intensive component of the large AI model on the edge server. Besides, we employ a memory-efficient zeroth-order fine-tuning algorithm to further reduce the GPU memory consumption. Furthermore, we conduct a rigorous convergence analysis to illustrate how device scheduling influences the learning performance. Based on the analysis, we formulate a global loss minimization problem that jointly optimizes device scheduling, transmit power, and receive beamforming under the average transmission latency constraint. To tackle this complex mixed-integer nonlinear programming problem, we apply Lyapunov theory to break down the long-term optimization problem into multiple subproblems, followed by designing an effective online algorithm. Simulation results show that the proposed framework can lower the GPU memory consumption by up to 84% and GPU hours by 39% compared to the baselines, while achieving comparable accuracy.

title: Illicit object detection in X-ray imaging using deep learning techniques: A comparative evaluation
authors: Jorgen Cani, Christos Diou, Spyridon Evangelatos, Vasileios Argyriou, Panagiotis Radoglou-Grammatikis, Panagiotis Sarigiannidis, Iraklis Varlamis, Georgios Th. Papadopoulos
abstract: Automated X-ray inspection is crucial for efficient and unobtrusive security screening in various public settings. However, challenges such as object occlusion/overlap, variations in the physical properties of the items of interest, diversity in the types of X-ray scanning devices used, and limited training data hinder accurate and reliable detection of illicit items. Despite the large body of research works in the field, the reported experimental evaluation is often incomplete, while the derived outcomes are frequently conflicting. In order to shed light on the research landscape of this field and to facilitate further research, a systematic, detailed, and thorough comparative evaluation study of recent Deep Learning (DL)-based methods for X-ray object detection is conducted in this work. For achieving this, a comprehensive evaluation framework is developed, composed of the following building blocks: a) Six of the most recent, large-scale and widely used public datasets for X-ray illicit item detection (namely, OPIXray, CLCXray, SIXray, EDS, HiXray, and PIDray), b) Ten different state-of-the-art object detection schemes, covering all main categories present in the literature, including generic Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), custom (X-ray-specific) CNN, generic transformer and generic hybrid CNN-transformer architectures, and c) Various detection (mAP<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">50</sup> and mAP<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">50:95</sup> mean Average Precision (mAP)) and time/computational-complexity (inference time (ms), parameter size (M), and computational load (GFLOPS)) performance metrics. A thorough analysis of the computed experimental results leads to the extraction of critical observations and detailed insights, emphasizing on the following key aspects: a) Overall behavior of the various object detection schemes, b) Object-level detection performance investigation, c) Dataset-specific observations, d) Statistical variance and empirical reliability examination, and e) Time efficiency and computational complexity analysis. In order to support reproducibility of the reported experimental results and to promote research in the field, the evaluation framework code and model weights are publicly available at https://github.com/jgenc/xray-comparative-evaluation, while a broad set of precomputed results (for all employed detectors and datasets) can be found at https://jgenc.github.io/ceasefire_demo/#/.

title: Optimizing Human-Exoskeleton Physical Interaction Through Spatial Trajectory Adaptation_supp1-3663979.mov
authors: Arash Arami

title: MetaCap: Adaptive Meta-Optimization for Few-Shot Remote Sensing Image Captioning
authors: Kangda Cheng, Jinlong Liu, Rui Mao, Zhilu Wu, Erik Cambria
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Remote sensing image captioning (RSIC) aims to translate visual content in remote sensing imagery into semantically coherent natural language descriptions. It has broad application value in fields such as disaster emergency response and cross-modal retrieval. Existing fully supervised approaches rely heavily on large-scale annotated data. However, due to factors such as scene diversity and long-tailed category distributions, many remote sensing scenarios are associated with limited annotated samples, resulting in few-shot RSIC. To address the few-shot RSIC challenge, we propose MetaCap, a novel cross-modal meta-captioning framework. Conventional metalearning methods suffer from optimization instability and cross-modal alignment difficulties when confronted with large inter-class variations in remote sensing imagery. To overcome these limitations, we propose a parameter-level adaptive optimization strategy that dynamically learns per-parameter update factors and weight decay factors during the inner loop, enabling precise adaptation to varying texture complexity across visual features. Furthermore, a visual-semantic consistency constraint and an inner-loop trajectory averaging mechanism are incorporated to effectively mitigate the semantic gap. Experimental results on the RSICD, UCM-Captions, and Sydney-Captions demonstrate the superior generalization ability of MetaCap.

title: Sub-Aperture Averaged Minimum Norm Eigen-Canceler for Airborne Radar Clutter Suppression
authors: Zihao Liu, Yi Liu, Huayu Fan, Quanhua Liu, Lixiang Ren, Erke Mao
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Airborne radars are essential tools in geoscience remote sensing, where robust clutter suppression is critical for extracting weak geophysical signals and target returns. In practice, complex terrain frequently induces highly non-homogeneous clutter, which severely limits the independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.) training samples available for clutter covariance matrix (CCM) estimation, thereby degrading the performance of space-time adaptive processing (STAP). This letter proposes a Sub-aperture Averaged Minimum Norm Eigen-Canceler (SA-MNEC) to enhance clutter suppression under sample-limited conditions. An improved sub-aperture averaging scheme is developed to achieve robust sub-aperture CCM estimation by mitigating the influence of inter-channel amplitude-phase errors. The averaged CCM is then integrated into an MNEC framework to exploit the low-rank clutter structure, ensuring effective clutter rejection while minimizing performance loss caused by insufficient training data. Simulation and experimental results using real airborne radar data demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: A Hybrid Game Theory and Machine Learning Framework for Renewable Energy Allocation in EV Charging Systems
authors: A. Punitha, NMG Kumar, Hariharan K.S., Vivekanand Pandey, Sushil Deshpande, Pawan Mandal
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The rapid growth of electric vehicles (EVs) requires effective and equitable distribution of renewable energy to facilitate sustainable charging infrastructure. Current methodologies often display insufficient dynamic adaptability and equitable distribution, resulting in inefficiencies and strain on the grid. This study presents a hybrid framework combining game theory and machine learning to predict renewable energy supply through deep learning models and to strategically distribute energy among electric vehicles using game-theoretic optimisation. The connection facilitates proactive management of charge demands through precise projections and user behaviour modelling. Experimental findings indicate that the proposed system attains a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.82 kWh, a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 1.14 kWh, and a Jain’s Fairness Index of 0.96, exceeding baseline models like First Come First Serve and Round-Robin in terms of both efficiency and fairness. This method stabilises system load and improves customer satisfaction by guaranteeing equitable access to renewable energy. The framework offers a scalable, intelligent solution for future smart grid applications, addressing the significant difficulties of renewable energy fluctuation and electric vehicle demand instability, thus promoting sustainable energy management.

title: Concrete Compressive Strength Estimation through the Application of Machine Learning Techniques
authors: C. Ramya, B. Kavya Santhoshi, A. Sai Kumar, S. Sharmila, Achal Agrawal, Lidiya Lilly Thampi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Concrete compressive strength is a critical property that directly influences the durability and performance of concrete structures. This study develops a unified Machine Learning (ML) pipeline to accurately predict compressive strength using a benchmark dataset of material composition and curing parameters. The pipeline integrates robust preprocessing (missing value imputation, outlier removal, normalization), domain-specific feature engineering (e.g., water–cement ratio), and multiple ML models including Support Vector Machine (SVM), XGBoost, and CatBoost. Experimental results demonstrate that SVM achieves the highest classification accuracy (93.2%) and precision (93.4%), while ensemble models such as XGBoost and CatBoost yield superior generalization and ROC-AUC performance (0.9809 and 0.9803, respectively). This estimation ensures structural safety, optimizes material usage, reduces construction costs, and supports quality control, making it an essential step in sustainable and reliable infrastructure development.

title: AC Loss Property of a Two-dimensional Array of REBCO Superconducting Tapes
authors: Hiromasa Sasa, Yuuki Himeno, Takaemi Suenaga, Shun Miura, Hiroshi Miyazaki, Masataka Iwakuma
abstract: To design a safe superconducting motor with high power density for aircraft applications, an accurate estimation of the AC loss in superconducting windings is required. This study investigates the AC loss characteristics of a two-dimensional array of REBa2Cu3Oy tapes arranged in both the width and thickness directions, representing the cross-section of a motor winding. The AC loss was evaluated experimentally and numerically, with the key parameters being the number of tapes and the gap distance between adjacent superconducting layers. The AC loss behavior is expressed as a power function, a'bmγ', where bm is the normalized field amplitude, and a' and γ' are the fitting parameters. Both a' and γ' were found to follow an exponential approach with respect to the number of tapes in the width direction. Additionally, a' was found to be inversely proportional to the number of tapes in the thickness direction. For these properties, quantitative expressions were derived and confirmed to correspond to the measured and calculated results.

title: ViT-TST: A Transformer-Based Hybrid Model for High-Precision Alzheimer’s Disease Classification Using 3D MRI
authors: Mrutyunjaya S. Hiremath, Rajashekhar C. Biradar, Suvarna Nandyal, Shantala S. Hiremath, Sujith Kumar Sivanandan, Jayaprada S. Hiremath
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Alzheimer’s Disease (AD) continues to be a major global health concern, affecting over 55 million individuals and demanding scalable, accurate diagnostic solutions. While promising, traditional deep learning models for neuroimaging often suffer from limitations in multiclass discrimination, interpretability, and computational overhead. In this study, we propose ViT-TST, a novel hybrid architecture that integrates Vision Transformers (ViT) with Temporal Shifted Transformers (TST) for the high-precision classification of AD, Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI), and Cognitively Normal (CN) subjects using 3D structural MRI data from the ADNI dataset.The model leverages overlapping convolutional patch embedding, adaptive attention mechanisms, and convolutional enhancement layers to capture local neurodegenerative features and global spatial dependencies. A multi-view fusion strategy is employed across axial, sagittal, and coronal slices to enhance spatial robustness. Experimental results on a cohort of 1,460 subjects demonstrate a peak multiclass classification accuracy of 99.014%, surpassing the previous best model (97.14%) by 1.87%. Area Under the Curve (AUC) scores ranged from 0.846 to 0.944 across 7-fold cross-validation, while confusion matrix analysis confirmed minimal misclassifications. The proposed framework offers a powerful and interpretable tool for AD diagnosis, setting a new benchmark in neuroimaging classification.Future work includes extending the model with real-time inference capabilities, multimodal fusion (e.g., PET, cognitive scores), and cross-dataset validation using external sources like OASIS and AIBL to promote translational utility in clinical environments.

title: Improving Cloud Resource Utilization and Cost Efficiency Through Data-Driven ML Algorithms
authors: Anirudh Parupalli
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Cloud computing dramatically enhances digital resource delivery as workload patterns keep changing and hinder effective resource sharing. This research develops an efficient resource management solution using machine learning algorithms. This paper presents an innovative neural network-inspired paradigm of improving cloud resource deployment in data centers using sophisticated machine learning tools. The approach includes the pre-processing of the data of managed cloud resources, feature extraction, normalization of data, and neural network model production aimed at learning the optimal patterns of resource allocation. The suggested architecture shows excellent statistics with an R 2 of 0.9523, RMSE of 0.0257, and the MAE of 0.0195, outperforming the traditional methods of Linear Regression (85.6%), Support Vector Regression (78.6%), and LSTM networks (88.82%). Experimental validation with real-life datasets proves satisfactory treatment of complex non-linear relationships in cloud infrastructure usage patterns over the ranges 0.85 to 1.4. The model is efficient in dealing with non-linear and intricate behaviours in cloud workloads, achieving precise resource prediction and better cost effectiveness Cloud computing environments. These findings show better performance in modeling non-linear, complex relationships in resource use patterns.

title: Optimizing Early Diabetes Detection Using Ensemble Learning Models
authors: Shivam Chaurasiya
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Diabetes is a serious community health issue that needs to be diagnosed properly and at the earliest time to reduce the long-term illness-related complications of the medical case. This study will discuss the ways in which an ensemble learning model can be used to predict the condition of diabetes using structured medical records that include valuable diagnostic factors, including glucose values, Body Mass Index (BMI) values, patient age, and insulin concentration. The evaluation was done in six ensemble approaches by cross-validation using the Random Forest, Bagging, AdaBoostM1, LogitBoost, Stacking, and Voting. The evaluation of the performance involved the standard classification metrics which are accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and area under the ROC curve(AUC). The use of feature selection methods assisted in making the models easy to generalize, at the same time reducing the dimensions. Experimental testing shows stacking with 98% accuracy and Random Forest with 97.11% accuracy as the leading ensemble methods through their superior performance, which exceeds other approaches in accuracy measurements and all balanced evaluation metrics. This study demonstrates the effectiveness of ensemble learning in building reliable diagnostic systems for the early detection of diabetes.

title: A Survey of Geospatial Computation Problems
authors: Sumit Sen
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Rapid proliferation of geospatial data from satellites, drones, IoT devices, and social media has triggered significant challenges in storage, processing, and analysis. This paper reviews emerging computational challenges and innovative solutions in geospatial science, organized into six key thematic areas: Infrastructure & Scalability; Integration & Interoperability; Advanced Computational Methods & Analysis; Visualization & Interaction; Data Security, Privacy & Provenance; and Emerging Directions & Future Trends. Robust, distributed storage architectures and advanced indexing techniques are crucial for managing petabyte-scale datasets, while adaptive compression strategies ensure data fidelity amid vast data volumes. The fusion of heterogeneous data sources demands standardized metadata and semantic ontologies to achieve seamless interoperability. Recent advances in artificial intelligence and machine learning, including spatio-temporal neural networks, enhance the modelling of complex spatial dynamics and irregular geometries. Concurrently, innovative visualization methods—including real-time rendering and immersive AR/VR interfaces—improve user interaction and accessibility. Data security remains a priority, with techniques like differential privacy and blockchain ensuring the integrity and provenance of sensitive spatial information. Emerging trends, such as spatial reasoning in large language models and federated edge computing, offer promising routes for next-generation geospatial systems. This review outlines a roadmap for interdisciplinary research toward creating adaptive, secure, and efficient geospatial solutions.

title: Automating Malware Detection and Response via Real-Time Threat Feed Integration with Wazuh SIEM
authors: Abdullah Al Siam, Md Maruf Hassan, Abdul Kadar Muhammad Masum, Touhid Bhuiyan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This paper presents an open-source, modular framework that enhances the Wazuh Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) system by integrating real-time threat intelligence feeds for improved malware detection and automated incident response. The proposed system dynamically queries live Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) from VirusTotal and AbuseIPDB to enrich incoming log events. A custom correlation engine evaluates enriched logs using threshold-based rules to generate precise alerts, while active response scripts enable real-time mitigation actions such as IP blocking and file quarantine. The architecture supports scalable JSON-based parsing and modular feed integration, enabling rapid adaptation to evolving threat landscapes. Experimental evaluation was conducted in a controlled enterprise-grade environment using a mix of malicious and benign events. The results demonstrate a threat detection rate of 95.0%, an alert precision of 94.2%, and an average mitigation delay of 2.8 seconds. These findings confirm the system’s effectiveness, low latency, and suitability for practical deployment in cybersecurity operations.

title: Anime Recommendations: Beyond Euclidean, Leveraging Distance Metrics for Better Suggestions
authors: Sandhya B R, Ankur Tiwary, Apurv Tiwari, Manash Anand
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: In today’s digital era, the rapid growth of online streaming platforms has revolutionized content consumption, including the anime industry. With an overwhelming abundance of anime titles available, viewers often face the challenge of discovering relevant content due to the paradox of choice, where excessive options lead to decision fatigue. This research introduces an anime recommendation system designed to enhance anime-watching experience by addressing this challenge. The system is based on the approach of Content-Based Filtering, achieved by calculating the Correlation similarity score between the input anime and other anime in dataset. The system provides personalized recommendations based on the features like, Tags/genre, Synopsis, Studios, Rating score, Number Votes. The paper compares results from five distance metrices - Euclidean, Correlation, Chebyshev, Manhattan and Minkowski – to evaluate their effectiveness in recommending anime based on their similarity score.
