Notably, the system was trained on a diverse dataset comprising 16,621 datapoints. Among the applied approaches, the best results were obtained using the Correlation distance metric, achieving similarity scores ranging from 0.9964 to 0.9885 for "Naruto" and 0.9943 to 0.9886 for "Attack on Titan".

title: Simultaneous Detection of Urea and Creatinine Using a Dual-Channel PCF-SPR Biosensor for Enhanced CKD Diagnostics
authors: Md. Abdullah, Md. Jahedul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The kidney is a vital multitasking organ, and detecting a single biomarker is often insufficient to confirm chronic kidney disease (CKD). To improve diagnostic accuracy, we propose a dual-channel surface plasmon resonance (SPR) based photonic crystal fiber (PCF) biosensor capable of simultaneously detecting urea and creatinine, two key indicators of kidney function. This study presents a highly sensitive dual-channel SPR biosensor based on a PCF design for concurrent detection of these analytes. Each sensing channel is tailored to a distinct biomarker, with Channel 1 optimized for urea and Channel 2 for creatinine. Finite Element Method simulations are conducted using COMSOL Multiphysics to analyze performance. The sensor demonstrates excellent results, with maximum sensitivities of 22,000 and 20,000 nm/RIU for channel 1 and channel 2, respectively, over a refractive index range of 1.33–1.4. The study explores three concentration levels of urea and creatinine in urine—low, normal, and high—to validate performance. At low concentrations indicating patients at high risk of CKD, the sensor achieves sensitivities of 1000 and 3000 nm/RIU for urea (1.20 and 3.00 g/dL), and 1000 nm/RIU for creatinine (0.049 g/dL), with corresponding resolutions as low as 1×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−4</sup> RIU, confirming effectiveness in early disease detection. The sensor also detects normal and high levels of urea and creatinine, enabling comprehensive monitoring of kidney health and function.

title: Secure Communication in WSN-Based VANETs Using IoT and Elliptic Curve Cryptography
authors: Raghavendra Maggavi, Basavaraj Patil, Anand Gudnavar, Keerti Naregal
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) are a cornerstone of intelligent transportation systems, enabling seamless real-time communication between vehicles and infrastructure. However, their open and dynamic nature exposes them to security threats such as spoofing, data tampering, and unauthorized access. To mitigate these vulnerabilities, this paper presents a secure and efficient communication framework for Wireless Sensor Network (WSN)-based VANETs using Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC). The proposed system integrates Raspberry Pi and the MQTT protocol to provide lightweight encryption and authentication, ensuring secure data exchange between an Onboard Unit (OBU) in emergency vehicles and Roadside Units (RSUs) managing traffic signals. This secure communication enables real-time emergency message transmission, allowing RSUs to dynamically prioritize ambulances and other emergency vehicles by adjusting traffic signals. By leveraging ECC for cryptographic security and MQTT for reliable, low-latency messaging, the system enhances data integrity, prevents unauthorized message manipulation, and reduces emergency response time. This cost-effective and scalable approach strengthens VANET security while improving traffic management and emergency response efficiency in smart city environments.

title: Secure Data Transmission in IoT-Cloud Systems: A Lightweight Encryption Approach
authors: Girish Rawat, Sathyanarayanan Sayiram, Saravanakumar Baskaran, Nasmin Jiwani, S.Dhanasekaran S. Dhanasekaran
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The emergence of the Internet of Things (IoT) with cloud-based devices has enormously generated and exchanged data, intensifying security and privacy issues. Although traditional encryption schemes are strong, they are computationally and energy-demanding and unsuitable for low-power IoT devices. This article proposes a lightweight encryption scheme designed for secure data dissemination in IoT-cloud environments. The method introduced in this paper is based on a sort of low-cost, hybrid cryptography block cipher with a key exchange protocol, which is well suited for low computation resources. Simulation results show that the scheme ensures data confidentiality and integrity with low overhead in terms of latency and bandwidth. Compared with prior conventional algorithms, like AES and RSA, our proposal only sacrifices little on security confidence but makes great improvements in the computation and power consumption. The scheme supports interoperability in heterogeneous IoT structures, and operations are workable on a large scale, along with the capability of enabling continuous key management and on-the-fly encryption. Our experiments demonstrate its practicality for IoT-cloud use cases, improving overall security without trading off device performance. This paper provides a stepping-stone for inefficient security strategies and secure and scalable data transmission so as to make the IoT cloud practicable in sensitive applications, including healthcare, smart cities, and industrial automation.

title: Strategic Website Development for Brand Growth: A Data-Driven Approach with Google Analytics and SEMRU
authors: Pankaj H. Chandankhede, Kanchan S. Vaidya, Pragati Fatinge, Mohini Thakre, Amreen Khan, Anita Puri
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This study explores how strategic website development can fuel brand growth by using data-driven insights from tools such as Google Analytics and SEMrush. Instead of relying on assumptions, the research focuses on understanding real audience behavior, measuring performance, and identifying opportunities for improvement through key metrics and SEO best practices. By blending numerical data with real-world case studies, the work shows how thoughtful website design, guided by analytics, can improve user experiences, strengthen brand visibility, and increase engagement rates. The findings highlight that brands adopting a continuous, data-informed approach achieve more sustainable results and stronger online presence than those relying on intuition alone.

title: Deep MIMO Channel Estimation for Gigantic MIMO 5G NR Wireless Communication Applications
authors: Sravan Kumar Jatangi, Yedukondalu Kamatham
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Millimetre-wave (mmWave) and gigantic MIMO systems have emerged as promising technologies for enhancing the performance of wireless communications, primarily by deploying many antennas at both the transmitter and receiver ends. To fully exploit the potential of gigantic MIMO systems, a comprehensive and realistic evaluation of the underlying channel attributes is crucial. The inherent complexity and dynamic behaviour of large MIMO systems render traditional approaches insufficient for effective system modelling and optimisation. In this paper, key parameters for channel estimation in Gigantic MIMO (gMIMO) systems are analyzed, including the 3GPP 5G NR Clustered Delay Line (CDL) channel model response for OFDM systems. Additionally, the precise positioning and path loss of the user (receiver side) for the base station are determined using the Deep gMIMO 5G NR datasets. This work introduces the Deep gigantic MIMO generator, a tool designed to train deep learning models using structured input data, enabling precise estimation of channel parameters in complex wireless environments. The significant benefits of parameter analysis using the Deep gMIMO generator are anticipated to be applied in various domains, including the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT), Intelligent Reflecting Surfaces (IRS), and blockage prediction mechanisms within mm-wave and gigantic MIMO systems.

title: Integrated Image and Video Analytics: Decentralized Deep Learning for Real-Time Anomaly Detection and Data Collection
authors: J. Sofia Bobby, Shobana D, V.Lavanya, Faten Amin Al-Naimi, M Harshini, M. Dinesh
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Realtime anomaly detection and data collection for decentralized deep learning takes a step toward this direction, by addressing the increasing demand of efficient, lightweight, scalable and privacy-preserving monitoring systems in a variety of application scenarios including smart cities, industrial surveillance, and security control. This study introduces a new approach that applies decentralized deep learning for real-time anomaly detection at edge in distributed sensor networks. Combining the multimodal inputs of image and video streams can improve the detection accuracy and reduce the latency and bandwidth requirement. Collaborative learning on edge devices It implements a decentralized structure of the collaborative learning among edge devices, which decreases the dependence on centralized server, and enhances robustness to failures and attacks. Furthermore, the framework integrates secure data acquisition methodologies in order to guarantee the integrity and confidentiality such that the life-cycle of the data is maintained. Comprehensive evaluations show that the proposed approach effectively recognizes anomalies in complicated scenarios with a variety of visual inputs with high accuracy, real-time performance and scalability. This work represents a major step forward over this state-of-the-art in intelligent surveillance with challenging new solutions combining decentralized learning, multi-modal analytics, and secure data management, for supporting proactive and adaptive monitoring solutions.

title: Implicit Bayesian Learning for Enhanced Sales Forecasting in Salesforce CRM
authors: Marx Prathap Singh Shanmugam
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Salesforce AI-driven analytics leverage predictive models for sales forecasting, lead scoring, and customer retention. Traditional deep learning methods in Salesforce CRM often lack coherent updates when new customer data arrives, leading to inconsistencies in predictions. To address this, proposed BayesSales, a novel implicit Bayesian learning framework for Salesforce AI models. Instead of explicitly setting priors and posteriors, the approach ensures that prediction rules naturally align with Bayesian principles, improving adaptive forecasting and decision-making in CRM. It introduce a metric to measure how close a prediction model is to being implicitly Bayesian and evaluates various sales forecasting strategies under this framework. The results demonstrate that BayesSales enhances predictive reliability, ensuring consistent, dynamically updated insights into customer behavior, deal closures, and revenue forecasts. The findings suggest that non-Bayesian approaches are more vulnerable to adversarial market shifts, emphasizing the need for Bayesian-consistent CRM strategies in Salesforce applications.

title: Personalized Therapy Recommendations in Depression Management with Hybrid AI Models
authors: Santosh Kumar Sahoo, A Neelima, K.Thirunadana Sikamani, R. Jagadeesh Kannan, T.John Peter, S Murugan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The therapy of depression needs personalized techniques to enhance treatment results. This research studies the usefulness of hybrid artificial intelligence (AI) models that blend Support Vector Machines (SVM) and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) to provide personalized therapeutic recommendations. SVM is applied to categorize patient profiles based on demographic and previous treatment response data, successfully identifying people more likely to benefit from certain therapeutic procedures. Simultaneously, RNN examines sequential patient data, including mood logs and therapy session notes, to identify temporal trends in treatment effectiveness. By combining these algorithms, the proposed system creates dynamic, personalized recommendations that adapt to the increasing requirements of patients. A complete study of the concept indicates considerable gains in patient involvement and treatment adherence compared to traditional techniques. The results emphasize the potential of hybrid AI techniques in boosting the accuracy of depression treatment tactics, opening the path for more effective and personalized therapy. It highlights the necessity of harnessing new computational tools in mental health to enhance patient outcomes and encourage collaborative treatment among healthcare practitioners.

title: Enhancing Anomaly Detection in Time-Series Data Using Transformer-Based Models
authors: Navya Krishna Alapati
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Anomaly detection has the key significance in time-series data, this problem still being a research field ever-increasing applications from industrial monitoring and healthcare to cybersecurity creates much demand, A modifier founded approach for period sequence anomaly detection that addresses many key limitations of traditional recurrent and convolutional methods. The proposed model combines attention mechanisms with multi-scale feature extraction, allowing it to better capture long-range dependencies while also being sensitive to local anomalies. A hierarchical transformer that operates on time-series data at multiple scales, An interpretable reconstruction-based anomaly scoring method by combining prediction errors and attention weights, and contrastive learning for improving the discrimination of low-abundance anomalies in imbalanced datasets. The use of sparse self-attention in the model to lessen the computational burden while maintaining temporal dependencies. New consequences on standard datasets prove that the proposed technique outperforms other state-of-the-arts. The framework is powerful in knowing background anomalies as well as in real-time allowing for continuous processing. Ablation studies confirm the individual effectiveness of each component, and attention visualizations offer insights into localizing anomalies in an interpretable manner. They also found that this work pushes the frontier of time-series analysis by leveraging the efficiency of transformers for more robust anomaly detection in a solution deployable to mission-critical monitoring systems.

title: Retinal Biomarkers for Cardiovascular Risk Prediction Using Deep Learning
authors: Spoorthi M, Prema N S, Rakshitha M S, Taarini B N
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) are a major cause of death worldwide and account for nearly 18 million deaths annually. Early diagnosis and precise risk estimation are critical in the prevention and management of CVDs. This research proposes a new strategy based on using retinal photographs as non-invasive biomarkers to estimate cardiovascular risk. By integrating self-supervised learning, unsupervised clustering, and ensemble learning, the approach obtains high classification accuracy for retinal images into low, medium, and high cardiovascular risk levels.

title: A Comparative Study of Gradient Based Optimizer Algorithms and Nature Inspired Optimizer Algorithm for Machine Learning Models: Case study on Image Classification
authors: Sameer Patil, Suresh Shirgave
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Optimization algorithms play an important role in the training of machine learning models. Gradient-based optimizers such as Adam, RMSprop, and Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) are widely used due to their efficiency, ease of implementation and availability in the standard libraries. The paper compares the performance of these optimizers with a metaheuristic approach - Manta Ray Foraging Optimization (MRFO) as an alternative strategy that may excel in complex or non-convex problem spaces. This paper presents a comparative analysis of Adam, RMSprop, SGD, and MRFO on benchmark image classification datasets—MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, and CIFAR-10. The performance is compared based on accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score. Based on these metrics we have discussed the strengths and limitations of each optimizer. The results demonstrate that while Adam performs robustly across tasks, MRFO shows potential in hyperparameter tuning and stability, especially in more complex datasets.

title: Smart Tourism Promotion in Bangalore Rural: Leveraging AI and Machine Learning for Destination Discovery
authors: Juno Ann Joseph, Divya Thankom Varghese
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The proposed system utilizes artificial intelligence (AI) to promote tourism in Bangalore’s rural areas. These overcome the limitations of traditional systems, which rely on static websites with generic packages that are not personalized or dynamic and cannot track user interests in real-time. Traditional methodologies struggle to adapt to changing tourist needs, compromising analytical power and resulting in inaccurate targeting and low conversion rates. The proposed system uses AI and ML methods like NLP (natural language processing)-based sentiment analysis, clustering with DBSCAN (Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise), K-Means, and a hybrid recommendation engine to generate personalized location recommendations based on data from social media, government databases, and individual reviews. Predictive analytics can help optimize resources by forecasting tourism flows. The proposed system improves rural tourism experiences with 87.5% precision, 83.2% recall, and an 85.3% F1-score for recommendations. Tourist recognition scores average 4.7/5, and users are 85% satisfied, demonstrating its effectiveness.

title: Self-Supervised Learning for Unstructured Data Analytics: A Unified Framework for Scalable Representation and Insight Extraction
authors: Rupesh Dabbir
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Unstructured data—ranging from text and images to audio and sensor streams—constitutes the majority of global digital content but remains largely untapped due to annotation scarcity and heterogeneity. In this paper, we propose a self-supervised learning method for analytics on unstructured data. Our approach integrates masked content prediction, contrastive learning, and cross-modal correlation objectives within a single architecture, enabling it to learn rich and transferable representations from raw, unlabeled inputs. Unlike modality-specific methods, our framework generalizes seamlessly across text, vision, and multimodal domains, making it particularly suitable for real-world deployment. Experiments on benchmark datasets such as ImageNet, AG News, and MSCOCO show state-of-the-art results on classification and retrieval tasks in the absence of human supervision. Strong robustness, transferability, and adaptability of the model make it a useful seed for next-generation unstructured data analytics.

title: Key Feature based Classification of Sugarcane Leaf Disease using CNN and SHAP
authors: Gautam Kumar, Vivek Bhatnagar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: India is the second-largest producer of sugarcane in the world and is primarily an agricultural nation. In the Indian economy, sugarcane is the most lucrative crop and has a very high economic biomass-to-total biomass ratio. Every year, sugarcane leaf diseases cause significant losses in sugarcane crop production worldwide. To manage sugarcane crops effectively, leaf diseases must be identified early and accurately. Automating the diagnosis of leaf diseases from image data has been accomplished with surprising effectiveness using Deep Learning (DL). With the appropriate feature selection methods, the accuracy of DL models can be improved. The primary goal of feature selection is to select the necessary and pertinent features and remove the unnecessary ones for precise prediction. Following several investigations, a unification-based feature selection technique was employed in this study to accurately classify a collection of images showing sugarcane leaf disease. The performance of the classification algorithm can be improved by using the best feature selection algorithm. We provide an unsupervised feature learning framework for classifying diseases in sugarcane leaf images using a feature selection method with the ResNet50 neural network architecture. The proposed model was interpreted using the SHapley Additive ExPlanations (SHAP) based global interpretable model. The implemented model with feature selection achieved the best performance, with a classification accuracy of 97.1%.

title: CapsNet-Transformer Based Model for Violent Content Detection
authors: Md.Mahmudul Arif, Sachin Biswas, Priyanti Paul Tumpa, Ariful Islam, Turja Talukder, Sayeda Rahman Ananna, Fariha Tasnim Arpa
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The rapid increase in Internet users is likely to cause great increase in textual content on social media platforms as well as online chat systems and internet portal pages. Textual communication misuse has become widespread because of which violence-inciting texts propagate freely. Recognizing the need to address such content a tool for detection purposes has become essential. Building a detection system for Bangla proves highly difficult because this language has limited resource availability. Previous research has mainly studied violence-inciting text detection yet failed to extract targeted entities from the content. Therefore, we introduce a modified dataset called modified Violence Inciting Text Detection (MVITD) dataset. The dataset contains 6046 text aimed at two separate tasks: (i) detecting violence inciting text and (ii) detecting entity targeted by such text (Individual, Organization and Community). We proposed a method that integrates a capsule network (CapsNet) with the BanglaBERT to classify violence inciting text in Bangla into direct, passive and non-violence and their target entities.The study explores various machine learning and deep learning models, including traditional classifiers such as Random Forest (RF), Multinomial Naive Bayes (MNB) and Support Vector Machine (SVM) as well as deep learning architectures like Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM),Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). Furthermore, transformer based models such as m-BERT, BanglaBERT and XLM-RoBERTa are explored, leveraging their ability to process Bengali text more effectively. The evaluation results indicate that BanglaBERT+Capsule Network achieves macro F1-score of 76.67 in violence inciting text detection and 77.32 for identifying their target entities.

title: Plasmonic Skyrmion-Based Microfluidic-Integrated Flexible Microwave Meta-Resonator for Noninvasive Intelligent Flow Rate Sensing
authors: Shiquan Wang, Xi Xie, Boshen Xu, Yijie Shen, Yuanjin Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: In this article, we propose a novel microfluidic-integrated flexible metamaterial resonator patch based on dynamically tuned plasmonic skyrmions for fluid flow rate sensing. The resonator patch is designed by integrating a flexible skyrmionic metamaterial resonator with a matching spiral microfluidic channel. The ultrathin metamaterial resonator can effectively excite multiple Néel-type electric and magnetic plasmonic skyrmion modes in the microwave regime, characterized by diverse multi-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pi $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-twist topological spin textures. Importantly, the resonance characteristics of these plasmonic skyrmion modes can be dynamically tuned by adjusting the fluid level within the matched microfluidic channel, thereby enabling real-time flow rate detection through resonance tracking. The proposed metamaterial resonator patch offers several distinct advantages, including compactness, robustness, mechanical flexibility, high sensitivity, and strong multimodal sensing capability. To further enhance the accuracy and robustness of flow rate detection, an artificial neural network (ANN)-enabled analytical framework is developed to interpret the multimodal resonance responses. A series of experiments is carried out to evaluate the performance of the proposed metamaterial resonator patch-based sensing system. The results strongly demonstrate and validate its effectiveness for real-time, continuous, accurate, and robust noninvasive fluid flow rate detection in microfluidic applications.

title: Manufacturing of the EuroSIG Dipole Demonstrator Magnet for Hadrontherapy
authors: M. Prioli, N. Ciarchi, G. Crespi, E. De Matteis, A. Palmisano, D. Pedrini, L. Rossi, A. Ruggiero, C. Santini, S. Sorti, R. U. Valente, E. Bianchi, S. Farinon, A. Gagno, R. Musenich, A. Pampaloni, G. Ceruti, L. Dassa, L. Gentini, D. Perini, R. Piccin, E. Felcini, M. Pullia, O. Duran, J. A. Garcia-Matos, C. Martins Jardim, F. Toral
abstract: This paper reports on the updated status and manufacturing results of the EuroSIG Superconducting dipole Demonstrator Magnet (SDM-c). The SDM-c is one of the key technologies for the next-generation European heavy-ion gantry and consists of a curved cos−θ NbTi dipole with a 30° magnetic length over a 1.65 m curvature radius and a 0.15 Ts<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> ramp-rate. We summarize the milestones reached in the last year, including the production of the curved dummy coil, employed to validate the manufacturing tooling and procedures, the production of the first curved NbTi coil, and the engineering and procurement of the mechanical structure, which was also complicated by the curvature. The results show that the project is in good track to prove the feasibility of the bending magnet concept. The paper includes a discussion of the remaining challenges for the final prototype magnet, which has a 45° magnetic length and a 0.4 Ts<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> ramp-rate.

title: Partially Superconducting Machines with Liquid Hydrogen for Marine Propulsion
authors: Roberto Oliveira, Tabea Arndt, J¨orn Grundmann
abstract: This paper contributes to the field of naval propulsion by designing and analyzing a partially superconducting motor with characteristics tailored to ships. It provides a comprehensive overview of Siemens' pivotal contributions to superconducting motor advancements and highlights the benefits of this technology in the naval sector. The latest Siemens motor, HTS-3, demonstrates remarkable performance. To advance the sector, a partially superconducting motor for modern maritime applications has been developed. Key performance data demonstrate its advantages: the 8-pole motor operates at a low speed of 225rpm, delivers torque exceeding 600kNm, and achieves a volumetric power density of 9.3MW/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. The field winding employs disk-up-down-assembly coils in a rectangular topology (DUDA-r). Its high power density, combined with a reduced carbon footprint when using hydrogen as both a coolant and a fuel, makes this motor highly attractive for propulsion applications.

title: Adaptive Resource Scheduling for Digital Twin-Based 6G Communication on Intelligent Computing-Integrated Network
authors: Bhoomi Gupta, Vishal Namireddy, Aadam Quraishi, A. Yasmine Begum, K. B. V. Brahma Rao, Ajith Sundaram, Aseel Smerat
abstract: With the rapid development of digital twin technology and the sixth generation (6G), holographic communication is emerging as a transformative feature in Digital twin, offering immersive experiences in smart education, entertainment, healthcare, and beyond. However, existing network resource scheduling methods often fall short in addressing the massive bandwidth, ultra-low latency, and strict data synchronization requirements essential for high-fidelity holographic services in 6G-enabled environments. To address these challenges, this paper explores a novel intelligent computing-integrated network (ICIN) architecture designed specifically for holographic communication in 6G-based Digital twin. Leveraging the inherent in-network computing capabilities of ICINs, the proposed system performs real-time computation and optimization during data transmission, effectively reducing latency and alleviating bandwidth constraints. Building upon prior research in intelligent identifier-based networking, we introduce a dynamic identifier mapping mechanism based on service identifiers, resource chain identifiers, network function identifiers, and component identifiers. This enables adaptive scheduling of heterogeneous network resources, such as computing, storage, and forwarding, tailored to the diverse demands of 6G-driven holographic services. A prototype system was developed using this approach, and experimental results demonstrate that, compared with traditional edge computing methods, the proposed ICIN-based system reduces network bandwidth load by 60% and transmission delay by 45%, while providing enhanced flexibility for resource orchestration in next-generation consumer 6G communication scenarios.

title: The Rest of the Robots: Generative AI in Post-introductory Computing Education
authors: Dennis J. Bouvier, Bruno Pereira Cipriano, Richard Glassey, Olga Petrovska, Emma Anderson, Anastasiia Birillo, Ryan Dougherty, Raymond Pettit, Nuno Pombo, Ebrahim Rahimi, Charanya Ramakrishnan, Alexander Steinmaurer, Shubbhi Taneja, Muhammad Usman, Annapurna Vadaparty
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 Working Group Reports on Innovation and Technology in Computer Science Education
abstract: None

title: Preserving Fine-Grained Droplet Structures by Spatial Gaussian Blurring Against Pooling Deformation in U-Net Based Semantic Segmentation
authors: Wei Lun Lim, Matthew YW Teow, Richard TK Wong, Sian Lun Lau
abstract: Pooling operations in convolutional neural networks often lead to the loss of fine-grained object features, particularly when those objects occupy only a few pixels (e.g., 5 × 5 or smaller). This problem is critical in droplet analysis, where pooling-induced feature loss causes deformation of finegrained droplet boundaries within the encoded representation. When fine-grained droplet features are structurally deformed and vanished in the encoder, the resulting ineffective representation impairs learning and leads to inaccurate droplet segmentation, which severely impairs downstream decision-making. We propose a data augmentation strategy using Gaussian blurring to mitigate this problem. By applying spatial blurring prior to encoding, the blur acts as a contour regularization, strengthening the network learning capability to retain fine-grained droplet structure through subsequent pooling layers and improving robustness against pooling deformation. To further strengthen spatial feature interaction, we integrate a graph convolution module at the bottleneck of a U-Net architecture. The graph is constructed from the bottleneck feature map, with edges weighted by raw intensity values of the corresponding feature elements. This non-parametric, intensity-based graph formulation captures structural relationships among features and complements spatial feature preservation. Our combined method achieves a 16.5% relative improvement in Recall (R), indicating that more fine-grained droplets are correctly segmented compared to the baseline U-Net. Ablation studies confirm that Gaussian blur augmentation is the dominant contributor to these gains, while the GraphSAGE provides complementary refinement. The code is available at: https://github.com/lynerlwl/blur-unet-segmentation.

title: IMS Announces the 2025 Undergraduate Scholarship Awardees! [Society News]
authors: Katelyn R. Brinker
abstract: The IEEE Instrumentation and Measurement Society (IMS) has recently completed the selection process for the first edition of the IEEE IMS Undergraduate Student Scholarship, marking an important milestone in our commitment to supporting young talent in instrumentation and measurement. We are particularly proud of this initiative, which reflects our Society's mission to inspire and support the next generation of engineers and scientists in the field of instrumentation and measurement.

title: Enabling Population-Level Parallelism in Tree-Based Genetic Programming for GPU Acceleration_supp1-3663396.pdf
authors: Ran Cheng
abstract: Tree-based Genetic Programming (TGP) is a widely used evolutionary algorithm for tasks such as symbolic regression, classification, and robotic control. Due to the intensive computational demands of running TGP, GPU acceleration is crucial for achieving scalable performance. However, efficient GPU-based execution of TGP remains challenging, primarily due to three core issues: (1) the structural heterogeneity of program individuals, (2) the complexity of integrating multiple levels of parallelism, and (3) the incompatibility between high-performance CUDA execution and flexible Python-based environments. To address these issues, we propose EvoGP, a high-performance framework tailored for GPU acceleration of TGP via population-level parallel execution. First, EvoGP introduces a tensorized representation that encodes variable-sized trees into fixed-shape, memory-aligned arrays, enabling uniform memory access and parallel computation across diverse individuals. Second, EvoGP adopts an adaptive parallelism strategy that dynamically combines intra- and inter-individual parallelism based on dataset size, ensuring high GPU utilization across a broad spectrum of tasks. Third, EvoGP embeds custom CUDA kernels into the PyTorch runtime, achieving seamless integration with Python-based environments such as Gym, MuJoCo, Brax, and Genesis. Experimental results demonstrate that EvoGP achieves a peak throughput exceeding 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup> GPops/s. Specifically, this performance represents a speedup of up to 304× over existing GPU-based TGP implementations and 18× over state-of-the-art CPU-based libraries. Furthermore, EvoGP maintains comparable accuracy and exhibits improved scalability across large population sizes. EvoGP is open source and accessible at https://github.com/EMI-Group/evogp.

title: A Scalable Potential of Alpha Earth and Multi-Sensor Datasets for Assessing Mangrove Intactness and Degradation using Deep and Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Akkarapon Chaiyana, Filippo Sarvia, Narissara Nuthammachot, Jaturong Som-ard
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Mangrove ecosystems act as highly efficient carbon sinks and provide critical ecological services for climate regulation; however, they are increasingly threatened by anthropogenic pressures and climate change. Recent advances in annual satellite-based Earth Observation (EO) embedding (EM) datasets have enabled the integration of multi-source data fusion, improving land use and land cover analyses. Nevertheless, EM datasets have rarely been applied for the monitoring of mangrove ecosystems. Within this context, the present study compares the performance of EM with combined Sentinel-1, Sentinel-2, and Landsat 8/9 datasets (S2S1L8/9) through the integration of a pretrained ResNet152–U-Net architecture and Random Forest (RF) modeling to assess spatio-temporal patterns of mangrove intactness and degradation from 2017 to 2024. A ResNet152 encoder pretrained on ImageNet was employed to train a U-Net model using the Global Mangrove Dataset (2020) for the mapping of potential mangrove extent. The EM dataset outperformed the S2S1L8/9 combination, achieving validation Intersection over Union (IoU) scores of 0.85 and 0.84, and Dice coefficients of 0.89 and 0.88, respectively. Spatial comparison further indicated that EM yielded a lower Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) (6.51 ha) compared to S2S1L8/9 (7.27 ha). The RF model, trained on intact and non-intact mangrove samples across multiple years, confirmed the superior performance of EM in delineating intact mangrove areas along coastlines, with an overall accuracy of 0.97, an F1-score of 0.97, and a Matthews Correlation Coefficient of 0.93. Degraded mangrove areas were identified by masking intact regions, and gain–loss analysis revealed a decline of approximately 3% in 2021 and 2022 relative to the 2017 baseline. These findings demonstrate that EM provides a more accurate and spatially consistent approach than conventional multisource datasets for mapping mangrove intactness and degradation. By minimizing classification errors and improving coastline delineation, EM establishes a robust framework for large-scale monitoring of mangrove dynamics, supporting conservation planning, carbon accounting, and climate resilience strategies across diverse coastal and terrestrial systems at global scales.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A

title: Enhancing XR Training with Interactive AI-Powered Virtual Instructors
authors: Mikołaj Maik, Krzysztof Walczak
abstract: This paper evaluates the effectiveness of interactive, AI-powered virtual instructors in an extended reality (XR) training environment for electrical technicians. A user study was conducted in a 3D reconstruction of an electrical substation, where participants completed a four-step technical training procedure using voice and gesture interactions with the AI instructor. The study examines the instructor’s ability to guide users through a complex, multi-stage workflow and assesses performance outcomes, cognitive load, user experience, and overall training effectiveness. The results demonstrate that AI-guided XR instruction can enhance user engagement and knowledge acquisition while maintaining a relatively low cognitive load in high-risk technical training scenarios. These findings highlight the potential of interactive AI instructors to improve the design of future XR training systems across industrial domains.

title: ASMARL: Optimizing Traffic Signal Coordination through Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning with Attention Mechanisms and Sequential Rollout
authors: Bo Wang, Jie Liu, Haoran Cheng, Tongchun Du
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning tasks, partial observability and non-stationarity from agents updating their parameters are significant challenges. A common solution is sharing all observations and actions among agents directly, but this can lead to excessive communication costs, especially with many agents, making it impractical. Hence, it’s crucial to selectively share important information to reduce redundancy and effectively represent and integrate the shared information. This paper proposes an Attention-based Shared Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (ASMARL) method for multi-intersection traffic signal coordination, introducing an optimal baseline to reduce variance. The approach includes constructing communication channels using attention mechanisms to integrate shared information, designing a sequential rollout mechanism to incorporate agents’ actions within the communication range into the Critic network’s input, and introducing an optimal baseline to mitigate high variance in multi-agent policy gradient. Simulations in the urban traffic software (SUMO) show that ASMARL outperforms current mainstream methods in reducing average traffic delay.

title: AI-Driven Multimodal Sensor Fusion for Predictive Maintenance of Smart Grid Equipment Using Vibration and Thermal Data
authors: Md Rifat Al Amin Khan, Md Omar Faruq, Md Nahian Suhaimee, Farhan Shakil, Sheikh Razia Sultana, Sabiha Firdaus
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Predictive maintenance plays a critical role in ensuring the reliability and efficiency of smart grid equipment, where early fault detection can significantly reduce operational costs and prevent unplanned downtime. This paper proposes an AI-driven multimodal sensor fusion framework that integrates vibration and thermal data to enhance predictive maintenance performance. The approach combines convolutional, recurrent, and temporal convolutional neural networks with an attention-based fusion mechanism to learn complementary features from heterogeneous sensor streams. Two publicly available datasets containing vibration, torque, process temperature, and IoT-based thermal sensor readings were used to train and evaluate the model. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms traditional machine learning and single-modality deep learning models, achieving superior accuracy of 96.74%, F1-score of 95.61%, and AUC metrics. An ablation study confirms the contribution of both sensor modalities and the fusion layer to fault detection capability. The results highlight the effectiveness of multimodal deep learning for robust equipment health monitoring and provide a scalable solution for intelligent predictive maintenance in smart grid systems.

title: EquiForensics-Net: A CNN-BiLSTM Framework for Detecting Police Atrocities Against Pawn Brokers
authors: Frederick Ruby Helen, Hanushka Srinivasan, Varsha Shankar, Aadhithyan S, E. Sridevi, R. Aishwarya
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Numerous urban regions have been facing ongoing police abuses together with human rights breaches targeting defenseless communities that include pawn brokers. The study introduces EquiForensics-Net which combines CNN-BiLSTM architecture to examine various forensic evidence from different modes to discover police misconduct toward pawn brokers. The model brings together CNNs for video spatial feature extraction together with BiLSTM networks for sequential processing of textual and auditory evidence. The model receives performance improvements from a Hybrid Attention Mechanism which strengthens its capability to analyze essential data sections. The data consists of CCTV videos together with audio witness statements and social media content and legal documents which undergone processing through expert NLP technology and spatio-temporal analytical tools. The evidence validation strength increases through text and audio and video inputs that work together in the multimodal fusion approach. The system EquiForensics-Net produced 96.3% accuracy which exceeded both SVM-LSTM (84.1%) and CNN-Transformer (93.8%). The system reached 95.2% precision and 95.8% recall and 95.5% F1-score during testing while manual legal expert reviews showed 93.9% agreement with system evaluations. The solution demonstrates its ability to support legal investigations and human rights advocacy by developing accurate AI-driven assessments.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Approach for Personalized Nutrition Recommendation Based on Health Profile and Dietary Preferences
authors: Raksha Puthran, Jeevan Bhandary, Anusha Prashanth Shetty, Sujith Sujith, Shetty Shamitha Vasanth
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This research introduces an innovative framework for personalized nutrition recommendation utilizing advanced machine learning techniques. The system incorporates comprehensive user health profiles, dietary constraints, and nutritional science principles to generate customized meal suggestions. By implementing a hybrid approach combining cosine similarity metrics and random forest classification algorithms, the framework identifies appropriate food options aligned with individual health conditions, nutritional requirements, and dietary preferences. Experimental evaluation demonstrates effective personalization capabilities, with recommendations successfully addressing diverse health scenarios including weight management, diabetes, hypertension, and several other conditions. The framework achieves notable recommendation accuracy and nutritional alignment, validating its potential application in digital nutrition platforms and dietary counseling services. This work advances personalized nutrition technology by establishing an algorithmic framework adaptable across diverse dietary cultures and health requirements.

title: An Internet-of-Disease (IoD) based Real-Time ECG Monitoring Framework using YOLOv11 for Smart Healthcare Applications
authors: M. Humayet Islam, Sabbir Hossain Durjoy, Rejowan Arifin Nayeem, Md. Emon Shikder, S. M. Abdullah Al Muhib, Emtiaz Niam Shaif Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Healthcare is one of humanity's fundamental rights, and the advent of embedded technology has made various healthcare devices accessible and efficient. In this study, we propose an Internet-Of-Disease (IoD) based cloud-integrated framework for real-time monitoring and classification of ECG signals to support smart healthcare applications. The proposed system employs an ECG sensor to continuously gather heart rate data, which is transmitted to cloud platforms via wireless communication protocols. We evaluated multiple machine learning and deep learning classifiers, including traditional ML models, CNN-based architectures, and YOLO variants. Among them, YOLOv11 achieved the highest performance with an accuracy of 99.50%, demonstrating its superiority in accurately classifying ECG signals and detecting abnormalities, while other models showed comparatively lower performance. Incorporating IoT capabilities with ML and DL approaches in the IoD platform enables automated, real-time collection, transmission, and analysis of diagnostic data, which is securely stored in cloud databases accessible only to authorized medical practitioners. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework provides high accuracy and reliability, making it highly suitable for deployment in smart healthcare environments to enhance early disease detection and continuous patient monitoring.

title: Advanced IoT-Enabled Wearable Technology for Maritime Personnel Safety: Real-Time Man Overboard Detection and Emergency Response System
authors: K.Bharath Kumar Reddy, G.N.S.S. Aditya, B. Yamini, G. Premalatha
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Maritime safety represents a fundamental concern across all naval operations, particularly for personnel aboard commercial vessels, passenger ferries, and recreational boats. Traditional man overboard (MOB) detection systems depend heavily on human observation, creating significant delays in emergency response protocols. This research presents an innovative smart wearable IoT device engineered for autonomous real-time MOB detection and immediate alert generation. The comprehensive system integrates multiple sensor technologies including MAX30100 for continuous heart rate and oxygen saturation monitoring, MPU6050 for precise motion and fall detection capabilities, and specialized pH sensors for water immersion identification. Upon detecting threshold violations across any monitored parameter, the device automatically activates the NEO-6M GPS module for precise location acquisition and transmits emergency alerts through integrated Wi-Fi communication modules to designated response teams and onboard safety systems. This autonomous monitoring solution leverages advanced IoT technologies to ensure continuous surveillance and instantaneous alerting, significantly reducing undetected MOB incidents while enhancing survival probability through rapid rescue coordination. The device minimizes reliance on human observation while providing enhanced security for personnel across water-based industries including maritime logistics, passenger transportation, naval operations, and recreational vessel activities. The solution ensures timely incident detection and accelerates search and rescue operations, potentially serving as a catalyst for widespread adoption of IoT-driven wearable technologies in life-critical maritime safety applications.

title: A Power-Efficient Hardware-Optimized Square Root Carry Select Adder Implementations for High-Speed Processors
authors: Radha. N, M. Maheswari, A. Abinaya
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This research delves the VLSI implementation of a 20-bit Square Root Carry Select Adder, emphasizing enhanced energy efficiency. This efficiency is achieved through the integration of parallel prefix adders, specifically the Kogge Stone, Brent Kung, Ladner Fischer, and Han Carlson variants, for the computation of the carry '0' path. Additionally, a Binary to Excess 1 converter is utilized for the carry '1' path. The proposed adder architectures are anticipated to have a trade-off between area and delay. Cadence 90 simulation yields better performance for the proposed SQRT CSLA architectures. The power utilization, area utilization, and latency are minimal when compared to the SQRT CSLA using Ripple Carry Adders. High-performance architectures utilizing the SQRT CSLA design demonstrate potential applicability within advanced digital signal processing systems.

title: Enhanced Prediction of Liver Disease Using Machine Learning: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Sonia Afroz, Atiqur Rahman, Md Mobasser Hosain, Nikunj Sharma, Kanika Rawat, Rakibul Alam, Sheikh Majedul Huq, Mst. Shuly Aktar, Md. Akkas Ali
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Liver disease presents a growing global health burden that often arises slowly without symptoms and hence remains underdiagnosed until late-stage disease, prompting the need for accurate early diagnostic capabilities. With this work, the problem of delayed diagnosis is addressed by using Machine learning (ML) techniques for predicting liver disease from clinical and lifestyle data. In this paper, the principal objective is to assess the accuracy of three existing ML classifiers (RF, LR, and SVM). Using a tidy dataset of 11 features for each of 1700 patients, we went through the usual steps to preprocess and clean our data before splitting it into stratified train-test sets, thereby ensuring an unbiased evaluation. All models were trained/tested using z-scored features and evaluated in terms of classification accuracy, confusion matrices, ROC curves, and statistical analysis. The deep learning models that exceed baseline models have achieved as accuracy (89%, 83%, 85%), sensitivity (87%, 84%, 84%), specificity (92%, 81%, 85%), precision (93%, 84%, 87%), recall (87%, 84%, 84%), F1-Score (90%, 84%, 86%), MSE (0.11,0.17, 0.15), and ROC-AUC (96%, 90%, 92%) for RF, LR, and SVM models, respectively. Random Forest achieved the highest accuracy of 90% and ROC-AUC score of 96%, proving to be very efficient in predicting nonlinear patterns and reducing misclassification errors. The model scored well across multiple metrics, but the weak score in Logistic Regression was due to poor prediction performance. Our results demonstrate the superior performance of ensemble learning models for clinical decision support in the early diagnosis and treatment planning of liver disease. This comparative analysis further supports the use of AI for healthcare diagnostics and serves as a foundation for future multimodal and deep learning approaches.

title: Automated Classification of Anemia Types Associated with Bacterial Infections Using CBC Parameters and Stacking Models
authors: Jasdeep Singh, Jasmeen Gill, Yogesh Kumar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Bacterial infections, including anemia caused by bacterial pathogens, pose significant health challenges globally. Accurate classification of anemia types is critical for effective management and treatment. In the present work, we consider the classification of anemia type based on the CBC data and propose the usage of the ML methods. After the comparison of eight different models, the three top-performing models are therefore Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, as well as the Stacking Classifier models. Random Forest and Gradient Boosting models yielded nearly similar accuracy of 99% while Stacking Classifier showed tremendous result with an accuracy of 98%, precision of 99% and F1 score of 98%. In class-wise analysis, it was confirmed that the training model gave perfect classification of both "Normocytic hypochromic anemia" and "Normocytic normochromic anemia" even though the real problem lays in the under-represented and diverse class such as Leukemia with thrombocytopenia.

title: Secure and Transparent Rural Health Data Management Using Blockchain Technology
authors: A. Arshad Ahmed, D. Kerana Hanirex
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: A more effective, safe, and transparent solution is required because the existing centralized rural health data management systems are challenged by single points of failure, non-interoperability, and data breaches. As a result, establishments with limited assets experienced delays and errors in accessing health care. The study suggests a blockchain-based system that uses smart contracts to enable safe and private data sharing, distributes data storage, and offers tamper-proof record management. The patient data is hashed, encrypted, and stored using a hybrid on-chain/off-chain model, enhancing its confidentiality and integrity. It creates an algorithm that uses PBFT consensus, Hyperledger Fabric, smart contracts, and machine learning (ML) to detect anomalies. With an accuracy of 96.4%, a patient record retrieval latency of 120 ms, and an overall proposed system uptime of 99.83%, the performance analysis demonstrates notable gains. These results demonstrate the proposed system outstanding dependability, speed, and security in adapting to rural health infrastructure.

title: Human-Less Toll Plaza Vehicle Recognition Using Deep Learning
authors: P. Leela Jancy, D. Muruga Radha Devi, Akash Raj L, Sangeethkumar M, Rahul M
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The toll booth operation tool based on artificial intelligence processing and OpenCV techniques functions without human intervention according to this research. After vehicles exit a toll gate the image processing system automatically classifies vehicles independently. The system applies deep learning processing together with OpenCV technology to extract important vehicle information by extracting license plates and model data from main attributes. The payment acceleration system accepts payments through data provided by FASTag options and other payment methods. Computing systems carry out their operations independently to optimize performance by making decisions on their own and shorten transaction time at toll stations. The system demonstrates operational functionality according to experimental results and therefore improves traffic management security and prevention from criminal activities.

title: DeepQ Classifier for Predicting of Brain Tumor using Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Gouri Bompem, Dhanalakshmi Pandluri
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Classifying Brain Tumors are very important to predict the disease level and abnormal category. Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scan is useful process to find the Tumor cells. Based on results abnormal status to be monitored and classify the results. In this paper, we use convolutional neural network process to classify the brain Tumor dataset based on nature and level. DeepQ classifier method is process to measure the accuracy, measure and score function. Here we selected three different datasets taken for evaluation and apply classification technique to predict the accuracy. In this case based on various iterations 512X 512X3 layered deep belief network is generated by using TensorFlow. The trained dataset as 30000 trained data, test dataset as 15000 are taken for evaluation. This process is consisting of cluster the dataset, regression process and measuring accuracy. In this approach our evaluation system achieves the accuracy as 95% and we compare the results with previous classifiers.

title: Augmented Lagrangian for Image Super-Resolution
authors: Sathvik Reddy V, Aaryan Gera, Aditya Kothapalli, Neetu Srivastava
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Image super-resolution (SR) is an important step in improving the quality of an image. Augmented Lagrangian techniques, especially the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM), have proven to be effective frameworks for solving such inverse problems. However, the performance of ADMM heavily depends on the optimal tuning of its internal parameters, particularly the regularization parameter (λ) and the penalty parameter (ρ). This project explores and tests new machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) pipelines to automatically determine the optimal λ and ρ values for ADMM-based image super-resolution using Total Variation (TV) regularization. A pipeline based on traditional regressors—Linear Regression and Support Vector Regression (SVR)—was constructed. These models were trained on handcrafted image features (mean intensity, standard deviation, and edge density) to predict the optimal parameters obtained via exhaustive grid search. Simultaneously, a DL pipeline was developed using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) that learns a direct mapping from low-resolution image tensors to the optimal parameters. Both methods attempt to adapt λ and ρ values to the specific content of each input image. The predicted parameters were then plugged into the ADMM algorithm to perform the SR task.

title: Early Detection of Robust Fetal Health Prediction Leveraging Using Machine Learning
authors: S. P. Santhoshkumar, Aarthi D, D. Jayasutha, Sunil Kumar Sahu, S.Vidhya, Kanakaprabha. S
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Early identification of the better maternal and newborn outcomes, this paper examines how glowing unusual machine learning models identify fetal health issues using cardiotocography (CTG) data. The XGBoost model proved to be the most dependable model for classifying fetal health following extensive training and testing, by an accurateness of 96.01% and a Phi Coefficient of 0.8899. Despite somewhat lagging behind XGBoost, ensemble approaches like stacking and blending also produced high accuracies of 95.54% with MCC values of 0.8768. The Random Forest model was situated as a good but less dependable extra with an impressive accuracy of 94.60% and MCC of 0.8474. The model employed Logistic Regression worst by an accuracy of 87.79% and an MCC of 0.6655, while K-Nearest Neighbors and Support Vector Machine models presented practical efficacy. Due to its wonderful accuracy and balanced performance, the XGBoost model is optional for clinical applications in fetal health classification based on these findings. The model’s possible to enable early diagnosis and decrease the maternal and newborn mortality to timely intervention.

title: Cost-Effective High-Reliability IoT-Based Multi-Modal Patient Monitoring with Real-Time Clinical Alerts and Cloud Analytics
authors: Sharna Rani Das, Abdul Hasib, T. M. Mehrab Hasan, Saurav Kundu, Arjina Nasrin Khan, Md. Meraj Serker
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Conventional patient monitoring systems exhibit critical limitations including fragmented physiological data acquisition, delayed critical event notification, and prohibitive deployment costs. These constraints impede timely clinical intervention and exacerbate healthcare accessibility challenges, particularly in resource-constrained environments. To address these deficiencies, this paper presents a novel dual-controller architectural framework integrating multi-modal biosensing capabilities with real-time analytical functions. The implemented system features comprehensive physiological surveillance of cardiac, oximetric, thermal, and mobility parameters through tightly synchronized edge processing. Core innovations include clinician-centric visualization via an embedded web dashboard, automated electronic alerting for critical health anomalies, cloud-enabled longitudinal analytics, and redundant local diagnostics. Rigorous operational validation confirms exceptional system reliability, responsive clinical alerting, significant cost efficiency, and measurable reduction in caregiver workload. This research establishes a new paradigm for accessible high-fidelity patient monitoring through harmonized hardware-software co-design, enabling deployable continuous health surveillance across tertiary hospitals, community clinics, and remote care settings.

title: Dynamic and Regularized Visual Prompt Tuning for Few-Shot Image Classification
authors: Tirth Mehta, Shruti Mathur, Nilesh Marathe
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs) have reached state-of-the-art performance in image classification tasks, frequently outperforming the traditional convolutional networks. Nevertheless, their use of large-scale datasets and heavy fine-tuning restricts their use in few-shot learning situations. Visual Prompt Tuning (VPT) overcomes this limitation by fine-tuning pretrained ViTs based on task prompts, but fixed prompts have limited adaptability and generalizability to various tasks. In this paper, we present Dynamic and Regularized Visual Prompt Tuning (DRVPT), a light-weight approach that uses a MetaNet to dynamically produce task-sensitive prompts and adds prompt-level regularization to make learning stable and prevent overfitting. Experiments on the Oxford 102 Flowers and CIFAR-100 datasets under 1-, 3-, 5-, and 10-shot conditions demonstrate that DRVPT outperforms fine-tuned ViTs and static VPT in all cases. Our method facilitates low-computation cost data-efficient transfer learning and is an efficient solution for few-shot image classification.

title: Multi-Modal Deep Learning for Stock Market Direction Prediction Using Time-Series Data
authors: Md Shujan Shak, Rahomotul Islam, Jannatul Maua, Sabiha Firdaus, Arifa Akter Eva, Anika Sayeema
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Stock market prediction remains a challenging task due to the complex interplay between historical trends, economic indicators, and real-time information flow. This paper proposes a multi-modal deep learning framework that integrates financial news headlines, macroeconomic indicators, and historical stock price sequences to forecast daily market direction. The model employs FinBERT and transformer layers to encode textual sentiment, a feedforward network to process macroeconomic features, and a bidirectional LSTM to model temporal stock patterns. These heterogeneous signals are fused through a joint representation layer and passed to a binary classifier. Experimental results on real-world datasets demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms several baseline and single-modal architectures in accuracy, F1 score, and AUC. Ablation studies further validate the contribution of each modality, while robustness tests show the model’s resilience to noise and temporal shifts. This work highlights the importance of multi-source learning for financial forecasting and provides a foundation for more adaptable and informative market prediction systems.

title: Digital Image Processing-Based Detection, Classification and Evaluation of Eye Cataracts
authors: Sheenam, Jaspreet Kaur, Divya Gupta
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: A cataract is a cloudy area that spreads in the lens of the eye, which is the clear part of the eye that helps focus light. Cataracts can cause blurry and double vision, sensitivity to light, and difficulty in seeing at night. The proteins in the lenses of the eyes start to break down and clump together at around age 40, but typically it's symptoms cannot be noticed until the age of 60 or more. Cataracts can be treated with surgery to remove the clouded lens and replace it with a clear artificial lens. Cataract surgery is typically done on an outpatient basis, and it's generally safe. But it is only possible with the help of early detection of the cataract. Therefore, many methods and techniques have been introduced by many doctors, professors, researchers and scientists. So, in this research paper we have studied recent research papers in which the authors of the papers have proposed different and efficient models to detect cataract. We have studied and collected the data of the accuracy of the models and have concluded that is one of the best and most efficient and reliable models for the eye cataract detection.

title: Tuberculosis Detection in Chest X-Ray Images Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: H K Virupakshaiah, Nagapooja T R
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Tuberculosis (TB) remains a leading cause of illness and death worldwide, affecting millions of people, particularly in lower-income regions. Existing diagnostic techniques like sputum smear tests and cultures, while commonly used, can be slow, demand significant resources, and are prone to mistakes due to their reliance on manual processes. Recently, innovations in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and deep learning have shown great promise as faster and more dependable tools for interpreting medical images. This study examines how several sophisticated transfer learning methods, specifically DenseNet-121, InceptionV3, ResNet-50, ConvNeXt, and Vision Transformer (ViT), perform when applied to chest X-ray images for identifying TB. Each neural network was carefully fine-tuned using an extensive, well-organized dataset of chest X-rays. Their effectiveness was measured using various criteria, including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and area under the ROC curve (AUC-ROC). The findings reveal that transfer learning noticeably improves classification outcomes, with DenseNet-121 standing out for its high accuracy and reliability, making it a strong candidate for supporting clinical decisions. By streamlining the diagnostic process and alleviating the burden on radiologists, the proposed approach offers a scalable, equitable, and consistent solution for TB screening.

title: Movie Recommendation System using Machine Learning Algorithm
authors: Vaibhav Shekhawat, Vikash Kumar, Pratibha Soni, Rahul Misra, Ravi Joshi, Tulsi Ram Sharma
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Customized Movie recommendation systems are very important for allowing users to improve their engagement and enjoyment with the digital environment. Traditional methods like content-based filtering and collaborative filtering commonly struggle to detect complex sequential customer actions. In this paper, we propose a recommendation system based on the Behavioral Sequence Transformer (BST), making use of positional encoding, contextual embeddings, and multi-head attention to represent both long-term and short-term customer interests. The methodology of this study consists of data preprocessing, feature extraction, and model training, with performance tested by applying MAP, NDCG, Recall, Precision and RMSE. Experimental findings indicate that the BST method has improved accuracy of recommendation compared to traditional methods. Future improvements like reinforcement learning and hybrid models can further optimize and improve the effectiveness of personalized recommendations.

title: Explainable and Uncertainty-Aware CO2 Emissions Forecasting Using Quantile Gradient Boosting, SHAP, and Logistic Trend Classification
authors: Most. Mazriha Akter Mohua, Md.Nazmus Sakib
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: An empirical investigation focuses on enhancing the explainability of Machine Learning (ML) models to forecast CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emissions across multiple emission sources, with a particular emphasis on models like QGB Regression, which are utilized to handle heteroscedastic data where standard regression may fail. However, complicated ML models' explainability issues have long been discussed, particularly in sustainability planning where policymakers prioritize reliable decision-making. Models like QGB often act as black boxes by concealing the prediction mechanism, although they outperform standard regression. This study addresses this gap by suggesting an eXplainable AI (XAI)-driven and uncertainty-aware CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emission prediction framework which allows for post-hoc analysis of forecast results. The proposed framework leverages Quantile Gradient Boosting (QGB) regression to forecast country-level CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emissions by estimating conditional quantiles. SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) enhance model transparency by interpreting how and why prediction occurs. Three quantile models are trained for addressing predictive uncertainty to generate 95% prediction intervals. In addition, logistic regression classifier (cross-validated) categorizes whether CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emissions have increased compared to the previous year. The experimental results demonstrate that by achieving MAE of 3.75%, a coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) of 99.87%, and classification accuracy of 91.99% with an AUC score of 0.98, the framework shows robust prediction. SHAP interpretability technique indicates temporal lag and rolling mean to be dominant features, while the achieved coverage of prediction interval of 94.97% indicates robust calibration, which suggests that the framework may aid climate policy with trustworthy and uncertainty-aware CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> forecasting.

title: ColoNetBlock: Attention Enhanced CNN for Colorectal Cancer Classification with Visual Explanation
authors: Shampa Banik, Sumaiya Binte Shahid, Maharin Afroj
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Colorectal cancer (CRC) continues to be a significant global health concern, with effective treatment relying heavily on early and precise detection. Many deep learning models developed for this purpose are trained on idealized datasets, which restricts their effectiveness in real-world clinical environments where endoscopic images often suffer from noise, artifacts, and high variability. In response to these challenges, we propose a novel framework tailored for such demanding conditions. Our method initiates with a thorough preprocessing pipeline that improves image clarity and reduces noise, all while maintaining essential tissue characteristics. At the core of our framework lies the ColoNetBlock—an attention-based CNN architecture that skillfully merges multi-scale convolutional features with an advanced attention mechanism. This architecture enables the model to effectively detect both broader structural patterns and intricate local textures, even within degraded images. Trained on a specially curated real-world dataset, our model achieves a classification accuracy of 95%, surpassing current leading methods. To promote clinical confidence and transparency, we incorporate explainability tools such as LIME and Grad-CAM++ to pinpoint diagnostically relevant areas, providing clarity into the model’s reasoning. These findings set a new performance standard for endoscopic CRC detection and highlight the considerable promise of attention-based models in medical image analysis.

title: AI-Driven Discovery of Money Laundering in Financial Transactions Using Ensemble Machine Learning Techniques
authors: P.Satyanarayana, N. Siva Vennela, C H. Preethi, Arun M, N. V. Phani Sai Kumar, V. Gokula Krishnan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Money laundering is a major threat to financial institutions as well as financial integrity of the world because it gives a false impression of the source of the money which has been acquired illegally. Rule based systems have been known to be limited in their flexibility and do not grasp the complex patterns of laundering. In our paper, we are offering detection of money laundering using Machine Learning (ML) methods to make the process of detecting tax suspect transactions more efficient and accurate. The offered system will use both supervised and unsupervised training functions to determine suspicious actions in financial transactions. Significant qualities (including transaction amount, frequency, customer profiles and transfer routes) are isolated and examined with the view to training models that include Random Forest, AdaBoost and XGBoost. Compared performance analysis is carried out to obtain the best algorithm in terms of accuracy, precision, recall and f1-score. To safeguard the training process, the system is trained using simulated and real data on banking. High level methods like pre-processing of data like feature scaling, missing value imputation and outliers are used to enhance the data. The proposed approach attained a detection rate of more than 97 percent and negligible false positives as opposed to traditional methods. Moreover, the system allows sustaining perpetual learning where the systems accommodate new money laundering techniques with time. This piece of work proves the capabilities of using AI-powered systems in protecting financial systems against crime exploitation.

title: Real-Time Dynamic Sign Language Recognition with MediaPipe, LSTM, and Dynamic Time Warping
authors: Rohit Raj, R Sreemathy, Jayashree Jagdale, Mousami Turuk
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Dynamic Sign Language Recognition is an active area of Sign recognition research. It is important and helpful to communicate with speech and hearing-impaired people. Hearing loss is the most common sensory deficit in humans today. According to the estimate by the World Health Organisation (WHO), approximately 6.30 crore people in India suffer from conductive hearing loss. Many models have been developed for this solution, but they still face the problem of recognising dynamic signs. Our approach is to use Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) and Long Short-Term Memory(LSTM) with the MediaPipe framework to recognise dynamic signs in real-time and translate into text or voice. For experimentation, we curated a custom dataset comprising 50 dynamic Indian Sign Language (ISL) gestures, extracting hand and upper body key points using MediaPipe. DTW enables robust similarity detection between sign sequences by aligning their temporal keypoints, while LSTM networks enhance sequential pattern recognition. The proposed model achieves an accuracy of 87.5% across all 50 dynamic sign classes in real-time scenarios, demonstrating significant potential for practical deployment. This work advances Dynamic Sign Language Recognition by improving temporal sign interpretation and offers a scalable solution for assistive communication technologies.

title: An RF-Switch-Enabled Low-Power Dual-Mode Reconfigurable Reflective Surface
authors: Hang Yu, Edward A Ball, Rola Saad
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a novel reconfigurable reflective surface capable of selective magnitude control at sub-6 GHz frequencies. Each unit cell integrates a low-power single-pole double-throw (SPDT) RF switch beneath the ground plane, providing two distinct impedance states that govern the reflection coefficient of the unit cell. The proposed configuration enables dynamic switching between reflective and absorptive modes at 5.50 GHz. A full system prototype comprising an 8 × 8 unit cells was fabricated and tested, the measured results exhibit good agreement with full-wave simulations, with the 1-bit (0°-180°) reflection phase control at 5.05 GHz and reflection magnitude switching between -2.4 dB and -26 dB at 5.50 GHz under TE-polarized excitation. In addition, various geometric coding patterns were explored and implemented to further assess the surface’s capability for a thinned reconfigurability and improved power efficiency. Three sets of ring-based surface configurations were investigated in detail, demonstrating that the surface is capable of selective magnitude control at both 5.05 GHz and 5.50 GHz under these pattern configurations. Specifically, these configurations enable quantized reflection magnitude control at 5.05 GHz, with an average step size of circa 5 dB. The results confirm the surface’s flexible reconfigurability and reveal that activating only 75% of the available switches achieves performance comparable to full-surface operation, effectively reducing control complexity and overall power consumption.

title: Suppression of Surface Charge Accumulation on GIS Insulator in Extremely Cold Environment by Functional Nonlinear Conductivity Coating Design
authors: Zhen Li, Xiwen Liu, Lei Sun, Tianyu Qi, He Gao, Yaoxin Wei, Ji Liu, Chunming Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Surface charge accumulation distorts the electric field and induces flashover along the GIS insulator, harming the reliability of power system.
