title: Toward Safe Driving: Efficient Detection of Small Blurred Signs in Real-World Scenarios
authors: Yibo Wang, Dekui Wang, Jun Feng, Qirong Bo, Yaqiong Xing, Wei Zhou, Xingxing Hao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Accurate traffic sign recognition is critical for safe driving, as over half of traffic accidents stem from drivers’ negligence of traffic signs. Thus, developing robust traffic sign detection methods is essential to improve road safety. While existing object detection methods have achieved remarkable success, their performance in traffic sign detection is often limited by small object sizes and low-resolution appearances. To address these issues, this study proposes a novel traffic sign detection framework with three innovative components for precise localization and classification: 1) a hierarchical feature aggregation module that emphasizes high-level semantic information for traffic sign localization; 2) a cross-layer semantic residual network that enhances recognition of small and blurred signs via hierarchical feature interaction and fusion; 3) a lightweight feature alignment unit that bridges semantic gaps between cross-level representations. These components jointly tackle the challenges of detecting small and blurred traffic signs in real-world driving scenarios. Experiments were conducted on three public datasets (TT100K, CCTSDB2021, GTSDB). Results show the proposed model outperforms other methods with comparable parameters. Additionally, dynamic motion blur augmentation was applied to datasets to simulate real driving scenarios, and experiments confirm the proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance under such challenging conditions. Code is publicly available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Mo7nex/SAttFusion-YOLO</uri>

title: Optimizing Classification of Infrequent Labels by Reducing Variability in Label Distribution
authors: Ashutosh Agarwal
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper presents a novel solution, LEVER, designed to address the challenges posed by underperforming infrequent categories in Extreme Classification (XC) tasks. Infrequent categories, often characterized by sparse samples, suffer from high label inconsistency, which undermines classification performance. LEVER mitigates this problem by adopting a robust Siamese-style architecture, leveraging knowledge transfer to reduce label inconsistency and enhance the performance of One-vs-All classifiers. Comprehensive testing across multiple XC datasets reveals substantial improvements in the handling of infrequent categories, setting a new benchmark for the field. Additionally, the paper introduces two newly created multi-intent datasets, offering essential resources for future XC research.

title: AutoPathNavigator: A Predictive Framework for Path Optimization in Dynamic Autonomous Vehicle Systems
authors: U.Ragavee, S.Sharadh Sureshbabu, Bhavana Selvakumar, Lisha Dharshini K.M, S.Sakthivel, K.Mohammed Suhail
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Operation in variable and unexpected situations requires autonomous vehicles to have real-time and adaptive path planning functions to stay safe and operate well. Dijkstra and A*, types of traditional algorithms, tend not to accommodate changes in the environment, the unexpected and movement properly in real-time. With this work, we suggest AutoPathNavigator, a path optimization framework that spots and learned upcoming issues to aid dynamic autonomous vehicles. Here, multi-sensor data is combined, HD maps are reconstructed, paths are predicted, motion is planned with reinforcement learning and MPC is used to improve trajectories in real time. It uses a combination of graph-based and sampling-based methods to create candidates and it relies on a Transformer to make predictions for environment and obstacle movements. Benchmarked datasets and highly detailed environment simulations were used to compare AutoPathNavigator with A*, PRM, RRT*, MPC-only planners and end-to-end CNNs. Data shows that the navigation accuracy of AutoPathNavigator is 95.61% which is significantly ahead of A* (82.01%) and Dijkstra (84.17%) planners. In addition, it gets the lowest collision risk rating of 1.10, the highest path optimality of 93.64% and the best energy efficiency of 5.96 km/kWh all with a short latency of 21.28ms.

title: AI-Driven Consumer Behaviour Prediction: A Machine Learning Based Application
authors: Sheikh Majedul Huq, Shuly Aktar, Gyanendra Kumar, R. Gopal, Sonia Afroz, Md. Akkas Ali
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Today, fashion retailers face immense pressure to adapt their marketing strategies to keep pace with changing shopper demands, rapid technological advances, and intense competition in the highly connected digital world. Social media has become a critical channel for brand engagement, allowing merchants unprecedented access to insights into consumer preferences and behaviors. However, contrast this with traditional digital marketing methods, which often do not offer the level of personalization, immediacy, and relevance in context needed by the modern-day consumer. AI in digital marketing has now bridged this gap by permitting fashion companies to automate decision-making using large user databases and generate personalized content strategies. This process focuses on the integration of AI in the field of digital marketing, considering in particular the ability of machine learning models to predict consumer decisions and engagement in the fashion retail sector. Given the real user and product-level data from the fashion marketplaces, this work investigates customer purchase intention, user social interaction, and purchase decision metrics using Random Forest Regressor (RF), Support Vector Regression (SVR), and XGBoost, and the features have been pre-processed through engineering, including integration, missing value handling, categorical encoding, and feature scaling. The performance of the proposed method is evaluated using the MAE, MSE, RMSE, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> metrics. The proposed ML models were more effective than the baseline models with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 99.67%, 30.09%, and 99.94%; MAE of (0.0310, 0.0792, 0.0297); MSE of (0.1673, 0.7708, 0.1389); and RMSE of (0.4090, 0.8780, 0.3727) for RF, SVR, and XGBoost, respectively. ANOVA and the Friedman test provisionally confirmed that the differences in model performance were significant. The results show that XGBoost has the best predictive capacity (R2 = 99.94%), outperforming Random Forest and SVR. The study contributes to the understanding of AI as it relates to CME profiling and efficient use of social media targeting.

title: Emotion Detection in Real-Time Using Computer Vision Techniques
authors: Srividya, Narendiran B S, Vibha Prabhu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: In recent years, the field of human-computer interaction has made significant strides with the application of emotional intelligence in systems. Understanding and appropriately responding to human emotion can greatly enhance user experience in education, healthcare, marketing, and entertainment sectors. The aim of this project is to do real-time facial emotion detection using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) with live image processing through OpenCV. The model is trained on FER dataset which provides numerous facial images with corresponding expressions representing different emotions. Using Flask along with HTML and CSS, a GUI was created that allows for real-time emotion prediction from webcam video feed. The model demonstrates an acceptable performance of accurately detecting 68-70% of the time, which is deemed effective for the recognition of common emotions such as happy, sad, fear, and neutral. This paper presents the implemented workflow, the challenges encountered, analysis of results, and the additional steps needed to enhance system reliability and interactivity. Real-time emotion recognition enables emotionally intelligent systems that support mental health monitoring and enhance personalized learning experiences.

title: Machine Learning-Driven Detection of UPI Fraudulent Activities
authors: Khedekar Ayuti, Prof. Ganesh Manerkar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The rapid adoption of Unified Payments Interface (UPI) has revolutionized digital payments in India, offering seamless and instant transactions. However, with increased usage, fraudulent activities like phishing, vishing, fake payments, and QR code scams have surged. Traditional rule-based fraud detection systems struggle to address these sophisticated tactics. This paper proposes a machine learning-based solution that integrates advanced models, including Random Forest, XGBoost, LSTM, and CNN, to detect and classify various fraud types in real-time, enhancing UPI transaction security.The system combines a hybrid approach that processes transaction data, QR code images, and audio from vishing attempts to identify fraud patterns. It is trained on a diverse dataset of labeled UPI transactions, QR codes, and voice data, enabling it to detect evolving fraud behaviors. The model’s real-time alert system ensures timely detection, with promising results in accuracy. This approach provides a scalable solution to improve the security of digital payments, with future enhancements aimed at refining detection capabilities and adapting to new fraud tactics.

title: Data-Driven Behavioral Segmentation in Retail using Ensemble Learning Techniques
authors: Neha Nahariya, Rohit Bajaj
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Behavioral segmentation has become one of the critical tools in the competitive world of retail in order to better engage customers and streamline operations. In this project, machine learning (ML) methods suggest classifying retail consumers using transactional and behavioral data. An array of classification models is developed, such as BayesNet, Naive Bayes(NB), Logistic Regression(LR), IBK, and Decision Table(DT), which allowed evaluating the consumer behavior tendencies. Iterative enhancement is based on adding data balance and feature selection to mitigate imbalances in classes and to decrease dimensionality, and to enhance subsequent models generalisation. Ensemble learning methods are used to maximize the performance of the predicted results and ended up with a Random Forest(RF) classifier. The model provided the highest accuracy of classification of 91.47 percent compared to any other models, and this indicates that it could model complicated behavioral patterns. The paper cements the need for preprocessing and ensemble approaches in behavioral segmentation polishing of modeling ML. This proposed framework facilitates the application of knowledge because retailers provide personalized experiences, enhance their loyalty programs, and make strategic decisions based on their information about their customers.

title: An Intelligent Approach for Breast Cancer Diagnosis Using Fuzzy Logic and Extreme Learning Machine
authors: Davinder Paul Singh, Rozy Rani, Sindhu V, V.M. Revathi, T. Kosalairaman, P. Mano Paul
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The long-term prognosis and mortality rates can be improved with early identification of breast cancer. The time-consuming and expensive procedures of mammography, MRI, ultrasound, CT, PT, and biopsy have been the subject of much research; nevertheless, these approaches are not suitable for younger women and can be rather expensive. This study employed cutting-edge image processing to improve early breast cancer detection. The researchers utilised anisotropic filtering to reduce background noise in medical images after picking mammograms at random from the Digital Database for Screening Mammography. The use of morphology-based feature extraction allowed for autonomous and accurate categorisation after mass segmentation using a genetic algorithm with recurrent thresholding. By merging a KF with an ELM enhanced with an AV, a new model named KF-av-elm improves diagnostic accuracy. Medical imaging noise and estimating errors are both significantly reduced by the combination method. Their accuracy rating of 98.28% allowed them to outperform other approaches. The KF-av-elm model appears to be a reliable, efficient, and effective diagnostic tool; its adoption may lead to better identification and outcomes for breast cancer patients.

title: Implementation of Quantum Key Distribution for Secure data transmission in Optical Communication
authors: S. Krithiga, Ajay V, Manoojitth R, Prasanna Guptha
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) is a potential defense against future quantum attacks that can break traditional encryption algorithms, such as RSA and ECC. The current work is based on the development of the BB84 protocol with the features of utilizing quantum mechanics and eavesdropping detection through signal disturbance. A simulation of the BB84 protocol was built using polarization encoding over a 10 km single-mode optical fiber using OptiSystem software. The system consists of standard telecom equipment, such as a continuous wave laser at a frequency of 193.1 THz, polarization controllers, and single-photon detectors. Both secure and insecure cases were simulated, where an attacker (Eve) had used an intercept-resend attack. The outcome showed significant Bit Error Rate (BER) and Quantum Bit Error Rate (QBER) increases during attacks, thus proving the protocol's vulnerability to interference. In addition, a special intrusion detection system based on BER was used to identify such anomalies in real-time. The simulation illustrates the feasibility of using QKD in today's optical networks to secure communications.

title: Identification of SMAW Defects Using Radiographic Image Analysis and Deep Learning: A Comparative Analysis between Binary and Multiclass Classification
authors: Nur Md Alif Ul Islam, Md Abdullah Mia, Omer Tahsin
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Shielded metal arc welding, commonly known as SMAW, is one of the manufacturing industry's most widely used joining processes. The quality of welding joints is often affected by welding defects and flaws. Most frequently ensued welding defects are internal defects by nature and are associated with difficulties in identification through visual inspection. Radiographic examination is highly popular for the successful identification of internal defects. Implementing computer vision techniques and deep learning algorithms in identifying manufacturing defects is highly recognized and famous worldwide for its higher accuracy and faster defect detection compared to manual and conventional quality monitoring techniques. This study proposes a defect detection system for detecting defects generated from the shielded metal arc welding process using a deep convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture. At first, the CNN model was trained with a classified image dataset of commonly occurring welding defects during the SMAW process. An image augmentation technique was incorporated with supervised learning to reduce the requirement of extensive training datasets without affecting prediction accuracy. A comparative analysis between binary classification and multiclass classification techniques has been carried out to justify the selection of the proposed methodology, which can identify defect images with higher accuracy. The deep CNN model developed in this study for binary classification problems can work with an accuracy of up to 97.6% with a lower prediction time in detecting commonly occurring welding defects. Similarly, the deep CNN model developed in this study for multi-class classification problems can work with an accuracy of up to 91% with a lower prediction time in detecting commonly occurring welding defects.

title: A Hybrid Wavelet Transform and Machine Learning Framework for Early Cataract Detection
authors: Nagarani Nagarajan, Somaskandan J S, Mathiezhilan P, Siva Surya Rajan S
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The early detection of cataracts is extremely important in the prevention of vision impairment and blindness. In this work, we propose a completely new approach, integrating Wavelet Transform-Based feature extraction and ML classification techniques such as SVM and RF, for the early detection of cataracts. Unlike other such approaches, which require a model with vast datasets, this constitutes good use of DWT and CWT in extracting frequency-domain features from fundus and slit-lamp images that allow for greater sensitivity to subtle opacity changes in the lens. Features are extracted and fed into ML classifiers for automatic detection. This method provides a computationally less intensive, interpretable, and scalable alternative when compared with CNNs. Results obtained using the proposed method will be evaluated against CNN model results on standard medical image datasets.Furthermore, the interpretability of our proposed method is higher than that of the CNNs, thus allowing ophthalmologists to appreciate the decision mechanism based on extracted wavelet features. Moreover, the model is scalable in terms of the number of labeled training samples so that it can work in a low-resource clinical setup where deep learning cannot be used due to insufficient annotated data.

title: META Surface Based Reconfigurable MIMO Antenna for 5G Communication
authors: S. Kumaran, R. Ramesh, M. Varun, Beulah Jackson, Anitha Gopalan, Harivijaya.K
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This work provides a new design of a metasurface-based reconfigurable Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (MIMO) antenna specifically for 5G wireless communication applications. Using the special electromagnetic characteristics of designed metasuraces, the suggested antenna achieves frequency agility, dynamic beam steering, and polarization reconfiguration without mechanical motion. To reduce mutual coupling and increase isolation, a single-layer reconfigurable metasurface is coupled with small MIMO components, hence improving system performance. PIN diodes included into its unit cells electrically regulate the metasurface structure, allowing quick reconfiguration throughout sub-6 GHz and millimeter-wave bands. Addressing the strict criteria of 5G networks including high data rate, low latency, and huge connection, the suggested antenna shows a small profile, high gain, low envelope correlation coefficient (ECC), and considerable diversity performance. Performance of the antenna is validated using full-wave electromagnetic models and prototype observations. Offering a scalable and reasonably priced solution for smart antenna uses, the findings show the viability and efficiency of the metasurface technique for next 5G and beyond wireless systems.

title: Edge-AI Enabled Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Botnet Intrusion Detection in Modern IoT-Driven Cyber Ecosystems
authors: Mridula, Shikha Shukla, Karn Singh, Jaya Malviya, Kirti Rawat, Rovin Tiwari
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The cyber threat environment, which was characterized by comparatively static and centralized attacks in form of botnets, has transformed with the explosive proliferation of IoT-enabled infrastructures. The proposed work presents an Edge-AI enabled hybrid deep learning architecture, being the first of its kind to combine both Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks in a single architecture to identify spatial traffic patterns and to capture the intricate temporal relationships between network flows, respectively. The proposed architecture targets the deployment of resource-constrained edge environments and satisfies the current cyber-physical systems with a compelling demand to have decentralized and low-latency threat intelligence. As compared to conventional cloud-based intrusion detection systems, we have reduced communication overhead and local intelligence allows detection in real-time. Broad empirical evaluation of hybrid CNN-LSTM model against current botnet datasets shows that hybrid network has an advantage over deep learning models employed alone in detecting stealthy and polymorphic botnet behaviors, displaying better accuracy, early-stage detection, and false positives. Potentially, this work goes beyond improving the existing paradigm of edge-enabled cybersecurity to create a basis of autonomous and adaptive threat detection systems in changing IoT-driven environments.

title: A Vision-Based Framework for Classifying Cricket Shots Using CNN and Random Forest
authors: Deepak Banerjee, Raghavendra Sridhar, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Gaurav Sharma, Manish Kumar Singla
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This investigation introduced a classification framework that derives from deep learning and focuses on the direct recognition of five unique cricket shots, namely: Pull, Cover Drive, Square Cut, Straight Drive, and Hook. Using a well-curated dataset composed of 10,700 labeled images, the proposed model attains an overwhelming overall accuracy of 98.59%. All classes pull (Precision, Recall, F1-Score – 98.59%): Cover Drive (98.65%): Square Cut (98.56%): Straight Drive (98.66%): Hook (98.52%). Additionally, the model has shown well-distributed class consistency with support values between 2,030 and 2,240 for each shot and support proportions between 0.19 and 0.21. Each class was able to yield an accuracy of 99%, confirming the efficiency and sturdiness of the model as well. The results prove that the model is quite capable of identifying cricket shots accurately, along with their classification, which has great value in the fields of sports analysis, training improvement, and intelligent video analysis systems.

title: Agentic AI-based Question Generator Using Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Iranna Rudrakumar Tonni, Sanjana Kavatagi, Rashmi Rachh
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: In academic environments, assessment process requires constructing descriptive question papers that reflect Bloom’s taxonomy levels and align with intended course outcomes. However, this is complex and time-intensive process and for structured, outcome-based assessment, there exists a scarcity of experienced educators. The proliferation of generative AI tools provides educators some aid in this process. These intelligent tools can support in generating high-quality, unit-wise questions tailored to curriculum needs. This paper proposes agentic AI based system designed to automatically generate personalized, descriptive question papers by processing academic inputs such as the syllabus, textbook, and a reference sample paper. The proposed system uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)and two offline LLM models used for experimentation are OpenChat and Mistral and Langchain framework. For the study purpose, syllabus and text books prescribed for Post graduate course and engineering courses of Visvesvaraya Technological University has been used. The performance of both the models has been evaluated using various aspects such as clarity, scope, time, redundancy etc.

title: Apple Plant Disease Detection Using Transfer Learning and Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Shivani Gupta, Pooja Rani
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Digital image processing is a crucial part of the information technology department as nearly all the multimedia data are captured, processed, presented and transmitted with the help of this technology. Prompt and accurate detection of plant infections is necessary for successful farming. Early diagnosis has been the most effective way of preventing waste of resources, which will then lead to more efficient and sustainable agriculture. The real problem is the detection and determination of the diseases within the countless plants in the different environmental conditions. The current study presents a plant disease detection model using a technique called transfer learning, which involves integrating VGG16 with CNN. In the proposed technique, the data is taken as input which is further processed to increase quality of the images using GAN Model. The proposed model is implemented in python and results are analyzed in terms accuracy, precision, recall. The proposed model achieves accuracy of 98.5% percent for the plant disease detection.

title: Uniaxial Tensile Mechanical Behavior and Deformation Mechanisms of Pristine and Defective SiGe Nanosheet: Molecular dynamics Study
authors: Md. Shahadat Akbar, A S M Jannatul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: SiGe, an intriguing semiconductor material, has drawn considerable attention from researchers, mainly because of its compatibility with silicon technology, enhanced carrier mobility, optical devices, thermal management features, and enormous possibilities for strain technology. As it is a highly promising material, extensive research on SiGe sheets will provide valuable insights into the material's fracture behavior at the nanoscale, offering design optimization and ensuring the structural integrity of SiGe-based devices. The investigation of the mechanical properties of all possible defective SiGe will guide the development of more reliable nanoscale devices. As well, the computation of stress intensity factors and notch sensitivity factors facilitates the prediction of failure conditions and the design of resilient SiGe-based components. This paper presents a thorough analysis of the mechanical characteristics of two-dimentional SiGe sheets with initial crack and notch defects using molecular dynamics simulations. This study explores the stress and strain till device failure, along with the elastic modulus of SiGe sheets, observing how these properties change eventually as defect sizes increase. Notably, the findings indicate that the fracture strain fluctuates with the size of the defect, while the fracture strength follows a similar trend. This research contributes to a greater comprehension of the mechanical behavior of SiGe sheets, providing valuable information for the design and optimization of SiGe-based structures and devices in nanotechnology and microelectronics.

title: SMOTE-Enhanced Ensemble Learning for Hepatitis C Risk Stratification with Feature Importance Analysis
authors: Sharmin Sultana Akhi, Md. Samiul Alom, Rukaiya Taha, Md. Anamul Hoque Tomal, Ferdaus Anam Jibon
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Hepatitis C virus (HCV) continues to pose a major global health burden, yet early risk stratification remains challenging due to class imbalance in clinical datasets. This study presents a novel ensemble-based machine learning framework enhanced by the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) to improve HCV risk classification. We systematically evaluated six classifiers—Support Vector Machine, k-Nearest Neighbors, Random Forest, XGBoost, Gradient Boosting, and Extra Trees—combined with hyperparameter optimization and rigorous statistical validation. The Extra Trees model achieved the highest performance, with an accuracy of 98.99% after SMOTE application. Cross-validation and statistical significance tests confirmed the reliability and superiority of ensemble methods. Our findings highlight the potential of SMOTE-boosted ensemble learning in advancing data-driven clinical decision support systems for hepatitis C management.

title: Federated Machine Learning for Smart Healthcare Sensor Calibration
authors: Jayakumar D, Sathiyavani V, Balamanikandan A, Sudharsan G, Ankala Satya Prabha, Venkata Karthika V
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This article presents a federated machine learning-based calibration scheme for privacy-preserving edge biomedical sensor networks. Traditional centralized calibration methods are plagued by data communication bottlenecks, vulnerability to compromise in sensitive biopotential measurements, and poor scalability in heterogeneous sensor environments. By way of response, we propose a decentralized system where each node of an adaptive biasing and multimodal signal conditioning analog front-end performs local model training employing light-weight machine learning techniques. The models are federated via a federated learning protocol to enable collaborative calibration parameter improvement without exposure to raw data. It is based on PyVISA-integrated automation pipelines and Python-driven inference pipelines to facilitate efficient measurement orchestration in sensor arrays. Cryptographic primitives like secure multiparty computation and differential privacy mechanisms are added to protect parameter exchanges during federated aggregation. Experimental validation across synthetic and real-world biopotential signals exhibits improved calibration accuracy, reduced convergence latency, and efficient privacy compliance. This approach delivers a scalable and computationally efficient cognitive biomedical sensing paradigm that facilitates secure, interoperable, and patient-aware Internet of Healthcare Things (IoHT) deployments.

title: Multilingual Deepfake Audio Detection Using Convolutional Neural Networks and Gradient Boosting
authors: Malar P, Narmadhadevi M, Mohammed Shimak A, Suvathi G, Naveen T
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Growing adoption of audio Deep fakes in light of innovation in text-to-speech (TTS) and voice conversion (VC) technologies calls for security, privacy, and public trust risks. Generated voices are used to mimic people and are capable of being voices that may be abused for fraud or spreading misinformation. Escalation of serious threats from deep fakes, hyper-realistic synthetic audio cloned to mimic any human voice, calls for crucial intervention in areas including digital security, media trustworthiness, and voice-based biometric applications. A hybrid framework for deep fake detection, fusion of best using Gradient Boosting (GB) and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), contour aware. CNNs analyze spectrograms to detect complex temporal and spectral patterns that distinguish tampered recordings, while ensemble learners are better at capturing craft in low-level extracted audio features such as Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs), spectral roll-off, zero-crossing rate, and chrome. Performance is higher for various deep fake creation approaches such as multilingual speech synthesis and emotional tone conversion by integrating prediction and offering a view into both model.bmp. Selected dataset was carefully chosen and system was redesigned to verify state-of-art systems to create valuable dataset; audio samples are utilized for training and validating framework. Combined approach significantly outperforms single classifiers in measures such as precision and recall. Under a wide range of man-made audio manipulations, proposed approach achieves accuracy of 97.4%, strengthening defenses against audio fraud in an era when digital fabrics are increasingly woven with voice, promoting greater trustworthiness and resilience in information systems.

title: Explainable AI-Based Detection of Zero-Day Attacks in Multi-Cloud Cybersecurity Systems
authors: Praveen Kumar Pemmasani, Krishna Chaitanya Gonugunta, Bharath Kishore Gudepu, B.Jegajothi
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Zero-day attacks represent a growing threat to multi-cloud cybersecurity systems, exploiting unknown vulnerabilities across distributed platforms. Traditional intrusion detection systems struggle to generalize to such attacks, particularly in heterogeneous cloud environments. This paper introduces X-ZeroNet, a novel explainable deep learning architecture designed to detect zero-day attacks with high accuracy and transparency. The proposed model integrates three key components: a Multi-Resolution Attention Encoder to extract temporal patterns, a Rule-Enhanced Sparse Graph Neural Network to model flow relationships, and a Counterfactual Explainability Layer to provide interpretable justifications for predictions. A Cloud-Aware Dispatcher ensures effective handling of multi-cloud traffic segmentation. Experimental evaluations were conducted using the UNSW-NB15 dataset. Compared to existing models like GNN and XGBoost, X-ZeroNet achieved superior results: an accuracy of 96.1%, precision of 95.7%, recall of 96.2%, and an F1-score of 95.9%. Additionally, it demonstrated a fast inference time of 1.5 seconds, making it practical for real-time deployment. The ROC curve yielded an AUC score of 0.96, highlighting its strong discriminative power. An ablation study confirmed the importance of each architectural module, and hyperparameter tuning identified the optimal configuration for performance. X-ZeroNet offers a powerful solution for zero-day detection in complex cloud ecosystems by combining accuracy, speed, and explainability. Its modular design makes it adaptable for deployment in varied multi-cloud infrastructures, laying the foundation for more secure and transparent AI-driven cybersecurity systems.

title: An Accelerated Sudoku Solver Using Deep Q-Learning with Heuristic Assistance
authors: Jyothsna Kopera, Sathvik Reddy Goguru, Vivek Reddy Yarram, Shaista Farhat, M. A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This paper introduces a hybrid method for solving Sudoku puzzles by combining Deep Q-Learning (DQN) with heuristic backtracking. Sudoku is treated as a Constraint Satisfaction Problem (CSP), and the solution approach merges the flexibility of reinforcement learning with the reliability of logic-based reasoning. A Deep Q-Network is trained in a custom Gym environment to develop strategies through interactive learning. When the agent is unable to proceed typically due to sparse rewards or more complex puzzle scenarios a heuristic backup is activated to finish the puzzle using rule-based logic. Experimental results show that the isolated DQN agent only managed to get an accuracy of 73.3% on simple puzzles, while only 31.4% and 14.3% on medium and hard puzzles, respectively. But the hybrid model obtained a 100% overall success rate by completing all 100 test puzzles at all difficulty levels. These system integrates the learning potential of DQN with deterministic reasoning, thus overcoming the constraints of learning-based techniques that are purely reliant in structured problem spaces like Sudoku.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework Combining Fourier Denoising and Dynamic Model Weighting for GHI Prediction
authors: Quazi Sarwar Muhtaseem, Bonolata Biswas Taya
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of Solar Irradiance is critical for the stable operation of power grids and the effective management of solar energy resources. The inherent intermittency of solar power and noise in measurement data, however, present significant forecasting challenges. This paper proposes a novel, physics-constrained, adaptively weighted ensemble model to enhance Global Horizontal Irradiance (GHI) prediction accuracy. Our approach first employs the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) to analyze and filter high-frequency noise from the input meteorological data. Subsequently, a diverse set of base models such as Random Forest, FFN, LSTM, 1D CNN, and a Transformer are trained on this filtered dataset. Instead of static stacking, we introduce dynamic weighting mechanisms. The final predictions are constrained by physical limits to ensure realistic outputs. When evaluated against models without noise filtering and ensembles with static weights, our proposed framework demonstrates a significant improvement in forecasting accuracy, reducing the MSE and other benchmarks. To ensure robustness, we used a dataset from a location with six distinct seasonal variations. The main purpose of the research is to understand the synergistic benefit of combining signal processing with adaptive deep learning ensembles for robust solar forecasting.

title: Descriptor: A Brazilian roadkill animals for object detection GAN-augmented dataset (BRAGAN)
authors: Henrique Souza de Abreu Martins, Gabriel Souto Ferrante, Rodolfo Ipolito Meneguette
abstract: Collisions between vehicles and wild animals pose a major threat to biodiversity and road safety in Brazil, resulting in the death of millions of animals each year. Despite the seriousness of this issue, there are still a few open datasets focused on computer vision research on collision detection. To fill this gap, we present BRAGAN, a new GAN-augmented dataset derived from the Brazilian Road Animals (BRA-Dataset). Although the BRA-Dataset provided an initial collection of real images of five species of medium and large mammals most frequently involved in roadkill, its limited size highlighted the difficulty of acquiring such images in natural conditions. BRAGAN expands this resource by leveraging several GAN architectures, trained separately for each species on carefully preprocessed subsets of data. The resulting dataset integrates real samples and those generated by the best network according to qualitative criteria, with Wasserstein Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP) standing out, increasing the variability in the animals’ appearance, posture, and background consistency. BRAGAN was designed to train and compare object detection models, such as YOLO, and is publicly available to support applications in computer vision, wildlife monitoring, conservation technology, and road safety planning.

title: Deep LoRA-Unfolding Networks for Image Restoration_supp1-3661406.pdf
authors: yongyong Chen
abstract: Deep unfolding networks (DUNs), combining conventional iterative optimization algorithms and deep neural networks into a multi-stage framework, have achieved remarkable accomplishments in Image Restoration (IR), such as spectral imaging reconstruction, compressive sensing and super-resolution. It unfolds the iterative optimization steps into a stack of sequentially linked blocks. Each block consists of a Gradient Descent Module (GDM) and a Proximal Mapping Module (PMM) which is equivalent to a denoiser from a Bayesian perspective, operating on Gaussian noise with a known level. However, existing DUNs suffer from two critical limitations: (i) their PMMs share identical architectures and denoising objectives across stages, ignoring the need for stage-specific adaptation to varying noise levels; and (ii) their chain of structurally repetitive blocks results in severe parameter redundancy and high memory consumption, hindering deployment in large-scale or resource-constrained scenarios. To address these challenges, we introduce generalized Deep Low-rank Adaptation (LoRA) Unfolding Networks for image restoration, named LoRun, harmonizing denoising objectives and adapting different denoising levels between stages with compressed memory usage for more efficient DUN. LoRun introduces a novel paradigm where a single pretrained base denoiser is shared across all stages, while lightweight, stage-specific LoRA adapters are injected into the PMMs to dynamically modulate denoising behavior according to the noise level at each unfolding step. This design decouples the core restoration capability from task-specific adaptation, enabling precise control over denoising intensity without duplicating full network parameters and achieving up to N times parameter reduction for an N-stage DUN with on-par or better performance. Extensive experiments conducted on three IR tasks validate the efficiency of our method.

title: Quantum Computing in Transportation Engineering: A Survey
authors: Shriyank Somvanshi, Subasish Das, Md Monzurul Islam, Sazzad Bin Bashar Polock, Gaurab Chhetri, Darrell Anderson
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Quantum computing introduces powerful computational paradigms capable of addressing optimization and machine learning challenges that are increasingly critical in transportation engineering. This paper presents a comprehensive survey of how quantum algorithms and models are being applied across transportation domains, including intelligent transportation systems, traffic management, vehicle routing, logistics, and autonomous systems. Unlike prior reviews that primarily introduce quantum theory, we systematically connect fundamental quantum principles-qubits, superposition, entanglement, and quantum gates-with specific transportation use cases. We synthesize recent findings showing that the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm, Grover’s search, and hybrid quantum–classical solvers can improve performance in combinatorial problems such as vehicle routing and dynamic traffic signal control, while quantum machine learning models (e.g., Quantum Convolutional Neural Networks, Quantum Generative Adversarial Networks, and clustering algorithms) demonstrate promise in traffic prediction, anomaly detection, and autonomous vehicle perception. Our review highlights that although quantum hardware limitations (noise, scalability, connectivity) remain significant, empirical studies already suggest measurable improvements in solution quality and computational efficiency when compared to classical heuristics. The key contribution of this survey is to map the landscape of quantum computing in transportation engineering, identify application-specific advantages and current bottlenecks, and outline future research directions toward scalable, domain-specific quantum algorithms. By doing so, we provide transportation researchers and practitioners with a structured foundation to integrate quantum approaches into emerging mobility systems, with implications for efficiency, safety, sustainability, and equity.

title: Collaborative Vehicular Perception Against Adversarial Attacks: Overview, Challenges, and Potential Solutions
authors: Zhiping Lin, Liang Xiao, Hongyi Chen, Zefang Lv, Rui Zhao
abstract: Collaborative vehicular perception enables connected autonomous vehicles (CAVs) to share sensing data such as point clouds, images and driving status to enhance perception accuracy and range, which is vulnerable to adversarial attacks such as jamming and data fabrication. This article provides an overview of collaborative vehicular perception and its associated security threats, with particular emphasis on intelligent attackers that leverage machine learning to craft dynamic and imperceptible perturbations to mislead CAV’s perception systems. Defense strategies such as spatial consistency checks and adversary learning are discussed, offering insights for future research on developing robust perception systems. Based on the autonomous driving dataset, we provide the case studies for showcasing the perception performance of the RL-based anti-jamming collaborative perception and the spatial consistency checking against data fabrication attacks, analyzing the impact of jamming types and attack intensity on the perception accuracy.

title: Deep Learning-Driven Acne Diagnosis: A CNN-Logistic Regression Approach
authors: Deepak Banerjee, Raghavendra Sridhar, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Anuj Kumar Jain, Gaurav Sharma
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: In this study, a deep learning-based model has been established for accurately classifying different types of acne: Nodules, Blackheads, and Papules. This classification model has achieved remarkable accuracy as well as reliability. The proposed model is found to perform extremely well in different metrics of evaluation. In case of Nodules, the precision, recall, and F1-score have achieved 95.89% in each of the metrics. Similar to this pattern, Blackheads were classified with 96.19% in all three metrics, while Papules achieved their superiority at 96.25%. Such findings reflect the consistent performance of the model in identifying different types of acne. The dataset comprised 11,590 images of annotated acne, which provided class-wise support of 3650 for Nodules (31%), 3940 for Blackheads (34%), and 4000 for Papules (35%). The model retained an overall classification accuracy of 97% across all three classes despite the unequal sample proportions, thus reflecting robustness and generalizability. Moreover, the overall accuracy of the model turns out to be 96.11%, which suggests an excellent predictive capability. This is an attempt toward building an automated dermatological diagnostic system that works as a good reference tool to classify acne types accurately and push forward the intelligent skincare solutions.

authors: N/A
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)

title:
Enhanced Radiation Hardness in Ferroelectric Field-Effect Transistors Using Compositionally Graded HfZrO
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    Films
authors: Jung-Ting Chang, Sheng-Yen Zheng, Wei-Ning Kao, Yung-Hsien Wu
abstract: The impact of γ-ray irradiation on ferroelectric field-effect transistors (FeFETs) with different HfZrO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> (HZO) compositions was systematically investigated in terms of polarization behavior, endurance, transfer characteristics, and retention. γ-ray exposure induces ionization and defect formation, particularly oxygen vacancies (Vo), whose aggregation into defect clusters significantly degrades device performance. The initial Vo concentration is shown to be a key factor determining radiation hardness. FeFETs with uniform HZO exhibit dense domain formation and severe defect clustering, leading to pronounced remanent polarization (P<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</sub>) and memory window (MW) degradation. By contrast, devices with compositionally graded HZO mitigates defect accumulation and those with bottom Zr rich composition demonstrate the best radiation resilience due to their inherently lower Vo concentration. After 1 Mrad irradiation and 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</sup> cycling, they retain a large MW of 1.86 V with only 11.1 % degradation of the initial MW. Moreover, retention measurements reveal excellent long-term stability, with ~88.6 % of the MW preserved after 10 years by extrapolation. These results highlight the critical role of Vo control through compositional engineering in achieving FeFETs with superior radiation tolerance.

title: Beyond the Black Box: Interpretable Machine Learning for Hospital Efficiency Assessment
authors: Agostino Marengo, Lerina Aversano, Anna Esposito, Michele Mastroianni, Vito Santamato
abstract: Hospital efficiency optimization remains a critical challenge as healthcare systems worldwide face mounting economic pressures. Traditional efficiency assessment methods often lack predictive capability, while machine learning approaches frequently operate as opaque "black boxes" unsuitable for high-stakes healthcare decisions. This study presents an integrated interpretable machine learning and multi-objective optimization framework for hospital resource allocation, bridging Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA) with explainable artificial intelligence and prescriptive analytics. The methodology comprises a four-stage analytical pipeline: (1) DEA-based efficiency scoring through Principal Component Analysis, (2) Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering with Ward linkage for hospital stratification into efficiency tiers, (3) Decision Tree classification with SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) interpretability analysis, and (4) NSGA-II bi-objective optimization with data-driven grid search for context-specific resource allocation strategies. Validated on 127 public hospitals across five institutional typologies, the framework achieved 94.87% classification accuracy (AUC = 0.993). SHAP analysis revealed that energy costs (mean |SHAP| = 0.2416) and medical staffing levels (0.2257) constitute the primary efficiency determinants, while equipment showed negligible contribution. Multi-objective optimization demonstrated substantial strategic heterogeneity: optimal weight configurations ranged from balanced (0.5/0.5) to energy-focused (0.9/0.1), with personnel ratios spanning 51%-77% across hospital types. Critically, 47% of hospitals require clinical staff reductions while 16% require increases, demonstrating that uniform resource allocation guidelines are inadequate for heterogeneous healthcare systems. By integrating explanatory analysis with prescriptive optimization, this framework transforms black-box predictions into transparent, context-specific, evidence-based recommendations for sustainable healthcare resource management.

title: Probabilistic Shaping for Peak Power Constrained IM-DD Optical Fiber Communication Systems: Perspective of Envelope Control
authors: Dongdong Zou, Wei Wang, Jiawen Yao, Fan Li, Gangxiang Shen, Yi Cai
abstract: Probabilistic shaping (PS) has attracted significant attention in intensity-modulation and direct-detection (IM-DD) optical fiber communication systems. Nevertheless, owing to the unique system model and inherent limitations, the practical applicability of the PS technique in IM-DD systems remains an open question, particularly in scenarios involving system memory. In this paper, diverging from the conventional PS technique, a novel indirect PS scheme tailored for practical peak power constrained (PPC) IM-DD optical fiber communication systems is proposed by taking the system memory into consideration. The key idea lies in strategically controlling the signal envelope to mitigate system memory-induced direct and indirect impairments, such as peak-to-average power ratio enhancement, overshoot, and nonlinearity distortions. The proposed scheme incorporates a dynamic selective mapping (DSLM) mechanism at the transmitter, enabling an untypical bit-to-symbol mapping rule in which the current symbol is not only determined by the current bit sequence but also by previously generated symbols within a specified memory length. At the receiver side, a modified turbo equalizer is proposed to achieve the recovery of ambiguous bits induced by DSLM. Experimental verification in a 56GBaud 8-ary pulse amplitude modulation (PAM8) system demonstrates that the proposed scheme exhibits 1 dB receiver sensitivity improvement over 2 km single-mode fiber transmission. In addition, the proposed scheme has also been demonstrated to be compatible with the typical probabilistic amplitude shaping architecture, enabling a simple and fine-granularity rate adaptation capability. To the best of our knowledge, this work opens a new sight for the application of PS technique in PPC IM-DD systems with memory.

title: Adaptive Shared Cascade Navigation Control of Magnetic Microrobots in Unstructured Dynamic Environments_supp3-3658831.mp4
authors: Huaping Wang
abstract: Precise motion control of magnetic microrobots in complex and dynamic environments remains a critical challenge for enabling key applications such as targeted therapy and micromanipulation. Purely manual teleoperation is prone to operator fatigue and error, while fully autonomous systems often lack the robustness and adaptability to handle. Here, we propose a human–machine shared cascade control method for magnetically driven microrobots, which effectively integrates human cognitive intelligence with machine autonomy for collision-free navigation in dynamic environments. The outer-loop hybrid shared control unit smoothly modulates control authority in response to real-time collision risk, dynamically integrating the operator instructions and the autonomous navigation system output guided by the enhanced artificial potential field method to formulate the guidance law. For the inner-loop motion tracking, a data-driven adaptive orientation controller is designed, which integrates a nonlinear feedforward compensator leveraging a Gaussian process regression (GPR) model with a linear feedback controller whose parameters are optimized using the virtual reference feedback tuning (VRFT) method, ensuring fast and precise tracking of the desired motion. The effectiveness of the proposed method was validated through both simulation and physical experiments. In human-subject studies conducted on a physical magnetic actuation platform featuring both static and dynamic obstacle scenarios, quantitative results demonstrate that the shared control strategy significantly outperforms both purely manual and fully autonomous modes across all key metrics, including success rate, task completion time, stability, and safety (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p \lt 0.001$</tex-math> </inline-formula>). Furthermore, successful navigation within a complex gastric model demonstrates the potential of the shared control system for practical application in unstructured environments.

title:
Unraveling Electric-field Dependent Surface Charge Accumulation Mechanisms of Insulator in SF
                    <sub>6</sub>
                    /N
                    <sub>2</sub>
authors: He Gao, Xiwen Liu, Heng Zhang, Tianyu Qi, Junren Liang, Yu Gong, Lei Zhang, Yongsen Han, Ji Liu, Zhen Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Surface charge accumulation at the gas-solid interface induces surface flashover for long-term operational DC GIS systems, which represents a critical failure form of insulators. However, owing to the absence of a charge accumulation equation for actual insulator surface protrusions, the experimental results are inconsistent with simulation data by the model, failing to adequately reveal the surface charge accumulation mechanism. This work develops a surface ionization equation for insulators and integrates it into the surface charge accumulation model to analyze the influence of micro-protrusions on charge accumulation characteristics. Unraveling the principles of surface charge accumulation under different electric fields through both experimental and simulation approaches. Experimental results demonstrate that when the electric field improves, surface charge accumulation shifts from homopolar to heteropolar, and the shift point moves toward higher electric fields as the concentrations of SF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sub> and Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> increase. Simulation results indicate that an enhanced gas collision ionization coefficient promotes the surface ionization process, consequently reducing the charge polarity shift voltage point as the SF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sub> decreases. The solid bulk conductivity improves, the bulk current density enhances, resulting in higher charge polarity shift voltage point with increased Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> particle concentration. This paper reveals the “gas phase-solid phase” competition dominated mechanism of insulator surface charge accumulation under different electric fields. This mechanism provides theoretical support for improving the performance modification strategies for insulation components.

title: LASTS: Toward Scalable Access Control and Resilient Network Management of Mobile IoT on the Edge
authors: Di Wu, Jinhui Ouyang, Qinghua Guan, Xiang Nie, Jinwen Liang, Yanwen Wang, Hanhui Deng
abstract: Edge computing has recently emerged as a promising paradigm to support mobile access in Internet of Things (IoT) multinetworks, where heterogeneous wireless communication solutions coexist. Meanwhile, software-defined networking (SDN) presents a potential infrastructure to monitor and manage mobile edge computing. However, resilient access in the integrated IoT-Edge-SDN environment is a key challenge. In this article, we present location-aware spatio-temporal solution (LASTS) as an edge computing-empowered software-defined system to scalably control mobile IoT access and detect sequential anomaly. LASTS utilizes a Personal access point protocol to enable switching between multiple networks. In addition, it supports efficient control and transfer of the mobile device’s spatio-temporal context. This context information plays an important role in a deep learning model employed for sequential anomaly detection in the LASTS system. Realistic testbed experiments confirm that LASTS can successfully achieve scalable access control and sequential anomaly detection in mobile IoT.
