title: SEGA: A Transferable igned nsemble aussian Black-Box ttack Against No-Reference Image Quality Assessment Models_supp1-3659164.pdf
authors: Tiejun Huang
abstract: No-Reference Image Quality Assessment (NR-IQA) models play an important role in various real-world applications. Recently, adversarial attacks against NR-IQA models have attracted increasing attention, as they provide valuable insights for revealing model vulnerabilities and guiding robust system design. Some effective attacks have been proposed against NR-IQA models in white-box settings, where the attacker has full access to the target model. However, these attacks often suffer from poor transferability to unknown target models in more realistic black-box scenarios, where the target model is inaccessible. This work makes the first attempt to address the challenge of low transferability in attacking NR-IQA models by proposing a transferable Signed Ensemble Gaussian black-box Attack (SEGA). The main idea is to approximate the gradient of the target model by applying Gaussian smoothing to source models and ensembling their smoothed gradients. To ensure the imperceptibility of adversarial perturbations, SEGA further removes inappropriate perturbations using a specially designed perturbation filter mask. Experimental results demonstrate the superior transferability of SEGA, validating its effectiveness in enabling successful transfer-based black-box attacks against NR-IQA models. Code for this paper is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/YogaLYJ/SEGA_IQA</uri>.

title: Perception-Driven Generative Model for High-Precision and Low-Cost Remote Sensing Image Generation in Resource-Constrained Cotton Health Monitoring
authors: Yang Li, Leifeng Wei, Jing Nie, Jikai Zhao, Sezai Ercisli
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Vegetation indices (VIs), such as the normalized difference vegetation index (NDVI) and the normalized difference red edge index (NDRE), derived from multispectral imagery is crucial for crop health monitoring in precision agriculture. However, the acquisition of key bands particularly the near-infrared (NIR) and red-edge (RE) bands relies on expensive and specialized multispectral sensors, which significantly limits their application in resource-constrained scenarios. To address this issue, this study explores cross-band generation methods to produce high-quality NIR and RE bands from widely available RGB images. Existing methods often prioritize pixel-level accuracy while neglecting the semantic utility of the generated bands for downstream tasks and are susceptible to background interference. To overcome these limitations, we propose a perception-driven generation framework. First, we perform vegetation segmentation on raw images using the excess green vegetation index to construct a purified dataset of cotton vegetation pixels, which directs the model's focus toward learning the core spectral mapping relationships of vegetation. Leveraging prior knowledge from semantic segmentation, we introduce a selective reconstruction process that prioritizes detailed spectral reconstruction in vegetation regions while simplifying non-critical background areas. This strategy enhances computational efficiency without compromising key information quality. Subsequently, we design a transformer-based multi-channel spectral fusion model incorporating a spectral-spatial attention mechanism utilizing strip convolution to adaptively fuse channel features and concentrate on key spatial regions. The model is optimized using a composite loss function that combines perceptual loss and pixel level loss, ensuring the generated bands are both spectrally accurate and semantically rich. Experiments on a cotton image dataset collected by a DJI Mavic3 multispectral drone demonstrate that the proposed method generates more visually realistic NIR and RE bands compared to existing approaches. Most importantly, the classification accuracy for cotton health status using VIs derived from the generated data is significantly improved, outperforming methods using only RGB data and approaching the performance achieved with real multispectral data. This study provides a cost-effective solution for high precision crop monitoring under resource constraints.

title: Arc Erosion Characterization of High-Power DC Contactors Based on Multimodal Sensing and Signal Analysis
authors: Yubin He, Wanbin Ren, Siyu Lu, Chao Zhang, Ming Li, Chengchuang Wu
abstract: Comprehensive arc erosion characterization in high-power DC contactors is essential for understanding the degradation mechanism and improving operational reliability in electric vehicles and aerospace systems. This article presents a novel in situ measurement system that integrates high-resolution 3-D laser scanning and synchronized electrical signal acquisition to characterize arc erosion processes on double-break contact bridge structures. The system enables synchronized acquisition of multimodal data, including 2-D contact surface images, 3-D height point cloud datasets, and arcing voltage/current waveforms under magnetic blow-out conditions. A robust data processing framework is established to extract arc ignition point, arithmetic average height, maximum height difference, and erosion volume from height point cloud datasets. Experimental results from 15 000 endurance cycles reveal a distinct asynchronous breaking arc (ABA) phenomenon, linked to mechanical assembly misalignments and uneven arc erosion distribution. The proposed method offers a noncontact, quantitative approach for failure precursor detection in high-power DC contactors, with implications for lifespan prediction and system-level reliability enhancement.

title: The Mediating Role of Entrepreneurial Orientation in Big Data Analytics Capability-Value Creation
authors: Ajax Persaud, Javid Zare
abstract: Many studies on big data analytics provide empirical evidence showing that Big Data Analytics Capabilities (BDAC) do not always result in value creation or competitive advantage. This study sheds light on how entrepreneurial orientation (EO) mediates the relationship between big data analytics capabilities (BDAC) and value creation in SMEs. The study employed a mixed-method design, integrating desk research, executive interviews, and a survey of 447 Canadian SME leaders. Partial least squares structural equation modelling(PLS-SEM) analysis, supported by reliability and validity diagnostics, common-method-bias tests, and model-fit assessments, ensured the robustness of results and enabled triangulation across methods. Findings reveal that EO mediates the BDAC–value creation relationship, suggesting that SMEs with stronger EO leverage BDAC more effectively. Thus, developing EO is essential for unlocking BDAC's value-creation potential. A practical implication for senior executives is that employing EO – proactiveness, risk-taking, and innovativeness – can lead to better leverage of BDAC insights for value creation. A theoretical implication is that EO can complement other organizational capabilities such as analytics culture to extract greater value from BDAC. The empirical evidence provided in this study complements previous studies that have examined other mediators such as analytics culture and absorptive capacity. It provides additional insights into how SMEs can leverage their BDAC for value creation by being more innovative, proactive and encouraging risk-taking across the organization.

title: Guest Editorial: Special Issue on Recent Advances in Integrated Sensing and Communications—Part III
authors: Fan Liu, Christos Masouros, Jie Xu, Tony Xiao Han, Sang G. Kim, Yonina C. Eldar, Stefano Buzzi, Anna Guerra, Shinya Sugiura, Sundeep Prabhakar Chepuri, Xianghao Yu
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications

title: A Deep-Learning Ensemble Pipeline for Skin Cancer Diagnosis Using Microarray Gene Expression Profiles
authors: Sailendra Kumar Parsa, Rayavarapu Raja Rajeswari Manaswini, Sathya Ashrith Reddy Sammeta, Swati Sucharita, M.A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The increasing global prevalence of melanoma underscores the critical need for new diagnostic tools that promote early detection and enhance patient outcomes. This paper introduces a deep learning ensemble pipeline that leverages microarray gene expression for improved skin cancer diagnosis. Utilizing sophisticated feature selection and a machine learning ensemble approach, this pipeline provides a non-invasive technique for identifying melanoma patterns. The method employs cutting-edge deep learning technologies to create a scalable diagnostic framework. The objective of this paper is to reduce dependence on traditional invasive diagnostic methods, thereby enhancing access to effective treatments, especially for underrepresented communities. The proposed model utilizes microarray gene expression data effectively, getting an Area Under Curve of 0.9339 and an accuracy of 89.1% during 3-fold cross-validation, along with a 92% accuracy level through Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation (LOOCV). This highlights its strong predictive capabilities and points to a promising direction for improving skin cancer diagnosis, with potential wider implications for healthcare and future work focused on developing and validating this method across diverse populations.

title: A Robust Deepfake Detection Framework Using AV-CATNet for Audio-Visual Consistency and Temporal Attention in Secure Social Media Authentication
authors: G. Anitha, Aakash Saran V, Nadish Loganathan, Arul Saravanan R K
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The swift evolution of deepfakes with significant implications on the credibility of multimedia content in social media provides us with a stern challenge. Current detection strategies are typically inadequate in modeling cross-modal inconsistency and temporal information. To this end, we introduce AV-CATNet (Audio-Visual Cross-Attentional Transformer Network), which is an innovative deepfake detection system that utilizes cross-modal attention to synchronize lip movement with audio and temporal self-attention to detect sequence anomalies. AV-CATNet proposes the Fake Consistency Score (FCS), a transparent metric measuring the synchronization of audio and visual streams throughout history, making the model more transparent. In contrast to existing CNN, RNN, or late-fusion AV models, AV-CATNet reaches up to 97% accuracy on standard datasets such as DFDC, FakeAVCeleb, and FaceForensics++. It has an end-to-end attention-based fusion mechanism without the requirement for handcrafted features or post-processing. Designed to be optimized for real-time execution and edge deployment, AV-CATNet is a robust and scalable platform for content authentication, multimedia forensics, and deepfake defense in high-throughput social media scenarios.

title: AI-Driven Platform for Real-Time Assessment and Tracking of Wound Recovery Dynamics
authors: Devika M, V. Niveathasaro, C. Gowri Shankar, Sivakumar J, K Venkatapathi, Ponnusamy Palanisamy
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Monitoring wound healing is an important part of post operative and chronic treatment, and this important process requires proper measurement and assessment so as to avoid infections and achieve positive outcomes. The standard wound tracking approaches are quite subjective since they are to be observed manually and no continuity, where recording of wound can be challenging in remote areas or where resources are scarce. The present study offers the idea of AI-enabled application that allows real-time evaluation and the dynamic monitoring of wound healing analysis through the implementation of deep learning. The platform is combined with a convolutional neural network (CNN) -based segmentation system to accurately segment the boundaries of the wound and a multi-stage classifier to distinguish the stages of the healing process such as inflammation, proliferation, and maturation stages. The wound is imaged using a mobile-integrated image capture apparatus, the images pre-processed and analysed locally with edge computing or at distance with cloud-based inference. It was trained and validated on publicly accessible wound and home-Labeled clinical images and reached segmentation Dice coefficient of 0.91 and classification accuracy of 94.8%. The suggested solution makes it possible to monitor on a 24/7 basis, visualize trends and report on them in a telemedicine-compatible manner, which makes it a scalable and convenient tool both to patients and clinicians. This AI platform drops the variability in assessments and enhances patient compliance and establishes a foundation to predictive wound care.

title: Assessing and Mitigating Intersectional Bias: A Multi-Domain Study on Race and Gender
authors: Sumaiya Binte Shahid, Bushra Akter, Md Biplob Hosen, Mehrin Anannya, Mohammad Abu Yousuf
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The use of algorithms to aid in decision-making is growing in important fields like healthcare, education, employment, criminal justice etc. However, there is a concern that these algorithms might treat some demographic groups unfairly, especially when biases result from overlapping attributes, like gender and race. In this paper, four real-world datasets— COMPAS, Adult Income, Student Performance, and Heart Disease have been used to do a cross-domain investigation of bias. Demographic Parity Difference (DPD), Disparate Impact (DI), Average Odds Difference (AOD), Equal Opportunity Difference (EOD), False Positive Rate (FPR), and False Negative Rate (FNR) Gap- six commonly accepted fairness measures, are used to assess bias across race, gender, and race-gender intersections. We have applied the Exponentiated Gradient (EG) method with Equalized Odds constraints from the Fairlearn framework to mitigate intersectional bias. The results indicate that bias levels dropped notably while predictive performance remained strong. This underscores the need to address intersectional bias and demonstrates that fairness-aware algorithms are crucial for achieving a balance between accuracy, equity, and accountability in real-world systems.

title: Leveraging CNN Architectures for Automated Detection of Breast Tumors in MRI Imaging
authors: Jeffin A I, Inbamalar T M, Amala Justus Selvam M, Chettiyar Vani Vivekanand, Anna Devi E, Sharu Shree M
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: A major research topic with vital applications in medical diagnosis is the detection of anomalies, such as malignant tumors or nodules, in medical imaging, such as mammograms, CT scans, and PET scans. A test sample can be classified as either positive or negative by a trained model. However, because learning algorithms are prone to favor the majority class, which is the negative samples, the imbalance between positive and negative samples in medical imaging poses challenges. We suggest an unsupervised learning method to describe the negative class in order to get over this problem and utilize the vast number of negative examples, like typical mammography pictures. We created an autoencoder utilizing a deep neural network to represent negative patches taken from high-resolution breast cancer photos in order to increase the model's adaptability and suitability for medical images of different scales. The results show that this method can work well for CNN-based abnormal image identification. Standard assessment measures were used to evaluate the model's performance, and the results showed an accuracy of 98.64%, an F-measure of 97.71%, and a precision of 96.72%.

title: Enhancing Predictive Maintenance in Smart Textile Manufacturing Using Machine Learning Models
authors: Md. Ibtihazzaman, Zuhaer Al Sadat, Sadman Sakib, Saiful Islam Oni, Mahfuza Khatun
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: In smart textile manufacturing, predictive maintenance is one of the innovations being implemented to reduce unplanned equipment downtime and enhance overall productivity. This research proposes a machine learning-driven solution based on the AI4I 2020 predictive maintenance dataset for equipment condition classification and failure prognosis. After feature extraction and preprocessing, two models Random Forest and XGBoost were developed and assessed. The Random Forest model scored 98.23% accuracy with AUC 0.97 while XGBoost also presented competitive results of 97.30% accuracy and a matching AUC score. Interpretability in model decisions through SHAP was performed to highlight the driving factors and best explainable predictions. The results highlight the opportunity that exists to employ machine learning techniques to improve maintenance in the textile sector, revealing the possibilities of shifting from manual to fully automated factory systems through intelligent data-driven machine learning algorithms.

title: Automated Infection Detection in Diabetic Foot Ulcer Images Using CNN and ELM Models
authors: Mannem Rajitha, Anfinston F. D., Nivedha S, Sudhagar N, Gururaj T, Deepan Chakravarthi A. V.
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Severe consequences of diabetes encompass diabetic foot ulcers (DFUs), which may result in limb or foot amputation and are a significant contributor to mortality, disability, and healthcare costs globally. Enhancing healing outcomes and reducing the likelihood of gangrene and amputation in diabetic foot ulcers necessitates prompt infection identification with the recognition of ischemia, attributable to diminished blood flow due to chronic diabetes. This study proposes a novel method for detecting infections in diabetic foot ulcers by a hybrid classification system. Subsequent to the fine-tuning of the dataset using precise edge detection, it was equilibrated by incorporating additional data through data augmentation techniques. The GLCM approaches were employed for feature extraction as they encapsulate essential second-order statistical texture information vital for effective categorization. A CNN-ELM model was developed, integrating the automated feature extraction skills of CNN with the efficiency and precision of ELM. The model discerned six stages of DFU development, achieving an exceptional classification accuracy of 98.31% and an F1-score of 98.87%, despite the constraints of limited training data. To enhance early identification, treatment outcomes, and the need for amputation, these findings indicate that the proposed method provides a rapid, precise, and efficient tool for detecting infections in diabetic foot ulcers.

title: A Scalable Framework for Real-Time Twitter Sentiment Monitoring and Hashtag Trend Analysis
authors: Thomas F. Wolobah, Kantipudi Chethana, J. C Harshini, S. Saravanan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The generated content on social media platforms, especially Twitter (now known as X), is grown rapidly in recent times. This generated content has a mix of people’s feelings. This creates a good chance to understand people’s feelings in real-time, known as sentiment analysis. However, the usual methods are less effective with such a large and fast-generating amount of data. In this, we found a solution for this challenge by creating a unique tool, the Twitter Sentiment Analysis Dashboard, which can rapidly sort and display the feelings in tweets, like positive, negative, and neutral, associated with popular hashtags. The research utilizes a substantial Twitter dataset from Bittensor Subnet 13 decentralized network, containing around 2.5 million tweets. The process involves several steps. First, Apache Spark is used to handle data quickly and efficiently across multiple systems. Next, we used polarity score to determine if the emotions in the tweets are positive, neutral, or negative. Finally, a web application is built using Flask to showcase the statistics of data live on a dashboard. The tweets are analyzed and assigned a polarity score for positive, neutral, or negative and these scores are displayed immediately on an interactive screen. This allows users to understand how sentiments about certain topics present over time. The system updates the dashboard with this information very quickly, within just 5 seconds, providing fast insights into public opinion. This platform proves to be very useful for businesses, policymakers, and researchers. This helps to monitor and understand the sentiment of social media content, which is important for making data-driven decisions.

title: Analysis of the Collaborative Development Model of Power Marketing and Comprehensive Energy Using Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
authors: Quan Sun, Wenjun Qi, Shuai Yuan, Tingting Zhang, Yan Pan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: To solve the problem of conflicting goals and disjointed cross domain optimization in the coordinated development of power marketing and comprehensive energy, this paper proposes a collaborative development model based on multiple swarm particle swarm optimization algorithms. This model constructs a closed-loop architecture of "data perception optimization decision-making execution feedback", integrating multi-dimensional data such as user electricity load, package preferences, combined cooling, heating, and power system output, wind and solar energy storage status, and achieving multi-objective optimization of comprehensive cost, energy utilization efficiency, and carbon emissions through a master-slave population collaborative particle swarm algorithm. We have researched and designed various engineering implementation schemes for swarm particle swarm optimization algorithms, clarified parameter configuration and operation flow, and verified the performance of the patterns through experiments. The results show that the proposed model improves the comprehensive cost reduction rate by 5.2 to 4.8 percentage points, energy utilization efficiency by 6.6 to 4.1 percentage points, and dynamic response delay by 65% to 34% compared to traditional particle swarm optimization algorithm and adaptive particle swarm optimization algorithm. It maintains linear scalability in large-scale user scenarios and effectively supports cross domain collaborative optimization of power marketing and comprehensive energy.

title: Down Syndrome Prediction from Facial Image Dataset using Explainable Deep Learning
authors: Md. Mamun Hossain, Muhammad Nazrul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Intellectual disabilities such as Down Syndrome (DS) require timely and accurate diagnosis to improve clinical outcomes, since the traditional assessment remains subjective and resource-intensive. This study presents a comparative evaluation of four hyperparameter-optimized deep learning models including Baseline CNN, ResNet50, MobileNetV2, and the optimized VNL-Net (VGG–NMF–LightGBM Network) for automated DS detection from facial images. Using a publicly available, pre-processed dataset of children’s facial photographs, each model was trained within a unified pipeline incorporating data augmentation, early stopping, and hyperparameter optimization. Performance was assessed with standard classification metrics, confusion matrices, and Grad-CAM visualizations. As outcome, the VNL-Net achieved the highest accuracy (96%), outperforming MobileNetV2 (90%), ResNet50 (89%), and the baseline CNN (88%), while consistently focusing on clinically relevant regions such as the midface and periorbital areas. These findings demonstrate that carefully tuned architectures, as well as coupled with explainability tools, can deliver high-performing, interpretable, and potentially scalable systems for DS diagnosis.

title: Improving Colorectal Cancer Detection: Leveraging EfficientNet-B0 and Explainable AI for High-Accuracy Classification
authors: Al Arian Ahmad, Nakib Aman
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Colorectal cancer (CRC) is one of the most serious and rapidly growing cancers, ranking among the leading causes of cancer-related deaths worldwide. It typically originates from polyps in the colon or rectum and can progress into malignancy over time. It needs early treatment, and recent advances in deep learning have shown great promise for the automation of the diagnosis. This paper presents an EfficientNet-B0-based deep learning framework with full incorporation of Explainable AI (XAI) techniques. The developed framework is trained on 11,340 CRC images and achieves a high accuracy of 99.95%, outperforming several state-of-the-art approaches. Incorporation of XAI methods such as Grad-CAM and LIME makes the framework produce interpretable decisions, making its predictions transparent and clinically trustworthy. The performance is validated using globally recognized metrics such as Precision, F1-Score and Recall, which all indicate the high credibility of the model. Grad-CAM visualizations are able to identify the significant regions in the images that have influenced the predictions, giving an idea of the concern areas for CRC detection. Not only is the model highly accurate, but it is also interpretable and easy to deploy, indicating its great potential for translation to real-world clinical practice. It could assist healthcare professionals in the identification and confirmation of CRC development and, thereby, enable timely and confident diagnosis. Such automated, explainable tools have the potential to be a mainstay of AI-powered healthcare diagnostics in the years to come.

title: Explainable AI Framework for Multiclass Attack Classification in Wireless Network Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Abu Saleh Molla, Tanmoy Datta, Francesco De Florence, Rakib Rayhan, Md. Khalilur Rahman, M. Humayun Kabir
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: In recent years, wireless networks have grown remarkably. The widespread usage of standalone, lightweight devices with integrated wireless networks is one of the leading causes of this rise. These gadgets are now necessary for everyday consumer electronics. Over time, as reliance on wireless networks has grown, so too have the threats directed against them. To find these threats, a network intrusion detection system (NIDS) with fast detection times and high accuracy is required. In this paper, we present an intrusion detection system utilizing machine learning models, including Random Forest, XGBoost, Decision Tree, and KNN. The Random Forest model achieved 99.90% accuracy with a reduced number of feature set in multi-class classification. Precision, recall, and F1-score were all nearly flawless. To address the issue of model interpretability, we used explainable AI techniques e.g., SHAP (Shapley Additive explanations) and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations). These methods increased confidence in model predictions by offering both local and global insights into feature contributions. The proposed system demonstrates high detection performance and transparency, making it appropriate for real-world applications, including protecting office and enterprise Wi-Fi, defense networks, and improving public Wi-Fi security.

title: MultiPC: Multi-Task Learning for Joint Pansharpening and Classification from Multispectral and Panchromatic Images
authors: Lu Chen, Hao Zhu, Pengyu Tian, Xiaotong Li, Ye Sun, Jinguo Hu, Biao Hou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Multitask learning has broad applicability in remote sensing. Although MS/PAN-based pansharpening and classification tasks are highly complementary at the feature level, they are usually modeled separately. To this end, this paper proposes the multitasking parallel architecture of MultiPC to achieve collaborative optimization. To address inconsistency among tasks, MultiPC introduces the Mutually Enhanced Feature Fusion (MEFF). For differential features between tasks, it achieves feature alignment through deformable convolution and combines overlapping and cross-attention to enhance feature complementarity between tasks. MEFF simultaneously introduces a joint optimization strategy to improve the synergy and complementarity of tasks. Furthermore, we propose the reflective learning loss function to effectively perceive the learning states across various tasks. It guides the two tasks to adaptively optimize the direction based on the historically learned knowledge, thereby clarifying the learning status of each task and promoting the overall collaborative optimization. Experiments on multiple real remote sensing datasets show that MultiPC significantly outperforms existing single-task methods in both classification and pansharpening tasks. The code is open from: https://github.com/Xidian-AIGroup190726/MultiPC.

title: Bridging Languages in Healthcare: A Comprehensive Review of Multilingual and Code-Switched Chatbot Interactions
authors: K Tejasri, M Lawanya Shri
abstract: The Growth of societies with multilingual populations and the expanded accessibility to the healthcare service digitally has led to an increasing demand. For intelligent conversational agents that can operate and manage interactions with complex languages. Especially in countries like India, there exists a common process of code-switching between English and the regional or local languages, where the monolingual system falls conventionally. The paper explores the healthcare chatbots in the current state of research and the developments in the response and detection of queries, which involve multilingual and code-switching. This review aims to analyze the detection and response in the existing approaches to multilingual and healthcare queries. Also, the objective is to identify strengths, limitations, and research gaps in the current methodology. We highlight the challenges produced by the variation in the linguistic, languages with low resources, and the ambiguity of the context. Our analysis highlights that the performance of transformer-based multilingual models remains limited in low-resource and highly code-mixed settings, whereas the robustness of the models is improved. The paper analyses the models that exist for the identification of languages, detection of code-switching at the token-level, multilingual embedding, and systems based on transformers. We further discuss evaluation benchmarks, moral considerations, with ethical factors to direct future works in the equity of healthcare enhancement through AI with inclusive language.

title: Deep Learning Based Pedestrian Dead Reckoning With Vision Anchor Augmentation for Seamless Centimeter-Level Indoor Positioning
authors: Lien-Wu Chen, Wei-Chu Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: In this article, we design a deep learning based pedestrian dead reckoning (DL-PDR) framework with vision anchor augmentation for seamless centimeter-level indoor positioning. The DL-PDR framework integrates deep learning with PDR to address the inherent inaccuracies associated with estimating step lengths and heading directions in PDR methods, as errors in these estimations propagate and accumulate over time, resulting in progressively larger positioning errors. In addition, DL-PDR explores vision-based positioning using existing surveillance cameras while incorporating dynamic anchor augmentation to achieve high-precision real-time localization and correction. According to our review of relevant research, this is the first framework that explores and integrates vision anchor augmentation with inertial data recognition in a deep learning manner to accurately recognize individual walking distances, heading angles, and up-to-date locations, seamless achieving positioning accuracy at the centimeter level. We have implemented an Android-based system with network cameras to validate the feasibility and superiority of the DL-PDR framework. Experimental results show that our framework outperforms existing methods and can accurately recognize the walking distances, moving angles, and located points of individuals with centimeter-level accuracy.

title: Unconditionally Stable One-Step Leapfrog ADI-FDTD Method for Magnetized Plasma and Graphene-Based Structures
authors: Jiayu Liu, Xianghua Wang, Shunchuan Yang, Zhizhang Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This communication presents an unconditionally stable one-step leapfrog alternating-direction-implicit finite-difference time-domain (Lp ADI-FDTD) method for magnetized plasma and graphene-based structures. The modified Euler method, with a two-step prediction-correction format, is employed to remove the Courant-Friedrichs-Lewy (CFL) stability condition. Its unconditional stability and accuracy are investigated and validated through numerical experiments. Results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior computational efficiency with a larger CFL number (CFLN), particularly when simulating magnetized plasma-graphene-based frequency-selective surface (FSS). It reveals that the biased static electromagnetic fields can be used to adjust the reflected and transmitted waves of the FSS, where the locations of the stop bands are mainly controlled by the biased electrostatic field, and the minimum values of the stop bands can be changed by the applied magnetostatic field.

title: A Green Approach to Stabilizing Soybean Oil Dielectric Fluids: The Role of Methyl Carnosate_supp1-3663348.pdf
authors: Jian Li
abstract: Natural ester dielectric fluids are increasingly favored as sustainable alternatives to mineral oils; however, their susceptibility to oxidation remains a major challenge. Conventional stabilization methods using synthetic antioxidants often pose health and environmental concerns. This study explores the use of methyl carnosate (MC), a bio-based antioxidant, to enhance the oxidation stability of soybean oil (SO). The addition of MC increases the oxidation onset temperature of SO by nearly 20 °C and effectively mitigates the rise in kinematic viscosity and soluble acidity during accelerated aging at 120 °C. These parameters remain significantly lower compared to neat SO and the commercial product FR3<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> fluid. Mechanistic investigations reveal that MC effectively inhibits the transfer of allylic and bisallylic hydrogens, thereby delaying the formation of aldehydes generated from peroxide decomposition. This study underscores the potential of MC as a sustainable and effective additive for enhancing the oxidation stability of natural ester dielectric fluids.

title: SCAP: Semantic Prototype Alignment for Robust Point Cloud Registration
authors: Jingyu Zhou, Yunfeng Ma, Shuai Jiang, Yaonan Wang, Min Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Point cloud rigid registration is a fundamental problem in robotics, 3D reconstruction, and augmented reality. However, existing methods predominantly rely on local geometric neighborhoods, which fail to capture higher-order semantic structures and thus degrade performance under noisy or complex geometry conditions. To address these limitations, we propose SCAP, a new point cloud registration paradigm that transforms feature interaction from geometry-driven to semantics–geometric co-driven. Specifically, a semantic prototype extractor is devised to abstract high-level semantic prototypes through graph embedding and clustering, thereby mitigating sensitivity to local feature noise. Since semantic abstraction alone cannot guarantee consistent correspondences across point clouds, SCAP performs a prototype alignment path learning to infer reliable semantic mappings through optimal transport. To enhance cross-layer feature integration and prevent redundant attention, an alignment-driven cross-layer transformer is proposed to incorporate the learned priors into the attention mechanism, thereby enabling feature aggregation with improved semantic coherence and local precision. Extensive experiments on ModelNet, ModelLoNet, 3DMatch, and 3DLoMatch demonstrate that our SCAP consistently surpasses state-of-the-art approaches, showing superior robustness and generalization in challenging scenarios with noise and partial overlap. The code will be available at https://github.com/Zhou-111jy/SCAP.git.

title: Design and Implementation of an Anthropomorphic Robotic Hand with Key Kinematic Properties of Human Hand_supp4-3663977.mp4
authors: Caihua Xiong
abstract: Designing an anthropomorphic robotic hand with few actuators while replicating the dexterous motion capabilities of the human hand remains a challenging problem. In this work, a kinematic synergy analysis method is proposed to identify joints with high motion independence and to quantify the synergistic motion characteristics of joints with strong motion dependence. Based on these findings, design principles for intra finger and inter-finger coupling–compliance mechanisms are developed, enabling the mechanical embodiment of joint kinematic synergies within and between fingers. Furthermore, a design principle for anthropomorphic robotic hands that reduces actuator count while retaining diverse motion functions is formulated. This principle is embodied in a 12-actuator, 21-joint prototype named SX-Hand, featuring a flexible thumb and an articulated palm. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that SX Hand achieves the maximum score in the Kapandji test, performs all 33 grasp types defined in the comprehensive GRASP taxonomy, and executes dexterous in-hand manipulations. The proposed approach provides an effective conceptual and technical route for reproducing rich motion functions with reduced actuation, offering valuable guidance for the development of efficient anthropomorphic robotic hands and other bionic systems.

title: Secure Messaging & E-Voting System Using Quantum Cryptography
authors: Kashvi Rita, Prisha Desai, Kashvi Mahetaliya, Richa Sharma
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The expansion of quantum computing puts classical cryptographic systems at risk, and thus there is a need for quantum-resistant solutions to secure messaging and e-voting. This paper introduces a two-in-one application developed with Flask (backend), Firebase (database), and IBM Quantum (live QKD operations), secured by the BB84 Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) protocol. The messaging module uses BB84 to produce unbreakable encryption keys by way of photon polarization, and the evoting system encrypts votes using quantum keys, providing end-to-end security. Firebase stores user information and anonymous votes securely, and Flask oversees serverside logic, including real-time quantum key generation through IBM Quantum’s cloud-based quantum processors. The incorporation of IBM Quantum’s Qiskit framework allows for real-time simulation and running of BB84, with error rates below 3% in quantum channel tests. Tamper-evident logs and decentralized verification are used in place of centralized databases for e-voting, which reduces fraud vulnerabilities. Real-world implementation shows that the system can scale, as Firebase supports 1,000+ concurrent users and IBM Quantum provides sub-second latency for key generation. This paper tackles weaknesses of traditional systems by combining quantum cryptography with contemporary full-stack technologies, offering a solid, deployable solution for electoral integrity and secure communication.

title: Deep Learning Models for Early Diagnosis of Parkinson’s Disease Using Voice and Gait Analytics
authors: Jeshua Ernest C, Sophia S
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Parkinson disease (PD) should be diagnosed early and correctly to be able to interfere and save more patients. Conventional diagnostic techniques depend extensively on clinical investigations, and these might be subjective, and they may not identify those symptoms in their early stages. In this work, we introduce a multimodal deep learning model which uses voice and the gait analytics to diagnose PD in the early stages. The implementation process includes individual preprocessing pipelines dedicated to voice and gait information and modality-specific feature extraction based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) in the case of voice signal extraction and one-dimensional CNN (1D-CNN), and Bidirectional LSTM background (Bi-LSTM) in the case of gait pattern extraction. The models are trained independently on the deep learning networks per modality, and the learned representations are concatenated together in a specific fusion layer with the aim of making better diagnoses. The last classification step forecasts about the healthiness of a subject or about his PD status with its correctness determined in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curves, and confusion matrices. The evidence provided by the experiment indicates that the suggested fusion-based model is actually good when it comes to capturing both time and spatial features of voice and gaits, achieving an overall increase in the diagnostic efficiency of the method, when compared to single modalities. The paper underscores the prospect of multimodal deep learning in aiding the objective, non-invasive, and early detection of Parkinson disease.

title: MindReg-VG: A VGG-GRU Framework for Mental Health Assessment and Legal Compliance in IT Industries
authors: Padmapriya S.S, C. Jayamala, B. Lavaraju
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Stress along with anxiety and burnout cases in IT work environments have become more common due to their fast nature and high pressure conditions. The research presents MindReg-VG as a VGG-GRU-based deep learning framework intended to evaluate workplace mental health conditions and determine legal workplace compliance in IT industries. The system performs visual analysis through VGG networks for facial emotion detection and sequential data processing through Gated Recurrent Units (GRU) for time-series workplace stress monitoring. NLP techniques support mental health prediction and enable analysis of corporate policies for their compliance with labor regulations regarding workplace mental health assistance. Tests were performed on a data collection that included survey responses along with mental health workplace parameters and organization policy documents obtained from multiple information technology companies. Research confirmed the superiority of MindReg-VG approach because its accuracy reached 97.55% while demonstrating better performance over three deep learning layers: VGG-LSTM (92.5%), ResNet-GRU (90.3%), and CNN-LSTM (87.4%). Research through sentiment analysis showed a majority of 71.3% of employees had positive opinions regarding workplace mental health policies because these organizations emphasized employee well-being. Employee stress decreased measurably when IT companies demonstrated legal compliance above 90% according to the framework assessment. These results confirm mental health policy support stands as a fundamental component in developing a wholesome work environment. The MindReg-VG framework functions as a dependable solution which allows HR professionals together with their legal teams to track developments in workplace mental health and confirm IT sector compliance with regulations.

title: Development of Automated System for Early Detection of Colorectal Cancer from Images Using Efficient Attention Net With Gated Recurrent Unit Layer
authors: Dhanoj M, Pamela D, P. Manimegalai, Wincy Pon Annal, G. Glan Devadhas
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Colorectal cancer (CRC) is the second most common disease in women and third most common disease in men. The pathology analyst complemented with predictive and prognostic biomarker detail that is the primary phase for personalized treatment since CRC has become a common health disorder day by day. The removal, detection, and diagnosis are famous CRC prevention approaches. Though effective endoscopic imaging mechanisms such as narrow-band imaging have been implemented, these models rely on the operator and explanations of the outcomes might change. Hence, in this paper, a deep learning model is implemented for detecting the CRC in deciding the proper treatment for the affected patient. The images required to perform the CRC are collected from the standard dataset. After that, the collected images are directly passed to the Efficient Attention net with a Gated Recurrent Unit layer (EAGRU) for detecting the CRC in humans. The developed EAGRU has the capability to detect noticeable symptoms caused by the human body so it is considered an effective tool for the early diagnosis process. Finally, the experimental analysis is conducted on the proposed model to successfully show the performance of the EAGRU in the CRC detection process.

title: Research on Risk Assessment and Response Strategies of Domestic Database Transformation in Electric Power Information Communication
authors: Ningxi Song, Qiang Li, Di Cai, Huichao Liang, Haitao Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: In the context of digital transformation of power information and communication systems, the localization transformation of databases is the core link to achieve independent and controllable key infrastructure. However, the transformation process faces multidimensional risks such as technical adaptation, engineering implementation, and safety compliance. This study constructed a risk assessment framework and response strategy system suitable for power communication scenarios: by integrating the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) with the Fuzzy Comprehensive Evaluation Model, a three-dimensional evaluation index system was established for technical adaptability, engineering implementation difficulty, and safety compliance, achieving quantitative analysis of transformation risks; Design a multilayer response strategy for syntax conversion middleware, dual active architecture deployment, and security permission control, forming a closed-loop management of "evaluation policy verification". The experiment shows that the framework has an accuracy rate of 92% in identifying the risks of power database transformation, and the response strategy reduces the delay of core business transaction processing by 42.7%, reduces the occurrence rate of risk events by 65%, and meets the real-time requirements of power system ⩽ 50ms and the compliance standards of equal protection 2.0. The research results provide a quantifiable risk management and control scheme for the localization of power industry database, verify the engineering feasibility of autonomous and controllable technology in the field of power communication, and further expand the strategy application to edge computing scenarios in the future.

title: Edge-to-Cloud Orchestrated Multi-Sensor IoT Framework for Real-Time WQI Classification and Predictive Assessment via Ensemble ML
authors: Abdul Hasib, T. M. Mehrab Hasan, Md. Meraj Serker, Md Mursalin Hossain Misat, Jakia Arthy, Arjina Nasrin Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The world is currently experiencing a serious issue of water quality due to urbanization, industrialization, and agricultural activities, necessitating urgent solutions to safeguard public health and ecosystems. This study introduces a versatile IoT framework for real-time Water Quality Index (WQI) prediction and classification, leveraging ensemble machine learning for global applicability. The system integrates an ESP32 microcontroller with six sensors (pH, Total Dissolved Solids, temperature, turbidity, dissolved oxygen, and electrical conductivity), transmitting data to a Web Dashboard and Firebase Realtime Database for cloud storage and synchronization. The framework demonstrates the excellent performance of advanced ensemble algorithms including LightGBM, CatBoost, Random Forest, and XGBoost, that have an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.9966 and 99.49% accuracy in classification, respectively, and confirmed with 17,367 records that have a timestamp of water bodies of diverse environments. The strength of this dataset is that it has been able to analyze temporal and seasonal patterns more than the conventional laboratory practices. The proposed framework's operations are cost and scalable, and they fill significant gaps in the real-time monitoring of water-quality, and they help in sustainable environmental management in resource-limited areas in the world.

title: Transformer-Based Classification of Protein Secondary Structures Using ESM2 and BiLSTM
authors: Diganta Das Droba, Abrar Hafiz Rabbani, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Prediction-based language models are key concepts in Natural Language Processing, gaining insights from unstructured text data. It is an important problem to extract the insights from sequential data such as protein structures. This study presents a hybrid model combining the pre-trained protein language model ESM2 with a BiLSTM network for protein secondary structure prediction. The proposed approach demonstrates strong performance, achieving a Q3 accuracy of 93.21% and a macro F1 score of 93% on a standardized dataset. The methodology effectively leverages ESM2 embeddings for contextual representation and BiLSTM layers to capture longrange bidirectional dependencies.

title: Fuzzy–Deep Learning Hybrid Model for Voice and Fluency Assessment in English Literary Performance
authors: Dr.Kamaludeen.P, P. Pirakatheeswari, Dr.J.Moushumi, Sweta Priya, V. Chithra Devi, Sivaganesan Sivanantham
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Reading fluency is an essential component of reading teaching, especially for students encountering literacy difficulties. It differentiates competent readers from those who struggle and serves as a robust indicator of reading comprehension challenges. Upon mastering decoding skills, the subsequent essential stage is to enhance fluency, particularly within the realm of English literary performance. This research presents a technique for evaluating Voice and Fluency in English literary performance utilising FuzzyRNN. The methodology underscores the significance of data preparation and feature extraction, since the calibre of input data profoundly affects model efficacy. Utilising fuzzy numbers for data modelling enables the system to encapsulate human-like perceptions and tackle the uncertainties present in voice and fluency evaluation. The recurrent connections of the FuzzyRNN facilitate a streamlined network architecture while improving its capacity to generalise from fuzzy input. Of the four models evaluated, FuzzyRNN exhibited the highest forecasting accuracy at 94.53%, surpassing its competitors. The results indicate that FuzzyRNN can function as an effective instrument for enhancing fluency assessment in educational settings. The adaptive and perceptual modelling features provide a viable solution for real-time, automated assessment systems, enhancing the efficacy of reading instruction and literary performance evaluation.

title: An AI-IoT Based Smart Wheelchair with Gesture-Controlled Mobility, Deep Learning-Based Obstacle Detection, Multi-Sensor Health Monitoring, and Emergency Alert System
authors: Md. Asiful Islam, Abdul Hasib, Tousif Mahmud Emon, Khandaker Tabin Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The growing number of differently-abled and elderly individuals demands affordable, intelligent wheelchairs that combine safe navigation with health monitoring. Traditional wheelchairs lack dynamic features, and many smart alternatives remain costly, single-modality, and limited in health integration. Motivated by the pressing demand for advanced, personalized, and affordable assistive technologies, we propose a comprehensive AI-IoT based smart wheelchair system that incorporates glove-based gesture control for hands-free navigation, real-time object detection using YOLOv8 with auditory feedback for obstacle avoidance, and ultrasonic for immediate collision avoidance. Vital signs (heart rate, SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, ECG, temperature) are continuously monitored, uploaded to ThingSpeak, and trigger email alerts for critical conditions. Built on a modular and low-cost architecture, the gesture control achieved a 95.5% success rate, ultrasonic obstacle detection reached 94% accuracy, and YOLOv8-based object detection delivered 91.5% Precision, 90.2% Recall, and a 90.8% F1-score. This integrated, multi-modal approach offers a practical, scalable, and affordable solution, significantly enhancing user autonomy, safety, and independence by bridging the gap between innovative research and real-world deployment.

title: Smart Detection and Counting of Soft Drink Brands using YOLO Variants
authors: Asha Gowda Karegowda, Manoj S Kantennavar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Accurate and efficient detection of soft drink products is vital for optimizing retail inventory management, minimizing waste, and ensuring consistent product availability, thereby enhancing customer satisfaction. Conventional stock monitoring methods, which rely on manual counting, are time-consuming, labor-intensive, and susceptible to errors, particularly in high-volume retail settings. This study employs a deep learning-based object detection system capable of identifying and counting seven soft drink brands in real time. Three YOLO model variants: v8l, v10m, and v11m, were trained and evaluated on a custom-labeled soft drink dataset. Model performance was assessed using standard metrics, including detection accuracy, precision, recall, mean Average Precision at IoU threshold 0.5 mAP@0.5, and mean Average Precision averaged over IoU thresholds from 0.5 to 0.95 mAP@0.5:0.95, along with confusion matrix, F1-confidence curve, and recall-confidence curve analyses. YOLOv11m achieved the highest detection accuracy at 97.27%, followed by YOLOv8l with 94.19% and YOLOv10m with 91.32%, with YOLOv8l delivering the fastest inference speed, making it ideal for latency-sensitive scenarios. The findings confirm that the proposed system offers reliable, accurate, and scalable solutions for real-time soft drink detection in retail environments.

title: Modeling and Performance Evaluation of Triboelectric Nanogenerators for Energy Harvesting
authors: G. Tharak Chowdary, Panchami J, H. Meghana Kashyap, K. Monalisa, Vinaykumar R
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Triboelectric nanogenerator (TENGs) have emerged as a promising energy harvesting technology for powering biomedical devices due to their high efficiency, flexibility, and biocompatibility. This paper presents the design, modeling, and simulation of a TENG tailored for biomedical applications using the COMSOL Multiphysics software. Various structural configurations and material combinations were analyzed to optimize the electrical output and mechanical durability under physiological conditions. A coupled electrostatic-mechanical simulation was performed to study the influence of material properties, surface morphology, and external force on the generated voltage and current. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed TENG design can achieve sufficient power output to support low-power biomedical devices, such as implantable sensors and wearable health monitors. This study provides valuable insights into the performance optimization of TENGs, offering a potential pathway for the development of self-powered biomedical systems.

title: Smart AGRO HUB: Integrated Sensor Network For Real-time Agriculture Monitoring
authors: Sanskriti Kumari, Pratham Arora, Sritama Roy
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Agriculture industry faces growing challenges due to climate change and evolving environmental conditions, requiring advanced solutions to optimize crop management. This research work introduces SMART AGRO HUB which is an integrated sensor network for real-Time Agriculture monitoring, a system is designed to monitor key environmental parameters, including temperature, humidity, and soil moisture, using strategically deployed sensors. The collected data is analyzed through advanced algorithms to detect deviations from optimal conditions, triggering timely alerts to farmers via email with actionable recommendations. By integrating IoT technologies and data analytics, the system enhances agricultural resilience, boosts crop productivity, and promotes sustainable resource management, offering a robust solution for modern farming challenges.

title: A Stacking Ensemble Framework for Predicting Employee Turnover: Explainable AI with SHAP
authors: Md Akash Rahman, Rimon Kanthi Devnath, Sourav Barua Niloy, Chowdhury Mohammed Mehedi, Taufiqul Hoque Chowdhury, Mahfuz Islam Khan Jabed
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Staff turnover, also known as employee attrition, refers to the occurrence of employees departing from an organization, whether by choice or through dismissal. High attrition rates can make it more expensive to hire new employees, cause the organization to lose knowledge, lower morale, and lower overall productivity. Early prediction and mitigation of attrition are vital for talent retention and strategic workforce planning. This study looks at how to use ML, ensemble learning, and DL methods to construct a model that can predict when employees will leave. A benchmark dataset, the IBM HR Analytics Employee Attrition dataset, comprising 1,470 employee records labeled as either "Yes" (attrition) or "No" (non-attrition), was utilized. Preprocessing included handling missing values, label encoding, and class balancing using SMOTE. Feature selection was performed using ANOVA to retain the most relevant predictors. Multiple models were trained and evaluated, including both traditional and advanced classifiers. The Stacking Classifier, an ensemble approach that combines predictions from several base learners, had the best accuracy of 94.72%. This indicates that it is more effective at making predictions than the other methods. SHAP was used as an explainable AI strategy to make the model easier to understand and see. It demonstrated how various features contributed to the model’s decisions and helped people understand how it arrived at its conclusions.

title: Bridging Climates: Weather Forecasting Using OLS and ARIMA Models with a Multi-Country Dataset
authors: Arafat Hossain, Md. Salauddin Sarker, Aditya Rajbongshi, Most. Rakiba Khanom Jisa, Maria Afrin Bindu, Kayes Mohammad Abdullah
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Weather forecasting involves predicting future atmospheric conditions at a specific location by analyzing historical and current meteorological data. Accurate forecasts are crucial for public safety, transportation, agriculture, disaster preparedness, and informed governmental decisions. This study proposes a system for predicting weather variations up to a decade, using data from 1961 to 2022 for Bangladesh, Saudi Arabia, Japan, and Russia regions. Various data preprocessing techniques have been applied upon the data, which is collected from an online repository, to solve the problems like missing and duplicate values, anomalies, categorical variables, column conversions, etc. The performance of two classifiers named Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) and Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) was measured by four different evaluation metrics. The analysis reveals that the OLS model outperforms the ARIMA model for the Bangladesh region, showing an Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Sum of Squared Errors (SSE), and Mean Squared Error (MSE) of 0.30, 274.45, 0.25, 5.47, and 0.09, respectively. This research highlights the OLS model’s superior performance and its potential for accurate long-term weather forecasting through time series analytics.

title: Prediction and Estimation of State of Charge (SoC) of EV Batteries Using Machine Learning Models
authors: Amisha Andurkar, Kushal Masarkar, Akanksha.Sontakke, Kunal Sawalakhe, Ganesh Wakte, Ritesh Banpurkar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The major difficulty with predicting the State of Charge (SoC) in electric vehicles lies in the fact that it is heavily coupled to durability and reliability considerations of battery management systems which themselves are very complex. Battery modeling and state estimate has underscored, in theory and practice, that accurate energy management enhances battery life cycles by reducing expenses connected with safe usage of batteries applied to EVs. However, practically on EV application, their nonlinear time-varying characteristics are highly influenced by such random factors as driving loads and operating conditions. This paper carries out an analytical comparative study of several machine learning (ML) methods that can be used to predict the state-of-charge (SoC) of electric vehicle batteries. The investigated models include Linear Regression (LR), Support Vector Regression (SVR), Random Forest (RF), eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), and Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) were all trained on real-world lithium-ion battery datasets and validated against them. Their results are measured using Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), and the coefficient of determination, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, using the standard errors in their predictions. Of all models assessed herein, the ANN-based approach provided accuracy at its best. Low error rates also indicate strength for XGBoost and RF. And finally, higher prediction errors over complex battery dynamics are recorded by LR and SVR to denote their deficiencies in such complicated systems. As such, this study finds ANN to be the best algorithm in the estimation of SoC with a recording 0.005 MAE, 0.010 RMSE, and 0.999 R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: CerviTrans-XAI: An Explainable Vision Transformer Ensemble for Accurate Cervical Cancer Classification
authors: Md. Delower Hossain, Md. Mijanur Rahman, Md. Abdur Rahman, Tanvir Ahmed, Sujay Dhar, Ornab Biswass
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Cervical cancer presents an important worldwide health challenge, especially in developing nations, where restricted access to screening and postponed diagnosis remain substantial obstacles. Traditional diagnostic methods including Pap smears and Visual Inspection with Acetic Acid suffer from subjective interpretation and significant inter-observer variability, leading to an urgent requirement for automated diagnostic tools. This paper presents CerviTrans-XAI, an ensemble framework that combines multiple Vision Transformer architectures to achieve accurate cervical cancer cell classification with integrated explainable artificial intelligence capabilities. The proposed approach employs four distinct ViT models: ViT-B/16, ViT-DINO, DeiT-B/16, and Swin Transformer. These models train from the SipakMed dataset, which has five types of cervical cells. Through a soft voting ensemble methodology, which averages probability distributions from individual models, the framework achieves superior generalization compared to single model implementations. To overcome the black-box challenge of deep learning models in clinical settings, we incorporate LIME, which offer visual insights by identifying key cellular regions that influence diagnostic outcomes. Experimental validation demonstrates that the classification accuracy of CerviTrans-XAI is 98.27%. on the SipakMed dataset, establishing a new standard for automated cervical cancer screening systems. The integration of accurate diagnostics with clear, explainable decision-making makes this framework a valuable asset for assisting healthcare providers in under-resourced areas where expert pathologists are limited.

title: Privacy-Preserving and Explainable Multi-Hospital CKD Prediction via Federated Learning with Blockchain Logging and Differential Privacy
authors: Gazi Tahsina Sharmin Jahin, Firoze Maliha, Fatema Binte Hassan, Abul Masud Ahmad Muztaba, M. Jamshed Alam Patwary, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) impacts millions worldwide and requires early detection for improved patient outcomes. However, clinical data remains scattered across institutions, and centralized analysis is hindered by privacy concerns. This work introduces a federated HistGradientBoosting (HGB) framework enriched with blockchain logging, differential privacy (DP-SGD), and SHAP-based explainability to enable transparent and secure CKD prediction. A multi-hospital benchmark was constructed using real (UCI, Kaggle) and synthetic datasets distributed across five hospitals, with careful consideration of heterogeneity in terms of class imbalance, missing features, and non-IID demographics. The proposed model achieved an accuracy of 0.97 and an AUROC of 0.99, outperforming baselines such as LR, SVR, ANN, LSTM, centralized pooling, and vanilla FL. Blockchain provided immutable integrity of model updates with negligible overhead, DP-SGD ensured differential privacy guarantees (ϵ = 3–8), and SHAP offered both global feature rankings and patient-level interpretability. These results highlight that our system enables robust, privacy-preserving, and explainable CKD prediction, paving the way for realistic use cases of federated healthcare AI.

title: EfficientNet-Based Deep Learning for Diabetic Retinopathy Classification Highlighting Overfitting and Severity Class Limitations
authors: Praveena Mallampalli, Mahaveerakannan R
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Highly crucial to find diabetic retinopathy (DR) early on in clinical settings where resources are limited, so that visual loss does not become permanent, especially when the disease is found. EffDR-Net is a deep learning framework that is based on the EfficientNet-B3 architecture. The goal of this study is to equip EffDR-Net with the ability to assess the severity of DR using high-resolution fundus photos automatically. EffDR-Net uses compound-scaled convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and was trained and tested on the Kaggle EyePACS dataset. Kaggle made EffDR-Net. This dataset contains a large number of retinal photos; however, they are not evenly distributed across the five distinct severity levels of diabetic retinopathy (DR). Computer networks (CNNs) are utilized to enhance the accuracy of models and streamline computer tasks. To address common issues such as overfitting, the system employs transfer learning, aggressive data augmentation, dropout regularization, and batch normalization, among other techniques. There are also dropout regularization and batch normalization. The model struggles to accurately identify moderate to severe stages of DR, as the class distributions are not evenly distributed, and the features distinguishing between classes are not clear. Most of the time, these limits are caused by factors other than the ones mentioned. Overall, the model can be very accurate and converge quickly, although it does have some problems with appropriately identifying these stages. A lot of testing using class-wise accuracy, recall, F1-scores, and Grad-CAM visualizations shows that EffDR-Net is quite effective at identifying early DR. Still, it requires further refinement to classify advanced phases reliably. The results of the exhaustive evaluation lead to this conclusion. The information collected during the evaluation supports this conclusion. This study makes a significant contribution to the development of artificial intelligence-based ophthalmic screening skills by demonstrating how EfficientNet can be utilized to categorize patients in a real-world emergency room setting, along with its advantages and disadvantages.

title: A Custom Neural Network Approach for Leaf Disease Detection Using CNN Features
authors: Jumana, Md. Sabbir Ahammed, Mst. Israt Jahan Eva, Zinia Akter, A.N.M Ehsanul Haque Sourav, Esfak Ahammed
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Plant disease is the most severe problem of agriculture, particularly in nations like Bangladesh and India with agrarian dominated economies. Early Disease diagnosis is necessary to prevent food insufficiency. and minimize loss of crops. Traditional diagnosis is usually error susceptible, involves high manual effort, and is time consuming; therefore, scalable and automated solutions are preferable. The paper proposes a robust deep learning model that combines DenseNet169 convolutional neural network with an in-house feed forward neural network (FFN) classifier for disease classification of plants. The Classes were balanced and generalization was enhanced. Using data augmentation methods including zooming, horizontal flip, shear, and rotation. DenseNet169 + FFN The model was developed and verified in the Plantvillage dataset for sick and healthy leaves like Brown Spot, Blast, and Bacterial Leaf Blight. The observations show that the model runs with a commendable accuracy is 99.73% and macro-averaged precision, F1-measures, and recall of the above 99% over advanced machine learning classifiers like XGBoost, SVM, and Random Forest. Comparison with other backbones of CNN, i.e., EfficientNetB3 (99.61%), and VGG19 (99.47%), confirms that deep CNN architectures along with tailored classifiers always offer better performances. The suggested approach gives a scalable, efficient and a robust platform for plant disease detection that can be applied to resource-limited farming environments and facilitates early intervention to achieve sustainable crop management.

title: Adaptive Neural Ensemble Model: Novel Framework for Behavioral Change Analysis
authors: M. Manonmani, Kirubagaran. K, Anju Mohan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Juvenile rehabilitation aims to foster behavioral reform and facilitate the reintegration of young offenders into society. This study presents a novel framework for analyzing behavioral change in juveniles post-rehabilitation using an Adaptive Neural Ensemble Model, achieving remarkable accuracy of 98.17%. The study focuses on evaluating reformative techniques employed in juvenile rehabilitation centers. Behavioral metrics such as emotional stability, social adaptability, response to conflict, and participation in rehabilitation programs were analyzed. The model's feature importance analysis revealed that emotional stability (SHAP value: 0.162) and social adaptability (0.148) were the most critical predictors of successful rehabilitation outcomes. Reformative techniques, including group therapy and vocational training programs, showed the highest effectiveness, with improvement rates of 30.2% and 29.3%, respectively. Comparative analysis of 10 rehabilitation centers indicated that centers offering diverse programs achieved the best outcomes, with Center A demonstrating the highest improvement score of 31.2%. The model’s high accuracy, combined with balanced recall (98.25%) and precision (98.02%), underscores its robustness in identifying significant behavioral trends. This research provides actionable insights to optimize rehabilitation strategies, paving the way for enhanced juvenile development and successful societal reintegration.

title: Hybrid Deep Ensemble Approach Using ResNet101 and RFGB for Cardiovascular Disease Prediction
authors: K Budda Vara Prasad, Godi Sushma, K.Pavan Raju, Seeniappan Kaliappan, Ramya Maranan, Siva Kumar Pathuri
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) are the top cause of death rates and morbidity all over the world, and the emergence of advanced and smart diagnostic measures to assess risks at early stages and prevent them is crucial. Conventional diagnostic methods cannot be well scheduled and responsive as expected in analysing contents that are complex and dimensional in medical data sets. To overcome these weaknesses, the proposed model in this research is a hybrid deep ensemble that encompasses deep feature extraction using ResNet101, a deep convolutional neural network, and generates an accurate prediction with the combined Random Forest based Gradient Boosting (RFGB) model. The passed features are then subjected to the RFGB ensemble, which is the combination of the capabilities of the Random Forest and Gradient Boosting classifiers to give a robust classification performance. The proposed ResNet101-RFGB model is compared to the standard machine learning classifiers, such as Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Decision Tree (DT). Where SVM and DT have both attained an 88% and 92% accuracy in classification of a Yes/No data respectively, the hybrid model still secured a better accuracy of 96% in the determination of the data. The remarkable enhancement highlights the fact that the model is capable of handling multidimensional medical data and correctly determining the accuracy of the diagnosis. In general, the paper indicates that the ensemble learning can result in a considerable increase in the level of reliability in cardiovascular disease diagnosis, providing a scalable approach to clinical decision support systems.

title: Ultra-Fast FPGA-Based Color Image Watermarking: A Real-Time Secure Embedding Framework
authors: Niharika S, Manojkumar S B, Naveen K B, M B Anandaraju, Srividya C N, Tejaswini M R
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Color image watermarking plays a crucial role in securing visual content in today’s digital ecosystem. This paper presents a novel algorithm that embeds a color watermark into a color cover image using a pixel-level similarity-based technique, guided by a tunable modulation index to balance watermark invisibility and robustness. A pseudo-noise code ensures that watermark extraction remains restricted to authorized users, enhancing security without compromising visual quality. The algorithm has been implemented on FPGA hardware, achieving real-time processing at 60 frames per second with a watermark extraction accuracy of 96.3% and minimal perceptual distortion (PSNR >42 dB ). The design operates at a maximum frequency of 222MHz(4.5 ns) without any setup or hold violations, confirming its high-speed and reliable performance. These results underscore the method’s effectiveness for secure image authentication in low-latency embedded applications. Although the system performs well under standard image processing attacks, future work will explore adaptive watermark modulation, video watermarking, and robustness under extreme compression and geometric distortions. This research contributes a hardware-efficient and scalable framework for real-time digital watermarking, with significant implications for multimedia security and embedded vision systems.

title: FusionGraphMapper: A Graph-Based Neural Framework for Integrating Multi-Modal Data in Complex Systems
authors: Murugan Ramu, Rakshana Vijay, Saravanan S, S. Pavana, R. S. Amirtha Varshny, Pooja. S
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: With digital transformation, a wide range of data is produced by complex systems using sensors, keeping logs, collecting pictures and accessing databases. Classic fusion designs are usually limited in handling the detailed links that exist between different kinds of data sources. Thus, we build FusionGraphMapper, a novel graph-based system that helps unite various kinds of data and make quick decisions. It uses a graph representation where each node represents unique entities to each modality and each edge shows the relationships in time, space and meaning. FusionGraphMapper uses Graph Attention Networks (GATs) and Temporal Graph Networks (TGNs) to support dynamic passing of messages and the modeling of space and time. In addition, it employs cross-modal contrastive learning to create rules that unite feature embeddings which makes semantic connections better and makes the representation more reliable. It was tested against nine best existing models including Early Fusion, BERT+ResNet and LSTM-CNN in several benchmark tasks. The results of the experiments revealed that FusionGraphMapper achieved better results, reporting 96.5% accuracy, 97.4% F1-score and 97.1% AUC which were higher than all the other approaches. Because it can combine different data sources and understand relationships, the model helps in labeling things, forecasting and finding anomalies in the real world. The results support the effectiveness of using attention and time-based analysis in multi-modal tasks. It is an important improvement in learning with multiple sources of data and opens up fresh options for making AI suitable for broad applications.

title: Explainable AI Based Multi-Class Pneumonia Classification and Anomaly Detection from Chest X-ray
