This study aimed to evaluate the functioning of deep learning (DL) methods like LSTM, CNN-LSTM, Bi-LSTM, and CNN-Bi-LSTM, along with the attention mechanism, based on the household data and forecast the hourly load during 24 hours. CNN-Bi-LSTM utilizes the attention mechanism for load forecasting. The forecasting is carried out every hour to minimize the root mean squared error (RMSE) between the predicted and actual load demand. The training is done using a publicly available data set (34 households), and the models are then used to predict the 24hour aggregate active power demand of all the households at each of the hourly periods of the 24 hours, with the previous knowledge of the 24-hour consumption load. The evaluation results of deep learning models in short-term residential load forecasting shows that CNN-LSTM has the lowest average, which is 1.513 RMSE. The LSTM and the BiLSTM models also performed well with mean RMSE scores of 1.539 and 1.787, respectively, compared to the TCN and CNN models. The lower error shows the efficiency of the deep learning methods in seizing the temporal connections. This reflects on the increased power of DL architectures toward handling the complexity involved in residential load data.

title: BioScanFusion: A Multi-Modal Imaging Framework for Early Disease Detection with High-Resolution Diagnostics
authors: J. Gayathri, S.Dharanibhalan, Harini M, Mariashwinkumar, G. Nivetha, Keertic Raj.M
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Early imaging of diseases is important for both patient recovery and prompt medical solutions. However, depending only on imaging such as MRI, CT or PET can leave physicians with insufficient information because of the single-modality limitations. BioScanFusion is the subject of this paper: a new framework that uses MRI, PET, CT, Ultrasound and Optical Imaging to detect early disease at higher resolution. It unites patient information and Electronic Health Records (EHRs) to assist with diagnoses and relies on domain-adaptive encoders with a cross-attention fusion network (CAFNet) for better compatibility with various patient features. Super-resolution technology and specific segmentation for different diseases help a lot for interpretation and utility in clinical settings. The experiments involved a benchmark set of multi-modal medical cases and BioScanFusion was tested against nine other cutting-edge methods. The framework suggested improved classification accuracy to 96.8%, surpassing Accuracy of DualStreamFusion (92.4%) and AttentionMedNet (91.2%). It also provides high F1-scores (96.7%), a good AUC-ROC (0.987) and is fast in its predictions (112 ms), indicating its strong performance and efficiency. Findings prove that BioScanFusion is accurate for diagnosis and can be used on-site without delays in clinical practice. The outcomes of this research make BioScanFusion useful for sophisticated radiological diagnosis and help prepare smart diagnostic systems to connect pictures and patient information.

title: An Advanced Ai-Integrated Hybrid Approach For Enhanced Brain Tumor Segmentation And Smart Classification Using Deep Learning
authors: Yathavaraj V, Tamilarasan M, Keshavamurthy T G, Sunil Kumar K M, Vidya K V, Revanna B R
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: "Deep learning algorithms" are a subtype of machine learning algorithms that extract features from any dataset using supervised learning. Multi-layered neurons are used in these methods. Convolutional neural networks are one type of deep learning method created especially for feature extraction from datasets with a lot of spatial information, such as images. A mind. A tumor is an aberrant growth of brain tissue that, if untreated, can evolve into cancer. After identifying the problem with an MRI scan, a trained radiologist does a visual examination. Because it is a challenging task in developing nations due to a shortage of skilled professionals, research on computerizing brain MRI classification is currently ongoing. This study evaluates the model's accuracy and compares it to models created by

title: Design and Performance Analysis of a 128-Bit Synchronized Binary Counter Using Clock Gating and H-Tree with Backward Carry Propagation
authors: Shiny M I, Arun Shankar T K, Bhanupriya, Aakash Murali Nair, Jarin T, Fathima Shilna M
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: In digital systems, the fundamental building block is binary counter, which serving the critical role applications namely control systems, cryptographic engines, memory addressing and digital signal processing. This paper presents the design and implementation of a 128-bit synchronized binary counter using a 1-bit Johnson counter architecture with Backward Carry Propagation (BCP). Two methodologies—clock gating and H-tree clock distribution are employed to optimize performance in terms of power consumption, propagation delay, and area. The H-tree clock distribution and clock gating are the models to improve the performance across key metrics like area, power consumption and propagation delay. However, the H-tree design achieves superior timing performance, reducing propagation delay to 0.23s, while the clock gating implementation records 0.27s. This reflects a 17.39% improvement in delay with H-tree. In terms of area, the clock gating design occupies 776.81 μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, whereas the H-tree implementation slightly increases it to 817.04 μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The proposed method effectiveness is highlighted in these and the flexibility based on particular low power performance offered

title: A Transformer-LSTM Model for Monitoring the Banking Sector
authors: Sona K, B. Lavaraju, Asha Sundaram, Raja Surya Ajieeth A, Deepak S, P. S. Giridharan
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Officer’s misconduct continues to affect different industry sectors including banking where highly vulnerable people suffer exploitation. The researchers developed Transformer and LSTM architecture to observe and deal with officer’s misconduct in banking domain. The combination of Transformer models which understand context with LSTM networks capable of sequence pattern recognition allows the model to effectively process data from law enforcement reports and case files along with public complaints which include text, audio and video data. The model displays successful performance by reaching 91% accuracy alongside 88% precision alongside recall at 92% and 90% F1-score performance which surpasses standard models like BERT-LSTM and CNN. Detailed pre-processing methods consisting of Named Entity Recognition (NER) together with Sentiment Analysis help the model recognize patterns of misconduct. The model evaluation process using actual cases confirmed its ability to categorize misconduct events while establishing their severity levels. The system incorporated ethical elements to both minimize bias and ensure fair operations during responsible AI implementation. Through a real-time dashboard deployment CivicAwareNet-TL offers regulatory authorities an automated system which enhances transparency by monitoring officer’s behavior.

title: NightLPBD: A YOLO-Based Single-Stage Framework for Nighttime License Plate Recognition
authors: Md. Tahmid Hasan, Sk. Md. Masudul Ahsan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Efficient and effective License Plate Detection and Recognition (LPDR) systems are critical in intelligent transportation, toll management, and security enforcement. However, most existing models are trained and tested under optimal daytime conditions, ignoring the significant performance drop under nighttime conditions. In contrast to conventional approaches, which consider License Plate Detection (LPD) as well as recognizing characters as distinct tasks, this study proposes a single-stage architecture focused on the efficiency and accuracy of LPDR that is capable of detecting plates and recognizing texts and numbers in a single pass. This study also introduces a newly constructed nighttime LPDR dataset, Nighttime License Plates of Bangladeshi vehicles (NightLPBD). The images were manually captured and compared the performance of models trained on nighttime, daytime, and combined datasets, utilizing real-world data collected from toll plazas, roadside parking, vehicle stands, and garages. YOLO (You Only Look Once) is trained using the transfer learning approach to localize, detect, and recognize license plates and numbers within a unified system under diverse and challenging conditions, especially at nighttime. Among all of the evaluated models, YOLOv10x outperformed others, achieving an F1-score of 0.88 and an Average Precision (AP) of 0.92 at a 0.5 threshold for Intersection over Union (IoU). Moreover, the results show that models trained on nighttime data generalize better across illumination variations, and combining both datasets leads to the highest accuracy. The findings emphasize the importance of including nighttime images in training datasets to improve LPDR robustness in real-world applications.

title: Mathematical Design and Analysis of Energy Aware Consensus Algorithms in Smart Grid Edge Systems
authors: Md. Showrov Hossen, Md.Taukir Ahmed, Mst.Suraiya Sultana, Rubait Hasan Safiq, Md.Jamal Ali, Md. Jubayel
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The transformation of traditional power systems into smart grids has created new opportunities and challenges in distributed control, energy management and communication at the edge. Consensus algorithms, widely adopted for achieving distributed coordination, often neglect the energy limitations of edge devices such as smart meters, IoT sensors and microgrid controllers. This paper proposes an Energy-Aware Distributed Consensus Framework (EADCF) that integrates residual energy, communication cost and network importance into the mathematical formulation of consensus protocols. By introducing energy weighted Laplacian matrices and adaptive topology control, the framework ensures efficient load balancing, reduced communication overhead and prolonged node lifetime. Stability and convergence are rigorously analyzed using Lyapunov theory and spectral graph analysis, while formal verification with model checking validates correctness under stochastic disturbances and adversarial scenarios. Simulation results demonstrate that EADCF achieves a 40.1% reduction in energy consumption compared to traditional consensus algorithms while maintaining high convergence accuracy and resilience to partial node failures. Furthermore, scalability analysis confirms logarithmic growth in convergence time with increasing network size, highlighting the practicality of EADCF for large scale deployments. The proposed framework bridges the gap between consensus theory and real-world constraints of energy constrained smart grid edge environments, offering a mathematically sound and operationally viable solution for next-generation distributed energy systems.

title: Real-Time Optimized Irrigation for Sustainable Agriculture Using Machine Learning and IoT Sensors
authors: Sneha Mallick, Deeksha Sonal, Shahrukh Parvez, Paras Bhushan, Akshit Shyam, Akanksha Teja
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Efficient water management in agriculture is fundamental to handling the twofold issues of world food security and asset protection. This study investigates how water system necessities can be upgraded progressively by utilizing AI models and information from Web of Things (IoT) sensors. The framework makes more precise expectations about how much water expected by consolidating ecological information (soil dampness, temperature, stickiness, wind speed, and weather conditions conjectures) with Arbitrary Woodland Relapse and Backing Vector Relapse (SVR) models. Our discoveries show that harvest yields can be raised and water utilization can be significantly diminished with a constant, mechanized water system framework. Despite these benefits, more examination is important to resolve remarkable issues like sensor exactness, whimsical weather conditions, and the incorporation of sustainable power sources.

title: ViT-SAGE Hybrid: Transformer Patch Embeddings with Graph Neural Network Aggregation for Kidney CT Classification
authors: Shakib Absar, Rahin Miah, Sonjoy Sutradhar Shuvo, Md. Jehadul Islam Mony, Md. Jalal Uddin Chowdhury
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Accurate classification of kidney conditions from CT scans is critical for timely diagnosis and treatment. Traditional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) struggle to capture both global context and local spatial relationships essential for distinguishing complex kidney structures. Vision Transformers (ViTs) excel in modeling long-range dependencies but often miss fine-grained local patterns, while Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) like GraphSAGE effectively capture local relationships but require robust feature inputs. We propose ViT-SAGE, a novel hybrid architecture that integrates ViT’s global feature extraction with an enhanced GraphSAGE model for superior kidney disease classification. ViT processes images into patches, embedding them into high-dimensional vectors, which are transformed into a nearest neighbor graph. Our optimized GraphSAGE leverages advanced aggregation techniques and Focal Loss to address class imbalance, enhancing local relationship modeling. Rigorous evaluations demonstrate ViT-SAGE’s superior performance, achieving high precision, recall, and F1-scores with a benchmark of 97% accuracy across kidney condition classes. By combining ViT’s global context with GraphSAGE’s local learning, ViT-SAGE outperforms standalone CNNs, ViTs, and other GNN variants, setting a new benchmark for medical image analysis. This hybrid approach offers potential for broader applications, such as segmentation and cancer detection, advancing automated diagnostic tools in clinical settings.

title: A Review on Islanding Detection Techniques and Control Strategies for Microgrid
authors: Balwant Patil, Diwakar Joshi, Shravani Palekar, Sudhakar C. J., Uttam S Satpute
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Microgrid related studies need special attention on islanded mode and grid connected mode of operation. This article reviews islanding detection methods and control strategies for microgrids in the context of transitioning grid connected and is-landed mode of operation. Various detection methods broadly categorized as local and remote techniques have been discussed along with the emerging trends. Control strategies in both modes of operation for smoothing the process of switching and control for power quality and operational stability have been summarized. The advanced control methods namely Improved Droop, Intelligent Control strategy, Phase Lock Loop (PLL)-less methods, and Active Disturbance Rejection Control (ADRC) have been reviewed. The conclusion provides future direction for research and emphasizes the importance of these strategies to achieve resilience and reliability for microgrid operation. The discussion has been done about each method with overall context of the complex nature of microgrid operation and the implications to determine strategies for islanding detection and control operation to ensure stable and continuous power supply.

title: Histopathological Disease Identification with Interpretable Region-Based Attribution, Expert Scoring, and Quantitative Analysis
authors: Md. Abid Hasan Rafi, Robinul Haque Robin, Sadia Mahmud, Md Fatin Fuad, Pankaj Bhowmik, Md. Fazle Rabbi
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Histopathological diagnosis is essential for precise cancer identification and treatment strategy formulation; yet, the procedure is laborious and prone to variability among experts. Deep learning facilitates automation in histopathology categorization; nevertheless, its clinical implementation is hindered by limited interpretability and integration with diagnostic procedures. A comprehensible deep learning framework is established for the classification of histopathological diseases, prioritising diagnostic precision and clinical relevance. ResNet-101 with custom pipeline attains nearly flawless classification performance (0.9994), significantly surpassing VGG-16 and DenseNet-121, as validated by t-tests, ANOVA, McNemar, and χ<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> tests. The system provides confidence-aware attribution maps, region-based morphological ratings, and automated report generation to enhance clinical reasoning. A gradient-based explainable AI (XAI) interface, implemented using Streamlit, ensures real-time functionality and clear outputs. A comparative investigation reveals that deep residual networks strike an effective balance between predictive capability and interpretability. Post-hoc analysis, utilizing Tukey’s HSD, indicates class-wise consistency in ResNet-101 (ANOVA, p = 0.2546), whereas models like Inception-v3 exhibit significant inconsistency across classes (p < 0.0001). Region-specific evaluation reports augment diagnostic certainty and diminish interpretative ambiguity. The framework enhances a dependable standard for implementing explainable AI in digital pathology by incorporating accuracy, interpretability, and clinical alignment.

title: Natural Language Processing (NLP) for Healthcare Records: AI for Analyzing and Summarizing Medical Records and Doctor Notes
authors: Yash Tanaji Shinde, Shradha Gopal Shinde, Vaishnavi Vasant Patil, Prakash K Sonwalkar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Natural Language Processing (NLP) is a powerful tool for extracting and summarizing medical entities from unstructured data in Electronic Health Records (EHRs). It is based on a combination of machine learning and deep learning techniques to extract useful information from EHRs. The application of NLP is expected to improve healthcare workflows and patient care. The healthcare industry has implemented NLP technology as a revolutionary technology that delivers essential medical data analysis and automation solutions. In this paper, we highlight how NLP technologies can facilitate improving clinical processes and decision support by leveraging sophisticated approaches like machine learning, deep learning, and data science.

title: Fault Detection in Predictive Maintenance of Industry 4.0 using a Lightweight Hybrid model in the FL System
authors: Lakshmaiya N
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Industry 4.0 is the revolution in the industrial setting where the sharing of information securely takes place using the decentralised system of the Federated Learning approach. Federated learning models suffer from a heavy training model that is distributed among global and local models. Predictive maintenance is a crucial application of Industry 4.0, where fault detection is the primary concern, necessitating immediate action. The efficient, lightweight models, integrated with both local and global models, enable the system to function at a faster rate. Thus, the proposed work utilises the ResNet 18, a shallower model of the residual network, for feature extraction, and the Self-attention model for classification purposes in both local and global models. The proposed method achieved 94.8% global accuracy, with effective fault detection of weld images from the Kaggle dataset. The proposed method outperforms the other FL systems by 10% compared to the standard ResNet and 17% compared to CNN models.

title: AI-Powered Product Purchase Prediction: A Multi-Modal Deep Learning Approach
authors: Shuly Aktar, Sheikh Majedul Huq, Gyanendra Kumar, R. Gopal, Sonia Afroz, Md. Akkas Ali
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: AI and digital platforms have changed how practical analysis has become in sectors like cosmetics. Consumer perception towards cosmetic brands is crucial in making purchase decisions in Bangladesh, but traditional methods of measuring lack the reality. However, no studies have been found on modeling trust in such a domain in the context of cosmetic buying behavior in Bangladesh. Modern methods commonly focus either on structured data or free text, where there is a lack of the combination, and often, they do not provide interpretability that poses an obstacle to business utilization. The purpose of this study is to propose a method for predicting customer trust in cosmetics using an AI-based approach. It combines structured demographic and behavioral data with unstructured textual reviews; uses CatBoost and DNN for classification learning of models by SHAP and LIME to increase interpretability, and checks deployment readiness. The approach is a mixture of a structured response survey (Sample size: 1000) and unstructured review text analyzed through BERT embeddings. CatBoost and DNN models are adopted to predict trust toward consumers, as well as purchase intention. Model effectiveness is evaluated by measures such as accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score, MAE, MSE, RMSE, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. SHAP and LIME are used to increase the transparency and interpretability. We have implemented the DL models that outperform the baseline models with accuracy (93%, 90%), Precision (93%, 90%), Recall (92%, 87%) F1 -Score (93%, 89%), MAE (0.047, 0.036), MSE (0.004, 0.002), RMSE (0.059, 0.045), and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score (98%, 92%) for CatBoost, and DNN, respectively for the purchase intention. SHAP identified essential factors related to trust, such as brand trust and review sentiments. The statistical analysis (ANOVA and Friedman test) supported the validity of the model. This AI-based methodology provides valuable results to cosmetic marketers operating in Bangladesh, presenting personalized strategies and better consumer engagement plans. Structured and unstructured data combined allow artificial intelligence in cosmetics to predict customer trust for scalable decision-making.

title: Design and Deployment of Lightweight CNN-Based Secure Web Portal for Patient-Centric Remote Medical Diagnosis
authors: Sanskruti Bedasur, Sudhiksha Hanji, Vijayalaxmi R Sheleppagol, Shobha. R Hiremath, Chitrashree Kurtakoti, Karuna C. Gull
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AI) has led to significant improvements in the healthcare sector, particularly in the area of remote medical diagnosis. AI-based systems, especially those utilizing convolutional neural networks (CNNs), have shown promising results in interpreting complex medical data such as diagnostic images. However, the deployment of these models often encounters challenges due to their high computational requirements and concerns related to the privacy and security of patient data. To address these issues, this project proposes the development of a secure, patient-centric web portal that employs lightweight CNN models— specifically MobileNetV3 and EfficientNet—for efficient and real-time medical image diagnosis. These models are optimized to operate effectively in resource-constrained environments without compromising diagnostic performance. The system integrates federated learning, a decentralized machine learning approach, to ensure that patient data remains on the local device while model updates are shared securely. This approach significantly enhances data privacy and complies with healthcare data protection standards. The web portal allows patients to upload their medical images securely, receive rapid and reliable predictions, and be notified promptly about diagnosis results. All sensitive medical data is encrypted throughout the process to ensure confidentiality and integrity. The experimental setup aims to validate the proposed system using real-world medical datasets, focusing on achieving minimal latency, robust scalability, and consistent accuracy. Future work will involve refining the models, expanding dataset support, and integrating advanced security protocols to further solidify the system’s role in remote, AI-assisted medical diagnostics.

title: DomainR: Domain-Based Dynamic Routing for Time-Sensitive Communication in Mega LEO Constellations
authors: Yajing Zhang, Xiaofan Xu, Yueyue Zhang, Mingji Dong, Cailian Chen, Xinping Guan, Ping Du
abstract: In mega low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellations, guaranteeing deterministic communication performance is essential for mission-critical applications and reliable service delivery. Unlike terrestrial networks, these large-scale satellite networks present unique challenges: highly dynamic topologies, significant propagation delays, limited on-board processing capabilities, and cumulative jitter uncertainty that increases with hop count. In this case, traditional deterministic mechanisms, i.e., Time-sensitive networking (TSN), become ineffective due to their inability to handle both the scale and dynamics of mega constellations. To address this issue, we propose a TSN-enabled LEO satellite network, whereby we derive single-hop delay upper bounds using network calculus, specifically accounting for high-priority preemption and dynamic satellite characteristics. Then we propose DomainR, a novel domain-based deterministic routing framework that effectively manages large-scale satellite networks through strategic network decomposition and adaptive routing. DomainR consists of two phases: first, a domain-based network compression phase that partitions mega constellations into manageable domains while preserving path continuity and routing efficiency, and, second, a delay budget-aware dynamic routing phase that adaptively allocates delay budgets based on aforementioned delay upper bounds and real-time network conditions. Through rigorous mathematical analysis, we derive the optimal domain sizing rules and establish a theoretical minimum domain count. Comprehensive simulations demonstrate that DomainR significantly reduces end-to-end jitter, improves network resource utilization, and capacity in mega LEO constellations.

title: Data-Driven Approach to Synthetic Inertia and Droop Estimation in Behind-the-Meter Renewable Energy Sources
authors: SungHoon Lim, Jihun Kook, Kwang Y. Lee, Jung-Wook Park
abstract: Renewable energy sources (RESs) are expected to play a key role in supporting frequency stability in modern power systems, and thus, a variety of inertia-control methods have been developed. However, many RESs are installed behind the meter (BTM), and their data often remain inaccessible to the energy management systems (EMS) operated by utilities. As a result, their contributions to system inertia are frequently overlooked in stability evaluations. Although several inertia estimation methods have been proposed, most assume full availability of RES data. In practice, however, access to some data is often restricted to utilities, which limits the applicability of conventional methods, particularly for BTM-installed RESs. This article presents two data driven approaches for estimating the synthetic inertia and droop coefficients of BTM RESs, explicitly considering data availability under both normal and dynamic conditions. The first approach uses steady state EMS and RES data with different sampling intervals, while the second relies only on EMS dynamic data recorded during contingencies, avoiding dependence on RES measurements. Verification on the Jeju Island power system with practical measured EMS and RES data validate the proposed approaches and emphasize the influence of sampling intervals on estimation accuracy. The results show that the proposed methods provide an effective solution for assessing inertia and droop of BTM RESs with only limited data access, enabling utilities to conduct more reliable frequency stability analysis in low inertia grids.

title: A Learnable Cross-Modal Adapter for Industrial Fault Detection Using Pretrained Vision Models
authors: Jonne van Dreven, Abbas Cheddad, Sadi Alawadi, A. N. Ghazi, J. Al Koussa, Dirk Vanhoudt
abstract: Automatic fault detection and diagnosis (FDD) are critical for maintaining reliable and efficient industrial systems. However, conventional methods rely heavily on manual inspections or threshold-based techniques, which often fail to capture the dynamic patterns in time series (TS) sensor data. As a result, faults persist for extended periods, leading to suboptimal system operations, increased energy waste, and significant economic losses. This work proposes a cross-modal framework that facilitates the efficient deployment of state-of-the-art pretrained vision models for enhanced FDD, with two novel TS-to-image transformations: first, an adapter deep encoder that learns optimal, task-specific representations from raw sensor data while generating outputs that are input-compliant with pretrained models. Second, an enhanced line plot that creates geometric shapes of two related signals. Comparative experiments against fixed methods, including spectrograms, Gramian angular fields, Markov transition fields, recurrence plots, and five deep learning baseline models, showed substantial performance gains across diverse domains. InceptionTime achieved the highest average baseline performance with an F<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_{1}$</tex-math></inline-formula> of 88.6%, while the adapter and shapes achieved 94.4% and 92.4%, respectively. The findings highlight the potential of the cross-modal framework for FDD to facilitate early intervention and efficient system maintenance in industrial settings.

title: Multivehicle Cooperative Lane-Change Trajectory Planning: An Imitation Learning Approach
authors: Jialing Zhou, Shuaiguang Chen, Yuezu Lv, Peihu Duan, Guanghui Wen
abstract: Multivehicle cooperative lane-change (MVCLC) is a challenging problem in multivehicle trajectory planning, as it involves complex coordination and collision avoidance among vehicles in dynamic environments. While optimal control-based methods have demonstrated promising performance, their high computational complexity often limits practical deployment. To address this, we propose a novel imitation learning (IL) framework for MVCLC trajectory planning, which integrates optimal control-based trajectory generation with neural network (NN) training for efficient real-time inference. Specifically, we first formulate an optimal control framework for MVCLC and employ numerical optimization to generate high-quality reference trajectories. These trajectories are then used to construct a diverse dataset for training two NNs, enabling fast trajectory generation via IL. To ensure the safety and feasibility of the generated trajectories, we introduce a safety monitoring module that guarantees collision-free execution. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves near-optimal performance with significantly improved computational efficiency—achieving a reduction in planning time by 97.1% compared to traditional methods.

title: Quantum Key Distribution Network Simulator with Hyperparameter Tuning for Realistic Performance Analysis
authors: Ju-Bong Kim, Hyun-Kyo Lim, Wonhyuk Lee, Dahyun Yum, Chankyun Lee
abstract: Realistic simulation of quantum key distribution (QKD) networks requires a combination of precise models of quantum channels and network functions. Unfortunately, existing QKD network simulators lack either rigorous quantum physics modeling or network key-management and routing protocols, which limit their practical applicability. To address this limitation, we propose a QKD network simulator with hyperparameter tuning (QKDSim-HT), a NetSquid-based simulator specialized for polarization-based discrete-variable QKD networks. Specifically, QKDSim-HT models properties in quantum physics (photon loss, polarization-dependent depolarization, detector inefficiencies, dark counts, and dead times) as well as advanced functionalities in networking algorithms (multi-hop key relay and adaptive routing). Extensive simulations over diverse topologies reveal that the networking performance varied with respect to the fundamental properties of quantum physics, such as depolarization, fiber length, and channel attenuation. As a representative use case of QKDSim-HT, simulation results from QKDSim-HT over simple examples clearly manifest that a design of QKD networks highly affects performance of the networks. Accordingly, QKDSim-HT can serve as a practical platform for performance evaluation and optimization of quantum secured network.

title: Investigating the Influence of Different Diverter Pipes on the Humidity for FOUP by Using Computational Fluid Dynamics
authors: Yen-Hsin Chan, Jia-Sheng Du, Ming-Xian Lin, Chi-Hua Hsu
abstract: Maintaining a controlled environment is essential for preserving wafer quality and manufacturing yield, as humidity, oxygen, and airborne contaminants can degrade wafer surfaces during fabrication and storage. Computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations based on the k–ε turbulence model were applied to reduce relative humidity inside a front opening unified pod (FOUP) using clean dry air (CDA) purging. A guiding duct was designed to enhance CDA circulation between wafer layers, and the influences of duct geometry and perforation configuration on flow distribution and dehumidification performance were systematically evaluated. The results indicate that increasing the number of perforations improves gas intake, while enlarging the duct diameter increases flow rate and suppresses jet effects, leading to improved flow uniformity and moisture removal. An optimized configuration combining an expanded duct with a 25-hole design achieves an average internal humidity of 3.2%, approximately 5% lower than that of a conventional cylindrical duct. These findings provide a clear direction for future FOUP design optimization and support humidity control strategies that can reduce average internal moisture levels, thereby improving process yield and enhancing production capacity in semiconductor manufacturing.

title: AGEP_TWAS: A Deep Learning-based Framework for Predicting Gene Expression Levels in Tissues_supp1-3663364.docx
authors: Quanzhong Liu
abstract: Accurate prediction of gene expression levels across different tissues is of great significance in understanding the functional roles of genes in various biological processes and assisting in transcriptome-wide association studies (TWAS). Traditional methods rely on the construction of a prediction model for each gene in a specific tissue, which is time-consuming and inefficient when dealing with numerous genes or tissues. In addition, current approaches do not consider missing single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) in the training population. These SNPs significantly affect gene expression levels and in turn limit the predictive capability of these approaches in new samples. Recent research indicates that by identifying a specific group of core genes (known as landmark genes) that accurately reflect the cellular states of samples across different experimental conditions, it is possible to predict the expression levels of other genes in the genome. In light of this, we propose AGEP_TWAS (Adaptive Gene Expression Predictor for TWAS), a gene expression prediction method that utilizes a dense connection network, adaptive activation functions, and parameter pruning strategies within a nonlinear feature extraction computational framework. AGEP_TWAS leverages landmark genes within a tissue to predict the expression levels of other genes that are challenging to predict using traditional methods. Results on the human GEO expression dataset demonstrate that AGEP_TWAS achieved a mean squared error (MSE) of 0.1821 and a Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) of 0.9004, outperforming existing state-of-the-art prediction models. Additionally, when applied to the CattleGTEx dataset to infer gene expression levels across different tissues in cattle, AGEP_TWAS exhibited superior predictive performance compared to existing methods. A TWAS on milk production traits in cattle highlights the practical utility of AGEP_TWAS, with six identified significant genes already reported in scientific literature to be associated with milk production traits.

title: Digital and Analog Hybrid (DAH) PWM Pixel Circuit Enabled by Multilevel Reference Voltage for AM μ LED Display
authors: Zhibang Song, Congwei Liao, Yuxuan Zhu, Tengyan Huang, Xin Zheng, Yuhan Zhang, Dandan Zhao, Shengdong Zhang
abstract: In this article, a digital and analog hybrid pulsewidth modulation (DAH-PWM) driven pixel circuit is proposed for active-matrix micro light-emitting diode (AM<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>LED) displays. The DAH mode is realized by integrating the sub-frame control scheme of digital PWM (D-PWM) into the driving architecture of analog PWM (A-PWM) using a multilevel reference voltage. It is shown that the DAH-PWM pixel circuit necessitates only half the data programming time compared to the conventional D-PWM approach. It achieves a notable reduction in pulsewidth modulation (PWM) conversion time, down to 11.2% of that in the A-PWM method. Furthermore, despite its compact 6T2C structure, the proposed circuit demonstrates a strong threshold voltage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{T}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> compensation capability. Experimental measurements confirm that the grayscale error keeps below 3.7%, even when the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{T}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> shifts by 2 V.

title: A Distributed Learned Hash Table
authors: Shengze Wang, Yi Liu, Xiaoxue Zhang, Liting Hu, Chen Qian
abstract: Distributed Hash Tables (DHTs) are pivotal in numerous high-impact key-value applications built on distributed networked systems, offering a decentralized architecture that avoids single points of failure and improves data availability. Despite their widespread utility, DHTs face substantial challenges in handling range queries, which are crucial for applications such as LLM serving, distributed storage, databases, content delivery networks, and blockchains. To address this limitation, we present LEAD, a novel system incorporating learned models within DHT structures to significantly optimize range query performance. LEAD utilizes recursive machine learning models as the Learned Hash Function to map and retrieve data across a distributed system while preserving the inherent order of data. LEAD includes the designs to minimize range query latency and message cost while maintaining high scalability and resilience to network churn. Our comprehensive evaluations, conducted in both testbed implementation and simulations, demonstrate that LEAD achieves tremendous advantages in system efficiency compared to existing range query methods in large-scale distributed systems, reducing query latency and message cost by 80% to 90%+. Furthermore, LEAD exhibits scalability and robustness against system churn, providing a robust, scalable structure for efficient data retrieval in distributed key-value systems.

title: SA.Resilience.Evaluator: Evaluate the Resilience of Software Architecture Based on Attack Surface Analysis
authors: Can Zhao, Jiaxin Pan, Bixin Li
abstract: Resilience is vital to software systems and helps them maintain an acceptable level of service in the face of various attacks. However, existing methods for evaluating the resilience of software system mainly focus on implemented software system, there is no effective method to evaluate resilience at design time. In addition, existing methods only concern specific types of software systems, lacking comprehensive consideration of multi-dimensional characteristics of software systems and they often have stringent conditions when evaluated in practical applications etc. In this paper, we propose a method for evaluating the resilience of software architecture based on attack surface analysis and it is applicable to various types of software systems. Our method first identifies potential security threats and vulnerabilities in software architecture through attack surface analysis technology. Then, our method analyzes the types and severity of potential external attacks on the software architecture by combining the CAPEC software attack database. After that, our method comes to evaluate the resilience of software architecture according to the safety, reliability, and restoration of software architecture. For the convenience of conducting the case studies, we first design and implement a tool called SA.Resilience.Evaluator and then we use the tool to evaluate the effectiveness of our method in two scenarios. In the first scenario, we use the UML activity diagram of software architecture model as the input of our tool, which was obtained from architecture designer; in the second scenario, we use architecture dependency graph as the input, which was obtained from architecture recovery techniques. The results of studies show that our method is better than existing methods in both effectiveness and efficiency.

title: Learning From Each Other: Generalized Federated Incremental Semantic Segmentation
authors: Jiahua Dong, Wenqi Liang, Yang Cong, Gan Sun, Lixu Wang, Henghui Ding, Yulun Zhang, Luc Van Gool
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Federated learning (FL) has advanced semantic segmentation through decentralized training to reduce annotation costs. However, most FL-based semantic segmentation methods assume fixed foreground classes, resulting in catastrophic forgetting of old categories when local clients continually collect streaming data of new classes without storing old categories. Moreover, the irregular participation of new local clients with novel classes unseen by others may exacerbate heterogeneous forgetting across clients during global FL training. To resolve the above challenges, we propose a <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">H</u>ierarchical <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">F</u>orgetting <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>lleviation (HFA) model. By tackling forgetting within and across local clients, our model ensures that all local clients learn from each other as they continuously learn new categories. Specifically, to alleviate class-imbalanced forgetting within local clients induced by background shift, we develop a confidence-regularized pseudo labeling strategy to produce class-balanced soft pseudo labels for old categories that are labeled as background. Guided by soft pseudo labels, we design a graph-induced relation matching loss and a forgetting-balanced gradient propagation module to tackle ambiguous inter-class relations and class-imbalanced gradient propagation among old classes. Besides, a novel task detection module and an adaptive DBSCAN clustering are devised to address inter-client heterogeneous forgetting. They detect the arrival of new tasks to store the old global model for local pseudo labeling and distillation, while supplying global class prototypes for modeling inter-class relations and warm-starting global classifier. Experiments on multiple datasets verify our model's superiority over other methods.

title: Editorial
authors: Aviral Shrivastava

title: A Configurable Infrared Communication Platform for Custom Protocol Development, Prototyping and Testing
authors: R. Blanco, L. Gavilanes, R. Garcia-Ordeig, J. Oreggioni
abstract: This work presents an open-source, configurable IR communication platform that supports both standard and custom protocols. Based on the MSP430G2553 microcontroller, the platform allows full configuration of IR protocol parameters via a PC-based graphical user interface. In particular, the carrier frequency can be configured between 3.9 kHz and 70 kHz, covering both standard IR and experimental protocols. The PWM duty cycle is adjustable from 10 % to 90 %, with a resolution of 1 %. Pulse and pause durations for logic “0” and “1” bits are configurable from 560 µs to 65 ms, supporting both fast and slow signaling schemes. The platform supports a wide range of protocols (NEC, SIRC, Samsung, Panasonic, and others). Experimental results confirm reliable signal generation, protocol compliance, and efficient memory usage, making the platform suitable for both the development and testing of standard and custom IR protocols. Additionally, the platform offers a high degree of customization while maintaining ease of use, making it particularly well suited for both research and prototyping environments.

title: CS-Mamba: A Novel Mamba-Based Framework for Landslide Detection with Attention Guided Cross-Scale Context Semantic Integration
authors: Yanrong Mao, Haiming Zhang, Rongfan Dai, Tingjian Yang, Guanting Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Landslides are a frequent natural hazard, and the precise detection of affected areas using high-resolution optical imagery is of great significance for geological disaster emergency response and risk assessment. In optical images, landslides often exhibit substantial scale variations, complex and diverse morphologies, and blurred boundaries, and are susceptible to environmental influences. Existing deep learning approaches have not fully exploited cross-scale dependencies among pixels or ensured the consistency between feature representations and semantic embeddings, leading to insufficient model robustness. To address these limitations, we propose a novel Mamba-based framework for landslides detection with attention-guided cross-scale context semantic integration (CS-Mamba). In the encoder, parallel multi-scale convolutional branches are introduced to enhance the network's representation capability. At the core, a multi-scale-aware state-space module in the neck adopts bidirectional information flows and a gated fusion mechanism to integrate global and local features, thereby improving semantic consistency in boundary segmentation. In the decoder, an attention-guided strategy is employed to fuse multi-level features, achieving precise preservation of boundary details and strong robustness to cross-domain data. The proposed method is evaluated on multiple representative landslide datasets, including the LMHLD, Bijie, and GLVM datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that CS-Mamba achieves superior performance on nearly all metrics and exhibits strong generalization ability in domain generalization analysis.

title: EnsembleVisionNet: A Hybrid Approach Using EfficientNet and Random Forests to Analyze IP Protection in Silk Design
authors: Sruthy Murugan Usha, Marimuthu U., B.Lavaraju, Vandana Pradeep, Ruthikaa Jayasree, Amitha Jayakumar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Intellectual Property (IP) protection in textile industries, particularly in traditional handloom sectors like Kanchipuram silk, is crucial to safeguarding cultural heritage and preventing unauthorized replication of designs. This study presents EnsembleVisionNet, a hybrid deep learning model that integrates EfficientNet for feature extraction with a Random Forest classifier to analyze and classify silk motifs based on their IP protection status. The proposed model achieves 94.7% accuracy, 93.8% precision, 94.2% recall, and 94.0% F1-score, outperforming traditional CNNs and boosting classifiers. A detailed confusion matrix analysis highlights minimal misclassification, with 462 protected and 455 unprotected designs correctly classified. Feature importance analysis using LIME reveals that texture complexity (32.5%) and symmetry (24.1%) are the most significant classification features. Comparative evaluation against VGG16, ResNet50, XGBoost, and MobileNetV2 demonstrates EnsembleVisionNet’s superiority in textile pattern analysis. Further, robustness analysis under data augmentation scenarios confirms high performance under rotations (93.5%) and lighting variations (92.8%), ensuring real-world applicability. This model serves as a decision-support tool for textile manufacturers, IP law experts, and policymakers. Future improvements will focus on GAN-based synthetic motif generation and real-time deployment for automated textile IP enforcement.

title: Implementation of EV fast Charging Technique in Islanded DC Microgrid
authors: Rahul Sharma
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: A novel fast charging control scheme is proposed for electric vehicle (EV) batteries by using capacitor properties in islanded microgrid to manage the adverse effect on the bus voltage and other loads of DC microgrid. The conventional fast charging algorithms negatively impact the power quality and performance of DC microgrid. Voltage swag, dangerously high transient current and high losses are occurred during activation of fast EV charging in DC microgrid. To handle the abovementioned problems, fast charging control of EV is designed using supercapacitor to improve the performance of charging infrastructure under DC microgrid environment. The DC microgrid structure is considered for the implementation of proposed technique which consists of a battery as energy storage device (BESS) and a PV used as generation unit (DGs). A bidirectional buck-boost converter has been incorporated to interface each charging unit with DC bus. The model is simulated in MATLAB Simulink and the results are analyzed. The effectiveness of the proposed control is shown using a comparative analysis in the DC microgrid model.

title: Performance and Interpretability in Diabetes Prediction: Optimized Ensemble Learning and Explainable AI
authors: Ferdous Ahmed, Isha Das, Samiha Nowrin, Jafry Mondol Deep, Shanto Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Predicting diabetes early and correctly is essential as it helps to avoid serious health outcomes and minimize the burden of the condition. This study provides the framework of machine learning approach to diabetes prediction using the combination of ensemble classifier and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) methods. The publicly available dataset on diabetes was preprocessed by outlier removal, feature scaling, and the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) method of class balancing. Three class incidence models (Random Forest, XGBoost and LightGBM) were trained and tested. The best performance was recorded in the optimized Random Forest model which had accuracy of 98.14%. SHAP, LIME, ICE/PDP and Yellowbrick visualizations were also utilized to make interpretability better, where glucose, BMI, and insulin were highlighted as the strongest predictors of the model. The model offers good balance between the accuracy of predictions and clinical transparency in comparison to the recent works, and as such, the model has potential as a useful candidate in support systems of actual diagnosis.

title: Enhancing Ayurvedic Panchakarma Through Machine Learning and Digital Health Technologies
authors: Tushar Dhar Shukla, S. Parvathi, Anuradha Parasar, Sharada K A, Kingshuk Das Bakshi, Dhivya T
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Supervised learning is essential in classification problems, and ML, a dynamic subfield of computer science, has found useful uses in many other domains. The five-thousand-year-old Indian holistic medical system known as Ayurveda places an emphasis on the individual's constitution and the harmony of the body, mind, and spirit. Modern classification approaches promote personalised healthcare delivery, benefiting the traditional rejuvenation and cleansing therapy of Ayurvedic Panchakarma. In order to classify Ayurvedic Panchakarma data, this work introduces a three-stage ML model. Training the model, selecting features, and preprocessing are all parts of the model. Two methods, one of which is a moving average filter, are used in the preprocessing phase to separate healthy participants from sick ones using spline data. The initial input data is classified using a CNN, and PCA is used for feature selection. With an impressive 98.31% classification accuracy, the suggested CNN model surpasses state-of-the-art approaches. The model's efficacy and its ability to assist in clinical decision-making are shown by these results. An encouraging path towards creating culturally grounded, intelligent healthcare systems is the combination of ML with traditional Ayurvedic therapies, particularly Panchakarma.

title: Optimized Feature Selection And Machine Learning Techniques for Early Detection of Chronic Kidney Disease
authors: K. Vengatesan, V.D. Ambeth Kumar, Jay Nilesh Jobanputra, Sakshi Raju Rothe, Shreya Pravin Patil, P. Sindhu, Anjali Avinash Jagtap
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive approach to early CKD detection by integrating optimal feature selection with strong machine learning. The study highlights data quality and model preparation by addressing missing values, outliers, and categorical encoding using the Kidney disease.csv dataset, which contains 26 demographic and clinical factors across 400 patient records. In order to enhance the model's performance, the optimal CKD predictors were identified using Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE) and Mutual Information Gain. In terms of accuracy and robustness, ensemble-based models—particularly Random Forest and XGBoost—bested traditional classifiers, according to an analysis of different algorithms, such as Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, XGBoost, K-Nearest Neighbours, Support Vector Machine, Naïve Bayes, and Voting Classifier. The reliability and generalisability of these models were demonstrated by their near-perfect performance in cross-validation. In order to improve patient outcomes and medical intervention, this research demonstrates that intelligent feature selection and machine learning may be used to construct diagnostic tools for early CKD detection that are both efficient and interpretable.

title: Scalable RNN-Based Transfer Learning for Patient Sentiment Monitoring in Telehealth Platforms
authors: Nusrat Yasmin Nadia, Habibor Rahman Rabby, Md Habibul Arif, Md Iftekhar Monzur Tanvir, Mumtahina Ahmed, Sabiha Firdaus
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Telehealth platforms are generating large amounts of patient feedback that can provide valuable insights into healthcare quality. However, analyzing this unstructured text in a scalable and reliable way is challenging. In this paper, we present a sentiment analysis framework that uses a BiLSTM with an attention mechanism. The model is first trained on a large medical reviews dataset (Drugs.com) and then adapted to telehealth app reviews (Google Play) using transfer learning. Our experiments show that the model achieves 85.67% accuracy and an 84.41% macro F1-score on the medical dataset, and after transfer learning, it reaches 82.85% accuracy and an 81.95% macro F1-score on telehealth reviews. We also test the framework under different data sizes and model depths, showing that it maintains strong performance while remaining scalable. These results demonstrate the potential of our approach for real-world deployment in monitoring patient sentiment on telehealth platforms.

title: Federated Intelligence for Secure Electoral Systems: A Privacy-Enhancing Smart Voting Architecture with Decentralized Biometric Verification and Blockchain-Based Auditability
authors: Swatha J, Gopika Shree A. S., Athyul Thomas V S, Dinesh Prabu M, Mithra K, Deepika L
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: To address critical vulnerabilities in traditional electoral systems, including data tampering and voter impersonation, this paper introduces a novel Smart Voting System (SVS). Our architecture ensures voter privacy, security, and transparency by integrating Federated Learning (FL), Edge AI, and blockchain technology. The system is designed for low-power edge devices like the Raspberry Pi and Jetson Nano, performing all biometric verification locally to prevent raw data transmission.Our methodology employs an adaptive ensemble of facial recognition models, optimized by AutoML, which achieves up to 98.6% verification accuracy under variable lighting conditions. We integrated MediaPipe Face Mesh for robust liveness detection, successfully preventing 100% of tested spoofing attacks. The FL framework enhanced the average model accuracy from a baseline of 87% to 90.3% by aggregating encrypted model updates from edge devices, preserving user privacy while improving performance. Each verified vote is immutably recorded on a private blockchain using SHA-256 hashing, guaranteeing a transparent and tamper-proof audit trail. This work presents a comprehensive, scalable, and secure digital voting infrastructure validated through rigorous experimentation

title: Explainable Triple Attention Dual Scale Residual Network for Diabetic Retinopathy Detection Using Retinal Fundus and OCT Images
authors: K.S.Nalini, Arunachalam A.S
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR), long recognized as an important cause of blindness, with timely detection essential for preventing visual loss. DR is traditionally diagnosed by manual evaluation of retinal images, which is fully labor involvement and major error causing system. Deep learning models are promising for automation of DR detection but many are not interpretable. This paper, we proposed an Explainable Triple Attention Dual Scale Residual Network (ET-DSRN) for GLIVEK classification using retinal fundus and OCT image datasets. Triple Attention, Dual Scale, Residual Learning Combined for Enhanced Predictive Power and understandability ET-DSRN alleviates the vanishing gradient problem and improves feature extraction at varying scales. We show from experimental results that the proposed method outperforms existing methods. The clarity of the model provides trust and transparency for its clinical use." The method is authenticate through publicly available datasets demonstrating enhanced detection accuracy. The techniques are based on Butt et al. (2023); the greatly improved the DR detection process

title: CNN-Based Multi-Class Classification of Brain Tumors from MRI Scans
authors: Apurva Apurva, D. Vetrithangam
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI)-based brain tumor classification is an important procedure to perform in the initial diagnosis and advanced treatment planning. This paper introduces a deep learning framework that would classify brain tumors into three classes, namely, glioma, meningioma, and pituitary tumor, and a class of no tumor and would be based on convolutional neural network classification (CNN). Data are composed of T1-weighted contrast-enhanced MRI scans that are obtained based on publicly available repositories. Task preprocessing of the images involved resizing, normalization and data augmentation to improve model generalizability.A CNN architecture is developed having several convolutional and pooling layers, then dense and softmax activation layer to perform multi-class prediction. The resulting model had a remarkable accuracy of 96.2% on the test set and it can be considered a high reliability in the differences between the tumor categories. The model also had an Area Under the Curve (AUC) score of 0.985 which represents an outstanding discriminating performance in all classes.Assessment parameters including confusion matrix, precision, recall and F1-score confirmed the strength of the model further. The interpretability of the resulting model was enriched by means of Grad-CAM, which shows the areas with the most significant impact on the decision of the classification process. The protocol has the potential of further clinical integration and it is a non-invasive, automatic, and precise diagnostic support tool to be used by the radiologists and the oncologists.

title: Plain-Polarization Insensitive Epsilon-Negative Metamaterial Absorber for Wireless Communication Applications
authors: Mohammad Raihan Jamil Asif, Md. Ismail Hossen, Saif Hannan, Md. Ibrahim, Md. Rihan, Robin Pal, Tasib Rahman, Md. Jafrul Hasan, Diganta Barua
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This study proposes a metamaterial absorber (MMA) with a split-maze-shaped multiple square-enclosed design, offering simplicity in structure yet delivering highly efficient performance, it covers the multiband C and X bands. With a design optimized for near-zero index metamaterial (NZIM) property, the absorber avoids the need for lumped components, hence simplifying manufacture using a standard FR4 substrate and a unit cell dimension of 10 × 10 × 1.6 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, all while ensuring excellent efficiency. The suggested absorber exhibits polarization insensitivity at incidence angles up to 180°. The suggested metamaterial absorber achieves peak absorption rates of 99.77%, 98.96%, and 92.73%, exhibiting polarization insensitivity in frequency ranges of 5.529 GHz, 6.501 GHz, and 9.534 GHz, respectively, rendering it appropriate for advanced communication systems. It demonstrates single negative (SNG) properties for permittivity(Epsilon-Negative), confirming the metamaterial features at the resonant frequencies. The commercially available simulator, Computer Simulation Technology (CST), is used to build and simulate the MMA design. As the absorber is insensitive to the incident wave’s polarization, this development can enhance the performance of WiMAX, radar, and satellite applications by offering a compact, efficient, and robust solution for multi-band absorption.

title: Ride Sharing Application- RIDEMATE
authors: Apurva Vande, Pratik Jaypurkar, Shraddha Panadwar, Asim Khan, Ankit Jambhulkar, Shilpa Ghode
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper describes the creation of an intelligent ride-sharing application that combines cloud computing, natural language processing (NLP), and machine learning (ML) to offer a user-centric, scalable, and effective platform for car and scooter ride-sharing. Through the analysis of massive amounts of data, such as user preferences, current traffic conditions, and past ride patterns, the application leverages machine learning (ML) to improve ride-matching accuracy. The system decreases waiting times, optimizes routes, and boosts vehicle utilization by anticipating demand and dynamically grouping users based on proximity and destination. With the use of chatbots, voice commands, and multilingual support, By facilitating smooth communication between drivers and passengers, this functionality improves the user experience, particularly in multicultural and multilingual settings.

title: RXK-VEM: A Novel Probabilistically Calibrated Hybrid Ensemble Model for Predicting Student Dropout in Higher Education
authors: Khaled Mahmud Sujon, Adnan Shafi, Md Monirul Islam Molla, Aminul Islam Miraz, Md Muzahidul Islam, Md. Tahmid Farabe Shehun
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Student dropout in higher education remains a critical global challenge, with significant implications for academic institutions and learners alike. While numerous machine learning models have been proposed for dropout prediction, many struggle with imbalanced datasets and poor generalization to minority classes—particularly the transitional "Enrolled" group. To address these limitations, we propose RXK-VEM, a novel hybrid ensemble model that combines Random Forest, XGBoost, and K-Nearest Neighbors, followed by probabilistic fusion and meta-level calibration using logistic regression. Evaluated on a real-world dataset from the UCI Machine Learning Repository, RXK-VEM outperformed traditional classifiers in key metrics such as F1 score, AUC-ROC, and MCC, especially improving recall for underrepresented classes. Our findings demonstrate that carefully designed ensemble strategies can mitigate individual model weaknesses and enhance predictive robustness in imbalanced, multiclass educational settings. The proposed approach has the potential to assist educational stakeholders in identifying at-risk students earlier and more accurately, enabling timely interventions and promoting student retention.

title: Comparative Analysis of Centralized and Federated Logistic Regression for Depression Detection Using Twitter and Reddit Data
authors: Yashvardhan Asthana
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Depression, a pervasive mental health disorder, increasingly manifests through linguistic patterns on social media platforms. This study presents a comparative evaluation of centralized and federated learning frameworks for depression detection, utilizing logistic regression on two curated textual datasets—Twitter and Reddit. After meticulous analysis, data preprocessing, and a stratified 4:1 train-test split, models were trained under both paradigms. Centralized learning leveraged pooled data, while federated learning across decentralized clients without transmitting raw data, thereby enhancing privacy preservation. Empirical results show that centralized models outperform federated counterparts across both platforms, particularly on the smaller and noisier Twitter dataset. However, federated learning achieves competitive accuracy on the Reddit dataset and offers a compelling alternative where data confidentiality is paramount. These findings highlight the trade-off between model performance and data confidentiality, reinforcing the potential of federated learning in scalable, privacy-aware mental health monitoring systems.

title: Price Surfer: A Dual-Platform E-Commerce Analytics Tool Using Real-Time Web Scraping and Fuzzy Matching
authors: Ramu Yadavalli, Ramachandra Rao Kurada, Srikanth Pala, Sunil Pattem, Majji Jaya Sri, Kolli Venkata Rushita
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: In today’s digital marketplace, consumers often face challenges when comparing product prices across multiple e-commerce platforms. Manual browsing between sites like Amazon and Flipkart can be time-consuming and inefficient. This paper presents Price Surfer, a modular web application that enables real-time product comparison through automated web scraping and intelligent product matching. The system employs Selenium for dynamic content extraction and and fuzzy matching techniques to identify similar products across platforms, despite naming inconsistencies. It allows users to filter results by brand, sort by price or relevance, and export data in CSV format for further analysis. Visual tools are integrated to support trend-based insights and decision-making. Designed with extensibility and ease of use in mind, Price Surfer offers a practical solution for both consumers and small-scale e-retailers seeking quick, data-driven purchasing decisions.

title: A Novel AI-Driven Environmental Assessment Model for Analyzing the Effectiveness of Rainwater Harvesting in Urban Area
authors: S. Gautham, Murugan Ramu, D. Vezhaventhan
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Rainwater harvesting represents an essential water conservation approach because it addresses the water-related problems faced by urban Chennai which cope with scarce resources and irregular rain patterns. RainImpact-AI functions as a computer-generated method for evaluating rainwater harvesting systems through hydrological simulations accompanied by machine learning and deep learning models. The model uses LSTM for rainfall prediction with 97.45% accuracy and CNN for rooftop rainwater structure identification reaching 98.34% accuracy and Random Forest models achieve 96.78% accuracy in groundwater recharge assessment. AI-enhanced SWAT and HEC-HMS tools combined in hybrid hydrological modeling produce reliable urban water flow and infiltration simulations. The RainImpact-AI system shows notable enhancements in groundwater recharge as it increases water levels by 42.5% across ten different urban zones. The AI-driven simulations for rainwater collection yield accurate results when compared to measured harvest volumes because they deviate only by 1.57% to 2.02% from the actual quantities. Policy evaluation through the study shows that AI-supported rainwater management approaches can reach 97.5% optimal efficiency in saving urban water supply under ideal implementation conditions. The combination of environmental modeling with AI at RainImpact-AI system generates data-based solutions to maximize RWH policies while supporting sustainability and stopping urban water scarcity. The model establishes itself as an adaptable decision-making tool which supports urban planning authorities and policy officials by delivering immediate predictive analysis solutions for better resource water management practices.

title: Enhancing Safety and Privacy in Autonomous Vehicle Networks through the Use of Blockchain and AI in Cybersecurity for Autonomous Systems
authors: Mohan Kumar Gajula, Akalanka Mailewa
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Autonomous vehicles (AVs) have reshaped the transportation industry, providing the potential for safer, more efficient, and greener mobility. However, the increasing sophistication of autonomous systems and their growing dependence on connected networks have made the safety and privacy of AVs a major challenge. A big user of MV with automated systems Tree, is susceptible to a variety of cyberattacks, such as: data leaks, system misconfiguration, or exploit of communication systems. In order to solve these issues, this study proposes some methods to improve the autonomous vehicle systems cybersecurity using Blockchain and AI technologies. By providing a secure data storage and transparent communication, blockchain's decentralized and immutable ledger system can guarantee data integrity, and AI can provide real-time threat detection and adaptive security controls. It explores ways to leverage the strengths of each technology to ensure the security of AV networks, including successful communication channels, basic data integrity, and detection and response to cyber threats in real-time. Utilization of Blockchain to create a secure and transparent system for the exchange of data between autonomous vehicles, infrastructure, and external networks enhances the integrity of communications by making them tamper-proof and traceable. ML-driven threat detection techniques can identify potential threats, and AI-driven security automation can adjust security parameters accordingly. It also investigates the incorporation of these technologies into autonomous vehicle systems for desired functionalities like secure vehicle-to-vehicle and vehicle-to-infrastructure communications, so that AVs can securely communicate with one another and with the infrastructure around it. This study intends to illustrate how combining Blockchain and AI can lead to a more resilient anti-cybersecurity construct for autonomous vehicle networks to lower the risks and improve their safety rollout on a broader scale.

title: Dynamic Graph Representation Spherical Convolutional Network with Fennec Fox Optimization for Iot Based Cyber Attack Detection
authors: Asok Kumar R, Krishnakumar K
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The rapid development of smart devices and a growing number of interconnected networks have led to a significant increase in the quantity and sophistication of cyberattacks in Internet of Things (IoT) systems. Conventional deep learning methods often struggle with high-dimensional data, lack flexibility, and have poor feature selection, which reduces their effectiveness in accurately detecting and classifying malicious traffic. In order to overcome these constraints,this study provide a novel approach for Cyber Attack detection in IoT environments using the BoT-IoT dataset: the Dynamic Graph Representation Spherical Convolutional Network with Fennec Fox Optimization (DGRSCNet-FFO) is proposed. The approach starts with data preparation using Min-Max Normalization, then Blood-Sucking Leech Optimizer (BSLO) for the feature selection, and last, the DGRSCNet model, which, for classification, combines the benefits of Spherical Convolutional Neural Networks (SCNN) and DRGAN. To further enhance training procedure and guarantee rapid convergence and consistent performance, Fennec Fox Optimization (FFO) is utilized. The proposed method has remarkable performance in experiments on the BoT-IoT dataset, with 99.85% accuracy and 99.62% precision, demonstrating its robustness and efficacy for Cyber Attack detection in complex IoT scenarios.

title: AI-Based Genomic Data Interpretation for Predicting Hereditary Disease Risks
authors: M. Moorthy, M. Vadivel, S. P. Vimal, V. Vijaya Baskar, A. Antony Luki, C Srinivasan
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: With the increasing development of genetic data, reliable interpretation techniques are required to predict hereditary disease risks. This paper presents an artificial neural network (ANN) technique for improving genetic data processing. Created a deep learning model trained on large genomic datasets, including variations associated with hereditary disorders and supplementary clinical data. The ANN successfully detected patterns and correlations in the data, outperforming standard analytical approaches in predicted accuracy. The results showed that risk classification for genetic disorders improved by more than 90% compared to current baseline models. Furthermore, the model revealed important genetic variations related to these risks. The inclusion of AI methods such as ANNs has the potential to revolutionize genetic data interpretation, allowing for more accurate risk assessments and new techniques for personalized treatment. Future research will concentrate on applying these results to clinical practice to enable targeted therapies and enhance patient outcomes.

title: Predicting Monkeypox Virus Epitopes Using Machine Learning: An in-Silico Approach
authors: Jugunta Prakash, Rohit Praveen Nair, Dinesh Somisetty, Neetu Srivastava
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Rapid mutation rates and human-to–human transmission of the zoonotic Orthopoxvirus monkeypox have become public health concerns. Given conventional approaches are time-consuming and expensive, the urgency of fast and efficient vaccine development has grown. Essential for vaccine development, B-cell epitopes in Monkeypox virus proteins are predicted in this work using computational methods combining machine learning and immunoinformatics. Epitope prediction was achieved using a balanced training dataset of peptide sequences from the Poxviridae family, processed with the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE), and a test dataset of 12,925 Monkeypox virus peptides with k-mer lengths of 4 to 14, a support vector machine (SVM) model.With an AUC value of 95.5/%, the SVM model proved to be really highly predictive. Out of the test dataset, 7,213 were categorized as non-epitopes and 5,712 were found as epitopes; the findings were kept in distinct Excel files for additional examination. This computer methodology speeds the selection of vaccination candidates by greatly lowering reliance on laboratory-based techniques. This work provides a necessary instrument for global health preparedness by reducing possible epitopes, therefore contributing to a scalable and efficient framework for vaccine development against Monkeypox and other emerging infections

title: 3D-CNN Approach for Spectral-Spatial Classification of Satellite Imagery
authors: Vijaylaxmi Kochari, Sanjeev S. Sannakki, Prakash K Sonwalkar, Lingaraj Hadimani, Pavan N Kunchur, Pavan K. Korlahalli
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This paper introduces a deep learning framework based on Three-Dimensional Convolutional Neural Networks (3D-CNN) for the classification of land cover types using multispectral satellite imagery. Focused on the Sundarbans region, the study utilizes Sentinel-2 data to build a spectral-spatial classification pipeline. Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is applied to reduce the spectral dimensionality, preserving key information across five components. Spatial context is captured using 15×15 image patches, which are then entered into a custom 3D-CNN architecture comprising volumetric and Two Dimensional (2D) convolutional layers followed by dense layers for multi-class classification.
