The dataset, containing over 85,000 samples, is split for training and testing, and the model is optimized using categorical cross-entropy with the Adam optimizer. Evaluation result shows an overall test accuracy of 96%, with F1-scores above 0.93 for most classes. The robust performance across six land cover categories stands validated by both a confusion matrix and classification map. TensorFlow, scikit-learn, and rasterio are used throughout the entire process to build a scalable remote sensing analysis system. Through this study we show that 3D-CNNs can successfully extract spectral and spatial features from satellite data and offer a practical solution for monitoring land use and environmental assessment.

title: Multimodal Emotion Recognition System
authors: Vijay Prakash Sharma, Shreyasi Ray, Siddhant Jaiswal, Shikha Sharma
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Emotion recognition is a vital component in enhancing human-computer interaction, especially for developing responsive and empathetic systems. In this paper, we present a Multimodal Emotion Recognition (MER) system that combines facial expressions and speech tone. By combining these two different modalities, the system effectively captures complementary emotional cues that a single source may not, thereby improving the robustness and accuracy of emotion detection systems. Facial expressions are analyzed using a CNN-based model, while MFCC features of audio files are processed and analyzed by CNN layers. Later, feature vectors from the two modalities are fused, allowing the system to collect complementary emotional information. Experimental results with FER2013 and multimodal speech datasets (CREMA-D, SAVEE, TESS, and RAVDESS) indicate that the validation accuracy of the multimodal representation to 69%, thus outperforming the unimodal models.

title: Smart Secure and Scalable Election System using Blockchain with Privacy Preservation and Anomaly Detection
authors: Madhavi Repe, Dr. Nilakshi Rajule, Vandana Katarwar, Ankita Bombatkar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: In response to the growing demand for secure and transparent digital elections, this paper presents a blockchain based Smart Election System that leverages advanced cryptographic and artificial intelligence techniques to ensure privacy, scalability, and verifiability. The proposed system integrates multi-factor authentication, Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs), smart contracts, and a sharded blockchain ledger to enable real-time, tamper-proof voting. Additionally, an Artificial intelligence based anomaly detection module monitors voting behaviour to flag suspicious patterns.Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the system achieves an average vote transaction latency of 1100ms with sharding, compared to 2800ms without it. The throughput increases from 130 to 220 votes/sec when sharding is enabled. ZKP integration ensures privacy at the cost of a moderate increase in validation time from 130ms to 230ms. The anomaly detection model, based on supervised learning, attained 92% precision, 88% recall, and an F1-score of 90%, ensuring proactive fraud detection. These results confirm the system’s effectiveness in delivering a smart scalable, private, and trustworthy e-voting platform suitable for national and institutional elections.

title: AI-Based Recommendation System for Weight Management Using User Feedback and Health Metrics
authors: Srinju.M, Dr.V.Dhanasekaran, S. Guruprasath, Dr.K.Edison Prabhu, K.J Godlin Debby, Dr.R.Senthilkumar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Proper weight control is a core component of preventive medicine, especially towards reducing the effects of obesity, metabolic syndrome and other non-communicable diseases. The current paper is a proposal of the creation of an interactive and AI-based recommendation system aimed at providing the personalization of the set calorie quotas and exercise routine based on the real-time measurements of physiological and behavioral indicators. The main inputs of the users, such as age, gender, BMI, level of activity, sleep quality, and indicators of stress, are processed into machine learning models that determine daily caloric intake and predict the weight changes in the near future. Random Forest and XGBoost regression models use K-Means grouping users into wellness categories including Average Wellness or High-Risk - Sedentary to make targeted interventions possible in comparison to other models. Moreover, a cooperative filtering system through K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) can be used to find peer profiles of similar nature that are recommended based on validated lifelong behavior-outcome patterns. One of the main characteristics of the system is the adaptive feedback loop: every week, the user provides inputs to the model, the model predictions are dynamically updated to help it progressively refine the calorie recommendations and exercise plans to ensure a better adherence and weight loss results. The system is executed by using a transformational Streamlit, which shows how scalable and feasible it is to combine predictive analytics with user-centered design to develop automated and tailored health management systems. The article emphasizes the opportunities of AI-based sites to facilitate the active health surveillance and customized behavioral coaching within the mass population.

title: Predicting Customer Churn in Financial Services Using Ensemble Machine Learning Models
authors: Anirudh Parupalli
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The early detection of client leave is critical for the survival of banks, making customer churn forecasting a long-standing problem in the banking industry. Predicting client attrition is challenging due to the data imbalance between the churn and no-churn classification groups. This study presents an ML-based approach to utilizing the Bank Customer Churn dataset to develop an accurate churn prediction model. Several classification models, such as Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), Random Forest (RF), and k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) were used, and their Hyperparameters were hyperparameter-optimized. An ensemble classifier using weighted soft voting was proposed by combining classifiers to achieve a performance of 99.8% accuracy and 99.49% F1-score. Models were evaluated using parameters like F1-score, accuracy, recall, and ROC-AUC. Experimental evidence demonstrates that the ensemble model significantly outperforms individual models and serves as a stable tool in proactive customer retention. Confusion matrices and ROC curves were among the visualizations that further confirmed each model's efficacy. Findings from this study suggest that ensemble learning may be a powerful data-driven tool for churn prediction, enabling financial institutions to identify high-risk customers and implement targeted retention strategies to reduce losses.

title: Fault Aware 3D Printing using LSTM based SENeT
authors: Lakshmaiya N
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The advanced additive manufacturing technique is the 3D printer, where the collaboration of physical systems and digital technologies is used in the production of 3D objects. The 3D printers are customizable and have the ability for rapid prototyping. Due to its ease of use, it finds application in various domains. The quality of the 3D object produced by the 3D printer should be high. The defects in the 3D objects are caused by faults in the 3D printers. Therefore, real-time monitoring and fault detection are necessary in 3D printing to enhance its efficiency. Based on this objective, the proposed work utilises an LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) based SENet (Squeeze-and-Excitation Neural Network) for classifying faults in 3D printer data. The simulation conducted for the proposed work yielded 96.2% efficiency, accompanied by favourable FPR and FNR values, using the 3D printing dataset from Kaggle.

title: Analysis of Chirality sensitive CNTFETs based on Carrier Transport
authors: Achsah Gladith N, T. J. Nagalakshmi
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This work investigates the output and conductance characteristics of Gate-All-Around (GAA) Carbon Nanotube Field Effect Transistors (CNTFETs) with chiralities (8,3), (7,5), and (10,0) under three transport regimes: Ballistic, Drift-Diffusion, and Ballistic Chain Non-Equilibrium. Simulations performed using Far from Equilibrium Transport in CNT/GNR FETs tool of nanoHUB reveals that ballistic transport yields superior current saturation and minimal channel resistance across all devices. The output and conductance plots provide comparative insights into carrier transport dynamics, emphasizing how scattering influences performance metrics. Input parameters are kept constant for all chirality. The device parameters though being specific to each chirality remain uniform across transport models of each chirality. Results obtained reinforce the importance of transport modelling in optimizing CNTFET behavior for nanoscale device applications.

title: Stock Price Prediction Using Real World Datasets
authors: Nageswara Rao Gundala, Karthesha Tata, Vasantha Lakshmi Lotla, Dasaradha Rama Bharadwaj Murikipudi, P Gopala Krishna, Swapna Raghunath
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The stock market prediction problem is one of the most difficult forecasting issues faced by people. The problem is difficult because stock markets are influenced by other factors over and above quantitative market indicators. This study proposes a complete hybrid model that combines numerical time-series analysis and textual sentiment analysis for improved accuracy in stock price prediction. We integrate both classical technical indicators and natural processing techniques. These special latest news techniques for predicting financial price movements enable a better prediction. The research used an extensive number of data points from the past decades from eight large companies, the tech giants which are Apple, Google, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, Netflix, Nvidia and the large IT services firm Tata Consultancy Services. A multi-tiered analytical framework on various machine learning models including Naive Bayes, MLP, logistic regression, and random forest algorithms is used. The Numerical Analysis part processes the opening, high, low, and closing prices, as well as the trade volume, RSI, Moving Average, etc. The textual analysis examines financial news headlines and reports by extracting sentiment score using NLTK and spaCy. These scores provide important indicators of market sentiment, complementing your quantitative analysis. In our approach, we found that our hybrid model beat the performance of standard numerical-only models. It also improved prediction accuracy for all securities tested. The system architecture consists of an Influx DB, Grafana dashboard, Streamlit App, and Flask App. And it all controlled by request-response. The experimental findings substantiate that the combined model enhances prediction capability, particularly during periods of market volatility. This research illustrates the advantages of leveraging multiple data modalities in the area of computational finance and combines what were previously two separate streams of research.

title: Using MobileNetV2 for Real-World Applications: Effective Animal Image Classification
authors: Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Raghavendra Sridhar, Jatin Sharma, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Deepak Banerjee, Gaurav Sharma
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Research work identically on animal image classification because it enables important applications for wildlife observation and agricultural production and security control systems. The paper proposes an efficient framework based on MobileNetV2 architecture to identify dog, horse, elephant, butterfly, chicken, cat, cow, sheep, spider, and squirrel species. The Kaggle platform provides 26,179 animal images which are distributed into ten distinct classes. This method comprises four distinct phases which start with data preparation after which comes preprocessing and then model development prior to performance assessment. MobileNetV2 operates as an advanced model designed for mobile and embedded systems through its light-weight architecture which maintains both accuracy performance and minimized computational requirements. Performance evaluation of the model depends on a classification report alongside a confusion matrix and an assessment of training and validation accuracy. The model achieved 95% total accuracy accompanied by very good precision and recall and F1 scores for every classification category. The model shows excellent robustness because the confusion matrix demonstrates only small numbers of incorrect classifications. The system implementation shows that MobileNetV2 functions efficiently for precise animal image classification making it suitable for animal conservation work and livestock management and commercial automation tasks.

title: Combating Misinformation: A Comparative Evaluation of Zero-Shot Models for Fake News Detection
authors: Sajin Mahmud Arpon, Md Ariful Islam, Mir Md Mofakkar Hossain, Md Saef Ullah Miah
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The number of fake content spread on the Internet platforms in rapid succession has led to the importance of the detection of fake news. One practical step is zero-shot classification with pre-trained language models that do not need further fine-tuning. In this paper, four transformer models DeBERTa, BART, ModernBERT, and CE-DeBERTa are evaluated on a smaller variant of the Kaggle Fake News dataset licensed under CC BY-4.0. Majority Voting and Soft Voting (with 0.85 and 0.92 thresholds) are also being used as ensemble learning methods to improve reliability. The highest accuracy (76.91%), the F1 score (72.64%), and the highest degree of uncertainty (59.40) were found in DeBERTa. However, ModernBERT showed less performance (F1 score: 62.40%) with the lowest uncertainty (31.09%). DeBERTa achieved the highest score of 0.6939 based on the composite performance score. The highest accuracy (70.23%) and the highest uncertainty (31.99%) were obtained with Soft Voting with a threshold of 0.92 among the ensemble methods. But Majority Voting turned out to have the strongest overall performance score (0.7089). In general, DeBERTa and the Majority Voting ensemble are rather successful in terms of trustworthy fake news identification.

title: Liquid Neural Networks for Autonomous Driving: A Framework for Intelligent Decision-Making
authors: Yashaswini Suresh, Saman Mallikarjun S, Vismaya Devashya, Harshitha G M
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Real-world environments in countries with unorganized traffic systems, limit the development of scalable, smart, and affordable self-driving solutions. This paper introduces a flexible self-driving system that uses Liquid Neural Networks (LNNs) to make quick decisions in tricky and changing traffic situations focusing on Indian roads. The system combines data from multiple sensors-LiDAR single-camera vision, and motion sensors-through an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) to improve awareness and understanding of the surroundings. It uses a Dynamic Window Approach (DWA) to plan routes and avoid obstacles allowing smooth movement in tight city spaces. Built on a edge-optimized platform using NVIDIA Jetson Xavier NX, the system processes information while using less power. Tests show that LNNs adapt and respond much better than regular neural models in unclear and blocked situations. The system's flexibility low cost, and open-source design make it a practical option for research, learning, and early use of self-driving solutions in different places. This work supports the bigger goals of smart transportation and helps make self-driving technology available to more people.

title: StackTrace-AI: Identifying Generative AI Text Origins using Ensemble Learning
authors: Pabon Shaha, Md. Sabbir Hossen, Md. Ibrahim Hosen Sojib, Sanjida Akter, Md. Saiduzzaman, Mostofa Kamal Nasir
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Generative AI models capable of producing human-like text, images, and other content are rapidly transforming the digital landscape. As their use becomes increasingly widespread across domains such as education, media, and communication, their outputs are shaping public discourse, influencing opinions, and even spreading misinformation. In this context, accurately identifying the source of AI-generated text is becoming increasingly crucial for ensuring transparency and accountability, protecting information integrity, mitigating deception, and fostering public trust in digital content. This study presents a source attribution framework designed to distinguish between texts generated by two leading large language models: ChatGPT and Gemini. A dataset of 2,786 AI-generated samples was constructed for this purpose. We employed transformer-based feature extraction techniques, including BERT, ALBERT, and RoBERTa, as well as a Hybrid model (AR-BERT, which combines ALBERT and RoBERTa), to capture distinctive linguistic signatures. This was followed by classification using nine traditional and ensemble machine learning algorithms. Among these, the AR-BERT-based features combined with a stacking ensemble classifier, StackTrace-AI, achieved the highest accuracy of 84.44%, surpassing all other combinations. The findings underscore the viability of transformer-driven source detection and offer valuable insights for advancing AI accountability, generative content auditing, and responsible AI governance.

title: Development of Knee Osteoarthritis Detection and Severity Prediction Using CNN Algorithm Based Deep Learning
authors: Veeraiah M, Sandhya M, Sanjeeva Chaithanya B, Sowjanya K
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Degenerative joint disease of the knee, also known as knee osteoarthritis (KOA), is typically brought on by articular cartilage that gradually loses its structure as a result of tension and use. Osteoarthritis (OA), the most common type of arthritis, causes considerable disability for sufferers worldwide, particularly for those with knee OA. One common degenerative musculoskeletal disorder is osteoarthritis (OA). Approximately 5 percent of people worldwide suffer from this ailment. The knee is most frequently affected by arthritis (OA), where the articular cartilage at the ends of bones degrades irreversibly. Knee osteoarthritis (OA) is a disorder that deteriorates with time and impacts the entire knee joint. Manual diagnosis, segmentation, and annotation of knee joints are still used in clinical practice to diagnose osteoarthritis, despite their time-consuming nature and sensitivity to user variables. In order to overcome the drawbacks of the traditional deep learning approach and increase the efficiency of clinical workflow, we created the proposed system employing the CNN model. However, when it comes to predicting the course of OA and identifying early changes, OA poses a clinically significant obstacle. While new quantitative methods can enhance imaging-based diagnostic methods, OA progression prediction and early change detection provide a clinically meaningful obstacle. In this study, we provide a novel Deep Learning (DL)-based approach (CNN) for predicting the progression of OA based on knee MRI images. Our suggested CNN model's remarkable 96% accuracy was obtained through validation on a sizable and varied dataset. This high accuracy demonstrates how well the model can differentiate between different severity levels of osteoarthritis in the knee and correctly identify patients as benign or malignant. The encouraging outcomes point to the suggested model's possible therapeutic use as a tool for early diagnosis and prognosis, allowing for prompt patient interventions.

title: GITCON 2025 Opinion
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)

title: Network Intrusion Detection System Based on Enhanced Dual Gaussian Mixture Variational Autoencoders for Internet of Vehicles
authors: Shizhao Tian, Yaowen Zheng, Laile Xi, Shenghao Lin, Yubo Li, Hongsong Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The Internet of Vehicles (IoV) is highly vulnerable to attacks due to its open communication environment, with new types of attacks continuously emerging. However, existing Network Intrusion Detection Systems (NIDS) often fall short in accuracy, recall rates, false positive rates, and they frequently prove ineffective in detecting new attacks. In this paper, we propose a NIDS for IoV based on Enhanced Dual Gaussian Mixture Variational Autoencoders (EDGMVAE) to detect various attack behaviors, including new attacks. We employ two separate Gaussian Mixture Variational Autoencoders (GMVAE) to conduct unsupervised reconstruction training on normal traffic and known attack traffic. During detection, we obtain the tested traffic’s reconstruction probabilities using these GMVAEs and determine whether an attack has occurred through logical fusion operation. We use two datasets for evaluation, i.e.,the Car Hacking Dataset (CHD) for in-vehicle communication and the UNSW-NB15 Dataset (UBD) for external network communication. The results demonstrate that our method achieves remarkable performance in detecting known attacks, with an accuracy rate of 98.54% on the CHD and 98.59% on the UBD. Moreover, it outperforms other state-of-the-art methods in detecting new attacks. Specifically, on the CHD, the accuracy of the method is 6.66% to 12.83% higher than other methods, while on the UBD, the accuracy is 5.13% to 21.91% higher than other methods.

title: A Two-Stage Co-Evolutionary Algorithm Enhanced by Reinforcement Learning for Efficient Aircraft Test Task Allocation
authors: Qiu Fang, Huiyou Zhu, Yi Mi, Hong Lu, Hao Song, Yaonan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Efficient long-term flight test task allocation is crucial for aircraft development, yet it poses significant challenges due to the complex, extended timelines and numerous interdependent tasks. Current methods often rely on expert judgment, leading to inefficiencies, delays, and suboptimal planning. This paper addresses these challenges by introducing a reinforcement learning-enhanced two-stage evolutionary algorithm. The algorithm incorporates a mixed-integer nonlinear programming model aimed at minimizing the maximum flight test duration while adhering to multiple constraints. Key innovations include a two-stage evolutionary framework with optimized encoding schemes and crossover operators, and dynamic adjustment of crossover and mutation probabilities using Q-learning. These features enable the algorithm to handle the complexity and unpredictability of long-term flight test planning. Extensive experiments validate the approach, demonstrating its ability to generate high-quality, adaptive flight test plans in real-world, complex scenarios.

title: Distributed Constrained Tracking Control of Continuous-Time Multi-Train Systems With Self-Adjusting Zones and Delays
authors: Jiahao Xu, Mengxiang Zeng, Peng Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In practical multi-train systems, it is not necessary for the desired relative distances between adjacent trains to remain fixed. Instead, trains only need to converge within specific zones, where they can adjust their own velocity without coordination. This paper investigates distributed tracking control for multi-train systems considering communication delays, self-adjusting zones, and constraints on velocity and input simultaneously. A distributed algorithm is proposed to drive each train into its self-adjusting zone while tracking the desired velocity. It is shown that collision avoidance and velocity constraints can be achieved through velocity damping feedback. The research methods adopted in this paper mainly include multiple model transformations and convex analysis. By analyzing the equivalent system, it is demonstrated that tracking control is achievable for multi-train systems with constraints, continuous-time dynamics, communication delays, and self-adjusting zones. Finally, numerical simulations are conducted to verify the effectiveness of the theoretical findings.

title: Towards Instance-Aware Swarm Intelligence for TSP via Distance Uniqueness
authors: Baker Abdalhaq, Ahmed Awad, Amjad Hawash
abstract: The Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP) is a classical Nondeterministic Polynomial time hard (NP-hard) combinatorial optimization challenge that seeks the shortest Hamiltonian cycle in a weighted graph. Swarm intelligence algorithms have been utilized intensively in solving TSP instances of varying sizes. However, the effectiveness of a swarm algorithm can be affected by the structural characteristics of the TSP instance being solved. Furthermore, there is no superior swarm algorithm that provably outperforms all others on all TSP instances. Consequently, there is a need to build a computationally efficient Algorithm Selector (AS) that automatically selects the proper swarm algorithm to be applied per instance. This paper deeply investigates the effectiveness of various swarm algorithms in solving TSP instances based on the structural characteristics of the TSP instance type. To capture this, we propose a comprehensive framework that initially builds a portfolio of efficient swarm algorithms to be utilized in solving the TSP. Equipped with an easily computable feature, namely the uniqueness factor, we introduce a classification scheme that divides TSP instances into “drilling” and “cities” categories. With intensive analysis on various TSP instances, the AS is built to further select the appropriate swarm algorithm to be applied per instance with a simple threshold-based approach.

title: SCOPE: A Transformer-based framework for analyzing sentiment of Chinese public toward delayed retirement
authors: Wanfeng Ding, Hanif Bhuiyan, Md Khalid Hossain
abstract: Individual sentiments and attitudes are the key to shaping the public’s understanding of policy changes. Regarding China’s delayed retirement policy, introduced in 2024, this study utilizes public posts on Weibo and proposes a data-driven analysis framework, SCOPE (Social media Comment-based Opinion and Policy Evaluation). The framework consists of four components: sentiment analysis, topical driver analysis, geographic analysis, and user-level analysis. Based on the interactive structure of social media, the sentiment analysis component proposed a thread-aware architecture, HCIN (Hierarchical Contextual Interaction Network), which models parent–child comment interactions via dual-path encoding and explicit cross-attention with bilinear fusion. HCIN outperforms strong baselines on the annotated dataset. To complement this, the study employs sentiment-specific topic modeling, geographic analysis using the entropy weight method, and user-level analysis through principal component analysis. The results show that most users express negative or neutral attitudes toward the policy. Negative sentiment is primarily driven by perceived injustice and concerns over pension security. Moreover, emotions toward the policy change vary significantly across regions, with individual users more inclined to voice negative attitudes. This study contributes a new hybrid modelling architecture for capturing the interactive structures of social media, provides actionable insights into public emotional responses to retirement reform, and establishes a reusable analytical framework for future public opinion research.

title: Inverse Design of Dielectric Superstrates Based on PINNs for High-Gain Antenna
authors: Junjie Shao, Mengyao Jia, Ren Wang, Bing-Zhong Wang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This work presents an inverse-design method for high-gain dielectric superstrates, integrating the reciprocity principle with physics-informed neural networks (PINNs). The method optimizes the dielectric distribution within a finite design region under the constraint of the wave equation, enabling the incident wave to form a localized field enhancement at a prescribed focal point. A straight-through discriminator layer is incorporated into the structure network, providing a binary representation of dielectric units and significantly reducing the extensive parameter sweeping and full-wave simulations required in conventional phase-compensated superstrate design. Upon completion of the inverse design, placing a radiating antenna at the focal point with polarization aligned to the designed wavefront yields a significant increase in main-beam gain. Simulation results show a maximum main-beam gain enhancement of up to 5.97 dB while maintaining the original operating bandwidth. Experimental fabrication and measurements further confirm improved antenna gain across the operating band after introducing the dielectric superstrate, with a maximum measured gain of 12.75 dBi.

title: A ML-Driven Study on Feature Descriptors and Mechanisms for Adsorption of Air Discharge Decomposition Components_supp1-3663510.docx
authors: Zhe Li
abstract: Insulation materials in air switchgear may degrade due to partial discharge (PD). Characteristic gases generated by PD (such as CO, NO, and NO₂) can be detected by gas sensors. However, existing gas-sensitive material screening relies on trial-and-error methods or costly density functional theory (DFT) calculations. Machine learning (ML)-driven DFT calculations have emerged as a new paradigm in gas-sensitive material research. This approach significantly accelerates the rational design of high-performance gas-sensitive materials by efficiently predicting adsorption behavior and revealing underlying microscopic mechanisms. This study screened 14 key features from 200 sets of adsorption data across 50 literature sources (2018–2024), then compared and optimized six ML models (XGBoost, GBR, etc.) to evaluate the predictive performance of D and E<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ad</sub>. Subsequently, SHAP and PDPs analyses were conducted to quantify feature contributions and identify core factors. Research findings indicate that XGBoost achieved prediction R² values of 91.55% for adsorption distance and 89.79% for adsorption energy, significantly outperforming other models (GBR ranked second with R² values of 89.79% and 83.56%, respectively). In terms of generalization ability, XGBoost outperforms GBR in extreme value usage and feature balancing utilization, which is overly dependent on substrate material features. Charge transfer (Qₜ) and dopant atom density (ρ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</sub>) are the core factors affecting the adsorption behavior, and the SHAP analysis shows that Qₜ has the highest contribution to the model output. Substrate and dopant characteristics contribute equally in the XGBoost model (∼45% each), while the GBR model is overly dependent on substrate characteristics (>60%).

title: Enhancing Autonomous Vehicle Localization through Cooperative LiDAR and Smart Infrastructure Integration
authors: Yuze Jiang, Ehsan Javanmardi, Manabu Tsukada, Hiroshi Esaki
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Autonomous driving systems rely on precise localization to ensure safety and performance in dynamic environments. However, standalone vehicle localization methods using onboard sensors often fail to deliver sufficient accuracy in adverse environments, such as tunnels or areas with limited distinguishable features. To address these challenges, we propose a novel cooperative localization framework that leverages roadside LiDAR-equipped infrastructure and vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I) communication. In our approach, roadside LiDARs detect and estimate vehicle positions using a refined L-shape fitting algorithm, complemented by the vehicle’s geometric data shared over the V2I network. This method mitigates errors associated with partial point cloud data and improves localization precision significantly. By integrating this infrastructure-based positioning data with onboard localization modules via sensor fusion, our framework enhances overall accuracy and robustness in real-time autonomous driving scenarios. Experimental results in a digital twin environment demonstrate that our system achieves over 70% improvement in localization accuracy compared to self-localization methods. Our work highlights the potential of smart infrastructure to enhance localization, reduce onboard sensor dependency, and improve autonomous driving reliability under challenging environment for self-localization.

title: Automated Mapping of Supraglacial Stream Networks on the Greenland Ice Sheet Using Dual U-Net Convolutional Neural Networks_supp1-3663855.docx

title: Accelerating Modern Database Systems by Leveraging Computational Storage and Cache-Coherent Interconnects
authors: S. Tamimi, A. Bernhardt, I. Petrov, A. Koch
abstract: Near-Data Processing (NDP) offers a promising approach to improve the performance of modern databases with hybrid workloads comprising long-running analytical and frequent short update transactions. This paper introduces an end-to-end NDP-capable system that integrates a computational storage device (CSD) with a database capable of offloading queries to storage while handling concurrent queries on the host. A novel host-device protocol combines high-throughput PCIe-DMA with low-latency messaging via cache-coherent interconnects (CCI) such as CXL or CCIX. The paper presents two systems, DANSEN and CINDA, each featuring a custom embedded NDP engine. DANSEN offloads read-only queries while ensuring consistency with concurrent host updates. CINDA extends this capability to update queries through a low-latency synchronization mechanism and a CCI-enabled communication protocol.

title: Predicting Potential Drug Candidates Using Machine Learning Models And Regression Analysis
authors: Syed Sadman Salahuddin, Ridwan Hamid Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Predicting molecular binding affinity via in silico screening offers a rapid, cost-effective complement to traditional high-throughput docking. In this work, we present a fully reproducible machine-learning pipeline trained on a unified dataset of 637,292 compounds, using docking scores as proxies for protein–ligand binding strength. Through numeric filtering, missing-value imputation, and a target-wise 25th-percentile preselection, we curate a high-affinity subset of approximately 50,000 molecules characterized by 23 standardized physicochemical and topological descriptors.Six regression algorithms—Linear Regression, Support Vector Regression, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, and a Multilayer Perceptron—are optimized via randomized hyperparameter search with five-fold cross-validation. On an unseen test set, XGBoost achieves the best balance between accuracy (MSE = 184.6; RMSE = 13.59) and explanatory power (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.106), with Random Forest and Gradient Boosting yielding comparable performance.While the absolute R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values appear modest, they reflect the inherent challenge of predicting binding affinity from simple 2D descriptors alone. Importantly, our models achieve strong ranking performance (Spearman correlation up to 0.42) and significant enrichment of true binders in top predictions, which is more relevant for virtual screening applications.Finally, post-hoc SHAP analysis reveals the most influential molecular descriptors driving model predictions and suggests pathways for descriptor enrichment. Our contributions include: (1) the first head-to-head benchmark of six diverse regression methods on a 600K-compound docking dataset; (2) an automated quantile-based filtering strategy to isolate top binders; and (3) model interpretability via SHAP to inform future feature development.

title: Cloud Service Models and Execution Architectures: A Unified Survey of IaaS to Serverless Computing
authors: Sesha Sai Sravanthi Valiveti
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Cloud computing has become the foundation of modern IT infrastructure, providing scalable, flexible, and cost-effective solutions for application deployment and management. As organizations adopt cloud technologies, understanding different service models and execution architectures is critical. This paper presents a unified survey of cloud service models-from Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) to Serverless Computing—highlighting their evolution and key features. The study explores core execution architectures including Virtual Machines (VMs), containers, microservices, and serverless platforms, focusing on how these models enable scalable and efficient application delivery. It discusses the role of hypervisors in managing VMs, along with orchestration tools such as OpenStack and AWS EC2 Auto Scaling for automating virtual infrastructure. Containerization technologies like Docker and Podman are analyzed for their lightweight design and portability, while orchestration tools like Kubernetes and Docker Swarm are highlighted for their role in automating complex deployments. A comparative analysis between VMs and containers reveals differences in performance, startup time, resource usage, and isolation. The paper also examines serverless computing (Function-as-a-Service), which abstracts away infrastructure management and enables event-driven function execution. Benefits such as automatic scaling and reduced operational overhead are balanced against limitations like cold start delays, vendor lock-in, and limited execution control. This survey provides a comprehensive overview of cloud service models and their supporting architectures, serving as a guide for practitioners and researchers to choose the most appropriate cloud execution model based on application requirements and operational goals.

title: A Hybrid Approach towards Multi-Label Semi-Supervised Emotion Classification
authors: Jannatul Ferdous Muna, Abier Farzana Hoque, Rahul Das, Mehrin Anannya, Rashed Mazumdar, MD Biplob Hosen
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Multi-label classification of textual emotion is a significant task in NLP(Natural Language Processing) that allows the tagging of a given text with multiple relevant labels and compared to single-label classification, which can oversimplify the expression of a statement. Traditional techniques of supervised learning require large amounts of labeled data, which are very expensive and time-consuming to gather or prepare. This paper explores this problem and how it can handle limited labeled, raw, imbalanced data using semi-supervised learning for the English language. For this work, a dataset of 13 emotions inspired by social networks is custom compiled from comments on YouTube Shorts, to reflect real-world emotional expressions. This research introduces semi-supervised learning that unifies consistency regularization and pseudo-labeling. Initially, it incorporates supervised Forward Feed Neural Network (FNN), a simple and lightweight classifier followed by a small amount (0.1%) of unlabeled data using a Gaussian Mixture Model(GMM), an unsupervised learning model. This approach achieves a high accuracy of about 96.21%, portraying the potential impact of semi-supervised methods toward the improvement of multi-label emotion classification with fewer labeled datasets required. For real-world applications like sentiment analysis, mental health monitoring, customer review analysis, and human-computer interaction can benefit from this approach for it’s ability to reduce dependence on large-scale labeled data and still maintain high accuracy.

title: Evolutionary Optimization of Hybrid Neuro-Fuzzy Controllers for Load Frequency Control in Multi-Area Systems
authors: V V Appalanaidu Menda, D. Vijaya Kumar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This study explores how to effectively evolve hybrid neuro-fuzzy controllers for load frequency control (LFC) in multi-area power systems. A new framework integrating a neural network trained under a supervised approach with a tuned fuzzy inference system is considered using a hybrid evolutionary algorithm (EA) combining genetic algorithms (GA) and particle swarm optimization (PSO). The purpose of the hybrid controller being to enable better dynamic performance, robustness against load disturbances, and damping of inter-area oscillations. The details surrounding the modeling of a two-area thermal power system is described first, followed by details of the controller design and tuning. Time-domain simulations of a two-area thermal power system under several scenarios of loading showed that with the proposed approach and design process, reductions in settling time, frequency deviation and oscillations in the tie-line power occurred, with both GA and PSO only tuned controllers achieving dynamic performance officially. Statistical analysis of the proposed approach and design process achieved the maximum robustness to the parameter uncertainties. The conclusions of this work highlight the potential of employing an evolutionary tuned hybrid neuro-fuzzy controller with their strict requirements of performance in modern interconnected grids. Future work will seek to extend the proposed approach and design process to include real-time implementation and simulation of larger interconnected power systems.

title: Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving AI Model Training
authors: Ajmeera Kiran, M. Padmavathi, A Srilatha, Shaikh Mohamad, Yeluri Lakshmi Prasanna, P. Chinnasamy
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has been used variably in healthcare diagnostics thereby demonstrating promising results especially in the way diagnosis is done. However, one of the strategies that appear to hinder its mass implementation is the lack of traceability of the process leading to the generation of the result through the AI models, thus creating a trust deficit between the medical practitioners and AI systems. In this paper, I discuss Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) with an aim disentangling the diagnostic domain of the healthcare system to improve the performance of AI models while making them more explainable. We overview existing methods in the field of XAI, the use of these techniques in the healthcare system and the need to develop trust between AI systems and healthcare workers. In addition, we outline recommendations for how to close this trust divide, which includes both understandable instructions and the benefits of the implementation of XAI in rendering health care services.

title: MAGRes-UNET Multi Attention Gated Residual for Medical Image Segmentation
authors: V. Pavan Sai Srinivas, T. Pavan Maanaj, K. Saketh Reddy, B. Lakshmi Prasanna
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Accurate medical image segmentation is critical for disease diagnosis, treatment planning, and monitoring in clinical practice. However, traditional models like U-Net face limitations when dealing with low contrast, noise, and irregular lesion shapes in modalities such as MRI and CT. This paper presents MAGRes-UNet, an enhanced architecture that integrates Multi Attention Gates (MAG) and Residual Connections into the UNet framework. The MAG module improves focus on relevant anatomical structures, while residual connections ensure efficient gradient flow and stable training. Evaluated on benchmark datasets—BraTS for brain tumors and HAM10000/ISIC for skin lesions—MAGRes-UNet achieves high segmentation performance, including a Dice Similarity Coefficient of 91.21 (brain) and 92 (skin), and IoU scores of 92.90 and 94.81, respectively. These results highlight its robustness across various imaging tasks and modalities, making it a scalable solution for clinical applications in radiology, oncology, and telemedicine.

title: Enhancing MMDiT-Based Text-to-Image Models for Similar Subject Generation_supp1-3663759.pdf
authors: Xingang Pan
abstract: Representing the cutting-edge technique of text-to-image models, the latest Multimodal Diffusion Transformer (MMDiT) largely mitigates many generation issues existing in previous models. However, we discover that it still suffers from subject neglect or mixing when the input text prompt contains multiple subjects of similar semantics or appearance. We identify three possible ambiguities within the MMDiT architecture that cause this problem: Inter-block Ambiguity, Text Encoder Ambiguity, and Semantic Ambiguity. To address these issues, we propose to repair the ambiguous latent on-the-fly by test-time optimization at early denoising steps. In detail, we design three loss functions: Block Alignment Loss, Text Encoder Alignment Loss, and Overlap Loss, each tailored to mitigate these ambiguities. Despite significant improvements, we observe that semantic ambiguity persists when generating multiple similar subjects, as the guidance provided by overlap loss is not explicit enough. Therefore, we further propose Overlap Online Detection and Back-to-Start Sampling Strategy to alleviate the problem. Experimental results on a newly constructed challenging dataset of similar subjects validate the effectiveness of our approach, showing superior generation quality and much higher success rates over existing methods. The consistent and substantial improvements observed across multiple MMDiT based text-to-image models such as SD3, SD3.5 and FLUX provide strong evidence of the general applicability of our method. Project page: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://wtybest.github.io/projects/EnMMDiT/</uri>

title: Knot resonator in U-shaped microfiber with multi-effect for seawater temperature and pressure dual-parameter measurement
authors: Feng Xia, Bo-wen Zheng, Shuang Yang, Yuan-lin Li, Jing Gao, Bo Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: A probe-type microfiber resonator with multi-effect is proposed and its temperature-pressure dual-parameter sensing characteristics are investigated. The sensing probe consists of a microfiber knot resonator (MKR) and an adiabatic U-shaped taper. The sensing probe, which is called U-shaped MKR for short, can excite two different spectra which superimpose with each other in the output end. One part of the superimposed output spectrum is the interference spectrum, which results from mode interference in the U-shaped adiabatic fiber taper. The other part of the superimposed output spectrum is Vernier spectrum, which results from resonance of various modes within the knot resonator. The experimental tests on the temperature and pressure sensing performance show that the pressure sensitivities of the interference spectrum and Vernier envelope are as high as -0.19 nm/N and -0.44 nm/N, respectively, when pressure is between 20 N and 150 N. The maximum pressure sensitivity is converted to be around -58.69 nm/MPa. The minimum resolvable seawater depth is calculated to be only 3.4 cm. Temperature sensitivities of the two spectra are up to -5.33 nm/°C and -6.30 nm/°C, respectively, in the temperature range of 18°C to 50°C. Concurrent demodulation of temperature and pressure can be realized via a demodulation matrix which is constructed by sensitivities of both interference and Vernier spectra. The sensor exhibits excellent hysteresis, repeatability, and long-term stability, which has certain research significance for developing compact, robust and highly sensitive two-parameter sensors.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: Vector Control of PMSM Based on Misaligned Hall-Effect Sensors
authors: Felipe Cardoso Pimenta, Eisenhawer De M. Fernandes, Rute Cartaxo Lima, Felype De L. Silva
abstract: Hall-effect sensors represent a low-cost and widely employed alternative for estimating position and speed in permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs). However, installation errors, such as angular misalignments between the sensors and the rotor, can introduce phase shifts and signal distortions, significantly compromising measurement accuracy. To mitigate these effects, this work proposes a method that converts the raw signals from the Hall-effect sensors into a Hall vector through the Clarke transformation and applies the adaptive NAF filter to extract the fundamental components of the signal. For validation, the performance of the proposed method is compared with that of the Adaptive Vectorial Filter (AVF), which is widely used in similar applications. The proposed approach is integrated into a synchronization structure based on the Improved Phase-Locked Loop (IPLL), enabling a more stable and accurate estimation of rotor position and speed. The results obtained from both simulations and practical experiments demonstrate that the proposed method exhibits greater robustness to mechanical misalignments, as well as significant improvements in terms of accuracy and dynamic response.

title: Solder Paste Segmentation Method Based on RGB and 3-D Height Modality Feature Fusion
authors: Yimin Wang, Xianqiang Yang, Chenhao Yuan, Hao Sun, Xinghu Yu, Juan J. Rodriguez-Andina
abstract: In surface mount technology, solder paste printing quality critically affects product reliability. Accurate segmentation of solder paste regions is essential for defect detection, quantitative analysis, and process optimization. Traditional threshold-based methods lack robustness under varying surface textures and lighting, while deep learning approaches require large annotated datasets and expensive hardware, limiting their use in cost-sensitive manufacturing. We propose a fast, annotation-free segmentation framework based on parameteric multimodal learning, integrating RGB color with 3-D height data. Height priors generate an initial mask, followed by a lookup table–based parameteric color model that adapts to different printed circuit board types and batches. A convolutional feature fusion operator then constructs a joint height–color probability space, suppressing interference from substrate variations and uneven illumination, yielding a refined probability map for final segmentation. Tests on a 3D-solder paste inspection industrial dataset achieve 96.0% mean intersection over union and 98.8% pixel accuracy, matching state-of-the-art deep learning performance while greatly improving efficiency and suitability for real-world deployment without annotated data.

title: Low-Bitwidth Generalized Feed-Forward Neural Network Digital Predistortion Based on Joint Optimization of Pruning and Quantization
authors: Zehao Chen, Cuiping Yu, Jiantao Huang, Hao Li, Xunnan Zhang, Botao Mu, Yuanan Liu
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a novel double-stage quantization technique, jointly optimized with two pruning strategies, for lightweight generalized feed-forward neural network (GFNN)-based digital predistortion of 6G wideband signals. The method effectively reduces deep network size and converts floating-point (FP) operations into integer ones, which achieves substantial reductions in parameter count and memory footprint (MF). The proposed double-stage quantization adaptively adjusts quantization thresholds per training batch, improving overall quantization precision. It successfully lowers the bitwidths of activations/weights while maintaining a normalized root-mean-square error (NMSE) close to the full-precision baseline. Measurements using 200-MHz wideband signals validate that a 74% reduction in MF compared to the augmented real-valued time-delay neural network (ARVTDNN) model, without noticeable degradation in linearization performance.

title: Design and Implementation of an Anthropomorphic Robotic Hand with Key Kinematic Properties of Human Hand_supp2-3663977.mp4
authors: Caihua Xiong
abstract: Designing an anthropomorphic robotic hand with few actuators while replicating the dexterous motion capabilities of the human hand remains a challenging problem. In this work, a kinematic synergy analysis method is proposed to identify joints with high motion independence and to quantify the synergistic motion characteristics of joints with strong motion dependence. Based on these findings, design principles for intra finger and inter-finger coupling–compliance mechanisms are developed, enabling the mechanical embodiment of joint kinematic synergies within and between fingers. Furthermore, a design principle for anthropomorphic robotic hands that reduces actuator count while retaining diverse motion functions is formulated. This principle is embodied in a 12-actuator, 21-joint prototype named SX-Hand, featuring a flexible thumb and an articulated palm. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that SX Hand achieves the maximum score in the Kapandji test, performs all 33 grasp types defined in the comprehensive GRASP taxonomy, and executes dexterous in-hand manipulations. The proposed approach provides an effective conceptual and technical route for reproducing rich motion functions with reduced actuation, offering valuable guidance for the development of efficient anthropomorphic robotic hands and other bionic systems.

title: Dual-Layer 5 × 6 SIW Butler Matrix with Minimized Components for Multibeam Application
authors: Ziyu Xu, Yizhu Shen, Song Xue, Sanming Hu
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: Conventional Butler matrix based multibeam antennas often face challenges of excluding a broadside beam, numerous components and large sizes. To address the problems, in this letter, a compact dual-layer 5 × 6 Butler matrix utilizing three-way coupler in substrate integrated waveguide (SIW) technology is proposed, which avoids the utilization of crossovers, comprising only 7 basic components. Notably, the SIW three-way coupler achieves a significant reduction in the coupling length by employing slow-wave SIW transmission line. The core footprint of the 5 × 6 Butler matrix is only 17.1 mm × 37 mm, i.e., 1.5λ0 × 3.2λ0. Based on the proposed dual-layer 5 × 6 Butler matrix, a multibeam antenna is finally implemented to generate five beams including a broadside beam. The experimental results demonstrate a measured peak gain of 13.8 dBi and 3-dB gain bandwidth of 15.4% covering 24-28 GHz.

title: A Dual-Layered NLP-Driven Framework for Mental Health Activity Recommendations Using Hybrid Sentiment Scoring and Matrix Factorization
authors: Kakarla Sumayah, Keerthana Menon, Neetu Srivastava
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: A dual-layered NLP-driven framework for tailored recommendations of mental health activities is presented in this paper. The system combines a matrix factorization engine with LightFM and a hybrid sentiment analysis module that combines transformer-based BERT models and VADER models. Text input from users is analyzed to determine their emotional state, which informs activity recommendations through collaborative filtering (for returning users) or mood mapping (for new users). Accessibility and engagement are improved by a conversational graphical user interface (GUI). Results from experiments on a dataset of cleaned tweets show how well the system detects sentiment and delivers tailored interventions.

title: Area-Efficient Neural Network Hardware via Integer-Based Probabilistic Computation
authors: Shivangi Gambhir
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The increasing demand for high-performance, low-power hardware for deep neural networks (DNNs) motivates the exploration of alternative computing paradigms. This paper introduces a novel approach to stochastic computation using integer-valued streams, along with fundamental circuit designs. A Very-Large-Scale Integration (VLSI) architecture for DNN implementation, leveraging the proposed integer-based probabilistic computation, is presented. Compared to conventional stochastic computing methods, the proposed architecture achieves significant reductions in both area and latency, as demonstrated by FPGA prototyping and CMOS synthesis. Furthermore, the design exhibits improved energy efficiency relative to binary radix implementations. The fault-tolerant characteristics of the architecture are also analyzed.

title: A Blockchain-Based Framework to Process and Validate Educational Certificates: Bangladesh Perspective
authors: Vashkar Kar, Ruhit Arman, Fardin Ahsan, Kazi Masudul Alam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Educational certificate verification in Bangladesh struggles with inefficiencies, a lack of transparency, and risks of fraud. This study introduces a blockchain-based framework to streamline certificate issuance, storage, and validation. Built using Hyperledger Fabric, CouchDB, and Docker on a Linux platform, the system utilizes JavaScript smart contracts to ensure secure and transparent management. It integrates private university networks with a blockchain led by a central validating organization, enabling QR code-based verification. The results show improved accessibility, reduced processing times, and enhanced security over traditional and existing blockchain approaches. This work delivers a scalable, tamper-proof solution that significantly advances certificate management in Bangladesh’s academic landscape.

title: PharmaShield: Optimized Blockchain Framework for Pharma Supply
authors: Muammar Shahrear Famous, Samia Sayed, Rashed Mazumder, Risala T Khan, M. Shamim Kaiser, Mohammad Shahadat Hossain, Karl Andersson, Rahamatullah Khondoker
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The infiltration of counterfeit and substandard medications in global pharmaceutical supply chains remains a critical public health challenge. This study presents an innovative and secure approach that integrates blockchain, polymorphic encryption, and cloud storage to strengthen the management of the drug supply chain. By combining decentralized consensus mechanisms on the Ethereum blockchain with advanced polymorphic encryption and off-chain cloud storage, the proposed system addresses key challenges such as data privacy, scalability, traceability, and storage optimization. The framework supports secure supplier registration, encrypted data handling, and realtime cloud-backed traceability, ensuring transparency and stakeholder trust throughout the supply chain. Extensive analysis demonstrates that the system improves security and efficiency over existing solutions by reducing blockchain bloat through optimized off-chain storage and strengthening data confidentiality with dynamic encryption schemes. While advanced encryption introduces higher computational costs, the model maintains a strong balance between performance and protection. Future work includes developing a decentralized application (dApp) to enable real-world testing under high transaction volumes, validating scalability, robustness, and practical deployment feasibility. This integrated approach offers a promising, scalable solution for combating pharmaceutical counterfeiting and improving the integrity of drug supply chains globally.

title: Traffic Forecasting and Energy-Aware Routing in SDNs Using GNN and MARL
authors: Aiman Maniyar, Achyut Yaragal, S.K.Padaganur, Mallikarjuna Dheshmukh
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Modern Software Defined Networks (SDNs) demand adaptive routing strategies to handle fluctuating traffic and reduce energy consumption. Traditional methods, such as shortest-path routing or rule-based policies, are insufficient in coping with dynamic and large-scale traffic behaviors. In this paper, we propose a novel framework that integrates Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for traffic forecasting with Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) for decentralized, energy-aware routing. The system learns traffic patterns from graph-structured flow data and proactively optimizes routing decisions to minimize latency and power usage. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms baseline methods in terms of delay reduction and energy efficiency, while maintaining high adaptability to varying traffic conditions.

title: Assessing the Impact of Architectural Design in Deep Learning Models for Fundus-Based Ocular Disease Detection
authors: Abdullah Al Alam Shanto, Mumtahina Esha, Sadia Alam Kotha, Riyadul Islam, Sefatul Wasi, Siam Tahsin Bhuiyan, Rashedur Rahman, Saadia Binte Alam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Ocular diseases such as Cataract, Age-Related Macular Degeneration, Diabetic Retinopathy, and uncorrected refractive errors like Myopia, constitute the leading causes of visual impairment globally, predominantly affecting the elderly populations. Early diagnosis is crucial to avert irreversible vision loss. Conventional diagnostic techniques like fundus fluorescein angiography (FFA), optical coherence tomography (OCT), and fundus image analysis can be resource-intensive and reliant on the expertise of specialists. In recent times, different automated methods for diagnosis have been developed to help specialists in their work. This study presents a comparative analysis of three deep learning models: EfficientNetB3 (CNN), ViT-B-16 (Vision-Transformer), and ConvNeXtXLarge (CNN inspired by Transformers), for multiclass ocular disease classification utilizing the ODIR-5K dataset. Among the three evaluated architectures, ConvNeXtXLarge demonstrated the highest performance, achieving an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.892 and EfficientNetB3 achieved an AUROC of 0.870, and ViT-B-16 achieved an AUROC of 0.864. Regarding classification accuracy, ConvNeXt-XLarge reached 73.6%, exceeding EfficientNetB3 by 4.0% and outperforming ViT-B-16 by 2.0%. Grad-CAM visualizations provided additional insight since the heatmaps generated by EfficientNetB3 appeared to align more closely with clinically relevant regions than those produced by ConvNeXtXLarge or ViT-B-16. This suggests that, while ConvNeXtXLarge and ViT-B-16 show better scores, EfficientNet gives a better clinically aligned decision. Architectural design plays a pivotal role in shaping both diagnostic accuracy and the transparency of models in ophthalmic imaging, as demonstrated by this comprehensive evaluation that combines quantitative metrics with interpretability insights.

title: Multi-Track Audio Fusion for Emotion Recognition Using Machine and Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Joyeta Banerjee, Mohiuddin Ahmad, Tahmida Tabassum
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Emotion recognition from audio signals is essential for human-computer interaction, affective computing, and mental health monitoring. However, traditional single-track systems often fail to capture the full range of emotional nuance in complex audio environments. Motivated by this challenge, we propose a multi-track audio fusion-based approach to improve emotion classification accuracy. Using Bangla song recordings, we extract Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients, spectral, and temporal features, then apply feature-level fusion to build enriched audio representations. These features are classified using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and compared against classical machine learning models, including SVM, RF, and CatBoost. The CNN model achieved superior performance, with a 99.45% accuracy rate on multi-emotion classification. Our results confirm the robustness of multi-track fusion and deep learning in building more precise and scalable emotion recognition systems.

title: Accurate Skin Disease Classification using Optimized Convolutional Neural Network
authors: K. Naveen Chary, Kommuri Srikar, Kothakapu Varshini, M.A.Jabbar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Skin illnesses touch millions of people worldwide and pose severe challenges to diagnosis and cure particularly in instances with complicated overlapping conditions. Conventional diagnosis is more dependent on dermatologists subjective evaluation, which can differ from one practitioner to another, and thus accurate and consistent detection is not to be relied upon. In this paper, a deep learning-based method for skin disease classification employing Convolutional Neural Networks is proposed. In particular, the model aims at classifying some of the skin diseases like Atopic Dermatitis, Bullous Disease,and Rosacea from medical images of the skin. The pre trained model used is EfficientNet which is used for transfer learning , allowing for improved extraction of deep discriminative features with efficiency. The model is trained and validated using the Derm7pt dataset. In order to handle the imbalance of data and enhance the generalization, methods like data augmentation and dropout regularization are used. The performance of the model is measured using typical classification measures like accuracy, precision, recall and F1 score. the CNN model achieves the accuracy of 87.36% which has outperfromed the existing models. In addition, the model supports real-time predictions with a portable and scalable solution for clinical and remote healthcare environments. This paper highlights the capability of deep learning to aid and augment dermatological diagnosis in a reliable and efficient way.

title: Efficient DDoS Attack Detection using Optimized Feature Selection and Machine Learning
authors: Shimul Bala, Sk. Md. Masudul Ahsan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks are continuously posing a substantial threat to network security, frequently resulting in significant service outages, financial losses, and reputational harm. Accurate and proficient detection of such attacks is a critical and formidable challenge, as the high dimensionality of network traffic data increases computational overhead and may introduce redundant features that degrade model performance. This paper proposes an optimized feature selection framework to improve DDoS detection using machine learning. The Extra Tree feature selection technique is applied to extract the most relevant attributes from the CICDDoS2019 dataset. The resulting reduced feature sets are evaluated using various machine learning classifier algorithms, including Decision Tree, Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, XGBoost, and Multilayer Perceptron. Experimental results demonstrate that the approach, based on a Decision Tree and ETC, achieves very high detection accuracy while significantly minimizing the feature set from 86 to 16 and lowering the processing time. This highlights the importance of efficient attribute selection during the deployment of lightweight and robust DDoS detection systems suitable for real-time networking environments.

title: Effective VLSI Implementation of Karhunen-Loeve Transform for Medical Electronic Signal Processing
authors: S. Karthick, Vignesh Prasanna Natarajan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The timely diagnosis of heart disease in its early stages provokes the need to develop advanced methods. Traditional methods of electrocardiogram (ECG) signal processing face challenges in computational efficiency and accuracy. Thus, an innovative approach namely, VLSI-based skill optimizer driven gated refined long short-term memory (SO-GRLSTM) method is implemented in this research for enhanced computational efficiency and detection of heart disease based on ECG recordings. Initially, the dataset comprising ECG signal recordings is collected and then preprocessed using a Wiener filter (WF) to reduce noise and improve the quality of the signal. Further, feature extraction is performed by employing the Karhunen-Loeve Transform (KLT), to effectively reduce the dimensionality of the input signal, while preserving the significant features. The extracted features are then fed into SO-GRLSTM, to optimize prediction accuracy and reduce overfitting. To ensure the feasibility of real-time deployment in medical devices, the entire system is implemented in a highly efficient very large-scale integration (VLSI) architecture. This design incorporates parallel processing and optimized hardware resources to provide low latency, frequency, area, and low power consumption. According to the results, the system achieves 97.2% accuracy in distinguishing between normal and abnormal heart rhythms. Thus, to enhance heart disease prediction, this research offers the potential to provide robust solutions for real-world settings.

title: Design and Analysis of a Low-Power 4-Bit Ripple Carry Adder Using MTCMOS-Based 10-Transistor Full Adders
authors: Ragul T, Senbagaseelan V, Subbulakshmi A, R. Rajesh Kanna
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Recent advances in low-power VLSI design aim at optimizing adders, mainly Ripple Carry Adders (RCAs), which are very common in digital systems. Traditional RCA designs often result in high transistor count, increased leakage power, and high propagation delay, limiting their efficiency in modern low-power applications. This paper achieves a new 10-transistor RCA implemented using the MTCMOS technique to overcome the limitations of traditional RCAs. It integrates sleep transistors to reduce leakage power while maintaining high-speed operation. The design, using 90nm CMOS technology in Cadence Virtuoso, provides improvements in Power Delay Product (PDP) and propagation delay over conventional methods. The MTCMOS-10T RCA achieves a 24.2% reduction in power consumption and a 24.8% reduction in delay compared to the 12T RCA, making it a highly efficient solution for energy-efficient, high-performance VLSI systems.

title: Severity Prediction of Hair Loss in Alopecia Areata with Hybrid Deep Learning Architecture
authors: Deepak Banerjee, Raghavendra Sridhar, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Gaurav Sharma, Manish Kumar Singla
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This particular study presents an efficient model for classifying different degrees of harm in the context of applying deep learning approaches. This dataset contains over 11,880 images that the model will use to distinguish among five specific degrees of harm. Excellent classification accuracy is reported on different evaluation metrics, such as precision, recall, and F1-score. The main focus was given to Harm Degree 1, which reached an unprecedented level of 97.97% on all the metrics based on a total of 2,460 images (an equivalent of 21% of the dataset) and class-specific accuracy of 0.99; similarly, Harm Degree 2 reached an overall 97.88% precision, recall, and F1-Score based on 2,360 images (20%) and 0.99 accuracy. Harm Degree 3 performed best across these indexes: 98.00% with all metrics across 2,500 images (21%) and a 0.99 accuracy. Harm Degrees 4 and 5 reported identical figures (97.81%) across all three metrics at 2,280 pictures (19% each); both attained 0.99 accuracy. These consistently high results demonstrate the strength of the proposed model in the correct identification and differentiation of harm severity levels. The impressive overall accuracy of 97.89% makes the model highly promising for practical deployment in automated harm detection and severity classification tasks, with guaranteed reliable and rapid evaluation from image data.

title:
Comparison study of DC and RF/Analog performance Analysis of germanium source vertical TFET (Ge-VTFET) and Si
                    <sub>0.5</sub>
                    Ge
                    <sub>0.5</sub>
                    source vertical TFET (Si
                    <sub>0.5</sub>
                    Ge
                    <sub>0.5</sub>
                    -VTFET)
authors: Debasish Mohanta, Sruti Suvadarshini Singh, Prasanna Kumar Sahu
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This article explores the comparative study of germanium source vertical TFET (Ge-VTFET) and Si<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>Ge<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf> source vertical TFET ( Si<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>Ge<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf> -VTFET) on DC with analog/RF performance by the help of Silvaco 2D TCAD simulator. The outcome shows the performance of Ge-VTFET surpass the Si<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>Ge<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>-VTFET with regard to DC characteristics, subthreshold swing (SS), $\left({{}^{{{\text{I}}_{{\text{ON}}}}}{/_{{{\text{I}}_{{\text{OFF}}}}}}}\right)$ ratio etc. The article emphasizes on essential figures of merit (FOM) like transconductance (g<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf>), gate-to-drain capacitance (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">gd</inf>), gate-to-source capacitance (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">gs</inf>), conductance (g<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</inf>) which are all improved in of Ge-VTFET as compared to Si<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>Ge<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf> VTFET. The observed on current I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf> is $2.49 \times {10^{ - 6}}(2.52 \times {10^{ - 5{\text{A}}}}{/_{\mu {\text{m}}}}$), off current I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> is $1.15 \times {10^{ - 16}}(1.6 \times {10^{ - 17}}^{\text{A}}{/_{\mu {\text{m}}}}$) and ratio ${}^{{{\text{I}}_{{\text{ON}}}}}{/_{{{\text{I}}_{{\text{OFF}}}}}}$ is of $2.16 \times {10^{10}}(1.57 \times {10^{12}}$) for Ge-VTFET (Si<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>Ge<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>-VTFET), respectively. The proposed Ge-VTFET has better temperature stability than the Si<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>Ge<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>-VTFET in terms of ${\text{SS}}{,^{{{\text{I}}_{{\text{ON}}}}}}{/_{{{\text{I}}_{{\text{OFF}}}}}}$ ratio. These results suggest that Ge-VTFET is a splendid performer in terms of RF and analog applications.

title: Pre-Routing Timing Analysis of Wallace Tree Multiplier Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: C. Paramasivam, P. Kushulu Kumari, Rakesh S
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Accurate timing analysis before routing is essential for optimizing high-speed VLSI circuits, such as Wallace Tree Multipliers. However, traditional methods often depend on post-routing data, which can cause delays in the design process. In this paper, we introduce a machine learning approach that utilizes Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to predict gate delays and pinpoint critical paths using Cadence LIB and DEF files. We model the circuit as a directed graph with NetworkX, extracting features like gate types, delays, and fan-in from an adjacency matrix. A CNN then processes this matrix to estimate delays, while Dijkstra’s algorithm is used to determine the critical path and timing slack. Our experimental results support this approach, showing accurate delay predictions and effective identification of timing bottlenecks. This method significantly enhances early-stage design optimization, minimizing iterations and improving timing closure for high-performance multiplier circuits.

title: EEG-Based Biomarkers for Early Detection of First Episode Psychosis Using Riemannian Geometry and Support Vector Machines
authors: Most. Nusrat Tasnim Ria, Md Jewel Rana, Shabbir Mahmood, Nakib Aman
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Psychosis is a severe neuropsychiatric disorder characterized by a loss of contact with reality. First Episode Psychosis (FEP) refers to the initial occurrence of psychotic symptoms, including cognitive disruption and memory loss. Early detection of FEP is essential for timely therapeutic intervention and improved clinical outcomes. This study presents a novel EEG-based classification framework that integrates Riemannian geometry with Support Vector Machine (SVM) learning to enhance the early identification of FEP. Covariance matrices were computed from preprocessed EEG signals and projected onto a Riemannian manifold to preserve the intrinsic geometry of brain signal variability.
