Tangent space features derived from these matrices were classified using a kernelized support vector machine (SVM) tailored for high-dimensional EEG data. The proposed model achieved a classification accuracy of 99.98%, with perfect precision, recall, and F1 Scores. The approach used 1,830 features and achieved a test inference speed of 0.37 milliseconds per sample. EEG topographic mapping of band power ratios—specifically Theta/Alpha, Theta/Beta, Alpha/Beta, Delta/Alpha, and Delta/Beta revealed spatial distribution differences, indicating distinct neurophysiological patterns associated with psychosis. These findings support the effectiveness of Riemannian geometry-based machine learning in identifying reliable EEG biomarkers for early psychosis detection, offering a noninvasive and computationally efficient diagnostic approach for clinical psychiatry.

title: Real-Time Incremental Transformer with Continual Learning for Adaptive Phishing Email Detection
authors: Md Nahian Suhaimee, Farhan Shakil, Md Rifat Al Amin Khan, Md Omar Faruq, Sheikh Razia Sultana, Sabiha Firdaus
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Phishing emails remain one of the most prevalent cybersecurity threats, evolving rapidly to evade traditional detection systems. This paper proposes a Real-Time Incremental Transformer with Continual Learning for adaptive phishing email detection in streaming environments. The model employs a lightweight DistilBERT/TinyBERT architecture combined with Elastic Weight Consolidation (EWC) to mitigate catastrophic forgetting while adapting to new phishing patterns over time. Evaluated on a phishing email dataset partitioned into seven temporal chunks, the proposed method achieved a peak accuracy of 98.5%, an F1-score of 98.3%, and maintained performance above 98.0% across all chunks despite distributional drift. Latency experiments demonstrated sub-50ms inference per email for batch sizes up to eight, making the system suitable for real-time deployment in enterprise email security infrastructures. Comparative analysis with static transformers and traditional deep learning baselines showed improvements of up to 5.8% in accuracy, while the ablation study highlighted the critical role of continual learning and temporal chunking. These results validate the proposed approach as a scalable and robust solution for combating evolving phishing attacks in real-world email systems.

title: Design and Implementation of an IoT-based Smart Energy-Harvesting Badminton Court Using Piezoelectric and Solar System
authors: Nur Hossain Bhuiyan, Dwin Islam, Amran Hossain, Md. Rashedul Islam, Thofazzol Hossen, Md. Abdul Monayem
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The increasing global demand for energy and reliance on conventional energy sources have significantly impacted the environment. While fuel-dependent methods of electricity generation are essential, they contribute to air pollution and increase the risks of climate change. As a result, renewable and environmentally friendly energy production methods have become crucial. The concept of Internet of Things (IoT) based energy generation from smart badminton courts offers a solution that balances environmental protection and sustainable energy use. It converts unnecessary vibrations generated by players’ movements into energy and combines it with solar power, creating a joint energy production system. This innovative approach integrates piezoelectric technology, solar power, and IoT, opening new possibilities in modern energy management. The entire process is automatically controlled using IoT technology, which efficiently monitors each stage from energy generation to storage and final consumption. The collected energy is used to power the court’s smart lighting, digital scoreboards, and charging stations, while any excess energy is stored for future use. This innovation not only provides a sustainable energy production method but also helps prevent energy waste and reduces environmental impact. By utilizing vibrations in specific locations, this technology enables energy generation in areas where vibrations occur.

title: EcoClassNet: A Lightweight CNN for Real-Time E-Waste Device Classification
authors: S.M. Shahriar Rahman Maruph, Mohiuddin Mohammad Sadik, Dewan Md. Easin, Md. Asif Karim Chayon, Md. Motaharul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The explosive increase in electronic devices and their accelerated obsolescence have made electronic waste (e-waste) a severe global environmental problem. Improper disposal of e-waste releases toxic chemicals that are hazardous to ecosystems and human health, and ineffective recycling causes the loss of valuable materials. To address this, we present EcoClassNet, a lightweight convolutional neural network for automated multi-class e-waste device classification in real-time environments. Rather than introducing a new dataset, we curate a diverse collection of 6,556 high-resolution images by combining and refining samples from publicly available datasets, including the E-Waste Image, E-Waste Vision, and Balanced E-Waste. These images contain sixteen common types of e-waste appliances, including laptops, mobile phones, and televisions. The proposed EcoClassNet model is based on MobileNetV2 and Efficient-NetB0 as a lightweight yet powerful backbone, supplemented with squeeze-and-excitation attention mechanisms and depthwise separable convolutions for optimal performance-resource tradeoff. Our model achieves 93.14% classification accuracy while reducing parameter count by over 90% compared to standard ResNet50 baselines. With a real-time inference speed of 24.14ms on Google Colab hardware, EcoClassNet offers a scalable and deployable solution for integration into smart recycling systems in resource-constrained settings.

title: Implementation of Sensor-less FOC Multi-Motor Control of BLDC for Vehicle Applications
authors: Rahul Sharma
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This paper focuses on the multi-motor control of BLDC motors using the sensor-less FOC technique. BLDC motors offer numerous advantages such as high efficiency, improved power density and reduced maintenance. The sensor-less FOC method, which enables precise motor torque and speed control due to its ability to enhance motor performance and efficiency. The paper presents the fundamental principles and mathematical models of BLDC motors and FOC. A detailed analysis of control algorithms, including current control, rotor position estimation, and torque control, is designed and presented. The paper proposed the extension of these concepts to the multi-motor control domain, considering the coordination and synchronization of motor actions. A hardware setup is implemented, consisting of multiple BLDC motors, an Arduino UNO and multiple TLE9879 BLDC shields. The control algorithms are implemented in real-time, enabling precise motor control and coordination. The experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed multi-motor control system, showcasing the ability to simultaneously control multiple motors, adjust their speeds, and coordinate their actions.

title: Hybrid PSO-JAYA Algorithm for Solving the Multi-Objective Electric Vehicle Routing Problem (EVRP)
authors: Tejaswini Patil, Sandip Mane
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The increasing adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) in logistics calls for improved routing systems that minimize energy consumption, operational costs, and fleet size. This rather complex multi-objective optimization problem relates to the Electric Vehicle Routing Problem (EVRP) and involves constraints placed by battery limitations, availability of charging stations, and associated time windows for deliveries. This paper proposes a hybrid optimization approach that employs Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Jaya Algorithm for solving the EVRP. The area of the hybrid model benefits from global exploration strength of PSO while at the same time using the Jaya Algorithm quality of not using any parameters in learning. The evaluation on standard benchmark datasets proves that the Hybrid PSO-Jaya Algorithm closely outperforms the individual PSO and Jaya methods with respect to energy savings, operational cost reduction, and optimal fleet use. The hybrid algorithm achieves much better convergence toward the great optimum solutions because of the equilibrium maintained between exploration and exploitation within the search space. The experimentation results further analyzed using the Inverted Generational Distance (IGD) metric shows definitely that it gets to prove the hybrid approach as superior over the current available solutions. The works contribute to developing sustainable logistics as it provides a reliable and parameter-free optimization technique for managing EVs.

title: Real-Time Obstacle Detection and Audio Navigation for the Visually Impaired Using YOLO
authors: Divya Kothapalli, Mrudhula Amarthaluri, Aishwarya Bollipogu, Naga Joshitha Ambati
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Navigating dynamic or unfamiliar environments poses significant challenges for visually impaired individuals, often limiting their independence and safety. This paper presents a real-time indoor obstacle detection and audio navigation system that combines YOLOv8, a high-speed object detection model, with MiDaS, a monocular depth estimation network, to identify obstacles and estimate their relative distances using a single RGB camera. Detected objects and depth data are converted into intuitive auditory cues via a text-to-speech engine, enabling context-aware, real-time guidance. The system is optimized for indoor environments and demonstrates robust performance under varying lighting and layout conditions. Trained for 50 epochs on a custom indoor dataset, it achieved 62.77% mAP@0.5, 41.31% mAP@0.5:0.95, 64.17% precision, and 59.42% F1-score, ensuring accurate and timely navigation feedback. Experimental results validate its applicability as a cost-effective, AI-driven assistive technology that enhances spatial awareness, autonomy, and confidence for visually impaired users.

title: Grapevine Disease Detection Using Self-Supervised Learning: A Comparative Analysis of SimCLR, MoCo, and BYOL
authors: Palash Hossen, Tanjim Mahmud, Abubokor Hanip, Mohammad Shahadat Hossain
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Grapevine diseases significantly reduce crop quality, posing severe economic challenges to viticulture. While deep learning methods, particularly Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), have shown promise in automated disease classification from leaf images, their effectiveness is dominated by the need for large, expertly labeled datasets, a major limitation for practical deployment in agriculture. To address this, we propose a self-supervised learning (SSL) approach that leverages unlabeled grape leaf images to learn rich visual representations without human annotation. We implement state-of-the-art SSL frameworks, including SimCLR, MoCo, and BYOL, combined with CNN backbones such as ResNet18 and MobileNetV2 for feature extraction. For downstream disease classification, we evaluate lightweight classifiers such as Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Logistic Regression. We achieve 94.78% F1 Score with SimCLR using ResNet18 and SVM, 88.49% with MoCo using ResNet18 and Logistic Regression, and 88.53% with BYOL using ResNet18 and Logistic Regression. For MobileNetV2, the highest F1 Score of 88.62% is achieved with SimCLR and Logistic Regression. Overall, SimCLR with the ResNet backbone and SVM classifier outperforms all other methods across metrics. Our results demonstrate that SSL can achieve competitive classification accuracy while significantly reducing the need for labeled training data, offering a scalable and cost-effective solution for early plant disease management.

title: On-Device Product Recognition and Billing: A Deep Learning Framework for Low-Cost Embedded Retail Systems
authors: Muzaddid Mehrab, Md Shohanur Rahman, Mrityunjoy Gain, Anupam Kumar Bairagi, Avi Deb Raha
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Manual billing in grocery stores often causes long queues and frequent errors, which lowers customer satisfaction. Deep learning offers a promising way to automate this process through real-time image-based product recognition. However, existing computer vision checkout systems depend on powerful hardware and cloud services, so they remain too costly and complex for small retailers. This paper fills that gap by proposing a low-cost self-checkout solution that combines image classification and weight sensing on the ESP32-CAM microcontroller. We constructed a five-class grocery image dataset under in-store conditions. The dataset was further expanded through targeted data augmentation techniques to improve model generalization. We evaluated several lightweight CNNs, including MobileNetV2, ShuffleNetV2, EfficientNet-B0, GoogLeNet, ResNet18, MNASNet-0.5, and SqueezeNet. Each model was pretrained on ImageNet and fine-tuned on the augmented dataset. Then each model was evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrices. After comparing several lightweight models, the most suitable one was selected and deployed on the ESP32-CAM microcontroller. Among the candidates, SqueezeNet exhibited the most favorable balance between classification performance and resource efficiency, requiring only 1.24 million parameters, 0.8 MB of flash memory, and 76 kB of RAM. Inference latency ranged from 50 to 100 milliseconds, with peak power consumption remaining under 0.8 watts. The selected model was deployed entirely on the ESP32 CAM, enabling on-device image capture, item recognition, weight measurement using an HX711 based load cell, and real time billing display via an OLED interface. These results validate the feasibility of deploying deep learning models for reliable, real-time retail automation on low-power, cost-sensitive embedded platforms.code: https://github.com/mehrab2031025/Grocery_product_detection_using_ESP32_CAM

title: Design and Development of an IoT-based CCTV Monitoring System using Deep Learning
authors: Md. Obaidur Rahman, Nayeem Ullah, Khawja Imran Masud, Mehedi Hasan Shuvo
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Security surveillance systems are essential for safe-guarding diverse environments, including residences, workplaces, and public areas. Conventional CCTV systems frequently depend on manual oversight, rendering them ineffective and susceptible to human error. To address such issues, deep learning-based facial recognition and IoT-integrated surveillance have been looked into in the past. Still, many current systems don’t have the ability to automatically automate tasks or track people in real-time. This research proposes an IoT-enabled CCTV surveillance system using PIR sensor-based motion detection, YOLOv9-based facial recognition, and a real-time Open-CV tracker. This system controls the camera, as it senses human movement; the camera activates automatically and takes facial pictures for identification. Moreover, the camera is controlled remotely through an Android application that can manually switch it on and off and upload labeled images to Firebase for model training. After training the data through the mobile app, the system can recognize authorized users and identify unauthorized faces as unknown.A small-scale dataset (three classes, limited samples) demonstrated that the developed system obtained 100% accuracy during experimental validation. Although these findings are encouraging, more testing on bigger and more varied datasets is required to prove robustness for practical, real-time applications.

title: Machine Learning-Enhanced Accessibility Framework in Banking Services for Blind and Visually Impaired Users
authors: Mamta B Savadatti, Vipul H. Kondekar, S Saranya, Anisha Kunjan S, Khobragade Pithamber, Selva Agnes S, Ajay Sudhir Bale
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Blind and visually impaired (BVI) people find it difficult to recognize the benefits of modern technology and everyday products due to challenges in accessibility. The various qualities of BVI consumers offer the user research a formidable challenge for those responsible to address the requirements of creating accessible goods. There is a need of a tool that offers comprehensive insights to understand the experiences of BVI individuals, instead of relying on standards and documentation without thorough explanations of practical implementations. Machine learning (ML) techniques, including Traditional ML and algorithms for Deep Learning are employed for the recognition of human activities within this domain. This study presents a thorough review of assisting the BVI users by monitoring their behaviors via machine learning methods and human activity recognition systems.

title: Filtering Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface With Out-of-Band Electromagnetic Transparency
authors: Qunqiang Hu, Huiping Yang, Gu-Ying Deng, Xiu Yin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) featuring both filtering response and out-of-band electromagnetic (EM) transparency. First, a theoretical method that simultaneously enables the functionalities of filtering and out-of-band EM transparency is established. Second, an equivalent circuit model and RIS element are designed based on the theoretical solutions. Full-wave simulations show agreement with the circuit calculations, confirming the targeted responses of out-of-band near-lossless transmission, in-band tunable phase shifts, and filtering. Finally, an RIS prototype is fabricated and measured for further validation. The measured results closely match the simulations, exhibiting the desired gain-filtering response. In addition, in-band beams achieve scanning up to 60°. Moreover, out-of-band radiation patterns transmitted through the RIS exhibit little distortion, verifying the EM transparency. To the best of the authors’ knowledge, this is the first RIS capable of achieving filtering and out-of-band EM transparency simultaneously. It offers a promising solution to address adjacent-band interference in RIS-assisted multiband wireless networks.

title: Multi-target Parameter Estimation for Cognitive Radar based on RIS
authors: Shaohang Jing, Hao Chen, Yechao Bai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Cognitive radar can dynamically adjust the resource allocation according to the mission requirements and environments, so as to improve the capability of target detection, identification and tracking. In this paper, a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) assisted cognitive radar is proposed, which controls the reflection coefficients of the RIS elements to alter the spatial energy distribution of signals, and finally reduces the uncertainty in the areas of interest. To enhance the response speed and robustness of the estimation module, a compressive sensing-based target parameter estimation model is developed by incorporating adaptive sparse Bayesian learning. By adopting the differential entropy of the target region as the loss function, RIS can serve as a feedback channel for cognitive radar, thereby enhancing the system’s estimation performance. The cognitive process is implemented by the multiple gradient descent algorithm that combines Frank-Wolfe solver and momentum gradient descent. Finally, simulation results show that the RIS can assist the radar to realize the cognitive function and improve the accuracy of multi-target parameter estimation.

title: A degradation analysis and prediction method oriented to the whole-life-cycle measurement signal of wind turbine gearboxes
authors: Xiaosheng Lan, Zhiyong Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Given the complexity of whole-life monitoring data processing for wind turbine gearboxes, this paper proposes a degradation analysis and prediction method oriented to the whole-life-cycle signal. First, based on historical measurement data and combined with fault category and condition assessments, the whole-life-cycle measurement signal of wind turbine gearboxes (WL-WTG) were compiled, thereby providing support for subsequent degradation analysis and prediction. Second, the Mean-Gray Relation Index (MGR) degradation analysis method was used to optimize the amount of complex data and visualize the process of degradation. Then, the segmented superposition training mechanism (ST) was designed, and Pl and Pi training methods were introduced to construct the Long short-term memory-Segmented superposition training mechanism (LSTM-ST) prediction model, and degradation prediction was performed via repeated cyclic testing. Finally, the feasibility analysis of deterioration and prediction of IMS 2-1 signal from the Intelligent Maintenance System Center (IMS) is extended to validate the effectiveness of WL-WTG signal. Tests show that the method enables deterioration analysis and prediction, and provides a reference for the study of whole life cycle signals in the field of wind power engineering.

title: WildTalker$^{\infty }$ : Pushing the Limits of 3D Talking Portrait Synthesis in Unconstrained Environments
authors: Seonghak Lee, Jisoo Park, Junseok Kwon
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: We propose WildTalker<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula>, a novel framework for synthesizing high-quality talking portraits capable of effectively addressing challenges encountered in real-world scenarios. Conventional techniques typically struggle to manage transient visual artifacts and auditory disturbances, thereby compromising the realism and synchronization of synthesized talking portraits. To overcome these limitations, WildTalker<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> introduces flow-guided temporal masking, a strategy specifically designed to accurately identify and mitigate transient movements within dynamic scenes, thereby enhancing visual coherence. Furthermore, WildTalker<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> integrates a multi-scale spectral subtraction method for robust audio denoising, significantly improving lip synchronization and ensuring natural audio-visual alignment even under challenging acoustic conditions. Our comprehensive experiments validate that WildTalker<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> considerably advances the synthesis quality of audio-driven 3D talking portraits, demonstrating superior lip synchronization performance in dynamic environments characterized by auditory and visual complexity. Comparative results confirm that WildTalker<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods across both controlled and uncontrolled scenarios, underscoring its practical efficacy and broad applicability.

title: Beyond Real versus Fake Towards Intent-Aware Video Analysis_supp1-3663642.pdf
authors: Saurabh Atreya
abstract: The rapid advancement of generative models has led to increasingly realistic deepfake videos, posing significant societal and security risks. While existing detection methods have focused on distinguishing real from fake videos, such approaches fail to address a fundamental question: What is the intent behind a manipulated video? Towards addressing this question, we introduce IntentHQ: the first benchmark for human-centered intent analysis, shifting the paradigm from authenticity verification to contextual understanding of videos. IntentHQ consists of 5168 videos that have been meticulously collected and annotated with 23 fine-grained intent-categories, including ’Financial fraud’, ’Indirect marketing’, ’Political propaganda’, as well as ’Fear mongering’. We perform intent recognition with supervised and self-supervised multi-modality models that integrate spatiotemporal video features, audio processing, and text analysis to infer underlying motivation and goals behind videos. Our proposed model is streamlined to differentiate between a wide range of intent-categories. This work establishes intent recognition as a new research direction beyond authenticity verification, enabling richer contextual understanding of video. The IntentHQ dataset is available under https://github.com/atrey-a/intent-hq.

title: Current transfer length and interface resistance of KC4 RE BCO tapes
authors: N. Bagrets, B. Holzapfel, K. P. Weiss, C. Barth, A. Ballarino
abstract: In recent years, high temperature superconductors (HTS) have been developed intensively for different applications. REBCO HTS tapes used for these applications are hybrid materials where the superconductor is thin layer within a multilayered Coated Conductor (CC) structure, and usually it is coated by metals, e.g. by silver, copper, or stainless steel. The interface resistance between the superconducting layer and the surrounding layers is an important characteristic of an HTS tape, along with current density and critical temperature, as it strongly influences the current transfer length (CTL) and the Normal Zone Propagation (NPZ) velocity. There exists a well-known method for the evaluation of the interface resistance between the HTS layer and the surrounding metal layer [1, 2], or metal contact embedded in HTS material [3]. The interface resistance often differs by orders of magnitude in commercially available HTS tapes [4]. The values of the interface resistance and CTL together with critical current can be affected by conditions of storage and application environment. Often the tapes are stored in atmospheric conditions with certain humidity before Ag depositing step. Nowadays, with growing interest to hydrogen applications, HTS tapes are often considered for usage in hydrogen environment. However, it is unclear how the hydrogen atmosphere or humidity are influencing tape´s performance. For the tapes produced by KC4, the KIT-CERN REBCO laboratory, the influence of air humidity on non-stabilized tapes and H2 exposure on silver stabilized tapes was investigated.

title: Combinatorial Security Testing—10 Years Later
authors: Dimitris E. Simos, Manuel Leithner, Rick Kuhn, Bernhard Garn, Raghu Kacker, Jeff Lei
abstract: Ten years ago, we introduced a research program applying high strength combinatorial test methods to vulnerability and fault detection for cybersecurity. This article discusses advances in the field and where these methods may be effective for today’s complex security problems.

title: SMA-EL:a Minimal 1-cycle Construction Algorithm with Simplicial Maps Annotation and Edge Loss for Emotional Brain Networks Analysis
authors: Kechen Hou, Yuhan Shi, Xiaowei Zhang, Guangyuan Gao, Kaiwen Hu, Jian Shen, Zhongfeng Kang, Weihao Zheng, Bin Hu
abstract: The brain patterns of emotional perception remain a pivotal research domain in affective neuroscience. Modeling the brain as a complex network has become a crucial approach to understanding its functions. However, traditional brain network research based on graph theory primarily focuses on dyadic interactions between brain regions, which cannot effectively characterize the information exchange process among multiple brain regions during emotional cognitive processes. To address these limitations, we shift our perspective from graph theory to the topological data analysis (TDA) of minimal 1-cycles. The 1 cycles or loops within a network represent the fundamental high order interactions in complex networks and serve as essential pathways for information transmission and integration among the distributed networks of brain regions. By focusing on cycle structures in affective brain networks, we propose a novel SMA-EL method based on the collaborative optimization of the Minimal 1-Cycle with Simplicial Maps Annotation (SMA-M1C) method and linear programming, which balances computational efficiency and method performance to reconstruct the optimal cycles in the brain network. This method is applied to the analysis of emotional brain networks in response to positive and negative emotions induced by naturalistic viewing. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that the 1-cycle structures of the brain's functional patterns exhibit differences at both individual and group levels, aligning with prior research. Furthermore, the 1 cycles we proposed can serve as a biological marker for emotion recognition. These findings may provide new insights into the organization patterns of functional brain networks under diverse emotional states.

title: Reconfigurable Bidirectional Interleaved Neutral-Point-Clamped Resonant DC/DC Converter for High and Wide Voltage Gain Range
authors: Muhammad Mubeen Khan, Tsegaab Alemayehu Wagaye, Eden Sibhat Temesgen, Dong-Yeob Kang, Minsung Kim
abstract: This article proposes the reconfigurable bidirectional interleaved neutral-point-clamped (NPC) resonant dc/dc converter that supports a high and wide voltage gain range. The proposed converter is capable of operating as an interleaved three-level half-wave NPC converter in both forward and backward power-flow directions. In forward operation, the voltage gain range is extended from [2, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{\infty}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>] to [1, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{\infty}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>], and in backward operation, it is extended from [0, 0.5] to [0, 1]. The interleaved operation of NPC resonant dc/dc converter generates two asymmetric currents that alternately charge and discharge the secondary-side capacitors, thus inherently balancing their voltages. Comprehensive voltage gain expressions are derived for both power flow directions, along with a detailed design methodology. The proposed converter achieves a wide voltage gain with reduced transformer turns-ratio. The feasibility of the proposed topology, along with its switching modulation strategy, are validated experimentally using a 1-kW prototype with a 125-V input and an output voltage range of 275–1000 V.

title: Optimizing Aesthetic Perception through Human-AI Teaming for Subtle Dimension Identification in Art Annotation
authors: Mo Wang, Ye Zhang, Jinlong He, Yupeng Zhou, Niantong Li, Jianan Wang, Yifei Sun, Minghao Yin
abstract: Aesthetic perception, the cognitive process through which individuals interpret and evaluate the expressive and emotional qualities of visual art, is fundamental to students' creative and emotional development. Recent progress in artificial intelligence has enabled computational models to assist in aesthetic analysis by identifying patterns in visual composition and affective expression. However, such models often struggle to recognize abstract or context-dependent aesthetic dimensions, and improving these aspects through comprehensive annotation remains costly and time-consuming. This study presents a human-AI teaming framework designed to identify the aesthetic perception dimensions that AI models find most difficult to interpret and to allocate these dimensions to human experts for annotation. The framework employs a multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) mechanism, where each agent is assigned to a specific aesthetic dimension and learns a policy for determining whether expert annotation is required. Two complementary state representation strategies are introduced: a statistical representation that captures the model's predictive distribution across dimensions, and a graph-based attention module that models interdependencies among aesthetic attributes. A reward mechanism further guides agents to balance the improvement of model perception with the minimization of human annotation effort. Experiments conducted on two real-world datasets demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively identifies the challenging dimensions for AI models and strategically delegates them for human evaluation. This targeted collaboration significantly enhances annotation efficiency and model interpretability, providing a scalable approach for improving human-AI synergy in aesthetic perception analysis.

title: Blank Page
authors: N/A

title: RBKWOT: Rough-Allowance Borderline -Mode Clustering, Weighted Oversampling Technique for Class Imbalance Handling_supp1-3627388.pdf
authors: Anima Pramanik
abstract: The performance of machine learning (ML) algorithms is impacted due to the class imbalance issue. Although there are several techniques developed to take care of this issue, research on handling categorical or mixed imbalanced data has not been explored much. Moreover, the existing class imbalance handling techniques may not preserve the original characteristics of the data. To address these issues, a new technique, namely, the rough-allowance borderline K-mode clustering-based weighted oversampling technique (RBKWOT), is developed. It consists of four steps: 1) extraction of rough-allowance borderline minority samples; 2) generation of optimum K-mode clusters; 3) assignment of sampling weights to these clusters; and 4) generation of rules for resampling instances corresponding to each cluster based on its sampling weight. The RBKWOT is capable of reducing the imbalance ratio without incurring any loss of information. It also handles the uncertainty in decision-making for the samples belonging to the boundary region. The efficacy of RBKWOT over some state-of-the-art techniques is demonstrated using 21 categorical, continuous, and mixed data that are acquired from the UCI Machine Learning Repository. Experimental results reveal the superiority of RBKWOT.

title: Review for "Descriptor: Synthetic Person Information Dataset for Entity Resolution (SPIDER)"
authors: N/A

title: Domain Generalization of RUL Prediction for Bearings via Multi-source Domain Mix-up Transfer
authors: Yi Liu, Chengluo Li, Jialiang Zhu, Kai Zhang, Xuejun Jiang, Qinmin Yang
abstract: Accurate prediction of the remaining useful life for bearings is essential to predictive maintenance and health management. Nonetheless, the initial operating phase often suffers from limited and incomplete data, which poses significant challenges to the development of robust life prediction models. To address this problem, this article introduces a multi-source domain mix-up transfer (MDMT) model, integrated with a data reproduction mechanism. The proposed approach utilizes a bidirectional gated mechanism to capture temporal dependencies from time-frequency domain signals and uncover intrinsic degradation dynamics. A domain mix-up strategy is incorporated to promote information fusion across multiple bearings, while the data reproduction mechanism integrates new source-domain data, enabling extraction of generalized degradation features across domains. Extensive experiments on the XJTU-SY bearing dataset and the C-MAPSS aircraft engine dataset show that the proposed method achieves noticeable improvement in performance under scenarios where target domain data are not involved in training. The MDMT model demonstrates potential for practical prognostics and predictive maintenance.

title: Sleipnir: Multi-agent based stock operator framework with interpretability
authors: Xiaojun Qian, Sidi Chen, Zhenqiang Sun
abstract: The increasing complexity and volatility of modern financial markets have exposed fundamental limitations in traditional investment decision-making systems, which struggle to capture nonlinear market dynamics and adapt to rapidly evolving conditions. While deep learning approaches have shown promise in financial modeling, existing single-model frameworks remain inadequate for handling the multifaceted nature of contemporary markets, including cross-asset contagion effects, multimodal data integration, and the need for interpretable decision-making that complies with regulatory transparency requirements. To address these challenges, we propose Sleipnir, a novel multi-agent collaborative framework that replaces monolithic architectures with a debate-driven ensemble of heterogeneous agents powered by state-of-the-art Large Language Models (LLMs) (e.g., GPT-4o, Claude, DeepSeek-Reasoning). To establish a scalable and interpretable automated investment system, our framework 1) introduces an Interface Module for orchestrating multi-agent collaboration via strategic leader-follower coordination, 2) deploys a Dynamic Router for intelligent agent selection based on market conditions and query complexity, 3) integrates a ReAct Module to deliver tool-augmented analytical capabilities, and 4) implements a Reflect Module that enables interpretable decision tracing through atomic operation decomposition. Furthermore, the system incorporates a sophisticated Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline that dynamically correlates macro-level news events with micro-level trading patterns, supporting minute-level market responsiveness. Our framework delivers three principal advances: 1) a multi-agent collaborative architecture that combines consensus-driven decision-making with specialized investment perspectives, 2) a RAG-enhanced real-time intelligence engine equipped with time-adaptive retrieval mechanisms, and 3) an interpretable decision-tracing system that provides transparent, auditable reasoning paths while ensuring regulatory compliance. Comprehensive evaluation across NIO stock (a Chinese electric vehicle manufacturer), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) index (2024), high-frequency Bitcoin (BTC) trading data, and U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) filings, and diverse investor profiles demonstrates substantial improvements: 38.7% Sharpe ratio enhancement, reduced maximum drawdown, and superior diversification. In cryptocurrency trading, the framework achieved 0.67% returns versus 0.35% buy-and-hold with -0.82% maximum drawdown, while reducing computational costs and providing confidence-calibrated allocation strategies. These enhancements demonstrate the framework’s potential to serve as a scalable, interpretable, and robust solution that bridges theoretical economic models with practical investment implementation, supporting automated investment management across diverse market conditions. The experimental code is available on GitHub: https://github.com/sdchen53/Sleipnir.

title: Deep Learning-Based Breast Cancer Diagnosis from Histopathology
authors: Saba Tahseen, Apurva Chaudhari
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This study investigates the detection and classification of Invasive Ductal Carcinoma (IDC) with a CNN put in to the Breast Histopathology Images (IDC) Dataset. The dataset, comprising 277,524 50x50 patches of pixel images taken from 162 full-mount slides, presents a notable class imbalance, with 198,738 IDC-negative and 78,786 IDC-positive samples. A custom CNN architecture featuring multiple convolutional and max-pooling layers, followed by dense layers and a 0.5 dropout rate, was developed to capture fine-grained spatial features. Images were used to train the model. Data generators with an early stopping callback to counteract overfitting. Analysis of training metrics revealed a consistent reduction in training loss and an increase in training accuracy. However, validation performance indicated overfitting, as validation accuracy plateaued or slightly declined while validation loss began to rise, despite continued improvements in training metrics. These findings underscore the CNN's capacity to extract suitable features for breast cancer classification while highlighting the critical need to manage class imbalance and overfitting for improved generalization. Incorporating bigger and more varied datasets, using transfer learning for generalization, and implementing the system as a real-time clinical system will be the main goals of future research.

title: Optimized Hybrid Model Integrating CNN and Image Processing for Lung Cancer
authors: Phaneendra Varma Chintalapati, A. Prasanth
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The number of deaths related to cancer, and particularly the well-known lung cancer, remains the leading cause of morbidity in most regions of the globe, and early detection of the disease is of immense importance to the overall survival of patients [1] [2]. Recent developments in medical imaging and artificial intelligence enable the recognition of pulmonary nodules on chest scans with greater precision by using computer-aided detection (CAD)[3]. We propose a hybrid strategy in this paper that would combine traditional image processing methods and modern deep learning methods for detecting lung cancer in CT images. It includes a combination of image pre-processing (of median filter and patch-based enhancement), image segmentation (involves k-means clustering and particle swarm optimization), as well as a convolutional neural network (CNN) to extract features and classify the image. Lung CT data was used to test the method, and it resulted in both excellent performance (accuracy = ~99 % chance of successfully distinguishing between a malignant and a benign case) and efficiency, which greatly surpassed the performance of most of the existing methods. Its results indicate that our hybrid algorithm can establish the presence of lung cancer more effectively and precisely than conventional techniques. Information about accuracy and computational cost lacked a tailored approach that achieved high accuracy and low computational cost. The proposed approach aimed to address these gaps, as its pursuit in both deep learning and image processing showed efficacy. Its findings comprise a microscopic comparison to 20 of the state-of- the-art methods, the comparison of the feature detection and prediction characteristics, and the investigation of scaling up the effect of a dataset size on the quality of the algorithm. The hybrid algorithm proposed is better and can improve the early detection of lung cancer diagnosis within the clinical practice environment.

title: A Dynamically Revocable Three-Factor MAKA Protocol with Schnorr Signatures for Secure Multi-Server Environments
authors: S. Karthiyayini, S. Faizal Mukthar Hussain, S. P. Santhoshkumar, Kamalakannan Machap, S. Ramamoorthi, Mohammed Uveise S A
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Many services are being implemented on cloud platforms at an increasing rate due to the quick development of cloud computing technology, which raises serious concerns about preserving network security while guaranteeing smooth service accessibility. The majority of Multi-Server Authentication Key Agreement (MAKA) protocols currently in use either have high computational and communication costs or lack formal security proofs, leaving them open to different types of attacks. Additionally, the static revocation mechanisms used by the three-factor MAKA protocols currently in use allow for possible abuse by hostile users. This paper suggests a dynamically revocable three-factor MAKA protocol that incorporates Schnorr signatures and offers a formal security proof in the random oracle model in order to get around these restrictions. Strong defense against security threats is ensured by the suggested scheme's support for dynamic user management. Performance analysis validates its effectiveness and suitability for resource-constrained smart devices, while security analysis shows its appropriateness for multi-server environments. A thorough security assessment and full implementation confirm the protocol's usefulness and efficacy.

title: Pneumonia Detection Using Deep Learning and Machine Learning Approaches from Chest X-Ray
authors: Md Shahoriar Hossain Shojib, Md. Musfiqur Rahman Mridha, Md. Fatin Nibbrash Nakib, Samun Islam, Sakib Imtiaz, Md Mizanur Rahman, Md. Tanvir Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Pneumonia is a lung disease that causes an infection that inflames the air sacs (alveoli) in one or both lungs. It is a very common problem in both children and the elderly. It can create a serious health issue, even death, if not detected in time. This study utilizes deep learning and Machine Learning techniques to detect pneumonia accurately. The proposed model identifies pneumonia accurately at the first stage. CNN-based Deep Learning models like DenseNet121, DenseNet201, EfficientNetB0, and ResNet152V2 are used as a feature extractor. Additionally, A custom Deep Learning-based classifier, and Machine Learning classifiers including XGBoost, and Support Vector Machine (SVM) are utilized. Various pre-processing techniques, such as Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE), Laplacian filtering, etc., on chest X-ray images from two publicly available datasets have been applied to enhance the detection. The best performance was achieved using DenseNet201 combined with the Deep Learning-based Custom classifier, achieving an accuracy of 99.68% on dataset-1. Correspondingly, the combination of ResNet152V2 and the Deep Learning-based custom classifier achieved an accuracy of 98.75% on dataset-2. This study demonstrates the effectiveness of Deep Learning techniques in pneumonia detection.

title: Design of an Ultra-Wideband Nickel based Metamaterial Absorber for Enhanced Solar Energy Harvesting Application
authors: Rahul Chowdhury, Muhammad Asad Rahman, Mohammed Saifuddin Munna, Abu Hanif, Asif Mohammed Siddiqee
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This work proposes the construction of a high-performance, ultra-wideband metamaterial absorber that is specifically suited for solar energy harvesting. It efficiently covers the ultraviolet (UV), visible, and infrared portions of the electromagnetic spectrum. The proposed absorber features a nickel-based resonator structure combining a cross and four isosceles triangles, placed on a polyimide dielectric substrate with a nickel ground plane. Simulation findings indicate significant absorption across a wide spectral range of 160-1688 nm (177.5-1873.7 THz), with absorption consistently surpassing 90% and an average absorption of 97.7%. Remarkably, within the 246-522 nm (574.1-1218.7 THz) spectrum, the absorber attains above 99% absorption efficiency. The design has strong polarization insensitivity and angular stability, achieving more than 90% average absorption up to the angle of incidence of 50° and more than 85% at 60° over the whole spectral range. This metamaterial absorber, exhibiting a solar absorption efficiency of 96.7%, is a feasible alternative for advanced high-efficiency solar energy harvesting systems.

title: Simulation-Based Multi-Spectral UAV Framework for Real-Time Casualty Detection
authors: S. Sreevidhya, Roopa Chandrika R, Laxmi Raja, R. Sathish Kumar, M. Nivaashini, N.B. Mahesh Kumar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: In disaster environments, the rapid detection and localization of human casualties is crucial for time-sensitive search-and-rescue (SAR) missions. In this paper, we present an open-source, simulation-based autonomous UAV framework for casualty detection in the ROS and Gazebo environments, which integrates state-of-the-art computer vision with autonomy. The system utilizes two YOLO detection models in the pipeline: one trained on an RGB Gazebo-generated human dataset and another on a Roboflow infrared dataset, enabling multispectral detection. Through simulated occlusion and pose variation, the RGB model achieves 92.8% accuracy with real-time inference capabilities at 20 25 FPS and detects objects at multiple scales using PANet feature fusion on the CSPDarknet53 backbone. Our thermal model, utilizing the already optimized YOLOv5m structure for infrared feature detection, achieves 90.4% accuracy at a frame rate of 9 to 11 FPS, demonstrating resilience in low-light as well as visually degraded situations. The flight control of the UAV is handled by the PX4 firmware, integrated with ROS-MAVROS, which enables it to navigate autonomous waypoints, track velocity setpoints, and monitor states through MAVLink communication. For path planning, the proposed system utilizes an A* search algorithm to generate obstacle-aware routes. This maps the detected objects onto a real-time GPS track and publishes them as ROS topics for ground station monitoring. This framework provides a reproducible, end-to-end simulation testbed for integrated visual thermal casualty detection. It is designed for future deployment on physical UAV platforms to be tested in operational field validation of SAR.

title: Mental Health Disorder Classification using RF-PSO: a Hybrid Machine Learning Approach
authors: Ravita Chahar, Ashutosh Kumar Dubey
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Mental health disorders are increasingly prevalent and pose significant challenges in timely diagnosis and intervention. This study presents a hybrid classification framework combining random forest (RF) with particle swarm optimization (PSO) for enhanced mental health disorder classification. The RF-PSO model leverages the predictive strength of RF and the optimization capability of PSO to finetune key hyperparameters: number of estimators, maximum tree depth, and minimum samples required for node splitting. Preprocessing, data splitting, and cross-validation were conducted to ensure robust evaluation. PSO initializes a swarm of particles representing potential hyperparameter configurations, and iteratively updates positions using cognitive and social components to guide convergence. Fitness is evaluated via classification performance, and the global best configuration is used to train the final RF model. Results demonstrate superior classification accuracy, particularly in binary and three-class models. The model achieved 93% accuracy for stress (all-age group) and 86% for depression (20-30 age group). Additionally, the AUC for anxiety-depression classification reached 0.94, reflecting strong discriminative capability. Multi-class settings showed performance variations, with lower accuracy in five-class classification due to class overlap and increased complexity. The proposed RF-PSO approach offers an effective and computationally efficient solution for mental health disorder prediction, with strong generalization capabilities and relevance for decision support systems.

title: Adaptive Batch Normalization with Latency-Aware Edge Computing in Self-Organizing Networks for Optimizing IoT Performance in Critical Applications
authors: Pankaj Rahi, Sivaneasan Bala Krishnan, Udit Mamodiya, Prasun Chakrabarti
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in critical applications, such as healthcare and autonomous vehicles, necessitates ultra-low latency and high reliability. The rapid proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in critical applications such as healthcare, industrial automation, and smart cities necessitates efficient, low-latency, and adaptive computing paradigms. Traditional cloud-based architectures struggle with latency and bandwidth constraints, prompting a shift toward edge computing. However, optimizing deep learning models for edge environments remains challenging due to dynamic network conditions and computational limitations. This paper proposes an Adaptive Batch Normalization (AdaBN) technique integrated with Latency-Aware Edge Computing (LAEC) in Self-Organizing Networks (SONs) to enhance IoT performance. Our approach dynamically adjusts batch normalization parameters based on real-time network latency and computational load, improving model convergence and inference speed. We evaluate our framework using a real-world IoT dataset and demonstrate significant improvements in inference latency (up to 32% reduction) and model accuracy (5.8% increase) compared to conventional methods. The results highlight the effectiveness of our approach in critical IoT applications where reliability and real-time processing are paramount.

title: Multi-view Clustering using K-Means, Co-Regularized Spectral Clustering, and Multi-View Spectral Clustering
authors: Pankaj Kumar, Rashmi Agarwal
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This work performs a detailed evaluation of the three most commonly used Multi-View Clustering (MVC) techniques, namely Multi-View K-Means, Co-Regularized Spectral Clustering (Co-RegSC), and Multi-View Spectral Clustering (MVSC) on five different benchmark datasets, including Multi Features, BBCSport, ORL, MSRC-v5, and 3Sources. Its performance was evaluated in terms of clustering quality (ARI, AMI, NMI) and computational efficiency. Results reveal that MVSC outperforms others in clustering accuracy and that multi-view K-means is much faster to run at the cost of its performance. The Co-RegSC offers a balanced trade-off between accuracy and efficiency. Conclusions are drawn for both strengths and limitations of each algorithm and how the suitability of the methods may be considered in light of application needs. This research, moreover, highlights the need for scalable, robust, and adaptive clustering models in real-world multi-view data. We propose some future directions, including: improving the scalability of the MVC interface, handling incomplete views, integrating deep learning, developing standardized benchmarks to enable easy comparison of MVC-based approaches, and exploring hybrid approaches to strengthen the capability of MVC in resolving problems in various applications.

title: Real-time Fatigue Detection Using OpenCV and CNN
authors: Krish Dahake, Jay Pawar, Ansh Parate, Nitin Rakesh, Monali Gulhane, Akhil Gupta
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Fatigue and drowsiness are significant causes of human error in safety-critical industries such as transportation and manufacturing. Early detection is crucial to avoid accidents and provide safety. This paper introduces a real-time, software-based system that employs deep learning and computer vision to track user vigilance through a typical laptop webcam. The system captures and processes video frames using OpenCV to detect faces and segment eye regions for tracking user alertness. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) subsequently classifies eye states (open, closed, slightly sleepy, sleepy) from grayscale images, maximizing performance and minimizing computational burden. The system operates at 15 FPS on standard laptops without GPU support and reaches up to 98.6% accuracy in identifying signs of drowsiness, showing its potential for real-world, low-cost drowsiness monitoring.

title: Autonomous Crop Health Diagnostics and Precision Agrochemical Application Using Multimodal Sensor Fusion and On-Device CNN-Based Deep Learning
authors: Isha Das, Md. Jisan Ahmed, S M Nazmuz Sakib
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The suggested system offers a novel model for the automatic crop health diagnostics and the accuracy application of agrochemicals in the field that exploits the multimodal sensor fusion combined with on-device deep learning. By mixing data from various sources such as visual images, environmental sensors, and spectral data, the system is able to provide a holistic understanding of crop conditions in real-time. At the core of this method is the use of a CNN-based deep learning model implemented directly on edge devices, thus enabling local decision-making without the need for continuous cloud connection. This on-device intelligence not only enables quick detection of crop stress or disease but also allows targeted agrochemical intervention as a result of which the waste and environmental impact are minimized. Multiple sensor modality fusion assures better diagnostic accuracy while the deep learning model is fine-tuned for lightweight deployment, thus allowing the spread of the system in different agricultural environments. The proposed approach is pivotal in promoting sustainable, productive, and smart farming through the implementation of advanced sensor technology and AI-driven processing at the field level.

title: ResumeInsight: An AI-Driven Framework for Semantic Resume–Job Matching and Skill-Gap Analysis
authors: Dhruv Prakash Daberao, Gitesh Dalal, R. Sreemathy
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The exponential growth of online job applications has overwhelmed recruiters, with corporate job postings receiving over 250 applications on average. Traditional Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) rely on keyword-based matching, resulting in low recall, bias toward specific formats, and no candidate feedback. ResumeInsight integrates natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning (ML) to deliver semantic resume–job matching, skill-gap analysis, and actionable upskilling recommendations. Our system reads heterogeneous resume structures in PDF, isolates major sections through rule-based segmentation and text preprocessing (spaCy and regex), and measures skill similarity through Levenshtein distance. It builds a 7-dimensional feature vector that includes academic metrics and skill similarity and passes it to multiple machine learning models including Random Forest, XGBoost, and an Artificial Neural Network (ANN) for fit score prediction, with Random Forest selected for its superior performance. The choice of Random Forest, XGBoost, and ANN as our composite matching algorithm resulted from a deliberate evaluation of model suitability and task-specific constraints. We have done clustering using K-Means to identify resume–company pair patterns and tested the system on actual resumes and job profiles; our system exhibits good accuracy in skill tagging and fit prediction with quick runtime performance. Our system achieves 89.3% F1-score in skill extraction and nearly 75% accuracy in fit prediction. This work presents dataset preparation, model structure, algorithmic details, experimental findings, and a deployment roadmap moving towards resume classification as per the requirements of the company.

title: Simulation and Performance Analysis of Low-Power Circuits using Power Optimization Techniques
authors: Gowthami G R, Harish T, Deepa N P, Kumar P
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Reducing power consumption in very-large-scale integration (VLSI) circuits has become increasingly critical due to the rising demand for portable and energy-efficient electronic systems. In this study, a unified low-power design methodology is presented, integrating clock gating (CG) and power gating (PG) techniques to address both dynamic and leakage power reduction. Clock gating minimizes unnecessary switching activity by disabling inactive clock signals, whereas power gating disconnects the power supply from idle circuit blocks, thereby reducing leakage currents. For the transmission-gate (TG)–based D flip-flop, the application of clock gating decreased dynamic power from 12.5916 ȝW to 12.0121 ȝW, representing an improvement of approximately 4.6%. Similarly, for a conventional D flip-flop, the use of combined fine-grained and coarse-grained power gating reduced leakage power from 72.05 nW to 67.84 nW, yielding savings of approximately 5.8%. All simulations were performed in Cadence Virtuoso using both 45-nm and 90-nm CMOS technology nodes.

title: Machine Learning-Based Prediction of Nightmares in Children: A Pediatric Psychological Integration
authors: Lamia Islam, Md. Basim Al Zabir Shammo, Md. Rashed, Md. Imran Hossain, Md Akash Miya, Moumita Haque Mohona
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Identifying fear-related nightmares in children is particularly challenging, as young children often struggle to articulate their experiences or recall dream content. These nightmares, however, can severely disrupt sleep, hinder academic performance, and heighten psychological vulnerability, underscoring the need foraccurate and early prediction for effective intervention. This study explores various ML approaches, including LR, DT, SVM, KNN, NB, and LightGBM, to develop a robust predictive framework. The dataset undergoes comprehensive preprocessing, incorporating categorical encoding, numerical feature scaling, insufficient data handling, and the SMOTE to mitigate class imbalance. Each model is systematically trained and key performance indicators such as precision, recall, and F1 score were used for evaluation, with confusion matrices providing further insight into classification performance. Among the models evaluated, Light-GBM demonstrated the highest predictive performance, achieving an accuracy of 99.77%, a precision of 100%, a recall of 99.53%, and an F1 score of 99.77%, surpassing all other approaches. In this research, LightGBM is introduced as the proposed model, demonstrating superior predictive capability compared to alternative approaches and underscoring its effectiveness in predicting fear-related nightmares with high reliability. The study advances ongoing research efforts focused on machine learning applications in psychological assessment, offering a data-driven approach to early detection and facilitating timely mental health interventions.

title: A Predictive Control Framework for Enhanced Dynamic Performance and Thermal Management of xEV Applications
authors: Sreedhar Reddy Aemalla, Subbarao Mopidevi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Conventional control methods for electric vehicles (EVs) often rely on rule-based strategies that separately manage power flow, battery charging, and thermal regulation. These approaches face limitations such as slower response times, suboptimal regenerative braking efficiency, and inadequate thermal management, affecting battery life and vehicle safety. This work proposes a Model Predictive Control (MPC)-based framework that dynamically optimizes power distribution during driving cycles. By predicting future vehicle states, the framework adjusts control inputs in real time to improve acceleration response, enhance energy recovery, and maintain battery thermal stability. Key features include adaptive prediction accuracy, dynamic power adjustment, and multi-objective optimization focused on energy efficiency and safety. Compared to traditional controls, the proposed method reduces acceleration time from 2.9 to 2.5 seconds, lowers average battery and motor temperatures, and achieves higher voltage stability with less variability. Simulation-based validation demonstrates the predictive control’s superiority in managing regenerative braking, thermal regulation, and overall vehicle performance.

title: A Method for Online Multi-Sensor Error Identification in RINS
authors: Ziqi Wang, Jiangnan Ye, Lei Wang, Hong Zhang
abstract: To address the problem in rotational inertial navigation systems (RINS) where residual sensor calibration errors lead to limited alignment convergence speed and insufficient instantaneous output accuracy, this paper proposes a multi-sensor error online identification and compensation method based on state-expanded self-alignment. Through theoretical analysis, the propagation mechanism of sensor errors under rotational modulation is clarified. On this basis, the traditional self-alignment model is expanded, and a more complete measurement equation is constructed. The QR-RLS algorithm is employed to achieve high-precision online identification and decoupling of errors. Simulation and experimental results show that this method can effectively realize online identification of various sensor errors in RINS, reducing platform misalignment angle estimation oscillations by 70% during alignment and improving the alignment speed by more than 40%. Subsequent navigation experiments show that, after decoupling and compensating for various sensor errors using this method, the velocity and azimuth attitude oscillations within the rotation period are reduced by approximately 60% and 50%, respectively. This study provides a systematic technical approach for online identification of multi-sensor errors in RINS, while improving the convergence speed of alignment and the instantaneous output accuracy of the inertial navigation system, and has important engineering value for enhancing the performance of high-precision inertial navigation systems.

title: Secure and Scalable Real-Time Pension Payment Systems Using AI and Predictive Machine Learning Models
authors: Satish Kabade, Akshay Sharma, Anup Kagalkar, Bhushan Balkrishna Chaudhari
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Pension plans influence investment allocation, savings, and consumption, all of which have a significant economic effect. The long-term financial stability and wellbeing of communities are impacted by the low pension enrolment rate, which persists despite decreasing death rates and rising life expectancy. This research was carried out in order to investigate the possibility of using predictive modelling approaches to enhance the pension payout system. The current research proposes an AI-based web-based application that integrates machine learning with current web tools to effectively identify fraudulent pension payments. The system is based on React.js front-end, Node.js/Express.js back end, and Python-powered Extra Trees Classifier model. The process of sending and storing data occurs through secure transmissions and JWTprotected APIs and MongoDB (transparency/auditing). The propose Extra Tree Classifier (ETC) was trained on the Kerala Pension Fraud dataset, comprising and achieved the best performance with 91.22% accuracy, 89.31% precision, 93.66% recall, and an F1-score of 91.43%. The real-time predictions were installed through an intuitive dashboard that provides administrators alerts and detailed logs. The real-time detection of fraud during the pension payment system is based on the use of AI models that guarantee secure disbursement, system scalability, and an interactive interface. This system solution moved onwards with raising the bar of pension security as well as benchmarking super-intelligent, real-time financial transaction systems.

title: LLM-Guided Multi-Agent Collaboration for Complex Task Automation
authors: Vishal Bharadwaj Meruga
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The combination of multi-agent systems and Large Language Models (LLMs) has become an exciting new paradigm capable of the real-time automation of complex tasks. In this paper, we introduce a framework of LLM-Guided Multi-Agent Collaboration that utilizes LLMs as high-level reasoning engines to coordinate, optimize and adjust the interactions between multiple autonomous agents. The proposed framework builds on almost zero task decomposition and intent interpretation in multitasking and resolution of conflict that is provided by the natural language understanding and decision support capability of the LLMs. Agents that have domain-specific knowledge will work in cooperation, under LLM supervision to deliver an agent-centric task execution that is scalable, explainable and adaptive. We consider the framework against the disparate domains of process automation, data-driven decision-making, and cybersecurity incident response. In the experimental results, better efficiency, resource use, and flexibility were observed in respect to dedicated multi-agent models. This paper indicates the promise that LLM-based coordination may hold to turn multi-agent collaboration into a more intelligent, context-aware, and robust paradigm of automating complex tasks in the real world.

title: Enhanced Flight Delay Prediction using Supervised Machine Learning with Optimized SVM Technique
authors: Pratibha Patil, Arun Kumar Jhapate, Ritu Shrivastava, Rajiv Shrivastava
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Flight delays are a burning issue in an aviation industry, which causes growing costs, dissatisfaction of passengers, and logistic misfortunes. In this study, a supervised machine learning implementation of an optimized Support Vector Machine (SVM) method is proposed to predict flight delays with the aim to achieve a better prediction model of flight delays. A synthesis of a full structured combination of features that enhances the reliance of the model is comprehensively included in the study such as older flights information, weather conditions, air traffic congestion above the atmosphere, as well as the specifics of the airport. The SVM model is effective to give nonlinear relationship and complex patterns and trends exhibited by air flight delay phenomena using more sophisticated parameter tuning and kernel optimisation methods. The high accuracy accuracy, precision and recall rates of the optimized SVM model as compared to those of the traditional methods have been confirmed after conducting extensive experiments on very large size, real-world datasets. The model developed in the study will not only allow making predictions that are more accurate and timelier, but will also enable airlines, airport operators, and air traffic controllers to analyze delays and apply their proactive response plans. Finally, the entire research can be used in the creation of intelligent, data-driven decision support systems that are expected to optimize transportation operations and induce better passenger experiences in the more complicated and dynamic air transport environment.

title: Predicting Heart Disease with Machine Learning: A Comparative Analysis of Multiple Models
authors: Sonia Afroz, Md Munjurul Islam, Nikunj Sharma, Kanika Rawat, Tuhalika Saha, Rana Das, Sheikh Urvana Akter Mim, Mst Sanjida Alam, Md. Akkas Ali
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: While cardiovascular disease (CVD) remains a leading cause of global mortality, accurate predictive models that enable timely diagnosis and intervention are critical. This paper provides an overview of a selection of ML models used for cardiac disease prediction through a comparative study concerning the performance, as well as explainability. We evaluate several algorithms LR, RF, SVM, and DT using a well-known dataset publicly available, which includes clinical measurements. The models are assessed using standard performance metrics. The deep learning models that exceed baseline models have achieved as accuracy (85%, 81%, 81%, 80%), sensitivity (recall) and specificity (80%, 80%, 80%, 77%), precision (92%, 86%, 86%, 85%), F1-Score (86%, 83%, 83%, 81%), MSE (0.15, 0.19, 0.19, 0.20), and ROC-AUC (90%, 87%, 89%, 80%) for LR, RF, SVM, and DT models, respectively. LR achieved the highest accuracy of 85% and ROC-AUC score of 90%, proving to be very efficient in predicting nonlinear patterns and reducing misclassification errors. These results will highlight the positive and negative aspects of each model, where Random Forest and Neural Networks are superior in terms of accuracy and ROC-AUC, whilst LR yields simplicity and interpretability. The work dissects the complex interplay of model complexity and interpretability and highlights which methods are best for predicting cardiac disease in clinical settings. The work will eventually transform how AI-enabled diagnostic devices evolve and set the bar for further research and real-world adoption in health matters.

title: AI-Driven Prediction of Anxiety Disorders using Physiological and Behavioral Data Fusion
authors: Sakshi Thakur, Dinesh Singh Dhakar, Ruchika Gupta
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Anxiety is psychological disorder that affects individuals both mentally and physically, often causing emotional stress, sleep issues, and behavioral changes. Early detection of anxiety can help prevent long-term mental health problems. The current research presents Machine Learning (ML) strategy utilised for identifying whether person is experiencing anxiety by utilizing physiological information (includes heart rate and EEG) along with behavioral data (includes sleep patterns and phone usage). Several hybrid models is developed and tested, including SVM+CNN, RF+CNN, XGBoost+CNN, GA+CNN, and PSO+CNN. The PSO+CNN model attained superior accuracy of 99.35%. The proposed model is evaluated against traditional methods to assess performance. The proposed model is lightweight and real-time anxiety detection in practical applications for emotional health support for emotional health support and improved user experience outcomes and strategies.

title: A Novel EcoProtect Framework for Evaluating E-Waste Management Regulations and Safeguarding Public Health
authors: Varsha K.S, Anju Mohan, Murugan Ramu
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The improper disposal and mismanagement of electronic waste (e-waste) pose significant environmental and public health challenges, particularly for vulnerable populations such as children. This study proposes the EcoProtect Framework, a comprehensive regulatory evaluation and health risk assessment model, to analyze the impact of e-waste exposure on children's health. Using a combination of primary data collection (surveys, medical records, and toxicological analysis) and secondary data (government reports and regulatory policies), this study examines toxic exposure levels, pediatric health implications, waste disposal trends, and high-risk geographic zones. The proposed framework integrates AI-driven forecasting models to predict future e-waste generation and its impact on respiratory disorders, neurological impairments, and overall public health risks. The machine learning model used for health impact prediction achieved an accuracy of 91.2%, while the risk assessment model for high-risk e-waste hotspots yielded 87.6% precision. The findings indicate a direct correlation between informal e-waste processing and elevated toxicity levels in children, emphasizing the need for stricter enforcement, increased formal recycling adoption, and targeted healthcare interventions. The study concludes with policy recommendations to enhance sustainability, compliance, and community involvement in e-waste management sector.

title: Hate Speech Detection in the Sylheti Language A Machine Learning and Deep Learning Approach
authors: Abdul Wahide Shipu, Mayami Das Purkayasta Purba, Md Shamihul Islam Khan Limon, Nasif Istiak Remon
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Hate speech detection has gained significant attention nowadays. However, there aren’t many resources available for low-resource languages like Sylheti. This study explores hate speech detection in the Sylheti language with six incrementally sized datasets (491, 2105 and 3183 instances) in two configurations, with and without preprocessing. Sylheti is an Indo-Aryan language. Mostly spoken in Sylhet region of Bangladesh and parts of Northeast India. Due to limited resources, despite having a distinct linguistic identity it often gets overlooked in NLP research. This is the first study addressing hate speech detection in Sylheti using machine learning and deep learning models. To ensure high-quality data for training and evaluation, we manually collected and annotated Sylheti social media texts and develop a robust dataset. Preprocessing techniques included punctuation removal, stemming, and stopword removal. We compared deep learning models (RNN and CNN) with classical machine learning models (SVM, Decision Tree, Random Forest, KNN, Logistic Regression, Naïve Bayes, BernoulliNB, and GaussianNB). Across all datasets we found deep learning models consistently outperform classical methods. RNN achieved the highest F1-score of 0.80 on the smallest preprocessed dataset and 0.75 on the largest non-preprocessed dataset. KNN exhibited lower effectiveness but Logistic Regression and SVM performed competitively. Notably, preprocessing improved performance for small datasets but had mixed effects on larger datasets. This work highlights the impact of dataset size and preprocessing in hate speech detection. It also emphasizes the importance of developing NLP solutions for low-resource languages. Future work will explore other preprocessing techniques and hybrid modeling approaches to significantly improve performance in Sylheti hate speech detection.

title: Intrusion Detection System (IDS) for Power System Control Networks Using Ensemble Learning
authors: Md. Showrov Hossen, Md.Taukir Ahmed, Mst.Suraiya Sultana, Md.Jamal Ali
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The integration of digital communication technologies into traditional power infrastructure has transformed power system control networks into highly connected, intelligent environments. Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems and Industrial Control Systems (ICS) form the backbone of smart grids, enabling real-time data exchange, remote monitoring, and automated control.
