However, their dependence on legacy protocols such as Modbus and DNP3—designed without strong cybersecurity—has exposed them to rising cyber threats, including false data injection, replay attacks, command spoofing, and denial-of-service attacks. Traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS), primarily rule- or signature-based, often fail to identify novel or subtle attacks in these evolving environments, driving the need for adaptive and intelligent solutions. This paper introduces a lightweight, scalable IDS framework specifically for power system control networks, utilizing ensemble learning to improve detection accuracy and resilience. The system combines Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) classifiers, aggregating their outputs to enhance generalization and minimize false alarms. Extracted network traffic features—packet headers, function codes, transmission patterns, and session behaviors—are preprocessed and used to train and validate the model on SCADA/ICS datasets. Results show the ensemble IDS adapts effectively to evolving attack vectors and suits deployment in resource-constrained environments, common in developing regions with limited cybersecurity capabilities. By embedding intelligent threat detection into SCADA communication layers, this research advances cyber-resilient power systems. The proposed IDS strengthens critical infrastructure security by providing an accurate, adaptive, and scalable defense against emerging cyber threats in modern power networks.

title: ABHAYA - Secure Communication Device for Women Safety
authors: E. Priya, Madhumitha Dasarathy, Kiruthika S, Madhushri L
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This paper introduces a compact and wearable safety device designed to enhance personal security through discreet, real-time communication and evidence gathering during critical emergencies. The device is activated by a simple triple-tap gesture, which triggers simultaneous audio and video recording while transmitting distress signals via radio frequency or Wi-Fi. This feature ensures functionality and connectivity even in areas with weak or no signal. To protect sensitive information, the device includes encrypted storage on an SD card. It continuously captures audio and video, providing vital evidence in emergency scenarios. With its portable and user-friendly design, the device is powered by a rechargeable lithium polymer battery. This innovative technology combines advanced features with ease of use, delivering a reliable, practical, and effective solution for individuals in high-risk situations, thereby enhancing personal safety and security.

title: A FixMatch-Driven Semi-Supervised Learning Framework for Diabetic Foot Ulcer Detection
authors: Monir Ahmad, Tanjim Mahmud, Abubokor Hanip, Mohammad Shahadat Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Diabetic Foot Ulcers (DFUs) are one of the most severe and common complications among individuals with diabetes. DFU often leads to prolonged hospitalization, amputation, and increased mortality if left untreated. Early and accurate detection of DFUs is vital for timely intervention and reducing the burden on healthcare systems. However, traditional clinical diagnosis relies heavily on expert evaluation, which can be subjective, resource-intensive, and inconsistent across settings, especially in under-resourced regions. While machine learning methods have shown promise in automating DFU identification, their effectiveness is largely dependent on the availability of large, annotated datasets, which is both costly and impractical in real-world clinical environments. Supervised learning models struggle to generalize well when faced with limited labeled samples and diverse wound appearances. To address these limitations, we investigate the use of FixMatch in this study. This semi-supervised learning technique effectively combines a small set of labeled images with a larger pool of unlabeled data through consistency training and pseudo-label generation. By leveraging unlabeled DFU images, the proposed framework minimizes the dependence on manual annotation while retaining strong predictive capabilities. Initially, several supervised models were evaluated, and ResNet-50 was found to offer the most robust baseline performance. Further analysis across multiple unlabel-to-label ratios showed that a 97:03 split enabled FixMatch to achieve an impressive F1 score of 99.53% on the DFU classification task. Moreover, the system was able to confidently assign pseudo labels to 93.65% of the unlabeled samples using a threshold of 0.95. We provide an interactive demo of our model through a public Hugging Face Space: https://huggingface.co/spaces/MonirAhmad/DFU-FixMatch.

title: A Comparative Evaluation of YOLOv7, Faster R-CNN and RetinaNet on MS COCO Dataset
authors: Gautam Arjun Dematti, Uttam Patil, Sushiladevi B. Vantamuri, Anjanabhargavi Kulkarni, Vidyadhar Hanji, Abhishek Gayakwad
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This paper presents an end-to-end approach to object detection using the YOLOv7 architecture, evaluated on the MS COCO dataset. MS COCO offers over 200,000 annotated images spanning 80 object categories, serving as a comprehensive benchmark for real-world detection tasks. A pre-trained YOLOv7 model is fine-tuned and assessed alongside two widely used object detection models—Faster R-CNN and RetinaNet. Comparative performance is analyzed using standard evaluation metrics, including mean Average Precision (mAP) at IoU thresholds ranging from 0.50 to 0.95. The results demonstrate that YOLOv7 consistently outperforms the other models across most object categories, offering a superior balance of speed and accuracy. This study confirms YOLOv7’s effectiveness in real-time object detection on MS COCO dataset.

title: High-Resolution Synchronous FPGA-Based Digital Pulse-Width Modulators for Precision Control Systems
authors: Prasann D Kulkarni, Mahantesh Laddi, Sanmati Bedakihale, Basavarajeshwari Nadgoudra
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Researchers have conducted substantial studies on system performance stabilization and dynamics through modern design approaches because semiconductor and microprocessor manufacturing needs to be combined with digital implementation growth. Digital control is increasingly chosen over analog counterparts within PWM converters because it shows three key advantages, including environmental robustness and high-speed microprocessor accessibility alongside versatile design options. The development of more straightforward digitally controlled converters has become possible through improvements in field-programmable gate arrays (FPGA). Every power electronic system heavily relies on digital controls instead of analog controls because digital controls execute complex tasks in a superior manner. Digital controls demonstrate select superiority toward analog controls in every power electronic application due to their ability to execute complex tasks, enhancing converter dynamic performance and power conversion efficiency. Through their implementation, they deliver protected component functions alongside digital control methods and enhance the capabilities of loop upgrades. Performance suffers because controllers experience higher power consumption and control loop delay overhead. New research aims to develop improved digital control systems that achieve higher performance efficiency with optimal overshoot and undershoot response and fast settling times throughout transient reactions.

title: Evaluation of Compact Blockchain Protocols to Embedded Systems: Analyzing Control of Access and Safe Recording to Data from Medical Devices
authors: V.S.Prakash, Nettem Adithya Valli, V.Samuthira Pandi, Hebatullah Awwad, Vemuri Nitin, M. Dinesh
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The swift expansion of embedded systems in the health sector, especially due to the pervasive use of IoT-enabled medical devices, has posed new challenges for securing the patient data while maintaining accessibility and operational efficacy, simultaneously. Blockchain and smart contracts technologies generally have their main focus on security and immutability, though they are not for every application, in terms of resource- constraint devices such as embedded systems—error: Language not defined., because of the computational and energy efficiency of them. In this work, we provide a thorough analysis of minimalist blockchain protocols designed for embedded systems, with a particular emphasis on their suitability for access control and secure data logging in the context of medical devices. By providing a rigorous treatment of lightweight consensus protocols, ledger size reduction, and cryptography optimization, we investigate the behavior of such efficient protocols when made to operate under the scarcity of computational power, storage, and energy resources. Experimental results are presented with benchmark data sets and simulated medical data streams from the sensors, analyzing for latency, throughput, access control granularity and integrity of the data, as well as the power usage. It turns out that these so-called lightweight blockchain schemes such as DPoS, PBFT, customised DAG, are applicable for integration to an embedded medical device albeit with minor degradation in security/efficiency. The work also introduces a hybrid protocol architecture to make trade-off between decentralized-isation and computability feasibility, so that the system can achieve tamper-evident logging and finegrained access control. The results indicate that compact blockchain protocols present a valid approach of satisfying both data security and system latency requirements in contemporaneous medical devices, thus facilitating the development of trusted real-time healthcare applications.

title: Enhanced Weather Forecasting Using Time-Series and Image Sequence Fusion with LSTM and MobileNetV2
authors: Mahesh M, Devi. T, N. Deepa
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Weather forecasting systems play vital roles in agriculture, transportation, energy and emergency management, but traditional Numerical Weather Prediction (NWP) methods suffer from high computational costs, processing delays, and limited local accuracy. While artificial intelligence shows promise for improving weather predictions through historical data analysis, our previous work combining single weather images with meteorological data using CNNs failed to capture temporal weather patterns effectively. This research presents an improved approach using dual data streams: sequential weather imagery and time-series meteorological measurements. Our framework applies TimeDistributed MobileNetV2 for image sequence processing and LSTM networks for extracting temporal patterns from temperature, humidity, and pressure data. Results show substantial improvements over the baseline model's 4.5% precision, 20% recall, 7.5% F1-score, and 19% accuracy, with the enhanced system achieving 13% precision, 30% recall, 19.5% F1-score, and 27.5% accuracy across all metrics. The system provides probabilistic forecasts for precipitation and storms within 48-hour windows using recent weather patterns, plus visualization tools for intuitive result interpretation. Future enhancements include real-time satellite integration, mobile deployment, and additional meteorological variables, making this framework suitable for modern weather prediction needs.

title: Digital Intelligent World: From Data-Driven AI to Knowledge-Enabled Intelligent Agents
authors: Xiaohu Ge, Zhiwei Chen, Litao Yan, Yang Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Although the latest artificial intelligence technologies can greatly improve work efficiency by automatically generating feasible solutions in the digital world (DW), they are incapable of discovering or creating new knowledge, i.e., lack of human intelligence or creativity. To break this limitation, this article describes and elaborates the masterplan of the digital intelligent world (DIW), wherein everyone has an intelligent agent (IA) for searching, exchanging, and processing information and knowledge autonomously. First, a data-information-knowledge-intelligence (DIKI) model is proposed to illustrate the challenges of creating intelligence from raw data, and of realizing the DIW from the DW. Specifically, the DIW adopts knowledge-driven approaches and could achieve huge productivity enhancement through cross-domain innovations and deep intelligentization with broader creativity. Second, at the individual level, a knowledge processing architecture of IA is defined to support knowledge-centric operations and services. Third, at the system level, a framework of knowledge market (KM) is established for fair, effective, and autonomous collaborations among massive IAs. Inspired by basic laws in statistical thermodynamics, information sciences, and economics, three fundamental principles are developed and discussed for guaranteeing a prosperous KM and the sustainable DIW.

title: Efficient Two-Way Cascode Doherty PA for RF Applications
authors: Chetan C Chowdhary, Runa Kumari, Harish Dixit
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel design of a 2-way Doherty Power Amplifier (PA) optimized for satellite communication systems. The architecture combines the switching class PA framework with the cascode technique to enhance efficiency, linearity, and power handling capabilities, particularly at high frequencies. The use of cascode topology improves gain and stability, while the class F configuration ensures high power-added efficiency (PAE) and extended linear output power. This hybrid design addresses the demanding requirements of modern satellite communication by offering a reliable and high-performance RF solution. Comprehensive simulations and experimental evaluations demonstrate the practicality and effectiveness of the proposed amplifier, making it a promising candidate for advanced communication networks. An amplifier with a gain of 12 dB achieves a power-added efficiency (PAE) of 73% while delivering an output power of 10 dBm, showcasing high efficiency at moderate output levels..

title: Design and Development of a Reinforcement Learning Approach for Robot Navigation in 2D Even-Terrain Environments
authors: Ashish Avasthi, Somnath H. Kadhane, K. Avinash Babu, Lavanya R., Saurabh Chandra, Manjunath T.C.
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: To this end, in the following, we provide a comprehensive review on their work on the use of developed reinforcement learnings (RL) in robotic structures, with particular emphasis on the navigation in complex environment tasks. The objectives are to improve the adaptability, efficiency, and generalization of robots. The paper reviews RL fundamentals, focusing on the robot navigation aspect, and reviews the different RL algorithms, including DRL. It investigates aspects, including state representation, action space, reward shaping, and the fusion of sensor and map information or Simultaneous Localizations & Mappings (SLAM) to endow the robot with more adequate perception within the reinforcement learning setting. The paper covers adaptation of RL to realistic and challenging task settings like dynamic obstacles and unknown terrains, and evidence from 4 case studies and 4 experiments showing how the method works in practice. The conclusion discusses the current challenges, future directions of research ad possible advances, with the intent to provide a comprehensive reference for the researcher & practitioner who wish to employ RL to improve autonomous robot’s navigation passage in complex & dynamic environment.

title: FinLuxNet: A Hybrid Deep Learning Model for Analyzing the Impact of Luxury Lifestyles on Financial Health Among IT Professionals
authors: A. Sivanandam, Charulathaa Yathev, Yazhini K, Shri Vidhushini Chandrasekaran, B. Tharani
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: FinLuxNet represents a state-of-the-art hybrid deep learning model which analyzes the financial health impacts of luxury lifestyles on IT professionals working in Chennai. To understand how luxury consumption affects professional financial health specifically among IT workers it becomes essential as their consumption habits increase. The model combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for pattern recognition in financial spending data together with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks which analyze time sequences of financial behavior patterns. Through this combined methodology the model tracks both immediate and lasting patterns in finances to predict stability levels that result from different lifestyle choices effectively. Training and testing estableshments use a diverse combination of financial variables that contains income information along with costs and savings levels as well as investments and credit utilization and luxury product consumption data. The dataset quality improves through preprocessing procedures which execute data cleaning and outlier detection as well as feature engineering steps to achieve better predictive results. The model shows outstanding performance by reaching 97.2% training accuracy and 97.0% validation accuracy as well as 96.9% test accuracy. The model functions effectively for classification due to its precise and significant scoring ability for financial stability categories. This model serves both to predict financial stability and provide customized advice about saving and investment optimization to help users maintain stability while controlling their luxury spending habits.

title: ML-Based Optimization of Image Rejection Ratio in Gilbert Cell Mixer
authors: Pradeep Kumar S, Arvind S, Jatin Sourav A S, Azra Jeelani, Mahesh Kumar, Keerthi Kumar M
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This project explores how artificial intelligence can be used to improve the Image Rejection Ratio (IRR) in RF communication systems, with a special focus on the Gilbert Cell Mixer. We start by simulating the mixer using LTspice and analyzing its performance with MATLAB’s Harmonic Balance (HB) analysis. A passive notch filter is added to help remove unwanted image signals, and we capture the initial IRR values to set a benchmark for later improvements. What makes this approach unique is how it brings together circuit simulation, machine learning, and a web-based interface to automatically fine-tune the system. Key performance indicators—like before-and-after IRR values, Q factor, and notch frequency—are extracted in JSON format and displayed in a user-friendly dashboard. In one example, we saw IRR improve from 20 dB to 52 dB in satellite communication setups—an impressive 160. The whole setup is designed to be modular, meaning it can be adapted to different applications with very little extra work. Looking ahead, there’s potential to make it even smarter with real-time signal processing, AI-powered predictions for filter settings, and automatic, on-the-fly adjustments—making this system ideal for next-gen RF designs that demand both intelligence and flexibility.

title: Enhancing Dietary Tracking Through AI: A CNN-Based Food Recognition and Nutrition Estimation System
authors: Kanukula Anshu, Yugandhar Manchala, Battula Bhoomika Naga Sai, Mungi Vverendhra Kumar, Nirmal Keshari Swain, Tirumala Keshav
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Food classification using deep learning has gained significant attention due to its applications in dietary tracking, meal planning, and nutrition analysis. This paper presents a deep learning-based food recognition system that utilizes a fine-tuned ResNet50 convolutional neural network (CNN) to classify food items from images. The system integrates an API to retrieve recipe details and applies a nutritional database to estimate caloric and macronutrient values. The model is trained on the Food-101 dataset, achieving high classification accuracy across 101 food categories. A Streamlit-based web application is developed to provide an interactive interface, allowing users to upload images, view top-5 food predictions, and access nutrition details with adjustable serving sizes. Experimental results demonstrate the efficiency of our approach in automating dietary assessment, making it useful for nutrition tracking, meal planning, and healthcare applications. This study highlights the potential of AI-driven dietary monitoring systems for improving food recognition and nutritional analysis.

title: Spectral Clustering & Toeplitz-ADMM for MRI Reconstruction
authors: KotlaVennela, Singam Bhavesh, S Shali
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: In this paper, a novel method for acceleration in the Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is proposed, which integrates the spectral graph-based K-space clustering into the reconstruction of Toeplitz-accelerated Alternating Directive of Multipliers (ADMM) under the assumption of sparsity in the wavelet domain. To address the issue of long MRI scan duration, in the methodology proposed, the K-space magnitude is cast as a graph and rather than being rendered as a sequence of numbers, nodes are frequency components, and the weights of the edges are forms of local intensity correlations. The spectral clustering using the graph Laplacian eigenvectors enables pinpointing the regions that are significant in the context of structure, and, consequently, could be divided with the help of K-means. Subsequently, heuristically optimizing this by Kirchhoff-style techniques, it gives an oversampling data-driven adaptive under-sampling mask. The data recovered is the masked data done by using iterative soft thresholding in the wavelet domain using ADMM, which leads to a fast convergence using Toeplitz preconditioning. The stable increase in quality is proved by quantitative results, and the mentioned pipeline will make it possible to achieve Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) 32.81 dB and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) 0.8854, and the computations will also require less time, 463.10 seconds. The outcome reflects the usefulness of graph-theoretic sampling and structured optimization combination in offering a most satisfactory strategy that has entire interpretability and extremely advantageous utilization in the high-quality construction of magnetic resonance imagery with aggressive under-sampling.

title: A Solar-Powered IoT System for Real-Time Water Quality Monitoring Using LoRa and Machine Learning
authors: Jabed Shariar, Reshad Rayhan, Un Nafeu Rohit, H M Rahad Ahammed, Tama Fouzder
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: In many areas in rural, disaster-prone and tourist of Bangladesh, clean and safe drinking water supply is seriously hampered — specially during the time of floods, power outages and disruption of communication facilities. The above investigative study has discussed an economically sophisticated, efficient and smart solar driven water quality monitoring system using IoT (LoRa) Technology. Several sensors and an ESP32 microcontroller are used to collect sensor data from the environment to cloud platforms. Field testing verified long-range data delivery at RSSI levels of –85 dBm or greater over 200 meters, even in harsh environments. Six machine learning models were assessed with water quality classification — Decision Tree, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors and SVM. The best performance with Decision Tree and Random Forest was in obtaining a 99.1% accuracy, which was also able to achieve precision, recall, and F1 scores of above 0.99. An easy-to-use web-based dashboard was also developed to display live sensor readings, current solar status, and machine learning (ML) water quality predictions, and to send real-time alerts when contamination is detected. This approach was tested in field trials conducted in remote offline areas, which validated that this system is universal, easily scalable and can be implemented on top of existing public health networks to help the environment and be economically viable.

title: Forearm Orientation Invariant Hand Gesture Recognition using sEMG and Feature Driven Machine Learning
authors: Umme Rumman, Tapodhir Karmakar Taton, Arifa Ferdousi, Md. Johirul Islam, Shamim Ahmad
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Achieving reliable hand gesture recognition using surface electromyography (sEMG) across varying forearm orientations remains a major challenge in the development of robust human–machine interfaces. This study explores the impact of feature engineering, classifier selection, and training strategies on orientation-invariant gesture recognition. We evaluated five feature extraction methods and three classical machine learning classifiers using a custom sEMG dataset recorded across pronation, rest, and supination orientations. Results show that feature type and training orientation have a significant influence on performance, while classifier choice has a minimal effect. Signal Normalized Time Domain Features (SNTDF) consistently outperformed other features, and Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA) proved to be an effective and stable classifier. Training with multiple orientations significantly improved generalization, with the highest F1 score (97.78%) achieved when using all orientations. Deep learning models were also tested, but classical approaches with engineered features delivered superior accuracy. These findings offer practical insights for designing orientation-invariant, sEMG-based control systems for wearable and assistive technologies.

title: Blockchain and Federated Learning for Safe Collaborative Machine Learning Systems with Differential Privacy
authors: N.Padmashri, V.V.Karthikeyan, P.Sumathi, Santhosh Kumar Potnuru, P B Aswathy, V.Baby
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is a way to train a shared model using data from many different devices or users without actually sharing the raw data itself. This helps protect privacy and makes the process more efficient by reducing the need to move large amounts of data. However, FL still has some security risks, such as model inversion attacks, where someone tries to guess the original data, and poisoning attacks, where bad data is introduced to mess up the model. Also, if the person or system that combines the models isn't trusted, it could be a problem. This paper introduces a new system that combines Differential Privacy and Blockchain technology into the FL process. Differential Privacy adds a little bit of random noise to the model updates so that individual data points can't be guessed. Blockchain keeps a secure, transparent record of all the model changes and verifies who is involved. Testing this system on standard datasets shows that it makes the model more resistant to attacks without significantly lowering its performance. This setup is especially useful in areas like healthcare, finance, and smart cities, where keeping data private and secure is very important.

title: EEG-Based Analysis and Recognition of Human Brain Activity Post Yoga and Meditation Using Vision Transformer with Residual Temporal Convolution Network
authors: Bandari Ramaraju, Ravichander Janapati, Sreedhar Kollem
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Although improvement in brain functionality through yoga practices and related interventions have been documented, the mechanisms responsible for these improvements are largely unexplored. Functional changes in the brain are highly caused by changes in neural activity and structural dynamics within the brain. Understanding the brain-related changes through the recognition model can lead to improved mental function and significantly designing enhancement strategies for both healthy individuals and clinical populations is essential. Therefore, this study aims to propose specific neural signal variations through Electroencephalogram (EEG) signal analysis that are associated with yoga and meditation practices that may contribute to enhancing mental health and overall well-being, particularly. The proposed model leverages the advanced deep learning approaches facilitating accurate recognition of neural patterns. The EEG signals gathered from the database are initially converted into spectrogram images, by applying the Short Time Fourier Transform (STFT). The frequency components in the signal as represented by the spectrogram are sent to the proposed Vision Transformer with Residual Temporal Convolution Network (ViT-Res-TCN). The Vision transformer helps in capturing the global context and offers better scalability for large datasets like EEG medical datasets. Further residual connections with TCN mitigate the vanishing gradients and engage in deeper training. After processing the spectrograms through ViT with Res-TCN, the network can show the changes in brain activity and track the impact of yoga and meditation. Extensive experiments show the effect of the developed ViT with Res-TCN in human brain activity recognition against state-of-the-art techniques.

title: Enhancement of Cybersecurity with Machine Learning: Applying Ensemble Classification and Anomaly Detection Approaches to Find Zero-Day Attacks in Network Traffic
authors: Paddabbai Chowdari Karanam, Sathiyandrakumar Srinivasan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Traditional security measures are faced with increasing challenges as cyber attacks become more sophisticated, including detecting and responding to zero-day attacks – vulnerabilities that are new and have not been previously reported or patched. This class of threat presents a significant threat to enterprise networks, government networks, and critical infrastructure because it is stealthy and unpredictable. In this study, we investigate whether machine learning methods, in particular, ensemble classification algorithms with anomaly detection approaches can be effective in the context of cybersecurity to detect zero-day attacks in aggregated network traffic flow data. The approach introduces hybrid model which is the combination of the ensemble-based classifiers like Random Forest, Gradient Boosting Machines (GBM), and Extra Trees and unsupervised anomaly detection algorithms, such as Isolation Forest, One-Class SVM and AutoEncoders. The ensemble classifiers are trained on the labeled data to capture the known attack behaviors, while the anomaly detectors check the deviation from the normal behavior and thus detect the possible zero-day attacks. This approach allows high detection rates of both known and unknown attacks with low false positives. For the fast and efficient processing of the high speed and high-volume traffic observed in real world scenarios the framework includes real-time processing functionalities for feature extraction based on flow metadata (packet payload, duration, source/destination ports, protocol type, and connection status). A feature selection pipeline tunes input parameter for classifiers and cuts computational load. Sliding window and incremental learning moves additionally improve system’s capability to adapt to the dynamic nature of the traffic. The benchmarks are based on commonly known datasets such as CICIDS2017 and UNSW-NB15. The models are evaluated by performance metrics such as precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC. The experiments results show that the hybrid method, especially that detecting stealthy or novel attack vectors, outperforms independent models. Ensemble learning is used for robustness (diversity) and anomaly detection (which captures rare behaviors that conventional classifiers fail to classify), and the application of the approach proposed here lays foundation for intelligent, adaptive, and preemptive cyber security paradigms. With a unified architecture of supervised learning and unsupervised learning, it solves the thorny issue of zero-day attack detection. The introduced model has practical scalability for deployment in diverse enterprise and distributed cloud networks. As such, it is a potential avenue for future AI-based security systems, attempting to defend against current and evolving threats in a highly dynamic cyber terrain.

title: Energy Efficiency Optimization of RIS-Enhanced SWIPT Systems under HPA Nonlinearity
authors: Yike Zheng, Jie Tang, Ruoyan Ma, Beixiong Zheng, Xiaokai Song, Nan Zhao, Kai-Kit Wong
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) have emerged as a crucial technology for making sustainable and green communication in future sixth-generation (6G) networks. By adaptively adjusting the wireless environment, RIS facilitates flexible control over signal propagation. When RIS is integrated into a simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) system, it can improve energy efficiency (EE) and link reliability. This enables low-power internet-of-things (IoT) devices to achieve continuous energy acquisition while maintaining reliable data access. This capability aligns well with the increasing demands of future 6G networks for enhancing EE and achieving ubiquitous connectivity. Motivated by this, we investigate a RIS-assisted multi-user SWIPT system that accounts for the nonlinear characteristics of the high power amplifier (HPA) at the transmitting end and the nonlinearity of the energy harvesting (EH) circuits at the receivers. These hardware imperfections pose major challenges for system modeling and optimization, particularly under stringent power limitations and quality of service (QoS) requirements, where improving overall EE is a key objective. Therefore, we proposed a joint optimization framework that simultaneously designs the access point (AP) beamforming, the RIS reflection coefficients, and the power splitting (PS) ratios at the receivers. The resulting problem is non-convex, with strong coupling among these variables. To tackle this issue, we propose an efficient alternating optimization (AO) framework. The fractional EE objective is handled using the Dinkelbach method, while each subproblem is iteratively convexified via successive convex approximation (SCA) and semi-definite relaxation (SDR) algorithms. Simulation results reveal that the proposed AO approach achieves substantial EE gains and confirm that integrating RIS conspicuous boosts the overall system EE performance.

title: Safety-Aware Optimal Control With Language-Guided Online Parameter Adjustment via Large Language Models
authors: Sanghon Song, Dongyeop Kang, Chan-eun Park
abstract: In this paper, we present LaMPC-CBF, a language-guided control framework that integrates model predictive control (MPC) with a control barrier function (CBF) to generate provably safe trajectories from free-form user instructions for robotic systems. Large language models translate natural language, including varying levels of user-expressed safety intent, into optimization objectives and CBF-based safety constraints. In particular, the expressed safety intent is mapped to numerical safety parameters within the CBF constraints, which directly govern the system’s obstacle avoidance behavior. This automated parameterization bridges the gap between abstract human linguistic expressions and rigorous mathematical safety guarantees, ensuring that the robot’s behavior aligns precisely with the user’s perceived safety preferences. Additionally, LaMPC-CBF supports interactive user feedback during task execution. This allows users to redefine safety intents via natural language on the fly, eliminating the need for controller recompilation. This approach allows non-experts to adjust parameters without manual tuning by experts easily and enables online adaptation to evolving user’s safety intent. Simulation results demonstrate that LaMPC-CBF improves safety and adaptability compared to the baseline, particularly in dynamic obstacle scenarios.

title: IEEE Sensors Council
authors: N/A

title: DApp Scheduling for Hybrid Computing in Edge Web 3.0: A Reinforcement Learning Framework with Heterogeneous Graph Neural Networks_supp1-3662425.pdf
authors: Lixing Chen
abstract: In the evolving landscape of Web 3.0, deploying and scheduling decentralized applications (DApps) presents significant challenges due to the complexity of heterogeneous nodes, edges, and their intricate interactions. Traditional approaches, particularly graph-based reinforcement learning (RL) methods, often rely on homogeneous graphs, which fail to capture the diverse relationships inherent in heterogeneous Web 3.0 environments. This limitation results in inefficient resource allocation, suboptimal task scheduling, and unclear security requirements. To address these issues, this paper introduces the Heterogeneous Graph Deployment Scheduler (HGDS), a novel framework that leverages Heterogeneous Graph Neural Networks (HGNNs) to model users, edge servers, and DApp tasks within Web 3.0 environments, and incorporates RL to optimize DApp scheduling policies. HGDS captures heterogeneity in Web 3.0 environments by jointly modeling node–edge interactions and heterogeneous edge relationships, enabling the generation of dynamic, task-aware embeddings that integrate both node and edge features. RL is further employed to adaptively optimize scheduling and resource allocation based on real-time network feedback. Experiments on a Web 3.0 testbed show that HGDS outperforms baseline methods by 12.2% in reward, while reducing service delay and gas consumption.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A

title: Experimental Demonstration of Eavesdropping Localization Based on Physics-Informed Neural Network
authors: Wenshuai Qin, Xiaoxue Gong, Weigang Hou, Lu Gan, Yuxin Xu, Lei Guo
abstract: This paper proposes a physics-informed neural network (PINN) scheme for localizing bending eavesdropping in coherent optical communication systems. First, we establish a signal transmission model under bending eavesdropping based on the Manakov equations to analyze its impact on physical information such as linear birefringence and nonlinearity. Subsequently, a PINN is developed by incorporating physical information such as linear birefringence and nonlinear effects into a convolutional neural network-bidirectional long short-term memory (CNN-BiLSTM) architecture. To validate the effectiveness of the scheme, we construct an experimental platform for bending eavesdropping localization in an 80 km, 168 Gbps quadrature phase shift keying (QPSK) coherent optical communication system. Polarization data during eavesdropping are collected from nine positions with bending radii of 10.8 mm and 15 mm. The performance of three models, including CNN, CNN-BiLSTM and PINN is evaluated for bending eavesdropping localization under both bending radii. Experimental results demonstrate that the PINN achieves localization accuracies of 100% and 99.8% under bending radii of 10.8 mm and 15 mm, respectively. This work offers a novel theoretical framework and methodological approach for localizing bending eavesdropping in optical fiber communication systems.

title: Management and Optimization of xApp and Near-Real-Time RIC Deployment in the Edge
authors: Juan Luis Herrera, Sofia Montebugnoli, Paolo Bellavista, Luca Foschini
abstract: O-RAN enables shared RAN Intelligent Controllers (RICs) hosting multiple xApps from different vendors, reducing operational costs. However, growing open-source RIC ecosystems face significant compatibility challenges, necessitating effective management of RIC deployment, xApp orchestration, and automated resource allocation strategies in the edge cloud continuum. We introduce MARRAS (Management and Allocation of Resources, RICs, and xAppS), a framework automating management and resource allocation in O-RAN environments. We formally define the underlying optimization problem and present a Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP)-based solver. Results demonstrate MARRAS’s efficacy in enhancing O-RAN deployment operability and efficiency.

title: Comparative Analysis of Loss Characteristics in DCCTs at Cryogenic Temperature with HTS and Copper Compensation Windings
authors: Jin Lu, Pai Peng, Jianye Chen, Shutong Deng, Shengnan Zou, Yawei Wang, Yue Zhao, Zhijian Jin
abstract: Accurate condition monitoring of high-temperature superconducting (HTS) cables in ultra-high-field magnets requires precise current measurement at cryogenic temperatures. Direct-Current Current Transformer (DCCT), a current measuring device based on zero-flux principle, is widely used as a high-precision measurement scheme. However, commercial DCCTs employing copper compensation coils exhibit significant operational losses at high currents, limiting their performance and range in low-temperature environments. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes a conceptual design of an HTS compensated DCCT, an innovative approach to cryogenic current sensing. A comparative analysis of the loss characteristics between conventional copper and the proposed HTS compensation coil is conducted through finite element method (FEM) simulations. The results demonstrate that the copper coil generates losses 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> to 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> times greater than the HTS coil under high-current conditions. Consequently, the HTS compensated DCCT achieves a superior current range of up to 70 kA at 20 K, with virtually no steady-state loss and significantly reduced ramping loss. These findings confirm that the minimal loss characteristics of the HTS winding establish a promising solution for high-precision, high-current measurement in cryogenic applications.

title: Smart Monitoring of Partial Discharges in Power Apparatus Using UHF Sensor
authors: Krishna Chaitanya Ghanakota, Vishal Ashokan, Sarathi Ramanujan, Kavitha Arunachalam
abstract: Partial Discharge (PD) activity is a pre-cursor for insulation degradation which may eventually lead to catastrophic failure of power apparatus with severe social and economic consequences. Electromagnetic emissions gathered over ultra-high frequency (UHF) range (0.5 to 3 GHz) during PD events is widely investigated for health monitoring of high voltage (HV) apparatus. However, large bandwidth of PD induced UHF signals requires high speed digitizer and large storage space which have restricted field deployment of UHF technique for online PD detection. In this work, we present UHF based PD pulse detection framework for remote monitoring and logging of faults in dielectric insulation using ThingSpeakTM cloud. PD events captured by a calibrated UHF sensor from HV PD test cell using the proposed online PD detection framework indicated throughput as high as 83.8% compared with reference UHF sensor measurements gathered by a digital storage oscilloscope. PD detection throughput was observed to be limited by the envelope detection threshold.Vin (10 mV) and cumulative propagation delay tpd (∼1 μs) in the architecture implementation. The proposed UHF sensing technique is low cost compared to conventional approaches, detects a wide range of insulation defects, and scalable for smart monitoring of HV assets using Internet of Things (IoT).

title: A Constraint Relaxation Co-Evolutionary Algorithm for the Two-Echelon Vehicle Routing Problem With Load-Dependent Drones
authors: Fan Yu, Jinlong Zhou, Qun Chen, Yange Li, Chao Li, Sheng Qi, Weixiong Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The growing adoption of drones in last-mile logistics has raised urgent demands for controlling energy consumption costs while ensuring delivery efficiency. To address this, we formulate the Two-Echelon Vehicle Routing Problem with Load-dependent Drones (2E-VRPLD) within a depot-satellite-customer framework, which reflects realistic logistics conditions where drones experience continuously changing payloads as they pick up and deliver parcels. These load fluctuations cause discrete shifts in power consumption, making their actual flight endurance highly uncertain under a fixed energy budget and thereby significantly increasing the complexity of planning feasible routes and minimizing overall transportation costs. To solve this complex VRP variant, we develop a novel Constraint Relaxation Co-evolutionary Algorithm (CRCEA) that integrates a customer-satellite matching mechanism to minimize drone endurance redundancy and a minimal impact assessment strategy for route segment merging. Experiments show that CRCEA attains nearly negligible optimality gaps relative to Gurobi on small-scale instances, significantly outperforms seven representative heuristics on large-scale instances, and consistently delivers superior solutions in real-world delivery scenarios from leading express carriers JD and SF Express in Changsha, China. Furthermore, ablation studies identify key factors affecting algorithmic performance, and sensitivity analyses illustrate how drone performance parameters influence the efficiency of solving the 2E-VRPLD. This work provides a robust theoretical and practical approach for solving the two-echelon pickup-and-delivery routing problem with load-dependent drones, offering important insights for advancing smart logistics and improving enterprise operational efficiency.

title: Comparative Analysis of Students' Perceptions of Programming Puzzles: Parson's and Wordle-Like_supp1-3663888.docx
authors: A. N. Varnavsky

title: Classification of Cloud Phases in Cold-Air Outbreak Events Based on Polarization Lidar and Cloud Radar Observations_supp1-3663561.docx
authors: Weiguo Zhang
abstract: Cold air outbreak (CAO) clouds significantly impact regional weather, precipitation and aviation safety. This impact is especially pronounced in confluence zones, where cold continental air masses interact with warm, moisture-laden air currents emanating from oceans or lakes. In this paper, an objective classification algorithm of cloud phase was suggested using K-means clustering algorithm based on PollyXT lidar method and a gradient based cloud identification algorithm, in order to determine depolarization ratio threshold for supercooled water, mixed-phase and ice clouds. The analyses of cloud phase characteristics and vertical structure were conducted for cold air outbreak events over the Shandong Peninsula, a region frequently affected by sea-effect snow disasters, using the winter period of 2024-2025 continuous observation data from the Eastern CHina cold-air OutbrEak Snowfall (ECHOES) campaign. The results show: the supercooled water clouds were predominantly located above the ice clouds in the mid-to-upper troposphere, implying heterogeneous ice nucleation processes; the analysis of diurnal variation reveals that the probability of occurrence of supercooled water clouds remains constant during the whole day because of the stable atmospheric conditions; synergy of lidar and radar observations clearly demonstrates their complementary detection abilities: radar outperforming lidar in lower troposphere and daytime conditions; the high coverage of the supercooled water in the lower troposphere poses a significant safety threat for aircraft icing. These results provide observational basis for improving the cloud microphysics parametric schemes in numerical weather prediction and aviation safety.

title: Classification of Seabed Types by OBIA with Recursive Feature Elimination Corrected by Expert Interpretation_supp5-3663162.pdf
