authors: Yujie Ding

title: Tiny-WiFo: A Lightweight Wireless Foundation Model for Channel Prediction via Multi-Component Adaptive Knowledge Distillation
authors: Haotian Zhang, Shijian Gao, Xiang Cheng
abstract: The massive scale of Wireless Foundation Models (FMs) hinders their real-time deployment on edge devices. This letter moves beyond standard knowledge distillation by introducing a novel Multi-Component Adaptive Knowledge Distillation (MCAKD) framework. Key innovations include a Cross-Attention-Based Knowledge Selection (CA-KS) module that selectively identifies critical features from the teacher model, and an Autonomous Learning-Passive Learning (AL-PL) strategy that balances knowledge transfer with independent learning to achieve high training efficiency at a manageable computational cost. When applied to the WiFo FM, the distilled Tiny-WiFo model, with only 5.5M parameters, achieves a 1.6 ms inference time while retaining over 98% of WiFo’s performance and its crucial zero-shot generalization capability, making real-time FM deployment viable.

title: IEEE Computer Society Call for Papers
authors: N/A

title: A 475-nW Area-Efficient Programmable Analog Feature Extraction Filterbank for Audio Classification
authors: Patrice Abbie D. Legaspi, Shubham Yadav, Mark S. Oude Alink, Bram Nauta
abstract: Audio classification in edge devices has many applications and can be implemented at varying levels of complexity, typically consisting of a feature extractor followed by a classifier. Such devices are often always-on, constantly listening to their surroundings, and have a small form factor; therefore, they require low-power operation and high area efficiency. A passive switched-capacitor (SC)-based analog feature extractor (AFEx) is good for programmability and energy efficiency. However, it suffers from area inefficiency due to the large capacitors needed to reduce <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$kT/C$</tex-math> </inline-formula> noise. To address this issue, a 16-channel analog <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-path filterbank with an amplifier and a feedback Miller capacitor is designed, significantly reducing the physical capacitance on chip by a factor of 30 compared to not using a Miller capacitor while achieving the same noise performance, and by a factor of 8 compared to the state-of-the-art (SOTA). The same amplifier also provides gain, eliminating the need for a low-noise amplifier (LNA) at the front end. Each <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-path filter uses only two paths with 50% duty-cycled clocks, simplifying clock generation and reducing power consumption. Moreover, all clock frequencies are derived from only a single 32.768-kHz low-power system clock. Using the zero-IF output of the two-path filter for amplitude extraction allows filtering and mixing to be reduced into a single step. The 65-nm low-power CMOS chip consumes 475nW, comparable to other works in the literature. However, this work includes amplification and uses only 12% of the capacitance per channel of other SC-based AFExs, making it more area efficient. The total input-referred noise (IRN) is <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$120~{\mu }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">RMS</sub>, which is equivalent to a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of 18dB for ~1-m speaker–microphone distance. Competitive maximum classification accuracy of 87.2% for keyword spotting (KWS) is achieved, as well as a classification accuracy robustness of >70% for an input voltage range from 1 to ~260mV<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">RMS</sub> (~50-dB SNR range) on the Google Speech Command Dataset (GSCD) for a 12-class (ten keywords) detection using a lightweight software classifier.

title: LLFeat: Noise-Aware Feature Matching under Various Low-Light Conditions_supp1-3662204.pdf
authors: Zunjie Zhu
abstract: Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) is a crucial technique in computer vision and has been widely used in robot navigation, virtual reality, and augmented reality. Although SLAM algorithms are relatively mature, their performance is severely degraded under low-light conditions. As a core module of SLAM algorithms, feature matching under low-light conditions faces huge challenges. Existing methods train a general feature matching model for all low-light conditions, ignoring the noise modeling for better accuracy. In this work, we introduce LLFeat, a noise-aware method for feature matching under various low-light conditions. To compress the models of various low-light conditions, we further introduce a Noise-aware Feature Modulation (NaFM) layer to the model structure. Therefore, the models can share the most of parameters and preserve only private NaFM layers for each low-light condition, boosting the accuracy of feature matching with negligible additional parameters. LLFeat achieves remarkable results in the highly challenging MID benchmark in both indoor and outdoor scenes, demonstrating the effectiveness of our method. The code will be released.

title: Introducing Timepix2-Lite: A Miniaturized Readout Interface Enabling Nanosecond-Scale Half-Life Measurement
authors: O. Pavlas, B. Bergmann, M. Holik, M. Malich, S. Pospisil, P. Smolyanskiy, V. Vicha, R. Filgas
abstract: This paper presents Timepix2 Lite, a compact readout interface for the Timepix2 hybrid pixel detector, designed to provide high spatial and temporal resolution in a wide range of radiation detection applications. The Timepix2 Lite enables simultaneous energy and precise timing measurements on the nanosecond scale and supports advanced real-time data acquisition and visualization through the TrackLab software framework. Its compact and flexible architecture, enabled by the miniaturized design, allows seamless integration into diverse experimental setups, including nuclear spectroscopy, stratospheric balloon missions, and nanosecond-scale half-life measurements. As a case study, the half-life of the second excited state in <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">237</sup>Np was determined to be 67.5(7) ns for the 59.5 keV transition, consistent with previously reported results.

title: Towards Refining Developer Questions using LLM-Based Named Entity Recognition for Developer Chatroom Conversations_supp1-3663599.pdf
authors: Pouya Fathollahzadeh
abstract: In software engineering chatrooms, communication is often hindered by imprecise questions that cannot be answered. Recognizing key entities (e.g., programming languages and libraries) and user intent (e.g., learning or requesting a review) can be essential for improving question clarity and facilitating better exchange. However, existing research using natural language processing techniques often overlooks these softwarespecific nuances. In this paper, we introduce SoftwarE-specific Named entity recognition, Intent detection, and Resolution classification (SENIR), a labelling approach that leverages a Large Language Model to annotate entities, intents, and resolution status in developer chatroom conversations. To offer quantitative guidance for improving question clarity and resolvability, we build a resolution prediction model that leverages SENIR’s entity and intent labels along with additional predictive features. We evaluate SENIR on the DISCO dataset using a subset of annotated chatroom dialogues. SENIR achieves an 86% F-score for entity recognition, a 71% F-score for intent detection, and an 89% F-score for resolution status classification. Furthermore, our resolution prediction model, tested with various sampling strategies (random undersampling and oversampling with SMOTE) and evaluation methods (5-fold cross-validation, 10-fold cross-validation, and bootstrapping), demonstrates AUC values ranging from 0.7 to 0.8. Key factors influencing resolution include positive sentiment and entities such as Programming Language and User Variable across multiple intents, while diagnostic entities (e.g., Error Name) are more relevant in error-related questions. Moreover, resolution rates vary significantly by intent: questions about API Usage and API Change achieve higher resolution rates, whereas Discrepancy and Review have lower resolution rates. A Chi-Square analysis confirms the statistical significance of these differences.

title: GITCON 2025 Preface
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)

title: ISO/IEC/IEEE International Standard - Systems and software engineering--Life cycle management--Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs
authors: N/A
abstract: This document implements ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 for application on defence programs, providing the defence-specific language and terminology to help ensure the correct application of acquirer-supplier requirements for a defence program. It provides the basis for selection, negotiation, agreement, and performance of necessary systems engineering activities and delivery of products, while allowing flexibility for both innovative implementation and tailoring of the specific systems engineering process(es) to be used by system suppliers, either contractors or government system developers, integrators, maintainers, or sustainers. This document includes the expected or required outputs and associated attributes.

title: Efficiency-Oriented Stepwise Optimization of Variable Flux Memory Machine Considering Torque Requirement and Operation Envelope Expansion_supp1-3663619.pdf
authors: Wei Liu
abstract: Existing variable flux memory machines (VFMMs) typically suffer from the unavailability of operation envelope expansion and insufficient torque at the high magnetization state (MS), which severely hinders their potential for overall high-efficient operation through MS regulation. In this article, a novel efficiency-oriented stepwise (EOS) optimization strategy for VFMM is proposed. This strategy can innovatively take the coil flux linkage directly as an optimization objective, which can flexibly regulate the operation envelopes and PM torques at different MSs. A 27-slot/4-pole VFMM with the magnetic circuit complementary (MCC) concept is taken as a design example. Firstly, the operation principle, multiple operation conditions, and key requirements of the VFMM are theoretically investigated. Then, the MCC-VFMM and the EOS optimization strategy are introduced and implemented respectively. Furthermore, the key electromagnetic characteristics of the initial and optimized VFMMs are comprehensively compared. Finally, an optimized prototype is fabricated and experimented to confirm the feasibility of the proposed optimization approach.

title: Zhang Neural Network Model for Time-Variant Convex Optimization Involving Nonlinear Inequality Constraints With Robotic Application
authors: Jielong Chen, Yan Pan, Yunong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: Time-variant convex optimization involving nonlinear inequality constraints (TVCOINICs) is a challenging problem due to the nonlinearity and time-variant nature of its objective function and multitype constraints. Differing from traditional slack variables and projection operator methods, this article proposes a novel differentiable transform function that is continuous and differentiable everywhere, while eschewing the need for additional tunable hyperparameters. On the basis of the Lagrange multiplier technique and Karush–Kuhn–Tucker (KKT) conditions, the initial time-variant optimization problem is converted into a time-variant nonlinear system comprising both equalities and inequalities. By employing the proposed differentiable transform function, this system is then further refined into an equivalent time-variant nonlinear system of equalities. Subsequently, the comprehensive design process of the differentiable transform function-based Zhang neural network (DTFZNN) model is delineated, which, through the comprehensive utilization of the time derivatives and error feedback information, is capable of adeptly addressing the TVCOINICs problem. Moreover, sliding mode control (SMC) is integrated into the proposed model to endow it with verified noise-suppression capability. The relevant theorems prove its convergence and robustness, and corresponding numerical experiments verify its effectiveness. Ultimately, to evaluate the practical efficacy of the proposed model, the proposed model is implemented to address the path tracking and repetitive motion problem associated with a robotic arm, thereby illustrating its superiority in solving practical problems.

title: Anti-Unwinding Active Fault-Tolerant Attitude Tracking Control for Uncertain Spacecraft via Fully Actuated System Approach
authors: Shixiang Jia, Li Yuan, Jianbin Qiu, Tong Wang, Min Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: This article proposes an active fault-tolerant control (AFTC) scheme for attitude tracking of uncertain spacecraft with actuator faults and unavailable angular velocity. First, a second-order fully actuated system (FAS) model is derived from the dynamics of the spacecraft. The unwinding phenomenon is solved by controlling the scalar quaternion. Then, the state observer and the FAS-based controller are designed, and the assumption that all the system states and their derivatives are requested to be known is relaxed, which is a typical assumption in the existing FAS approach. The proposed method facilitates the detection and analysis of the fault information for a spacecraft, based on which an AFTC scheme is designed to address uncertain spacecraft attitude tracking under uncertain time-varying inertia parameters, faults, and disturbances. Moreover, angular velocity measurements are not needed. The uniformly bounded stability of the proposed control scheme is theoretically proved. Finally, the numerical simulation results are provided to illustrate the performance of the proposed control scheme.

title: Safe reinforcement learning-based guidance strategy against active defense target in three-player engagement
authors: Yuting Shang, Yuanli Cai, Haonan Jiang, Yifan Deng
abstract: In the three-player engagement, the missile must confront an active defense target—comprising both the target and the defender—while simultaneously pursuing the target and evading the defender. Existing intelligent missile guidance strategies against an active defense target integrate the reward for pursuing the target with the reward for evading the defender to improve guidance performance. However, setting the appropriate weights for these rewards poses a significant challenge. As a result, these strategies struggle to effectively optimize mission performance (i.e., pursuing the target) while simultaneously ensuring safety (i.e., evading the defender). Therefore, we propose a safe reinforcement learning-based guidance strategy against an active defense target in three-player engagement, enabling the missile to pursue the target while evading the defender efficiently. First, we model the kinematic and relative kinematic equations in a three-player engagement scenario. To apply safe reinforcement learning in the missile guidance against an active defense target, the Constrained Markov Decision Process framework for the missile is constructed. Next, from a probabilistic inference perspective, we propose a safe reinforcement learning-based guidance strategy in an Expectation Maximization style to efficiently guide the missile: the optimal non-parametric variational distribution is calculated using convex optimization (E-step), and policy parameters are updated through supervised learning (M-step). Finally, a wide range of simulation experiment results show the feasibility and superiority of the proposed guidance strategy in both static target and dynamic target environments.

title: AC loss reduction in a REBCO coil assembly by applying superconducting shielding coils
authors: Yueming Sun, Chengliang Yu, Taotao Huang, Takanobu Kiss, Zhenan Jiang
abstract: AC loss in REBCO coils is one of the critical issues for applications such as superconducting rotating machines and fast-ramping magnets. One approach to reducing AC loss is to place open-loop superconducting shielding coils (SSC) concentrically with the REBCO coil, near the end of the coil, thereby shielding it from time-varying magnetic fields. However, there is no experimental validation on the effectiveness of SSC. In this work, AC loss in a REBCO coil assembly was measured and numerically calculated at 77 K with and without SSC. The coil assembly consists of three double pancake coils (120 turns in total). The coils assembly and SSC are wound from 4-mm-wide SuperPower and 12-mm-wide Fujikura coated conductors, respectively. Both the simulation and experiment show that SSC effectively reduces losses by 30 – 50%. Through experimental benchmarking, we found that accurate loss prediction requires considering the uniformity of the lateral critical current density in the coated conductors used in SSC.

title: Wideband RF Self-Interference Cancellation With Three Taps Using Trigonometric Frequency-Domain Padding Method
authors: Gisung Yang, Hwan-Jae Cho, Byung-Wook Min
abstract: This paper proposes a novel RF interference cancellation method based on Fourier analysis and enhanced by trigonometric frequency-domain padding to enable active cancellation in in-band full duplex radio front-ends. Active cancellation uses a multi-tap RF canceller to mimic the self-interference (SI). The SI within the target bandwidth is first characterized using Fourier analysis. Based on this analysis, a tap implementation strategy is proposed. To achieve the desired SI cancellation (SIC) performance with three taps, at least one tap should be implemented with the dominant delay path. Additionally, the other taps should be spaced uniformly in delay, including the dominant delay path. However, this approach introduces a band-edge overshooting problem, narrowing the bandwidth. To address this issue, this paper introduces a trigonometric frequency-domain padding method. By applying Fourier analysis to this padded SI, the proposed method effectively mitigates the band-edge overshooting problem. An RF canceller is also introduced, featuring a group delay controller to implement the dominant delay path and fixed delays to maintain uniform delay intervals between taps. Consequently, the proposed method achieves a total 60-dB SIC without band-edge overshooting over the wide bandwidth of 3.6–3.7 GHz.

title: Closed-Loop Mutual Compensation of Nonlinearities in MEMS Resonators
authors: Christian Padovani, Paolo Frigerio, Chiara Agrusta, Gabriele Gattere, Giacomo Langfelder
abstract: The research proposes a new technique for automatic, closed-loop, mutual compensation of hardening and softening nonlinearities in microelectromechanical system (MEMS) resonators. The technique exploits the change in sign of the frequency-amplitude relationship of resonators when passing from hardening to softening: by applying a small modulation to the displacement amplitude, and observing the frequency changes, it is possible to trigger a feedback mechanism which keeps the resonator in its linear condition for, in principle, unboundedly large amplitudes. The method is verified on a 37-kHz device with $10^{\mathbf {4}}$ quality factor (Q), showing linear operation beyond 500 nm for a $2.2~\mu $ m gap. This is about 5 times the critical displacement in absence of nonlinearity nulling, and yields a 5-fold improvement in frequency noise floor.[2025-0219]

title: An Efficiency-Improved and Conditional Privacy-Preserving Authentication Scheme Based on Merkle Hash Tree in MEC
authors: Yan Zhang, Chunsheng Gu, Peizhong Shi, Zhengjun Jing, Bing Li, Weizhi Meng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Authentication is an important security issue for multi-access edge computing (MEC). However, the existing authentication schemes have not achieved a good balance between privacy preserving, efficiency, and low computation overhead on the device side. To address this issue, we propose an efficiency-improved and conditional privacy-preserving authentication scheme suitable for resource-constrained MEC devices. Our core idea is integrating the merkle hash tree (MHT) into the anonymous authentication scheme constructed by the blockchain and key derivation function (KDF) to improve efficiency. The MHT not only reduces the on-chain storage overhead brought by the increasing pseudo-public keys of KDF, but also utilizes few hash functions to achieve lightweight <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\bm {k}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-times authentications with the same edge server. Despite these advantages, managing pseudo-key pairs in the form of MHT leafs still brings efficiency and unlinkability problems. We construct the partially shuffled merkle hash tree to only shuffle leafs within the device group, and combine with the KDF to update MHTs in a public manner by synchronizing pseudo-key pairs. Consequently, the efficiency of key update can be ensured. Moreover, a time-bound key derivation function based on physically unclonable function and BIP-32 is developed to provide immediate and permanent device revocation. Only the remaining valid pseudo-public keys of the revoked device will be recorded on the blockchain, which reveals no linkable information and avoids frequently reconstructing all the MHTs. We prove the authentication security and discuss other security features. A proof-of-concept prototype was implemented to conduct experiments and comparative analysis for performance evaluation.

title: Enhancing Road Safety through a Fuzzy-Based Adaptive Alert System for Critical Motorcycle-Car
authors: Carlos V. S. Augusto, Pietro L. Campos, Johannes Lochter, P.Santo Felipe, A.Faria Lester
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Ensuring safety in road traffic remains a critical challenge, particularly in scenarios involving motorcycles approaching the blind spot of larger vehicles. This paper proposes an intelligent, context-aware alert system based on Fuzzy Logic, capable of generating gradual warning signals to assist car drivers in anticipating potential collisions. Grounded in real-world accident data, the system integrates Responsibility-Sensitive Safety (RSS) equations with the ISO 17387 safety standard to define critical interaction zones. A custom simulation environment was developed using the CARLA platform, enabling realistic vehicle dynamics and sensor modeling. The proposed fuzzy controller processes longitudinal and lateral safety parameters, along with real-time inter-vehicle distance, to classify the level of danger and modulate the alert intensity accordingly. Unlike traditional binary systems, the fuzzy engine provides a continuous output ranging from low to high criticality, ensuring timely and non-intrusive driver assistance. Simulation results across nine accident-based scenarios demonstrate the system’s effectiveness in anticipating risks, minimizing false positives, and enhancing driver response time. This work contributes to a scalable, human-centric safety mechanism for Advanced Driver-Assistance Systems (ADAS), with strong potential for real-world application in environments involving mixed traffic and vulnerable road users.

title: Early Uterine Cancer Detection Using Ultrasound Images by Different Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Sunil Tekale, Shyamsunder Prabhakar Kosbatwar, J Gladson Maria Britto, Vaishali Chauhan, Abha Dargar, Amit Gupta
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The most frequent gynaecologic cancer in developed countries is uterine cancer, which is the second most prevalent in underdeveloped nations. Most women who are diagnosed with endometrial cancer do so with early-stage malignancies, which are linked to favourable prognoses.A subset of women with early-stage uterine cancer, however, are more likely to die and have recurrence. This is an important global health hazard, impacting over 382,000 women globally yearly and adding to a worrisome 90,000 annual fatality rate. Through tackling the worldwide issue of uterine cancer diagnosis, this research not only advances healthcare technology but also has the potential to save lives globally.In order to meet the pressing demand for better uterine cancer diagnosis, the suggested research introduces a unique machine learning-based ultrasound image processing method. This system automatically detects suspicious areas in ultrasound pictures by utilizing cutting-edge deep learning algorithms and image processing methodologies. The model helps with early detection and prompt intervention by differentiating between normal and malignant tissue by extracting pertinent traits and patterns.The proposed paper results should lead to better early identification of uterine cancer and a more accurate classification of the disease as benign, malignant, and subcategorized benign as fibroid, water molecules, normal cells, and ending on their characteristics, malignant with their sub stages, resulting in more efficient therapies and ultimately preserving human life. Additionally, the approach created can be used as a basis for related applications in computer-aided diagnostics and medical imaging, furthering the advancement of healthcare technology.

title: Cyber Threat Intelligence: Malware, Targets, and Emerging Attack Trends
authors: Venubabu Chekuri
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: A more sophisticated and ever-changing cyber threat landscape needs more sophisticated and reactive security solutions. The many methods and tactics provided by Machine Learning (ML) that may effectively and efficiently detect, anticipate, and reduce cyber risks have shown their usefulness in enhancing cybersecurity. The paper describes a new ML-based framework for identifying cyber threats by using the TII-SSRC-23 dataset and its set of statistical and network traffic characteristics. An Extra Trees Classifier (ETC) and an Artificial Neural Network were made and tested using several metrics to measure their performance. ETC performs much better than both ANN and the standard models and has nearly perfect results: an accuracy of 98.98%, precision of 100%, recall of 97.95% and an F1-score of 98.96% with almost no false positives. The model's effectiveness and reliability are shown using ROC-PR graphs and confusion matrices. However, the ANN model is not able to match the recall and sensitivity levels of other AI methods. The ETC model outperforms the Logistic Regression models, according to further investigation. According to the study, using ensemble learning is beneficial for building reliable and robust intrusion detection systems.

title: Harnessing Blockchain and AI for Innovative Solutions in Land Administration in Bangladesh
authors: Ridwan Hamid Khan, Syed Sadman Salahuddin, Tanvir Ahmed, Saha Reno, Md. Khairul Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Land administration in Bangladesh faces significant challenges including corruption, inefficiency, and reliance on conventional paper based systems. These issues lead to frequent land ownership disputes, data fraud, and delayed transaction processing. This paper proposes a novel framework that integrates blockchain technology with artificial intelligence to transform land administration. The blockchain component ensures data integrity through a tamper resistant decentralized ledger, while AI enables intelligent analysis for fraud detection, dispute resolution, land classification, and valuation. Smart contracts automate transactions without intermediaries, reducing processing time significantly. The system employs IPFS for decentralized document storage and machine learning models for anomaly detection and geospatial analysis. Experimental results demonstrate strong performance across all critical functions, confirming the technical viability of the approach. The proposed solution offers a transparent, efficient, and secure approach to land administration that addresses both legal and socio economic needs in Bangladesh.

title: Rice Quality Prediction using Honey Badger Approach
authors: Shravani J, Bhargavi K
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Rice serves as a primary dietary component for a large proportion of the global population and plays a vital role in sustaining food availability worldwide. Rice quality can be affected by several factors, each of which can impact its nutritional value, taste, texture, and overall usability. Environmental conditions play a significant role; factors such as soil quality, water availability, and climate conditions can influence the growth and final quality of rice grains. Consuming bad quality rice can lead to various health issues, largely due to contamination, spoilage, or the presence of harmful substances. The Honey Badger Algorithm is a nature-inspired optimization technique motivated by the adaptive foraging behavior of honey badgers and is designed to efficiently explore complex solution spaces. In this study, the algorithm is employed to evaluate and predict the quality of rice grains. The adaptive search mechanism of the algorithm, which incorporates both digging and honey guided behaviors, is modeled through exploration and exploitation phases. The resulting quality prediction policies demonstrate strong reliability by satisfying robustness and correctness criteria. Results obtained ensure that performance is encouraging towards the metrics like learning rate, cost of operations, accuracy, and execution time.

title: Proxy Re-Encryption Scheme Using Secure Enclave For Cyber Security Applications
authors: Chilukuri Harshavardhan Reddy, Boggala Sai Pranathi, Boda Revanth Kumar, Ch.Anusha, M.A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper presents a proxy re-encryption framework aimed at enhancing data security in distributed settings by employing secure enclaves for advanced cybersecurity applications. The rise of cloud computing, Internet of Things (IoT), and blockchain technologies has revealed substantial shortcomings in existing Proxy Re-Encryption (PRE) techniques, which frequently face key exposure issues, high computational demands, and scalability problems that hinder secure and efficient data exchange among multiple users. To address these issues, the proposed framework merges PRE with Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs), specifically making use of Intel Software Guard Extensions (SGX) to guarantee that essential cryptographic processes are performed within a secure enclave, thereby protecting keys and ensuring data confidentiality even in untrusted situations. The framework utilizes a lightweight hybrid model that supports unidirectional multi-hop re-encryption while keeping a consistent ciphertext size, improving performance through the application of AES-GCM for in-enclave computation. The system was tested in simulated scenarios to assess its efficiency, achieving an impressive 64% decrease in re-encryption time, averaging 90 ms compared to 250 ms for traditional PRE approaches. This enhancement in performance renders the framework particularly suitable for real-time secure data delegation in dynamic, multi-user environments. Implemented on standard SGX-enabled hardware, this system demonstrates effectiveness for live data-sharing scenarios. This paper highlights the promise of blending PRE with TEEs, presenting a scalable and secure solution for boosting cybersecurity in cloud, IoT, and blockchain ecosystems, and laying the groundwork for future developments in secure data management.

title: IoT-Based Smart Healthcare Monitoring Systems: A Comprehensive Review of Technologies, Challenges, and Innovations
authors: Sanzida Rahman, Shamema Afruza Jouty, Md. Sazed Ul Karim, Md Monirul Islam Molla, Mohammad Shafayet Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) has transformed the healthcare space with efficient, constant management of vital health measures using wearables, ambient or embedded systems, and mobile platform applications. The concept of these systems has great utility for chronic disease patients and those coming from low resource settings and rural areas. This paper provides a systematic review of IoT systems applied in healthcare, emphasizing on healthcare monitoring technologies through wearable devices, ambient systems, implantable devices, mobile applications, and cloud edge computing frameworks. The contributions of each category are presented along with barriers and key applications in healthcare settings for the respective subcategories, with particular consideration for the issue of the value to the patient and the extent of health system resource use provision. IoT systems for healthcare also have barriers for a system such as challenges of integration, security of data, safety of devices, battery consumption, and legislation policies.Before the healthcare sector can realize the full prospect of the IoT, issues like these will have to be resolved. Furthermore, an objective of this review is to identify areas where additional research is needed to create a resilient, adaptable, and inclusive IoT architecture for use in the healthcare sector facilitating efficiency and fairness.

title: Stability and Consistency Analysis of SHAP and LIME Explanations Across Machine Learning Models
authors: Samarth Satish Airani, Sumana Sinha, Shreya R Bhat, Deepthi K
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: In the era of interpretable machine learning, explaining model predictions is essential for transparency, trust, and accountability, particularly in critical domains. This project presents a comprehensive comparative study of two widely adopted model-agnostic explanation techniques: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations). We evaluate their stability and consistency across various machine learning models, including Random Forests, Support Vector Machines, and Neural Networks, using stability metrics such as Jaccard similarity and Spearman rank correlation to assess the robustness of the top-k important features identified by each explainer under repeated sampling. Our findings reveal significant differences in the behavior of SHAP and LIME across models, indicating that SHAP generally provides more consistent and stable explanations. This study not only offers insights into the reliability of feature attributions but also aids practitioners in selecting appropriate interpretability tools based on their specific use case and model type.

title: Vision Transformer-based Welding Defect Classification using ResNet-50 Feature Embeddings
authors: L. Natrayan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Welding is the primary steps in the fabrication process where the joining of materials are done either using high temperature or pressure. Many factors cause defects in the welding material, which need to be detected at an early stage to reduce faulty conditions. The welding defects are of two kinds: Internal and External. External surface defects are detected. Surface defects can be detected using computer vision technologies. The defects have various structures; therefore, classification using a convolutional neural network becomes inefficient. Thus, this paper proposes welding defect detection using the Vision Transformer model, which utilizes features extracted from the ResNet 50 model. The proposed work was simulated using the Welding Defect image dataset from Kaggle, achieving 94.08% accuracy and a 93.35% F1 score. The False Positive Rate and False Negative Rate are classified as shown, 7% and 6.3% respectively; therefore, the proposed work is classified efficiently.

title: A Multi-Layered Cryptographic Model for Biometric Data Security Using Fuzzy Vault and Lightweight AES Variants
authors: Sumesh Mohan, V Sheeja Kumari, N. Muthuvairavan Pillai, R H Aswathy, S. P. Santhoshkumar, Prince Arora
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: By presenting a multi-layered cryptographic framework that combines the Fuzzy Vault scheme with lightweight versions of the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES), this paper tackles the problem of protecting biometric data in settings with constrained computational resources. The framework uses error-tolerant biometric template protection methods and symmetric key cryptography to reduce computational overhead, storage efficiency, and privacy concerns. The method improves system reliability without requiring a large amount of resources by fusing strong cryptographic security with tolerance for biometric variability. In spite of low latency and low energy consumption, experimental results show robust defense against biometric and traditional cryptographic attacks. The framework's suitability for deployment in edge computing environments where efficiency and security are crucial is highlighted by additional evaluations of its robustness and scalability. In situations where computing resources are limited, this work provides a workable and flexible solution for protecting biometric data.

title: XAI-Driven Approach to Predicting Diabetes Risk with Machine Learning
authors: Arati Deshpande, Atul Dubey, Aditya Dhule, Rajat Kumar, Akshat Rathi, Aayush Kore, Harsh Shah
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Early onset of diabetes plays the most vital role in eliminating any severe health issues. This paper introduces a machine learning version using the Pima Diabetes dataset in terms of predicting diabetes risk using features such as the level of glucose, BMI, age, statistics of pregnancies, and the pedigree of diabetes. Data preprocessing has encompassed data cleaning involving missing values, imbalanced data using SMOTE, and data reduction undertaken by Recursive Feature Elimination. Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, and a Stacking Ensemble scored best at 83 percent. We used Explainable AI methods, SHAP, to decipher the model prediction and indicate which features contributed to the output, in order to create more transparency and build trust. The system gives explicit and personalized risk estimation to the user which qualifies it as a viable screening tool of early diabetes risk assessment.

title: Performance Analysis of 3D Finger Knuckle for Biometric Application
authors: G Divya, A S Mamatha, H C Nagaraj, Mahesh Naik L, Manoj R, Medha K M
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The increasing demand for accurate and secure biometric authentication has highlighted the limitations of current 3D knuckle recognition systems, particularly under variable lighting and noise conditions. This study introduces an enhanced 3D reconstruction approach for finger knuckle recognition by incorporating statistical parameters and varying kernel sizes. 3D Gaussian and Wiener filters are applied for image smoothing, and performance is evaluated using Mean Squared Error (MSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Noise Figure metrics. The Frankot-Chellappa algorithm demonstrated reduced pixel variation, with reconstructed images yielding MSE and MAE values of 7.437 and 1.5316, respectively. Preprocessing with a 3D Gaussian filter (kernel size [3×3]) further improved accuracy, achieving MSE and MAE of 2.677 and 0.743, along with a noise figure of 5.9375 dB. The proposed method exhibited superior noise reduction and a low computation time of 0.631 seconds, making it well-suited for real-world biometric security applications requiring reliable knuckle-based identification.

title: Elderly Activity and Emotion Recognition System Using Deep Learning
authors: Sunitha M, Dhananjaya V
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The increasing elderly population necessitates intelligent monitoring systems to ensure safety, independence and well-being. This study introduces a deep learning-based real-time video-based system for identifying the emotional and physical states of senior citizens. The system classifies everyday behaviours like walking, sitting, and falling as well as facial emotions like happy, sadness, and neutrality by integrating Convolution Neural Networks(CNNs) and Long Short-Term Memory(LSTM) networks. OpenCV is used to process real-time video and a Flask web application is used to deploy deep learning models for ongoing monitoring. The system performs in real-time and exhibits great accuracy on typical datasets, making it appropriate for use in healthcare and smart home applications. By facilitating prompt responses in the event of aberrant behaviour or emotional distress, this dual-recognition technique improves care for the elderly.

title: DeepBNet: A Modified DL Model for More Accurate Classification of Bengali Compound Handwritten Characters
authors: Moinul Hossain, Tahmid Enam Shrestha, Muhammad Abdullah, Md. Ashik Ikbal, Tripti Ahmed, Shakila Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Bengali handwritten character recognition is a major challenge in pattern recognition owing to the intricacy of the script, diverse writing styles, and similarities between compound characters. This paper presents a modified deep learning model architecture named DeepBNet, designed for recognizing handwritten Bengali compound characters with greater precision. The proposed model builds upon DenseNet-121, incorporating enhancements such as optimized hyperparameters and regularization techniques to improve classification performance. The model is trained and evaluated using the publicly available Ekush Dataset, which contains 31,500 total images and represents 24 classes of Bengali compound characters. Several data pre-processing techniques were applied, including cleaning and removing invalid and incorrect data. Additional Pre-Processing steps, including resizing to (32x32) pixels, normalization, and data augmentation techniques such as rotation, flipping, and scaling, were used to improve data diversity and robustness. The proposed model achieves an outstanding validation accuracy of 98.9%, with precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC of 98.9%, 98.8%, 98.85%, and 0.995, respectively, demonstrating the model’s ability to accurately classify most character classes with minimal misclassifications.

title: GI-TransCNN-Xplainer: A Hybrid Transformer–Convolutional Neural Network Framework with Explainable AI for Multi-Modal Gastrointestinal Disease Diagnosis in Endoscopic and Ultrasound Imaging
authors: Fatema Tuj Johora Liyana, Shaikat Hazra Pranto, Saif Uddin Vuia, K. M. Safin Kamal, Ahmed Wasif Reza
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Interpretation of imaging studies is subjective, and performance is often modality-specific, resulting in gastrointestinal (GI) diseases being a leading diagnostic challenge. We systematically compare six CNN architectures—ResNeXt-50, DenseNet-201, ShuffleNet v2, MNASNet, ConvNeXt-Tiny, and Xception—using three different GI imaging datasets: an endoscopic dataset for GERD and polyp detection (GastroEndoNet), a multi-class ultrasound image dataset for gallbladder diseases, and a binary capsule endoscopy dataset directed at GI bleeding. A standardized pipeline including preprocessing, data augmentation, transfer learning, and Grad-CAM was used for visualizing class-discriminative regions to enhance interpretability of the model. Across the datasets, DenseNet-201 regularly performed quite nicely, attaining an F1-score of 0.96 on the capsule endoscopy data with robust results when compared to the multi-class ultrasound task. These results indicate high metrics but come with the caveats of a small dataset, class imbalance, and overfitting. This work provides a reproducible benchmark for CNN-based GI disease classification tasks and points out the potential of interpretable and lightweight deep learning models to provide potential insights in clinical decision-making across various imaging modalities.

title: Intelligent Detection of Fileless Malware Using Autoencoder and Hyperdimensional Computing from Memory Snapshots
authors: Sk. Md. Rakibul Islam, Abdullah All Ahhad, Imran Hasan, Faridul Alam, Mehedi Hasan, Naeem Al Koreshi, Md Mamun, Muhammad Tanveer Ehsan, Md Habibur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Fileless malware constitutes a formidable cyberecurity threat, as it operates entirely within volatile memory and evades detection by traditional file-based mechanisms. Such attacks utilise legitimate system utilities alongside memory-resident strategies including process hollowing and DLL injection, thereby rendering conventional static and signature-based approaches largely ineffective. To address these limitations, this study introduces a novel hybrid detection framework that integrates Autoencoder-Based unsupervised representation learning with Hyperdimensional Computing (HDC), aiming to achieve lightweight, signature-free identification of fileless malware. The framework processes volatile memory snapshots and applies a deep autoencoder to derive latent feature representations. These representations are subsequently binarised and transformed into high-dimensional hypervectors, facilitating efficient and interpretable classification via cosine similarity. Experimental evaluation reveals an accuracy of 98.32%, with precision and recall scores of 98.07% and 98.78% respectively, underscoring the model’s robustness in detecting polymorphic and previously unseen malware. By eliminating dependence on static signatures and enabling rapid, low-resource inference, the proposed framework offers a scalable and transparent solution for real-time fileless malware detection within volatile memory environments.

title: Multi Classification of Brain Tumor Using CNN with GRAD-CAM
authors: Adyasha Pattanayak, Shipra Tanvi, Avinash Sharma, Anjela Tripathy, Smita Rath
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) have become the most effective neural network architecture for image classification, especially in the field of medical imaging. Brain tumor detection through MRI scans is a complex and time-consuming task that requires expert analysis. Traditional diagnostic methods such as texture analysis, edge detection, and segmentation, while useful, are labor-intensive and prone to inconsistencies among specialists. To address these challenges, this project proposes a CNN-based model enhanced with geometric transformations for data augmentation, aiming to automate brain tumor classification more efficiently and accurately. The model uses a public brain tumor dataset from Kaggle to classify MRI images into four categories: glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, or no tumor. To improve generalization and model robustness, data augmentation techniques such as rotation, flipping, scaling, and brightness adjustments are applied. These transformations increase the diversity of training images, allowing the CNN to learn more meaningful patterns and reduce overfitting. Additionally, Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) is utilized to visualize which regions in the MRI images influence the model’s predictions the most. This enhances the interpretability of the CNN and builds trust in clinical settings by showing how decisions are made. The CNN model not only boosts classification accuracy but also significantly reduces false positives, minimizing unnecessary procedures and reducing stress for patients. It also lightens the radiologists' workload and speeds up the diagnostic process. Overall, this AI-based solution contributes to faster, more reliable brain tumor diagnosis, ultimately supporting better treatment planning and improved patient outcomes.

title: Deep Learning Approaches for Enhanced Automatic Text Summarization
authors: Rakshitha, Pushpa Mohan, Rashmitha Shettigar, Disha D N, Sudesh Rao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Although there is a wealth of information on the internet these days, it can be challenging to find it quickly and effectively. You may have trouble finding the precise information you need to comprehend a given subject. The summary of a huge text document is exceedingly hard for humans to manually extract. The Internet is a great source of textual content. Searching through the large number of papers accessible and extracting pertinent information from them is therefore a challenge. To address the two issues mentioned above, automatic text summarizing is essential. One famous method for distilling a document to its essential points is automatic summarization. It refers to the technique of condensing a lengthy text document into a succinct and well-written summary that captures the essential information and main ideas of the original text, achieved by highlighting the significant points of the document. Presenting a condensed, semantically sound version of the original text is the aim of automatic text summarization. The main benefit is that reading time is decreased.

title: Enhancing Security in Radio Frequency Wireless Networks with Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface
authors: A. Hasan, M. Z. I. Sarkar, M. N. T. Sadhona, M. R. Aktar
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: In this paper, the potential of Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) to enhance the security of radio frequency (RF) wireless networks is explored. The rapid advancement of wireless communication technologies has revolutionized connectivity, but it has also introduced significant challenges in ensuring the security and reliability of data transmission, especially with eavesdroppers. Recently, the incorporation of RIS has surfaced as a potential solution to tackle these security challenges. The authors aim to secure the signals from eavesdropping in Rician fading channels considering the effects of shape parameters, scale parameters and the reflecting elements number of RIS. Analytical models for the key security metrics include the analytical expressions in closed-form for the probability of nonzero secrecy capacity (PNZSC) and the secure outage probability (SOP), based on the statistical characteristics of the received signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), with and without RIS. Results show that the loss of security in the RF wireless networks can be compensated by using RIS without increasing the transmit signal power.

title: Performance Analysis and Financial Viability of a Grid-Connected PV System
authors: Uttam S Satpute, Balwant Patil, Suresh D Mane
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive simulation study of a proposed 278 kWp grid-connected photovoltaic (PV) system at Dr. D Y Patil Pratishthan’s College of Engineering, Salokhenagar, Kolhapur, India (16.67°N, 74.21°E). Using PVsyst V8.0.9 software and Meteonorm 8.2 weather data, the study evaluates the technical performance, energy yield, system losses, financial viability, and environmental impact of the project. The system comprises 540 WSMD-515 modules (515 Wp each) and 5 generic 50 kWac inverters, configured with a fixed tilt of 21° and zero azimuth. Simulation results indicate an annual energy production of approximately 433 MWh, corresponding to a specific production of 1557 kWh/kWp/year and a performance ratio (PR) of 79.20%. A detailed loss analysis identifies key areas of energy reduction. The financial assessment reveals a total investment of 8,380,000 INR, a Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) of 1.50 INR/kWh, a payback period of 6.6 years, and an Internal Rate of Return (IRR) of 18.36%. The project is estimated to save 10,038 tCO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> over its lifetime. The findings suggest the project is technically sound and financially attractive under the simulated conditions.

title: A Bio-Optimized rule mining framework for finding frequent patterns of Myopia Risk Factors
authors: Subrina Akter, Kaniz Fatema Tanni, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Myopia is a growing global health concern influenced by genetic, clinical, and behavioral factors. Early detection and personalized treatment require identifying meaningful patterns among these risk factors. Traditional Association Rule Mining (ARM) algorithms commonly used to identify patterns in large datasets, often overlook rare but clinically significant associations due to their reliance on high frequency thresholds. Additionally, these methods generate large numbers of candidate rules, leading to high memory usage and computational costs. To overcome these issues, this study proposes a novel ARM approach that combines Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Genetic Algorithm (GA) to efficiently uncover hidden patterns related to myopia risk factors. Using a myopia dataset from Kaggle, experimental results show that factors such as children aged 8 with myopic parents, low computer usage, and high nearwork hours are strongly associated with thin lens thickness, medium TV viewing, high sports activity, and an increased likelihood of myopia. This highlights the significant role of genetics and near work in myopia development, even with protective habits. Other significant rules identify clinical factors, such as low spherical equivalent (SPHEQ), normal anterior chamber depth (ACD), and medium axial length (AL), as important contributors to myopia risk. These rules exhibit high confidence and lift despite low support, demonstrating the model’s ability to detect rare yet meaningful patterns. The proposed PSO-GA ARM method outperforms the Apriori algorithm in space efficiency, achieving 91.77% less memory usage and 99.31% smaller rule storage. Additionally, it improves rule quality, with a mean confidence of 1.0000 compared to 0.9278 for Apriori, showing strong statistical significance (pvalue = 0.0001). These results highlight the potential of the PSO-GA approach for accurate, efficient, and clinically relevant myopia risk assessment and early intervention.

title: A Hybrid framework for CKD Diagnosis by Random Forest with XGBoost Classifiers
authors: S. Ohm Shankar, M Revathi, M. Hema, K. Ganesh Kumar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is the topic of a lot of discussion nowadays and is a health matter that is being talked about worldwide. The search for reliable and fast diagnosis tools is a must to address the challenge posed by CKD. This work is presenting the use of a machine learning model that is a combination of two models, specifically, Random Forest and XGBoost, for effective CKD diagnosis. The architecture of this model is focused on taking advantage of the strength that Random Forest has in managing data diversity and noise together with the high prediction accuracy and scalability that XGBoost is offering. The proposed system adopts a balanced way of classification by combining these two algorithms, consequently, it minimizes the chance of overfitting and at the same time it is able to follow the intricate connections in the data. The ensemble method here leads to further gains in generalization, which in turn empowers the system to produce trustworthy results witnessing today’s different clinical situations. This hybrid model is developed on a selected dataset, and it is subsequently checked through the implementation of dependable validation methods to prove its superiority over the solo ones. The here presented research spotlights the capability of ML merged strategies in boosting the diagnostic potency, thus facilitating early intervention and better clinical outcomes in CKD management.

title: AI-Driven Underwater Object Detection Using Image Processing and Neural Networks
authors: Shaik Arshiya Kousar, Sania Sania, Gummi Sai Lalith Reddy, Sumaiya Shaikh
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The pollution of oceans and water bodies is a great concern in relation to the preservation of nature and life on Earth. In this assignment, an attempt has been made towards developing an AI model using YOLOv8, EfficientNet and Autoencoders for underwater pollution recognition at a multi-level and automated scale. YOLOv8 sets new standards in object detection by allowing real time tracking and localization of pollutants. EfficientNet features enhanced extraction and classification abilities which results in improved efficiency of feature recognition. Unsupervised anomaly detection is facilitated through the application of Autoencoders that enable recognition of concealed or irregular pollution patterns. In the given paper, the proposed hybrid framework is assessed on underwater datasets and achieves a high degree of accuracy in the identification of contaminants which include plastic debris and chemical waste. From the results of this approach, it is possible to enhance underwater pollution surveillance and monitoring by developing a more complex, scalable and automated system to preserve the environment.

title: Quantum Based Navigation Systems: Principles, Technologies, and Applications
authors: J R K Kumar Dabbakuti, LS Madhurima, N Shiva Krishna, D K N V S Kavya Sri, Amarnath. G, A Dhanush
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The growing reliance on Global Positioning System (GPS) technologies across critical sectors such as defense, aerospace, and autonomous systems highlights the need for navigation solutions that are robust against signal loss, spoofing, and jamming. Quantum-based navigation systems offer a promising alternative, leveraging the fundamental principles of quantum mechanics to achieve unprecedented precision and resilience. This paper provides an overview of the core quantum technologies enabling this next generation of navigation, including atomic interferometry, quantum accelerometers, gyroscopes, and ultra-precise quantum clocks. Cold atom techniques and laser cooling play pivotal roles in enhancing measurement stability and accuracy. The integration of these technologies into inertial navigation systems (INS) significantly reduces drift and extends operational reliability in GPS-denied environments. Quantum navigation technologies are finding applications across a wide range of domains, including military defense, underwater missions, space travel, and autonomous transportation. Despite existing obstacles such as high development costs, environmental sensitivity, and the need for further miniaturization, progress in quantum sensor development and engineering continues at a steady pace. This article presents an overview of the fundamental concepts, present capabilities, and prospective advancements in quantum navigation. It also highlights the technology's promise as a viable alternative or even a possible replacement for conventional GPS systems.

title: Architectural Style Classification using EfficientNet-B7: A Deep Learning Approach for Visual Pattern Recognition
authors: Om Prakash Suthar, Suraj Kumar Thakur, Mukesh Kumar Gupta, Rahul Bhandari, Pappu Kumar Rai
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Identifying architectural styles through computer vision remains a complex challenge due to the visual diversity of building structures. Achieving high classification accuracy is central to overcoming this complexity. To tackle this issue, our approach leverages supervised machine learning techniques, employing EfficientNet-B7—a convolutional neural network (CNN)—as the core model. The model is trained using the "architectural-styles-dataset", a curated collection of images representing multiple architectural styles. Prior to training, preprocessing steps such as data augmentation, normalization, and feature extraction are applied to optimize learning performance. Experimental results reveal that the model attains an accuracy of 82.45%, highlighting its capability in accurately distinguishing architectural styles. This system offers promising applications in areas such as heritage preservation, urban design, and automated analysis of architectural patterns.

title: Miniaturized Antenna Structure Supporting C and X Band Resonances for Integrated Wireless System
authors: Saikat Barua, Nirzar Barua, MD. Akter Hossain, Sujoy Barua, Ayon Dhar
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This paper presented a compact tri-band antenna with symmetric microstrip patch antenna, designed for high-performance wireless communication systems. The suggested antenna uses a specialized radiating structure with several parallel slots symmetrically etched on either side of the patch to improve performance. It shows mechanical stability and compatibility with next-generation wireless platforms by using a Rogers RO4350B (ε<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf> = 3.7, tanδ = 0.0037) substrate that is 1.6mm thick. Simulating in the CST software, the resonant frequencies are depicted at 4.19GHz (C-band), 7.51GHz, and 8.76GHz (X-band), shows reflection gains of –30.7dB, –45.5dB, and –34.4dB. In addition, the radiation pattern shows the directional characteristics and gains of 5.77dBi, 7.89dBi, and 5.94dBi. The antenna achieves proper impedance matching and wide multi-band functionality while operating efficiently in the 2–10GHz range. These unique characteristics in a single device, the simulated structure has great potential for emerging wireless standards like WiFi-6/7, especially in the C- and X-band domains, satellite mega-constellations, AI-enabled radar systems, and modern communication technologies like 5G and beyond.

title: FreNTS: Neural Texture Synthesis in Frequency Domain_supp1-3663389.pdf
authors: Xinyi Liu
abstract: Although existing texture synthesis methods perform well in generating large images with irregularly repeated textures to avoid visually unrealistic repetitions, they still face significant challenges in synthesizing regular textures with densely interconnected structures. In this paper, we propose a novel neural texture synthesis method, FreNTS, which uses frequency domain information to enhance the texture synthesis process, synthesizing textures with continuous, complete, and visually realistic overall structures. The core idea is to perform the Discrete Cosine Transform on image patches to obtain the corresponding frequency domain rate information features, and then use the designed adaptive guided correspondence (AGC) loss to calculate the correlation difference between the source image and the target image in the frequency domain and spatial domains, thereby constraining the optimization of the target image to achieve high-quality texture synthesis. In addition, to better evaluate the effect of texture synthesis, we introduce Tile LPIPS as the metric for quantitative evaluation. Experimental results show that the proposed FreNTS can effectively accelerate the process of neural texture synthesis and use high-frequency information to capture better structural details to synthesize realistic textures.

title: DreamBarbie: Text to Barbie-Style 3D Avatars_supp1-3663253.mp4
authors: Zhenyu Zhang
abstract: To integrate digital humans into everyday life, there is a strong demand for generating high-quality, fine-grained disentangled 3D avatars that support expressive animation and simulation capabilities, ideally from low-cost textual inputs. Although text-driven 3D avatar generation has made significant progress by leveraging 2D generative priors, existing methods still struggle to fulfill all these requirements simultaneously. To address this challenge, we propose DreamBarbie, a novel text-driven framework for generating animatable 3D avatars with separable shoes, accessories, and simulation-ready garments, truly capturing the iconic “Barbie doll” aesthetic. The core of our framework lies in an expressive 3D representation combined with appropriate modeling constraints. Unlike prior methods, we use G-Shell to uniformly model watertight components (e.g., bodies, shoes) and non-watertight garments. By reformulating boundaries as Euclidean field intersections instead of manifold geodesics, we propose an SDF-based initialization and a hole regularization loss that together achieve a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$100\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> speedup and stable open topology without image input. These disentangled 3D representations are then optimized by specialized expert diffusion models tailored to each domain, ensuring high-fidelity outputs. To mitigate geometric artifacts and texture conflicts when combining different expert models, we further propose several effective geometric losses and strategies. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DreamBarbie outperforms existing methods in both dressed human and outfit generation. Our framework further enables diverse applications, including apparel combination, editing, expressive animation, and physical simulation.

title: Rendezvous Optimization for Second-Order Multiagent Systems: A Neurodynamic Approach With Generalized Compensation Term
authors: Zhanshan Wang, Yiyang Ge, Xiaolu Ye
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: This article investigates the rendezvous optimization problem for second-order multiagent systems. This task is typically framed as a convex optimization with equality constraints, which must also adhere to the system dynamics. Traditional neurodynamic approaches may lead to strong oscillations or instability due to uncoordinated intrinsic system dynamics. Inspired by proportional-derivative control and accelerated optimization techniques, a neurodynamic approach with a generalized compensation term (NGCT) is proposed to achieve oscillation suppression. The advantages of the approach include: 1) using state derivatives to provide predictions for oscillation suppression and 2) employing a compensation term based on the equality constraint coefficient matrix <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$A$</tex-math> </inline-formula> to enhance constraint matching sensitivity. The transformation properties of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$A$</tex-math> </inline-formula> enable convergence to specified geometries. It is proven that the proposed approach exponentially converges to the optimal solution. Numerical experiments have demonstrated the effectiveness of the proposed approach in different rendezvous optimization problems.

title: MCP-Secure: A Runtime Access Control Layer for Privilege-Aware LLM Agent Tooling
authors: Gamini Singh, Vijay K. Madisetti
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: Tool-enabled language-model agents introduce new security risks because their behavior evolves over multi-step workflows, yet existing defenses primarily rely on static allowlists or infrastructure isolation. This paper presents MCP-Secure, a lightweight, host-side enforcement layer for the Model Context Protocol (MCP) that applies scoped access, read-only defaults, and approval-gated privilege elevation at runtime. MCP-Secure tracks permissions through a session-level state machine and mediates every tool invocation without modifying agents or MCP servers. We evaluate the framework across 1,080 executions spanning multiple models, tasks, and adversarial simulations. Results show that scoped access alone blocks most unsafe behaviors, read-only enforcement reliably neutralizes all mutating attack vectors, and approval-gated elevation maintains strong safety while enabling controlled write operations. Across configurations, the wrapper also shapes agent planning, reducing unsafe attempts as policies tighten. These findings demonstrate that MCP-Secure provides a practical, reproducible mechanism for enforcing least-privilege constraints in tool-enabled LLM systems, offering strong adversarial resistance with interpretable safety-utility trade-offs.

title: Aligning Damages of Heavy-haul Rail from Images Inspected on Different Dates: An Adaptive Feature Matching Method
authors: Xincheng Zhu, Yuan Cao, Feng Wang, Yongkui Sun, Shuai Su, Wenkun Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Rails are vital components of heavy-haul railway infrastructure, playing a crucial role in ensuring the safety and efficiency of train operations. Analyzing the evolution of rail conditions is essential for effective maintenance planning, particularly when it involves aligning the same damages across different dates. However, due to random mileage deviations in inspection images, it is labor-intensive and highly inefficient to align damages manually and analyze its evolution. To address this issue, we propose a damage alignment method based on adaptive feature matching for inspection images. First, to address the challenge posed by truncated features, we propose a solution using feature size matching to actively identify reference features in a vast collection of inspection images. Next, a key feature selection method, based on multijump differences and Gaussian attenuation coefficients, is designed to retain features with high localization value. Then, a shortest path-based feature matching method is developed to align the same reference features across different dates, achieving the alignment and localization of damages on different dates. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified using inspection image data collected from a heavy-haul line on two different dates. Experimental results show that our method achieves accurate alignment of damages in images, with the error between the predicted and actual locations of damages within 1.23 meters.

authors: N/A

title: Tailoring Patient-Specific Cranial Implants for Bone Reconstruction via End-to-End Deep Learning Image-to-Print Approach
authors: Mahmoud Salem, Omar Wael, Moataz M. Attallah, Ahmed Elkaseer
abstract: Timely, patient-specific cranial implants are critical for restoring skull integrity after trauma, accidents or surgery, yet off-the-shelf devices arrive in coarse, fixed size increments that often fail to match irregular defects. Surgeons are therefore forced to open and try multiple implants intra-operatively, adding infection risk, operative time, and likelihood of early implant failure. This paper presents an end-to-end workflow that predicts “print-ready” cranial implants directly from defective CT volumes and validates them through physical prototyping. The pipeline couples a five-stage volumetric preprocessing routine with a systematic exploration of neural architectures. A baseline and two deeper 3-D U-Nets are benchmarked alongside the proposed version 3 model, a channel-rebalanced U-Net that shifts capacity from shallow texture filters to boundary-aware decoding paths. Two public datasets, SciData and SkullFix, were merged, and split for training the model. On 40 unseen skulls, the proposed version 3 achieved a mean Dice of 0.901, Boundary Dice of 0.908, and HD95 of 1.52 mm, which is surpassing all alternatives and reducing the number of intra-operative trials predicted by simulation from 3-4 to zero. Bench-top tests confirmed that implants printed directly from the network’s STL output seated flush on three morphologically diverse phantoms without CAD edits or sanding, demonstrating true “push-button” manufacturability. These results show that a carefully optimized architecture, paired with robust pre-processing, can supply size-perfect implants using modest data and commodity hardware.

title: A Fault Diagnosis Method Based on Adaptive Threshold Judgment in Zero-crossing Interval for Aviation Three-phase Inverter
authors: Zhanjun Huang, Jun Xie, Maning Zhang, Kang Hu, An Zhang, Weiheng Shao, Yanning Xiong
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Most existing inverter fault diagnosis methods simplify actual fault conditions and operating scenarios for research expediency. However, their diagnostic reliability is compromised when confronted with such as non-simultaneous secondary switch faults or multiple complex operating conditions. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel open-circuit fault diagnosis method. First, the three-phase current is measured, and a mean filtering is applied to enhance the waveform smoothness. Next, a zero-crossing-driven interval determination method is proposed for the current positive-half and negative-half. An adaptive threshold is then calculated in each interval, and the comparison between the current and this threshold effectively captures the loss of the waveform. Finally, a mapping from the fault signature vector to the fault mode is established to precisely localize the fault switch. Compared to existing advanced methods, the proposed approach can effectively handle the feature aberrance caused by system feedback. It also accurately identifies non-simultaneous secondary switch fault and demonstrates diagnostic consistency in various load scenarios. Experimental results, comparisons, and analyses confirm the superiority of the proposed method.

title: Dual Quaternion-Based Dynamic Modeling and Adaptive Neural Identification for Load-Assist Exoskeletons
authors: Ángel Eduardo Zamora-Suárez, Fermin Castillo Anaya, Pamela Vera-Tizat, Rosalío Reyes
abstract: This article addresses the challenge of modeling and controlling load-assist exoskeletons operating under dynamic and uncertain conditions. Traditional approaches often rely solely on dual quaternions for kinematic modeling, limiting their applicability in scenarios that require dynamic interaction. Most existing works focus only on kinematic formulations, leaving the dynamic coupling effects underexplored. To overcome this limitation, we propose a novel adaptive identifier based on Dual Quaternion Neural Networks (ADQNN), integrated within a dynamic modeling framework that includes inertial, Coriolis, and gravitational forces using dual quaternion algebra. This formulation enables a compact, singularity-free representation of rigid-body dynamics, facilitating real-time estimation of unmodeled dynamics.Unlike conventional dual-quaternion formulations, the proposed model extends their use from purely kinematic to fully dynamic representations, thereby ensuring higher numerical stability and physical consistency in modeling exoskeletons under uncertain payloads.The ADQNN is embedded within a Proportional-Derivative (PD) controller to improve trajectory tracking accuracy under unknown payloads. The proposed methodology is validated through simulations of two exoskeleton subsystems—the upper and lower limbs—under varying external loads. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly enhances angular position estimation, adaptability, and robustness compared to traditional models. The main contributions of this paper can be summarized as follows: (1) development of a singularity-free dynamic model based on dual quaternion algebra that includes inertial, Coriolis, and gravitational terms; (2) design of an Adaptive Dual Quaternion Neural Network (ADQNN) for online identification of unmodeled dynamics; (3) integration of the ADQNN into a PD control law with Lyapunov-based stability analysis; and (4) validation through simulations on upper and lower limb subsystems demonstrating improved performance under variable loads.These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach for adaptive control of exoskeletons in changing environments, extending the applicability of dual quaternions beyond purely kinematic frameworks. This constitutes the first adaptive identification framework embedded in a full dual-quaternion dynamic model.

title: Visible Light Uplink Communication Using D-shaped Side-Emitting Plastic Optical Fibers
authors: Longzhu Shang, Aiying Yang, Lihui Feng, Zhenrong Zhang, Minglun Zhang
abstract: A distributed visible light communication (VLC) uplink system employing a D-shaped side-emitting plastic optical fiber (POF) receiver is proposed. The D-shaped fiber is fabricated by truncating one side of a conventional circular POF, enabling enhanced lateral light coupling and increased received irradiance. To mitigate inter-symbol interference, a least mean squares (LMS) adaptive equalizer is implemented at the receiver. Experimental results obtained at symbol rates of 50 kbps, 500 kbps, 1 Mbps, and 5 Mbps show that the achievable transmission distance decreases with increasing symbol rate, and a maximum distance of 13 m is achieved at 50 kbps under 7% FEC limit. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed D-shaped side-emitting POF receiver combined with LMS equalization for enhancing distributed VLC uplinks.

title: CRAB-EDM: A Multi-Modal Underwater Crab-inspired Robot with Temporally Sequenced Electro-Discharge Modulation_supp1-3663817.mp4
authors: Yanhong Peng
abstract: Dielectric elastomer actuators (DEA) have emerged as promising candidates for underwater exploration due to their high energy density and flexibility.
