However, the accumulation of residual charges compromises actuation efficiency, and conventional single-mode discharge strategies struggle to balance dynamic responsiveness with locomotion stability. Therefore, we present CRAB-EDM, a multi-modal underwater soft robot inspired by crab locomotion and powered by temporally modulated electro-discharge control. By integrating the distributed coordination mechanism of crab-like multi-foot motion with the electromechanical coupling behavior of DEA, we design a six-foot soft robotic architecture coupled with a tri-modal control framework: high-frequency fast discharge enhances dynamic actuation response, medium-frequency delayed discharge improves displacement-frequency synergy, and low-frequency coordinated discharge reinforces gait stability. Through ballast-limb coordination mechanism with a multi-modal control strategy, the nine-gram robot demonstrates versatile behaviors such as turning, jumping, and overing obstacle. Experimental results validate that the synergy between bio-inspired morphology and residual charge modulation significantly improves locomotion efficiency and terrain adaptability, offering a low-noise, highly adaptable solution for constrained underwater exploration.

title: Spotlight on I&Mer: 2024 IMS Technical Award
authors: Max Cortner
abstract: The IEEE Instrumentation and Measurement Society is a small but very successful part of the world's largest professional organization. We operate through the efforts of volunteers, and because we are small, we get to know each other. So, when the annual society awards are presented, it's good to learn a bit more about them than the photo of the handshake and plaque presentation can convey. Again, this year, I have endeavored to write a series of articles for the IEEE Instrumentation & Measurement Magazine to highlight the five individuals who were selected to receive an award in 2024 (presented at the 2025 I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>MTC in Chemnitz).

title: Adaptive Shared Cascade Navigation Control of Magnetic Microrobots in Unstructured Dynamic Environments_supp1-3658831.mp4
authors: Huaping Wang
abstract: Precise motion control of magnetic microrobots in complex and dynamic environments remains a critical challenge for enabling key applications such as targeted therapy and micromanipulation. Purely manual teleoperation is prone to operator fatigue and error, while fully autonomous systems often lack the robustness and adaptability to handle. Here, we propose a human–machine shared cascade control method for magnetically driven microrobots, which effectively integrates human cognitive intelligence with machine autonomy for collision-free navigation in dynamic environments. The outer-loop hybrid shared control unit smoothly modulates control authority in response to real-time collision risk, dynamically integrating the operator instructions and the autonomous navigation system output guided by the enhanced artificial potential field method to formulate the guidance law. For the inner-loop motion tracking, a data-driven adaptive orientation controller is designed, which integrates a nonlinear feedforward compensator leveraging a Gaussian process regression (GPR) model with a linear feedback controller whose parameters are optimized using the virtual reference feedback tuning (VRFT) method, ensuring fast and precise tracking of the desired motion. The effectiveness of the proposed method was validated through both simulation and physical experiments. In human-subject studies conducted on a physical magnetic actuation platform featuring both static and dynamic obstacle scenarios, quantitative results demonstrate that the shared control strategy significantly outperforms both purely manual and fully autonomous modes across all key metrics, including success rate, task completion time, stability, and safety (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p \lt 0.001$</tex-math> </inline-formula>). Furthermore, successful navigation within a complex gastric model demonstrates the potential of the shared control system for practical application in unstructured environments.

title: Adaptive Granular-Ball Concept-Cognitive Learning for Efficient and Robust Fuzzy Knowledge Representation in Classification Tasks
authors: Doudou Guo, Weihua Xu, Shuyin Xia, Weiping Ding, Yuhua Qian, Kehua Yuan
abstract: In many real-world scenarios, data unreliability and information-processing uncertainty seriously affect the credibility and reliability of artificial intelligence models. Probing a valuable cognitive learning paradigm is crucial for supporting and implementing the proposed artificial intelligence hypothesis. Concept-cognitive learning (CCL), as an emerging cognitive learning technology, investigates the knowledge-learning process using limited data through the lens of concepts. However, the existing CCL research has one prevalent disadvantage: instability and complex cognition. Thus, this work focuses on constructing an effective and interpretable cognitive learning paradigm for acquiring knowledge. Inspired by a multi-granularity knowledge representation, this paper proposes an adaptive and robust fuzzy knowledge representation paradigm, namely granular-ball concept-cognitive learning(GB-CCL). Unlike existing CCL systems, GB-CCL is a non-parameter cognitive learning system that achieves more accurate, faster adaptive capabilities for concept learning. The concept acquired through cognitive learning is structured conceptual knowledge, which is naturally interpretable and robust. Finally, extensive experiments verify that GB-CCL is an effective cognitive learning method with significantly better performance than state-of-the-art models in classification and robust learning.

title: Formal Specification and Control Synthesis of Autonomous Robots Using Rulebooks_supp1-3663976.mp4
authors: Tichakorn Wongpiromsarn
abstract: This paper presents a formal specification framework for planning and control of autonomous robots, focusing on the challenge of managing complex trade-offs among multiple, potentially conflicting objectives. These include hierarchical relationships and non-comparable objectives, some of which may be too complex to be captured by standard additive cost functions. We leverage the rulebook formalism to represent such objectives and their relationships and formulate two control synthesis problems: single-strategy synthesis, which seeks one optimal strategy, and complete synthesis, which computes the full set of optimal strategies with respect to a rulebook, analogous to the Pareto front in multi-objective planning. We show that our formulation generalizes existing temporal logic-based and optimization-based planning and control, providing a unifying framework across robotics, formal methods, control theory, and operations research. For single-strategy, we identify tractable subclasses and present a polynomial-time algorithm that accommodates richer combinations of objectives than prior work. For complete synthesis, we introduce an algorithm to compute all optimal solutions and analyze its computational complexity. In both cases, we present case studies that include complex multi-objective planning problems and demonstrate the practical effectiveness of our approach compared to existing methods.

title: A Low Phase Noise Circular-Coupled Quad-Core Oscillator Employing Dual-Path Synchronization Technique
authors: Xiangxun Zhan, Pui-In Mak, Rui P. Martins, Jun Yin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This article presents a millimeter-wave (mm-Wave) quad-core oscillator utilizing a circular transformer and dual-path synchronization technique to achieve low phase noise (PN). We analyze the mechanism of the PN degradation induced by frequency mismatch between individual cores in the conventional quad-core oscillator utilizing a circular inductor, which reveals that the frequency mismatch between the non-adjacent cores will induce an off-resonance issue, which could significantly degrade the PN. This PN degradation induced by the off-resonance is acerbated in the quad-core oscillator utilizing a circular transformer. Based on the analysis, a dual-path synchronization technique is proposed to eliminate the off-resonance issue by providing a direct synchronization path for either two cores in a circular-coupled quad-core oscillator. The proposed technique can be applied to both inductor- and transformer-based circular-coupled oscillators, preventing PN degradation due to the frequency mismatch. Fabricated in 65-nm CMOS, our quad-core oscillator prototype measures a frequency tuning range of 18.2%, from 22.4 to 26.8 GHz. At the 25.8 GHz carrier frequency, the oscillator achieves a low PN of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\!-\!138$</tex-math> </inline-formula> dBc/Hz at 10 MHz offset while consuming 19.7 mW, corresponding to an excellent FoM of 193.3 dBc/Hz.

title: Calorie Estimation using Ensemble Technique from Recipes of Bangladeshi Foods
authors: Md. Siam Ansary, Amina Brinto
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Chingri malai curry, Ilish macher paturi, Patishapta pitha - these are well known foods of Bangladesh that we have all grown up with. Culturally rich Bangladesh has lots of foods native to it. The recipes of such food items are passed down from generation to generation and gradually they have gained perfection. In this research, we have focused on calorie estimation of these Bangladeshi foods. A dataset containing recipes of Bangladeshi foods has been created and using an BERT based stacking ensemble model, the calories have been predicted. We have observed that the ensemble model can do prediction with great efficiency using the text based dataset. The dataset, codes and all relevant resources of the experiment will be made public for reproduction and verification purposes.

title: Energy-Efficient IoT Sensor Networks Enabled by Reinforcement Clustering Optimization for Prolonged Node Lifespan
authors: Varsha K.S, Charan Nithish Senthil, Keerthitha S, Dhileepan R.K, Krishna Kanth A.T, Vijay S
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) technology transformed manufacturing operations alongside domestic environments and medical facilities and industrial processes and environmental systems. This paper establishes an innovative framework named Energy-Efficient IoT Sensor Networks Enabled by Reinforcement Clustering Optimization (RCO) to maximize operational lifespan and efficiency of IoT sensor networks. The framework integrates reinforcement learning with dynamic clustering algorithms to optimize energy usage and extend node operational time and maintain efficient data packet transmission. The proposed model obtained performance assessments through comparison with three traditional clustering methods, LEACH and K-Means and Fuzzy C-Means. Assessment results included network duration, energy efficiency, packet delivery ratio (PDR), latency and achieved a 95.4% data delivery success and 900 rounds of increased overall network operation length. RCO model resulted in a 95.4% accurate data transfer rate which increased the total network operational time to 900 rounds while providing 50% better performance than LEACH. Readings of the model indicate it cuts average power usage for nodes down to 40% below traditional approaches. Deep Q-Learning and Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) enabled dynamic near-real-time decision systems which improved cluster formation and energy management thus extending network operational lifetime and reliability. The framework shows valuable potential in smart cities coupled with environmental surveillance and industrial IoT because it provides critical energy efficiency alongside real-time data transfer capabilities.

title: Enhanced Cyber Threat Detection Using Hybrid Deep Learning Models
authors: Gannoju Vinod Kumar, Sampath Kumar Tallapally
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The high rate of complex cyber threats created is challenging to the traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) whose false-positive rates are commonly high, and they have low adaptability to the changing trends of attacks. Overall, this paper describes a hybrid-type deep learning architecture, which combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to extract features automatically and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to learn time dependencies to boost the accuracy of detecting cyber threats. The model was trained and tested in the NSL-KDD, and CICIDS2017 datasets with overall accuracy at 98.72%, precision of 98.41%, recall of 98.19%, and improving up to 6.3 percent compared to the conventional machine learning baselines with false positives minimised by 4.8 percent. Comparison to independent CNN and LSTM architectures showed the hybrid model raised the F1-score by 5.1 percent. These findings prove the capability of spatio-temporal coupled spatial and sequential learning to identify complex attack patterns with greater strength. This hybrid deep learning architecture can deliver an effective scalable system to the next generation of the IDS solution with a high potential to be flexible to address dynamic threat environments.

title: Design of Polygon Shape SRR and CSRR on Circular Patch Antenna for Satellite Application
authors: P.Amala Vijaya Sri, B.Kireeti, P.V.N.Lakshmi, P.Bajibalu, Ketavath Kumar Naik
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: A compact circular shape patch antenna loaded with SRR and CSRR for satellite application is presented in this paper. The circular shape antenna model enhances the gain of the antenna and octagonal shape SRR and CSRR enhances the bandwidth and antenna radiation characteristics. An asymmetrical CPW feed is designed to enhance the reflection coefficient and to operate the antenna for dual band frequency. The antenna produces a maximum bandwidth of 2.79 GHz (7.3 GHz – 10.09 GHz) with two operating frequencies 9 GHz and 9.75 GHz respectively. A maximum gain of 10.9 dB and 12.1 dB has been obtained for two operating frequencies. Hence the antenna is used for uplink and downlink data transmission at satellite communication application.

title: Design of Super Lift-LUO DC-DC Converter for Electric Vehicle Applications
authors: Gunda Anuhya, Mohammad Mahabube Sunayn, A. R. Vijay Babu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The transition from conventional automobiles to electric vehicles (EVs) is transforming the transportation sector in response to growing apprehensions regarding environmental sustainability and energy efficiency. An essential component of electric vehicle power design is the necessity to transform low-voltage direct current from battery sources (often 24 V to 48 V) into elevated voltage levels (e.g., 200 V or greater) needed for propulsion and auxiliary systems. Traditional DC-DC converter topologies, including Boost, SEPIC, Cuk, and Buck-Boost, encounter constraints such as increasing duty cycle demands, negative output polarity, and efficiency degradation at high gain levels. This work examines the design and simulation of a Super Lift Luo Converter (SLLC), a sophisticated DC-DC architecture that attains elevated voltage gain using cascaded energy-lifting methodologies. The converter is designed to elevate a 48 V input to a consistent 200 V output, appropriate for electric vehicle traction and onboard electronics. A Proportional-Integral (PI) controller is incorporated into the system to ensure output regulation amidst varying input and load conditions. The simulation utilizes MATLAB/Simulink, and the findings validate the converter's capacity to provide high voltage gain, enhanced stability, and little ripple. This research demonstrates the SLLC as a reliable, efficient, and scalable converter for future electric vehicle applications.

title: Edge-AI Enabled Telemetry System for Real-Time Predictive Maintenance in Commercial Computing Frameworks
authors: Dharmeandra Ahuja
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Commercial computing systems such as cloud infrastructures and data centers must be very reliable and able to recover rapidly from faults in their current, data-heavy environment. To address critical thermal and hardware anomalies, this paper introduces a novel Edge-Ai-enabled telemetry framework designed for predictive maintenance. This paper presents an Edge-Ai-enabled telemetry framework that leverages environmental sensor data to detect faults with high accuracy and low latency. By utilizing data from 54 sensor nodes monitoring temperature, humidity, light, and voltage, and applying the CatBoost algorithm optimized for categorical features, the proposed framework achieves superior predictive performance on an ARM-based edge computing platform. The model attains an accuracy of 99.80%, precision of 99.81%, recall of 99.65%, and an F1score of 98.70%, outperforming benchmark models such as K-Nearest Neighbors and Naïve Bayes. The framework's suitability for real-world usage is shown by its capacity to recognize and address errors in commercial contexts, as tested thoroughly using confusion matrices and learning curves. The overall findings demonstrate that the Edge-Ai-enabled telemetry framework, powered by the CatBoost algorithm, delivers highly accurate and reliable real-time fault detection for commercial computing systems.

title: Deepfake Detection for Images Using Inception Resnet V2 Architecture Along With CNN
authors: Sangavi V, Abishake S, Dhayal S R
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Deepfake technology has proliferated quickly, posing challenges in distinguishing genuine and altered images, particularly in critical sectors like social media, media, and security. This study introduces a novel approach for detecting image-based deepfakes using convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and the Inception-ResNet v2 architecture. The proposed system utilizes the Inception-ResNet v2 model to process both overarching and localized visual data, capturing intricate patterns and distinguishing characteristics at various scales. MTCNN (Multi-task Cascaded Convolutional Networks) is employed for precise facial recognition, with subsequent image classification into "real" or "fake" categories by the Inception-ResNet v2 model based on learned facial features Additionally, the system employs Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) visualization to highlight areas of the image that influenced the deep fake prediction. This incorporation enhances both detection precision and interpretability by providing visibility into the model's decision-making process. The resultant program provides an excellent solution for deep fake detection in photos, addressing the rising worry about synthetic media in a variety of applications.

title: Enhanced Intrusion Detection Using Stacked Autoencoder with XGBoost Classifier
authors: Hari Vinayak M.V., Jarin T.
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of digital infrastructure, safeguarding networks against the continually advancing cyber threats has become a critical challenge. Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) serve as a frontline defense by identifying suspicious activities and unauthorized access attempts. Among various detection approaches, anomaly-based techniques are particularly effective in recognizing previously unknown attacks. The primary aim of this research endeavor is to develop an efficient IDS model that can accurately detect network anomalies and optimize detection performance using intelligent deep learning (DL) strategies. This work introduces a novel hybrid framework that integrates a Stacked Sparse Autoencoder (SSA) with the eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) classifier, termed SSA-XGB. The SSA component is responsible for extracting deep latent features from network traffic, while XGBoost is employed to perform high-performance classification based on these features. Experimental evaluations were conducted using the CICIoT2023 dataset, and the results demonstrate that the proposed SSA-XGB model achieves high accuracy, improved detection rates, and reduced false positives compared to traditional techniques, confirming its robustness and effectiveness in detecting emerging cyber threats.

title: LSTM networks based Random Forest and XGBoost Classifiers for Social Network
authors: Thilagavathi K, Aarya. V, Raghavee. V. N, Joothi Shree. S. J, V Leena Catherine, Tharshini S
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The LegalInsights-AI framework presents a hybrid deep learning and machine learning approach to evaluating judicial decisions under the Criminal Procedure Act, 2022 in India's justice system. This model integrates Legal-BERT, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, and an ensemble of Random Forest and XGBoost classifiers to assess procedural compliance, identify misapplications of legal provisions, and analyze case outcomes. The framework was trained and validated on a diverse dataset comprising Supreme Court, High Court, and District Court rulings. Experimental results demonstrate that LegalInsights-AI achieves an accuracy of 96.59%, significantly outperforming traditional NLP techniques and rule-based models. The model effectively detects procedural compliance in 80% of analyzed cases, identifies misapplications in 25.9% of cases, and provides a time reduction of 81.2% in case analysis compared to human legal review. Furthermore, the AI model closely aligns with expert legal assessments, achieving a 93.2% match in procedural compliance detection and 91.5% in legal provision application accuracy. These results indicate the potential of LegalInsights-AI to improve judicial transparency, optimize case evaluations, and assist legal professionals in ensuring procedural fairness. Future work will focus on enhancing interpretability, expanding datasets, and integrating explainable AI (XAI) techniques to further refine legal compliance analysis.

title: Development And Enhancement Of Bengali Paraphrasing Model Using Domain Specific Knowledge
authors: Faisal Ibn Aziz, Muhammad Nazrul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Paraphrase generation in low-resource languages, such as Bengali, remains a challenging task owing to the insufficient availability of high-quality, domain-adapted datasets and the limited support from existing pre-trained models. Building upon the "BanglaHealth" dataset, which was curated to address the scarcity of Bengali paraphrasing resources in the health domain, this research presents the development of a domain-specific paraphrasing model named "BanglaT5-mHealth". This model is based on a Transformer-based encoder-decoder architecture and fine-tuned through an incremental training strategy using progressively larger subsets of health-related Bengali sentences. A domain-specific approach was adopted to better capture the linguistic nuances and specialized vocabulary of the healthcare context, ensuring accurate and diverse paraphrased outputs. Key training innovations include a custom learning configuration and controlled generation settings to optimize fluency and semantic preservation. The proposed model demonstrates significant improvements in generating contextually appropriate paraphrases compared to baseline Bengali language models. This work contributes a robust domain-aware paraphrasing framework and paves the way for downstream applications such as medical chatbots, patient education systems, and clinical text simplification in the Bengali language.

title: Deep Learning Architectures For Trait Gene To Phenotype Prediction
authors: Kavali Pavan, Mahaboob Shaik Basha, Sunkari Sai Krishna, Rama Chandra Rao M, M. A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Understanding how genetic makeup influences physical traits remains one of the key challenges in modern biology and agriculture. As the demand for sustainable food production continues to grow, there’s a pressing need for advanced methods that can reliably predict crop performance based on genetic data. In this project, we introduce a deep learning-based approach called Trait Gene to Phenotype (TrG2P), which uses single-nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) data to model the complex relationship between genotype and phenotype.Our system combines convolutional neural networks (CNNs), transfer learning, and ensemble techniques to extract meaningful patterns from high-dimensional genetic datasets. Using the Rice299 dataset, which comprises over 44,000 SNPs, the model demonstrates strong performance in predicting various traits, including yield, growth rate, and disease resistance. To assess accuracy, we used regression metrics including Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and R-squared (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>). The final ensemble model achieved an impressive R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.867, indicating a high level of predictive accuracy.

title: Predictive Analytics for Human Resource Management: A Hybrid AI Model for Talent Acquisition and Retention
authors: Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed, Md Eahia Ansari, Md. Naimul Ahsan, Towfika Salam, Rakibul Islam, Robiul Hoque, Nusrat Ameri
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Accurate and readable human resource (HR) projection is crucial for strategic personnel planning, especially in talent recruitment and turnover. The study presents HR-SelectRetain-XGDL, a combined machine learning model that merges Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), Deep Learning (DL), and Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE) to address the dual task of candidate selection and attrition prediction. When utilizing the IBM HR Analytics dataset, our model performs better when run through hierarchical feature selection, deep neural networks, and ensemble classification. The accuracy of 98.41% for candidate selection and 97.00% for retention prediction by the system developed is both better than two state-of-the-art baselines, DNN-RF and RF-RFE, on all the most essential measures of precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC. Systematic evaluations such as confusion matrices, ROC curve analysis, and training dynamics continually validate the stability and generalizability of the model. By incorporating explainability and achieving low misclassification error rates, HR-SelectRetain-XGDL offers a transparent, stable, and scalable framework for data-driven HR decision-making. This research contributes a deployable AI system to enable intelligent talent management in enterprise systems.

title: Scalable Training of Artificial Neural Network With Adaptive Sparse Connectivity Inspired By Network Science Strategies
authors: Chinni Naidu, Arvapally Ashish, Sakila Kiran, Yogash Chandra Joshi, M A Jabbar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Deep Neural Networks(DNNs) have achieved significant success across various domains, but they usually need a lot of memory and computing power.This makes it hard to use them on devices like mobile phones or embedded systems.Addressing these limitations is critical for real-world deployment, especially on resource-constrained devices.To overcome this limitations,this paper presents a Sparse CNN model with adaptive connectivity inspired by network science principles. Unlike traditional dense CNNs, the proposed model dynamically prunes and regrows connections during training, preserving essential features while significantly reducing active parameters.The model architecture is initialized and is evaluated on the CIFAR-10 dataset. Implemented using PyTorch, the Sparse CNN achieves comparable or superior classification accuracy 84 with lower memory usage and faster convergence.The results confirm that adaptive sparse connectivity in CNNs enhances scalability and efficiency, making them ideal for devices with limited resources.

title: Brain Tumor Detection in MRI Images with YOLOv12
authors: Md. Uzzal Mia, Md Sarwar Hosain, Md. Taz Warul Mulk, Md. Noman Bhuiyan, Md. Rifat Hossen, Liton Chandra Paul
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The precise identification of brain tumors via MRI imaging continues to pose a considerable challenge within the domain of medical diagnostics. Although conventional deep learning models have shown effectiveness, they often encounter difficulties in detecting accuracy and efficiency in various types of tumors. In this research, we introduce an enhanced approach for brain tumor detection that employs the latest YOLOv12 object detection framework. We assess and contrast the performance of YOLOv12 with several other leading models, illustrating its superior detection accuracy. The YOLOv12n model notably achieves the highest mAP@50 of 93.3%, outperforming previous YOLO versions and conventional techniques. The model is trained and evaluated using a comprehensive MRI dataset that includes various tumour types, thereby ensuring its generalisability and robustness. These findings highlight YOLOv12’s potential as a reliable, quick, and accurate method for real-time brain tumor diagnosis and medical picture analysis.

title: Air Quality Prediction Using Ensemble Gradient Boosting Model
authors: Rajeev Ranjan, Shubham Chandel, Devanshi Dhiman, Harshita Kadian, Garv Saxena
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Air pollution poses a significant environmental threat, particularly in densely populated cities like Delhi. Accurate forecasting of air pollutant levels is essential for public health planning and policy- making. This research focuses on predicting PM2.5 concentrations using ensemble gradient boosting regression models. A dataset of hourly air quality measurements from Delhi was collected from Kaggle, including features such as PM10, NO, NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, NOx, and CO. After preprocessing the data handling missing values, normalizing features, and splitting into training and testing sets, multiple models were trained: Gradient Boosting Regressor, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, HistGradientBoosting, NGBoost, and AdaBoost. Each model’s performance was evaluated using R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, RMSE, and MAE metrics. Gradient Boosting and NGBoost achieved the highest accuracy and closely aligned with actual PM2.5 values. Visual comparisons further confirmed the robustness of these models in tracking pollution trends. The study concludes that ensemble gradient boosting models offer reliable prediction capabilities and can support early warning systems. Future enhancements may include integrating weather data, hybrid deep learning models, and deployment in real-time monitoring systems to improve forecasting accuracy and real-world applicability.

title: Interpretable Fine-Grained Hate Speech Detection in Bangla: CNN with LIME and Comparative Evaluation
authors: Chowdhury Mohammad Mehedi, Fariha Tasnim, Nusrat Jahan, Mohammed Borhan Uddin, Tanvinur Rahman Siam, Masum Ahmed Mozumder, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Detecting hate content in Bangla is notably difficult because of primarily stemming from a scarcity of labeled datasets and the nuanced complexity of fine-grained hate categories. Existing approaches have largely relied on traditional ML models trained on imbalanced datasets, limiting their effectiveness and generalization capabilities. To bridge these gaps, this paper presents a novel, manually annotated Bangla hate speech dataset with three fine-grained categories : Personal Attack, Misogyny/Sexist Abuse, and Ideological Hate. To mitigate the inherent class imbalance, synonym-based data augmentation was employed to create a balanced corpus for robust model training. A comprehensive comparative study was conducted between classical ML models LR and SVM and DL models based on LSTM, BiLSTM, and CNN based deep learning model. Experimental results demonstrate that the CNN model , trained on the augmented and balanced dataset, significantly outperforms the classical models, achieving an accuracy of 94%. In comparison, SVM and LR achieved accuracies of 83% and 69%, respectively. Furthermore, LSTM and BiLSTM models achieved accuracies of 92.47% and 92.36%, respectively.To enhance interpretability and trust in model decisions, this study also applies Explainable AI (XAI) techniques using the LIME framework, providing insights into how the models make predictions and highlighting the most influential features for each hate category. Ultimately, this paper’s findings confirm the fundamental need for data augmentation and highlights the superior capability of DL models in discerning the underlying context for effective fine-grained hate speech classification in languages with limited available resources, such as Bangla.

title: Machine Learning-Powered Defect Prediction Models for Scalable Software Testing Development
authors: Anil Kumar Devarapalem
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Software defect prediction (SDP) is an essential part of ensuring the reliability and quality of software. In order to find problematic systems, such as units or classes, early on in the process, a defect prediction method is used. The primary goal of software defect prediction is to lower the time, effort, and money spent by identifying the most likely software flaws to be introduced into the program. This study offers a new, state-of- the-art method for predicting software defects using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) that can increase software quality while decreasing development costs. It can make the following contributions to the software defect prediction field. The suggested convolutional neural network (CNN) model was trained and tested on PyTorch, and it achieved an impressive 99.80% accuracy, 99.78% precision, 99.98% recall, and 98.88% F1-score. A baseline comparison of models, text logistic regression, text support vector and text deep neural networks, was conducted to suggest the better performance and stability of CNN model. There is little overfitting as observed by the training and validation curves which implies a high generalization ability. These findings support the outcome that Deep Learning can be effectively used to combine with advanced preprocessing methods to produce a very reliable software defect prediction that is quite accurate.

title: An Interpretable Machine Learning Model for Oral Cancer Survival Rate Prediction
authors: Saravanan Parthasarathy, Vaishnavi Jayaraman, Pagalavan N, Jude Januarius S M, Sathya P
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Oral cancer is a significant global health problem with high mortality rate. To provide guidelines for treatment procedure and personalized care, forecasting the survival rate is essential. This study proposes a robust machine learning (ML) framework to predict survival rates of oral cancer patients. The dataset was normalized using the min-max scaler, then various ML algorithms were evaluated, with AdaBoost emerging as the top performer. To refine the predictive capability of the models, Principal Component Analysis (PCA) was applied. The key components were identified by PCA, and models were re-tuned using Bayesian Optimization (BO). The proposed Survival-Predictor model is a combination of PCA-Bayesian Optimized Decision Tree model achieved the R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value of 0.9613 with the run time of 0.42 seconds. It demonstrates that well-optimized traditional models could offer both high accuracy and interpretability, making them suitable for clinical applications.

title: Bio-AI Fusion: A Deep Learning Framework for Type 2 Diabetes Detection and Clinical Risk Pathway Analysis
authors: Satyanarayana Nimmala, K Purushotham, Shankar Nayak Bhukya, Sagar Mekala, Pallati Narsimhulu, Maragoni Mahendar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Type 2 diabetes (T2D) is a major concern for public health because more people are getting it, and many of the risks are linked to lifestyle. If problems are found early and treated, it is possible to avoid more serious consequences. In this work, we introduce Bio-Ai Fusion, a deep learning model for finding and analyzing T2D risks in clinical settings. The Experiments are conducted on the Pima Indians Diabetes Dataset, which has eight physiological and biochemical parameters, to train and evaluate a multilayer neural network model for predicting diabetes presence. Steps in the methodology are data preprocessing, normalizing features, and training the network using a fully connected feedforward neural network. The results of the model are compared to those of logistic regression, SVM, and random forest, which are traditional machine learning classifiers. The proposed model reaches an accuracy of 86% and also shows better F1-score and sensitivity. Using SHAP, we can see which factors are most important in predicting the outcome, which helps suggest ways to reverse the risk. Our approach enables the quick deployment of mobile and hospital devices, supporting real-time diabetes screening and helping with planning a healthy lifestyle. The research uses AI and clinical data mining to give personalized care to people with diabetes.

title: Advanced Phishing Detection Using Boosting and Voting-Based Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Kavitha M, Eniya P, Archana Devi S, Kavinkumar C, Badrinath S P
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Phishing, as a type of cyber-attack, is still considered as one of the most significant security threats everywhere on the planet, which makes deception and malicious URLs an ideal solution to steal sensitive data like credential, finance, etc. Zero-day attacks are difficult to detect using common detection approaches such as blacklist and rule-based systems. In this study, a high-frequency phishing detection system based on ensemble machine learning models (XGBoost, CatBoost, Voting Classifier, Stacked Ensemble) is proposed. The classifier is based on lexical and statistical URL features only, without depending on third-party services for on-the-fly classification. Some features - such as URL length, special character rate, IP address presence - were designed and extracted based on feature ranking, correlation analysis and dimensionality reduction. Every weighted model has its own individual merits. Scalability and robustness are one of the strengths of XGBoost and CatBoost. The Voting Classifier aggregates predictions from several models to improve the consistency. The Stacked Ensemble uses the base model predictions as input to a logistic regression meta-learner, resulting in significant improvement in accuracy. In experiments on a set of 11k URLs, the Stacked Ensemble consistently produced the best accuracy (98.6%) and F1-value (98.55%). This architecture has high potential for real-time applications such as web browsers, email gateways, cyber security.

title: Classification of Soybean Leaf Diseases Using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
authors: Vidyarani R. Chogule, P. J. Kulkarni
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Soybean (Glycine max) is a globally significant legume crop, playing a vital role in food, feed, and industrial applications. However, its cultivation is frequently hampered by foliar diseases such as Bacterial Blight and Downy Mildew, leading to substantial yield losses. Traditional methods of disease detection rely on manual inspection, which is time-consuming, subjective, and often lacks accuracy under field conditions. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a deep learning-based system using a custom-designed Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for the automated classification of soybean leaf diseases. A balanced dataset comprising 3,000 labeled images across three classes—Healthy, Bacterial Blight, and Downy Mildew—was used. The images underwent preprocessing steps such as resizing, normalization, and data augmentation to improve model robustness. The CNN architecture, optimized through iterative experimentation, achieved a validation accuracy of 96.7%, with precision, recall, and F1-scores exceeding 95% across all classes. Comparative analysis with existing methods highlights the proposed model’s lightweight nature, reduced complexity, and improved classification performance. The results underscore the potential of integrating CNN-based models into precision agriculture systems for early disease detection, thereby enabling timely intervention and reducing economic losses for farmers.

title: Secure Multi-Satellite Collaborations With ISAC
authors: Xuyang Zhang, Zihan Ni, Xuanhe Yang, Xiaqing Miao, Shuai Wang, Gaofeng Pan, Jianping An, Dusit Niyato
abstract: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite systems with sensing capabilities are widely regarded as promoting reliable and efficient communication services globally. This paper proposes a Multi-Satellite Collaborative Security System with Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC-MSC). Considering the potential benefits of LEO satellites and ISAC, we exploit sensing performance in the MSC system by jointly optimizing LEO satellite assignments, communication Beamforming (BF) vectors, and sensing BF vectors. Specifically, we design improved Continuous Particle Swarm (CP) optimization and Discrete Particle Swarm (DP) optimization algorithms to maximize the target sensing Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) for LEO satellite assignments. Additionally, with respect to the BF vector optimization, we develop Power Approximation (PA) optimization algorithm, Inner Approximation (IA) optimization algorithm, and Joint Sensing and Communication BF (JSC-BF) optimization algorithm. Multiple algorithms are tightly integrated and alternately iterated. Numerical results show that: 1) the JSC-BF algorithms outperform the PA and IA algorithms in terms of sensing performance and communication secrecy rate; 2) compared to the single satellite case, the ISAC-MSC system performance approximately linear growth, and has strong extensibility; 3) with imperfect MSC synchronization case, the communication secrecy rate appears inflection point and stabilization, but the JSC-BF algorithms still have excellent performance.

title: LungXResViT: A Robust Hybrid DL Framework for Accurate Lung Cancer Classification with XAI
authors: Tahmid Enam Shrestha, Jariatun Islam, Afsana Rabeya Rukaia, Md. Fazle Rabbi Rizon, Sawvik Kar Dipto, Md. Asraf Ali
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Lung cancer continues to be the top cause of cancer-related fatalities globally, making early detection crucial for improving survival rates. Traditional diagnostic approaches are slow and prone to delays, necessitating the development of effective automated strategies. This research introduces a robust hybrid deep learning framework that combines Vision Transformer (ViT) with ResNet-152 for precise lung cancer classification, integrating explainable AI methods such as Grad-CAM++ and LIME to improve the transparency and interpretability of the model's predictions. The suggested model was trained and validated on a publicly accessible Kaggle dataset, categorized into three classes. Multiple preprocessing strategies, including resizing, normalization, and data augmentation, were employed on the dataset. The model secured an excellent testing accuracy of 99.99%, surpassing recent state-of-the-art approaches. Results indicate enhanced diagnostic performance, accompanied by clear visual explanations, which promote clinician confidence and informed decision-making. The proposed method could help reduce diagnostic delays and support the early detection of lung cancer.

title: RAGClin: A Privacy-Preserving Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework for Clinical Question
authors: Basavaraj N Hiremath, Savitha Hiremath, Nandini R, Neethu J, Nisarga V P, G S Tejas
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Generative AI is rapidly transforming healthcare by enabling smarter tools for clinical decision support, automated documentation, and patient interaction. However, alongside these opportunities, current Large Language Models (LLMs) present significant challenges: they frequently produce hallucinations—information that appears credible but is factually incorrect—and they often depend on cloud-based deployment, raising critical concerns about patient data privacy and compliance with healthcare regulations.As our key contribution, we present RAGClin, a privacy-preserving Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework designed for clinical question-answering. RAGClin combines synthetic medical documents with a local semantic search pipeline to supply verifiable context during inference. Using MiniLM for lightweight dense embeddings and a FAISS vector database for top-k cosine similarity retrieval, relevant context is dynamically fetched and appended to the user query. This combined input is processed by a locally hosted LLaMA 3.1 model via the Ollama runtime, ensuring that sensitive information remains fully local.By grounding generative outputs in domain-specific retrieved knowledge, our contribution mitigates hallucinations, improves factual accuracy, and upholds strict privacy requirements. Preliminary experiments show high semantic precision, reduced hallucination rates, and near real-time performance on both high-performance compute nodes and consumer-grade hardware. These results highlight RAGClin as a practical and secure approach for deploying trustworthy Generative AI in privacy-sensitive healthcare applications.

title: Hybrid Protocol Design Leveraging MAC Operation to Optimize Routing Decisions in UAV-Aided IoT Networks
authors: Saud Al Abedin, Mushtaree Iffat Mouree, Md. Obaidur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: In UAV-aided IoT networks, data can be collected from IoT devices by a UAV, which can act as a mobile sink also. These collected data can be transferred to the cloud or base station. In this paper, a hybrid protocol is designed combining medium access control (MAC) layer and routing layer for UAV-aided IoT networks. Instead of a fixed terrain in an IoT network, a fixed-wing UAV is used to calculate terrain size, device density using a meander scanning approach, which ensures network scalability. Moreover, Ordering Points to Identify the Clustering Structure (OPTICS) is used for density-based clustering of IoT devices. This protocol incorporates carrier sense multiple access with collision avoidance (CSMA/CA) for the registration purpose of IoT devices and time division multiple access (TDMA) with fixed time-slots for the device to cluster-head data transmissions. The solution follows the route of a cluster-heads location utilizing the Zigzag routing strategy, and the UAV adjusts its altitude employing the Multiplicative Increase/Multiplicative Decrease (MIMD) for energy efficiency. Extensive simulation verifies the improvement in performance of the proposed hybrid protocol compared with the modified CSMA/CA and traditional TDMA in terms of energy efficiency, network throughput, delay minimization, and network lifetime.

title: Special Issue on Human Meets AI
authors: Nadin Kökciyan, Pradeep K. Murukannaiah, Pinar Yolum
abstract: The collaboration between humans and artificial intelligence (AI) presents both immense opportunities and significant challenges. On the one hand, as AI systems get integrated into daily life and professional environments, they can enhance productivity, creativity, and decision making. On the other, ensuring how humans will interact with, use, and benefit from these technologies remains a central challenge. This special issue brings together three articles that analyze this central challenge.

title: NeuroPhysio: Explainable EEG-Based AI for Affective and Cognitive Load Recognition in Digital Health-Integrated Learning Systems
authors: Ambati Rami Reddy, Sridevi Veerasingam, B. Shameedha Begum
abstract: The ability of intelligent health-aware e-learning systems to recognize Affective States (AS) and Cognitive Load (CL) determines their success in personalizing instruction and improving learners' engagement. The proposed NeuroPhysio framework integrates an EEG-based analytic system that uses handcrafted feature extraction and deep neural model to study AS and CL for 3s and 5s windows of EEG. This research work applied statistical methods and entropy analysis to extract handcrafted features from frequency bands to identify brief and prolonged neurophysiological patterns during learning activities. The subject-dependent experimental results of 22 participants showed XGBoost achieved the high recognition accuracy of 88.00% for AS and 89.13% for CL assessment using a 5s window. The CNN model obtained good results by reaching 82.01% for AS and 84.83% for CL assessment of the same 5s window. The EEG-augmented models demonstrated exceptional accuracy in AS with XGBoost reaching 97.27% and for CL assessment CNN reaching 96.19%. The subject-independent results of the XGBoost and CNN models demonstrated stable performance within individual participants. However, their ability to generalize across participants showed a noticeable decline. The NeuroPhysio framework for affect-awareness that relates EEG to an adaptive feedback system for health, technology, and education.

title: A Faster μ-PMU Algorithm using Arctangent Framework with Parameter Step Change
authors: Kuber Saxena, Subhransu Ranjan Samantaray, Sarita Nanda
abstract: Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs) play a vital role in modern power systems by enabling real-time monitoring, control, and protection under dynamic conditions. However, their performance exhibits inaccuracy and longer response time when encountered with abrupt transients. To address these limitations, the paper introduces a highly responsive and precise algorithm for estimating synchrophasor, frequency and rate of change of frequency (ROCOF) that effectively cope with sudden amplitude or phase changes. The approach integrates the initial frequency estimation with a Logarithmic Entropy Ratio strategy for effective transient detection. A step modeling is used based on the Arctangent Laplacian Continuous Mixed p-Norm (ALCMpN) algorithm on asymmetric windows. Subsequently, a compensation technique enables blend between pre and post estimates which eliminates the influence of phase step and reduces the ROCOF error. IEC/IEEE 60255-118-1a compliance tests show that the proposed algorithm achieves TVE < 0.001%, FE < 0.05 mHz, and RFE < 0.005 Hz/s which are within the P-class constraint. Field validation using Grid Event Signature Library (GESL) test data further verifies robust performance under real fault conditions. Real-time validation using RTDS–DSP hardware demonstrates reliable operation confirming its suitability for real-time PMU and protection applications in active distribution networks.

title: Blank Page
authors: N/A

title: Impact-Based Maintenance Efficiency Modeling for Multi-Component Systems with Condition-Based Component Selection
authors: Lamia May, Mohamed Arezki Mellal, Sameer Al-Dahidi, Michael Pecht, Youcef Khelfaoui
abstract: This paper develops an impact-based maintenance efficiency model that dynamically links maintenance effectiveness to the number and criticality of replacement components in multi-component repairable systems. Unlike existing approaches that assume static efficiency parameters, the model enables maintenance decisions that adapt to actual system degradation states. The efficiency formulation decomposes into a risk fraction capturing the proportion of weighted system degradation addressed, and a coverage factor with a diminishing-returns parameter reflecting the criticality distribution of maintained components. A condition-based selection rule achieves natural component rotation without explicit constraints, ensuring an equitable distribution of maintenance efforts while prioritizing high-criticality degraded components. Statistical validation using maximum likelihood estimation demonstrates parameter recovery within 2–8% of true values, while predictive performance evaluation shows 93% reduction in failure prediction error compared to approaches ignoring maintenance effects. A case study on a heterogeneous 20-component industrial system demonstrates that the proposed condition-based approach achieves a 30% cost reduction versus a no-maintenance baseline and a 5% improvement over fixed-selection strategies. Furthermore, the natural rotation mechanism maintains 65% of system components across maintenance epochs.

title: Non-Line Of Sight (Nlos) Material Classification Using Moulti-Modal Fusion And Attention Mechanism-Based U-Net Approach: Anlos-R Dataset
authors: Dilan Onat Alakuş, İbrahim Türkoğlu
abstract: Non-Line-of-Sight (NLOS) object detection is an important research problem for inferring position, shape, and material properties in situations where direct optical access is not possible. This study proposes a multi-modal learning architecture with attention mechanisms to classify material types using only reflected acoustic waves. The proposed system consists of an attention-supported U-Net-based Reflection Isolation Network, which isolates reflection regions, and a multi-modal fusion classifier that combines spectral-temporal features. Within the scope of the study, the ANLOS-R (Acoustic Non-Line-of-Sight with Reflection) dataset was created, consisting of 1,440 echo samples collected using three different microphone–speaker configurations. This dataset was used for attention-based segmentation of echo intensity maps and extraction of acoustic features; the obtained spectral and temporal representations were evaluated using both deep learning and machine learning-based classifiers. Experimental results show that the single feature-based GRU (Gated Recurrent Unit) model achieved 65% accuracy, while the proposed fusion model reached 74% accuracy. Furthermore, the system demonstrated high generalization capability in both single and multiple material combinations. The proposed method improves NLOS detection in acoustic environments with low signal-to-noise ratios and provides a robust foundation for future acoustic imaging and autonomous detection systems.

title: STARNet: Stacked Transfer-Aware for Robust Remaining Useful Life Prediction for C-MAPSS Multi-Regime Engines
authors: Dimple Kapoor, Deepali Gupta, Shivani AgarwaI, Mudita Uppal, Sapna Juneja, Manoj Kumar Sharma
abstract: Predictive Maintenance (PdM) plays a critical role in reducing unplanned downtime and extending asset longevity in industrial systems like aircraft engines, manufacturing equipment, and energy infrastructure. Proper estimation of the Remaining Useful Life (RUL) of parts allows for preventive maintenance scheduling and increased operational efficiency, safety, and cost savings. However, the development of robust RUL prediction models remains challenging due to high variability in operating conditions and fault modes. Existing deep learning models tend to demonstrate exceptionally good performance on individual datasets but are unable to generalize across diverse regimes without retraining, which makes them less reliable and scalable for real-world deployment. To overcome existing constraints, this study proposes STARNet, a robust RUL prediction framework that incorporates a multi-scale Conv–BiLSTM with attention pooling, window statistics fusion, and mixture-of-experts encoding. The framework incorporates phase-aware normalization and delta features to improve adaptability across multi-regime subsets (FD002/FD004) of the NASA CMAPSS dataset. Furthermore, self-supervised pretraining and targeted transfer learning between subsets are employed to improve representation quality and reduce overfitting, while Monte Carlo dropout provides reliable uncertainty-aware inference. A multi-seed ensemble with nonlinear stacking ensures stable and accurate predictions across all four subsets. The model is thoroughly tested using a multi-metric approach consisting of Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), Coefficient of Determination (R²) and NASA Score. In this study, STARNet, a robust RUL prediction network that illustrates steady performance gains across all subsets of C-MAPSS, with average RMSE and score improvements of 5.91% and 13.23%, respectively, has been proposed over existing state-of-the-art models. The findings consistently surpass traditional and cutting-edge deep learning benchmarks, confirming the efficacy, generality, and deployment suitability of the developed framework for real-world predictive maintenance tasks.

title: SpinOut: Enhanced Rotation-based Quantization for LLM by Outlier Injection
authors: Sangki Park, Ki-Seok Chung
abstract: Quantization is a crucial technique for deploying Large Language Models (LLMs) in resource-constrained environments. However, minimizing performance degradation due to outliers in activation distributions remains a significant challenge, especially in low-precision quantization. Rotation-based quantization methods have emerged as promising approaches to mitigate outlier effects by transforming the distribution of weights and activations. However, existing methods either suffer from high performance variance due to random rotations or performance degradation when the calibration sample is not sufficient. In this paper, we propose SpinOut, a novel method that enhances rotation-matrix training for LLM quantization by selectively injecting outliers into outlier-sensitive layers.We introduce a method to score layer sensitivity that quantitatively measures each layer’s responsiveness to outliers using Kurtosis and performance metrics, and propose a search algorithm to determine the best subset of layers for outlier injection. By intentionally injecting artificial outliers during training, SpinOut makes rotation matrices more robust to outliers, leading to improved quantization performance. Experimental results on the Llama-2 7B and the Llama-3.2 1B/3B models demonstrate that SpinOut outperforms existing rotation-based quantization methods across various bit configurations. In the W4A4KV4 quantization setting, SpinOut achieves 0.09, 0.83, and 0.12 lower WikiText2 perplexity compared to widely-known SpinQuant, QuaRot, and AMXFP4, respectively, on Llama-2 7B. Furthermore, SpinOut reduces the required number of training samples and the iteration counts by 75% and 50% compared to SpinQuant while achieving a lower performance variance (0.23 vs. 0.3), demonstrating both efficiency and stability. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in most experimental settings, including W4A4KV16 quantization, and in the W4A8KV16 configuration, it even surpasses weight-only quantization methods.

title: Anomaly Detection for Vehicle Diagnostics based on OBD Snapshots with Cause Investigation
authors: Veljko Vučinić, Luca Seidel, Nikola Lukežić, Frank Hantschel, Thomas Kotschenreuther, Dragan Aleksendrić, Eric Sax
abstract: Vehicle diagnostic systems are crucial for the normal operation of vehicles and their propulsion-related systems. Undetected unusual behaviour of such systems makes the vehicle diagnostic system unreliable. Current diagnostic systems, such as On-Board Diagnostics (OBD), are limited to monitoring only specific systems in order to make fault decision. However, various anomalies, including drastic performance drops, vehicle tampering, and changes in the driving environment, often go undetected during OBD system testing, validation, and inspection. This research presents a novel explainable OBD anomaly detection pipeline that is able to detect anomalies based only on OBD data snapshots during processes of OBD validation and inspection. The novel approach is implemented using combined dimension reduction and data clustering methodologies. First, the data is transformed into a latent space using t-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE), where the general structure of the anomaly in the data can be exploited. Subsequently, clustering using Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN) is applied to group similar normal data and identify anomalous patterns. The novelty of the solution is further extended with a feedback loop that suggests the root cause of OBD signals for individual anomalies using explainable AI (XAI) methodology, in this case Shapley additive explanations (SHAP). The proposed concept was verified and evaluated using real OBD snapshots with synthetically generated anomalies in two scenarios with different engine status, engine off and on, with an achieved accuracy of 92.89% and 96.45% for anomaly detection, respectively. The majority of anomaly causes in the form of specific OBD signals from propulsion- and emission-related systems were successfully explained using SHAP.

title: Optical Image-Assisted Zero-Shot Learning for Unknown Target Recognition in SAR Images
authors: Xiaobao Tong, Yong Wang, Zhiyong Song, Tong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: Zero-shot learning (ZSL) for unknown target recognition in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images is of great significance as the training samples of certain targets may not be available under real-world scenarios. Existing ZSL algorithms usually integrate SAR images with semantics for unknown target recognition in SAR images. However, for these ZSL algorithms, semantics can only provide very limited information and some semantics may not be helpful compared with optical images. To this end, we develop an optical image-assisted ZSL (OZSL) algorithm, which includes three loss terms. Specifically, the first loss term explores intermodal correlation between SAR images and optical images. Subsequently, the second loss term explores the interview correlation among SAR images. However, combining these two loss terms is still unable to recognize unknown SAR images. Considering this, the third loss term is designed by exploiting additional optical images to assist the above two loss terms. Afterward, these three loss terms are jointly optimized to acquire the optimal solutions of the related variables involved in them by the corresponding optimization algorithms. Finally, according to the Kullback–Leibler divergence, OZSL exploits the optimal solutions to achieve unknown SAR image recognition. The results verify the superiority of OZSL.

title: Co-EDR: Cooperative Event-Driven Fuzzing for Efficient Vulnerability Detection in Firmware
authors: Seung-Ha Jee, So-Eun Jeon, Il-Gu Lee
abstract: With the Internet of Things (IoT) becoming increasingly integrated into everyday life and industry, ensuring efficient and secure data processing in large-scale IoT networks is critical. Typically, vulnerabilities in wireless firmware are patched via over-the-air updates. However, timely patching remains a challenge. Efficient fuzzing techniques have been proposed to address this problem, but conventional fuzzing techniques often rely on random input generation, resulting in poor code coverage, high overhead, and limited detection performance because of the exhaustive injection of all possible input combinations. To address these limitations, this study proposes a cooperative event data recording (Co-EDR) technique to efficiently verify firmware stability in wireless communication systems. Co-EDR improves fuzzing efficiency by recording event data that previously triggered crashes and reusing these data as inputs. In addition, it uses cooperative fuzzing across multiple devices to improve code coverage and vulnerability detection. Experimental results indicate that Co-EDR results in an 8.52-fold reduction in the time required to achieve the same vulnerability detection rate as conventional random fuzzing, using approximately 2.47 times less memory and enhances the fuzzing efficiency by up to 32.5 times. These improvements enable expedited vulnerability detection and timely firmware patching with reduced resource expenditure compared with conventional techniques, rendering the proposed method a practical solution for improving the security of IoT systems.

title: A Lightweight Frequency-Selection-based Progressive Patch Transformer Network for Single Image Super-Resolution_supp1-3663242.pdf
authors: Shu Tang
abstract: Recently, lightweight networks for single image super-resolution (SISR) have surged due to the need of resource-constrained devices, where divide-and-conquer multi-route model exhibits impressive trade-off between performance and computational cost. However, most existing divide-and-conquer multi-route models face two key limitations: (1) possible suboptimal decoupling of image components (e.g. smooth regions, edges and texture details) due to spatial-domain-only processing, and (2) inability to model global dependencies explicitly and capture structural information, hindering further performance gains. To address these drawbacks, we propose a lightweight frequency-selection-based progressive patch Transformer network (FSPPTN) for higher-quality SISR reconstruction. Specifically, we first propose a frequency selection module, in which we develop a frequency enhancement branch (FEB) to dynamically decouple different image components by introducing the window-based Fast Fourier transform (WFFT) and a learnable weight matrix, and a spatial restoration branch (SRB) to recalibrate and fuse cross-granularity features by designing a multi-gate mechanism for reconstructing the component information screened out by the FEB at current level. Secondly, we propose a lightweight multi-branch gradient-guided inter-patch self-attention to explicitly capture global structural similarities by summarizing structural information of each patch into a lower-dimensional space using the statistical properties of first-order gradients, thereby achieving explicit global dependencies modeling and lightweight. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that, in the vast majority of cases, FSPPTN outperforms state-of-the-art lightweight SISR methods in terms of both performance and computational overhead, especially for ×3 and ×4 SR, e.g. FSPPTN outperforms MaIR-Small by even 0.14dB PSNR on Manga109 dataset for ×4 SR even with 48.3% fewer parameters and 63.5% lower FLOPs. The code is available at: https://github.com/yslyangshuli/FSPPTN-main.

title: IEEE Open Access Journal of Power and Energy Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Open Access Journal of Power and Energy

title: Analog Convolution Circuit System Implemented with Capacitor Array Superposition
authors: Chung-Yi Wang, Hsieh, Ping-Hsuan, Zi-Cin Wang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Circuits and Systems
abstract: This paper presents an architecture for analog convolution computing based on fundamental series-parallel capacitor configurations. The convolution results are digitized through a capacitance-difference-to-digital converter (CDDC), enabling efficient analog-to-digital conversion. Furthermore, an offset calibration mechanism is incorporated into the CDDC to compensate for offset-induced errors, thereby preserving the accuracy of AI model inference. The proposed architecture was evaluated on the MNIST and EMNIST datasets. With offset calibration enabled, the system achieved classification accuracies comparable to those of the ideal model. Therefore, the proposed system can be adapted to various semiconductor processes, facilitating the realization of analog convolution computing chips.

title: Addressing the Complexity of Computation
authors: Leah Hoffmann
abstract: None

title: IEEE Standard for Data Acquisition, Filtering, and Buffering Protocols for Edge Computing Node
authors: N/A
abstract: This standard specifies the protocols used by the edge computing node for buffering, filtering, and pre-processing data collected from industrial controllers on the shop floor, including programmable logic controllers, microcontrollers, industrial robots, etc. The data acquisition from field devices with different interfaces shall be automatically stored, filtered, and pre-processed according to reconfigurable rules, including buffering data from selected time frames, converting data formats, and filtering invalid data from industrial clouds and other edge computing nodes.

title: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
authors: N/A

title:
Sensing and Adsorption for Transformer Oil-dissolved Gases by Rh-doped TiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    : First-Principle Study
authors: Jingyi Wu, Sheng-Yuan Xia, Hao Sun, Xiaolong Du, Baohong Guo, Yuesheng Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Detecting oil-dissolved gases (H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>, C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> and CH<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>) in transformers is increasingly important, which is beneficial for reducing maintenance costs and improving the reliability of transformer. This study investigates Rh-doped TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (Rh-TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>) primarily as a sensing material for these gases, with its strong adsorption capability serving as a key enhancing mechanism. Based on density functional theory (DFT) calculations, we established stable Rh-TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/gas configurations. Analysis of adsorption energy and band gap reveals that Rh-TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> exhibits particularly strong adsorption for H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> and C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>, which translates into a promising and selective sensing response towards H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>. Furthermore, density of states (DOS) analysis confirms the strong chemisorption mechanism involved. This strong interaction not only underpins the high sensitivity required for detection but also suggests a dual-function potential for gas scavenging. Consequently, this study provides a theoretical foundation for developing high-performance Rh-TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>-based sensors to enhance power transformer monitoring.

title: Forecasting Stock Market Trends with an Optimized Fuzzy Hybrid SVM Technique
authors: Kafila, Saurabh Singh, Nagella Venkata Ramana, RVS Praveen, Sivaganesan Sivanantham, M. Balamurugan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Because of its inherent volatility and unpredictability, stock market forecasting continues to rank high among analysts' most formidable challenges. Traders and investors rely on accurate market forecasts to help them choose the correct assets, plan their purchases and sales, and set realistic investment goals. Chartists, a subset of technical analysts, use patterns in the visual representation of market data to make predictions about the future. To improve the precision of predictions, this research suggests a hybrid model. In order to handle problems like outliers and missing values, the system starts with a preprocessing phase. The TF-IDF method is used for feature extraction. The Fuzzy-Twin SVM which combines fuzzy logic with the twin SVM framework forms the backbone of the model. In order to fine-tune the parameters and achieve the best possible performance, a grid search is employed. The experimental findings show that the suggested Fuzzy-Twin SVM model outperforms traditional models by a wide margin, reaching an impressive 99.26% accuracy. These results indicate that the suggested approach provides a solid basis for predicting stock market trends, which aids analysts in making better decisions and improves financial trading decision-making.

title: Tea Leaves Disease Multi Class Classification using MobileNet-V2
authors: Sridhar Dachepelly, K. Kishore Kumar, Banoth. Janaki, Abha Dargar, Sudhanshu Maurya, Amit Gupta
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This paper introduces an innovative approach to tea leaf disease prediction, focusing on the utilization of MobileNet-V2 for accurate classification of six distinct class labels: Algal spot, brown blight, healthy, helopeltis, and red spot. Tea leaf diseases significantly impact global tea harvests, necessitating early and precise diagnosis for effective control measures. Leveraging deep learning algorithms, MobileNet-V2 is explored as a suitable model for embedded systems due to its efficiency in small end devices. The dataset used in the research comprises several categories of tea leaf conditions to be trained and tested against the model which results in an excellent prediction accuracy of 99%. This research has some relevance to the improvement of agricultural disease detection and presents real time possibilities to tea plantations improving the protection of crops together with their yields.

title: Heuristic-Flexible Ant Colony Optimization for Efficient Task Offloading in Fog Computing
authors: Md. Mahmodul Hasan, Md. Emran Biswas, Mst. Tanbin Yasmin Tanny, Md. Delowar Hossain, Tangina Sultana
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Efficient task offloading in fog computing environments is critical for minimizing latency and ensuring responsive service delivery in delay-sensitive applications. This paper proposes a novel optimization approach, termed Heuristic-Flexible Ant Colony Optimization (HF-ACO), designed to address the challenges of irregular task-option mappings and constrained resource conditions. Unlike classical Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), HF-ACO incorporates a flexible task representation model and a heuristic-aware solution construction mechanism, enabling intelligent, adaptive decision-making in heterogeneous fog architectures. Extensive experimental evaluations demonstrate that HF-ACO consistently outperforms state-of-the-art metaheuristic algorithms, including Genetic Algorithm (GA), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Gravitational Search Algorithm (GSA). Specifically, HF-ACO achieves the lowest best fitness value and reduces task offloading delay by over 82% compared to GA and up to 97% compared to GSA. These results highlight HF-ACO’s strong potential to serve as a robust and scalable solution for delay-sensitive fog computing systems.

title: Satellite Image detection and classification by Convolutional Neural Network: A deep learning Approach
authors: Taib Hasan, Md Mahedi Hasan, Md Rocky, Prapti Das, Anamika Rani, Mst. Naznin Nahar Joba
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Satellite image classification is a fundamental task in remote sensing, transforming raw imagery into meaningful insights for environmental monitoring and land management. This paper proposes a deep learning framework based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to achieve robust and efficient classification of satellite images. The RSI-CB256 dataset, derived from Google Maps and various satellite sensors, is used to assess the model on three main land cover classes: Waterland, Greenland, and desert. Cloudy regions are considered as an additional class to account for atmospheric interference in image classification. Leveraging CNNs’ capacity to learn hierarchical spatial features, the proposed approach effectively addresses the complexity of satellite imagery. Experimental evaluation shows that the model attains a training accuracy of 98% and a validation accuracy of 97.6%, reflecting high reliability and strong generalization capability. These results demonstrate the promise of deep learning in advancing large-scale land cover classification and supporting sustainable environmental observation systems.

title: Spinal Cord Injury Detection and Classification using Siamese Convolutional Neural Network with Adaptive Spider-Tailed Horned Viper Optimization
authors: Nalajam Geethanjali, Gurram Sunitha
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Timely diagnosis and effective treatment planning rely heavily on the detection and classification of Spinal Cord Injuries (SCI) employing Computed Tomography (CT) images. However, they often struggle with achieving reliable accuracy and tend to generate many false positives.
