To address these issues, this work proposes an advanced hybrid optimization-based Deep Learning (DL) method called Siamese Convolutional Neural Network (SCNN) with Adaptive Spider-Tailed Horned Viper Optimization (SCNN_ASTHVO) for the detection and classification of SCI. To begin with, a CT image is considered as input and subjected to spinal cord segmentation, which is conducted by employing soft segmentation (SoftSeg). This is followed by disc localization via Sparse Fuzzy C-Means (Sparse FCM). In the feature extraction phase, a combination of geometric, connectivity, image-level, and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) -based features is extracted. The SCNN is then trained using the ASTHVO algorithm, which is developed by incorporating an adaptive mechanism in the Spider-Tailed Horned Viper Optimization (STHVO) for improved performance. Finally, injury-level classification is conducted using the SCNN_ASTHVO model. Experimental results show that the SCNN_ASTHVO achieves an accuracy of 97.172%, True Positive Rate (TPR) of 97.912%, and True Negative Rate (TNR) of 95.928%, demonstrating its effectiveness in improving SCI classification and detection.

title: Comparative Analysis of CNNs and Vision Transformers for Brain Tumor Diagnosis using MRI
authors: Priyanka Shinde, Lanson Bardeskar, Kanif Kumbhar, Varsha Desai, Sandeep Thorat
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Efficient identification of brain tumors using magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is important for early detection and treatment. Although Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) serve as a baseline, architectural biases of CNNs, which are local features, means that it is possibly not up to the task in complex diagnosis. This paper performs a critical direct comparison of a fine-tuned ResNet50, a CNN baseline, with a ViT, an architecture that is very expressive on global context. In a publicly available data set of 7,023 MRI scans with four classes (glioma, meningioma, pituitary, and no tumor), the two models were trained and tested. Performance measurement was carried out with the help of accuracy, precision and recall, F1 score and Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUC). Our quantitative findings confirm that the ViT is more accurate to evaluate the classes (88.4%) compared to the ResNet50 (85.0%). Moreover, qualitative analysis with GradCAM and self-attention maps shows that the learnings of ViT are more focused on a less localised decision-making process including the tumor and the surrounding anatomical contexts. The findings of this study have concluded that the ability of the ViT to represent global relationships presents a solid benefit to the classification problem of brain tumors and, therefore, holds a lot of promise as an alternative architecture to traditional CNN models.

title: Smart Healthcare: Parkinson's Disease Detection Using Deep Learning and Attention-Enhanced VGG16
authors: Anuj Kumar Jain, Raghavendra Sridhar, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Deepak Banerjee, Vishnu Kant
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The progressive neurological disorder Parkinson's Disease manifests as a disease which damages motor and cognitive abilities throughout patients' lives making worldwide healthcare systems face tremendous challenges. The early identification of Parkinson's disease remains vital because it improves therapeutic results and provides better quality care to patients. The research introduces a deep learning framework to detect PD at its early stages through hand-drawn spiral and wave pictures. The dataset obtained from Kaggle features 102 images that demonstrate healthy subjects and patients with Parkinson's disease. A specialized VGG16 model combined with Multi-Head Attention operations throughout with Gaussian noise components and dropout regularization functions was designed to identify slight motor movement signs in the data. Advanced data augmentation techniques trained the model until the Adam optimizer reached the optimal performance using binary cross-entropy loss. The model achieved a classification success rate of 88% in experimental testing which established its high level of effectiveness. The research supports seven Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) including Good Health and Well-being, Innovation, Reduced Inequalities, Quality Education, Industry and Healthcare Access, Early Diagnosis and AI for Health through deep learning methods which create non-invasive diagnostic tools for accessibility at early stages.

title: Smart Gesture: A Multi-sensor Glove with Embedded Deep Learning for Real Time Windows Interaction
authors: Nilakshee Rajule, Mithra Venkatesan, Sheetal Pawar, Chandan Kumar, Abhinil Rajgire, Atharva Tummulwar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of Smart Gesture, a wearable glove-based system enabling intuitive, real-time gesture control for Windows 11 environments. The system leverages a multi-sensor fusion architecture comprising inertial measurement units (MPU6050), flex sensors, and force-sensitive resistors (FSRs), integrated with the ESP32-S3 Zero microcontroller for efficient on-device processing. A machine learning-based gesture recognition framework, utilizing a hybrid CNN-BiLSTM architecture, is employed to accurately classify both static and dynamic hand gestures. The recognized gestures are mapped to operating system-level commands such as mouse control, scrolling, clicking, and media navigation through a custom-built Electron.js desktop application. Experimental evaluations demonstrate high gesture recognition accuracy, low latency, and consistent performance across varied hand movements and users. The Smart Gesture system offers a scalable and accessible human-computer interaction solution with broad applications in assistive technologies, AR/VR interfaces, touchless control systems, and immersive computing.

title: Predictive Modeling of Video Streaming Effectiveness Using Artificial Neural Network and Sentiment Analysis
authors: Shakthi S., Sonallika V., K.R.M. Vijaya Chandrakala
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Video streaming platforms have now evolved into indispensable platforms for entertainment and education, and brands have even been promoted to audiences by appointing influencers and branded channels. The traditional metrics of views, likes, and comments, however, cannot encompass the much more complex factor of viewer engagement and sentiment. This paper describes a neural network-based predictive framework using engagement measures, sentiment analysis by VADER (Valence Aware Dictionary for Sentiment Reasoning), and a log-transformed reach. For predicting video effectiveness, the framework provides the output as an effectiveness score ranging from 0 to 100; hence, businesses can evaluate individual videos and campaign-wide trends. Testing on synthetic data yielded 86.72% prediction accuracy, confirming model reliability. The study thus bridges raw video streaming data sourced via API to actionable insights for marketers’ payoff from optimizing content strategy and better returns on investment.

title: META Surface-Based High Isolation MIMO Antenna For Wifi Applications
authors: S. Kumaran, M. Varun, Beulah Jackson, R. Ramesh, Anitha Gopalan, S. Haripriya
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The wireless world requires higher speed communication, and this necessitates a smaller MIMO antenna with improved isolation, especially in WiFi applications. The traditional designs experience excessive interconnection, low bandwidth, and poor efficiency. To overcome these shortcomings, a miniaturised metasurface-based MIMO antenna with an isolation-enhancing layer of metasurfaces placed between radiating elements is suggested. The CST and HFSS tools were used to perform full-wave simulations. The antenna has isolation of -32.1, and the return loss of - 26.7 and 25.9, and the bandwidth of 150MHz at 2.4 and 300MHz at 5.2. It is characterized by a 6.3 dBi peak gain, an ECC of 0.005, diversity radiation efficiency and a gain of 9.99 dB of more than 94. It provides a great deal of improvement over previous designs in terms of performance, size 35x35mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, and dual-band operation. This design presents a good prospect of being incorporated into the present bulk WiFi networks in need of smaller size, reliability, and minimum interference in the MIMO applications.

title: TinySenseNet: A Lightweight sEMG-IMU Fusion Network Using TinyML for Mechanical Arm Control in Low-Resource Settings
authors: Fazlay Rabby, Md. Rifat Aknda, Mumtahina Tasnim Mahi, Shaikh Radwan Ahmed Ratul, Bahadur Zamn Shezan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This paper introduces TinySenseNet, a lightweight and memory-efficient deep learning model for real-time robotic arm control using multimodal surface electromyography (sEMG) and inertial measurement unit (IMU) signals. Optimized for embedded deployment, the system processes 150 ms input windows to achieve low-latency gesture recognition with TinyML. The architecture employs depthwise 1D convolutions and an attention mechanism for efficient temporal feature extraction and robust multimodal fusion. Unlike CNNs, LSTMs, and SVMs, which are either computationally heavy, slow, or noise-sensitive, TinySenseNet offers both efficiency and resilience on low-power hardware. Key innovations include cross-modal delay features that capture timing between muscle activation and motion, a confidence-based actuation mechanism with haptic feedback to improve safety, and a culturally adapted gesture set for enhanced usability. A custom hardware platform integrating an ESP32 microcontroller, Myo Armband, and servo-driven robotic arm validates the approach. Evaluation across lab settings, real-world noisy conditions, and clinical trials with transradial amputees demonstrates strong generalization. TinySenseNet achieved 97.2% accuracy for able-bodied users, 95.7% for amputees, and 93.3% in real-world scenarios, outperforming CNN, LSTM, and SVM baselines. With a compact size of 1.46 KB and 23.8 ms latency, TinySenseNet provides an affordable, scalable, and clinically viable solution for assistive robotic control in low-resource environments.

title: Deep Analysis of MFCC, LPC, MEL Spectrogram, and Weighted Features to Classify Stuttered and Non-Stuttered Speech
authors: Nilanjan Banerjee, Nilambar Sethi, Samarjeet Borah
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Speech serves as the verbal tool for expressing thoughts, ideas, and emotions, and is essential for human interaction. Stuttering is a speech disorder that impacts millions of individuals worldwide. The determination of whether an individual stutters or does not stutter is conducted through assessments in Speech Language Pathology (SLP). Traditionally, this evaluation involved manually counting the instances of stuttering within speech to classify individuals as either stutterers or non-stutterers. Therefore, the process is time-consuming and susceptible to mistakes. Stuttering speech recognition has been a well-focused area of study within speech signal processing for researchers in recent decades. The present research focuses on identifying stuttered and fluent speech and highlighting its performance in deep learning methodologies. This study examines an in-depth evaluation of the CNN & Bi-LSTM model utilizing MFCC, LPC, and MEL spectrogram features. Additionally, the weighted features of these have also been evaluated by our deep learning methods for the classification of stuttered and fluent speech. A comparison has been carried out on the efficiency achieved from the CNN & Bi-LSTM models utilizing different types of features. Research indicates that the CNN model achieves an impressive accuracy rate of 92% compared to the Bi-LSTM model when utilizing weighted MFCC.

title: Deep Learning-Based Multi-Class Chest X-Ray Classification: A CNN Approach for COVID-19, Pneumonia, Tuberculosis, and Normal Diagnosis
authors: Y.Venkata Reddy, Harshith N Bali
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: A chest X-ray (CXR) disease refers to abnormalities visible in thoracic imaging that indicate conditions such as COVID-19, Pneumonia, Tuberculosis, and Normal (healthy lungs). Early and accurate diagnosis is essential to ensure timely treatment and prevent complications. Most of the earlier work in this domain has been carried out using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) in deep learning frameworks, with features extracted using architectures such as InceptionV3, MobileNetV3, ResNet50,aConvNeXt-Tiny, EfficientNetB0, VGG19,UNet, NN_19, and DenseNet201. In this proposed work, we have collected CXR images representing four disease categories from various Kaggle datasets and evaluated the performance of the CNN models. The effectiveness of the each model is assessed using performance metrics including ROC curve, precision, recall, and accuracy. Among these, the InceptionV3 model yielded the highest accuracy. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of deep learning-based feature extraction methods for CXR disease classification, contributing toward real-time diagnostic support and improved healthcare delivery in thoracic imaging.

title: Hybrid Architecture for Accurate ECG-Based Heart Disease Detection using Deep Learning
authors: Shivam Chaurasiya, Rohit Bajaj
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) is the general term describing a wide category of heart diseases and is one of the main health issues worldwide, killing millions of people year after year. The insidious character of heart disease, along with its tendency to remain silent, makes early detection the key to decreasing mortalities, as well as improving patient conditions. Although angiograms and Echocardiograms (ECG) are conventional and effective methods of diagnosing heart diseases, they can be costly, invasive, and inapplicable in continuous heart disease monitoring. This study presents a Deep Learning (DL)-based automated heart disease detection framework utilizing the ECG signals in the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia dataset. The method examines various architectures, such as 1D Convolutional neural networks (CNNs), Long short-term memory (LSTM) networks, and hybrid CNN+LSTM networks. Each of them is assessed based on raw ECG inputs as well as a combination of handcrafted clinical characteristics. The hybrid model, which combines signal as well as feature-based inputs, is the best model, and an accuracy of 98.00% is reached. The findings show that combining DL and domain-driven features can improve the accuracy of the diagnosis, and at the same time, the computational efficiency. This is a scalable and non-invasive method of conducting real-time cardiac screening, and can be used in both clinical and mobile health care.

title: Smart Grid Energy Optimization with Edge Computing and Deep Learning for Residential Applications
authors: Smitha G Prabhu, Anarghya Amarnath, Saagar E P, ShishirAlevoor
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: This paper proposes an edge computing-based smart grid framework to enhance residential energy management through localized decision-making and predictive load control. Traditional cloud-reliant systems face latency and scalability challenges, which this architecture addresses using edge processing and IoT integration. The system employs ESP32 microcontrollers with PZEM-004T energy meters for real-time monitoring, while a Raspberry Pi 3B+ handles edge analytics. Load forecasting is performed using LSTM neural networks trained on historical consumption data. The system achieves an 80% latency reduction and forecasting accuracy with an MAE of 0.13 and RMSE of 0.16. Dynamic load balancing improves energy efficiency by 48% and reduces peak demand by 56%, resulting in a 15% reduction in energy costs. Home Assistant enables automation and user interaction. With a high R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value of 0.98, the model performs reliably across varying usage patterns. This study demonstrates a low-cost, scalable solution for smart residential grids using IoT, edge computing, and machine learning.

title: Multi-Modal AI for Unified Knowledge Extraction
authors: Ranjith Goud Karvanga, Ravi Kiran Kanneganti, Sai Susmitha Gollapudi, Sravan Kumar Reddy Bolla, Susmith Barigidad
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: The explosive proliferation of heterogeneous data sources, including text, images, audio, video as well as sensor and structured content demand unstandardized methods of extracting knowledge in a homogenous manner. Conventional unimodal or silo-based approaches tend to miss the semantic dependences that exist across heterogeneous data streams, and hence are not effective in practice. The paper proposes a full-scale study of Multi-Modal AI for Unified Knowledge Extraction, architectures that combine deep learning, attention, and cross-modal to provide coherent representations of heterogeneous data. The framework proposed reduces challenges like modality alignment, semantic fusion, and knowledge graph construction which allows accurate reasoning, and context-aware insights. Moreover, the system supports scalability to work with multimodal categories of very large-scale multimodal data sets and be able to ensure robustness and interpretability. Experimental tests on benchmark datasets showed large gains over state-of-the-art unimodal and early-fusion models with respect to all of extraction accuracy, semantic consistency, and generalizability. Results demonstrate the power of multimodal AI as the technology that can enable the next-generation solutions in healthcare, cybersecurity, smart cities, and human-computer interaction where comprehensive knowledge extraction is vital to make informed decision-making.

title: Intelligent Threat Detection in HS-RAN: A Machine Learning Approach to Secure the Future Wireless Network
authors: Leela V, Roshini P, Swetha S, Praneush M K, Thanvanthri S
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: There is a rapidly growing area of Heterogeneous and Secure Radio Access Networks (HS-RAN) with many smart city / Internet of Things (IOT), applications now being developed and deployed since the rapid expansion of wireless networks that have been fueled by the successful speed of 5G and now 6G. However, HS-RAN's open and decentralized architecture poses serious cybersecurity risks, such as spoofing, zero-day attacks, and Denial of Service (DoS) attacks. Because they can't adapt quickly enough and don't respond quickly enough, traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) don't work well in these kinds of changing environments. It suggests an AI-based threat detection framework for HS-RAN that combines supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning methods. To find both known and unknown threats, the system continuously examines behavioral data and edge telemetry. Components of explainable AI (XAI) are also included to improve interpretability and transparency. The effectiveness of the suggested method in safeguarding next-generation wireless networks is confirmed by experimental results that show high accuracy and low false positive rates across benchmark datasets.

title: Analysis of Energy Efficiency of Smart Bandwidth Reduction and Redistribution Allocation Algorithm in TDM EPON
authors: Khandaker Al Arafat, Monir Hossen
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: In a traditional passive optical network (PON), the optical network units (ONUs) remain in an active state for the whole time of every time cycle, i.e., 100% time in active mode of every time cycle. As a result, a considerable amount of energy is wasted. This paper analyzes the energy efficiency of the Smart Bandwidth Reduction and Redistribution Allocation (SBRRA) algorithm, where ONUs are in active mode only during their transmission time based on this algorithm; otherwise, they are in sleep mode. The performance of the energy efficiency of the SBRRA algorithm is evaluated using a numerical simulation model in comparison with the existing delay and energy efficient dynamic bandwidth allocation (DEE-DBA) algorithm and a traditional bandwidth allocation algorithm in the time-division multiplexing Ethernet-PON (TDM EPON). The SBRRA algorithm saved 14.28% and 12.86% energy even at 100% network load than DEEDBA and Traditional EPON for 1.0 ms and 1.5 ms cycle-time, respectively. So, the SBRRA algorithm ensures energy efficient application in TDM EPON.

title: A Novel Hybrid Method for Fault Analysis of HVDC Transmission Link Fed by Renewable Energy Sources
authors: Rajesh Babu Damala, Rambabu Muppidi, Laveti Sai Priya, Pandranki Padma, Viswas Nelli, Vasupalli Manoj
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Detecting and classifying problems on High Voltage DC transmission (HVDC) lines and pinpointing their locations is a crucial task for the power system's efficient operation. This paper presents a Teager-Kaiser Energy Operator (TKEO) technique with a decision tree-based fault type classifier to monitor power system interruptions on the HVDC transmission system. The differential (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">diff</inf>) current signals used at the Change Initiation Point (CIP) of the HVDC link by Change Initiation Filter Method. The raw current signal is used to generate four indices for fault analysis. This state-of-the-art technique improves fault categorization and identification efficiency. It also reduces the amount of time and computational complexity needed to find faults. The power transmission networks can be restored as soon as possible and minimizing economic losses to the greatest extent possible. The suggested method shows a significant improvement in accuracy with a fault detecting efficiency of 98.5%.

title: Mathematical Modeling and Predictive Analysis of Financial Indicators in the Indian IT Sector
authors: Madhavi Sharma, Chandan Kumar, Nitin Choudhary
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: During the year 2020, the global stock market experienced considerable fluctuations, leading to a significant dip in financial indicators. This study presents a machine learning-based approach for the mathematical modeling and predictive analysis of key financial indicators in the Indian IT sector, focusing specifically on the NIFTY-IT Index. The investigation spans the period from January 2020 to December 2020, a time characterized by significant market volatility. The objective is to develop precise regression-based prediction models to support informed financial decision-making. Key market variables analyzed include opening and closing prices, volume of shares traded, turnover metrics, and valuation ratios such as P/E and P/B. The dataset is segmented into three distinct phases: pre-disruption (first quarter), disruption period (lockdown phase), and post-disruption (recovery phase). In the first phase, mathematical regression models are applied to identify underlying relationships among market variables prior to major disruptions. The second phase involves cross-validating predicted trends with actual observed data to measure the impact on market behavior during the disruption period. In the final phase, Support Vector Regression (SVR) is used to forecast valuation metrics and assess long-term effects on market stability. The proposed models demonstrate high predictive accuracy, with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values of 0.9773 for initial estimates, 0.94 for cost estimation, and 0.96 for valuation ratio predictions, underscoring the robustness of the methodology.

title: GAN-Based Speaker Identification and Age Estimation Using a Deep Learning Approach
authors: Banala Saritha, G Purnachandrarao, Kalali Nandini, Durgappagari Vaishanvi, K. Meghana, Anish Monsley K
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Inthis work, we present a dual-task Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) model designed for simultaneous speaker identification and age estimation using the LibriSpeech dataset. The system leverages MFCC features extracted from .flac audio files to represent speaker characteristics. A Generator network produces synthetic MFCC features from random noise, while a Discriminator network evaluates the authenticity of these features and concurrently performs speaker classification and age regression. The Discriminator architecture is enhanced with a shared feature extractor followed by task-specific branches for adversarial learning, classification, and regression. The model was trained and evaluated on a subset of LibriSpeech'sdev-clean split with speakers manually annotated with age metadata. Performance evaluation on unseen data yielded a speaker classification accuracy of approximately 98.53%, demonstrating strong identity recognition capability. Additionally, age estimation was evaluated using a Regressor with mean squared error, achieving a mean squared error (MSE) of 28.29, suggesting the model learns meaningful age-related representations from the acoustic features. Sample predictions illustrate that the system is generally able to distinguish between speakers and infer approximate age values.

title: An Explainable Multi-Class Brain Tumor Classifier Using EfficientNetV2 with Hybrid Attention Mechanisms
authors: Sanjida Ferdousi, Utsha Das, Tanvir Mahtab Zihan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The identification of brain tumors using MRI continues to be a challenge mainly because of the complexity exhibited by tumor morphology and because an expert-based interpretative approach is employed. Hence, we proposed an explainable deep-learning framework for efficient multi-class classification of brain tumors. The architecture is founded on EfficientNetV2 with an added hybrid attention mechanism combining the GAM and Efficient Channel Attention to better represent features spatially and channel-wise while remaining computationally efficient. Also, three lightweight variants are proposed by replacing the SE blocks in EfficientNetV2 with: (a) GAM+ECA, (b) CBAM, and (c) CBAM+ECA. These variants pared down the parameters and FLOPs to such an extent that they can be used in real-time clinical settings, especially in resource-constrained centers. The models were trained and tested over a four-class brain MRI dataset (glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and no tumor) in a stratified manner through 5-fold cross-validation. The proposed hybrid model attained an accuracy of 98.93%, with average precision, recall, and F1- score greater than 98.80%, outperforming both the baseline and lightweight models.

title: BDaquaFish: A Comparative Study of YOLO11 and RT-DETR for Real-Time Detection of Bangladeshi Fish Species
authors: Sumitra Rani Das, Sayeda Sazia Kalam, Sumaiya Ali Shafa, Abdullah Al Noman
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: For ecological research, sustainable fisheries and biodiversity monitoring precise fish species identification is crucial. However, existing fish detection systems often suffer from limited species diversity, poor generalization across real-world conditions. Furthermore, a notable research gap exists due to the limited focus on region-specific species. This study assesses two cutting-edge object identification models—RT-DETR, a transformer-based real-time detector, and YOLO11, a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based detector—on a fresh dataset of 23 frequently occurring fish species in Bangladesh in order to fill these gaps. Our dataset, curated from public online sources and local fish markets, surpasses previous collections in species diversity and real-world applicability, providing a robust benchmark for multi-species fish detection tasks. Both models were initialized with pretrained weights and then fine tuned on our dataset. Results show that RT-DETR outperforms YOLO11 in precision (0.878 vs. 0.844), recall (0.849 vs. 0.797), F1-score (0.863 vs. 0.820), and mAP50 (0.895 vs. 0.879). These findings highlight RT-DETR’s superior performance in comprehensive and reliable detection, a result also enabled by the overall quality and variety of our dataset. This study contributes a strong benchmark and provides practical insights for advancing robust fish detection systems in ecological and commercial domains.

title: Advancing Brain Stroke Prediction Through Attention-Based Neural Architectures for Temporal Data Analysis
authors: Tasnim Tabassum, Md. Appel Mahmud Pranto, Mursheda Nusrat Della, A.B.M. Kabir Hossain, Md. Arafat Hossain, Partha Pratim Debnath
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Stroke is one of the leading causes of both death and disability, which makes accurate early prediction necessary. This paper proposes an attention-based neural network framework combining bidirectional LSTM and a custom attention mechanism to improve stroke risk prediction from structured clinical data. The model adaptively focuses on critical features while modelling temporal dependencies. A preprocessing pipeline including imputation, one-hot encoding and scaling ensures input consistency. Trained on a dataset of 5,110 samples, the model achieves 95.11% accuracy, 0.93 precision, 0.91 recall, 0.92 F1-Score, and an AUC-ROC of 0.96. Comparative evaluation confirms superior performance over baseline models. The inter-pretability and effectiveness of the framework support its use in real-world clinical settings for reliable assessment of stroke risk.

title: Optimizing Crop Recommendation with IoT and Machine Learning: A Smart Agriculture Framework 4.0
authors: Diganta Dey, Najmus Sakib Sizan, Khondokar Fida Hasan, Md. Abu Layek
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Global agricultural demands are growing exponentially and require innovative measures to optimize production while ensuring resource efficiency. In this paper, a holistic multilayer smart agriculture framework is developed that enables differentiated and information-oriented crop recommendations, comprising IoT, edge, fog computing, and cloud-based technologies. In this paper, we propose a machine learning based, cloud-based, Internet of Things (IoT)-driven agricultural forecasting system that provides predictions tailored to farmers’ needs. Data from sensor networks in real time are used to monitor soil nutrient levels and ambient conditions utilizing the Internet of Things. With an exceptional accuracy record of 99.28%, machine learning techniques, more specifically the gradient booster model, enable accurate crop estimates and personalized suggestions for effective resource management. This framework bridges technological advances with agricultural practices, providing farmers and stakeholders with a robust tool to enhance decision-making and sustainability. Using IoT-driven data collection, advanced pre-processing, and machine learning techniques, the proposed system shows significant potential to revolutionize modern agriculture through precision and intelligence.

title: Design Robust Authentication and Real-Time Threat Detection for Wearable Biomedical Device of Cybersecurity: Provide Constant Health Monitoring Privacy
authors: Perumalsamy Devaraj, Sathiyandrakumar Srinivasan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Wearable biomedical devices have become a revolutionary technology in today’s healthcare, providing continuous health surveillance, early disease diagnosis and real-time feedback to patients. These devices are built to collect and transmit private health information (PHI) including heart rates, blood oxygen, glucose results and ECGs. Although such advancements are beneficial in terms of patient care and workflow, they introduce substantial cybersecurity threats. Attacks against wearable health devices could result in unauthorized access, data alteration, or falsification of health readings, threatening patient safety as well as privacy. In this work, we cope with both the security challenges for the robust authentication and real-time threat detection in wearable biomedical systems toward continuous secure health monitoring. The proposed framework presents a context-aware multi-factor authentication method, designed for such resource limited wearable computing devices. It incorporates physiological biometrics (such as ECG patterns), behavioral traits (like gait), and environmental context (for example, distance from trusted devices) to provide always-on authentication with little resistance. Meanwhile, at the edge, a lightweight continuous threat detection module based on edge machine learning algorithms, detects anomalies, both at packet injection, spoofing and unauthorized access to the data transmission. To enhance performance under constrained processing and battery budget, the methodology uses energy-efficient cryptographic protocols and hierarchical authentication layers. The real-time anomaly detection engine leverages unsupervised models to discover irregular activity patterns without relying on pre-labelled samples so that it can be more adaptive to change of cyber threats. These are built in the device firmware to provide a proactive mitigation not depending on any cloud servers which will reduce latency and ensure data privacy. Experimental results are validated by means of a simulated healthcare system where wearable biosensors are connected through secure Bluetooth Low-Energy (BLE) and Wi-Fi channels. The proposed approach is evaluated in different threat scenarios (man-in-the-middle, replay attacks and physical tampering). Results show that over traditional ones it delivers noticeable increase in the accuracy of authentication in securing threats at an early stage for the component and an improvement in the energy utilization efficiency. The proposed work offers an integrated security model for wearable health devices with the goal to balance between usability, privacy and computational cost.

title: Hybrid Filtering Techniques for Image Denoising: A Comparative Analysis Using PSNR and SSIM
authors: Ratnam Dodda, Prateeka Munugala, Roshini Rajan, Desu Om Keerthi, Sri Varsha Cheruku
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Image denoising is a prominent preprocessing step in computer vision and medical imaging, where noise can degrade the quality of an image and impact downstream tasks such as segmentation and recognition. Traditional denoising techniques, including Mean, Median, Gaussian, and Bilateral filters, offer varying levels of effectiveness in noise reduction and edge preservation. However, individual filters have limitations, either blurring fine details or failing to remove noise efficiently. This research explores the effectiveness of hybrid filtering techniques, where multiple filters are sequentially applied to reduce noise. Various hybrid combinations of Gaussian, Bilateral, and Median filters are evaluated against traditional methods using Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio and Structural Similarity Index as performance metrics. Results from our experiment showcase that certain hybrid approaches, particularly the Bilateral-Gaussian-Median combination, outperform traditional filters by achieving a PSNR of 26.40 and SSIM of 0.784. These findings suggest that hybrid filtering can significantly enhance image quality in denoising applications, paving the way for improved image processing methodologies in real-world scenarios.

title: An Ensemble Model for Attack Detection in Internet of Things Edge Cloud Environment
authors: Firoz Khan, Sunil Kumar Billahalli Shivabasappa
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) has led to an exponential increase in connected devices, generating massive volumes of data. While this enables real-time automation and monitoring, it also introduces significant security vulnerabilities. Existing intrusion detection models often fail to adapt to dynamic environments, struggling with concept drift, class imbalance, and ineffective feature selection, which limits their detection accuracy and scalability. To address these issues, this work presents Concept-Drift Imbalance-Focused XGBoost (CDIF-XGB), a novel ensemble-based intrusion detection model designed for edge-cloud IoT systems. The CDIF-XGB incorporates three key strategies: concept drift adaptation to handle evolving attack behaviors, imbalance-aware training using dynamic class weights, and feature-level k-fold optimization to enhance generalization. Experimental evaluations on three benchmark datasets, Edge-IIoTset, CICIoT2023, and CICIoV demonstrate the model’s effectiveness, achieving high accuracy of 99.99%, 99.89%, and 99.96%, respectively. These results outperform existing models in terms of precision, recall, and F-score. Nevertheless, CDIF-XGB offers a robust, adaptive, and scalable solution for IoT security.

title: Intrusion Detection System Using Optimized Deep Machine Learning with Explainable AI
authors: Mamunur Rashid Alex, Progga Parmita Roy, Md. Maruf Hassan, Touhid Bhuiyan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Growing complexity of cyber threats requires more sophisticated IDS not only with high accuracy but also with interpretability. the performance of ML and DL models has been confirmed to be higher in network intrusion detection, good performance is not accompanied by their black-box character that doesn't allow us to trust them and implement them in practice. As per IDS, the main function of enhancing trustworthiness is to clearly acknowledge the impact of attacks for detecting intrusion in the specific system. The proposed system is an integrated IDS framework, which uses both ML and DL to detect threats with high accuracy and XAI to provide clear decision-making. Models that are being trained on benchmark IDS dataset are the RF, XGBoost, KNN, DT and LSTM. Besides, the generalizability of the models is evaluated by testing on the NSL-KDD dataset. Experimental results demonstrate high performance, XGBoost with 99.5% accuracy and our optimized LSTM achieving over 98.79% accuracy for the IDS dataset. After that, it has been tested on NSL-KDD dataset and evaluation of the performance directs towards the test accuracy of 98.62% (XGBoost) and 97.78% (LSTM) which refers that our proposed model performance most likely the same and negligible changes are noticed in this test. The sort of methodological and empirical contributions that come with applying XAI techniques to ML models is also discussed in the study, providing a balance between accuracy and transparency. In practice, this study provides human analysts with mechanisms to interpret and have confidence in the predictions made by IDS systems so that it is easier to respond swiftly about security threats. This work fills a major literature gap by expanding high performing ML and DL models with explainability to be deployed in the intrusion detection systems.

title: Quantum and Edge Computing for Bridging the Healthcare Gap in Underserved Regions
authors: Kartik Jadhav, Sanika Gurav, Kuldeep Sambrekar
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Healthcare accessibility in rural regions remains a significant challenge due to inadequate infrastructure, limited diagnostic tools, and a shortage of medical professionals. This paper presents a cost-effective hybrid model that integrates Edge and Quantum Computing to address these issues. Edge Computing enables real-time diagnostics through wearable IoT devices, providing immediate, localized patient monitoring. Quantum Computing handles complex tasks like genome sequencing and disease progression modelling, offering advanced insights for personalized care. The hybrid system delegates computationally intensive jobs to quantum systems while ensuring responsive diagnostics through edge devices. Performance evaluations and mathematical models confirm improvements in accuracy, latency reduction, and resource optimization. This framework reduces the urban-rural healthcare divide and enables broader access to modern medical treatment in underserved communities. It demonstrates how advanced technologies can create accessible and sustainable healthcare solutions for global challenges.

title: TriFusion-CardioNet: A Multi-Modal Attention and Graph-Enhanced Deep Framework with Evolutionary Feature Selection for Cardiovascular Disorder Detection from PTB-XL ECG Images
authors: Pushpa P, Deepa J
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Timely and accurate diagnosis is crucial for cardiovascular diseases (CVDs), which are the top cause of death worldwide. Manual interpretation of electrocardiograms (ECGs) is not without its limitations, but they are still an essential diagnostic tool. For automated CVD detection using the PTB-XL ECG dataset, this study suggests TriFusion-CardioNet, a deep learning framework that is tri-modal, physiology-aware, and optimization-enhanced. The model captures complementary temporal, spectral, and morphological features by integrating CWT scalograms, STFT spectrograms, and morphograms. A Physio-Aware Attention Module (PAAM) directs attention to P-QRS-T segments that are clinically relevant, and the Lead-Graph Relational Encoder (LGRE) models the dependencies between leads. By fusing Genetic Algorithm with Golden Jackal Optimization, a CardioGenetic Optimization (CGO) algorithm finds the best subset of deep features to use, improving efficiency without sacrificing accuracy. Compared to state-of-the-art baselines, TriFusion-CardioNet performs better on PTB-XL. The full model yielded a 91.1% accuracy rate, a macro precision of 0.881, a recall of 0.872, a macro F1-score of 0.876, and a macro AUC-ROC of 0.967, all while maintaining the lowest possible Hamming loss of 0.058. Testing for robustness showed that performance degraded very little when subjected to cross-domain shifts (CORAL-aligned cross-hospital F1 = 0.859) and Gaussian noise (SNR = 15 dB, F1 = 0.862). By reducing GPU memory usage from 6.7 GB to 5.3 GB and inference time from 23.8 ms to 19.6 ms per sample, CGO was found to be computationally effective. Highlighting physiologically meaningful regions, Grad-CAM++ and lead-attention visualizations were used to achieve explainability. The results show that TriFusion-CardioNet is a good fit for cardiovascular care real-time remote monitoring and clinical decision support due to its high diagnostic accuracy, robustness, and efficiency.

title: CardioAI-Risk: An Interpretable and Fair Machine Learning Pipeline for Personalized Cardiovascular Risk Stratification
authors: Al Mamun, Farhan Tanvir, Effat Ara, Md. Parvezur Rahman Mahin, Raihan Ul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease continues to be the leading cause of death globally, yet current predictive models often fall short in offering both individualized explanations and equitable performance across diverse populations. This study introduces CardioAI-Risk, an interpretable and fairness-conscious machine learning framework tailored for clinical application in preventative cardiology. Utilizing a logistic regression model guided by SHAP values, it incorporates ten key clinical and behavioral features such as blood pressure, cholesterol, age, gender, and lifestyle habits to assess patient risk. A refined risk threshold strategy enhances high-risk detection, while group fairness is systematically assessed using demographic parity and equalized odds metrics. The model attained an AUC of 0.87, with smoking, systolic pressure, and age identified as primary contributors to risk. Evaluation across demographic segments showed minimal performance gaps (∆parity <0.015), reinforcing its ethical validity. CardioAI-Risk offers clinicians a reliable, interpretable, and demographically balanced tool for personalized cardiovascular risk assessment.

title: FASAL: Ensemble Crop Recommendation System using Google Earth Engine and XAI Interpretability
authors: Maithili Divecha, Mallank Gogri, Priyansh Shah, Lakshya Joshi, Savyasaachi Pandit, Neha Katre
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Agriculture is transforming at a very fast pace, previously being one of the biggest sources of livelihood for individuals. But with technology coming into play, becoming part of one’s daily life and with its implementation of cutting-edge technologies like machine learning, satellite remote sensing, and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI). In this paper, we outline a complete intelligent agricultural solution to help farmers both choose crops and to calculate the NDVI index. Our method utilises an ensemble-based classification model, Random Forest, Support Vector Classifier (SVC), and Gradient Boosting Classifier to suggest the best crops in line with environmental and soil factors such as NPK values, temperature, humidity, rainfall, and pH values. The model shows superior classification performance, with macro and weighted averages of around 0.98 and an accuracy of 98% overall. To promote transparency and trust, we integrate Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME) for interpreting model predictions and additionally use a Large Language Model (LLM) to explain these interpretations in farmer-friendly natural language. We utilise Google Earth Engine (GEE) to, compute NDVI over time. The convergence of explainable AI, natural language interfaces, and remote sensing provides a comprehensive, data-driven decision support system for new-age agriculture in our endeavour to assist the backbone of our country - the hardworking farmers.

title: A Cognitive-Analytical Framework Merging Doctrinal and Empirical Approaches in Cyberspace
authors: Alwin Fredrick Y, Asha Sundaram, Frederick Ruby Helen
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Digital intellectual property rights (IPR) management in cyberspace faces numerous challenges, including cross-border enforcement complexities, fair use ambiguities, and emerging technology disputes. This study proposes a Cognitive-Analytical Framework integrating doctrinal and empirical approaches with advanced technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI) and blockchain to enhance IPR enforcement. Using survey data, case studies, and scenario-based simulations, the framework demonstrated its effectiveness in resolving key issues. The refined framework improved infringement detection accuracy from 85% to 92%, reduced dispute resolution time by 22% (from 7.4 to 5.8 months), and increased stakeholder satisfaction from 73% to 89%. Technologies like image recognition AI and blockchain were found to be highly effective, achieving simulated effectiveness of 93% and 87%, respectively. Legal text mining revealed that fair use disputes (34%) and cross-border issues (29%) are the most frequent challenges. The results validate the proposed framework's ability to address diverse IPR challenges while promoting harmonized global standards. This study paves the way for future integration of quantum encryption and predictive analytics to further enhance digital IPR management.

title: Optimizing Cloud Load Balancing Performance Using Hybrid Ant Colony and Artificial Bee Colony Optimization Technique
authors: Yennam Praneetha, Madishetty Vignesh, Kamsani Akash, Saroja Kumar Rout, V N L N Murthy, Shekharesh Barik
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: In the quickly changing world of cloud computing, efficient load balancing is crucial for improving customer happiness and resource efficiency. This article suggests a potent hybrid model that combines the Artificial Bee Colony (ABC) optimization methodology with the Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) method to solve load balancing issues. The technique replicates the ants' and honeybees' natural search patterns by dynamically distributing workload among virtual machines (VMs), which leads to balanced loads, quicker reaction times, and greater resource efficiency. Simulations demonstrate that the Hybrid ACO-ABC algorithm outperforms traditional methods in throughput, latency, and energy efficiency. In this paper, the ability of nature-inspired solutions to optimize cloud infrastructure efficiency is proven by providing a scalable and robust load-balancing system that adjusts to varying workloads and resource availability.

title: Automatic Modulation Recognition in OTFS Systems Using Optimized SE-CNN Architecture
authors: Md Ehsanur Rahman, Aliul Hassan Olee, Tyseer Ninad, Md Sakir Hossain, Md Akhter Jamil
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Automatic modulation recognition (AMR) plays an important role in wireless communications due to its ability to enable adaptive modulation to cope with dynamic nature of the wireless channels. However, the implementation of AMR in delay-Doppler channels presents significantly greater challenges due to the complex time-variant and frequency-selective characteristics of such environments. Very little work is done to date, most of which suffers from the limited accuracy in low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) cases. %, yet classical likelihood- and feature-based methods either impose prohibitive complexity or degrade sharply at low SNR—challenges amplified for delay-Doppler waveforms like OTFS. Prior SE-CNN designs reach near-perfect accuracy only above 8 dB SNR. Here, In this paper, we propose an improved machine learning model exploiting squeeze-and-excitation CNN (SE-CNN) that fuses, and task-specific hyper-parameter optimization. Trained on 90,000 IQ samples covering OTFS, OFDM, 2-ASK, PAM-4, and BPSK over –10 to 12 dB, the model attains 100% accuracy at 6 dB and 98.7% at 0 dB, a 6 dB gain over the baseline. ROC analysis yields macro-AUC ≈ 1.000 with 0.99 TPR at FPR ≤10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup>, extending reliable AMR 4 dB deeper into noise for real-time, high-mobility applications.

title: HD-Net: Cognitive-Theory-Powered Trajectory Prediction for Autonomous Driving
authors: Jiuyu Chen, Zhongli Wang, Jian Wang, Baigen Cai
abstract: Existing trajectory prediction models often rely on attention mechanisms to aggregate interaction features among scene elements, while simply stacking layers tends to compromise their feasibility. Recent researches have attempted to boost accuracy by introducing driving emotion or style into consideration. Even so, these approaches usually depend on data augmentation and rarely leverage cognitive theories for modeling. To advance research on cognitive-based methods, a heuristic-driven method called HD-Net is proposed in this work, which leverages cognitive theory without any expanded data. The core insight of the method lies in first defining an implicit formula for the emulation of information retrieval process, which is then used to efficiently model a graph attention framework for continuous predictions. Extensive experiments are performed on the ETH/UCY and Argoverse datasets, which demonstrate that HDNet outperforms baselines for both pedestrian and vehicle prediction tasks. Moreover, our work show that the continuous adjustment process of information can significantly enhances contextual feature and improves the prediction accuracy.

title: Thermal3D-GS: Physics-induced 3D Gaussians for Thermal Infrared Novel-view Synthesis with a Large-Scale Dataset_supp1-3663966.pdf
authors: Qian Chen
abstract: Thermal infrared imaging has attracted widespread attention in many fields due to the advantages of all-weather imaging and strong penetration. However, existing methods for thermal infrared novel-view synthesis often produce results with coarse details and floating artifacts, primarily caused by physical factors such as atmospheric transmission effects and thermal conduction. These challenges hinder accurate reconstruction of intricate structures and temperature distributions in thermal scenes, limiting the practical utility of previous approaches. To address these limitations, this paper introduces a physics-induced 3D Gaussian splatting method named Thermal3D-GS, the first novel-view synthesis method that relies exclusively on thermal infrared image. Thermal3D-GS begins by modeling atmospheric transmission effects and thermal conduction in three-dimensional media using neural networks. Additionally, considering the sparse features of infrared images, sparse feature priors are designed to improve the reconstruction accuracy of thermal infrared images. Furthermore, to validate the effectiveness of our method, the first large-scale benchmark dataset named Thermal Infrared Novel-view Synthesis Dataset (TI-NSD) is created. This dataset comprises 50 authentic thermal infrared video scenes, covering indoor, outdoor, traffic and UAV(Unmanned Aerial Vehicle) scenarios, with a total of 15,213 frames of thermal infrared image data. In addition, an expanded validation thermal infrared dataset, which includes three high-resolution scenes and five special scenes under varying atmospheric conditions and complex propagation media is constructed to assess generalization performance of the proposed method. Based on this dataset, this paper experimentally verifies the effectiveness of Thermal3D-GS. The results indicate that our method outperforms the baseline method with a 3.19 dB improvement in PSNR and significantly addresses the issues of floaters and indistinct edge features present in the baseline method. The dataset and our code are both publicly available in <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/mzzcdf/Thermal3DGS</uri>.

title: Machine Learning Predictive Modeling and Multi-Objective Optimization of 2.3D Chip-Last Fanout Wafer-Level Packages_supp1-3663333.docx
authors: Mohammad Rafiee
abstract: This paper presents a surrogate-driven, multi-objective co-design methodology combined with multi-criteria decision making for 2.3D Chip-last (RDL Redistribution layer-first) fan-out wafer-level packages (FOWLP). A 500-point finite-element dataset spanning discrete materials and geometries is used to learn Random Forest and XGBoost surrogates for four objectives: junction-to-ambient thermal resistance (ΘJA) and volume-averaged strain-energy density at the flip-chip bump, C4, and BGA (ΔWbump,ΔWc4,ΔWBGA). The surrogates guide NSGA-II exploration; RadViz provides a compact four-objective view; a NetFlow-based decision step selects a balanced design. Feature-importance analyses agree across models and match packaging physics. ΘJA is governed primarily by the mold path—mold thermal conductivity and mold cap thickness—followed by heatsink and TIM resistances; board-downward terms are secondary. Reliability metrics are controlled by levers local to each tier: silicon thickness and underfill CTEs near the die/interposer region, and BGA alloy with board/package CTEs at the board interface. A cooperative trend is observed for die thickness: within the explored range, thinning the die reduces ΔW near the die side without materially degrading ΘJA in a mold-dominated stack. Pareto sets and their RadViz embeddings reveal broad regions of low ΘJA coexisting with acceptable ΔW, from which a reliability-first champion is selected. The framework yields interpretable trade-offs and accelerates search for manufacturable 2.3D FOWLP configurations while reducing reliance on exhaustive FEA sweeps.

title: WMINet: A Wheel-Mounted Inertial Learning Approach for Mobile-Robot Positioning
authors: Gal Versano, Itzik Klein
abstract: Autonomous mobile robots are widely used for navigation, transportation, and inspection tasks indoors and outdoors. In practical situations of limited satellite signals or poor lighting conditions, navigation depends only on inertial sensors. In such cases, the navigation solution rapidly drifts due to inertial measurement errors. In this work, we propose WMINet a wheel-mounted inertial deep learning approach to estimate the mobile robot’s position based only on its inertial sensors. To that end, we merge two common practical methods to reduce inertial drift: a wheel-mounted approach and driving the mobile robot in periodic trajectories. In that sense, we demonstrate that the best place to mount a single IMU will be on the wheel, not on the chassis. We further incorporate a wheelbase constraint as a physically informed loss term that enforces the known physically fixed distance between wheels to enhance prediction accuracy. To evaluate our proposed approach we recorded using the Rosbot-XL a wheel-mounted initial dataset totaling 190 minutes, which is made publicly available. Our approach demonstrated a 66% improvement over state-of-the-art approaches. As a consequence, our approach enables navigation in challenging environments and bridges the pure inertial gap. This enables seamless robot navigation using only inertial sensors for short periods.

title: Polyethyleneimine/polyethylene glycol/cerium oxide porous core-shell structure for room-temperature carbon dioxide gas sensing
authors: Feiyan Pan, Keqi Wu, Shuo Wang, Ke Tang, Xuewei Zhu, Xiaofeng Wei
abstract: Hollow nanostructured materials possess a large specific surface area and excellent adsorptive properties, rendering them highly attractive for gas-sensing applications. In this work, a porous core-shell CeO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> architecture was synthesized via a hydrothermal route and subsequently functionalized with polyethyleneimine/polyethylene glycol (PEI/PEG) to yield PEI/PEG/CeO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> composites for room-temperature CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> detection. Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface-area analyses confirmed the high specific surface area and the formation of the porous core-shell CeO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> morphology, while SEM coupled with energy-dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS) verified successful immobilization of PEI/PEG on the CeO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> surface. Gas-sensing tests demonstrated that the PEI/PEG/CeO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> sensor achieved a detection limit as low as 200 ppm CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>; at 1500 ppm CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>, the sensor exhibited a frequency shift of 86 Hz and rapid response/recovery times of 22 s and 70 s, respectively. Furthermore, the sensor showed high sensitivity,excellent repeatability and strong resistance to humidity. The outstanding CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>-sensing performance is attributed to the synergistic effects of the porous core-shell structure, the selective recognition afforded by the amine functional groups, and the formation of n-n heterojunctions at the CeO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>-PEI interface. These findings provide deeper insight into the gas-sensing mechanisms and performance characteristics of PEI/PEG-functionalized CeO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> composites.

title: Q-Advantage Integrated Human-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Safe End-to-End Autonomous Driving
authors: Yong Wang, Pei Wang, Hongwen He, Jingda Wu, Yingjuan Tang, Zirui Kuang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) is a promising approach for end-to-end autonomous driving, but its practical deployment remains challenging due to low sample efficiency and sensitivity to reward design. To address these challenges, this study presents a novel Q-advantage integrated human-guided reinforcement learning (QIHG-RL) framework that effectively combines the strengths of machine learning and human expertise. The QIHG-RL framework features: 1) an ensemble Q-advantage function that aggregates multiple value networks to enhance value estimation, and 2) an integration mechanism that embeds the Q-advantage into both the actor-critic network and the prioritized experience replay. This design allows the agent to leverage sparse and sub-optimal human demonstrations, accelerating policy learning in the early training phase while gradually enhancing exploration as training progresses. The framework is evaluated across three safety-critical driving tasks. Experimental results show a 167% improvement in sample efficiency compared to standard RL methods and a 14% performance gain over a state-of-the-art human-guided RL baseline. Furthermore, a Sim2Real pipeline combining domain randomization and semantic denoised remapping facilitates successful deployment on a real-world autonomous vehicle.

title: Criticality Assessment Model for Intelligent Vehicle Test Scenario Based on Interactive Field Feature and Hypergraph Learning
authors: Yinzi Huang, Bing Zhu, Jian Zhao, Jiayi Han, Dongjian Song, Peixing Zhang, Shizheng Jia, Ming Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Scenario-based testing is an important part in intelligent vehicle (IV) development. The data volume of collected test scenarios is extremely large, and directly using all collected scenarios to test IVs will lead to extremely low testing efficiency. To solve this problem, a criticality assessment model (CAM) for IV test scenario based on interactive field feature (IFF) and hypergraph learning is proposed to quantify the test scenario criticality to improve the test efficiency. The IFF is constructed based on the potential field-based method to integrally consider the multidimensional coupling of scenario elements. In addition, the interaction between the fields generated by the ego vehicle and the driving environment is modeled based on Delaunay triangulation discretization method to accurately quantify the driving environment risk to the ego vehicle. The node and hyperedge of the hypergraph are used to model the individual dynamic evolution and group interaction characteristics of vehicles, respectively. Subsequently, a hypergraph learning network is constructed to extract features from the built hypergraph, IFF and traffic elements. Finally, the effectiveness, reasonableness and accuracy validation experiments are designed to validate the proposed CAM. Ablation experiment results show that the proposed IFF and hypergraph learning network enhance the CAM accuracy. The reasonableness validation results show that the proposed CAM can better find critical test scenarios than time-to-collision and time-head-way methods. The accuracy of the proposed CAM is compared through four real validation scenarios in the proving ground. The comparison results show that the proposed CAM can accurately output the quantified test scenario criticality.
