title: Detection of Fake Reviews Using Machine Learning and Behavioural Analysis: A Study on Yelp Dataset
authors: Surbhi Goyal, Pooja, Anjali Verma, Nandini Rathod
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Online reviews are very common, which has made them an important tool for both consumers and businesses trying to keep their good name. But the growing number of fake or misleading reviews is very dangerous for both customers and platforms. This study presents a comprehensive analysis for fake review detection utilizing supervised machine learning classifiers incorporating both textual and behavioral features. Analysing a Yelp dataset, linguistic patterns through NLP techniques including tokenization, lemmatization, sentiment analysis, and n-gram modelling while simultaneously capturing behavioral signals such as review frequency, temporal burstiness, and rating deviation patterns were extracted. Experimental results demonstrate that the integration of content-based and behavioral features yields superior detection performance compared to single-modality approaches. The SVM, Random Forest, and XGBoost models achieve accuracy rates of 89.7%, 91.3%, and 92.6% respectively. The article shows mixing text and behaviour analysis can help make strong and scalable systems that find fake reviews.

title: SmartCluster Decision Model (SCDM): A Hybrid Approach for Insider Threat Detection Using Clustering and Decision Trees with Iterative Feedback
authors: Shayan Abrar, Md Yeasin Newaz, Mohaimen-Bin-Noor, Mahedi Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Insider threats are still one of the most significant threats in cyber security and can escape existing detection methods through their subtle and sophisticated behaviors. These insider threats, which are often conducted by trusted insiders who have authorized access, may lead to irreversible damage to data consistency, credibility, and solvency of an enterprise. Although much work has been devoted to detecting such outliers, current approaches have difficulty in distinguishing benign from malicious behavior with high accuracy. In this paper, we propose a new hybrid method of insider threat detection which combines unsupervised clustering with decision tree classification with the aid of iterative feedback loops to enhance the model throughout. Leveraging the information from various data sets including HTTP logs, device interactions, authentication records, etc., we intended to build a framework that captures complex behaviors that may indicate suspicious behavior. Incorporation of clustering techniques facilitates flexible adjustment to change insider behaviors, whereas the decision tree classifier accomplishes a clear and interpretable classification. This method establishes a base for comprehensive, real-time threat detection systems, delivering a scalable solution for companies looking to defend themselves against insider threats.

title: Enhancing Intrusion Detection in Cyber-Physical Systems: A Hybrid Graph Neural Network and Explainable AI Approach for Classification
authors: Dipannita Chakrobortty Nijhum, Fowzia Binta Faruk Ananna, Md. Hamid Hosen, Md. Hanif Khan, Mohammed Nazim Uddin
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) and the Internet of Things (IoT) are increasingly vulnerable to sophisticated cyberattacks due to their complexity, distributed nature, and real-time operations. Traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) often struggle to manage the dynamic behaviors and topological patterns inherent in CPS environments, leading to high false positive rates and reduced adaptability. To address these challenges, we propose a multi-phase, hybrid intrusion detection framework that integrates ensemble models, traditional machine learning, and graph-based deep learning techniques. Through comprehensive data preparation, feature engineering, and statistical feature selection, we identify critical patterns in the UNSW-NB15 dataset. Advanced models, including Graph Attention Networks (GAT) and Residual Graph Convolutional Networks (Res-GCN), are employed to capture relational dependencies in network traffic. Ensemble methods like bagging and stacking further enhance model generalization. To improve transparency and trust, we incorporate Explainable AI (XAI) techniques such as LIME for model interpretability. Experimental results demonstrate that our graph-based models outperform traditional baselines, achieving over 99% accuracy while maintaining strong interpretability. This framework offers a scalable, accurate, and explainable IDS solution for modern CPS and IoT security applications.

title: Brain Tumor Classification using MRI Images with CNN and SVM Classifiers
authors: Deepak H A, Babita Jain, Naziya Farheen H S, Arpitha U R, Prajwal H S, Ayush S I
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Brain tumors present a considerable risk to human health, requiring prompt and precise identification for successful treatment strategies. This initiative aims to create an automated mechanism for identifying brain tumors utilizing MRI (Magnetic Resonance Imaging) scans through CNN and SVM. The suggested method utilizes sophisticated image processing technologies and machine learning algorithms to accurately pinpoint and categorize tumor areas. Techniques for preprocessing, including noise reduction, intensity normalization, and segmentation, are utilized to improve image quality and separate areas of interest. Methods for extracting features, along with supervised learning models, facilitate the categorization of tumor types and stages. Experimental findings reveal that the system attains elevated accuracy of 83%, sensitivity of 86%, and specificity of 79% in identifying brain tumors, offering a dependable and effective resource for clinical assessment. The suggested framework decreases reliance on manual analysis and supports radiologists in making decisions based on data, thereby enhancing patient outcomes.

title: Electromagnetic Analysis Of Double-Rotor Permanent Magnet Generator With Various Rotational Configurations
authors: Vallamula. Prudhvi Raju, Sk.Sumaya, A.Rajya Lakshmi, B.Syam Babu, K.Maruthi Sivamani, K.Kiran Kumar, M.Raja Nayak
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The Double rotor permanent magnet generator (DRPMG) is a one of the new inventions. This type of generator has two rotors, both of these rotors embedded with permanent magnets. Due to these permanent magnets, the rotors do not need separate excitation. Moreover, due to the double rotor the production of electromagnetic energy is higher and also the rate of change of flux linkages are more compared to single rotor generator. Due to this, effective utilization of electromagnetic energy the double rotor can produce more output power and efficiency compared to single rotor generator. By using these advantage, here installed two rotors of the same size of existing conventional single rotor generator. In this experimental research, conducted with transparent acrylic material which has a tremendous advantage that was a light weight material than other conventional metal material. Due to this acrylic rotor, turbines can run effectively and also get higher speeds and achieve higher electro-mechanical efficiency. Therefore, based on these advantages, this generator can be called as a future generator. This research aims to optimize DRPMG performance by using acrylic stator and rotors with different rotational configurations, especially co-rotating (same direction) and counter-rotating (opposite direction) configurations driven by two external motors. This research focuses on induced voltage and power output and efficiency with different operating conditions. This experimental data reveals significant differences in induced voltage and power output due to the electromagnetic energy interactions in both co-rotating and counter-rotating configurations. Specially the counter-rotating configuration produces higher power output and induced voltage and greater efficiency due to more rate of change of electromagnetic flux linkages and effective utilization of electromagnetic energy. Finally, this research highlights the potentiality of double rotor permanent magnet generator by utilizing the electromagnetic energy effectively. These types of generators are suggested in renewable energy applications mainly in wind power plants for effective conversion of electro-mechanical systems.

title: Optimized XGBoost Model for Binary Classification of Rice Seed Quality Using Feature Weighting
authors: B. Nazia Hassan, Somashekara M. T
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Rice is a staple food for over half of the global population, making the assessment of rice seed quality essential for food security and agricultural productivity. Despite advancements in machine learning (ML) and artificial intelligence (AI), accurately classifying rice seed quality remains challenging, particularly due to class imbalance in datasets. This study proposes an optimized XGBoost (XGB) model for binary classification of rice seed varieties—Osmancik and Cammeo— incorporating a feature weighting strategy to enhance performance. By prioritizing the most informative attributes and reducing data noise, the model achieves a high classification accuracy of 99.92%, outperforming existing ML approaches. The results highlight the model’s robustness in handling imbalanced data and its potential as a scalable, accurate solution for rice seed quality assessment. The key innovation lies in integrating effective feature selection with a fine-tuned XGB framework to improve both accuracy and generalizability.

title: Lightweight ML-Based Air Quality Prediction for IoT and Embedded Applications
authors: Md. Sad Abdullah Sami, Mushfiquzzaman Abid
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This study investigates the effectiveness and efficiency of two variants of the XGBoost regression model namely, the full-capacity and lightweight (tiny) models for predicting concentrations of carbon monoxide (CO) and nitrogen dioxide (NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>). Utilizing the AirQualityUCI dataset, collected over one year in an urban area, we comprehensively evaluated the predictive performance based on widely accepted metrics, including Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), Mean Bias Error (MBE), and the Coefficient of Determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>). Additionally, we assessed resource-focused metrics such as inference time, model size, and peak RAM usage. Results reveal that the full XGBoost model offers superior predictive accuracy across both pollutants. However, the tiny model, while slightly less precise, provides significant computational advantages in terms of drastically reduced inference time and model storage requirements. These findings highlight the feasibility of deploying simplified models in resource-constrained environments with-out substantially compromising performance, making the tiny XGBoost model particularly suitable for real-time air quality monitoring in IoT and embedded applications. This makes the work relevant for smart city air monitoring applications using edge devices.

title: Deep Learning-Based Ripeness Detection and Quality Grading of Mangoes Using Real-Time Image Processing
authors: S. Kumaran, V. Rekha, Sreelakshmi. S, Senthil Murugan. M, A. Celine Kavida, Venmani. N
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper introduces a deep learning approach to the real-time ripeness detection and quality grading of the mangoes based on image processing. Images were gathered and labeled at varying conditions to respond to part of variation in appearance and setting, and of high resolution. The pipeline uses EfficientNet-B0 as the backbone network and trains with these and performs transfer learning and augmented data and finally quantizes the network to be native to the edge. The model testing shows that the classification accuracy based on the tests conducted on the sample is 96.2% (ripeness) 94.7% (quality grading) which is significant in all possible categories. Inference latency on a Raspberry Pi 4 is reliably less than 100 millisecond per frame, proving that they are suitable to operate in real-time. The graphical representation of the system allows presenting instant and interpretable predictions, underpinning transparent decision-making by actors. Constant testing and improvement in field conditions guarantee rugged mechanical and practical performance, providing scalable automation to automate agricultural sorting tasks on a commercial scale.

title: Explainable AI-Driven Tree-Selection Stacking Random Forest with Hybrid Feature Synthesis for Lung Cancer Survival Time Prediction
authors: Mohammed Ibrahim Hussain, Kawsher Rahman, Mohammad Mamun, Mir Mohammad Azad, Safiul Haque Chowdhury, Muhammad Minoar Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Accurately predicting lung cancer patient survival duration is crucial for medical research and informed clinical decision-making. This study presents a comprehensive regression framework introducing a novel methodology called Tree Selection-based Stacking Random Forest (TSRF), specifically developed to improve Lung Cancer survival time prediction using advanced machine learning (ML) and explainable AI techniques. To enhance model learning capability, we designed Hybrid Deep Feature Synthesis (HDFS), which combines traditional clinical variables with deep-learned representations to capture complex relationships and latent patterns within the data. This integrated feature space significantly strengthens predictive performance. We evaluated five state-of-the-art ML regressors: Random Forest Regressor (RFR), Extreme Gradient Boosting Regressor (XGBR), Gradient Boosting Decision Trees Regressor (GBDTR), Light Gradient Boosting Machine Regressor (LGBMR), and our proposed TSRF Regressor (TSRFR). Among these, TSRFR achieved the best performance, attaining the highest R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.91 and the lowest Mean Squared Error (MSE) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE), demonstrating superior robustness and predictive accuracy for lung cancer survival regression. To ensure the reliability and generalizability of the results, we applied ten-fold validation and reported confidence intervals and standard deviations for all metrics. Additionally, advanced Explainable AI (XAI) methods, namely SHAP and LIME, were employed to interpret model predictions and identify the most influential clinical features, thereby improving transparency and clinical trust. This research introduces a high-performing, interpretable, and clinically relevant solution for predicting lung cancer survival time. The proposed TSRFR, supported by hybrid feature synthesis and explainability, shows strong potential to assist healthcare professionals in making precise and informed treatment decisions.

title: Kinematic Coordination and Muscle Synergy Patterns of Grasping during Lower-limb Locomotion_supp1-3663964.pdf
authors: Yan Huang

title: ARViP: Adversarial Regularization in Visuomotor Policies for Robotic VLA Purpose_supp1-3643921.pdf
authors: Bo Wu
abstract: With the development of embodied intelligence, current vision-language-action (VLA) models are difficult to deploy due to their large number of parameters thus high computational load, while the vision-based action models suffer from insufficient action accuracy in grasping tasks. To address these issues, this article proposes a novel Adversarial Regularization in Visuomotor Policy (ARViP) model. First, a proposed conditional variational autoencoder generative adversarial network architecture is applied to the existing generative action models. By discriminator distinguishing between real and generated samples, the generator is forced to produce increasingly realistic outputs, thereby increasing the accuracy of grasping. Second, to address the issue of causal confusion, we introduce a target detection model incorporating prior knowledge of grasped objects as the vision backbone. It allows the vision backbone to have prior knowledge of the feature space for grasped objects, which strengthens the object localization capabilities, and, in turn, improves vision understanding and processing capabilities. Finally, to address the issue of current models typically using uniform time-step optimization for all robotic arm joints, we propose a time-based joint regularization method. It introduces specialized loss functions tailored to the individual joint states at different time steps, thereby improving the prediction accuracy, smoothness, and robustness of motion sequences. In several real-world experiments, including assembling, disassembling, and stacking tasks, the proposed ARViP model improves grasping accuracy by 32% compared to the studied models.

title: Strategic HR Management for Employee Retention: A Machine Learning Approach Using ANN
authors: Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed, Towfika Salam, MD Amaddus Bepary Asif, Mahathir Mohammad, Rakibul Islam, Md. Yaqub Arafat, Saiful Islam Nuhash
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: The retention of employees has emerged as a primary concern in the rapidly evolving business environment of today, particularly for SMEs. When it comes to boosting employee retention and productivity, HR policies have a significant impact, particularly in SMEs. According to the findings of this research, we present an approach for predicting employee retention that is based on artificial neural networks (ANN). The suggested artificial neural network (ANN) model has been visually contrasted with the baseline models (LR and DT) in order to demonstrate how the proposed model provides more accurate predictions regarding employee retention. A score of 0.96 was obtained for the AUC among the three models, which demonstrated an accuracy of 92.10%. In comparison, the LR model earned an accuracy of 88.30%, and the DT model only achieved 85.62%. In addition, the ROC curve study provides evidence that the ANN is much superior to either the LR or the DT in terms of the employee retention anticipated, demonstrating that it possesses the highest possible discriminatory power. The findings of this study provide credence to the idea that strategic management of human resources is a significant component in the process of retaining employees. According to the findings of the research, competitive compensation packages, career development pathways, and employee-centric well-being initiatives are fundamental factors that play a significant role in reducing staff turnover rates and ensuring the organization’s long-term stability.

title: Smart Intrusion Identification System with Machine Learning-Based Data-Driven Methods for Secure Cloud Infrastructure
authors: Akhil Reddy Duggasani
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: IDS, which can identify many forms of network attacks, has been the subject of a thorough review of mountain of literature on subject. Cloud computing simplifies the process of utilizing resources on a demand-by-demand basis; however also exposes users to sophisticated hacking exploits. In such ever-changing settings, traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) often miss new threats. The paper introduces a Smart Intrusion Detection System based on ANN that effectively classifies normal and malicious traffic in networks as evidenced by using the NSL-KDD benchmark. Evaluation and training with standardized performance measures demonstrate that the model exceeds baseline models in relation to accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score. The experimental results show that the ANN model detects common and uncommon types of attacks with the minimum number of false positives and negatives, which indicates its accuracy as 97.5 percent. Comparatively, baseline models, including K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Random Forest (RF), registered lower performances. The findings show that the suggested ANN-based strategy for intelligent intrusion detection in current cloud computing systems is effective, reliable, and can be used in real-time.

title: 5G Edge-Enabled Cognitive IoT Framework for Dynamic Healthcare Monitoring and Personalized Health Recommendation System
authors: Anjanabhargavi Kulkarni, R. H. Goudar, Gautam Arjun Dematti
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The convergence of Game Theory, 5G edge-based Cognitive IoT (CIoT), and Cloud-Edge Computing has revolutionary potential for healthcare to overcome the challenges of the rapid expansion of the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT). As IoMT technologies facilitate extensive inhome health monitoring, growing patient needs to overload spectrum resources and threaten communication bottlenecks. Cloud-Edge architecture supported by 5G technology is found to be an efficient solution, consisting of three fundamental layers: data collection, edge-cloud-based security/diagnosis, and delivery of health services. The architecture facilitates high-level medical services and accurate patient suggestions through instant processing of data. Wearables and sensors input real-time health data into a centralized platform. The system utilizes DBSCAN++, a highly effective density-based clustering algorithm, to process diverse patient data. Clustering and analyzing this data, the system creates personalized recommendations—ranging from treatment plans to lifestyle changes—with 98.27% operational efficiency, enabling proactive health participation. The paradigm also facilitates real-time global monitoring by healthcare practitioners, caregivers, and family members, supporting interdisciplinary care. By integrating adaptive decision-making, real-time analytics, and secure edge-cloud infrastructure, the system closes the gap between patient-focused care and scalable resource management, paving the way for smart, responsive, and connected healthcare environments.

title: RideSure: YOLOv8-Powered Non-Helmet Detection and License Plate Recognition in Traffic Environments
authors: Sangeetha R, Dharshini S, Harivignesh T, Dharani Jayan E, Abimathi S
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Wearing a helmet is one of the best strategies to stop serious injuries in motorcycle accidents, and there is an immediate need to develop smart systems that promote compliance with traffic safety laws. The proposed solution presents an automated, real-time system used to detect motorcycle riders who don't wear helmets and get their license plate numbers. The system incorporates You Only Look Once, version 8 (YOLOv8) is a deep learning model for object detection, which allows it to find riders, helmets, and license plates in each video frame. Furthermore, the system utilizes Deep Simple Online and Realtime Tracking (Deep SORT), which assigns tracking IDs to each rider, preserving each rider’s identity across a series of video frames. When A rider without a helmet is found for a pre-defined number of video frames, the license plate is identified, and Paddle Optical Character Recognition (PaddleOCR) is then analyzed to extract the license plate number. This combined system reduces reliance on human monitoring and increases the speed and accuracy of violations. The proposed system achieved a total accuracy of 85.2% in real-time detection of helmet violations. The goal was to create a scalable and efficient way to enforce helmet laws while also improving safety on roads with video inputs in real time.

title: A Novel Deep Hybrid Learning Model for Automated Brain Tumor Detection in MRI Scans
authors: Ashok Pal Emon, Nazia Sultana Plabon, Mohammed Saifuddin Munna, Nazma Akther, Joy Dey, Indra Jit Barua
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Brain tumor diagnosis from MRI scans remains challenging due to subtle tumor features and inter-observer variability among radiologists. This study developed an automated classification system to improve diagnostic accuracy by integrating three key innovations: a hybrid ResNet101-Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) architecture, Contrast-Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) preprocessing, and domain-specific test-time augmentation (TTA). The model was trained and validated on a multi-institutional dataset of 7,023 MRI scans (glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumors, and no tumor). Results demonstrated 99.15% classification accuracy, surpassing prior state-of-the-art methods by 6–8%. The system achieved 99.6% recall for pituitary tumors and 94.3% agreement with radiologist annotations via visual explanations. With DICOM compatibility and <20ms inference time per scan, the solution integrates seamlessly into clinical workflows. This work bridges critical gaps between experimental deep learning and practical medical AI by delivering: (1) unprecedented accuracy through synergistic architecture-design optimization, (2) radiologist-aligned interpretability, and (3) hospital-ready deployment capabilities. The technology has been piloted at three medical centres, reducing preliminary reporting time by 37% in a pilot validation at three medical centres while maintaining zero false negatives in critical cases. These advancements establish a new paradigm for AI-assisted neuro-oncology diagnostics, enhancing both efficiency and reliability in clinical practice.

title: Design and Development of an Efficient Routing Protocol for WSN
authors: Raghunandan G H, S Vishali, Raman R
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Wireless sensor networks are used widely to monitor environmental and physical conditions, collect information from the surroundings, and deliver all that information toward a central-distribution point. Despite their proven effectiveness, network longevity, energy consumption, and coordinated transmission continue to present major challenges to these networks. This work introduces an entirely new method of improving the network endurance as well as the overall performance of the WSN. Particularly, a hybrid hierarchical routing scheme is being suggested to overcome some of the constraints posed by some of the existing protocols such as excessive energy consumption and high latency. The proposal offers to enhance the lifespan of WSN by decreasing intra-cluster traffic and reducing direct communication with the base station. The new paradigm combines gateway nodes with a hybrid time synchronization mechanism, an locality-sensitive hashing (LSH) based data aggregation scheme, and a centrality-based cluster selection strategy to minimize the energy consumed while transmitting data to distant BSs (Base Stations). The proposed approach is validated through a comparative analysis by which the performance metrics like latency, throughput, dead nodes, network energy consumption, and data packet transmission have been compared with conventional methods such as DR-LEACH, E-PEGASIS, and Mod LEACH. The results indicate that the hybrid method is more efficient than the traditional ones for each of these performance parameters.

title: NexaBill: Revolutionizing Retail Billing with IoT and Mobile Technology
authors: Rohit Arer, Netravati Shivasharanayya Shivayogimath, Prakash Sonwalkar, Yash Mulay
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The modern retail industry urgently needs more efficient billing systems because of the high numbers of customers and fraud attacks. This paper introduces NexaBill, a smart solution that uses AI and IoT to redefine the process of paying for things. Developed using Flutter and Firebase, NexaBill uses Google's Gemini API for input of natural language in combination with an ESP32-CAM to detect in real-time if what's left in your shopping basket matches what you've just bought. Its role-based architecture is designed to support managers, customers and cashiers with methods such as multimodal entry for product details, OTP-based security checks and intelligent computer vision to combat fraud. Rigorous evaluations have shown a more than 99% detection rate, with all checkout times exceeding 30 seconds. This is a special tweak so your NexaBill becomes the leading solution for modern high-traffic retail automation.

title: FabGPT: An Efficient Large Multimodal Model for Complex Wafer Defect Knowledge Queries
authors: Yuqi Jiang, Qian Jin, Xudong Lu, Xiangyue Lyu, Zhuolun He, Xunzhao Yin, Yali Fu, Dawei Gao, Bei Yu, Qi Sun, Hanming Wu, Cheng Zhuo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Advancements in large multimodal models (LMMs) for text and image understanding have provided a new paradigm for semiconductor intelligent manufacturing. However, due to the knowledge-intensive nature of the integrated circuit (IC) domain, their effectiveness in real-world applications is limited. To address this, we propose a customized model, FabGPT, to enhance the wafer image detection and domain knowledge querying in real manufacturing environments. The model includes a modal enhancement stage for optimized wafer feature representation, a detection stage for identifying tiny wafer defects, and a question-answering (Q&A) stage that combines modulation modules with an interactive corpus training strategy, improving the performance of LMM in semiconductor manufacturing scenarios. To further improve FabGPT’s performance in scenarios with limited annotations, we introduce an unsupervised learning paradigm, utilizing selective storage and matching strategies to achieve pixel-level defect localization and querying across various scenarios. Additionally, in the Q&A stage, we upgrade a multi-objective modulation module and enrich a specialized corpus to systematically assess the relationships between modalities, seamlessly integrating IC knowledge into the pre-trained model. This improves the accuracy of root cause analysis and addresses the “modality bias”. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FabGPT excels at pixel-level defect detection without the need for defect labels and surpasses other large models in expert-level process Q&A tasks, showcasing its potential as a transformative tool in IC manufacturing.

title: Understanding Over-Squashing in Dynamic Graphs_supp1-3658409.pdf
authors: Jianghong Ma
abstract: Graph neural networks (GNNs) have demonstrated significant success in solving real-world problems using both static and dynamic graph data. While static graphs remain constant, dynamic graphs evolve over time, presenting unique challenges that necessitate integrating GNN computations with sequential models. Despite advancements, existing research has primarily focused on static graphs, with dynamic graphs receiving comparatively less attention. This study extends the investigation of over-squashing—a phenomenon where excessive information compression leads to the loss of distant node information—from static to dynamic graphs. Over-squashing is exacerbated in dynamic graphs due to the combined compression of spatial and temporal information into narrow time windows. To address this issue, we propose the spatial and temporal compensation model for dynamic graphs, which is theoretically validated and incorporates two key modules: the structural similarity-based spatial compensation (SSSC) module and the representation and trend similarity-based temporal compensation (RTSTC) module. The former module mitigates spatial information loss by leveraging structural similarities among nodes, while the latter module addresses temporal information loss by integrating historical data and trends. The extensive experiments on real-world dynamic graph datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance. The datasets and source codes are released at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/wuchaokai/STCDG/</uri>

title: Data-Driven Adaptive Cruise Control for Trucks with Lane-Keeping Model
authors: Shuyou Yu, Junting Liu, Shuo Wang, Huan Chang, Zhuqing Shi, Hong Chen
abstract: This paper proposes a data-driven adaptive cruise control (ACC) strategy for trucks, incorporating the lane-keeping assistance. It addresses both the maintainace of a safe longitudinal distance and lateral tracking of the road centerline. To handle system constraints, model predictive control (MPC) is employed. The nonlinear characteristics of trucks are approximated using Koopman operator theory and deep neural network (DNN) techniques, yielding a global linear predictive model. Furthermore, a comparative analysis between first-principle modeling and data-driven modeling methods is conducted using TruckSim and Simulink. The results demonstrate the proposed ACC strategy's effectiveness in reducing computational time while ensuring longitudinal safety and lateral lane-keeping capabilities.

title: Vulnerability Assessment of AI-Based Privacy Mechanisms in Industrial IoT Consumer Systems
authors: Ahmed Alsayat, Hayam Alamro, Mohammed Abaker, Ibrahim Abunadi, Saleh Alharbi, Mohammed Aljaafari, Turke Althobaiti, Bandar M. Alghamdi
abstract: The secure development of consumer systems with Artificial Intelligence (AI) strength within the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) has gotten more obscure. These systems are being dependent on sensitive information and are secured with state-of-the-art Privacy-Preserving Machine Learning (PPML) methods like Federated Learning (FL). But there are still weaknesses at the consumer side, and these are largely involving mobile companion applications that manage the IIoT units. This leads to a feedback loop in vulnerability where protection of the model can be broken through the vulnerability of consumer-sensitive software. To counteract these interrelated risks, the new proposed model, the Robust Privacy-Preserving Vulnerability Assessment Model (RPVAM), presented in this paper, provides a holistic risk assessment of systems. Federated Defense Mechanism (FDM) after robust aggregation, and Privacy-Enhanced Federated Defense Mechanism (PE-FDM) after Local Differential Privacy (LDP) are two defense strategies that are tested against each other in this framework. Attacks of data poisoning are tested in different strengths. It is shown that the two mechanisms perform more strongly than the standard FL in terms of utility and robustness in the face of an attack. It also shows the trade-offs that will always exist between the three dimensions of balancing: privacy, robustness, and utility, and explains the shortcomings of the existing siloed-based approaches. It is suggested that the paradigm change of security evaluation practice should be proposed and that the idea of unified models is in respect of the entire data lifecycle subscribing to the consumer endpoint through to the AI core.

title: CGCN-DF: A Cascade GCN Vulnerability Detection Framework for Secure CIoT Smart Contracts
authors: Keqing Wang, Wenyin Zhang, Mengru Tu, Weihao Su
abstract: With the widespread use of smart contracts in blockchain applications, particularly in the consumer Internet of Things (CIoT), the security of smart contracts has become increasingly critical. CIoT refers to an IoT environment where various consumer devices are intelligently connected and interact via the internet. In this context, smart contracts are frequently used to automate tasks such as device control, data sharing, and transaction verification. However, vulnerabilities in smart contracts can lead to system attacks, thereby compromising the security of the entire IoT network. The Cascade Graph Convolutional Network-based Vulnerability Detection Framework for smart contracts (CGCN-DF) proposed in this study effectively enhances the security assessment of smart contracts in CIoT applications. Devices and systems in CIoT environments typically exhibit high heterogeneity and complex interaction patterns, but a single graph structure cannot fully capture the multidimensional behavioral features and complex structural relationships of smart contracts. To address this issue, this paper introduces the Semantic Contract Graph (SCG), which integrates three graph representations—Abstract Syntax Tree (AST), Control Flow Graph (CFG), and Data Flow Graph (DFG)—into a unified graph structure, comprehensively covering different aspects of the code. Furthermore, as smart contracts in CIoT environments often involve real-time data flows and complex execution paths, the CGCN-DF framework employs a cascading mechanism that performs three-level graph convolutional processing through relational, Meta-path structures, and cyclic structures. This approach extracts rich and complementary information from multi-level features, explicitly models the dynamic interactions among syntax, execution paths, and data flows, and enhances the model’s contextual awareness of vulnerability-triggering conditions. Ultimately, the framework achieves coarse-grained detection at the contract level and fine-grained detection at the line level. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively localize vulnerabilities down to specific code lines. This not only enhances the precision and practicality of smart contract vulnerability detection but, more importantly, contributes an innovative technical framework and theoretical methodology to the CIoT field—particularly in areas such as DSL program analysis and the intersection of IoT and blockchain security. This work thereby helps advance the field toward more refined and context-aware security analysis aligned with the realistic characteristics of complex systems.

title: Differentiable Grouped Feedback Delay Networks for Learning Coupled Volume Acoustics_supp1-3663925.mp4
authors: Orchisama Das
abstract: Rendering dynamic reverberation in a complicated acoustic space for moving sources and listeners is challenging but crucial for enhancing user immersion in extended-reality (XR) applications. Capturing spatially varying room impulse responses (RIRs) is costly and often impractical. Moreover, dynamic convolution with measured RIRs is computationally expensive with high memory demands, typically not available on wearable computing devices. Grouped Feedback Delay Networks (GFDNs), on the other hand, allow efficient rendering of coupled room acoustics. However, its parameters need to be tuned to match the reverberation profile of a coupled space. In this work, we propose the concept of Differentiable GFDNs (DiffGFDNs), which have tunable parameters that are optimised to match the late reverberation profile of a set of RIRs captured from a space that exhibits multi-slope decay. Once trained on a finite set of measurements, the DiffGFDN interpolates to unmeasured locations in the space. We propose a parallel processing pipeline that has multiple DiffGFDNs with frequency-independent parameters processing each octave band. The parameters of the DiffGFDN can be updated rapidly during inferencing as sources and listeners move. We evaluate the proposed architecture against the Common Slopes (CS) model on a dataset of RIRs for three coupled rooms. The proposed architecture generates multi-slope late reverberation with low memory and computational requirements, achieving a better energy decay relief (EDR) error and slightly worse octave-band energy decay curve (EDC) errors compared to the CS model. Furthermore, DiffGFDN requires an order of magnitude fewer floating-point operations per sample than the CS renderer.

title: Swapping and Purification Scheme Optimization for Entanglement Distribution in Quantum Networks_supp1-3663243.pdf
authors: Yu Wang
abstract: Swapping and purification are the two fundamental building blocks for high-fidelity entanglement distribution in multi-hop quantum networks. Unfortunately, it is still a mystery how they intertwine with each other to affect the fidelity and cost of end-to-end entanglements. Current scheduling algorithms consider this problem under relatively limited assumptions and a critical yet unjustified conjecture. In this work, we first consider more general assumptions with operation failures and, accordingly, extend a tree-based modeling for joint swapping and purification. Then, we analytically prove the previous conjecture that the optimal strategy under Binary system is always to purify the entanglements before any swapping. This sheds light on the protocol and device design for quantum networks. We then further propose a tree-based algorithm, which can efficiently schedule swapping and purification along a path for both Binary and Werner systems. Extensive simulations of the proposed method against state-of-the-art solutions show that our method uses fewer entanglements to establish qualified end-to-end entanglements, and thus achieves higher network throughput.

title: Interpretable Semantic Image Segmentation Using U-Net and Visual Diagnostics
authors: Afzalul Abid Nazir, Islam Saiful, Md. Faruk Abdullah Al Sohan, Debajyoti Karmaker
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Semantic image segmentation is crucial for the accurate interpretation of geological imagery, facilitating informed decision-making in resource management, environmental monitoring, and geological exploration. Predictive segmentation models using deep learning techniques enable automated analysis and enhance decision-making processes. To precisely segment geological imagery, this research uses a U-Net-based segmentation model that includes a pre-trained convolutional neural network (CNN) encoder (VGG16), enhanced by Batch Normalization and dropout regularization. Intersection over Union (IoU) and total pixel accuracy metrics are used to assess the model’s performance. The model achieves an overall pixel accuracy of 96.98% and an overall mean IoU of 90.28%, according to the results. The IoU scores per tile ranged from 88% to 93%, and the Dice Coeﬃcient Score was 89.98%, indicating robust segmentation performance across diverse image tiles. The U-Net-based model, combined with visual diagnostics, including Grad-CAM heatmaps and pixel-wise error analysis, shows a powerful capability to provide both high accuracy and interpretability. Thus, this method offers valuable information to improve the analysis of geological data, resource allocation, and decision-making in relevant industries.

title: Design and Implementation of a Real-Time Water Quality Analyzer Using Arduino-Based TDS and Temperature Sensors
authors: Vikas Nandeshwar, Tejas R. Kulkarni, Samiksha S. Lad, Aditya V. Kulsange, Sharvari R. Kukade, Om D. Kumbhar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Access to clean drinking water is critical for public health, yet many water sources contain dissolved solids, salts, and microbial contaminants that are not visually detectable but pose serious health risks. This paper presents the design and implementation of a portable, low-cost water quality monitoring device that measures three key parameters: Total Dissolved Solids (TDS), temperature, and electrical conductivity. The system is built using an Arduino UNO microcontroller, a TDS sensor, a digital temperature sensor, and a 16×2 LCD display. Real-time readings are displayed to the user, with integrated LED indicators and a buzzer for threshold-based alerts. When the TDS level exceeds a predefined safety limit, a red LED and buzzer are activated, while a green LED indicates safe water quality. The device is intended for applications in domestic settings, water treatment units, and rural field testing. Its ease of construction, affordability, and real-time feedback make it a practical solution for enhancing water safety awareness and supporting localized water quality assessment.

title: Sun-driven Contactless Real-Time Charging System
authors: S J Savita, Raksha G, Shefali R Jain, Varshini M
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) offer significant environmental benefits by reducing greenhouse gas emissions and fuel costs. However, a significant part of the challenges in the acceptance of EVs is the prolonged charging time and lack of involving charging facilities in remote areas. This paper proposes a novel system that utilizes solar-powered contactless Real-time charging system using Wireless Power Transmission (WPT) technology through inductive coupling, allowing vehicles to charge while in motion, thus eliminating the need for stops at recharge stations. The system is designed using a combination of solar panels, including copper coils, 2N2222 transistors, solar panels, Li-ion batteries, voltage regulators, and LED indicators. These components work together to create a seamless charging experience for EV users.

title: Multi-model Fusion of Cardiac Image for Risk Prediction with Deep Learning
authors: Beena R, Umagandhi R, Arunpriya C
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease remains the leading cause of mortality worldwide, motivating improved risk assessment tools. Traditional risk scores (e.g. Framingham, Pooled Cohort Equations) rely on demographics and lab values, but fail to fully capture an individual’s anatomical risk factors. Modern medical imaging provides rich information on cardiac and vascular structure that can enhance risk prediction. Recent work has applied deep learning to cardiac CT and MRI to automatically extract prognostic biomarkers and predict adverse outcomes. This paper review recent advances in cardiac risk prediction using CT/MRI, with a focus on deep learning and transformer models, and discuss how the new CardioScans dataset (paired cardiac CT angiography and MRI) can enable multimodal fusion experiments.

title: HealthSenseFusion for Healthcare Industry: IoT based Integrated Model for Remote Monitoring and Disease Prediction
authors: Petikam Sailaja, Asmitha Dinesh, Laura Blossom, Soundharya, Avanthika, Mudhuraanjani
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The rapid advancement of IoT-driven healthcare monitoring has enabled real-time disease prediction and patient management. HealthSenseFusion is an integrated AI model that leverages edge computing, federated learning, and deep learning to enhance remote healthcare monitoring and anomaly detection. The model was evaluated across multiple health conditions, achieving an accuracy of 94.2% for cardiovascular disease detection, 92.8% for respiratory disorders, 91.5% for diabetes monitoring, and 96.1% for general anomaly detection. By integrating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) with an attention mechanism, HealthSenseFusion significantly improves prediction accuracy, inference speed, and computational efficiency. In comparison with existing models, HealthSenseFusion reduced inference time by 50% and improved privacy compliance with federated learning and homomorphic encryption. The real-time anomaly detection module demonstrated 96.5% accuracy in identifying sudden heart rate drops and 97.1% accuracy in detecting abnormal Sp0<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> fluctuations. Additionally, early intervention using this system reduced mortality rates by up to 54.5% in diabetic complications and 48.4% in cardiac arrest cases. With its optimized energy efficiency (10W power consumption) and low-cost processing ($1.00 per 1000 predictions), HealthSenseFusion presents a scalable, secure, and highly accurate healthcare monitoring solution for real-world deployment.

title: Adaptive System Transition for Cloud-Native Platforms: Iterative Architectural Refinement
authors: Shikha Parekh
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper addresses the complexities of transforming legacy application environments into modern containerized infrastructures. We present the architectural design and implementation of an automated system for black-box migration from virtual machines to container platforms. The development employs a cyclical methodology of analysis, design, prototyping, and evaluation, where insights from testing and expert assessments act as a continuous feedback loop for system improvement. This iterative refinement process ensures robust and adaptable migration capabilities, facilitating the seamless transition of applications to cloud-native ecosystems while enhancing operational stability and portability.

title: Deep Learning-Based Sarcasm Detection with Oxymoron Feature Integration for Sentiment Analysis
authors: R. Kowsalya, S. Reshmi, S. Ahamed Johnsha Ali, R. Durai Vasanth, B. Senthilkumaran, J. Viji Gripsy
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Sarcasm detection is crucial for accurate sentiment analysis in social media but remains difficult due to sarcasm’s figurative nature and subtle linguistic cues. Traditional sentiment classifiers often misinterpret sarcastic text, as literal sentiment words (e.g. "love" in "I love working here for nothing") convey opposite intent. This research propose a novel deep learning framework that explicitly incorporates oxymoron and other linguistic features to improve sarcasm classification. Our model combines pre-trained word embeddings with CNN and BiLSTM layers to capture n-gram and contextual patterns, and it integrates engineered features for sentiment polarity, punctuation, syntax, and oxymora (contradictory word pairs). Experiments on a labeled Twitter dataset (tweets annotated via hashtags like #sarcasm) show that the proposed model outperforms baseline CNN, LSTM, BiLSTM, and MLP classifiers. The model achieves 90.2% accuracy versus 89.9% for the best baseline, and similarly higher precision, recall, and F1-score. The inclusion of the oxymoron feature yields noticeable performance gains, addressing an often-overlooked cue. These results demonstrate that integrating rich linguistic indicators, especially oxymora, with deep learning substantially enhances sarcasm detection in sentiment analysis.

title: Enhancing Diabetes Prediction with a Random Forest Ensemble of Extreme Learning Machine and Long Short-Term Memory Models
authors: Moonisah Nisar, Narinder Kumar, Pooja Sharma, Kiranpreet Kaur, Shilpa, Rajdeep Kaur
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This study introduces a novel hybrid ensemble approach for enhanced diabetes prediction, combining the strengths of an Extreme Learning Machine (ELM), a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network, and a Random Forest (RF) classifier. The proposed framework addresses limitations of existing models by leveraging ELM’s rapid training, LSTM’s capacity to capture temporal dependencies (although less critical for this dataset), and RF’s robust predictive power. Specifically, ELM and LSTM models are trained independently, and their predictions are then used as input features for the RF classifier. Evaluated on the widely used Pima Indians Diabetes Dataset, the ensemble model achieves a remarkable 93% accuracy and an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 89%, significantly outperforming individual models and demonstrating improvements over previous hybrid approaches. This optimized balance between computational efficiency and predictive accuracy makes the proposed ensemble highly suitable for practical clinical applications in diabetes diagnosis and management

title: Fuzzy Logic and Deep Learning-Based Framework for Accurate Classification of Lung Cancer in CT Images
authors: L. M. I. Leo Joseph, Ahmar Husain, Siraj Hussain, Manoj Senthil K, R. Nithya, S. Devisri
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Lung cancer (LC) is a very widespread malignancy impacting both genders and remains the foremost cause of cancer-related mortality globally, with a notable increase in incidence since the onset of the century. Precise detection and staging are crucial for efficient therapy, and imaging techniques like PET/CT have demonstrated their utility in augmenting traditional CT scans by boosting the assessment of nodal and metastatic locations, as well as therapeutic response. This work utilised CT images of the lungs as input for processing, employing the GLCM technique to diminish the dimensionality of deep features and facilitate more effective differentiation of malignant nodules. A fuzzy logic model was then developed with four input variables, producing a singular output variable to facilitate categorisation decisions. Additionally, ANN models were assessed using various activation functions and optimizers to ascertain their diagnostic efficacy. The optimal result was achieved with an ANN employing the softmax activation function and Adam's optimizer, resulting in an accuracy of 96.23%. The results indicate that the integration of GLCM, fuzzy logic, and ANN modelling provides a potential computational framework for enhancing the early diagnosis and therapeutic evaluation of lung cancer in CT images.

title: Exploring the Foundations of Academic Excellence: Advanced Data-Driven Approaches to Student Outcome Prediction
authors: Abu Bakkor, Rawshan Jahan Ruku, Md. Farukuzzaman Khan, Mohammed Ibrahim Hussain, Md. Shafiul Alam Chowdhury, Muhammad Minoar Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This study aimed to predict student academic performance using data from 1,000 students, including study habits, lifestyle, and exam scores. After preprocessing based on observations from the visualization of different plots and heatmaps, several ensemble regression models were executed. These models are Bagging Regressor (BAGGINGR), Random Forest Regressor (RFR), Voting Regressor (VOTINGR), Extreme Gradient Boosting Regressor (XGBOOSTR), Gradient Boosting Decision Tree Regressor (GBDTR), and Stacking Regressor (STACKINGR). Performance was evaluated using Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Mean Squared Error (MSE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Coefficient of Determination (R-squared), Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), and Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR). Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methods, namely Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP)and Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME), detected key features, with study hours per day being the most important. Stacking Regressor (STACKINGR) outperformed others with a Mean Absolute Error of 4.21, Root Mean Squared Error of 5.30, and R-squared of 0.89. The research offers an accurate, interpretable tool for data-driven educational support.

title: Forecasting Crime Trends in Bangladesh Through a Pre and Post 2024 Political Shift Analysis Using Machine Learning
authors: Tonmoy Kumar Sarker, Dipankar Roy Dip, Abdullah Al Hasib, Md. Tafhimul Haque Sadi, Dip Sarker, Md. Rakibul Islam, Dipta Gomes
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: This research explores the effects of major political disruptions on crime dynamics, focusing on Bangladesh. Leveraging machine learning techniques, we analyzed how crime behavior changed before and after a significant political event in mid-2024, which led to widespread unrest and a shift in governance. A variety of regression models were trained on monthly crime data preceding the transition, including Linear Regression, Polynomial Regression, XGBoost, LightGBM, Prophet, and Random Forest. Among them, Random Forest produced the best predictive accuracy, with a mean absolute error (MAE) of 2302 and root mean squared error (RMSE) of 3131 on the pre-transition data. The model was then used to forecast crime behavior during the post-transition period. The results revealed a sharp decline in performance, with tolerance-based accuracy falling from 96.9% before the event to 50% after the event. This significant drop highlights a measurable shift in crime dynamics, likely driven by heightened political uncertainty, weakened law enforcement, and changing public behavior. Supporting visualizations, including heat maps and trend comparisons, confirmed alterations in both crime seasonality and intensity. Our findings underscore the importance of incorporating socio-political awareness into predictive policing models, and demonstrate that traditional forecasting techniques may fail when confronted with sudden systemic disruptions. This study contributes to the growing field of AI-driven criminology by illustrating how machine learning can help quantify the societal impact of political instability on public safety.

title: MiniGCN: Mini-Batch Graph Convolutional Networks for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Jayant Shekhar, M. Ramya, A. Chandrasekar, S. Nithyanandh, Deepa P, A. Madhan
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Information Technology and Communication Networks (GITCON)
abstract: The problem in the classification of hyperspectral images has been tough since the spectral dimension is high, the number of labeled sample data is low, and there are computing limitations. Conventional convolutional tools tend to fail in modeling long-range multispectral dependencies, and full-batch Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs) are bad in terms of scalability. It proposes using MiniGCN, which is a mini-batch graph convolutional architecture capable of combining spectral-spatial compositions of features and localizing subgraph sampling to make the training effective. The model unites convolutions of 1D spectra and 2D space, and then performs feature aggregation in sampled neighborhoods using graphs. Examples of experimental evaluation on benchmark datasets indicate accuracies of 98.1%, 99.04%, and 99.26% on Indian Pines, Pavia University, and Salinas, respectively, when compared to other approaches. MiniGCN also results in a more compact memory size, 65% less, inference time 50% less, and very few parameters, 1.47 million, and therefore has a practical implementation speed. The scalably and accuracy in hyperspectral classification make it quite useful in large-scale remote sensing applications in resource-limited contexts.

title: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Low-Resource Languages: A Comparative Study of LLMs for Bengali Hate Speech Detection
authors: Akif Islam, Mohd Ruhul Ameen
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Bengali social media platforms have witnessed a sharp increase in hate speech, disproportionately affecting women and adolescents. While datasets such as BD-SHS provide a basis for structured evaluation, most prior approaches rely on either computationally costly full-model fine-tuning or proprietary APIs. This paper presents the first application of Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) for Bengali hate speech detection using LoRA and QLoRA. Three instruction-tuned large language models—Gemma-3-4B, Llama-3.2-3B, and Mistral-7B were fine-tuned on the BD-SHS dataset of 50,281 annotated comments. Each model was adapted by training fewer than 1% of its parameters, enabling experiments on a single consumer-grade GPU. The results show that Llama-3.2-3B achieved the highest F1-score of 92.23%, followed by Mistral-7B at 88.94% and Gemma-3-4B at 80.25%. These findings establish PEFT as a practical and replicable strategy for Bengali and related low-resource languages.

title: PhishXDefender: An Innovative Approach to Automated Phishing and XSS Detection Using Proprietary Algorithms
authors: Pavan B, Sanjay Saravanan N, H. Nishanth Babu, Pranathi Chowdary Atluri, Trisha. V, Veena K
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Phishing and cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks are major cyber security threats, damaging financial reputations. Traditional detection methods struggle to keep pace with changing attack patterns, and comprehensive solutions are needed. This review introduces PhishXDefender, a new approach that uses proprietary algorithms for automated phishing and XSS detection. The ultimate goal is to enhance cyber security security through the integration of code detection, machine learning, and anomaly detection techniques. Unlike existing solutions, PhishXDefender's proprietary algorithms feature unique feature extraction and hybrid detection techniques that address current knowledge and practice gaps Phishing and XSS content-specific custom features, such as script tags, and keyword occurrences, used alongside most voting methods to improve search accuracy Are, can also reduce false-positives This multi-faceted approach ensures robust security, real-time and adapts to threats if is coming, providing consistent and reliable protection. The importance of PhishXDefender lies in its ability to seamlessly integrate with existing security infrastructure and deliver to organizations of varying sizes Comprehensive assessment through multilevel authentication methods highlights its efforts, providing detailed insights and reporting to help mitigate risk. By addressing the limitations of traditional methods, PhishXDefender sets a new standard in phishing and XSS detection, ultimately enhancing the overall cyber security infrastructure

title: Adaptive AI for Cyber Defense: Research-Driven Optimization of Threat Detection, Response, and Data Integrity
authors: Mohanraju Muppala
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper examines how artificial intelligence can be systematically leveraged to optimize core cybersecurity functions across modern, data-intensive environments. We synthesize and critique AI-driven methods for real-time anomaly detection, adversarially robust classification, and automated incident triage, alongside optimization strategies for firewall rule analysis, policy hardening, and risk-based prioritization. The paper highlights integration patterns that combine machine learning with distributed and edge-centric architectures to reduce latency, improve data locality, and harden telemetry pipelines against tampering. We assess privacy-preserving learning paradigms (e.g., federated and split learning) for cross-domain collaboration without centralizing sensitive data, and evaluate blockchain-backed timestamping for auditability and forensic integrity. The analysis identifies recurring failure modes—model drift, evasion, data quality blind spots—and proposes a research agenda centered on self-learning defense loops, explainable detection, continuous red-teaming, and quantum-resilient cryptographic controls. The result is a consolidated, research-based blueprint for applying AI to measurably enhance detection accuracy, accelerate response, and strengthen end-to-end security assurance in dynamic enterprise and edge settings.

title: Time Series Anomaly Detection using Bidirectional Generative Adversarial Network
authors: N. Nithin Goud, S. Meena Sri, CH. Nagaraju, M. A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: Anomaly detection in time series data is essential in most real applications like system monitoring, fraud detection, and predictive maintenance. Traditional approaches based on rules or statistics will be unable to deal with complicated patterns or other types of anomalies. In this paper, we present an unsupervised deep learning method for time series anomaly detection from a Bidirectional Generative Adversarial Network (BiGAN). Contrary to typical GANs that produce data out of noise only, BiGANs have an encoder which maps real data onto latent space. This allows the model to learn not only to generate data, but also to decipher its underlying structure. To manage the time- dependent structure of sequential data, we use Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks in all components of the BiGAN generator, discriminator, and encoder. During training, the model learns to differentiate between actual time-series sequences and those caused by noise, and the encoder captures significant hidden patterns. When it comes time to identify anomalies, the model compares how well it can build a sequence and how similarly the encoded representations coincide. Larger differences usually indicate an anomaly. Our experiments on standard datasets show that this BiGAN-based approach can detect even highly subtle and complex anomalies without requiring labeled data, making it particularly helpful in day-to-day circumstances where annotated diagrams are tough to find. The model achieved an accuracy of 91.74%, demonstrating its effectiveness in real-time anomaly detection scenarios.

title: Toward Inclusive AI: Building a Bangla Chatbot Using GPT-Neo and Optimized BPE Tokenizer
authors: Nafiul Alam, K. M. Azharul Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: An advancement in the field of Ai and natural language processing (NLP) is the leveraging the GPT-Neo architecture to develop an interactive chatbot. This study highlights the significance of providing NLP tools for Bangla, spoken by over 230 million people but underrepresented in computational linguistics, using GPT-2 by employing GPT-Neo for its superior language understanding and generation capabilities. The research involves curating a custom dataset from various Bangla sources to reflect the language’s complexities, followed by a rigorous pretraining and fine-tuning process with a conversational dataset aimed at enhancing the chatbot’s ability to produce contextually coherent and fluent dialogues. A notable contribution is the creation of an advanced Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) tokenizer optimized for Bangla, significantly improving the model’s efficiency in language processing. The paper details the methodology for dataset preparation, model training, and tokenizer development, positioning the chatbot as a pivotal tool for Bangla language processing and setting the stage for further efforts to make the AI more inclusive of linguistically diverse communities. Finally it discusses the potential applications, ethical considerations, and directions for future research.

title: A Deep Learning Framework Based on DenseNet Architecture for Accurate Diagnosis of Neurodegenerative Diseases Using Multimodal Medical Imaging Data
authors: Ragini Y P, Ahmad Abdelhafiz Ali Samhan, T Aswani, B Rajalakshmi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper presents a deep learning framework, which utilizes DenseNet structure, to diagnose neurodegenerative diseases using multimodal medical images accurately. The new design takes advantage of the efficient feature interdependency and gradients of DenseNet and uses them to enhance normal to abnormal brain status classification. The performance was analyzed at length with various indices. The model had an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.88 which implies high discriminatory power. The confusion matrix lifted a high level of true positive rate with minimum false negative rate especially in abnormal cases. To display the plot of the separability of classes in the feature space, the dimensionality reduction (PCA and t-SNE) were applied. Moreover, precision-recall analysis and training-validation curves proved the strength and generalizing of the model. The outcomes confirm the utility of DenseNet-related designs in healthcare diagnostics and point to the promise of the incorporation of deep learning in clinical practice in the early-stage diagnosis of neurodegenerative disorders.

title: SafeHaven-FOSS: Offline Emergency Communication App Using Bluetooth Mesh and Wi-Fi Direct
authors: Chempavathy B, Jahnavi Lakarapu, Megha Senthil Kumar, Nithyashree C, Harini Mukunda
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: During times of natural disasters, network failure, or in remote locations, unavailability of internet and cellular networks significantly restricts communication, slowing rescue and endangering lives. This study addresses the issue of communication breakdown by developing SafeHaven-Foss, an offline emergency communication application that operates independently of traditional network infrastructure. The system employs Bluetooth Mesh for proximate connectivity and Wi-Fi Direct for distant peer-to-peer networking, creating an ad hoc decentralized network of communications. The app features real-time messaging, alert distribution, and location sharing, guaranteeing secure transmission of critical information among nearby users in isolated settings. SafeHaven-Foss, being Free and Open-Source Software (FOSS), ensures transparency, flexibility, and scalability across various devices and settings.

title: Contextual Language Modeling for Bangla Using Word Embeddings
authors: Sanjoy Kumar Roy, K. M. Azharul Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Computing, Applications and Systems (COMPAS)
abstract: Language modeling is a fundamental task in natural language processing (NLP) that involves predicting the next word (or character, or token) in a sequence based on the words that came before it. Text-based communication remains a dominant form of interaction, where predictive text input systems significantly reduce typing effort and improve user experience. Although such systems are well studied in resourceful languages like English, limited research exists for a resource-scarce language like Bangla. This paper proposes a context-sensitive word prediction model for the Bangla language using the Skip-Gram variant of the Word2Vec algorithm. Unlike traditional N-gram models that rely on word frequency and fail with unseen vocabulary, our approach learns dense vector representations that capture contextual similarity. A domain-specific Bangla corpus related to "কোভিড-১৯" consisting of 64,000 rows was used for training. The model effectively clusters semantically similar words and predicts the next probable word with approximately 87% accuracy. This work demonstrates the potential of neural embedding techniques for the prediction of Bangla sequences and addresses the challenge of unseen words by leveraging contextual embeddings.
